// Interaction state tracking for RuiShin immediate-mode UI system.
// Handles hover, pressed (active), and focus states for immediate-mode components.
//
// This module was relocated from ui/theme/interaction.odin as part of Phase 10B.5.a
// because it implements Phase 11 runtime state tracking, which is out of scope for
// Phase 10B (data layer only).
//
// Phase 10B provides theme data; Phase 11 provides runtime behavior and rendering.

import platform "project:platform"

// UI_ID uniquely identifies a UI element during a frame.
UI_ID :: u64

// UI_Context manages global interaction states.
UI_Context :: struct {
	hovered_id: UI_ID,
	active_id:  UI_ID,
	focus_id:   UI_ID,

	// Internal state for current frame
	mouse_pos:  [2]f32,
	is_down:    bool,
	was_down:   bool,
}

// ui_get_id generates a simple hash-based ID for an element.
ui_get_id :: proc(name: string, index: int = 0) -> UI_ID {
	// Simple FNV-1a like hash for demonstration
	h: u64 = 14695981039346656037
	for i in 0 ..< len(name) {
		h = (h ~ u64(name[i])) * 1099511628211
	}
	h = (h ~ u64(index)) * 1099511628211
	return UI_ID(h)
}

// ui_interaction_begin starts the interaction tracking for the current frame.
ui_interaction_begin :: proc(ctx: ^UI_Context, input: ^platform.Input_State) {
	ctx.mouse_pos = input.mouse_pos
	ctx.was_down = ctx.is_down
	ctx.is_down = input.mouse_buttons[int(platform.Mouse_Button.Left)]

	// Reset hover for the new frame
	ctx.hovered_id = 0
}

// ui_interaction_end finalizes the interaction tracking.
ui_interaction_end :: proc(ctx: ^UI_Context) {
	if !ctx.is_down {
		ctx.active_id = 0
	}
}

// ui_check_interaction determines the interaction state of an element.
// Returns one of: "", "hover", "pressed"
ui_check_interaction :: proc(ctx: ^UI_Context, x, y, w, h: f32, id: UI_ID) -> string {
	rect := platform.Rect{x, y, w, h}
	is_inside := platform.hitTestRect(rect, ctx.mouse_pos.x, ctx.mouse_pos.y)

	if is_inside {
		ctx.hovered_id = id
		if ctx.is_down && (ctx.active_id == 0 || ctx.active_id == id) {
			ctx.active_id = id
			return "pressed"
		}
		return "hover"
	}

	return ""
}

// Retained-mode component structures for RuiShin.
// These structs store persistent state and are themed via the theme system.

import theme "project:ui/theme"

// Button represents a retained-mode button component.
Button :: struct {
	label:  string,
	id:     theme.UI_ID,
	style:  theme.Button_Style,
	active: bool,
}

// TextField represents a retained-mode text input component.
TextField :: struct {
	text:       string,
	id:         theme.UI_ID,
	style:      theme.TextField_Style,
	is_focused: bool,
	cursor_pos: int,
}

// Checkbox represents a retained-mode toggle component.
Checkbox :: struct {
	label:   string,
	id:      theme.UI_ID,
	style:   theme.Checkbox_Style,
	checked: bool,
}

// Card represents a layout container component.
Card :: struct {
	id:    theme.UI_ID,
	style: theme.Card_Style,
}

// Platform override system for RuiShin theme system.
// Applies platform-specific style adjustments to all theme layers:
// - Layout overrides (dialog button alignment, window title alignment)
// - Command overrides (platform-specific keyboard shortcuts)
// - Component overrides (borderRadius, fontSize, padding adjustments)
// - Accessibility overrides (minimum touch target sizes)
// - Global scale factor (DPI scaling)
//
// This module integrates with platform/detect.odin to get the current platform
// and applies the appropriate overrides from the theme data.

import "core:fmt"
import "core:strings"
import platform "project:platform"

// applyPlatformOverrides applies all platform-specific overrides to a theme.
// This function should be called after loading the theme and before resolving styles.
// Returns true if platform overrides were applied, false if platform not found.
//
// Resolution order (per Phase 10B invariants):
// 1. Base theme values
// 2. Platform-specific overrides (applied by this function)
// 3. State overrides (component-specific)
// 4. Accessibility overrides (applied last)
//
// Example:
//   theme, ok := load_theme("themes/default.yaml")
//   if !ok { return }
//   platform_info := platform.detectPlatform()
//   platform_name := platform.get_platform_name(platform_info)
//   success := applyPlatformOverrides(&theme, platform_name)
applyPlatformOverrides :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data, platform_name: string) -> bool {
	if platform_name not_in theme.overrides.platforms {
		// No overrides for this platform, that's okay
		return false
	}

	p_over := theme.overrides.platforms[platform_name]

	// Apply overrides in order: layout, commands, components, accessibility
	applyLayoutOverrides(theme, platform_name)
	applyCommandOverrides(theme, platform_name)
	applyComponentOverrides(theme, platform_name)
	// Note: apply_accessibility_overrides now takes only theme parameter
	// Platform-specific accessibility handling should be done elsewhere

	return true
}

// applyLayoutOverrides applies platform-specific layout settings.
// Merges platform layout settings into the base layout map.
// Platform settings override base settings for the same keys.
//
// Example YAML:
//   overrides:
//     platforms:
//       macos:
//         layout:
//           dialog: { button_alignment: "trailing", close_side: "left" }
applyLayoutOverrides :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data, platform_name: string) {
	if platform_name not_in theme.overrides.platforms {
		return
	}

	p_over := theme.overrides.platforms[platform_name]

	// Merge platform layout settings into base layout
	for section_name, section_map in p_over.layout {
		if section_name not_in theme.layout {
			// Create new section if it doesn't exist
			theme.layout[strings.clone(section_name)] = make(map[string]string)
		}

		base_section := theme.layout[section_name]

		// Override base settings with platform settings
		for key, value in section_map {
			if old_val, found := base_section[key]; found {
				delete(old_val)
			}
			base_section[strings.clone(key)] = strings.clone(value)
		}

		theme.layout[section_name] = base_section
	}
}

// applyCommandOverrides applies platform-specific keyboard shortcuts.
// Replaces base command shortcuts with platform-specific ones.
// This is critical for cross-platform keyboard parity (e.g., Cmd+S on macOS vs Ctrl+S on Windows).
//
// Example YAML:
//   overrides:
//     platforms:
//       macos:
//         commands:
//           file_save: { shortcut: "cmd+s" }
applyCommandOverrides :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data, platform_name: string) {
	if platform_name not_in theme.overrides.platforms {
		return
	}

	p_over := theme.overrides.platforms[platform_name]

	// Override base commands with platform-specific shortcuts
	for cmd_name, cmd_def in p_over.commands {
		if cmd_name not_in theme.commands {
			// Create new command if it doesn't exist
			theme.commands[strings.clone(cmd_name)] = Command_Def{}
		}

		base_cmd := theme.commands[cmd_name]

		// Override shortcut if provided
		if cmd_def.shortcut != "" {
			if base_cmd.shortcut != "" do delete(base_cmd.shortcut)
			base_cmd.shortcut = strings.clone(cmd_def.shortcut)
		}

		// Override label if provided (rare, but possible)
		if cmd_def.label != "" {
			if base_cmd.label != "" do delete(base_cmd.label)
			base_cmd.label = strings.clone(cmd_def.label)
		}

		theme.commands[cmd_name] = base_cmd
	}
}

// applyComponentOverrides applies platform-specific component style adjustments.
// Merges platform component properties into base component styles.
// Platform settings override base settings for the same properties.
//
// Example YAML:
//   overrides:
//     platforms:
//       macos:
//         components:
//           Button: { borderRadius: 6, fontSize: 13 }
applyComponentOverrides :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data, platform_name: string) {
	if platform_name not_in theme.overrides.platforms {
		return
	}

	p_over := theme.overrides.platforms[platform_name]

	// Override base component styles with platform-specific adjustments
	for comp_name, comp_props in p_over.components {
		if comp_name not_in theme.components {
			// Create new component if it doesn't exist
			theme.components[strings.clone(comp_name)] = Component_Def {
				base   = make(map[string]string),
				states = make(map[string]map[string]string),
			}
		}

		base_comp := theme.components[comp_name]

		// Override base properties with platform properties
		for prop_name, prop_value in comp_props {
			if old_val, found := base_comp.base[prop_name]; found {
				delete(old_val)
			}
			base_comp.base[strings.clone(prop_name)] = strings.clone(prop_value)
		}

		theme.components[comp_name] = base_comp
	}
}

// apply_accessibility_overrides applies platform-specific accessibility settings.
// Merges platform accessibility settings into the base accessibility map.
// Platform settings override base settings for the same keys.
//
// Example YAML:
//   overrides:
//     platforms:
//       ios:
//         accessibility:
//           minimum_touch_target: 44

// get_platform_scale returns the global scale factor for a platform.
// This scale factor is used for DPI-aware calculations in Phase 11.
// Returns 1.0 if platform not found or no scale specified.
//
// Example usage:
//   scale := get_platform_scale(&theme, "ios") // Returns 1.2
//   scaled_padding := base_padding * scale
//
// Common scale factors:
//   - Desktop (macOS, Windows, Linux): 1.0
//   - Mobile (iOS, Android): 1.2
//   - Web: 1.0
getPlatformScale :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data, platform_name: string) -> f32 {
	if platform_name not_in theme.overrides.platforms {
		return 1.0
	}

	return theme.overrides.platforms[platform_name].global_scale
}

// hasPlatformOverrides checks if a platform has any overrides defined.
// Returns true if the platform exists in the overrides section.
hasPlatformOverrides :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data, platform_name: string) -> bool {
	return platform_name in theme.overrides.platforms
}

// getPlatformLayoutSetting retrieves a specific platform layout setting.
// Returns the value if found, empty string otherwise.
//
// Example:
//   alignment := getPlatformLayoutSetting(&theme, "macos", "dialog", "button_alignment")
getPlatformLayoutSetting :: proc(
	theme: ^Theme_Data,
	platform_name: string,
	section: string,
	key: string,
) -> string {
	if platform_name not_in theme.overrides.platforms {
		return ""
	}

	p_over := theme.overrides.platforms[platform_name]

	if section not_in p_over.layout {
		return ""
	}

	section_map := p_over.layout[section]

	if key not_in section_map {
		return ""
	}

	return section_map[key]
}

// getPlatformCommand retrieves a platform-specific command definition.
// Returns the command if found, nil otherwise.
//
// Example:
//   cmd := getPlatformCommand(&theme, "macos", "file_save")
//   if cmd != nil {
//       fmt.println("Shortcut:", cmd.shortcut)
//   }
getPlatformCommand :: proc(
	theme: ^Theme_Data,
	platform_name: string,
	command_name: string,
) -> ^Command_Def {
	if platform_name not_in theme.overrides.platforms {
		return nil
	}

	p_over := theme.overrides.platforms[platform_name]

	if command_name not_in p_over.commands {
		return nil
	}

	return &p_over.commands[command_name]
}

// getPlatformComponentProperty retrieves a platform-specific component property.
// Returns the value if found, empty string otherwise.
//
// Example:
//   radius := getPlatformComponentProperty(&theme, "macos", "Button", "borderRadius")
getPlatformComponentProperty :: proc(
	theme: ^Theme_Data,
	platform_name: string,
	component_name: string,
	property_name: string,
) -> string {
	if platform_name not_in theme.overrides.platforms {
		return ""
	}

	p_over := theme.overrides.platforms[platform_name]

	if component_name not_in p_over.components {
		return ""
	}

	comp_map := p_over.components[component_name]

	if property_name not_in comp_map {
		return ""
	}

	return comp_map[property_name]
}

// getPlatformAccessibilitySetting retrieves a platform-specific accessibility setting.
// Returns the value if found, empty string otherwise.
//
// Example:
//   min_target := getPlatformAccessibilitySetting(&theme, "ios", "minimum_touch_target")
getPlatformAccessibilitySetting :: proc(
	theme: ^Theme_Data,
	platform_name: string,
	key: string,
) -> string {
	if platform_name not_in theme.overrides.platforms {
		return ""
	}

	p_over := theme.overrides.platforms[platform_name]

	if key not_in p_over.accessibility {
		return ""
	}

	return p_over.accessibility[key]
}

// applyPlatformOverridesAuto is a convenience function that detects the current platform
// and applies the appropriate overrides automatically.
// This is the recommended way to apply platform overrides.
//
// Example:
//   theme, ok := load_theme("themes/default.yaml")
//   if !ok { return }
//   success := applyPlatformOverridesAuto(&theme)
applyPlatformOverridesAuto :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data) -> bool {
	platform_info := platform.detectPlatform()
	platform_name := platform.get_platform_name(platform_info)
	return applyPlatformOverrides(theme, platform_name)
}

// getCurrentPlatformScale is a convenience function that returns the scale factor
// for the current detected platform.
//
// Example:
//   scale := getCurrentPlatformScale(&theme)
getCurrentPlatformScale :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data) -> f32 {
	platform_info := platform.detectPlatform()
	platform_name := platform.get_platform_name(platform_info)
	return getPlatformScale(theme, platform_name)
}

// String Utilities for RuiShin Theme System
// Phase 10C3.1.b: String Building API Implementation
// Provides theme-specific string building functions that wrap core:strings
// with consistent allocator handling and domain-specific utilities.

import "core:strings"
import "core:mem"

// Theme-Specific String Building Utilities
// ========================================

// string_build_gradient_token builds a gradient token string with proper prefix.
// Phase 10C3.1.b: Specific utility for gradient token building
// This replaces the manual concatenation pattern from Phase 10C3.1.a
string_build_gradient_token :: proc(token: string, allocator: mem.Allocator = context.allocator) -> string {
    return strings.concatenate({"gradients.", token}, allocator)
}

// string_build_color_token builds a color token string with proper prefix.
// Phase 10C3.1.b: Specific utility for color token building
string_build_color_token :: proc(token: string, allocator: mem.Allocator = context.allocator) -> string {
    return strings.concatenate({"colors.", token}, allocator)
}

// string_build_theme_reference builds a theme reference string with namespace and token.
// Phase 10C3.1.b: Theme reference building utility
string_build_theme_reference :: proc(namespace, token: string, allocator: mem.Allocator = context.allocator) -> string {
    return strings.concatenate({namespace, ".", token}, allocator)
}

// Convenience Wrappers for Common Operations
// ==========================================

// string_prefix adds a prefix to a string efficiently.
// Phase 10C3.1.b: Helper for common prefix operations
// NOTE: This is a convenience wrapper around strings.concatenate
string_prefix :: proc(prefix, str: string, allocator: mem.Allocator = context.allocator) -> string {
    return strings.concatenate({prefix, str}, allocator)
}

// string_suffix adds a suffix to a string efficiently.
// Phase 10C3.1.b: Helper for common suffix operations
// NOTE: This is a convenience wrapper around strings.concatenate
string_suffix :: proc(str, suffix: string, allocator: mem.Allocator = context.allocator) -> string {
    return strings.concatenate({str, suffix}, allocator)
}

// string_wrap wraps a string with prefix and suffix.
// Phase 10C3.1.b: Helper for common wrapping operations
// NOTE: This is a convenience wrapper around strings.concatenate
string_wrap :: proc(prefix, str, suffix: string, allocator: mem.Allocator = context.allocator) -> string {
    return strings.concatenate({prefix, str, suffix}, allocator)
}

// Advanced String Building Utilities
// ===================================

// string_build_with_condition builds a string conditionally.
// Phase 10C3.1.b: Conditional string building utility
// This provides a cleaner API than manual if/else for string selection
string_build_with_condition :: proc(
    condition: bool,
    true_part, false_part: string,
    allocator: mem.Allocator = context.allocator,
) -> string {
    if condition {
        return true_part
    }
    return false_part
}

// string_build_safe provides safe string building with fallback for empty parts.
// Phase 10C3.1.b: Safe string building with fallback handling
// This is useful for building strings from potentially empty components
string_build_safe :: proc(
    parts: [dynamic]string,
    fallback: string = "",
    allocator: mem.Allocator = context.allocator,
) -> string {
    non_empty := make([dynamic]string, 0, len(parts))
    for part in parts {
        if len(part) > 0 {
            append(&non_empty, part)
        }
    }
    
    if len(non_empty) == 0 {
        return fallback
    }
    
    return strings.concatenate(non_empty[:], allocator)
}

DEBUG: Source content: package theme

// Command integration for RuiShin theme system.
// Provides keyboard shortcut parsing from theme YAML to platform.Key_Code and modifier flags.
// Integrates with existing platform/input.odin infrastructure.

import "core:strings"
import platform "project:platform"

// Command_Binding represents a resolved keyboard shortcut.
// Stores the keycode and modifier flags for efficient command matching.
Command_Binding :: struct {
	keycode:   platform.Key_Code,
	modifiers: u32, // platform.MODIFIER_* flags
	label:     string,
}

// parse_shortcut parses a keyboard shortcut string from theme YAML.
// Format: "modifier+key" (e.g., "ctrl+s", "cmd+shift+z", "f11")
//
// Supported modifiers:
//   - ctrl  -> platform.MODIFIER_CTRL
//   - cmd   -> platform.MODIFIER_SUPER (Command key on macOS)
//   - alt   -> platform.MODIFIER_ALT
//   - shift -> platform.MODIFIER_SHIFT
//
// Supported keys:
//   - Single characters: "a", "s", "z", "0", "1", etc.
//   - Function keys: "f1", "f2", ..., "f12"
//   - Special keys: "plus", "minus", "comma", etc.
//
// Returns (Command_Binding, true) on success, (Command_Binding{}, false) on failure.
parse_shortcut :: proc(shortcut: string) -> (Command_Binding, bool) {
	if len(shortcut) == 0 {
		return Command_Binding{}, false
	}

	// Split by '+' to separate modifiers and key
	parts := strings.split(shortcut, "+")
	defer delete(parts)

	if len(parts) == 0 {
		return Command_Binding{}, false
	}

	binding := Command_Binding{}

	// Last part is the key, everything before is modifiers
	key_str := parts[len(parts) - 1]

	// Parse modifiers
	for i in 0 ..< len(parts) - 1 {
		modifier := parts[i]
		switch modifier {
		case "ctrl":
			binding.modifiers |= platform.MODIFIER_CTRL
		case "cmd":
			binding.modifiers |= platform.MODIFIER_SUPER
		case "alt":
			binding.modifiers |= platform.MODIFIER_ALT
		case "shift":
			binding.modifiers |= platform.MODIFIER_SHIFT
		case:
			// Unknown modifier
			return Command_Binding{}, false
		}
	}

	// Parse key
	keycode, ok := parse_key(key_str)
	if !ok {
		return Command_Binding{}, false
	}

	binding.keycode = keycode
	return binding, true
}

// parse_key converts a key string to platform.Key_Code.
// Handles single characters, function keys, and special keys.
parse_key :: proc(key: string) -> (platform.Key_Code, bool) {
	if len(key) == 0 {
		return .Invalid, false
	}

	// Function keys
	switch key {
	case "f1":
		return .F1, true
	case "f2":
		return .F2, true
	case "f3":
		return .F3, true
	case "f4":
		return .F4, true
	case "f5":
		return .F5, true
	case "f6":
		return .F6, true
	case "f7":
		return .F7, true
	case "f8":
		return .F8, true
	case "f9":
		return .F9, true
	case "f10":
		return .F10, true
	case "f11":
		return .F11, true
	case "f12":
		return .F12, true
	}

	// Special keys
	switch key {
	case "plus":
		return .Equal, true // '+' is Shift+Equal
	case "minus":
		return .Minus, true
	case "comma":
		return .Comma, true
	case "period":
		return .Period, true
	case "space":
		return .Space, true
	case "enter":
		return .Enter, true
	case "tab":
		return .Tab, true
	case "escape":
		return .Escape, true
	case "backspace":
		return .Backspace, true
	case "delete":
		return .Delete, true
	}

	// Single character keys (a-z, 0-9)
	if len(key) == 1 {
		c := key[0]

		// Letters (a-z)
		if c >= 'a' && c <= 'z' {
			offset := i32(c - 'a')
			return platform.Key_Code(i32(platform.Key_Code.A) + offset), true
		}

		// Numbers (0-9)
		if c >= '0' && c <= '9' {
			offset := i32(c - '0')
			return platform.Key_Code(i32(platform.Key_Code._0) + offset), true
		}
	}

	// Unknown key
	return .Invalid, false
}

// Command registry for RuiShin theme system.
// Maps logical action names to resolved keyboard bindings.
// Integrates with existing engine/input.odin infrastructure for command dispatch.
//
// This module provides:
// - Command_Registry: Maps action names to Command_Binding objects
// - Registry initialization from theme data
// - Query functions for command lookups
// - Input event matching for command dispatch
//
// Phase 10B.4.a: Command Registry - Map logical actions to platform-specific keycodes

import "core:fmt"
import "core:strings"
import platform "project:platform"

// Command_Registry maps logical action names to resolved keyboard bindings.
// This registry is created after theme loading and platform overrides are applied.
// Provides efficient lookup for command dispatch during runtime.
//
// Example:
//   registry, ok := init_command_registry(&theme)
//   binding := get_command_binding(&registry, "file_save")
Command_Registry :: struct {
	bindings: map[string]Command_Binding,
}

// init_command_registry creates a command registry from theme data.
// Parses all command definitions (with platform overrides already applied)
// into resolved Command_Binding objects.
//
// Returns (Command_Registry, true) on success, (Command_Registry{}, false) on failure.
//
// Integration with theme loading:
//   - Should be called after load_theme() completes
//   - Platform overrides are already applied by applyCommandOverrides()
//   - Shortcut strings are parsed using parse_shortcut() from commands.odin
//
// Example:
//   theme, ok := load_theme("themes/default.yaml")
//   if !ok { return }
//   registry, ok := init_command_registry(&theme)
//   if !ok { return }
//   binding := get_command_binding(&registry, "file_save")
init_command_registry :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data) -> (Command_Registry, bool) {
	registry := Command_Registry{}
	registry.bindings = make(map[string]Command_Binding)

	// Iterate through all commands (platform overrides already applied)
	for cmd_name, cmd_def in theme.commands {
		// Skip commands without shortcuts
		if cmd_def.shortcut == "" {
			fmt.printf("[Command Registry] Warning: Command '%s' has no shortcut\n", cmd_name)
			continue
		}

		// Parse shortcut string into Command_Binding
		binding, ok := parse_shortcut(cmd_def.shortcut)
		if !ok {
			fmt.printf("[Command Registry] Failed to parse shortcut for '%s': %s\n",
			          cmd_name, cmd_def.shortcut)
			continue
		}

		// Set label from command definition
		binding.label = strings.clone(cmd_def.label)

		// Store in registry
		registry.bindings[strings.clone(cmd_name)] = binding
	}

	return registry, true
}

// get_command_binding retrieves a command binding by logical action name.
// Returns binding if found, nil otherwise.
//
// Example:
//   binding := get_command_binding(&registry, "file_save")
//   if binding != nil {
//       fmt.println("Keycode:", binding.keycode, "Modifiers:", binding.modifiers)
//   }
get_command_binding :: proc(registry: ^Command_Registry, command_name: string) -> ^Command_Binding {
	if command_name not_in registry.bindings {
		return nil
	}
	return &registry.bindings[command_name]
}

// has_command checks if a command exists in the registry.
// Returns true if command is registered, false otherwise.
//
// Example:
//   if has_command(&registry, "file_save") {
//       // Command is available
//   }
has_command :: proc(registry: ^Command_Registry, command_name: string) -> bool {
	return command_name in registry.bindings
}

// get_all_commands returns all command bindings in the registry.
// Useful for building menus, help dialogs, or command palettes.
//
// Note: This returns a copy of the bindings map to avoid external modification.
// Caller is responsible for deleting the returned map.
//
// Example:
//   all_cmds := get_all_commands(&registry)
//   defer delete(all_cmds)
//   for name, binding in all_cmds {
//       fmt.printf("%s: %s\n", name, binding.label)
//   }
get_all_commands :: proc(registry: ^Command_Registry) -> map[string]Command_Binding {
	result := make(map[string]Command_Binding)
	for name, binding in registry.bindings {
		result[strings.clone(name)] = binding
	}
	return result
}

// find_command_by_binding finds a command name by its keycode and modifiers.
// Useful for reverse lookup when handling input events.
// Returns command name if found, empty string otherwise.
//
// Example:
//   if input.type == .Key_Down {
//       cmd_name := find_command_by_binding(&registry, input.key, input.modifiers)
//       if cmd_name != "" {
//           execute_command(cmd_name)
//       }
//   }
find_command_by_binding :: proc(
	registry: ^Command_Registry,
	keycode: platform.Key_Code,
	modifiers: u32,
) -> string {
	for name, binding in registry.bindings {
		if binding.keycode == keycode && binding.modifiers == modifiers {
			return name
		}
	}
	return ""
}

// match_input_event checks if an input event matches any command binding.
// Returns the command name if matched, empty string otherwise.
//
// This is the primary function for command dispatch during runtime.
// Integrates directly with engine/input.odin Input_Event structure.
//
// Only matches Key_Down events to avoid duplicate command execution.
//
// Example:
//   if input.type == .Key_Down {
//       cmd_name := match_input_event(&registry, input)
//       if cmd_name != "" {
//           handle_command(cmd_name)
//       }
//   }
match_input_event :: proc(
	registry: ^Command_Registry,
	event: platform.Input_Event,
) -> string {
	// Only match key down events
	if event.type != .Key_Down {
		return ""
	}

	return find_command_by_binding(registry, event.key, event.modifiers)
}

// get_command_label retrieves the display label for a command.
// Returns label if found, empty string otherwise.
//
// Example:
//   label := get_command_label(&registry, "file_save")
//   // Returns "Save"
get_command_label :: proc(registry: ^Command_Registry, command_name: string) -> string {
	binding := get_command_binding(registry, command_name)
	if binding == nil {
		return ""
	}
	return binding.label
}

// get_command_shortcut_string retrieves the shortcut string for a command.
// Returns shortcut if found, empty string otherwise.
//
// Note: This reconstructs the shortcut string from the binding,
// which may differ from the original YAML format (e.g., "cmd+s" vs "CMD+S").
//
// Example:
//   shortcut := get_command_shortcut_string(&registry, "file_save")
//   // Returns "cmd+s" on macOS, "ctrl+s" on Windows
get_command_shortcut_string :: proc(registry: ^Command_Registry, command_name: string) -> string {
	binding := get_command_binding(registry, command_name)
	if binding == nil {
		return ""
	}

	// Reconstruct shortcut string from modifiers and keycode
	sb := strings.builder_make()
	defer strings.builder_destroy(&sb)

	// Add modifiers
	if binding.modifiers & platform.MODIFIER_CTRL != 0 {
		fmt.sbprintf(&sb, "ctrl+")
	}
	if binding.modifiers & platform.MODIFIER_SUPER != 0 {
		fmt.sbprintf(&sb, "cmd+")
	}
	if binding.modifiers & platform.MODIFIER_ALT != 0 {
		fmt.sbprintf(&sb, "alt+")
	}
	if binding.modifiers & platform.MODIFIER_SHIFT != 0 {
		fmt.sbprintf(&sb, "shift+")
	}

	// Add key
	key_str := keycode_to_string(binding.keycode)
	fmt.sbprintf(&sb, "%s", key_str)

	return strings.to_string(sb)
}

// keycode_to_string converts a platform.Key_Code to its string representation.
// Returns the key name as a string.
//
// This is the inverse of parse_key() from commands.odin.
//
// Example:
//   key_str := keycode_to_string(platform.Key_Code.S)
//   // Returns "s"
keycode_to_string :: proc(keycode: platform.Key_Code) -> string {
	// Function keys
	#partial switch keycode {
	case .F1:   return "f1"
	case .F2:   return "f2"
	case .F3:   return "f3"
	case .F4:   return "f4"
	case .F5:   return "f5"
	case .F6:   return "f6"
	case .F7:   return "f7"
	case .F8:   return "f8"
	case .F9:   return "f9"
	case .F10:  return "f10"
	case .F11:  return "f11"
	case .F12:  return "f12"
	}

	// Special keys
	#partial switch keycode {
	case .Equal:     return "plus"
	case .Minus:     return "minus"
	case .Comma:     return "comma"
	case .Period:    return "period"
	case .Space:     return "space"
	case .Enter:     return "enter"
	case .Tab:       return "tab"
	case .Escape:    return "escape"
	case .Backspace: return "backspace"
	case .Delete:    return "delete"
	}

	// Letters (A-Z)
	if keycode >= .A && keycode <= .Z {
		offset := int(keycode) - int(platform.Key_Code.A)
		return string([]u8{byte('a' + offset)})
	}

	// Numbers (0-9)
	if keycode >= ._0 && keycode <= ._9 {
		offset := int(keycode) - int(platform.Key_Code._0)
		return string([]u8{byte('0' + offset)})
	}

	// Unknown key
	return ""
}

// delete_command_registry cleans up all resources associated with a command registry.
// Should be called when the registry is no longer needed.
//
// Example:
//   delete_command_registry(&registry)
delete_command_registry :: proc(registry: ^Command_Registry) {
	for k, v in registry.bindings {
		delete(k)
		delete(v.label)
	}
	delete(registry.bindings)
}

DEBUG: Source content: package theme

//
// Theme Token Export API for RSD Generation (Phase 10C2.11)
// Provides procedures to export theme tokens in a format suitable for RSD generation.
// Used during SVG→RSD conversion to provide metadata about theme tokens.
//

import "core:mem"
import "core:time"

// THEME_METADATA_VERSION defines the format version for RSD theme metadata.
// Increment this when the export format changes.
THEME_METADATA_VERSION :: 1

// RSD_Theme_Metadata contains theme information for RSD generation.
// This metadata is used during SVG→RSD conversion to track theme dependencies
// and enable runtime theme switching.
RSD_Theme_Metadata :: struct {
    version:    u16,                     // Theme format version (THEME_METADATA_VERSION)
    theme_name: string,                  // Name of the theme
    colors:     []Theme_Color_Token,     // Exported color tokens
    gradients:  []Theme_Gradient_Token,  // Exported gradient tokens
    created:    i64,                     // Unix timestamp when metadata was created
}

// Theme_Color_Token represents a color token for RSD export.
// Contains the token path and resolved RGBA value for use in RSD files.
Theme_Color_Token :: struct {
    path:  string,    // Token path (e.g., "colors.primary", "button.background")
    value: [4]f32,    // RGBA color value (normalized 0..1)
}

// Theme_Gradient_Token represents a gradient token for RSD export.
// Contains the token path and gradient specification for use in RSD files.
Theme_Gradient_Token :: struct {
    path:  string,               // Token path (e.g., "gradients.primary_button")
    value: Gradient_Spec,        // Gradient specification (type, stops, transform)
}

// export_theme_for_rsd exports a theme's tokens in a format suitable for RSD generation.
// This procedure is the main entry point for the export API.
//
// Parameters:
//   - theme: Pointer to the Token_Store containing resolved theme tokens
//   - allocator: Memory allocator for allocating exported token arrays
//
// Returns:
//   RSD_Theme_Metadata containing all color and gradient tokens from the theme
//
// Usage example:
// ```odin
// theme := theme.load_from_file("themes/dark.json")
// defer theme_delete(theme)
//
// metadata := export_theme_for_rsd(theme, context.allocator)
// defer theme_metadata_destroy(&metadata, context.allocator)
// ```
export_theme_for_rsd :: proc(theme: ^Token_Store, allocator: mem.Allocator = context.allocator) -> RSD_Theme_Metadata {
    metadata: RSD_Theme_Metadata
    metadata.version = THEME_METADATA_VERSION
    metadata.theme_name = ""  // Set by caller if needed
    metadata.colors = extract_color_tokens(theme, allocator)
    metadata.gradients = extract_gradient_tokens(theme, allocator)
    metadata.created = i64(time.to_unix_nanoseconds(time.now()) / 1_000_000_000)
    return metadata
}

// extract_color_tokens extracts all color tokens from a theme store.
// Creates Theme_Color_Token entries for each color in the theme.
//
// Parameters:
//   - theme: Pointer to the Token_Store containing resolved color tokens
//   - allocator: Memory allocator for the returned array
//
// Returns:
//   Slice of Theme_Color_Token containing all color tokens from the theme
extract_color_tokens :: proc(theme: ^Token_Store, allocator: mem.Allocator = context.allocator) -> []Theme_Color_Token {
    if theme == nil {
        return nil
    }

    color_count := len(theme.colors)
    if color_count == 0 {
        return nil
    }

    tokens := make([]Theme_Color_Token, color_count, allocator)
    i := 0
    for path, color in theme.colors {
        tokens[i] = Theme_Color_Token{
            path = path,
            value = [4]f32{color.r, color.g, color.b, color.a},
        }
        i += 1
    }

    return tokens
}

// extract_gradient_tokens extracts all gradient tokens from a theme store.
// Creates Theme_Gradient_Token entries for each gradient in the theme.
//
// Parameters:
//   - theme: Pointer to the Token_Store containing resolved gradient tokens
//   - allocator: Memory allocator for the returned array
//
// Returns:
//   Slice of Theme_Gradient_Token containing all gradient tokens from the theme
extract_gradient_tokens :: proc(theme: ^Token_Store, allocator: mem.Allocator = context.allocator) -> []Theme_Gradient_Token {
    if theme == nil {
        return nil
    }

    gradient_count := len(theme.gradients)
    if gradient_count == 0 {
        return nil
    }

    tokens := make([]Theme_Gradient_Token, gradient_count, allocator)
    i := 0
    for path, spec in theme.gradients {
        tokens[i] = Theme_Gradient_Token{
            path = path,
            value = spec,
        }
        i += 1
    }

    return tokens
}

// theme_metadata_destroy frees memory allocated by export_theme_for_rsd.
// Call this when done using the metadata to avoid memory leaks.
//
// Parameters:
//   - metadata: Pointer to RSD_Theme_Metadata to destroy
//   - allocator: Memory allocator used for the metadata
theme_metadata_destroy :: proc(metadata: ^RSD_Theme_Metadata, allocator: mem.Allocator = context.allocator) {
    if metadata == nil {
        return
    }

    // Free color tokens array
    if len(metadata.colors) > 0 {
        delete(metadata.colors, allocator)
    }

    // Free gradient tokens array
    if len(metadata.gradients) > 0 {
        delete(metadata.gradients, allocator)
    }

    metadata^ = RSD_Theme_Metadata{}
}

// Layered resolver for RuiShin theme system.
// Handles token interpolation and merging of theme layers:
// Base -> State (platform and accessibility already applied during theme loading).

import "core:fmt"
import "core:strconv"
import "core:strings"
import assets "project:assets"
import immediate "project:ui/immediate" // Phase 10C3.3.b: For UI_Context and UI_ID

// resolve_token_ref looks up a token reference and returns its value.
// Supports: "tokens.colors.<name>", "tokens.spacing.<name>", "tokens.type.<name>"
resolve_token_ref :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data, ref: string) -> (val: Type_Property, ok: bool) {
	parts := strings.split(ref, ".")
	defer delete(parts)

	if len(parts) != 3 do return nil, false
	if parts[0] != "tokens" do return nil, false

	section := parts[1]
	key := parts[2]

	switch section {
	case "colors":
		if c, found := theme.resolved.colors[key]; found {
			return c, true
		}
	case "spacing":
		if s, found := theme.resolved.spacing[key]; found {
			return s, true
		}
	case "type":
		if t, found := theme.resolved.type[key]; found {
			return t, true
		}
	}

	return nil, false
}

// resolve_string_to_property interpolates tokens in a string and returns a Type_Property.
// If the input is exactly "{tokens.xxx.yyy}", it returns the typed value.
// Otherwise, it returns the string with interpolated values.
resolve_string_to_property :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data, input: string) -> Type_Property {
	trimmed := strings.trim_space(input)

	// Exact match for a single token reference
	if strings.has_prefix(trimmed, "{") && strings.has_suffix(trimmed, "}") {
		ref := trimmed[1:len(trimmed) - 1]
		if val, ok := resolve_token_ref(theme, ref); ok {
			return val
		}
	}

	// Mixed interpolation (e.g. "rgb({tokens.colors.primary})")
	// For now, only simple interpolation is supported.
	res := interpolate_tokens(theme, input)
	return res
}

// TEMPORARILY DISABLED - Type safety issues
// resolve_array_to_properties resolves token references in an array of strings.
// Task 10B.5.b: Returns a []Type_Property with resolved values.
// Phase 10C3.3.a: Restored with proper type safety by handling array properties separately.
//
// This function resolves an array of string properties that may contain token references.
// Each element in the input array is resolved individually using resolve_string_to_property.
// The result is an array of Type_Property values that can be used in the theme system.
//
// Example:
//   Input: ["{tokens.colors.primary}", "{tokens.spacing.unit}"]
//   Output: [assets.Color{0.0, 0.47, 1.0, 1.0}, 4.0]
//
// Note: This function does not modify the original array and creates new resolved values.
resolve_array_to_properties :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data, array: []string) -> []Type_Property {
	// If the input array is empty, return an empty result
	if len(array) == 0 {
		return []Type_Property{}
	}
	
	// Create a result array with the same length as the input
	result := make([]Type_Property, len(array))
	
	// Resolve each element in the array
	for i := 0; i < len(array); i += 1 {
		result[i] = resolve_string_to_property(theme, array[i])
	}
	
	return result
}

// interpolate_tokens replaces all occurrences of {tokens.xxx.yyy} with their string values.
// Task 10B.5.c: Fixed memory leak by properly cleaning up allocated strings.
interpolate_tokens :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data, input: string) -> string {
	res := input
	for {
		start := strings.index(res, "{")
		if start == -1 do break
		end := strings.index(res[start:], "}")
		if end == -1 do break
		end += start

		ref := res[start + 1:end]
		val, ok := resolve_token_ref(theme, ref)

		val_str: string
		val_str_allocated := false // Task 10B.5.c: Track if val_str was allocated
		if ok {
			switch v in val {
			case assets.Color:
				val_str = fmt.aprintf(
					"#%02X%02X%02X%02X",
					u8(v.r * 255),
					u8(v.g * 255),
					u8(v.b * 255),
					u8(v.a * 255),
				)
				val_str_allocated = true // Task 10B.5.c: Mark as allocated
			case f32:
				val_str = fmt.aprintf("%f", v)
				val_str_allocated = true // Task 10B.5.c: Mark as allocated
			case int:
				val_str = fmt.aprintf("%d", v)
				val_str_allocated = true // Task 10B.5.c: Mark as allocated
			case string:
				val_str = v
				val_str_allocated = false // Task 10B.5.c: Reference, not allocated
			}
		} else {
			val_str = "MISSING_TOKEN"
			val_str_allocated = false // Task 10B.5.c: String literal, not allocated
		}

		new_res := strings.concatenate({res[:start], val_str, res[end + 1:]})
		if res != input do delete(res) // Clean up intermediate strings
		res = new_res

		// Task 10B.5.c: Clean up val_str if it was allocated
		if val_str_allocated {
			delete(val_str)
		}
	}
	return res
}

// resolve_component_style merges layers and resolves tokens to produce a final style map.
// Layers: Base -> State
// Note: Platform and accessibility overrides are already applied during theme loading (Phase 10B.3.a and 10B.3.b)
// This returns a map that should be deleted by the user via delete_resolved_style.
// Phase 10C3.3.a: Added resolved_arrays to handle array properties separately.
resolve_component_style :: proc(
	theme: ^Theme_Data,
	comp_name: string,
	platform: string,
	state: string = "",
) -> (
	map[string]Type_Property,
	map[string][]Type_Property, // Phase 10C3.3.a: Resolved array properties
	bool,
) {
	if comp_name not_in theme.components {
		return nil, nil, false
	}

	result := make(map[string]Type_Property)
	result_arrays := make(map[string][]Type_Property) // Phase 10C3.3.a: Resolved array properties

	// 1. Base style (platform overrides already applied during theme loading)
	if comp, ok := theme.components[comp_name]; ok {
		for k, v in comp.base {
			result[strings.clone(k)] = resolve_string_to_property(theme, v)
		}
		// Task 10B.5.b: Include base arrays
		// Phase 10C3.3.a: Restored with proper type safety
		for k, v in comp.base_arrays {
			result_arrays[strings.clone(k)] = resolve_array_to_properties(theme, v)
		}
	}

	// 2. State overrides
	if comp, ok := theme.components[comp_name]; ok {
		if state != "" {
			if s_over, ok := comp.states[state]; ok {
				for k, v in s_over {
					if old_v, found := result[k]; found {
						delete_resolved_prop(old_v)
					} else {
						result[strings.clone(k)] = nil
					}
					result[k] = resolve_string_to_property(theme, v)
				}
			}
			// Task 10B.5.b: Include state arrays
			if s_arrays, ok := comp.state_arrays[state]; ok {
				for k, v in s_arrays {
					if old_v, found := result[k]; found {
						delete_resolved_prop(old_v)
					} else {
						result[strings.clone(k)] = nil
					}
					// Phase 10C3.3.a: Restored with proper type safety
					result_arrays[k] = resolve_array_to_properties(theme, v)
				}
			}
		}
	}

	return result, result_arrays, true
}

// resolve_style_from_context is a high-level helper that picks the state automatically.
// This function uses the UI context to determine the appropriate state (hover, pressed) for a component.
//
// Phase 10C3.3.b: Restored with proper type definitions and enhanced return signature.
//
// Parameters:
//   theme: The theme data containing component definitions
//   ctx: UI context containing interaction states (hovered_id, active_id)
//   comp_name: Name of the component to resolve
//   id: Unique identifier for the UI element
//
// Returns:
//   (resolved_style, resolved_arrays, success) where:
//   - resolved_style: Map of resolved property values
//   - resolved_arrays: Map of resolved array properties (Phase 10C3.3.a)
//   - success: Boolean indicating if resolution was successful
//
// Example usage:
//   style, arrays, ok := resolve_style_from_context(&theme, &ui_ctx, "Button", button_id)
//   if ok {
//       // Use resolved style and arrays for rendering
//   }
resolve_style_from_context :: proc(
	theme: ^Theme_Data,
	ctx: ^immediate.UI_Context,
	comp_name: string,
	id: immediate.UI_ID,
) -> (
	map[string]Type_Property,
	map[string][]Type_Property, // Phase 10C3.3.a: Resolved array properties
	bool,
) {
	state := ""
	
	// Determine the appropriate state based on interaction context
	if ctx.active_id == id {
		state = "pressed"
	} else if ctx.hovered_id == id {
		state = "hover"
	}
	
	// Check if state actually exists in theme, if not fallback to empty
	if comp, ok := theme.components[comp_name]; ok {
		if state not_in comp.states {
			state = ""
		}
	}
	
	// Platform parameter removed - platform and accessibility overrides already applied during theme loading
	// Phase 10C3.3.a: Updated to use the enhanced resolve_component_style with array support
	return resolve_component_style(theme, comp_name, "", state)
}

// delete_resolved_style cleans up a style map returned by resolve_component_style.
delete_resolved_style :: proc(style: map[string]Type_Property) {
	for k, v in style {
		delete(k)
		delete_resolved_prop(v)
	}
	delete(style)
}

// delete_resolved_prop cleans up a resolved property value.
// Task 10B.5.b: Added support for cleaning up array properties.
// Phase 10C3.3.a: Restored with proper type safety.
// Phase 10C3.4.b: Enhanced with comprehensive union type handling.
// Note: Array properties are handled separately in the theme system and don't need
// special cleanup in this function since they're stored as []string in the parser.
delete_resolved_prop :: proc(prop: Type_Property) {
	// Comprehensive union type handling for all Type_Property variants
	#partial switch v in prop {
	case assets.Color:
		// Color values don't need special cleanup in current implementation
		// In production, might want to track color allocations
	case string:
		// In a production version, we would track if this string was allocated.
		// For the current implementation, we assume ownership depends on allocation source.
	case f32:
		// Numeric values don't need cleanup
	case int:
		// Integer values don't need cleanup
	case:
		// This handles any future types added to the union
		// Provides forward compatibility
	}
	// Array properties are handled separately and don't use the Type_Property union
	// They are stored as []string in Component_Def.base_arrays and state_arrays
}

// safe_get_map_value provides type-safe access to map values with a default fallback.
// Phase 10C3.4.a: Type safety enhancement for map operations.
//
// This helper function safely retrieves a value from a map, returning a default
// value if the key doesn't exist. This prevents runtime errors and provides
// better type safety than direct map access.
//
// Example:
//   value := safe_get_map_value(map, "key", "default")
//   // Instead of: if "key" in map { value = map["key"] } else { value = "default" }
safe_get_map_value :: proc(m: map[string]string, key: string, default: string) -> string {
	if key in m {
		return m[key]
	}
	return default
}

// safe_get_map_value_with_check provides type-safe access with additional validation.
// Phase 10C3.4.a: Enhanced type safety with validation.
//
// This function not only checks for key existence but also validates the value
// meets certain criteria (e.g., non-empty, valid format).
//
// Example:
//   color := safe_get_map_value_with_check(colors, "primary", "#000000", is_valid_color)
safe_get_map_value_with_check :: proc(
	m: map[string]string,
	key: string,
	default: string,
	validator: proc(value: string) -> bool,
) -> string {
	if key in m {
		value := m[key]
		if validator(value) {
			return value
		}
	}
	return default
}

// resolve_property_to_string converts a Type_Property to its string representation.
// Phase 10C3.4.b: Union type handling helper for debugging and serialization.
//
// This function safely converts any Type_Property value to a string representation,
// which is useful for debugging, logging, and serialization purposes.
//
// Example:
//   prop_str := resolve_property_to_string(some_property)
//   fmt.println("Property value: " + prop_str)
resolve_property_to_string :: proc(prop: Type_Property) -> string {
	#partial switch v in prop {
	case assets.Color:
		c := v
		return fmt.tprintf("Color(%f, %f, %f, %f)", c.r, c.g, c.b, c.a)
	case string:
		return v
	case f32:
		return fmt.tprintf("%f", v)
	case int:
		return fmt.tprintf("%d", v)
	case:
		return "<unknown type>"
	}
}

// is_valid_color checks if a string is a valid color value.
// Phase 10C3.4.a: Type safety helper for color validation.
is_valid_color :: proc(value: string) -> bool {
	// Check if it's a hex color
	if strings.has_prefix(value, "#") {
		// Remove the # and check if remaining characters are valid hex
		hex_part := value[1:]
		if len(hex_part) == 3 || len(hex_part) == 6 || len(hex_part) == 8 {
			for i in 0 ..< len(hex_part) {
				c := hex_part[i]
				if !((c >= '0' && c <= '9') || (c >= 'a' && c <= 'f') || (c >= 'A' && c <= 'F')) {
					return false
				}
			}
			return true
		}
	}
	// Check if it's a named color (simplified check - would need full list in production)
	if value == "red" || value == "blue" || value == "green" || value == "black" || value == "white" {
		return true
	}
	return false
}

// resolve_color_property resolves a property that is expected to be a color.
resolve_color_property :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data, value: string) -> (assets.Color, bool) {
	prop := resolve_string_to_property(theme, value)

	#partial switch v in prop {
	case assets.Color:
		return v, true
	case string:
		return resolve_color(v)
	case:
		return assets.Color{}, false
	}
}

// resolve_f32_property resolves a property that is expected to be a float.
resolve_f32_property :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data, value: string) -> (f32, bool) {
	prop := resolve_string_to_property(theme, value)

	#partial switch v in prop {
	case f32:
		return v, true
	case int:
		return f32(v), true
	case string:
		return strconv.parse_f32(v)
	case:
		return 0, false
	}
}

// Themed drawing helpers for RuiShin theme system.
// Provides wrappers for g2d draw calls that consume resolved style maps.

import "core:strings"
import assets "project:assets"
import renderer "project:renderer"

//first lets get the active renderer 
render_api := renderer.active_renderer

// ui_draw_rect draws a rectangle themed by a style map.
// Properties used: "backgroundColor", "borderColor", "borderWidth", "borderRadius"
ui_draw_rect :: proc(x, y, w, h: f32, style: map[string]Type_Property) {
	bg_col, bg_ok := style["backgroundColor"].(assets.Color)
	radius, rad_ok := style["borderRadius"].(f32)

	if !bg_ok do bg_col = assets.Color{0, 0, 0, 0}
	if !rad_ok do radius = 0

	if radius > 0 {
		render_api.fillRectRounded(x, y, w, h, radius, renderer.paint_solid(bg_col))
	} else {
		render_api.fillRect(x, y, w, h, renderer.paint_solid(bg_col))
	}

	// Border
	border_col, border_ok := style["borderColor"].(assets.Color)
	border_w, bw_ok := style["borderWidth"].(f32)
	if border_ok && border_w > 0 {
		// Note: c2d might need a specific stroke function for rounded rects if not already present
		//  render_api.drawRectRoundedStroke(x, y, w, h, radius, border_w, border_col)
	}
}

// ui_draw_text draws a text themed by a style map.
// Properties used: "foregroundColor", "fontSize", "fontWeight", "fontFamily"
ui_draw_text :: proc(x, y: f32, text: string, style: map[string]Type_Property) {
	fg_col, fg_ok := style["foregroundColor"].(assets.Color)
	if !fg_ok do fg_col = assets.Color{1, 1, 1, 1}

	// Font resolution will be more complex in 10B.2.b.
	// For now, we use a placeholder or the global font.
	// render_api.drawText(x, y, text, global_font_normal, fg_col)
}

// ui_get_f32 safely retrieves a float property from a style map.
ui_get_f32 :: proc(style: map[string]Type_Property, key: string, fallback: f32 = 0) -> f32 {
	if val, ok := style[key]; ok {
		if f, f_ok := val.(f32); f_ok do return f
		if i, i_ok := val.(int); i_ok do return f32(i)
	}
	return fallback
}

// ui_get_color safely retrieves a color property from a style map.
ui_get_color :: proc(
	style: map[string]Type_Property,
	key: string,
	fallback: assets.Color = {0, 0, 0, 0},
) -> assets.Color {
	if val, ok := style[key]; ok {
		if c, c_ok := val.(assets.Color); c_ok do return c
	}
	return fallback
}
