// Token parser for RuiShin theme system.
// Provides minimal YAML subset parsing for themespec.yaml files.
// Supports key-value pairs, nested maps, comments, and basic type conversion.

import "core:fmt"
import "core:strconv"
import "core:strings"
import vfs "project:vfs"

// Theme_Data represents the parsed theme file structure.
// Task 10B.5.b: Added layout_arrays for array property support.
Theme_Data :: struct {
	metadata:      map[string]string,
	tokens:        Theme_Tokens,
	resolved:      Token_Store,
	layout:        map[string]map[string]string,
	layout_arrays:  map[string]map[string][]string, // Task 10B.5.b: Array values in layout
	commands:      map[string]Command_Def,
	services:      map[string]string,
	components:    map[string]Component_Def,
	accessibility: map[string]map[string]string,
	overrides:     Theme_Overrides,
}

// Component_Def stores base and state styles for a component.
// Task 10B.5.b: Added base_arrays and state_arrays for array property support.
Component_Def :: struct {
	base:        map[string]string,
	base_arrays: map[string][]string, // Task 10B.5.b: Array values in base
	states:      map[string]map[string]string,
	state_arrays: map[string]map[string][]string, // Task 10B.5.b: Array values in states
}

// Theme_Tokens stores design token values.
// Colors and type properties are stored as strings for later resolution.
// Spacing values are parsed to f32 for direct use.
// Phase 10C2.10: Added gradients for vector asset support.
Theme_Tokens :: struct {
	colors:    map[string]string, // Raw string values (hex or named colors)
	spacing:   map[string]f32,    // Parsed numeric values
	type:      map[string]string, // Font properties (families, sizes as strings)
	gradients: map[string]string, // Phase 10C2.10: Gradient definitions (inline or references)
}

Theme_Overrides :: struct {
	platforms: map[string]Theme_Platform_Override,
}

// Theme_Platform_Override stores platform-specific overrides.
// Task 10B.5.b: Added layout_arrays and component_arrays for array property support.
Theme_Platform_Override :: struct {
	layout:          map[string]map[string]string, // Platform-specific layout overrides
	layout_arrays:    map[string]map[string][]string, // Task 10B.5.b: Array values in layout
	commands:        map[string]Command_Def,
	components:      map[string]map[string]string,
	component_arrays: map[string]map[string][]string, // Task 10B.5.b: Array values in components
	accessibility:    map[string]string, // Platform-specific accessibility overrides
	global_scale:    f32, // DPI scaling factor for this platform
}

// Command_Def represents a keyboard command definition.
Command_Def :: struct {
	shortcut: string,
	label:    string,
}

// load_theme loads and parses a theme YAML file.
// Returns (Theme_Data, true) on success, (Theme_Data{}, false) on failure.
//
// Supports a minimal YAML subset:
//   - Key-value pairs: "key: value"
//   - Nested maps: indentation (2 spaces) indicates nesting
//   - Comments: lines starting with '#'
//   - Empty lines: ignored
//
// Uses vfs.loadAsset() for platform-agnostic file loading.
// Example:
//   theme, ok := load_theme("ai-memory/focus-chains/themespec.yaml")
//   if !ok {
//       fmt.println("Failed to load theme")
//   }
load_theme :: proc(filepath: string) -> (Theme_Data, bool) {
	// Read file using VFS
	data, ok := vfs.loadAsset(filepath, .Theme)
	if !ok {
		return Theme_Data{}, false
	}
	defer delete(data)

	content := string(data)
	lines := strings.split_lines(content)
	defer delete(lines)

	theme := Theme_Data{}
	theme.metadata = make(map[string]string)
	theme.tokens.colors = make(map[string]string)
	theme.tokens.spacing = make(map[string]f32)
	theme.tokens.type = make(map[string]string)
	theme.tokens.gradients = make(map[string]string)  // Phase 10C2.10
	theme.layout = make(map[string]map[string]string)
	theme.layout_arrays = make(map[string]map[string][]string) // Task 10B.5.b: Initialize layout arrays
	theme.commands = make(map[string]Command_Def)
	theme.services = make(map[string]string)
	theme.components = make(map[string]Component_Def)
	theme.overrides.platforms = make(map[string]Theme_Platform_Override)

	// Parse state
	current_section := ""
	current_subsection := ""
	current_platform := ""

	for line in lines {
		// Trim whitespace
		trimmed := strings.trim_space(line)

		// Skip empty lines and comments
		if len(trimmed) == 0 || trimmed[0] == '#' {
			continue
		}

		// Calculate indentation level (2 spaces per level)
		indent_level := get_indent_level(line)

		// Parse key-value pair
		key, value, has_value := parse_key_value(trimmed)

		// Determine section based on indentation
		switch indent_level {
		case 0:
			// Top-level section (metadata, tokens, commands, overrides)
			current_section = key
			current_subsection = ""
			current_platform = "" // Reset platform when moving to a new top-level section

		case 1:
			// Subsection (colors, spacing, type, platforms) or direct value
			if current_section == "metadata" && has_value {
				theme.metadata[strings.clone(key)] = strings.clone(value)
			} else if current_section == "tokens" {
				current_subsection = strings.clone(key)
			} else if current_section == "commands" && has_value {
				// flow style commands handled below
			} else if current_section == "overrides" {
				current_subsection = strings.clone(key)
			}
		case 2:
			// Token values or command properties or platform names
			if current_section == "tokens" && has_value {
				k := strings.clone(key)
				switch current_subsection {
				case "colors":
					theme.tokens.colors[k] = strings.clone(value)
				case "spacing":
					if num, ok := strconv.parse_f32(value); ok {
						theme.tokens.spacing[k] = num
					}
				case "type":
					theme.tokens.type[k] = strings.clone(value)
				case "gradients":  // Phase 10C2.10
					theme.tokens.gradients[k] = strings.clone(value)
				}
			} else if current_section == "overrides" && current_subsection == "platforms" {
				current_platform = strings.clone(key)
				if current_platform not_in theme.overrides.platforms {
					theme.overrides.platforms[current_platform] = Theme_Platform_Override {
						commands = make(map[string]Command_Def),
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}

	// Second pass for complex nested structures
	parse_overrides_extended(&theme, lines)
	parse_components_extended(&theme, lines)
	parse_layout_extended(&theme, lines)
	parse_accessibility_extended(&theme, lines)

	// Resolve tokens into native types
	theme.resolved = init_token_store()
	resolve_tokens(&theme.tokens, &theme.resolved)

	// Apply platform-specific overrides (Phase 10B.3.a)
	// Platform overrides are applied after token resolution but before validation
	// This ensures platform-specific adjustments are part of the final theme data
	applyPlatformOverridesAuto(&theme)

	// Apply accessibility overrides (Phase 10B.3.b)
	// Accessibility overrides are applied after platform overrides but before validation
	// This ensures accessibility adjustments are part of the final theme data
	apply_accessibility_overrides(&theme)

	// Validate integrity
	if valid, err := validate_theme(&theme); !valid {
		fmt.printf("[Theme] Validation Error: %s\n", err)
		delete_theme(&theme)
		delete(err)
		return Theme_Data{}, false
	}

	return theme, true
}

// load_theme_with_registry loads a theme and creates a command registry.
// This is the recommended way to load themes when command dispatch is needed.
//
// Returns (Theme_Data, Command_Registry, true) on success,
// (Theme_Data{}, Command_Registry{}, false) on failure.
//
// Integration with Phase 10B.4.a Command Registry:
//   - Loads theme using load_theme()
//   - Creates command registry from theme data
//   - Platform overrides are already applied during theme loading
//   - Shortcut strings are parsed into resolved Command_Binding objects
//
// Example:
//   theme, registry, ok := load_theme_with_registry("themes/default.yaml")
//   if !ok {
//       fmt.println("Failed to load theme")
//       return
//   }
//   defer delete_theme(&theme)
//   defer delete_command_registry(&registry)
//   // Use theme for styling, registry for command dispatch
load_theme_with_registry :: proc(filepath: string) -> (Theme_Data, Command_Registry, bool) {
	theme, ok := load_theme(filepath)
	if !ok {
		return Theme_Data{}, Command_Registry{}, false
	}

	registry, registry_ok := init_command_registry(&theme)
	if !registry_ok {
		delete_theme(&theme)
		return Theme_Data{}, Command_Registry{}, false
	}

	return theme, registry, true
}

// delete_theme cleans up all resources associated with a theme.
delete_theme :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data) {
	delete(theme.metadata)

	// Tokens
	for k, v in theme.tokens.colors {delete(k); delete(v)}
	delete(theme.tokens.colors)
	for k, _ in theme.tokens.spacing {delete(k)}
	delete(theme.tokens.spacing)
	for k, v in theme.tokens.type {delete(k); delete(v)}
	delete(theme.tokens.type)
	// Phase 10C2.10: Clean up gradients
	for k, v in theme.tokens.gradients {delete(k); delete(v)}
	delete(theme.tokens.gradients)

	// Resolved
	delete_token_store(&theme.resolved)

	// Layout
	for k, v in theme.layout {
		delete(k)
		for pk, pv in v {delete(pk); delete(pv)}
		delete(v)
	}
	delete(theme.layout)

	// Task 10B.5.b: Clean up layout_arrays
	for k, v in theme.layout_arrays {
		delete(k)
		for pk, pv in v {
			delete(pk)
			delete(pv)
		}
		delete(v)
	}
	delete(theme.layout_arrays)

	// Services
	for k, v in theme.services {delete(k); delete(v)}
	delete(theme.services)

	// Components
	for k, v in theme.components {
		delete(k)
		for pk, pv in v.base {delete(pk); delete(pv)}
		delete(v.base)
		// Task 10B.5.b: Clean up base_arrays
		for pk, pv in v.base_arrays {
			delete(pk)
			delete(pv)
		}
		delete(v.base_arrays)
		for sk, sv in v.states {
			delete(sk)
			for pk, pv in sv {delete(pk); delete(pv)}
			delete(sv)
		}
		delete(v.states)
		// Task 10B.5.b: Clean up state_arrays
		for sk, sv in v.state_arrays {
			delete(sk)
			for pk, pv in sv {
				delete(pk)
				delete(pv)
			}
			delete(sv)
		}
		delete(v.state_arrays)
	}
	delete(theme.components)

	// Commands
	for k, v in theme.commands {
		delete(k)
		delete(v.shortcut)
		delete(v.label)
	}
	delete(theme.commands)

	// Overrides
	for k, v in theme.overrides.platforms {
		delete(k)
		// Clean up layout maps
		for lk, lv in v.layout {
			delete(lk)
			for pk, pv in lv {delete(pk); delete(pv)}
			delete(lv)
		}
		delete(v.layout)
		// Task 10B.5.b: Clean up layout_arrays
		for lk, lv in v.layout_arrays {
			delete(lk)
			for pk, pv in lv {
				delete(pk)
				delete(pv)
			}
			delete(lv)
		}
		delete(v.layout_arrays)
		// Clean up commands
		for ck, cv in v.commands {
			delete(ck)
			delete(cv.shortcut)
			delete(cv.label)
		}
		delete(v.commands)
		// Clean up components
		for ck, cv in v.components {
			delete(ck)
			for pk, pv in cv {delete(pk); delete(pv)}
			delete(cv)
		}
		delete(v.components)
		// Task 10B.5.b: Clean up component_arrays
		for ck, cv in v.component_arrays {
			delete(ck)
			for pk, pv in cv {
				delete(pk)
				delete(pv)
			}
			delete(cv)
		}
		delete(v.component_arrays)
		// Clean up accessibility
		for ak, av in v.accessibility {
			delete(ak)
			delete(av)
		}
		delete(v.accessibility)
	}
	delete(theme.overrides.platforms)
}

// save_theme serializes the theme data to YAML and writes it to disk.
save_theme :: proc(theme: Theme_Data, filepath: string) -> bool {
	sb := strings.builder_make()
	defer strings.builder_destroy(&sb)

	// Metadata
	fmt.sbprintln(&sb, "metadata:")
	for k, v in theme.metadata {
		fmt.sbprintf(&sb, "  %s: \"%s\"\n", k, v)
	}
	fmt.sbprintln(&sb, "")

	// Tokens
	fmt.sbprintln(&sb, "tokens:")

	fmt.sbprintln(&sb, "  colors:")
	for k, v in theme.tokens.colors {
		fmt.sbprintf(&sb, "    %s: \"%s\"\n", k, v)
	}

	fmt.sbprintln(&sb, "  spacing:")
	for k, v in theme.tokens.spacing {
		fmt.sbprintf(&sb, "    %s: %f\n", k, v)
	}

	fmt.sbprintln(&sb, "  type:")
	for k, v in theme.tokens.type {
		fmt.sbprintf(&sb, "    %s: \"%s\"\n", k, v)
	}

	// Phase 10C2.10: Gradients
	fmt.sbprintln(&sb, "  gradients:")
	for k, v in theme.tokens.gradients {
		fmt.sbprintf(&sb, "    %s: \"%s\"\n", k, v)
	}
	fmt.sbprintln(&sb, "")

	// Base Commands
	fmt.sbprintln(&sb, "commands:")
	for k, v in theme.commands {
		fmt.sbprintf(&sb, "  %s:\n", k)
		fmt.sbprintf(&sb, "    shortcut: \"%s\"\n", v.shortcut)
		fmt.sbprintf(&sb, "    label: \"%s\"\n", v.label)
	}
	fmt.sbprintln(&sb, "")

	// Overrides
	fmt.sbprintln(&sb, "overrides:")
	fmt.sbprintln(&sb, "  platforms:")
	for p_name, p_over in theme.overrides.platforms {
		fmt.sbprintf(&sb, "    %s:\n", p_name)
		if len(p_over.commands) > 0 {
			fmt.sbprintln(&sb, "      commands:")
			for k, v in p_over.commands {
				fmt.sbprintf(&sb, "        %s:\n", k)
				fmt.sbprintf(&sb, "          shortcut: \"%s\"\n", v.shortcut)
				// Label is not typically overridden at platform level, or if it is, it's not explicitly saved here.
				// If needed, add: fmt.sbprintf(&sb, "          label: \"%s\"\n", v.label)
			}
		}
	}

	res_str := strings.to_string(sb)
	return vfs.save_asset(filepath, transmute([]u8)res_str)
}

// get_indent_level calculates the indentation level (2 spaces = 1 level).
get_indent_level :: proc(line: string) -> int {
	count := 0
	for c in line {
		if c == ' ' {
			count += 1
		} else {
			break
		}
	}
	return count / 2
}

// parse_key_value splits a line into key and value.
// Returns (key, value, true) if value exists, (key, "", false) otherwise.
parse_key_value :: proc(line: string) -> (string, string, bool) {
	// Find first ':'
	colon_idx := strings.index(line, ":")
	if colon_idx == -1 {
		return line, "", false
	}

	key := strings.trim_space(line[:colon_idx])
	value_part := line[colon_idx + 1:]

	// Handle inline comments (must have a space before # to avoid capturing hex colors)
	// Or just check if it's not the very first character of the untrimmed value part
	hash_idx := -1
	for i := 0; i < len(value_part); i += 1 {
		if value_part[i] == '#' {
			// If it's the first non-whitespace char, it's a comment if the whole line was a comment
			// but here value_part is after ':', so it's an inline comment.
			// Standard YAML requires a space before the # for it to be an inline comment.
			if i > 0 && value_part[i - 1] == ' ' {
				hash_idx = i
				break
			}
		}
	}

	if hash_idx != -1 {
		value_part = value_part[:hash_idx]
	}

	value := strings.trim_space(value_part)

	// Remove quotes if present
	if len(value) >= 2 {
		if (value[0] == '"' && value[len(value) - 1] == '"') ||
		   (value[0] == '\'' && value[len(value) - 1] == '\'') {
			value = value[1:len(value) - 1]
		}
	}

	has_value := len(value) > 0
	return key, value, has_value
}

// parse_array_value parses a YAML array value like "[a, b, c]" into a []string.
// Supports quoted strings, token references {tokens.xxx.yyy}, numeric values, and unquoted strings.
// Returns ([]string, true) on success, (nil, false) if value is not an array.
// Task 10B.5.b: Array parsing support for theme system.
parse_array_value :: proc(value: string) -> ([]string, bool) {
	// Check if value is an array
	trimmed := strings.trim_space(value)
	if len(trimmed) < 2 || trimmed[0] != '[' || trimmed[len(trimmed) - 1] != ']' {
		return nil, false
	}

	// Extract inner content
	inner := trimmed[1:len(trimmed) - 1]
	inner = strings.trim_space(inner)

	if len(inner) == 0 {
		// Empty array
		return []string{}, true
	}

	// Parse elements
	elements := make([dynamic]string)
	defer delete(elements)

	curr_start := 0
	in_quotes := false
	quote_char := rune(0)
	in_braces := false
	brace_depth := 0

	for i := 0; i < len(inner); i += 1 {
		c := inner[i]

		if in_quotes {
			// Inside quoted string
			if rune(c) == quote_char {
				// Check for escaped quote
				if i > 0 && inner[i - 1] == '\\' {
					continue
				}
				in_quotes = false
				quote_char = 0
			}
		} else if in_braces {
			// Inside token reference {tokens.xxx.yyy}
			if c == '{' {
				brace_depth += 1
			} else if c == '}' {
				brace_depth -= 1
				if brace_depth == 0 {
					in_braces = false
				}
			}
		} else {
			// Outside quotes and braces
			if c == '"' || c == '\'' {
				in_quotes = true
				quote_char = rune(c)
			} else if c == '{' {
				in_braces = true
				brace_depth = 1
			} else if c == ',' {
				// Found element separator
				elem := strings.trim_space(inner[curr_start:i])
				if len(elem) > 0 {
					append(&elements, elem)
				}
				curr_start = i + 1
			}
		}
	}

	// Add last element
	last_elem := strings.trim_space(inner[curr_start:])
	if len(last_elem) > 0 {
		append(&elements, last_elem)
	}

	// Convert to []string
	result := make([]string, len(elements))
	for i := 0; i < len(elements); i += 1 {
		result[i] = strings.clone(elements[i])
	}

	return result, true
}

// parse_overrides_extended handles platform-specific commands and component adjustments
parse_overrides_extended :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data, lines: []string) {
	current_section := ""
	current_command := ""
	current_comp := ""
	current_platform := ""
	current_subsection := "" // layout, commands, components, accessibility
	in_platforms := false
	in_commands := false
	in_components := false
	in_layout := false
	in_accessibility := false

	for line in lines {
		trimmed := strings.trim_space(line)
		if len(trimmed) == 0 || trimmed[0] == '#' {
			continue
		}

		indent_level := get_indent_level(line)
		key, value, has_value := parse_key_value(trimmed)

		if indent_level == 0 {
			if current_section != "" do delete(current_section)
			current_section = strings.clone(key)
			in_platforms = false
			continue
		}

		if current_section == "commands" {
			if indent_level == 1 {
				if current_command != "" do delete(current_command)
				current_command = strings.clone(key)
				if current_command not_in theme.commands {
					theme.commands[strings.clone(current_command)] = Command_Def{}
				}

				// Handle flow style: key: { shortcut: "...", label: "..." }
				if has_value && value[0] == '{' && value[len(value) - 1] == '}' {
					inner := value[1:len(value) - 1]

					parts := make([dynamic]string)
					defer delete(parts)
					curr_start := 0
					in_quotes := false
					for i := 0; i < len(inner); i += 1 {
						if inner[i] == '"' || inner[i] == '\'' {
							in_quotes = !in_quotes
						} else if inner[i] == ',' && !in_quotes {
							append(&parts, inner[curr_start:i])
							curr_start = i + 1
						}
					}
					append(&parts, inner[curr_start:])

					cmd := theme.commands[current_command]
					for part in parts {
						trimmed_part := strings.trim_space(part)
						if len(trimmed_part) == 0 do continue

						k_raw, v_raw, ok := parse_key_value(trimmed_part)
						if ok {
							switch k_raw {
							case "shortcut":
								if cmd.shortcut != "" do delete(cmd.shortcut)
								cmd.shortcut = strings.clone(v_raw)
							case "label":
								if cmd.label != "" do delete(cmd.label)
								cmd.label = strings.clone(v_raw)
							}
						}
					}
					theme.commands[current_command] = cmd
				}
			} else if indent_level == 2 && has_value {
				cmd := theme.commands[current_command]
				switch key {
				case "shortcut":
					if cmd.shortcut != "" do delete(cmd.shortcut)
					cmd.shortcut = strings.clone(value)
				case "label":
					if cmd.label != "" do delete(cmd.label)
					cmd.label = strings.clone(value)
				}
				theme.commands[current_command] = cmd
			}
		} else if current_section == "overrides" {
			if indent_level == 1 && key == "platforms" {
				in_platforms = true
			} else if indent_level == 2 && in_platforms {
				if current_platform != "" do delete(current_platform)
				current_platform = strings.clone(key)
			} else if indent_level == 3 && in_platforms {
				// Track which subsection we're in: layout, commands, components, accessibility
				if current_subsection != "" do delete(current_subsection)
				current_subsection = strings.clone(key)
				in_layout = (key == "layout")
				in_commands = (key == "commands")
				in_components = (key == "components")
				in_accessibility = (key == "accessibility")
			} else if indent_level == 4 && has_value && in_platforms {
				if current_platform not_in theme.overrides.platforms {
					p_key := strings.clone(current_platform)
					theme.overrides.platforms[p_key] = Theme_Platform_Override {
						layout          = make(map[string]map[string]string),
						layout_arrays    = make(map[string]map[string][]string), // Task 10B.5.b: Initialize layout arrays
						commands        = make(map[string]Command_Def),
						components      = make(map[string]map[string]string),
						component_arrays = make(map[string]map[string][]string), // Task 10B.5.b: Initialize component arrays
						accessibility    = make(map[string]string),
						global_scale    = 1.0, // Default scale
					}
				}
				p_over := theme.overrides.platforms[current_platform]

				// Handle global_scale at platform level
				if key == "global_scale" {
					if num, ok := strconv.parse_f32(value); ok {
						p_over.global_scale = num
						theme.overrides.platforms[current_platform] = p_over
					}
				} else if in_layout {
					// Platform layout overrides: layout: { dialog: { ... } }
					if current_subsection not_in p_over.layout {
						p_over.layout[strings.clone(current_subsection)] = make(map[string]string)
					}
					if current_subsection not_in p_over.layout_arrays {
						p_over.layout_arrays[strings.clone(current_subsection)] = make(map[string][]string) // Task 10B.5.b: Initialize layout arrays
					}
					// Task 10B.5.b: Check if value is an array
					if array_elems, ok := parse_array_value(value); ok {
						la_map := p_over.layout_arrays[current_subsection]
						la_map[strings.clone(key)] = array_elems
						p_over.layout_arrays[current_subsection] = la_map
					} else {
						l_map := p_over.layout[current_subsection]
						l_map[strings.clone(key)] = strings.clone(value)
						p_over.layout[current_subsection] = l_map
					}
					theme.overrides.platforms[current_platform] = p_over
				} else if in_accessibility {
					// Platform accessibility overrides
					p_over.accessibility[strings.clone(key)] = strings.clone(value)
					theme.overrides.platforms[current_platform] = p_over
				} else if in_commands {
					c_key := strings.clone(key)
					cmd := p_over.commands[c_key]
					if cmd.shortcut != "" do delete(cmd.shortcut)
					cmd.shortcut = strings.clone(value)
					p_over.commands[c_key] = cmd
				}
				// Platform component overrides can be one-liners like: Button: { borderRadius: 6 }
				// but our parser handles mostly key: value.
				// For now, support "Component: property" if it happens at indent 3?
				// No, the yaml shows: Button: { ... } at indent 4
			} else if indent_level == 4 && in_platforms && in_components {
				// Handle Button: { ... } or nested
				if current_comp != "" do delete(current_comp)
				current_comp = strings.clone(key)
				if current_comp not_in theme.overrides.platforms[current_platform].components {
					p_over := theme.overrides.platforms[current_platform]
					p_over.components[strings.clone(current_comp)] = make(map[string]string)
					// Task 10B.5.b: Initialize component arrays
					if current_comp not_in p_over.component_arrays {
						p_over.component_arrays[strings.clone(current_comp)] = make(map[string][]string)
					}
					theme.overrides.platforms[current_platform] = p_over
				}

				if has_value && value[0] == '{' {
					// Minimal flow style parsing for component overrides
					inner := value[1:len(value) - 1]
					p_over := theme.overrides.platforms[current_platform]
					comp_map := p_over.components[current_comp]
					comp_array_map := p_over.component_arrays[current_comp] // Task 10B.5.b: Get component array map

					parts := strings.split(inner, ",")
					defer delete(parts)
					for part in parts {
						tk, tv, ok := parse_key_value(strings.trim_space(part))
						if ok {
							// Task 10B.5.b: Check if value is an array
							if array_elems, arr_ok := parse_array_value(tv); arr_ok {
								comp_array_map[strings.clone(tk)] = array_elems
							} else {
								comp_map[strings.clone(tk)] = strings.clone(tv)
							}
						}
					}
					p_over.components[current_comp] = comp_map
					p_over.component_arrays[current_comp] = comp_array_map // Task 10B.5.b: Save component array map
					theme.overrides.platforms[current_platform] = p_over
				}
			}
		}
	}

	if current_section != "" do delete(current_section)
	if current_command != "" do delete(current_command)
	if current_comp != "" do delete(current_comp)
	if current_platform != "" do delete(current_platform)
	if current_subsection != "" do delete(current_subsection)
}

// parse_accessibility_extended handles the accessibility section
parse_accessibility_extended :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data, lines: []string) {
	theme.accessibility = make(map[string]map[string]string)
	current_section := ""
	current_sub := ""

	for line in lines {
		trimmed := strings.trim_space(line)
		if len(trimmed) == 0 || trimmed[0] == '#' do continue

		indent := get_indent_level(line)
		key, value, has_value := parse_key_value(trimmed)

		if indent == 0 {
			if current_section != "" do delete(current_section)
			current_section = strings.clone(key)
			continue
		}

		if current_section != "accessibility" do continue

		if indent == 1 {
			if current_sub != "" do delete(current_sub)
			current_sub = strings.clone(key)
			if current_sub not_in theme.accessibility {
				theme.accessibility[strings.clone(current_sub)] = make(map[string]string)
			}
		} else if indent == 2 && has_value {
			a_map := theme.accessibility[current_sub]
			a_map[strings.clone(key)] = strings.clone(value)
			theme.accessibility[current_sub] = a_map
		}
	}

	if current_section != "" do delete(current_section)
	if current_sub != "" do delete(current_sub)
}

// parse_components_extended handles complex nested component styles
parse_components_extended :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data, lines: []string) {
	current_section := ""
	current_comp := ""
	current_mode := "" // "base" or "states"
	current_state := ""

	for line in lines {
		trimmed := strings.trim_space(line)
		if len(trimmed) == 0 || trimmed[0] == '#' do continue

		indent := get_indent_level(line)
		key, value, has_value := parse_key_value(trimmed)

		if indent == 0 {
			if current_section != "" do delete(current_section)
			current_section = strings.clone(key)
			continue
		}

		if current_section != "components" do continue

		switch indent {
		case 1:
			if current_comp != "" do delete(current_comp)
			current_comp = strings.clone(key)
			if current_comp not_in theme.components {
				theme.components[strings.clone(current_comp)] = Component_Def {
					base        = make(map[string]string),
					base_arrays = make(map[string][]string), // Task 10B.5.b: Initialize base arrays
					states      = make(map[string]map[string]string),
					state_arrays = make(map[string]map[string][]string), // Task 10B.5.b: Initialize state arrays
				}
			}
		case 2:
			if current_mode != "" do delete(current_mode)
			current_mode = strings.clone(key)
			if current_mode == "states" {
				// No immediate action
			} else if current_mode == "base" {
				// Base properties might follow
			}
		case 3:
			comp := theme.components[current_comp]
			if current_mode == "base" {
				if has_value {
					// Task 10B.5.b: Check if value is an array
					if array_elems, ok := parse_array_value(value); ok {
						comp.base_arrays[strings.clone(key)] = array_elems
					} else {
						comp.base[strings.clone(key)] = strings.clone(value)
					}
				}
			} else if current_mode == "states" {
				if current_state != "" do delete(current_state)
				current_state = strings.clone(key)
				if current_state not_in comp.states {
					comp.states[strings.clone(current_state)] = make(map[string]string)
				}
				// Task 10B.5.b: Initialize state_arrays if needed
				if current_state not_in comp.state_arrays {
					comp.state_arrays[strings.clone(current_state)] = make(map[string][]string)
				}
			}
			theme.components[current_comp] = comp
		case 4:
			if current_mode == "states" && current_state != "" {
				comp := theme.components[current_comp]
				state_map := comp.states[current_state]
				if has_value {
					// Task 10B.5.b: Check if value is an array
					if array_elems, ok := parse_array_value(value); ok {
						sa_map := comp.state_arrays[current_state]
						sa_map[strings.clone(key)] = array_elems
						comp.state_arrays[current_state] = sa_map
					} else {
						state_map[strings.clone(key)] = strings.clone(value)
					}
				}
				comp.states[current_state] = state_map
				theme.components[current_comp] = comp
			}
		}
	}

	if current_section != "" do delete(current_section)
	if current_comp != "" do delete(current_comp)
	if current_mode != "" do delete(current_mode)
	if current_state != "" do delete(current_state)
}

// parse_layout_extended handles layout and services simple maps
parse_layout_extended :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data, lines: []string) {
	current_section := ""
	current_sub := ""

	for line in lines {
		trimmed := strings.trim_space(line)
		if len(trimmed) == 0 || trimmed[0] == '#' do continue

		indent := get_indent_level(line)
		key, value, has_value := parse_key_value(trimmed)

		if indent == 0 {
			if current_section != "" do delete(current_section)
			current_section = strings.clone(key)
			continue
		}

		if current_section == "services" {
			if indent == 1 && has_value {
				theme.services[strings.clone(key)] = strings.clone(value)
			}
		} else if current_section == "layout" {
			if indent == 1 {
				if current_sub != "" do delete(current_sub)
				current_sub = strings.clone(key)
				if current_sub not_in theme.layout {
					theme.layout[strings.clone(current_sub)] = make(map[string]string)
				}
				if current_sub not_in theme.layout_arrays {
					theme.layout_arrays[strings.clone(current_sub)] = make(map[string][]string) // Task 10B.5.b: Initialize layout arrays
				}
			} else if indent == 2 && has_value {
				// Task 10B.5.b: Check if value is an array
				if array_elems, ok := parse_array_value(value); ok {
					// Store in layout_arrays
					la_map := theme.layout_arrays[current_sub]
					la_map[strings.clone(key)] = array_elems
					theme.layout_arrays[current_sub] = la_map
				} else {
					// Store as regular string value
					l_map := theme.layout[current_sub]
					l_map[strings.clone(key)] = strings.clone(value)
					theme.layout[current_sub] = l_map
				}
			}
		}
	}

	if current_section != "" do delete(current_section)
	if current_sub != "" do delete(current_sub)
}

// DPI scaling utilities for RuiShin theme system.
// Provides density-independent units (dp) to pixels (px) conversion
// for cross-device support across different screen densities.
//
// This module integrates with platform.odin to get platform-specific
// scale factors and applies them to UI measurements.
//
// Phase 10B.3.c: DPI Scaling - Data layer only
// Phase 11 will use these utilities during widget rendering.

import "core:fmt"
import platform "project:platform"

// DPI_Scale_Info contains DPI scaling information for a theme.
// This struct is used to cache scale factors and avoid repeated lookups.
DPI_Scale_Info :: struct {
	scale:          f32, // Global scale factor (from platform overrides)
	platform_name:  string, // Name of the platform (for debugging)
}

// init_dpi_scale_info initializes DPI scale information from a theme.
// Detects the current platform and retrieves the appropriate scale factor.
// Returns DPI_Scale_Info with scale factor (defaults to 1.0 if not found).
//
// Example:
//   dpi_info := init_dpi_scale_info(&theme)
//   scaled_value := dp_to_px(dpi_info, 16.0)
init_dpi_scale_info :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data) -> DPI_Scale_Info {
	platform_info := platform.detectPlatform()
	platform_name := platform.get_platform_name(platform_info)
	scale := getPlatformScale(theme, platform_name)

	return DPI_Scale_Info {
		scale         = scale,
		platform_name = platform_name,
	}
}

// dp_to_px converts density-independent pixels (dp) to physical pixels (px).
// Uses the scale factor from DPI_Scale_Info to perform the conversion.
// Returns the scaled value in pixels.
//
// Example:
//   dpi_info := init_dpi_scale_info(&theme)
//   padding_px := dp_to_px(dpi_info, 16.0) // 16dp -> 19.2px on iOS (scale 1.2)
dp_to_px :: proc(dpi_info: DPI_Scale_Info, dp: f32) -> f32 {
	return dp * dpi_info.scale
}

// px_to_dp converts physical pixels (px) to density-independent pixels (dp).
// Uses the scale factor from DPI_Scale_Info to perform the conversion.
// Returns the unscaled value in dp.
//
// Example:
//   dpi_info := init_dpi_scale_info(&theme)
//   padding_dp := px_to_dp(dpi_info, 19.2) // 19.2px -> 16dp on iOS (scale 1.2)
px_to_dp :: proc(dpi_info: DPI_Scale_Info, px: f32) -> f32 {
	if dpi_info.scale == 0 {
		return px // Avoid division by zero
	}
	return px / dpi_info.scale
}

// scale_rect scales a rectangle's dimensions by the DPI scale factor.
// Returns a new rect with scaled width and height.
// This is useful for scaling widget dimensions.
//
// Example:
//   dpi_info := init_dpi_scale_info(&theme)
//   rect := Rect { x = 0, y = 0, w = 100, h = 50 }
//   scaled_rect := scale_rect(dpi_info, rect)
scale_rect :: proc(dpi_info: DPI_Scale_Info, rect: Rect) -> Rect {
	return Rect {
		x = rect.x,
		y = rect.y,
		w = dp_to_px(dpi_info, rect.w),
		h = dp_to_px(dpi_info, rect.h),
	}
}

// scale_point scales a point's coordinates by the DPI scale factor.
// Returns a new point with scaled x and y coordinates.
// This is useful for scaling widget positions.
//
// Example:
//   dpi_info := init_dpi_scale_info(&theme)
//   point := Point { x = 10, y = 20 }
//   scaled_point := scale_point(dpi_info, point)
scale_point :: proc(dpi_info: DPI_Scale_Info, point: Point) -> Point {
	return Point {
		x = dp_to_px(dpi_info, point.x),
		y = dp_to_px(dpi_info, point.y),
	}
}

// scale_spacing scales a spacing value by the DPI scale factor.
// Returns the scaled spacing value.
// This is useful for scaling padding, margins, and gaps.
//
// Example:
//   dpi_info := init_dpi_scale_info(&theme)
//   padding := scale_spacing(dpi_info, 16.0)
scale_spacing :: proc(dpi_info: DPI_Scale_Info, spacing: f32) -> f32 {
	return dp_to_px(dpi_info, spacing)
}

// scale_font_size scales a font size by the DPI scale factor.
// Returns the scaled font size.
// This is useful for scaling text sizes.
//
// Example:
//   dpi_info := init_dpi_scale_info(&theme)
//   font_size := scale_font_size(dpi_info, 14.0)
scale_font_size :: proc(dpi_info: DPI_Scale_Info, font_size: f32) -> f32 {
	return dp_to_px(dpi_info, font_size)
}

// scale_border_width scales a border width by the DPI scale factor.
// Returns the scaled border width.
// This is useful for scaling border thickness.
//
// Example:
//   dpi_info := init_dpi_scale_info(&theme)
//   border_width := scale_border_width(dpi_info, 1.0)
scale_border_width :: proc(dpi_info: DPI_Scale_Info, border_width: f32) -> f32 {
	return dp_to_px(dpi_info, border_width)
}

// scale_border_radius scales a border radius by the DPI scale factor.
// Returns the scaled border radius.
// This is useful for scaling rounded corner radii.
//
// Example:
//   dpi_info := init_dpi_scale_info(&theme)
//   radius := scale_border_radius(dpi_info, 4.0)
scale_border_radius :: proc(dpi_info: DPI_Scale_Info, border_radius: f32) -> f32 {
	return dp_to_px(dpi_info, border_radius)
}

// scale_icon_size scales an icon size by the DPI scale factor.
// Returns the scaled icon size.
// This is useful for scaling icon dimensions.
//
// Example:
//   dpi_info := init_dpi_scale_info(&theme)
//   icon_size := scale_icon_size(dpi_info, 24.0)
scale_icon_size :: proc(dpi_info: DPI_Scale_Info, icon_size: f32) -> f32 {
	return dp_to_px(dpi_info, icon_size)
}

// scale_touch_target scales a touch target size by the DPI scale factor.
// Returns the scaled touch target size.
// This is useful for ensuring minimum touch target sizes on mobile devices.
//
// Example:
//   dpi_info := init_dpi_scale_info(&theme)
//   min_target := scale_touch_target(dpi_info, 44.0)
scale_touch_target :: proc(dpi_info: DPI_Scale_Info, touch_target: f32) -> f32 {
	return dp_to_px(dpi_info, touch_target)
}

// get_scale_factor returns the scale factor from DPI_Scale_Info.
// This is a convenience function for direct access to the scale factor.
//
// Example:
//   dpi_info := init_dpi_scale_info(&theme)
//   scale := get_scale_factor(dpi_info)
get_scale_factor :: proc(dpi_info: DPI_Scale_Info) -> f32 {
	return dpi_info.scale
}

// get_platform_name returns the platform name from DPI_Scale_Info.
// This is useful for debugging and logging.
//
// Example:
//   dpi_info := init_dpi_scale_info(&theme)
//   platform := get_platform_name(dpi_info)
//   fmt.printf("Using DPI scale for platform: %s\n", platform)
get_platform_name :: proc(dpi_info: DPI_Scale_Info) -> string {
	return dpi_info.platform_name
}

// is_high_dpi checks if the current platform has a high DPI scale factor.
// Returns true if scale factor > 1.0, false otherwise.
//
// Example:
//   dpi_info := init_dpi_scale_info(&theme)
//   if is_high_dpi(dpi_info) {
//       // Apply additional scaling for high DPI displays
//   }
is_high_dpi :: proc(dpi_info: DPI_Scale_Info) -> bool {
	return dpi_info.scale > 1.0
}

// get_default_scale returns the default scale factor for a given platform.
// This is useful for fallback when theme data is not available.
//
// Common scale factors:
//   - Desktop (macOS, Windows, Linux): 1.0
//   - Mobile (iOS, Android): 1.2
//   - Web: 1.0
//
// Example:
//   scale := get_default_scale("ios") // Returns 1.2
get_default_scale :: proc(platform_name: string) -> f32 {
	switch platform_name {
	case "macos", "windows", "linux":
		return 1.0
	case "ios", "android":
		return 1.2
	case "web":
		return 1.0
	case:
		return 1.0 // Default fallback
	}
}

// Rect represents a 2D rectangle with position and dimensions.
// This is a simple struct for DPI scaling operations.
// Phase 11 will use the actual engine's Rect type.
Rect :: struct {
	x: f32,
	y: f32,
	w: f32,
	h: f32,
}

// Point represents a 2D point with coordinates.
// This is a simple struct for DPI scaling operations.
// Phase 11 will use the actual engine's Point type.
Point :: struct {
	x: f32,
	y: f32,
}

// Convenience functions for direct theme access without DPI_Scale_Info

// dp_to_px_auto converts dp to px using the current platform's scale factor.
// This is a convenience function that automatically detects the platform.
//
// Example:
//   padding_px := dp_to_px_auto(&theme, 16.0)
dp_to_px_auto :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data, dp: f32) -> f32 {
	dpi_info := init_dpi_scale_info(theme)
	return dp_to_px(dpi_info, dp)
}

// px_to_dp_auto converts px to dp using the current platform's scale factor.
// This is a convenience function that automatically detects the platform.
//
// Example:
//   padding_dp := px_to_dp_auto(&theme, 19.2)
px_to_dp_auto :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data, px: f32) -> f32 {
	dpi_info := init_dpi_scale_info(theme)
	return px_to_dp(dpi_info, px)
}

// get_current_scale returns the scale factor for the current platform.
// This is a convenience function that wraps platform.getCurrentPlatformScale().
//
// Example:
//   scale := get_current_scale(&theme)
get_current_scale :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data) -> f32 {
	return getCurrentPlatformScale(theme)
}

//
// Token storage for RuiShin theme system.
// Provides native Odin types for design tokens (Color, f32, etc.)
// to avoid expensive string parsing during rendering.
// Phase 10C2.10: Extended with gradient token support for SVG/RSD integration.

import "core:fmt"
import "core:strconv"
import "core:mem"
import "core:strings"
import assets "project:assets"
import string_utils "project:ui/theme"

// Vector_Token_Kinds defines the types of vector-related tokens supported.
// Phase 10C2.10: Added for SVG/RSD gradient preset integration.
Vector_Token_Kinds :: enum {
    Color,     // Color token (rgba)
    Gradient,  // Gradient token (linear or radial)
}

// Gradient_Spec represents a gradient definition for theme tokens.
// Phase 10C2.10: Enables theme-defined gradients for reuse in SVG/RSD assets.
Gradient_Spec :: struct {
    type:      Gradient_Type,
    stops:     []Gradient_Stop,
    transform: matrix[3, 3]f32,
}

// Gradient_Type defines the type of gradient.
// Matches RSD Gradient_Type for seamless integration.
Gradient_Type :: enum u8 {
    Linear = 0,
    Radial = 1,
}

// Gradient_Stop represents a single color stop in a gradient.
Gradient_Stop :: struct {
    t:     f32,        // Position (0.0 to 1.0)
    color: [4]f32,     // RGBA color
}

// Vector_Token represents a theme token for vector graphics (colors or gradients).
// Phase 10C2.10: Enables theme-consistent styling for SVG/RSD assets.
Vector_Token :: struct {
    kind:  Vector_Token_Kinds,
    path:  string,           // Token path (e.g., "colors.primary", "gradients.button_hover")
    value: union {
        [4]f32,             // Color value (when kind = .Color)
        Gradient_Spec,      // Gradient specification (when kind = .Gradient)
    },
}

// Token_Store holds resolved design tokens in native types.
Token_Store :: struct {
    colors:    map[string]assets.Color,
    spacing:   map[string]f32,
    type:      map[string]Type_Property,
    gradients: map[string]Gradient_Spec,  // Phase 10C2.10: Theme gradient presets
}

// Type_Property represents a typography-related token value.
// Since typography tokens can be numeric or strings, we use a tagged union.
Type_Property :: union {
    f32,
    int,
    string,
    assets.Color,
}

// init_token_store initializes a new token store.
init_token_store :: proc(allocator: mem.Allocator = context.allocator) -> Token_Store {
	return Token_Store {
		colors = make(map[string]assets.Color, allocator),
		spacing = make(map[string]f32, allocator),
		type = make(map[string]Type_Property, allocator),
		gradients = make(map[string]Gradient_Spec, allocator),  // Phase 10C2.10
	}
}

// delete_token_store cleans up resources in the token store.
delete_token_store :: proc(store: ^Token_Store) {
	delete(store.colors)
	delete(store.spacing)

	// Note: Strings in Type_Property are owned by the raw Theme_Tokens
	// until the layered resolver is implemented (10B.1.c).
	delete(store.type)

	// Phase 10C2.10: Clean up gradient stops
	for k, spec in store.gradients {
		delete(k)
		delete(spec.stops)
	}
	delete(store.gradients)
}

// resolve_tokens converts raw string tokens from Theme_Tokens into typed tokens.
// This is called during the theme loading process to ensure tokens are ready for use.
// Phase 10C2.10: Extended to resolve gradient tokens.
resolve_tokens :: proc(raw: ^Theme_Tokens, store: ^Token_Store, allocator: mem.Allocator = context.allocator) {
	// 1. Resolve Colors
	for k, v in raw.colors {
		if c, ok := resolve_color(v); ok {
			store.colors[k] = c
		} else {
			fmt.printf("[Theme] Warning: Failed to resolve color token '%s': '%s'\n", k, v)
		}
	}

	// 2. Resolve Spacing (already parsed to f32 in parser, just copy)
	for k, v in raw.spacing {
		store.spacing[k] = v
	}

	// 3. Resolve Type properties
	for k, v in raw.type {
		// Try parsing as numeric first
		if val, ok := strconv.parse_f32(v); ok {
			store.type[k] = val
		} else if i_val, i_ok := strconv.parse_int(v); i_ok {
			store.type[k] = i_val
		} else {
			// Store as string reference (owned by raw tokens)
			store.type[k] = v
		}
	}

	// 4. Phase 10C2.10: Resolve Gradient tokens
	for k, v in raw.gradients {
		if spec, ok := resolve_gradient(v, allocator); ok {
			store.gradients[k] = spec
		} else {
			fmt.printf("[Theme] Warning: Failed to resolve gradient token '%s': '%s'\n", k, v)
		}
	}
}

//
// Gradient Parsing and Resolution (Phase 10C2.10)
// Supports inline gradient definitions in theme tokens for SVG/RSD integration.

// resolve_gradient parses a gradient string and returns a Gradient_Spec.
// Format: "type:linear|stops:color1@t1,color2@t2,..." or reference to another gradient token
// Phase 10C2.10: Enables theme-defined gradients for vector assets.
resolve_gradient :: proc(gradient_value: string, allocator: mem.Allocator = context.allocator) -> (Gradient_Spec, bool) {
	// Check if it's a gradient token reference (starts with "$" or "ref:")
	if len(gradient_value) > 0 {
		first_char := gradient_value[0]
		if first_char == '$' || strings.has_prefix(gradient_value, "ref:") {
			// This is a reference to another gradient token
			// Return empty spec - actual resolution happens at runtime via theme_get_gradient
			return Gradient_Spec{}, false
		}
	}

	// Parse inline gradient definition
	// Format: "type:linear|stops:#RRGGBB@t1;#RRGGBB@t2"
	spec: Gradient_Spec
	spec.type = .Linear  // Default to linear
	spec.transform = matrix[3, 3]f32{}  // Identity matrix

	parts := strings.split(gradient_value, "|")
	defer delete(parts)

	for &part in parts {
		trimmed := strings.trim_space(part)
		if strings.has_prefix(trimmed, "type:") {
			type_str := trimmed[5:]
			switch type_str {
			case "linear":
				spec.type = .Linear
			case "radial":
				spec.type = .Radial
			}
		} else if strings.has_prefix(trimmed, "stops:") {
			stops_str := trimmed[6:]
			stops_parts := strings.split(stops_str, ";")
			defer delete(stops_parts)

			spec.stops = make([]Gradient_Stop, len(stops_parts), allocator)

			for idx := 0; idx < len(stops_parts); idx += 1 {
				stop_str := stops_parts[idx]
				trimmed_stop := strings.trim_space(stop_str)
				if trimmed_stop == "" { continue }

				// Parse stop format: "#RRGGBB@t" or "r,g,b,a@t"
				at_idx := strings.last_index(trimmed_stop, "@")
				if at_idx == -1 { continue }

				color_part := trimmed_stop[:at_idx]
				t_part := trimmed_stop[at_idx + 1:]

				// Parse position t
				if t, ok := strconv.parse_f32(t_part); ok {
					spec.stops[idx].t = t
				} else {
					continue
				}

				// Parse color
				color: [4]f32
				if strings.has_prefix(color_part, "#") {
					if c, ok := parse_hex_color(color_part); ok {
						color = c
					} else {
						continue
					}
				} else {
					// Parse r,g,b,a format
					rgba_parts := strings.split(color_part, ",")
					defer delete(rgba_parts)

					if len(rgba_parts) >= 3 {
						if r, ok := strconv.parse_f32(rgba_parts[0]); ok { color[0] = r }
						if g, ok := strconv.parse_f32(rgba_parts[1]); ok { color[1] = g }
						if b, ok := strconv.parse_f32(rgba_parts[2]); ok { color[2] = b }
						if len(rgba_parts) >= 4 {
							if a, ok := strconv.parse_f32(rgba_parts[3]); ok { color[3] = a }
							else { color[3] = 1.0 }
						} else { color[3] = 1.0 }
					}
				}
				spec.stops[idx].color = color
			}
		}
	}

	return spec, len(spec.stops) > 0
}

// parse_hex_color parses a hex color string (#RGB, #RRGGBB, #AARRGGBB) to [4]f32.
parse_hex_color :: proc(hex_str: string) -> ([4]f32, bool) {
	color: [4]f32
	if len(hex_str) < 4 || hex_str[0] != '#' {
		return color, false
	}

	// Use a mutable variable instead of reassigning parameter
	mut_hex := hex_str[1:]
	if len(mut_hex) == 3 {
		mut_hex = string([]u8{mut_hex[0], mut_hex[0], mut_hex[1], mut_hex[1], mut_hex[2], mut_hex[2]})
	}

	if len(mut_hex) != 6 && len(mut_hex) != 8 {
		return color, false
	}

	parse_hex_byte :: proc(s: string) -> (u8, bool) {
		if len(s) != 2 { return 0, false }
		high := s[0]
		low := s[1]
		to_nibble :: proc(c: u8) -> u8 {
			if c >= '0' && c <= '9' { return c - '0' }
			if c >= 'a' && c <= 'f' { return c - 'a' + 10 }
			if c >= 'A' && c <= 'F' { return c - 'A' + 10 }
			return 0
		}
		return to_nibble(high) << 4 | to_nibble(low), true
	}

	if r, ok := parse_hex_byte(mut_hex[0:2]); ok { color[0] = f32(r) / 255.0 }
	if g, ok := parse_hex_byte(mut_hex[2:4]); ok { color[1] = f32(g) / 255.0 }
	if b, ok := parse_hex_byte(mut_hex[4:6]); ok { color[2] = f32(b) / 255.0 }

	if len(mut_hex) == 8 {
		if a, ok := parse_hex_byte(mut_hex[6:8]); ok { color[3] = f32(a) / 255.0 }
	} else {
		color[3] = 1.0
	}

	return color, true
}

// theme_get_gradient retrieves a gradient token from the theme store.
// Phase 10C2.10: Added for SVG/RSD integration.
// Phase 10C3.1.a: Restored gradient token prefix functionality with proper string building.
theme_get_gradient :: proc(store: ^Token_Store, token: string) -> (Gradient_Spec, bool) {
	if store == nil { return Gradient_Spec{}, false }
	if spec, ok := store.gradients[token]; ok {
		return spec, true
	}

	// Also check with "gradients." prefix for backward compatibility
	// Phase 10C3.1.b: Using new string building API
	prefixed_token := string_utils.string_build_gradient_token(token)
	if spec, ok := store.gradients[prefixed_token]; ok {
		return spec, true
	}

	return Gradient_Spec{}, false
}

DEBUG: Source content: package theme

// Color integration for RuiShin theme system.
// Provides color resolution from theme YAML values to assets.Color type.
// Integrates with existing assets/colors.odin infrastructure.

import assets "project:assets"
import vfs "project:vfs"

// _resolve_color parses a color value from theme YAML.
// Accepts two formats:
//   1. Hex strings: "#RRGGBB", "#RRGGBBAA", "RRGGBB", "RRGGBBAA"
//   2. Named colors: "systemblue", "red", "white", etc. (lowercase)
//
// Returns (Color, true) on success, (Color{}, false) on failure.
//
// Integration with assets/colors.odin:
//   - Uses assets.Color :: [4]f32 type (normalized RGBA)
//   - Calls assets.colorFromHex() for hex parsing
//   - Calls assets.ColorByName() for named color lookup
//
// Examples:
//   _resolve_color("#007AFF")      -> Color{0.0, 0.478, 1.0, 1.0}, true
//   _resolve_color("systemblue")   -> Color{0.039, 0.518, 1.0, 1.0}, true
//   _resolve_color("#0000001A")    -> Color{0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.102}, true
//   _resolve_color("invalid")      -> Color{}, false
resolve_color :: proc(value: string) -> (assets.Color, bool) {
	// Try hex format first: "#RRGGBB", "#RRGGBBAA", "RRGGBB", "RRGGBBAA"
	if c, ok := assets.colorFromHex(value); ok {
		return c, true
	}

	// Fall back to named color lookup (lowercase keys)
	// Named colors include CSS colors, Raylib colors, and system colors
	if c, ok := assets.colorByName(value); ok {
		return c, true
	}

	// Neither format matched
	return assets.Color{}, false
}

// Accessibility overrides for RuiShin theme system.
// This module provides functions to apply accessibility-specific overrides to themes.
//
// Override pipeline order:
// 1. Base theme values
// 2. Platform overrides (e.g., iOS, Android)
// 3. State overrides (component-specific)
// 4. Accessibility overrides (applied by this module, last in pipeline)
//
// Example:
//   theme, ok := load_theme("themes/default.yaml")
//   if !ok { return }
//   apply_accessibility_overrides(&theme)
//   if get_high_contrast_enabled(&theme) {
//       // High contrast colors are already applied to components
//   }

import "core:fmt"
import "core:strconv"
import "core:strings"
import assets "project:assets"
import parser "project:ui/theme" // For Theme_Data type

// TEMPORARILY SIMPLIFIED - Full accessibility features disabled due to type safety issues
// The original implementation had complex type mismatches that need to be resolved

// apply_accessibility_overrides applies all accessibility-specific overrides to a theme.
// This function should be called after platform overrides and before resolving styles.
// Returns true if accessibility overrides were applied, false if no accessibility settings found.
//
// Example:
//   theme, ok := load_theme("themes/default.yaml")
//   if !ok { return }
//   apply_accessibility_overrides(&theme)
//   if get_high_contrast_enabled(&theme) {
//       // High contrast colors are already applied to components
//   }
// apply_accessibility_overrides applies all accessibility-specific overrides to a theme.
// This function should be called after platform overrides and before resolving styles.
// Returns true if accessibility overrides were applied, false if no accessibility settings found.
//
// Resolution order (per Phase 10B invariants):
// 1. Base theme values
// 2. Platform-specific overrides (applied by platform.odin)
// 3. State overrides (component-specific)
// 4. Accessibility overrides (applied by this function, last in pipeline)
//
// Example:
//   theme, ok := load_theme("themes/default.yaml")
//   if !ok { return }
//   applyPlatformOverridesAuto(&theme)
//   apply_accessibility_overrides(&theme)
apply_accessibility_overrides :: proc(theme: ^parser.Theme_Data) -> bool {
	if len(theme.accessibility) == 0 {
		// No accessibility settings defined
		return false
	}

	// Apply accessibility overrides in order: high contrast, focus indicators, reduced motion
	// Phase 10C3.2.a: Restored high contrast overrides with proper type safety
	apply_high_contrast_overrides(theme)
	// Phase 10C3.2.b: Restored focus indicator overrides with proper type safety
	apply_focus_indicator_overrides(theme)
	// Phase 10C3.2.c: Restored reduced motion overrides with proper type safety
	apply_reduced_motion_overrides(theme)
	
	return true
}

// Placeholder types and functions to maintain API compatibility
Focus_Indicator_Style :: struct {
	width:  f32,
	color:  assets.Color,
	style:  string, // "solid", "dashed", "dotted"
	offset: f32,
}

// TEMPORARILY DISABLED - Original implementations had type safety issues
// These functions are stubs to allow compilation while the proper implementations
// are being developed with correct type handling.

// apply_high_contrast_overrides applies high contrast color overrides to component styles.
// When high contrast mode is enabled, component colors are forced to high contrast values
// to improve visibility for users with visual impairments.
//
// This implementation uses proper type safety by:
// 1. Checking if high contrast is enabled first
// 2. Getting the high contrast colors map
// 3. Iterating through components and applying color overrides safely
// 4. Using the existing helper functions for type-safe operations
//
// Example YAML:
//   accessibility:
//     high_contrast:
//       enabled: true
//       colors:
//         primary: "#0000FF"
//         text: "#000000"
//         background: "#FFFFFF"
//         border: "#000000"
apply_high_contrast_overrides :: proc(theme: ^parser.Theme_Data) {
	// Check if high contrast mode is enabled
	if !get_high_contrast_enabled(theme) {
		return
	}
	
	// Get the high contrast colors map
	hc_colors := get_accessibility_colors(theme)
	if len(hc_colors) == 0 {
		// No high contrast colors defined
		return
	}
	
	// Apply high contrast colors to all components
	// We need to be careful with map mutation - create new maps for modified components
	for comp_name, &comp_def in theme.components {
		modified := false
		new_base := make(map[string]string)
		
		// Copy base properties and apply color overrides
		for prop_name, prop_value in comp_def.base {
			if is_color_property(prop_name) {
				// Get the appropriate high contrast color key for this property
				color_key := get_high_contrast_color_key(prop_name)
				if color_key != "" && color_key in hc_colors {
					// Apply the high contrast color
					new_base[prop_name] = hc_colors[color_key]
					modified = true
				} else {
					// Keep the original value
					new_base[prop_name] = prop_value
				}
			} else {
				// Keep non-color properties as-is
				new_base[prop_name] = prop_value
			}
		}
		
		new_states := make(map[string]map[string]string)
		
		// Copy state properties and apply color overrides
		for state_name, state_map in comp_def.states {
			new_state := make(map[string]string)
			state_modified := false
			
			for prop_name, prop_value in state_map {
				if is_color_property(prop_name) {
					// Get the appropriate high contrast color key for this property
					color_key := get_high_contrast_color_key(prop_name)
					if color_key != "" && color_key in hc_colors {
						// Apply the high contrast color
						new_state[prop_name] = hc_colors[color_key]
						state_modified = true
					} else {
						// Keep the original value
						new_state[prop_name] = prop_value
					}
				} else {
					// Keep non-color properties as-is
					new_state[prop_name] = prop_value
				}
			}
			
			new_states[state_name] = new_state
			if state_modified {
				modified = true
			}
		}
		
		// If this component was modified, update it in the theme
		if modified {
			comp_def.base = new_base
			comp_def.states = new_states
			theme.components[comp_name] = comp_def
		}
	}
}

// apply_focus_indicator_overrides applies focus indicator styles to component styles.
// Focus indicators provide enhanced visual feedback for keyboard navigation.
// This function adds or updates focus-related properties in component state definitions.
//
// This implementation uses proper type safety by:
// 1. Checking if focus_indicators section exists
// 2. Extracting focus indicator settings safely
// 3. Creating new state maps instead of modifying during iteration
// 4. Using the existing Focus_Indicator_Style struct for type safety
//
// Example YAML:
//   accessibility:
//     focus_indicators:
//       width: 2
//       color: "{tokens.colors.focus}"
//       style: "solid"
//       offset: 2
apply_focus_indicator_overrides :: proc(theme: ^parser.Theme_Data) {
	// Check if focus_indicators section exists
	if "focus_indicators" not_in theme.accessibility {
		return
	}

	fi_map := theme.accessibility["focus_indicators"]

	// Get focus indicator settings
	width_str, has_width := fi_map["width"]
	color_str, has_color := fi_map["color"]
	style_str, has_style := fi_map["style"]
	offset_str, has_offset := fi_map["offset"]

	if !has_width && !has_color && !has_style && !has_offset {
		// No focus indicator settings defined
		return
	}

	// Apply focus indicator styles to all components
	// We need to be careful with map mutation - create new maps for modified components
	for comp_name, &comp_def in theme.components {
		// Check if component has a "focused" state
		if "focused" in comp_def.states {
			// Create a new focused state map with the original properties
			new_focused_state := make(map[string]string)
			
			// Copy existing focused state properties
			for prop_name, prop_value in comp_def.states["focused"] {
				new_focused_state[prop_name] = prop_value
			}
			
			// Apply focus indicator properties
			if has_width {
				new_focused_state["focusWidth"] = width_str
			}
			if has_color {
				new_focused_state["focusColor"] = color_str
			}
			if has_style {
				new_focused_state["focusStyle"] = style_str
			}
			if has_offset {
				new_focused_state["focusOffset"] = offset_str
			}
			
			// Update the focused state in the component
			comp_def.states["focused"] = new_focused_state
		} else {
			// Component doesn't have a focused state, create one
			new_focused_state := make(map[string]string)
			
			// Apply focus indicator properties
			if has_width {
				new_focused_state["focusWidth"] = width_str
			}
			if has_color {
				new_focused_state["focusColor"] = color_str
			}
			if has_style {
				new_focused_state["focusStyle"] = style_str
			}
			if has_offset {
				new_focused_state["focusOffset"] = offset_str
			}
			
			// Add the focused state to the component
			comp_def.states["focused"] = new_focused_state
		}
		
		// Update the component in the theme
		theme.components[comp_name] = comp_def
	}
}

// apply_reduced_motion_overrides applies reduced motion settings to component styles.
// When reduced motion is enabled, animations and transitions are disabled or scaled down
// to accommodate users with vestibular disorders or motion sensitivity.
//
// This implementation uses proper type safety by:
// 1. Checking if reduced motion is enabled
// 2. Extracting settings safely
// 3. Creating new property maps instead of modifying during iteration
// 4. Using helper functions for property type checking
//
// Example YAML:
//   accessibility:
//     reduced_motion:
//       enabled: true
//       disable_transitions: true
//       disable_animations: true
apply_reduced_motion_overrides :: proc(theme: ^parser.Theme_Data) {
	// Check if reduced_motion section exists
	if "reduced_motion" not_in theme.accessibility {
		return
	}

	rm_map := theme.accessibility["reduced_motion"]

	// Check enabled flag
	if "enabled" not_in rm_map {
		return
	}

	enabled_str := rm_map["enabled"]
	if enabled_str != "true" && enabled_str != "1" {
		// Reduced motion is not enabled
		return
	}

	// Check if transitions should be disabled
	disable_transitions := false
	if "disable_transitions" in rm_map {
		disable_transitions_str := rm_map["disable_transitions"]
		disable_transitions = (disable_transitions_str == "true" || disable_transitions_str == "1")
	}

	// Check if animations should be disabled
	disable_animations := false
	if "disable_animations" in rm_map {
		disable_animations_str := rm_map["disable_animations"]
		disable_animations = (disable_animations_str == "true" || disable_animations_str == "1")
	}

	// Apply reduced motion settings to all components
	// We need to be careful with map mutation - create new maps for modified components
	for comp_name, &comp_def in theme.components {
		modified := false
		
		// Apply to base styles
		if disable_transitions || disable_animations {
			new_base := make(map[string]string)
			
			for prop_name, prop_value in comp_def.base {
				if disable_transitions && is_transition_property(prop_name) {
					new_base[prop_name] = "none"
					modified = true
				} else if disable_animations && is_animation_property(prop_name) {
					new_base[prop_name] = "none"
					modified = true
				} else {
					new_base[prop_name] = prop_value
				}
			}
			
			comp_def.base = new_base
		}
		
		// Apply to state styles
		if disable_transitions || disable_animations {
			new_states := make(map[string]map[string]string)
			
			for state_name, state_map in comp_def.states {
				new_state := make(map[string]string)
				state_modified := false
				
				for prop_name, prop_value in state_map {
					if disable_transitions && is_transition_property(prop_name) {
						new_state[prop_name] = "none"
						state_modified = true
					} else if disable_animations && is_animation_property(prop_name) {
						new_state[prop_name] = "none"
						state_modified = true
					} else {
						new_state[prop_name] = prop_value
					}
				}
				
				new_states[state_name] = new_state
				if state_modified {
					modified = true
				}
			}
			
			comp_def.states = new_states
		}
		
		// If this component was modified, update it in the theme
		if modified {
			theme.components[comp_name] = comp_def
		}
	}
}

// Helper functions for property type checking

// is_color_property checks if a property name represents a color
is_color_property :: proc(prop_name: string) -> bool {
	color_props := []string{
		"color", "backgroundColor", "borderColor", "textColor",
		"fill", "stroke", "background", "foreground",
		"primaryColor", "secondaryColor", "accentColor",
	}
	
	for color_prop in color_props {
		if prop_name == color_prop {
			return true
		}
	}
	return false
}

// is_transition_property checks if a property name is a transition-related property.
// Returns true if the property is a transition property, false otherwise.
is_transition_property :: proc(prop_name: string) -> bool {
	transition_props := []string {
		"transition",
		"transitionProperty",
		"transitionDuration",
		"transitionTimingFunction",
		"transitionDelay",
	}

	for prop in transition_props {
		if prop_name == prop {
			return true
		}
	}

	return false
}

// is_animation_property checks if a property name is an animation-related property.
// Returns true if the property is an animation property, false otherwise.
is_animation_property :: proc(prop_name: string) -> bool {
	animation_props := []string {
		"animation",
		"animationName",
		"animationDuration",
		"animationTimingFunction",
		"animationDelay",
		"animationIterationCount",
		"animationDirection",
		"animationFillMode",
		"animationPlayState",
	}

	for prop in animation_props {
		if prop_name == prop {
			return true
		}
	}

	return false
}

// get_high_contrast_color_key maps property names to high contrast color keys
get_high_contrast_color_key :: proc(prop_name: string) -> string {
	// Simplified mapping for reference
	switch prop_name {
	case "color", "textColor", "foregroundColor": return "text"
	case "backgroundColor", "background": return "background"
	case "borderColor": return "border"
	case "primaryColor": return "primary"
	case "secondaryColor": return "secondary"
	case "accentColor": return "accent"
	case "fill": return "fill"
	case "stroke": return "stroke"
	}
	return ""
}

// get_high_contrast_enabled checks if high contrast mode is enabled
get_high_contrast_enabled :: proc(theme: ^parser.Theme_Data) -> bool {
	if "high_contrast" not_in theme.accessibility {
		return false
	}
	
	hc_map := theme.accessibility["high_contrast"]
	if "enabled" not_in hc_map {
		return false
	}
	
	return hc_map["enabled"] == "true" || hc_map["enabled"] == "1"
}

// get_focus_indicator_style returns the configured focus indicator style
get_focus_indicator_style :: proc(theme: ^parser.Theme_Data) -> Focus_Indicator_Style {
	// Default style
	return Focus_Indicator_Style{}
}

// get_reduced_motion_enabled checks if reduced motion is enabled
get_reduced_motion_enabled :: proc(theme: ^parser.Theme_Data) -> bool {
	if "reduced_motion" not_in theme.accessibility {
		return false
	}
	
	rm_map := theme.accessibility["reduced_motion"]
	if "enabled" not_in rm_map {
		return false
	}
	
	return rm_map["enabled"] == "true" || rm_map["enabled"] == "1"
}

// get_minimum_touch_target_size returns the minimum touch target size for accessibility
get_minimum_touch_target_size :: proc(theme: ^parser.Theme_Data) -> f32 {
	// Default minimum touch target size (WCAG recommendation: 44x44 pixels)
	default_min_target := f32(44.0)
	
	if "high_contrast" not_in theme.accessibility {
		return default_min_target
	}
	
	hc_map := theme.accessibility["high_contrast"]
	if "minimum_touch_target" not_in hc_map {
		return default_min_target
	}
	
	min_target_str := hc_map["minimum_touch_target"]
	if num, ok := strconv.parse_f32(min_target_str); ok {
		return num
	}
	
	return default_min_target
}

// get_accessibility_colors returns a map of accessibility color overrides
// This function extracts the colors from the high contrast accessibility settings.
//
// Based on the current parser implementation, colors are stored with dot notation:
//   theme.accessibility["high_contrast"]["colors.text"] = "#000000"
//   theme.accessibility["high_contrast"]["colors.background"] = "#FFFFFF"
//
// Returns a map of color keys to color values.
get_accessibility_colors :: proc(theme: ^parser.Theme_Data) -> map[string]string {
	colors_map := make(map[string]string)
	
	if "high_contrast" not_in theme.accessibility {
		return colors_map
	}
	
	hc_map := theme.accessibility["high_contrast"]
	
	// Look for all keys that start with "colors."
	for key, value in hc_map {
		if strings.has_prefix(key, "colors.") {
			// Extract the color name (remove "colors." prefix)
			color_name := key[len("colors."):]
			if color_name != "" && value != "" {
				colors_map[color_name] = value
			}
		}
	}
	
	return colors_map
}

// TEMPORARILY DISABLED - Type safety issues with append

// Types of warnings collected:

collect_accessibility_warnings :: proc(theme: ^parser.Theme_Data) -> []string {
	// TODO: Implement comprehensive warning collection
	// Current implementation returns empty array - functionality will be completed in future phase
	return []string{}
}

// Validation logic for RuiShin theme system.
// Ensures theme integrity by checking token references and component existence.

import "core:fmt"
import "core:strings"

// validate_theme performs comprehensive integrity checks on theme data.
// Returns (true, "") if valid, (false, error_message) otherwise.
validate_theme :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data) -> (bool, string) {
	// 1. Validate Token References in Components
	for comp_name, comp_def in theme.components {
		// Check Base Styles
		for prop_name, prop_val in comp_def.base {
			if ok, err := check_interpolation_refs(theme, prop_val); !ok {
				return false, fmt.aprintf(
					"Component '%s' base property '%s' has invalid reference: %s",
					comp_name,
					prop_name,
					err,
				)
			}
		}

		// Check State Overrides
		for state_name, state_props in comp_def.states {
			for prop_name, prop_val in state_props {
				if ok, err := check_interpolation_refs(theme, prop_val); !ok {
					return false, fmt.aprintf(
						"Component '%s' state '%s' property '%s' has invalid reference: %s",
						comp_name,
						state_name,
						prop_name,
						err,
					)
				}
			}
		}
	}

	// 2. Validate Platform Overrides
	for plat_name, plat_over in theme.overrides.platforms {
		for comp_name, comp_props in plat_over.components {
			// Check if component exists in base definitions
			if comp_name not_in theme.components {
				return false, fmt.aprintf(
					"Platform '%s' overrides non-existent component '%s'",
					plat_name,
					comp_name,
				)
			}

			// Check properties
			for prop_name, prop_val in comp_props {
				if ok, err := check_interpolation_refs(theme, prop_val); !ok {
					return false, fmt.aprintf(
						"Platform '%s' component '%s' property '%s' has invalid reference: %s",
						plat_name,
						comp_name,
						prop_name,
						err,
					)
				}
			}
		}
	}

	// 3. Validate Commands (Phase 10B.4.b)
	if ok, err := validate_commands(theme); !ok {
		return false, err
	}

	return true, ""
}

// check_interpolation_refs scans a string for {tokens.xxx.yyy} and verifies they exist.
@(private)
check_interpolation_refs :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data, input: string) -> (bool, string) {
	res := input
	for {
		start := strings.index(res, "{")
		if start == -1 do break
		end := strings.index(res[start:], "}")
		if end == -1 do break
		end += start

		ref := res[start + 1:end]
		if _, ok := resolve_token_ref(theme, ref); !ok {
			return false, ref
		}

		res = res[end + 1:]
	}
	return true, ""
}

// validate_commands performs comprehensive validation of keyboard command definitions.
// Validates shortcut syntax, labels, duplicate shortcuts, and platform overrides.
// Returns (true, "") if valid, (false, error_message) otherwise.
//
// Phase 10B.4.b: Command Validation - Validate keyboard shortcut definitions
//
// Validation Rules:
//   - Shortcut must be parseable by parse_shortcut()
//   - Label must be present and non-empty
//   - No duplicate (keycode, modifiers) combinations
//   - Platform overrides must reference valid base commands
validate_commands :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data) -> (bool, string) {
	// 1. Validate base commands
	if ok, err := validate_base_commands(theme); !ok {
		return false, err
	}

	// 2. Validate platform command overrides
	if ok, err := validate_platform_command_overrides(theme); !ok {
		return false, err
	}

	// 3. Check for duplicate shortcuts across all commands
	if ok, err := check_duplicate_shortcuts(theme); !ok {
		return false, err
	}

	return true, ""
}

// validate_base_commands validates all base command definitions.
// Checks shortcut syntax and labels for each command.
@(private)
validate_base_commands :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data) -> (bool, string) {
	for cmd_name, cmd_def in theme.commands {
		// Validate shortcut syntax
		if ok, err := validate_shortcut_syntax(cmd_def.shortcut); !ok {
			return false, fmt.aprintf(
				"Command '%s' has invalid shortcut: %s\nValue: \"%s\"\nExpected: \"modifier+key\" (e.g., \"ctrl+s\", \"cmd+z\", \"f11\")\nSuggestion: Use valid modifier (ctrl, cmd, alt, shift) and key (a-z, 0-9, f1-f12, plus, minus, etc.)",
				cmd_name,
				err,
				cmd_def.shortcut,
			)
		}

		// Validate label
		if ok, err := validate_command_label(cmd_def.label); !ok {
			return false, fmt.aprintf(
				"Command '%s' has invalid label: %s\nValue: \"%s\"\nExpected: Non-empty display string",
				cmd_name,
				err,
				cmd_def.label,
			)
		}
	}

	return true, ""
}

// validate_shortcut_syntax validates that a shortcut string can be parsed.
// Returns (true, "") if valid, (false, error_message) otherwise.
@(private)
validate_shortcut_syntax :: proc(shortcut: string) -> (bool, string) {
	// Empty shortcut is allowed (command without keyboard binding)
	if len(shortcut) == 0 {
		return true, ""
	}

	// Try to parse the shortcut
	binding, ok := parse_shortcut(shortcut)
	if !ok {
		return false, "Invalid shortcut format"
	}

	// Check if keycode is valid (not INVALID)
	if binding.keycode == .Invalid {
		return false, "Invalid key name"
	}

	return true, ""
}

// validate_command_label validates that a command label is present and non-empty.
// Returns (true, "") if valid, (false, error_message) otherwise.
@(private)
validate_command_label :: proc(label: string) -> (bool, string) {
	// Empty label is allowed (command without display label)
	if len(label) == 0 {
		return true, ""
	}

	// Label should be a reasonable display string
	// Check for obviously invalid labels (e.g., only whitespace)
	trimmed := strings.trim_space(label)
	if len(trimmed) == 0 {
		return false, "Label is empty or whitespace only"
	}

	return true, ""
}

// check_duplicate_shortcuts detects duplicate (keycode, modifiers) combinations.
// Returns (true, "") if no duplicates, (false, error_message) otherwise.
//
// Checks across:
//   - Base commands
//   - Platform-specific overrides (within each platform)
@(private)
check_duplicate_shortcuts :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data) -> (bool, string) {
	// Track seen shortcuts: map of "keycode:modifiers" to command name
	seen_shortcuts := make(map[string]string)
	defer delete(seen_shortcuts)

	// Check base commands
	for cmd_name, cmd_def in theme.commands {
		if len(cmd_def.shortcut) == 0 {
			continue // Skip commands without shortcuts
		}

		binding, ok := parse_shortcut(cmd_def.shortcut)
		if !ok {
			continue // Already validated in validate_base_commands
		}

		// Create unique key for this shortcut
		shortcut_key := fmt.tprintf("%d:%d", binding.keycode, binding.modifiers)

		// Check for duplicate
		if existing_cmd, exists := seen_shortcuts[shortcut_key]; exists {
			return false, fmt.aprintf(
				"Duplicate shortcut detected\nCommand '%s' uses same shortcut as '%s'\nShortcut: \"%s\" (keycode=%d, modifiers=%d)\nSuggestion: Change one of the shortcuts to avoid conflict",
				cmd_name,
				existing_cmd,
				cmd_def.shortcut,
				binding.keycode,
				binding.modifiers,
			)
		}

		seen_shortcuts[shortcut_key] = cmd_name
	}

	// Check platform-specific overrides
	for plat_name, plat_over in theme.overrides.platforms {
		// Clear seen shortcuts for this platform (platform overrides can duplicate base commands)
		for k in seen_shortcuts {
			delete(k)
		}

		for cmd_name, cmd_def in plat_over.commands {
			if len(cmd_def.shortcut) == 0 {
				continue // Skip commands without shortcuts
			}

			binding, ok := parse_shortcut(cmd_def.shortcut)
			if !ok {
				continue // Already validated in validate_platform_command_overrides
			}

			// Create unique key for this shortcut
			shortcut_key := fmt.tprintf("%d:%d", binding.keycode, binding.modifiers)

			// Check for duplicate within this platform
			if existing_cmd, exists := seen_shortcuts[shortcut_key]; exists {
				return false, fmt.aprintf(
					"Duplicate shortcut detected in platform '%s'\nCommand '%s' uses same shortcut as '%s'\nShortcut: \"%s\" (keycode=%d, modifiers=%d)\nSuggestion: Change one of the shortcuts to avoid conflict",
					plat_name,
					cmd_name,
					existing_cmd,
					cmd_def.shortcut,
					binding.keycode,
					binding.modifiers,
				)
			}

			seen_shortcuts[shortcut_key] = cmd_name
		}
	}

	return true, ""
}

// validate_platform_command_overrides validates platform-specific command overrides.
// Checks that platform overrides reference valid base commands and have valid shortcuts.
// Returns (true, "") if valid, (false, error_message) otherwise.
@(private)
validate_platform_command_overrides :: proc(theme: ^Theme_Data) -> (bool, string) {
	for plat_name, plat_over in theme.overrides.platforms {
		for cmd_name, cmd_def in plat_over.commands {
			// Check if command exists in base definitions
			if cmd_name not_in theme.commands {
				return false, fmt.aprintf(
					"Platform '%s' overrides non-existent command '%s'\nSuggestion: Define the command in the base 'commands' section first",
					plat_name,
					cmd_name,
				)
			}

			// Validate shortcut syntax
			if ok, err := validate_shortcut_syntax(cmd_def.shortcut); !ok {
				return false, fmt.aprintf(
					"Platform '%s' command '%s' has invalid shortcut: %s\nValue: \"%s\"\nExpected: \"modifier+key\" (e.g., \"ctrl+s\", \"cmd+z\", \"f11\")\nSuggestion: Use valid modifier (ctrl, cmd, alt, shift) and key (a-z, 0-9, f1-f12, plus, minus, etc.)",
					plat_name,
					cmd_name,
					err,
					cmd_def.shortcut,
				)
			}
		}
	}

	return true, ""
}
