// Component style structs and resolution for RuiShin theme system.
// Provides typed structures for component styling to be consumed by UI rendering logic.

import "core:strings"
import assets "project:assets"

// Button_Style holds parameters for drawing a themed button.
Button_Style :: struct {
	background_color: assets.Color,
	foreground_color: assets.Color,
	border_radius:    f32,
	font_size:        f32,
	border_color:     assets.Color,
	border_width:     f32,
	opacity:          f32,
	scale:            f32,
}

// TextField_Style holds parameters for drawing a themed text input field.
TextField_Style :: struct {
	background_color: assets.Color,
	foreground_color: assets.Color,
	border_radius:    f32,
	font_size:        f32,
	border_color:     assets.Color,
	border_width:     f32,
}

// Checkbox_Style holds parameters for drawing a themed checkbox.
Checkbox_Style :: struct {
	size:             f32,
	border_radius:    f32,
	border_width:     f32,
	border_color:     assets.Color,
	background_color: assets.Color,
	check_color:      assets.Color,
}

// Modal_Style holds parameters for drawing a themed modal dialog.
Modal_Style :: struct {
	background_color: assets.Color,
	border_radius:    f32,
	padding:          f32,
	shadow_color:     assets.Color,
	max_width:        f32,
	overlay_color:    assets.Color,
	backdrop_blur:    f32,
}

// Card_Style holds parameters for drawing a themed card or container.
Card_Style :: struct {
	background_color: assets.Color,
	border_radius:    f32,
	padding:          f32,
	border_color:     assets.Color,
	border_width:     f32,
	shadow_color:     assets.Color,
}

// Tooltip_Style holds parameters for drawing a themed tooltip.
Tooltip_Style :: struct {
	background_color: assets.Color,
	foreground_color: assets.Color,
	border_radius:    f32,
	padding:          [2]f32,
	font_size:        f32,
	max_width:        f32,
}

// ui_resolve_button_style converts a property map into a typed Button_Style.
ui_resolve_button_style :: proc(style_map: map[string]Type_Property) -> Button_Style {
	style := Button_Style{}

	style.background_color = ui_get_color(style_map, "backgroundColor", {0.2, 0.4, 0.8, 1.0})
	style.foreground_color = ui_get_color(style_map, "foregroundColor", {1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0})
	style.border_radius = ui_get_f32(style_map, "borderRadius", 4.0)
	style.font_size = ui_get_f32(style_map, "fontSize", 14.0)
	style.border_color = ui_get_color(style_map, "borderColor", {0, 0, 0, 0})
	style.border_width = ui_get_f32(style_map, "borderWidth", 0.0)
	style.opacity = ui_get_f32(style_map, "opacity", 1.0)
	style.scale = ui_get_f32(style_map, "scale", 1.0)

	return style
}

// ui_resolve_textfield_style converts a property map into a typed TextField_Style.
ui_resolve_textfield_style :: proc(style_map: map[string]Type_Property) -> TextField_Style {
	style := TextField_Style{}

	style.background_color = ui_get_color(style_map, "backgroundColor", {1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0})
	style.foreground_color = ui_get_color(style_map, "foregroundColor", {0.1, 0.1, 0.1, 1.0})
	style.border_radius = ui_get_f32(style_map, "borderRadius", 4.0)
	style.font_size = ui_get_f32(style_map, "fontSize", 14.0)
	style.border_color = ui_get_color(style_map, "borderColor", {0.8, 0.8, 0.8, 1.0})
	style.border_width = ui_get_f32(style_map, "borderWidth", 1.0)

	return style
}

// ui_resolve_checkbox_style converts a property map into a typed Checkbox_Style.
ui_resolve_checkbox_style :: proc(style_map: map[string]Type_Property) -> Checkbox_Style {
	style := Checkbox_Style{}

	style.size = ui_get_f32(style_map, "size", 18.0)
	style.border_radius = ui_get_f32(style_map, "borderRadius", 2.0)
	style.border_width = ui_get_f32(style_map, "border", 2.0)
	style.border_color = ui_get_color(style_map, "borderColor", {0.8, 0.8, 0.8, 1.0})
	style.background_color = ui_get_color(style_map, "backgroundColor", {1, 1, 1, 1})
	style.check_color = ui_get_color(style_map, "checkColor", {0.2, 0.4, 0.8, 1.0})

	return style
}

// ui_resolve_modal_style converts property maps into a typed Modal_Style.
ui_resolve_modal_style :: proc(
	base_map: map[string]Type_Property,
	overlay_map: map[string]Type_Property,
) -> Modal_Style {
	style := Modal_Style{}

	style.background_color = ui_get_color(base_map, "backgroundColor", {1, 1, 1, 1})
	style.border_radius = ui_get_f32(base_map, "borderRadius", 8.0)
	style.padding = ui_get_f32(base_map, "padding", 24.0)
	style.max_width = ui_get_f32(base_map, "maxWidth", 600.0)

	style.overlay_color = ui_get_color(overlay_map, "backgroundColor", {0, 0, 0, 0.5})
	style.backdrop_blur = ui_get_f32(overlay_map, "backdropBlur", 4.0)

	return style
}

// ui_resolve_card_style converts a property map into a typed Card_Style.
ui_resolve_card_style :: proc(style_map: map[string]Type_Property) -> Card_Style {
	style := Card_Style{}

	style.background_color = ui_get_color(style_map, "backgroundColor", {1, 1, 1, 1})
	style.border_radius = ui_get_f32(style_map, "borderRadius", 8.0)
	style.padding = ui_get_f32(style_map, "padding", 16.0)
	style.border_color = ui_get_color(style_map, "border", {1, 1, 1, 0.1})

	return style
}

package main

import "base:runtime"
import "core:fmt"
import "core:math"
import "core:math/linalg"
import "core:math/rand"
import "core:mem"

import "core:encoding/json"
import "core:strings"
import logging "logging"
import vfs "vfs"

import config "configpkg"
import engine "engine"
import platform "project:platform"
// g2d imported directly for experimental features: sprite animation, tweens,
// gesture recognition, material/UV drawing. These are demo-app internal.

import assets "assets" // Now imports from flattened graphics package
import g2d "graphics"
import rsd "graphics"
import layout "layout"
import renderer "project:renderer"

// ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// main.odin — App Entry, Infrastructure, Demo Lifecycle
//
// This file is the heart of the demo app. It contains:
//   - Package-level constants, types, and global variables
//   - Tile system infrastructure (Tile_Node, tile_render, create_tile, etc.)
//   - Demo lifecycle callbacks: demo_init, demo_event, demo_scene,
//     demo_post_flush, demo_draw, demo_cleanup, main()
//   - Font utilities: refresh_fonts, ensure_script_fonts
//
// Tile render procs (render_tile_*) live in demo_tiles.odin.
// STRESS_2D burn mode (stress types + render_stress_* procs) live in demo_stress.odin.
// All three files share package main — no package boundary between them.
// ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

// --- Configuration & Constants ---
MIN_WIDTH :: 800
MIN_HEIGHT :: 600
STATUS_BAR_H :: 24.0
STRESS_TEST :: #config(STRESS_TEST, false) // Enable all debug/perf logging at once
STRESS_2D   :: #config(STRESS_2D,   false) // 2D stress mode — replaces normal demo with 6 GPU burn tiles
GRID_COLS :: 24
GRID_ROWS :: 32

// Stress tile load — overridable at compile time: -define:STRESS_N_SHADOW=200
STRESS_N_SHADOW   :: #config(STRESS_N_SHADOW,  120)
STRESS_N_STENCIL  :: #config(STRESS_N_STENCIL, 250)
STRESS_N_TEXT     :: #config(STRESS_N_TEXT,    300)
STRESS_N_FILL     :: #config(STRESS_N_FILL,   2000)
STRESS_N_PARTICLE :: #config(STRESS_N_PARTICLE, 800)
STRESS_N_TWEEN    :: #config(STRESS_N_TWEEN,    60)
STRESS_N_ORBS     :: #config(STRESS_N_ORBS,    150)  // S7: glow orbs (×3 circles each)
STRESS_N_VORTEX   :: #config(STRESS_N_VORTEX, 1200)  // S8: black hole spiral particles
STRESS_N_TOASTER  :: #config(STRESS_N_TOASTER,  40)  // S9: flying toasters


// --- Constants ---
// Color Palette
// Unified Color Palette
COLOR_BG :: [4]f32{0.30, 0.30, 0.34, 1.0} // Lighter mid-grey (needed for shadow visibility)
COLOR_BAR :: [4]f32{0.22, 0.22, 0.26, 1.0} // Status/nav bar (kept darker than BG)
COLOR_DIVIDER :: [4]f32{0.18, 0.18, 0.22, 1.0} // Same as bar (user request)
COLOR_TEXT :: [4]f32{0.90, 0.90, 0.95, 1.0} // Soft white


// Layout state calculated per-frame
Layout_State :: struct {
	width, height: f32, // Logical dimensions
	content_y:     f32, // Top of content area (below status bar)
}

global_layout: Layout_State

// --- Shared Resources ---
// active_renderer is now accessed via renderer.active_renderer
test_img: renderer.Image_Handle
lava_lamp_img: renderer.Image_Handle
now: f32 = 0
prev_dt: f32 = 0
last_rss: i64 = 0
current_dpi: f32 = 1.0 // Phase 22: Initialize to 1.0 to prevent 0-sized texture creation in demo_init

// Font handles (opaque — use renderer.Font_Handle, not ^Font directly)
global_font_small: renderer.Font_Handle
global_font_normal: renderer.Font_Handle
global_font_large: renderer.Font_Handle
global_font_arabic: renderer.Font_Handle
global_font_hebrew: renderer.Font_Handle
global_font_arabic_small: renderer.Font_Handle
global_font_hebrew_small: renderer.Font_Handle
global_font_cjk: renderer.Font_Handle
global_font_cjk_small: renderer.Font_Handle

// Lazy script font state — Arabic, Hebrew, CJK are not loaded at startup
script_fonts_loaded: bool
script_fonts_dpi:    f32

// Scene Graph
current_scene: ^engine.Scene

// Single content node that owns all tiles on the unified 24×32 grid.
Content_Node :: struct {
	using node: engine.Node,
}

Tile_Node :: struct {
	using node: engine.Node,
	gx, gy:    int, // Grid position (top-left corner) on the 24×32 grid
	gw, gh:    int, // Grid size in cells
	title:     string,
	render_fn: proc(node: ^Tile_Node, r: ^renderer.Renderer, x, y, w, h: f32),
}

tile_render :: proc(node: ^engine.Node, ctx: rawptr) {
	t := cast(^Tile_Node)node
	r := cast(^renderer.Renderer)ctx

	// Shadow must draw first so the card face renders on top and covers the shadow center.
	// drop-shadow commands now use .Content layer — batch order within Content is push order,
	// so shadow (pushed first) renders before card face (pushed second).
	tile_render_shadow(node, ctx)

	// Card face (covers center of shadow, corners stay rounded)
	bg := COLOR_BAR
	r.fillRectRounded(0, 0, node.bounds.w, node.bounds.h, 12, renderer.paint_solid(bg))
	// Subtle bright rim so the card "pops" against the background
	r.drawRectRounded(0, 0, node.bounds.w, node.bounds.h, 12, 1, {1, 1, 1, 0.18})

	// Tile Number and Title
	label := t.title

	r.drawText(10, 12, label, global_font_small, COLOR_TEXT)

	// Local content area
	if t.render_fn != nil {
		pad :: 5.0
		// Tight clipping for content area
		r.pushClip(pad, pad + 15, node.bounds.w - 2 * pad, node.bounds.h - 2 * pad - 15)

		t.render_fn(t, r, pad, pad + 15, node.bounds.w - 2 * pad, node.bounds.h - 2 * pad - 15)
		r.popClip()
	}
} //end of tile_render

tile_render_shadow :: proc(node: ^engine.Node, ctx: rawptr) {
	r := cast(^renderer.Renderer)ctx
	// nodeRender sets a tight clip to tile bounds before calling vtable.render.
	// Shadow must bleed outside tile bounds, so reset clip first.
	r.resetClip()
	r.dropShadowRect(
		0,
		0,
		node.bounds.w,
		node.bounds.h,
		12, // corner radius
		6,
		9, // offset_x, offset_y
		16, // blur
		{0, 0, 0, 0.65},
	)
	// Restore tile clip for anything rendered after shadow.
	r.setClip(0, 0, node.bounds.w, node.bounds.h)
}

tile_update :: proc(node: ^engine.Node, dt: f32) {
	// The engine's nodeRender procedure handles automatic transformation propagation
	// and recursive rendering of children. We only need to draw the quadrant's
	// own background and lines here.
}

tile_vtable := engine.Node_VTable {
	render        = tile_render,
	render_shadow = tile_render_shadow,
	update        = tile_update,
}

demo_star_node_ptr: ^Demo_Star_Node
demo_transformer: ^engine.Transformer

Demo_Star_Node :: struct {
	using node: engine.Node,
	num_points: int,
	color:      [4]f32,
}

demo_star_render :: proc(node: ^engine.Node, ctx: rawptr) {
	t := cast(^Demo_Star_Node)node
	r := cast(^renderer.Renderer)ctx

	// renderer already has the local transform applied via node_render recursive calls
	final_color := t.color

	// Hit test in screen space for hover effect
	sb_input := cast(^platform.Input_State)r.getInputState()
	if engine.nodeHitTest(node, sb_input.mouse_pos.x, sb_input.mouse_pos.y) == node {
		final_color = {1, 1, 1, 1}
	}

	r.fillStar(0, 0, t.num_points, 14, 35, renderer.paint_solid(final_color))
}

demo_star_update :: proc(node: ^engine.Node, dt: f32) {}

demo_star_vtable := engine.Node_VTable {
	render = demo_star_render,
	update = demo_star_update,
}

// SVG/RSD Tiger asset state
tiger_svg_render_handle_new: assets.RenderHandle
tiger_rsd_render_handle_new: assets.RenderHandle
tiger_svg_loading_handle: assets.LoadingHandle
tiger_rsd_loading_handle: assets.LoadingHandle

// Offscreen rendering configuration and state
OFFSCREEN_RENDERING_ENABLED :: true // Set to false to disable offscreen rendering entirely
BLUR_TEST_ENABLED :: true // Set to false to disable blur test

tiger_offscreen_rt: renderer.Render_Target
tiger_offscreen_rt_initialized: bool = false
tiger_offscreen_rt_dirty: bool = true // true = needs re-render
tiger_needs_blur: bool = false        // Set in demo_scene, consumed in demo_post_flush

// Gaussian blur ping-pong temporary resources
tiger_blur_temp_rt: renderer.Render_Target
tiger_blur_temp_rt_initialized: bool = false
tiger_blur_temp_rt2: renderer.Render_Target
tiger_blur_temp_rt2_initialized: bool = false

// --- 1. Top Bar Node ---
Star :: struct {
	x, y, z: f32,
}

Points_Tile_Node :: struct {
	using node: engine.Node,
	stars:      [1000]Star,
}

points_tile_render :: proc(node: ^engine.Node, ctx: rawptr) {
	snode := cast(^Points_Tile_Node)node
	r := cast(^renderer.Renderer)ctx

	// Use parent's bounds because children bounds might not be updated by layout
	if snode.parent == nil do return
	w, h := snode.parent.bounds.w, snode.parent.bounds.h
	hw, hh := w * 0.5, h * 0.5

	for &s in snode.stars {
		if s.z <= 0.01 do continue

		// Perspective projection
		depth_factor := 200.0 / s.z
		sx := hw + s.x * depth_factor
		sy := hh + s.y * depth_factor

		// Bounds check
		if sx >= 0 && sx < w && sy >= 0 && sy < h {
			// Non-linear brightness mapping (SHALLOWER curve for deeper space)
			t := 1.0 - (s.z / 1000.0)
			bright := math.pow(t, 0.20) // Much shallower falloff
			bright = math.clamp(bright, 0.2, 1.0) // Higher floor for distant stars

			// Color Ramp: Muted gold -> Pure White
			col_far := [4]f32{0.7, 0.6, 0.3, 1.0}
			col_near := [4]f32{1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0}
			col := linalg.lerp(col_far, col_near, t)
			col *= bright
			col.a = 1.0

			r.drawPoint(sx, sy, col)
		}
	}
}

points_tile_update :: proc(node: ^engine.Node, dt: f32) {
	snode := cast(^Points_Tile_Node)node
	speed: f32 = 180.0 * dt

	for &s in snode.stars {
		s.z -= speed
		if s.z <= 0 {
			s.z = 1000.0
			s.x = (rand.float32() - 0.5) * 1000.0
			s.y = (rand.float32() - 0.5) * 1000.0
		}
	}
}

points_tile_vtable := engine.Node_VTable {
	render = points_tile_render,
	update = points_tile_update,
}

render_tile_points :: proc(node: ^Tile_Node, r: ^renderer.Renderer, x, y, w, h: f32) {
	// Points are drawn by Points_Tile_Node (child)
}
top_bar_render :: proc(node: ^engine.Node, ctx: rawptr) {
	r := cast(^renderer.Renderer)ctx
	// Background
	r.fillRectSimple(0, 0, global_layout.width, STATUS_BAR_H, COLOR_BAR)
	// Divider line at the bottom
	r.drawLine(0, STATUS_BAR_H - 1, global_layout.width, STATUS_BAR_H - 1, 1, COLOR_DIVIDER)

	// Status text (Mouse, FPS)
	sb_input := cast(^platform.Input_State)r.getInputState()
	fps := 1.0 / math.max(f32(engine.frame_duration()), 0.001)
	status := fmt.tprintf(
		"Mouse: %.1f, %.1f | FPS: %.1f",
		sb_input.mouse_pos.x,
		sb_input.mouse_pos.y,
		fps,
	)
	r.drawText(10, 14, status, global_font_small, COLOR_TEXT)
}

top_bar_vtable := engine.Node_VTable {
	render = top_bar_render,
}


// --- 3. Quadrant Node ---
// --- Node Creation Helpers ---
create_content_node :: proc() -> ^Content_Node {
	c := new(Content_Node)
	c.name = "Content"
	c.vtable = &content_vtable
	c.children = make([dynamic]^engine.Node)
	c.is_visible = true
	c.is_active = true
	c.transform = 1
	return c
}

create_tile :: proc(
	name: string,
	gx, gy, gw, gh: int,
	title: string,
	render_fn: proc(node: ^Tile_Node, r: ^renderer.Renderer, x, y, w, h: f32),
) -> ^Tile_Node {
	t := new(Tile_Node)
	t.name = name
	t.gx = gx
	t.gy = gy
	t.gw = gw
	t.gh = gh
	t.title = title
	t.render_fn = render_fn
	t.vtable = &tile_vtable
	t.children = make([dynamic]^engine.Node)
	t.is_visible = true
	t.is_active = true
	t.transform = 1
	t.use_clipping = true
	return t
}

// Positions all tiles from the unified 24×32 grid every frame.
content_update :: proc(node: ^engine.Node, dt: f32) {
	spacing: f32 = 10.0
	unit_w := (node.bounds.w - spacing) / f32(GRID_COLS)
	unit_h := (node.bounds.h - spacing) / f32(GRID_ROWS)

	for child in node.children {
		t := cast(^Tile_Node)child
		tx := f32(t.gx) * unit_w + spacing * 0.5
		ty := f32(t.gy) * unit_h + spacing * 0.5
		tw := f32(t.gw) * unit_w - spacing
		th := f32(t.gh) * unit_h - spacing
		child.transform = linalg.matrix4_translate_f32({tx, ty, 0})
		child.bounds = {0, 0, tw, th}
		child.clipping_bounds = child.bounds
	}
}

content_vtable := engine.Node_VTable {
	update = content_update,
}

@(private)
profiler_allocator: mem.Tracking_Allocator
// Memory tracking state — only used when MEMORY_TRACKING is enabled
@(private)
mem_track_prev_count: int

// --- Demo animation and interaction state ---
@(private)
Demo_MM_State :: struct {
	inited:     bool,
	anim_img:   renderer.Image_Handle,
	sprite:     g2d.G2D_Sprite_Anim,
	hover_tw:   g2d.Tween_V2,
	hover_prev: bool,
	hover_curr: [2]f32,
	rot_tw:     g2d.Tween_F32,
	tl_anim:    g2d.Timeline,
	tl_bars:    [3]f32,
	bl4_gs:     g2d.Gesture_Set,
	bl4_rect:   [4]f32,
	bl4_color:  [4]f32,
	last_ie:    platform.Input_Event,
	emitter:    g2d.Particle_Emitter, // simple system — left fire
	fx_fire:    g2d.FX_Emitter,       // complex system — right fire
	fx_embers:  g2d.FX_Emitter,       // drifting embers above the complex fire
	postfx_rt1: renderer.Render_Target,
	postfx_rt2: renderer.Render_Target,
	postfx_rt3: renderer.Render_Target,
	masking_rt:  renderer.Render_Target, // Pre-rendered content for stencil masking

	// Tile 29: Render Recording demo
	rec29_mode:         enum u8 {Idle, Recording, Playing},
	rec29_frame:        int,
	rec29_elapsed:      f32,   // ms since recording started
	rec29_play_elapsed: f32,   // ms since playback started (reset after 3s)
	rec29_data:         []u8,  // recording loaded once on entering .Playing; freed on reset

	// Tile 32: GPU Callback demo
	cb32_fire_count: int,  // incremented by the GPU callback each frame

	// Tile 30: Tweens & Springs demo
	tw30_ease:       [5]g2d.Tween_F32,  // easing race — one tween per row
	tw30_cycle_ms:   f32,               // restart cycle timer
	tw30_sp:         [3]g2d.Spring_F32, // springs: underdamped, critical, overdamped
	tw30_sp_tgt:     f32,               // spring target (0 or 1) — set after jump delay
	tw30_sp_tgt_disp: f32,              // target LINE position — flips at cycle start
	tw30_sp_jumped:  bool,              // whether springs were kicked this cycle
	tw30_sp_ms:      f32,               // timer within current flip cycle
}
@(private)
demo_mm: Demo_MM_State

// Settings state
App_Settings :: struct {
	window_title: string,
	clear_color:  [4]f32,
	width:        i32,
	height:       i32,
	use_msaa:     bool,
}

global_settings: App_Settings

load_settings :: proc() {
	data, ok := vfs.loadAsset("settings.json", .Settings)
	if !ok {
		global_settings = {
			window_title = "RuiShin Alpha",
			clear_color  = {0.30, 0.30, 0.34, 1.0}, // Matches COLOR_BG
			width        = 1024,
			height       = 900,
			use_msaa     = false,
		}
		return
	}
	defer delete(data)

	err := json.unmarshal(data, &global_settings)
	if err != nil {
		logging.error(.Config, fmt.tprintf("Error parsing settings.json: %v", err))
	}
}


// ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// DEMO LIFECYCLE — resources, state, init/event/scene/cleanup
// ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

demo_init :: proc() {
	// Tracking allocator is native-only: on WASM the emscripten allocator
	// (set in main) is used through the context instead.
	when ODIN_ARCH != .wasm32 && ODIN_ARCH != .wasm64p32 {
		mem.tracking_allocator_init(&profiler_allocator, runtime.default_allocator())
		context.allocator = mem.tracking_allocator(&profiler_allocator)
	}

	// Initialize new logging system
	logging.init_default() //this is the .minimlal setup, we can customize it later with profiles and subsystems

	// Enable RSD/SVG logging for our specific diagnostics
	when logging.DEBUG_RSD {
		logging.enable_subsystem(.RSD, .DEBUG)
	}
	when logging.DEBUG_SVG {
		logging.enable_subsystem(.SVG, .DEBUG)
	}

	// Initialize core math/rand seed (once only)
	rand.reset(12345)

	// Note: Sokol GFX, g2d, renderer, and asset manager are set up
	// by engine/app_sokol.odin before this callback runs.

	// 2d. Load Images
	test_img = renderer.active_renderer.loadImage("picture.jpg")
	lava_lamp_img = renderer.active_renderer.loadImage("lava-lamp.jpg")
	if lava_lamp_img.id == 0 {
		logging.info(.IO, "Warning: lava-lamp.jpg failed to load.")
	}
	when STRESS_2D {
		stress_toast_img   = renderer.active_renderer.loadImage("toast.png")
		stress_toaster_img = renderer.active_renderer.loadImage("flyingtoaster.png")
	}

	// 2d. Init Materials & Motion Demo State
	demo_mm.anim_img = renderer.active_renderer.loadImage("animation.jpg")
	if demo_mm.anim_img.id != 0 {
		// animation.jpg is a 4x4 sprite sheet; tiles are ~100x100 (content centered)
		demo_mm.sprite = renderer.active_renderer.spriteMake(demo_mm.anim_img, 100, 100, 0, 15, 6, true)
	}
	demo_mm.rot_tw = g2d.tweenF32Make(0, math.PI * 2, 4000.0, g2d.easeLinear)
	demo_mm.bl4_color = {0.8, 0.2, 0.2, 1.0} // defaults to red
	
	// Timeline Sequencer Init
	demo_mm.tl_anim = g2d.g2d_timeline_make(true)
	g2d.g2d_timeline_add_action_f32(&demo_mm.tl_anim, &demo_mm.tl_bars[0], 0,    500, 0, 1, g2d.g2d_ease_out_back)
	g2d.g2d_timeline_add_action_f32(&demo_mm.tl_anim, &demo_mm.tl_bars[1], 200,  500, 0, 1, g2d.g2d_ease_out_back)
	g2d.g2d_timeline_add_action_f32(&demo_mm.tl_anim, &demo_mm.tl_bars[2], 400,  500, 0, 1, g2d.g2d_ease_out_back)
	g2d.g2d_timeline_add_action_f32(&demo_mm.tl_anim, &demo_mm.tl_bars[0], 1500, 500, 1, 0, g2d.g2d_ease_in_back)
	g2d.g2d_timeline_add_action_f32(&demo_mm.tl_anim, &demo_mm.tl_bars[1], 1700, 500, 1, 0, g2d.g2d_ease_in_back)
	g2d.g2d_timeline_add_action_f32(&demo_mm.tl_anim, &demo_mm.tl_bars[2], 1900, 500, 1, 0, g2d.g2d_ease_in_back)
	// Padding at end: action that does nothing just sets the maximum duration.
	g2d.g2d_timeline_add_action_f32(&demo_mm.tl_anim, &demo_mm.tl_bars[2], 2600, 1,   0, 0, nil)

	// Particle System Init
	emitter_config := g2d.Emitter_Config {
		max_particles = 400,
		spawn_rate    = 90,
		life_min      = 600,
		life_max      = 2200,
		pos_spread    = {14, 0},
		vel_min       = {-45, -210},
		vel_max       = {45,  -90},
		gravity       = {0, 160},
		size_start    = 12,
		size_end      = 0,
		color_start   = {1.0, 0.85, 0.2, 1.0},
		color_end     = {1.0, 0.15, 0.0, 0.0},
		angle_start   = 0,
		ang_vel_min   = -1.2,
		ang_vel_max   = 1.2,
	}
	demo_mm.emitter = g2d.emitterMake(emitter_config)

	// FX fire — complex system: Cone spawn, drag, additive blending
	demo_mm.fx_fire = g2d.g2d_fx_make(g2d.FX_Config{
		max_particles  = 600,
		spawn_rate     = 110,
		life_min       = 700,
		life_max       = 2600,
		spawn_shape    = .Cone,
		dir_angle      = -math.PI / 2,
		dir_spread     = 0.40,
		speed_min      = 110,
		speed_max      = 260,
		gravity        = {0, 160},
		drag_min       = 5,
		drag_max       = 12,
		size_start     = 16,
		size_end       = 0,
		color_start    = {1.0, 0.30, 0.04, 1.0},
		color_end      = {0.55, 0.04, 0.0, 0.0},
		angle_start    = 0,
		ang_vel_min    = -1.2,
		ang_vel_max    = 1.2,
		draw_shape     = .Circle,
		blend_mode     = .Add,
	})

	// Embers — slow-drifting burned sparks above the complex fire
	demo_mm.fx_embers = g2d.g2d_fx_make(g2d.FX_Config{
		max_particles  = 100,
		spawn_rate     = 12,
		life_min       = 2000,
		life_max       = 4500,
		spawn_shape    = .Rect,
		shape_radius   = {18, 4},
		vel_min        = {-28, -70},
		vel_max        = {28,  -8},
		gravity        = {0, 10},
		drag_min       = 1,
		drag_max       = 3,
		size_start     = 2.5,
		size_end       = 0.4,
		color_start    = {0.9, 0.22, 0.03, 0.85},
		color_end      = {0.45, 0.03, 0.0, 0.0},
		ang_vel_min    = -2.5,
		ang_vel_max    = 2.5,
		draw_shape     = .Circle,
		blend_mode     = .Add,
	}, 0xC0FFEE42)

	// PostFX Demo targets (small size for efficiency)
	// has_depth=false: these RTs only hold colored geometry; stencil is not needed in their
	// population pass. Using false avoids a Sokol depth-view validation skip in
	// _g2d_flush_offscreen_passes if the depth-stencil image creation fails.
	demo_mm.postfx_rt1 = renderer.active_renderer.createRenderTarget(250, 250, false, current_dpi)
	demo_mm.postfx_rt2 = renderer.active_renderer.createRenderTarget(250, 250, false, current_dpi)
	demo_mm.postfx_rt3 = renderer.active_renderer.createRenderTarget(250, 250, false, current_dpi)
	// Masking demo target — matches tile size
	demo_mm.masking_rt  = renderer.active_renderer.createRenderTarget(300, 200, false, current_dpi)

	// Tile 30: Tweens & Springs — easing race
	ease_fns := [5]g2d.Ease_Fn{
		g2d.easeLinear,
		g2d.g2d_ease_out_expo,
		g2d.g2d_ease_out_back,
		g2d.g2d_ease_out_elastic,
		g2d.g2d_ease_out_bounce,
	}
	for i in 0..<5 {
		demo_mm.tw30_ease[i] = g2d.tweenF32Make(0, 1, 1800, ease_fns[i])
	}
	// Springs: slower omega so each behaviour is clearly readable over the cycle.
	// Underdamped z=0.35 → overshoots + oscillates.  Critical z=1.0 → smooth snap.
	// Overdamped z=2.5 → sluggish no-overshoot approach.
	demo_mm.tw30_sp[0] = g2d.g2d_spring_f32_make(0, 0, 4.5, 0.35)
	demo_mm.tw30_sp[1] = g2d.g2d_spring_f32_make(0, 0, 4.0, 1.0)
	demo_mm.tw30_sp[2] = g2d.g2d_spring_f32_make(0, 0, 2.5, 2.5)
	// Target line shows 1 immediately; springs jump after JUMP_DELAY_MS.
	demo_mm.tw30_sp_tgt_disp = 1
	demo_mm.tw30_sp_tgt      = 0  // springs haven't been kicked yet
	demo_mm.tw30_sp_jumped   = false

	demo_mm.inited = true

	// Load and parse Tiger SVG using new architecture
	tiger_svg_loading_handle, svg_load_error := renderer.active_renderer.loadAsset(
		"artifacts/resources/tiger.svg",
		assets.Asset_Type.SVG,
	)

	if svg_load_error {
		svg_parse_success: bool
		tiger_svg_render_handle_new, svg_parse_success = renderer.active_renderer.parseAsset(
			tiger_svg_loading_handle,
		)
		if !svg_parse_success {
			logging.error(.SVG, "Tiger SVG parse failed")
		}
	} else {
		logging.error(.SVG, fmt.tprintf("Tiger SVG load failed: %s", svg_load_error))
	}

	// One-time Tiger RSD export: generates tiger.rsd from the parsed SVG if it doesn't exist yet.
	if tiger_svg_render_handle_new.id != 0 && tiger_svg_render_handle_new.raw_parsed != nil {
		if !renderer.file_exists("artifacts/resources/tiger.rsd") {
			svg_el := cast(^g2d.SVG_Element)tiger_svg_render_handle_new.raw_parsed
			rsd_drawing, warnings := rsd.svg_to_rsd_drawing(svg_el^, context.temp_allocator)
			if len(warnings) > 0 {
				for w in warnings do logging.warn(.RSD, fmt.tprintf("Export Warning: %s", w))
			}
			if rsd.save_rsd_to_file(&rsd_drawing, "artifacts/resources/tiger.rsd") {
				logging.info(.RSD, "Tiger RSD exported successfully")
			} else {
				logging.error(.RSD, "Tiger RSD export failed")
			}
		}
	}

	// Load and parse Tiger RSD using new architecture
	// This happens AFTER any potential export to ensure we load the latest version
	tiger_rsd_loading_handle, rsd_load_success := renderer.active_renderer.loadAsset(
		"artifacts/resources/tiger.rsd",
		assets.Asset_Type.RSD,
	)

	if rsd_load_success {
		rsd_parse_success: bool
		tiger_rsd_render_handle_new, rsd_parse_success = renderer.active_renderer.parseAsset(
			tiger_rsd_loading_handle,
		)
		if rsd_parse_success {
			tiger_offscreen_rt_dirty = true

			if OFFSCREEN_RENDERING_ENABLED {
				tiger_offscreen_rt = renderer.active_renderer.createRenderTarget(512, 512, true, current_dpi)
				if tiger_offscreen_rt.image.id != 0 {
					tiger_offscreen_rt_initialized = true
				} else {
					logging.error(.Lifecycle, "Failed to create tiger RSD offscreen render target")
				}
			}
		} else {
			logging.error(.RSD, "Tiger RSD parse failed")
		}
	} else {
		logging.error(.RSD, "Tiger RSD load failed")
	}


	// Optional test: force a small shared atlas to reproduce churn/eviction behavior
	// Must be set before any fonts are created (before fm_init/fm_get -> atlas_init_if_needed)
	when #config(ATLAS_SMALL_512, false) {
		renderer.SetAtlasSizeOverride(512, 512)
	}

	// 5. Now we load/cache fonts (small/normal/large)
	// G.5.4: fm_init() is now called inside g2d_setup(); removed explicit call here.
	refresh_fonts(engine.dpi_scale())

	// Debug-only: run atlas eviction smoke test if enabled
	when #config(ATLAS_RUN_TESTS, false) {
		renderer.run_atlas_eviction_smoke_test()
	}

	// 7. Setup Layout Engine
	layout.layout_init(f32(engine.width()), f32(engine.height()))
	layout.layout_register_font(0, global_font_normal)
	layout.layout_register_font(1, global_font_large)

	// Init Scene
	current_scene = new(engine.Scene)
	current_scene.root = engine.create_element("Root")

	// --- Unified 24×32 Grid Layout ---
	top_bar := engine.create_element("TopBar")
	top_bar.vtable = &top_bar_vtable
	engine.appendChild(current_scene.root, top_bar)

	content := create_content_node()
	engine.appendChild(current_scene.root, content)

	when !STRESS_2D {
		// ── Top-left area: cols 0-11, rows 0-13 ──────────────────────────────
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("TL_Merged",  0,  0, 12,  6, "1. Primitives and Fills", render_tile_tl_merged))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("TL_3",       0,  6,  4,  4, "2. Arcs",              render_tile_tl6))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("TL_Spiro",   4,  6,  4,  4, "3. Spiro",             render_tile_tl3))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("TL_4",       8,  6,  4,  4, "4. Wave",              render_tile_tl5))

		points_tile := create_tile("TL_5",  0, 10,  4,  4, "5. Points",   render_tile_points)
		engine.appendChild(content, points_tile)
		p_node := new(Points_Tile_Node)
		p_node.name = "PointsDemo"
		p_node.vtable = &points_tile_vtable
		p_node.is_visible = true
		p_node.is_active = true
		p_node.transform = 1
		p_node.children = make([dynamic]^engine.Node)
		for &s in p_node.stars {
			s.x = (rand.float32() - 0.5) * 1000.0
			s.y = (rand.float32() - 0.5) * 1000.0
			s.z = rand.float32() * 1000.0
		}
		engine.appendChild(points_tile, p_node)

		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("TL_6",       4, 10,  4,  4, "6. Polygon",           render_tile_tl7))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("TL_7",       8, 10,  4,  4, "7. Photo",             render_tile_tl8))

		// ── Top-right area: cols 12-23, rows 0-13 ────────────────────────────
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("TR_1",      12,  0, 12,  2, "8. Modifiers",         render_tile_tr1))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("TR_2",      12,  2,  5,  6, "9. Haiku",             render_tile_tr2))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("TR_3",      17,  2,  7,  6, "10. Multi-Lang",       render_tile_tr3))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("TR_4",      12,  8, 12,  2, "11. Ellipsis",         render_tile_tr_ellipsis))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("TR_5",      12, 10, 12,  4, "12. SDF & Path Text",  render_tile_tr4))

		// ── Bottom-left area: cols 0-11, rows 14-31 ──────────────────────────
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("BL_Combined", 0, 14,  4,  6, "13. Strokes & Dash",  render_tile_bl_strokes_and_dash))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("BL_Miters",   4, 14,  4,  6, "14. Miters, Beziers", render_tile_bl_svg_parity))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("BL_Stencil",  8, 14,  4,  6, "15. Strokefill",      render_tile_bl_stencil))

		bl_tile_interact := create_tile("BL_4",  0, 20,  3,  6, "16. Interact", render_tile_bl_drag)
		engine.appendChild(content, bl_tile_interact)

		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("BL_5",       3, 20,  5,  6, "17. Fill Rules",       render_tile_bl_fill_rules))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("BL_6",       8, 20,  4,  6, "18. Sprite",           render_tile_bl1))

		// ── Bottom-left area row 3: cols 0-11, rows 26-31 ───────────────────
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("BL_Grouping", 0, 26,  4,  6, "19. setFilter",          render_tile_br_set_filter))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("BL_Particles",4, 26,  4,  6, "20. Particles",          render_tile_br_particles))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("BL_PostFX",   8, 26,  4,  6, "21. FX & Shadows",       render_tile_br_postfx))

		// ── Bottom-right area: cols 12-23, rows 14-31 ────────────────────────
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("BR_Tiger",    12, 14,  4,  6, "22. SVG Tiger",  render_tile_tiger))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("BR_Tiger_RSD",16, 14,  4,  6, "23. RSD Tiger",          render_tile_tiger_unified))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("BR_New1",    20, 14,  4,  6, "24. 2D Camera",           render_tile_br_camera))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("BR_Masking",  12, 20,  4,  6, "25. Masking",            render_tile_br_masking))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("BR_9Slice",   16, 20,  4,  6, "26. 9-Slice UI",         render_tile_br_9slice))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("BR_Timeline", 20, 20,  4,  6, "27. Sequencer",          render_tile_br_timeline))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("BR_New2",    12, 26,  4,  6, "28. Blend Modes",         render_tile_br_blend_modes))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("BR_New3",    16, 26,  4,  6, "29. Rec & Replay",        render_tile_br_recording))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("BR_TwSp",   20, 26,  4,  6, "30. Tweens & Springs",    render_tile_br_tweens_springs))
		// ── Extended row 32-37 ────────────────────────────────────────────────
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("BR_SaveLayer", 12, 32,  4,  6, "31. saveLayer",         render_tile_br_save_layer))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("BR_GpuCB",    16, 32,  4,  6, "32. GPU Callback",      render_tile_br_gpu_callback))

		// Interactive Demo Star — child of the Interact tile
		demo_star_node_ptr = new(Demo_Star_Node)
		demo_star_node_ptr.name = "Demo_Star"
		demo_star_node_ptr.children = make([dynamic]^engine.Node)
		demo_star_node_ptr.vtable = &demo_star_vtable
		demo_star_node_ptr.is_visible = true
		demo_star_node_ptr.is_active = true
		demo_star_node_ptr.num_points = 6
		demo_star_node_ptr.color = {1, 0.8, 0, 1} // Gold
		demo_star_node_ptr.hit_type = .Rect
		demo_star_node_ptr.bounds = {-35, -35, 70, 70}
		demo_star_node_ptr.transform = linalg.matrix4_translate_f32({58, 80, 0})
		demo_star_node_ptr.is_visible = false // transform tracked by red star in render proc
		engine.appendChild(bl_tile_interact, demo_star_node_ptr)

		demo_transformer = engine.transformerCreate(demo_star_node_ptr)
		engine.appendChild(bl_tile_interact, demo_transformer)
	}

	when STRESS_2D {
		// ── 6-tile 3×2 stress grid (cols 0-23, rows 0-19) ────────────────────
		// 11 + 11 + 10 = 32 = GRID_ROWS — fills window vertically
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("ST_Shadow",   0,  0, 8, 11, "S1. Shadow Flood",   render_stress_shadow))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("ST_Stencil",  8,  0, 8, 11, "S2. Stencil Storm",  render_stress_stencil))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("ST_Atlas",   16,  0, 8, 11, "S3. Atlas Hammer",   render_stress_atlas))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("ST_Fill",     0, 11, 8, 11, "S4. Fill Flood",     render_stress_fill))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("ST_Particle", 8, 11, 8, 11, "S5. Particle Storm", render_stress_particle))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("ST_Tween",   16, 11, 8, 11, "S6. Tween Storm",    render_stress_tween))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("ST_Orbs",    0,  22, 8, 10, "S7. Glow Orbs",      render_stress_orbs))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("ST_BHole",   8,  22, 8, 10, "S8. Particle Demo",  render_stress_blackhole))
		engine.appendChild(content, create_tile("ST_Toaster",16,  22, 8, 10, "S9. Flying Toasters", render_stress_toasters))
	}

} // end init_scene()


build_star_poly :: proc(cx, cy: f32, r_out, r_in: f32, num_pts: int, rot: f32 = 0) -> [][2]f32 {
	pts := make([dynamic][2]f32, 0, num_pts * 2, context.temp_allocator)
	angle_step := math.PI / f32(num_pts)
	for i in 0 ..< num_pts * 2 {
		r := (i % 2 == 0) ? r_out : r_in
		a := rot + f32(i) * angle_step
		append(&pts, [2]f32{cx + math.sin(a) * r, cy - math.cos(a) * r})
	}
	return pts[:]
}

demo_event :: proc(ev: engine.Event) {
	when ODIN_ARCH != .wasm32 && ODIN_ARCH != .wasm64p32 { context.allocator = mem.tracking_allocator(&profiler_allocator) }
	defer free_all(context.temp_allocator)

	// Bridge input to Scene Graph
	// Note: updateInputState is called by engine/app_sokol.odin before this callback.
	engine.sceneHandleInput(current_scene, ev)
	// Persist last input event for gesture hit-testing (logical px)
	demo_mm.last_ie = ev

	// Unified Multi-Gesture Routing (BL_4 Demo)
	// Build the hit polygon on the fly for SDF hit-testing
	star_cx := demo_mm.bl4_rect.x + demo_mm.bl4_rect.z / 2
	star_cy := demo_mm.bl4_rect.y + demo_mm.bl4_rect.w / 2
	r_out := demo_mm.bl4_rect.z / 2
	r_in := r_out * 0.5
	hit_poly := build_star_poly(star_cx, star_cy, r_out, r_in, 5)

	tapped, _, pan_began, pan_delta, pan_ended := g2d.g2d_gesture_set_update(
		&demo_mm.bl4_gs,
		ev,
		demo_mm.bl4_rect,
		0,       // dt_ms
		hit_poly,
	)
	if tapped {
		demo_mm.bl4_color = {0.2, 0.8, 0.2, 1.0} // tap makes it green
	}
	if pan_began {
		demo_mm.bl4_color = {0.8, 0.6, 0.1, 1.0} // pan makes it orange
	}
	if pan_delta.x != 0 || pan_delta.y != 0 {
		move_m := linalg.matrix4_translate_f32({pan_delta.x, pan_delta.y, 0})
		demo_star_node_ptr.transform = move_m * demo_star_node_ptr.transform
	}
	if pan_ended || (ev.type == .Mouse_Up && !tapped && !demo_mm.bl4_gs.pan.active) {
		demo_mm.bl4_color = {0.8, 0.2, 0.2, 1.0} // reset to red
	}

	// Handle window close (quit requested) and forced quit
	if ev.type == .Quit_Requested {
		// Request a clean quit so demo_cleanup() runs deterministically
		engine.request_quit()
	} else if ev.type == .Key_Down {
		// Allow ESC to request quit for convenience
		if ev.key == .Escape {
			engine.request_quit()
		} else if ev.key == .T {
			if OFFSCREEN_RENDERING_ENABLED && tiger_offscreen_rt_initialized {
				tiger_offscreen_rt_dirty = true
				logging.info(.Lifecycle, "[T KEY] Forced tiger offscreen re-render")
			} else {
				logging.warn(.Lifecycle, "[T KEY] Offscreen rendering not available")
			}
		}
	}
}

// demo_scene — called by engine after beginFrame, before flushGpuPrePass.
// Handles layout, offscreen RT work, sceneUpdate, sceneRender.
demo_scene :: proc() {
	when ODIN_ARCH != .wasm32 && ODIN_ARCH != .wasm64p32 { context.allocator = mem.tracking_allocator(&profiler_allocator) }
	// Note: context.temp_allocator is the renderer arena, set by engine before this callback.
	// Engine frees the arena after sg.commit() — do NOT free it here.



	dt_s := f32(engine.frame_duration())
	dt_ms := dt_s * 1000.0 // Correctly scale to milliseconds for animations
	g2d.animTick(dt_ms) // Phase 1.1: Standardized global animation tick
	g2d.g2d_timeline_update(&demo_mm.tl_anim, dt_ms) // Fix: Centralized timeline update for Sequencer demo
	g2d.emitterUpdate(&demo_mm.emitter,    dt_s * 100.0)
	g2d.g2d_fx_update(&demo_mm.fx_fire,   0, 0, dt_s * 100.0)
	g2d.g2d_fx_update(&demo_mm.fx_embers, 0, 0, dt_s * 100.0)

	// RSS Tracking & Jump Detection
	dpi := engine.dpi_scale()
	when logging.MEMORY_TRACKING {
		if engine.frame_count() % 300 == 0 && engine.frame_count() > 0 {
			curr_count := len(profiler_allocator.allocation_map)
			if curr_count > mem_track_prev_count {
				fmt.printf(
					"[MEMTRACK] frame=%d  live allocs: %d (+%d)  heap: %d KB\n",
					engine.frame_count(),
					curr_count,
					curr_count - mem_track_prev_count,
					profiler_allocator.current_memory_allocated / 1024,
				)
				mem_track_dump_leaks()
			} else {
				fmt.printf(
					"[MEMTRACK] frame=%d  live allocs: %d (stable)  heap: %d KB\n",
					engine.frame_count(),
					curr_count,
					profiler_allocator.current_memory_allocated / 1024,
				)
			}
			mem_track_prev_count = curr_count
		}
	}
	when #config(LOG_MEMORY, false) {
		curr_rss := get_rss()
		if last_rss == 0 {
			last_rss = curr_rss
		}

		rss_diff := curr_rss - last_rss
		if math.abs(f32(rss_diff)) > 1024 * 1024 { 	// Log jumps > 1MB
			logging.info(
				"mem",
				fmt.tprintf(
					"[MEM_JUMP] frame=%d | Jump: %4d KB | OS Current: %4d MB",
					engine.frame_count(),
					rss_diff / 1024,
					curr_rss / (1024 * 1024),
				),
			)
			last_rss = curr_rss
		}

		if engine.frame_count() % 120 == 0 {
			logging.info(
				"mem",
				fmt.tprintf(
					"[MEM] OS: %4d MB | HEAP: Cur: %4d KB (Total: %4d MB, Peak: %4d MB, Count: %d) | [ARENA] %d KB",
					curr_rss / (1024 * 1024),
					profiler_allocator.current_memory_allocated / 1024,
					profiler_allocator.total_memory_allocated / 1024 / 1024,
					profiler_allocator.peak_memory_allocated / 1024 / 1024,
					len(profiler_allocator.allocation_map),
					renderer.active_renderer.getArenaUsage() / 1024,
				),
			)
			last_rss = curr_rss
		}
	}

	// Dynamic DPI Font Scaling: Detect display switch
	if dpi != current_dpi {
		refresh_fonts(dpi)
	}

	// 1. Dynamic Layout Metrics
	w_raw := engine.widthf()
	h_raw := engine.heightf()

	// Enforce Minimum Size in logical pixels
	logical_w := math.max(w_raw / dpi, f32(MIN_WIDTH))
	logical_h := math.max(h_raw / dpi, f32(MIN_HEIGHT))

	global_layout.width = logical_w
	global_layout.height = logical_h
	global_layout.content_y = STATUS_BAR_H

	// 2. Update Area Nodes Transforms based on dynamic layout
	if current_scene != nil && current_scene.root != nil {
		for child in current_scene.root.children {
			switch child.name {
			case "TopBar":
				child.transform = linalg.Matrix4f32(1)
			case "Content":
				child.transform = linalg.matrix4_translate_f32({0, global_layout.content_y, 0})
				child.bounds = {0, 0, logical_w, logical_h - global_layout.content_y}
			}
		}
	}

	// ── Offscreen pre-render: MUST happen before sceneRender ──────────────
	// All RT fills must precede any main-pass drawing.
	tiger_needs_blur = false
	if OFFSCREEN_RENDERING_ENABLED &&
	   tiger_offscreen_rt_initialized &&
	   tiger_rsd_render_handle_new.id != 0 {
		if tiger_offscreen_rt_dirty {
			offscreen_config := renderer.active_renderer.defaultAssetRenderConfig()
			//offscreen_config.x = (512 - 900) / 2.0
			//offscreen_config.y = (512 - 900) / 2.0
			//offscreen_config.width  = 900
			//offscreen_config.height = 900
			offscreen_config.x = 0
			offscreen_config.y = 0
			offscreen_config.width = 512
			offscreen_config.height = 512
			renderer.active_renderer.setRenderTarget(&tiger_offscreen_rt)
			// renderer.active_renderer.fillRectSimple(0, 0, 512, 512, {0, 0, 0, 0}) // In case we ant white background
			renderer.active_renderer.renderAsset(tiger_rsd_render_handle_new, offscreen_config)
			renderer.active_renderer.setRenderTarget(nil)
			tiger_offscreen_rt_dirty = false
			tiger_needs_blur = BLUR_TEST_ENABLED

			// Lazy-init ping-pong blur render targets
			if BLUR_TEST_ENABLED {
				// Create temporary render targets for ping-pong blur (lazy init)
				if tiger_blur_temp_rt.image.id == 0 {
					tiger_blur_temp_rt = renderer.active_renderer.createRenderTarget(512, 512, true, current_dpi)
					if tiger_blur_temp_rt.image.id != 0 {
						tiger_blur_temp_rt_initialized = true
					} else {
						logging.error(.Lifecycle, "Failed to create blur temp RT")
					}
				}

				if tiger_blur_temp_rt2.image.id == 0 {
					tiger_blur_temp_rt2 = renderer.active_renderer.createRenderTarget(512, 512, true, current_dpi)
					if tiger_blur_temp_rt2.image.id != 0 {
						tiger_blur_temp_rt2_initialized = true
					} else {
						logging.error(.Lifecycle, "Failed to create blur temp RT2")
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

	// Scene Graph update & render
	dt := f32(engine.frame_duration())
	now += dt
	engine.sceneUpdate(current_scene, dt)
	engine.sceneRender(current_scene, renderer.active_renderer)
} //end of demo_scene()

// demo_post_flush — called by engine after flushGpuPrePass, before beginScreenPass.
// Applies gaussian blur to tiger RT when dirty.
demo_post_flush :: proc() {
	when ODIN_ARCH != .wasm32 && ODIN_ARCH != .wasm64p32 { context.allocator = mem.tracking_allocator(&profiler_allocator) }

	r := renderer.active_renderer

	if tiger_needs_blur && tiger_blur_temp_rt_initialized && tiger_blur_temp_rt2_initialized {
		r.applyGaussianBlur(
			&tiger_offscreen_rt,
			&tiger_blur_temp_rt,
			&tiger_blur_temp_rt2,
			0.8,
		)
		tiger_needs_blur = false
	}

	// --- Masking RT Population ---
	if demo_mm.inited {
		// Use logical coordinates: apply_transform multiplies by ctx.dpi_scale, so
		// we divide the physical RT size by dpi_scale to get the logical extent.
		// This ensures vertices land in [0, rt.width] physical pixels after scaling.
		dpi := current_dpi if current_dpi > 0 else 1.0
		rt_w := f32(demo_mm.masking_rt.width) / dpi
		rt_h := f32(demo_mm.masking_rt.height) / dpi
		r.setRenderTarget(&demo_mm.masking_rt)

		r.pushMatrix()
		r.identityMatrix()

		// Dark striped background
		r.fillRect(0, 0, rt_w, rt_h, renderer.paint_solid({0.12, 0.12, 0.15, 1}))
		for i in 0 ..= 20 {
			lx := f32(i) * (rt_w / 20.0)
			r.fillRect(lx, 0, rt_w / 40.0, rt_h, renderer.paint_solid({0.4, 0.4, 0.55, 0.25}))
		}
		// White text centred in the RT
		tw, th := r.measureText("MASKED", global_font_large)
		r.drawText((rt_w - tw) * 0.5, rt_h * 0.5 + th * 0.4, "MASKED", global_font_large, {1, 1, 1, 1})

		r.popMatrix()
		r.setRenderTarget(nil)
		r.flushGpuPrePass() // GPU-render masking_rt before it is sampled
	}

	// --- PostFX RT Population ---
	if demo_mm.inited {
		// Same DPI fix: use logical coordinates (physical / dpi_scale).
		dpi := current_dpi if current_dpi > 0 else 1.0
		rt_dim := 250.0 / dpi

		r.setRenderTarget(&demo_mm.postfx_rt1)
		// Reset matrix to identity so RT coordinates are not polluted by any
		// screen-space transform left in ctx.curr_matrix from the draw-list flush.
		r.pushMatrix()
		r.identityMatrix()

		// Bright saturated objects — bloom glow is visible even without a dark background
		pulse := 0.88 + math.sin_f32(now * 1.8) * 0.12
		r.fillCircle(rt_dim*0.55, rt_dim*0.48, rt_dim*0.22*pulse, renderer.paint_solid({1, 0.85, 0.1, 1}))
		r.fillRect(rt_dim*0.08, rt_dim*0.18, rt_dim*0.28, rt_dim*0.28, renderer.paint_solid({0.1, 1, 0.35, 1}))
		r.fillCircle(rt_dim*0.22, rt_dim*0.75, rt_dim*0.13, renderer.paint_solid({0.2, 0.6, 1, 1}))
		r.fillRect(rt_dim*0.52, rt_dim*0.72, rt_dim*0.38, rt_dim*0.16, renderer.paint_solid({1, 0.15, 0.7, 1}))

		r.popMatrix()
		r.setRenderTarget(nil)
		r.flushGpuPrePass() // GPU-render postfx_rt1 before bloom reads it

		// Apply bloom only — vignette darkens transparent pixels → black square on tile
		// Result lands in postfx_rt2; tile draws from rt2.
		r.applyBloom(&demo_mm.postfx_rt1, &demo_mm.postfx_rt2, &demo_mm.postfx_rt3, 0.3, 2.5)
	}
}

// demo_draw — called by engine after beginScreenPass, before endFrame.
// Handles immediate/3D content via sceneDraw.
demo_draw :: proc() {
	when ODIN_ARCH != .wasm32 && ODIN_ARCH != .wasm64p32 { context.allocator = mem.tracking_allocator(&profiler_allocator) }
	engine.sceneDraw(current_scene)
}

demo_cleanup :: proc() {
	// Release renderer resources before shutting down Sokol Gfx
	// 1) Destroy any GPU images we created via the renderer
	if test_img.id != 0 {
		renderer.active_renderer.destroyImage(test_img)
	}

	// Destroy the animation image if it exists
	if demo_mm.anim_img.id != 0 {
		renderer.active_renderer.destroyImage(demo_mm.anim_img)
	}

	// 2a) G.5.4: fm_shutdown() is now called inside g2d_shutdown(); removed explicit call here.

	if tiger_blur_temp_rt_initialized {
		renderer.active_renderer.destroyRenderTarget(tiger_blur_temp_rt)
		tiger_blur_temp_rt_initialized = false
	}
	if tiger_blur_temp_rt2_initialized {
		renderer.active_renderer.destroyRenderTarget(tiger_blur_temp_rt2)
		tiger_blur_temp_rt2_initialized = false
	}

	// Note: renderer.shutdown(), shutdownAssetManager(), and sg.shutdown()
	// are all handled by engine/app_sokol.odin after this callback returns.

	// Cleanup demo star and transformer
	if demo_star_node_ptr != nil {
		// Clean up the demo star node
		for child in demo_star_node_ptr.children {
			// Recursively clean up children if any
		}
		// Free the demo star node
		free(demo_star_node_ptr)
		demo_star_node_ptr = nil
	}

	if demo_transformer != nil {
		// Clean up the transformer
		free(demo_transformer)
		demo_transformer = nil
	}

	// Clean up the scene graph
	if current_scene != nil {
		// Recursively free all nodes in the scene graph
		// Note: Assuming the engine module has a function to free the scene graph
		// If not, this will need to be implemented in the engine module
		// For now, we will manually free the root node and its children
		if current_scene.root != nil {
			// Free the root node and its children
			// This is a simplified approach; a more robust solution would require
			// a recursive function to free all nodes in the scene graph
			free(current_scene.root)
			current_scene.root = nil
		}
		free(current_scene)
		current_scene = nil
	}

	// Font handles are opaque — fm_shutdown() (called by g2d_shutdown) handles all font cleanup.
	// Do not free font handles here.

	when logging.MEMORY_TRACKING {
		fmt.println("[MEMTRACK] Final leak report:")
		mem_track_dump_leaks()
	}

	logging.shutdown()
} //end of cleanup()

// mem_track_dump_leaks prints all live allocations grouped by call site.
// Enabled only when MEMORY_TRACKING=true. Call from cleanup() or on demand.
@(private)
mem_track_dump_leaks :: proc() {
	when logging.MEMORY_TRACKING {
		if len(profiler_allocator.allocation_map) == 0 {
			fmt.println("[MEMTRACK] No live allocations — clean exit.")
			return
		}

		// Group by (file, line) → {count, total_bytes}
		Site_Stats :: struct { count: int, bytes: int }
		sites := make(map[string]Site_Stats, context.temp_allocator)
		for _, entry in profiler_allocator.allocation_map {
			key := fmt.tprintf("%s:%d", entry.location.file_path, entry.location.line)
			s := sites[key]
			s.count += 1
			s.bytes += entry.size
			sites[key] = s
		}

		fmt.printf(
			"[MEMTRACK] %d live allocations (%d KB) — call sites:\n",
			len(profiler_allocator.allocation_map),
			profiler_allocator.current_memory_allocated / 1024,
		)
		for key, s in sites {
			suspect := s.count > 5 ? " *** SUSPECT ***" : ""
			fmt.printf("  %5d x %6d KB  %s%s\n", s.count, s.bytes / 1024, key, suspect)
		}

		if len(profiler_allocator.bad_free_array) > 0 {
			fmt.printf("[MEMTRACK] %d bad frees recorded:\n", len(profiler_allocator.bad_free_array))
			for entry in profiler_allocator.bad_free_array {
				fmt.printf("  ptr=%p  %s:%d\n", entry.memory, entry.location.file_path, entry.location.line)
			}
		}
	}
}





// ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// UTILITIES
// ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

refresh_fonts :: proc(dpi: f32) {
	logging.info(.Lifecycle, fmt.tprintf("Refreshing fonts for scale: %.2f", dpi))
	current_dpi = dpi

	// Recreate render targets for New DPI
	if demo_mm.inited {
		logging.info(.Lifecycle, "Recreating render targets for new DPI scale...")
		renderer.active_renderer.destroyRenderTarget(demo_mm.postfx_rt1)
		renderer.active_renderer.destroyRenderTarget(demo_mm.postfx_rt2)
		renderer.active_renderer.destroyRenderTarget(demo_mm.postfx_rt3)
		renderer.active_renderer.destroyRenderTarget(demo_mm.masking_rt)

		demo_mm.postfx_rt1 = renderer.active_renderer.createRenderTarget(250, 250, false, current_dpi)
		demo_mm.postfx_rt2 = renderer.active_renderer.createRenderTarget(250, 250, false, current_dpi)
		demo_mm.postfx_rt3 = renderer.active_renderer.createRenderTarget(250, 250, false, current_dpi)
		demo_mm.masking_rt  = renderer.active_renderer.createRenderTarget(300, 200, false, current_dpi)
	}

	// Register fallback paths (no-cost — just appends strings, no file I/O)
	g2d.fm_add_fallback_font(config.FONT_PATH_PERSIAN)
	g2d.fm_add_fallback_font(config.FONT_FALLBACKS_CJK[1])

	ok: bool
	font_path := config.FONT_PATH_DEFAULT

	global_font_small, ok = g2d.fm_get_handle(font_path, u32(12 * dpi))
	if !ok {
		logging.error(.Fonts, "Failed to load small font!")
	}
	global_font_normal, ok = g2d.fm_get_handle(font_path, u32(18 * dpi))
	if !ok {
		logging.error(.Fonts, "Failed to load normal font!")
	}
	global_font_large, ok = g2d.fm_get_handle(font_path, u32(24 * dpi))
	if !ok {
		logging.error(.Fonts, "Failed to load large font!")
	}

	// Script fonts (Arabic, Hebrew, CJK) are loaded lazily on first use.
	// Reset the flag so ensure_script_fonts reloads at the new DPI.
	script_fonts_loaded = false

	// Update Layout Engine font registry
	layout.layout_register_font(0, global_font_normal)
	layout.layout_register_font(1, global_font_large)
}

// ensure_script_fonts loads Arabic, Hebrew and CJK fonts on first call (or after
// a DPI change). Safe to call every frame from any tile render proc — returns
// immediately if already loaded at the current DPI.
ensure_script_fonts :: proc(dpi: f32) {
	if script_fonts_loaded && script_fonts_dpi == dpi do return

	ok: bool
	fallbacks_arabic := config.FONT_FALLBACKS_ARABIC
	fallbacks_hebrew := config.FONT_FALLBACKS_HEBREW
	fallbacks_cjk    := config.FONT_FALLBACKS_CJK

	arabic_font_path := fallbacks_arabic[len(fallbacks_arabic) - 1]
	hebrew_font_path := fallbacks_hebrew[len(fallbacks_hebrew) - 1]
	cjk_font_path    := fallbacks_cjk[1] // NotoSansCJKsc-Regular

	global_font_arabic, ok = g2d.fm_get_handle(arabic_font_path, u32(18 * dpi))
	if !ok {
		for p in fallbacks_arabic {
			global_font_arabic, ok = g2d.fm_get_handle(p, u32(18 * dpi))
			if ok { arabic_font_path = p; break }
		}
	}

	global_font_hebrew, ok = g2d.fm_get_handle(hebrew_font_path, u32(18 * dpi))
	if !ok {
		for p in fallbacks_hebrew {
			global_font_hebrew, ok = g2d.fm_get_handle(p, u32(18 * dpi))
			if ok { hebrew_font_path = p; break }
		}
	}

	global_font_arabic_small, _ = g2d.fm_get_handle(arabic_font_path, u32(12 * dpi))
	global_font_hebrew_small, _ = g2d.fm_get_handle(hebrew_font_path, u32(12 * dpi))

	global_font_cjk, ok = g2d.fm_get_handle(cjk_font_path, u32(18 * dpi))
	if !ok {
		for p in fallbacks_cjk {
			global_font_cjk, ok = g2d.fm_get_handle(p, u32(18 * dpi))
			if ok { cjk_font_path = p; break }
		}
	}
	global_font_cjk_small, _ = g2d.fm_get_handle(cjk_font_path, u32(12 * dpi))

	script_fonts_loaded = true
	script_fonts_dpi    = dpi
}
