//central render Linrary with public 2D functions (camelCase)

import txt "project:graphics/text"
import "core:fmt"
import "core:math"
import "core:math/linalg"
import "core:mem"
import assets "project:assets"
import logging "project:logging"
import sg "vendor-local:sokol-odin/sokol/gfx"

// Renderer Pass Type enumeration
Renderer_Pass_Type :: enum {
	Screen, // Main window/swapchain rendering
	Offscreen, // Offscreen render target rendering
}

// Shadow System Types - moved here to break circular dependency
// These are rendering concepts that belong in the renderer layer, not graphics implementation
Shadow_Key :: struct {
	kind:               u8, // 0 = Rect, 1 = Circle, 2 = Ngon, 3 = InsetRect
	_padding1:          [3]u8, // Explicit padding to achieve 4-byte alignment
	w, h, r:            f32, // dimensions/radius
	sides:              i32, // for Ngons (switched from int to i32 for predictable size)
	offset_x, offset_y: f32,
	blur:               f32,
	// color is intentionally NOT part of the key — the RT stores a white silhouette
	// and color is applied as a tint at draw time via g2d_draw_render_target.
}

Shadow_Cache_Entry :: struct {
	rt_shadow:          Render_Target,
	rt_temp:            Render_Target,
	width, height:      f32,
	offset_x, offset_y: f32,
}

// Stores render target VALUE copies (not pointers) — safe against
// any reallocation that might occur between queueing and execution.
Pending_Blur :: struct {
	target: Render_Target,
	temp:   Render_Target,
	radius: f32,
}

// Use shared types from assets package to avoid circular dependencies
// Phase DX: Asset Management Types are now in assets/types.odin

// Default Asset Manager Configuration
defaultAssetManagerConfig :: proc() -> assets.Asset_Manager_Config {
	return assets.Asset_Manager_Config {
		max_assets = 1024,
		max_memory_mb = 512,
		enable_background_loading = true,
		cache_directory = "artifacts/cache/",
	}
}

// Math utility functions that might be missing from core:math

// Normalize a 3D vector
normalize3 :: proc(v: [3]f32) -> [3]f32 {
	len := math.sqrt(v[0] * v[0] + v[1] * v[1] + v[2] * v[2])
	if len > 0.0 {
		return [3]f32{v[0] / len, v[1] / len, v[2] / len}
	}
	return [3]f32{0.0, 0.0, 0.0}
}

// Cross product of two 3D vectors
cross3 :: proc(a, b: [3]f32) -> [3]f32 {
	return [3]f32{a[1] * b[2] - a[2] * b[1], a[2] * b[0] - a[0] * b[2], a[0] * b[1] - a[1] * b[0]}
}

// Dot product of two 3D vectors
dot3 :: proc(a, b: [3]f32) -> f32 {
	return a[0] * b[0] + a[1] * b[1] + a[2] * b[2]
}


// Re-export core text types for API compatibility
// G.5: Font :: txt.Font alias removed (font_manager/font_atlas moved to graphics/).
// External callers: use Font_Handle (opaque). Internal graphics/ layer: use txt.Font directly.
Glyph_Info :: txt.Glyph_Info
Atlas_Page :: txt.Atlas_Page
Shared_Atlas :: txt.Shared_Atlas
Visual_Line :: txt.Visual_Line
Shaped_Span :: txt.Shaped_Span
Direction :: txt.Direction
Script_Kind :: txt.Script_Kind
Shaped_Glyph :: txt.Shaped_Glyph
itemizedRun :: txt.ItemizedRun
Atlas_Row :: txt.Atlas_Row
Shelf :: txt.Shelf
Glyph_Row_Ref :: txt.Glyph_Row_Ref
Text_Modifiers :: txt.Text_Modifiers
Text_Modifier :: txt.Text_Modifier
FONT_STYLE_BOLD :: txt.FONT_STYLE_BOLD
FONT_STYLE_ITALIC :: txt.FONT_STYLE_ITALIC

// Sampler filter modes for image drawing (matches G2D_Sampler_Filter in g2d)
Sampler_Filter :: enum {
	Nearest,
	Linear,
	Linear_Mipmap,
}

// Material system for advanced image rendering
G2D_Material :: struct {
	image: Image_Handle, // bound texture (white_image for solid-color)
	tint:  [4]f32, // vertex color multiplier (uniform-like bundle)
}

// Sprite animation system for game development
G2D_Sprite_Anim :: struct {
	image:   Image_Handle,
	frame_w: i32,
	frame_h: i32,
	start:   i32, // inclusive
	end:     i32, // inclusive
	fps:     f32,
	loop:    bool,
	frame:   i32, // current frame index
	acc_ms:  f32, // accumulator in milliseconds
	playing: bool,
	on_complete: proc(anim: ^G2D_Sprite_Anim),
}

// Phase DX: Image Handle Conversion Functions
// These functions provide compatibility during the transition from sg.Image to Image_Handle

// Convert Image_Handle to sg.Image for compatibility with Sokol
// NOTE: This function should only be used during the transition phase
// and will be removed when all code uses Image_Handle exclusively
handle_to_sg_image :: proc(handle: Image_Handle) -> sg.Image {
	return sg.Image{id = handle.id}
}

// Convert sg.Image to Image_Handle for Layer 3 usage
// NOTE: This function should only be used during the transition phase
sg_image_to_handle :: proc(img: sg.Image) -> Image_Handle {
	return Image_Handle{id = img.id}
}

// Removed from here to avoid circular dependencies; located in text/types.odin
// persistent_allocator: mem.Allocator

// Phase 10D.2.3 --- Path-Based Clipping Support
// Simple path structure for clipping operations
Path :: struct {
	commands: [dynamic]Path_Command,
}

Path_Command :: struct {
	type: Path_Command_Type,
	data: Path_Command_Data,
}

Path_Command_Type :: enum {
	MoveTo,
	LineTo,
	CubicBezier,
	QuadraticBezier,
	Arc,
	ClosePath,
}

Path_Command_Data :: union {
	MoveTo_Data,
	LineTo_Data,
	CubicBezier_Data,
	QuadraticBezier_Data,
	Arc_Data,
	ClosePath_Data,
}

MoveTo_Data :: struct {
	x, y: f32,
}

LineTo_Data :: struct {
	x, y: f32,
}

CubicBezier_Data :: struct {
	cx1, cy1, cx2, cy2, x, y: f32,
}

QuadraticBezier_Data :: struct {
	cx, cy, x, y: f32,
}

Arc_Data :: struct {
	rx, ry, x_axis_rotation, start_angle, end_angle: f32,
	clockwise:                                       bool,
}

ClosePath_Data :: struct {}

// Re-export core text procedures
shape_text_itemized :: txt.shape_text_itemized


shape_text_basic :: txt.shape_text_basic
get_font_size :: txt.get_font_size

/**
@documentation: Renderer/Vertex
- Standard vertex structure for 2D batching.
- pos: 2D Screen coordinates.
- uv: Texture coordinates (0..1).
- color: RGBA normalized float color.
*/
G2D_Vertex :: struct {
	pos:   [2]f32,
	uv:    [2]f32,
	color: [4]f32,
}

// Gate 4.1: Per-instance data for GPU-instanced circle particles.
// Matches the vertex-buffer-slot-1 layout expected by particle_instanced.glsl:
//   inst_pos_size: (cx, cy, radius) — pixel-space
//   inst_color:    (r, g, b, a) — normalised float
Particle_Instance :: struct #packed {
	pos_size: [3]f32,  // x, y, radius
	color:    [4]f32,  // r, g, b, a
}

Renderer_Image :: struct {
	handle: Image_Handle,
	width:  i32,
	height: i32,
}

// @documentation: Renderer/Offscreen
// Render_Target wraps a texture and a pass for offscreen drawing.
Render_Target :: struct {
	image:         Image_Handle,
	stencil_image: Image_Handle, // new: handle to depth-stencil image for proper cleanup
	// TEMPORARY VIOLATION: sg.View types needed for Sokol integration
	// TODO: Replace with opaque handles in Phase G
	view:          sg.View, // color-attachment-view  — used in begin_pass attachments
	tex_view:      sg.View, // texture-view           — used in apply_bindings when drawing to screen
	ds_view:       sg.View, // depth-stencil-attachment-view — used in begin_pass attachments
	width:         i32,
	height:        i32,
	dpi_scale:     f32, // new: track the DPI scale this RT was created with
	has_depth:     bool, // new: explicitly track if this RT has a depth buffer
}

// @documentation: Renderer/Types
// Line_Cap mirrors the HTML Canvas lineCap property.
Line_Cap :: enum {
	Butt, // Ends exactly at coordinates (Default)
	Square, // Extends by half-thickness with a square box
	Round, // Extends by half-thickness with a semi-circle
}

// Line_Join determines how path segments are joined.
Line_Join :: enum {
	Miter, // Sharp corners (Default)
	Bevel, // Flat corners
	Round, // Rounded corners
}

Gradient_Stroke_Options :: struct {
	edge_lines: bool,
	edge_color: [4]f32,
	edge_width: f32,
}

/**
@documentation: Renderer/API
- This struct defines the virtual interface for our rendering engine.
- naming follows camelCase-style conventions (fillRect, strokeRect).
*/
// Reference: Adding rounded rectangle support to the virtual interface.
Renderer :: struct {
	setup:                     proc(), //setup renderer
	shutdown:                  proc(), // shutdown renderer
	beginFrame:                proc(width, height: f32),
	flushGpuPrePass:           proc(), // Phase 10-01.1: CamelCase version
	endFrame:                  proc(),
	setColor:                  proc(r, g, b, a: f32), // Phase 10-01.2: CamelCase version

	// API - Offscreen
	createRenderTarget:        proc(w, h: i32, has_depth: bool = true, dpi_scale: f32 = 1.0) -> Render_Target,
	destroyRenderTarget:       proc(rt: Render_Target),
	setRenderTarget:           proc(rt: ^Render_Target), // nil for default

	// API - Primitives
	fillRect:                  proc(x, y, w, h: f32, paint: Paint, shadow: Shadow = {}),
	drawRect:                  proc(
		x, y, w, h, thickness: f32,
		color: [4]f32,
		shadow: Shadow = {},
	),
	fillRectRounded:           proc(x, y, w, h, radius: f32, paint: Paint, shadow: Shadow = {}),
	drawRectRounded:           proc(
		x, y, w, h, radius, thickness: f32,
		color: [4]f32,
		shadow: Shadow = {},
	),
	// Phase F.2 — Proper directional drop shadows & inset shadows
	dropShadowRect:            proc(
		x, y, w, h, radius, offset_x, offset_y, blur: f32,
		color: [4]f32,
	),
	dropShadowCircle:          proc(cx, cy, radius, offset_x, offset_y, blur: f32, color: [4]f32),
	dropShadowNgon:            proc(
		cx, cy, radius: f32,
		sides: int,
		offset_x, offset_y, blur: f32,
		color: [4]f32,
	),
	insetShadowRect:           proc(x, y, w, h, top_depth, side_depth: f32, color: [4]f32),
	fillRectSimple:            proc(x, y, w, h: f32, color: [4]f32),
	fillRectGradient:          proc(x, y, w, h: f32, c_tl, c_tr, c_br, c_bl: [4]f32),
	fillRectRoundedGradient:   proc(
		x, y, w, h, radius: f32,
		c_top, c_bottom: [4]f32,
		vertical: bool = true,
	),
	drawLine:                  proc(
		x1, y1, x2, y2, thickness: f32,
		color: [4]f32,
		lineCap: Line_Cap = .Butt,
	),
	// Convenience function for simple line drawing
	drawLineSimple:            proc(x1, y1, x2, y2: f32, color: [4]f32, thickness: f32 = 1.0),
	fillTriangle:              proc(
		x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, y3: f32,
		paint: Paint,
		shadow: Shadow = {},
	),
	drawTriangle:              proc(
		x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, y3, thickness: f32,
		color: [4]f32,
		shadow: Shadow = {},
	),
	drawPolyline:              proc(
		points: [][2]f32,
		thickness: f32,
		color: [4]f32,
		lineCap: Line_Cap = .Butt,
	),
	fillPolygon:               proc(points: [][2]f32, paint: Paint, shadow: Shadow = {}),
	drawPolygon:               proc(
		points: [][2]f32,
		thickness: f32,
		color: [4]f32,
		shadow: Shadow = {},
	),
	fillCircle:                proc(x, y, radius: f32, paint: Paint, shadow: Shadow = {}),
	fillCircleSimple:          proc(x, y, radius: f32, color: [4]f32),
	drawCircle:                proc(
		x, y, radius, thickness: f32,
		color: [4]f32,
		shadow: Shadow = {},
	),
	fillEllipse:               proc(x, y, rx, ry: f32, paint: Paint, shadow: Shadow = {}),
	drawEllipse:               proc(
		x, y, rx, ry, thickness: f32,
		color: [4]f32,
		shadow: Shadow = {},
	),
	// Advanced Stroke API (Phase 11)
	strokeRect:                proc(x, y, w, h: f32, stroke: Stroke, shadow: Shadow = {}),
	strokeRectRounded:         proc(x, y, w, h, radius: f32, stroke: Stroke, shadow: Shadow = {}),
	strokeLine:                proc(x1, y1, x2, y2: f32, stroke: Stroke),
	strokePolyline:            proc(points: [][2]f32, stroke: Stroke),
	strokePolygon:             proc(points: [][2]f32, stroke: Stroke, shadow: Shadow = {}),
	strokeCircle:              proc(x, y, radius: f32, stroke: Stroke, shadow: Shadow = {}),
	strokeEllipse:             proc(x, y, rx, ry: f32, stroke: Stroke, shadow: Shadow = {}),
	strokeArc:                 proc(
		x, y, radius, start_angle, end_angle: f32,
		stroke: Stroke,
		shadow: Shadow = {},
	),
	strokeStar:                proc(
		x, y: f32,
		num_points: int,
		inner_radius, outer_radius: f32,
		stroke: Stroke,
		shadow: Shadow = {},
	),
	strokeBezierQuadratic:     proc(x1, y1, cx, cy, x2, y2: f32, stroke: Stroke),
	strokeBezierCubic:         proc(x1, y1, c1x, c1y, c2x, c2y, x2, y2: f32, stroke: Stroke),

	// Phase 11: Gradient Stroke
	strokeGradient:            proc(
		points: [][2]f32,
		paint: Paint,
		width: f32,
		options: Gradient_Stroke_Options,
	),
	pathStroke:                proc(stroke: Stroke),


	// --- Phasse 2.4 New Point Primitives ---
	drawPoint:                 proc(x, y: f32, color: [4]f32),
	drawPointScale:            proc(x, y: f32, color: [4]f32),
	//  Added matrix functions ---
	pushMatrix:                proc(),
	popMatrix:                 proc(),
	identityMatrix:            proc(),
	translate:                 proc(x, y: f32),
	rotate:                    proc(angle_radians: f32),
	scale:                     proc(sx, sy: f32),
	applyMatrix:               proc(m: matrix[3, 3]f32),
	applyMatrix4:              proc(m: matrix[4, 4]f32),
	//  Perspective Transform Support
	applyPerspective:          proc(fov: f32, aspect: f32, near: f32, far: f32),
	applyPerspectiveMatrix:    proc(m: matrix[4, 4]f32),
	pushPerspective:           proc(),
	popPerspective:            proc(),
	// Phase 3.1--- Image Management ---
	loadImage:                 proc(path: string) -> Image_Handle,
	destroyImage:              proc(img: Image_Handle),
	// Phase 3.2 --- Image Drawing and getting indo on size of image
	// Architecture: tint multiplies fragment color
	drawImage:                 proc(
		x, y, w, h: f32,
		img: Image_Handle,
		color: [4]f32 = {1, 1, 1, 1},
	),
	// Phase 10D.1.1 - Convenience function for simple texture drawing
	drawTextureSimple:         proc(
		x, y, w, h: f32,
		img: Image_Handle,
		color: [4]f32 = {1, 1, 1, 1},
	),
	//Phase 3.3 add clipping functions
	// --- Soft Clipping (UV Sub-rects) ---
	// Architecture: draw a specific part of an image to a specific part of the screen
	drawImageRect:             proc(
		dest: [4]f32,
		src: [4]f32,
		img: Image_Handle,
		color: [4]f32 = {1, 1, 1, 1},
	),
	// Phase 3.2 --- Hard Clipping (GPU Scissor) ---
	// Architecture: restricts all subsequent draw calls to this box
	setClip:                   proc(x, y, w, h: f32),
	resetClip:                 proc(),
	pushClip:                  proc(x, y, w, h: f32),
	popClip:                   proc(),

	// Phase 10D.2.3 --- Path-Based Clipping ---
	// Architecture: uses stencil buffer for complex path clipping
	beginClipPath:             proc(),
	endClipPath:               proc(),
	clipPath:                  proc(path: ^Path),
	
	// Phase 12 --- Stencil Masking ---
	beginMask:                 proc(),
	endMask:                   proc(),
	beginMaskedDraw:           proc(),
	endMaskedDraw:             proc(),
	
	// Phase 13 --- Post-Effects ---
	applyBoxBlur:              proc(source, output, temp: ^Render_Target, radius: f32),
	applyBloom:                proc(source, output, temp: ^Render_Target, threshold, intensity: f32),
	applyVignette:             proc(source, output: ^Render_Target, radius, softness: f32),

	//Phase 3.3 get size info in image
	getImageInfo:              proc(img: Image_Handle) -> (width, height: i32),

	// Phase 10D.1 - Material System for Advanced Image Rendering
	createMaterial:            proc(image: Image_Handle, tint: [4]f32) -> G2D_Material,
	createMaterialSolid:       proc(tint: [4]f32) -> G2D_Material,

	// Material-aware image drawing functions
	draw9Slice:                proc(image: Image_Handle, dest: [4]f32, slices: [4]f32, tint: [4]f32 = {1, 1, 1, 1}),
	drawImageMat:              proc(x, y, w, h: f32, mat: G2D_Material),
	drawImageRectMat:          proc(dest: [4]f32, src_px: [4]f32, mat: G2D_Material),
	drawImageRectUvMat:        proc(dest: [4]f32, uvs: [4]f32, mat: G2D_Material),
	// Sampler control — must be called before drawImageRectUvMat; resets after
	setSampling:               proc(filter: Sampler_Filter, repeat: bool, mipmap: bool),
	resetSampling:             proc(),
	getInputState:             proc() -> rawptr,
	//Phase 4.1 draw text function
	drawText:                  proc(
		x, y: f32,
		text: string,
		font: Font_Handle,
		color: [4]f32,
		mods: Text_Modifiers = {},
	),
	// Phase 4.2 M3: advanced (bidi + itemization + font fallback) shaping
	drawTextAdvanced:          proc(
		x, y: f32,
		text: string,
		primary: Font_Handle,
		fallbacks: []Font_Handle,
		color: [4]f32,
		mods: Text_Modifiers = {},
	),
	// Phase 4.3: Layout-based drawing
	drawTextLayout:            proc(
		x, y: f32,
		lines: []Visual_Line,
		y_offsets: []f32,
		color: [4]f32,
		mods: Text_Modifiers = {},
	),
	measureText:               proc(text: string, font: Font_Handle) -> (width, height: f32),

	// --- Phase 6B: Advanced Vector / Path API ---
	pathBegin:                 proc(),
	pathMoveTo:                proc(x, y: f32),
	pathLineTo:                proc(x, y: f32),
	pathArcTo:                 proc(x, y, r, start_angle, end_angle: f32, clockwise: bool = true),
	pathQuadTo:                proc(cx, cy, x, y: f32),
	pathCubicTo:               proc(c1x, c1y, c2x, c2y, x, y: f32),
	pathClose:                 proc(),
	pathDraw:                  proc(
		thickness: f32,
		color: [4]f32,
		join: Line_Join = .Miter,
		cap: Line_Cap = .Butt,
	),
	pathFill:                  proc(paint: Paint),

	// Bezier Primitives (Fill via paint)
	fillBezierQuadratic:       proc(x1, y1, cx, cy, x2, y2: f32, paint: Paint),
	fillBezierCubic:           proc(x1, y1, c1x, c1y, c2x, c2y, x2, y2: f32, paint: Paint),

	// Bezier Primitives (Direct Access)
	drawBezierQuadratic:       proc(x1, y1, cx, cy, x2, y2, thickness: f32, color: [4]f32),
	drawBezierCubic:           proc(
		x1, y1, c1x, c1y, c2x, c2y, x2, y2, thickness: f32,
		color: [4]f32,
	),

	// --- Phase 9B: Beautification Primitives ---
	fillNgon:                  proc(
		x, y, radius: f32,
		sides: int,
		paint: Paint,
		shadow: Shadow = {},
	),
	drawNgon:                  proc(
		x, y, radius, thickness: f32,
		sides: int,
		color: [4]f32,
		shadow: Shadow = {},
	),
	fillPie:                   proc(
		x, y, radius, start_angle, end_angle: f32,
		paint: Paint,
		shadow: Shadow = {},
	),
	drawArc:                   proc(
		x, y, radius, start_angle, end_angle, thickness: f32,
		color: [4]f32,
		cap: Line_Cap = .Butt,
		shadow: Shadow = {},
	),
	fillArc:                   proc(
		x, y, radius, start_angle, end_angle: f32,
		paint: Paint,
		shadow: Shadow = {},
	),
	fillStar:                  proc(
		x, y: f32,
		num_points: int,
		inner_radius, outer_radius: f32,
		paint: Paint,
		shadow: Shadow = {},
	),
	drawStar:                  proc(
		x, y: f32,
		num_points: int,
		inner_radius, outer_radius, thickness: f32,
		color: [4]f32,
		shadow: Shadow = {},
	),
	fillRing:                  proc(
		x, y, inner_radius, outer_radius: f32,
		paint: Paint,
		shadow: Shadow = {},
	),
	drawRing:                  proc(
		x, y: f32,
		inner_radius, inner_thickness: f32,
		inner_color: [4]f32,
		outer_radius, outer_thickness: f32,
		outer_color: [4]f32,
		shadow: Shadow = {},
	),
	fillWedge:                 proc(
		x, y, radius, start_angle, end_angle: f32,
		paint: Paint,
		clockwise: bool = true,
		shadow: Shadow = {},
	),
	drawWedge:                 proc(
		x, y, radius, thickness: f32,
		start_angle, end_angle: f32,
		color: [4]f32,
		clockwise: bool = true,
		shadow: Shadow = {},
	),
	drawTextOnPath:            proc(text: string, font: Font_Handle, color: [4]f32),

	// Sprite Animation System
	spriteMake:                proc(
		image: Image_Handle,
		frame_w, frame_h: i32,
		start, end: i32,
		fps: f32,
		loop: bool,
	) -> G2D_Sprite_Anim,
	spriteUpdate:              proc(anim: ^G2D_Sprite_Anim, dt_ms: f32) -> bool,
	spritePlay:                proc(anim: ^G2D_Sprite_Anim),
	spritePause:               proc(anim: ^G2D_Sprite_Anim),
	spriteResume:              proc(anim: ^G2D_Sprite_Anim),
	spriteFrameUV:             proc(anim: ^G2D_Sprite_Anim) -> [4]f32,
	drawSprite:                proc(
		anim: ^G2D_Sprite_Anim,
		dest: [4]f32,
		tint: [4]f32 = {1, 1, 1, 1},
	),
	drawSpriteFrame:           proc(
		anim: ^G2D_Sprite_Anim,
		frame: i32,
		dest: [4]f32,
		tint: [4]f32 = {1, 1, 1, 1},
	),
	// Hierarchical Grouping (Needed for SVG and complex UI)
	beginGroup:                proc(
		transform: matrix[3, 3]f32 = linalg.MATRIX3F32_IDENTITY,
		opacity: f32 = 1.0,
	),
	endGroup:                  proc(),
	// Gate 3.2: saveLayer / group compositing
	pushLayer:                 proc(x, y, w, h: f32, opacity: f32 = 1.0, blend_mode: Blend_Mode = .Normal),
	popLayer:                  proc(),

	// Phase DX: Asset Management
	loadAsset:                 proc(
		path: string,
		type: assets.Asset_Type,
	) -> (
		assets.LoadingHandle,
		bool,
	),
	parseAsset:                proc(
		loadingHandle: assets.LoadingHandle,
	) -> (
		assets.RenderHandle,
		bool,
	),
	renderAsset:               proc(
		handle: assets.RenderHandle,
		config: assets.Asset_Render_Config,
	) -> bool,
	defaultAssetRenderConfig:  proc() -> assets.Asset_Render_Config,
	defaultAssetManagerConfig: proc() -> assets.Asset_Manager_Config,
	initAssetManager:          proc(config: assets.Asset_Manager_Config) -> bool,
	shutdownAssetManager:      proc(),

	// Phase E: Gaussian Blur
	applyGaussianBlur:         proc(source, output, temp: ^Render_Target, radius: f32),

	// Phase DX: Render Target Management
	drawRenderTarget:          proc(rt: ^Render_Target, dx, dy, dw, dh: f32, color: [4]f32 = {1, 1, 1, 1}),

	// Phase DX: Rendering Pipeline
	beginPass:                 proc(pass: sg.Pass, pass_type: Renderer_Pass_Type),
	endPass:                   proc(),

	// Phase DX: Memory Management
	getArenaUsage:             proc() -> i32,
	getArenaAllocator:         proc() -> mem.Allocator,

	// Gate 1.3: Blend modes
	setBlendMode:              proc(mode: Blend_Mode),
	resetBlendMode:            proc(),

	// Gate 1.4: Dash patterns
	// array — dash/gap lengths in pixels; nil or empty to disable
	// offset — starting position within the pattern
	setDash:                   proc(array: []f32, offset: f32),
	resetDash:                 proc(),

	// Gate 1.5: Camera2D
	pushCamera2D:              proc(cam: Camera2D),
	popCamera2D:               proc(),

	// Gate 3.4: GPU callback command.
	// callback is invoked at the correct point in the sorted layer stream.
	// Use layer = .Content for normal order; .Overlay for top-most.
	// The active Sokol render pass is live when the callback fires.
	addGpuCallback:            proc(callback: proc(userdata: rawptr), userdata: rawptr, layer: Draw_Layer),

	// Gate 3.5: Draw-list recording and replay for golden-image CI tests.
	beginRecord: proc(path: string) -> bool,
	endRecord:   proc(),
	replay:      proc(path: string) -> bool,

	// Gate 3.3: Image filter API.
	// Captures all draws between setFilter/clearFilter to an offscreen RT,
	// applies the filter, then composites the result back to the parent target.
	setFilter:   proc(filter: Image_Filter, x, y, w, h: f32),
	clearFilter: proc(),

	// Gate 4.1: GPU instanced circle particles.
	// Submits all instances as a single draw call via the particle_instanced shader.
	// `instances` must remain valid until g2d_flush_gpu_pre_pass (frame-arena lifetime is fine).
	drawParticlesInstanced: proc(instances: []Particle_Instance, blend_mode: Blend_Mode, layer: Draw_Layer),
}

// Compositing blend modes.
// Normal (SRC_OVER) is the default.  Set before drawing; reset after.
Blend_Mode :: enum u8 {
	Normal,   // src*α + dst*(1-α)   — default Porter-Duff SRC_OVER
	Add,      // src*α + dst          — additive, for particles/glows
	Multiply, // src*dst + src*(1-α)  — darken, for shadows/overlays
	Screen,   // src + dst - src*dst  — lighten, for highlights
}

// Gate 3.3: Image filter types.
// Pass one of these to setFilter(); all draws until clearFilter() are captured,
// filtered, and composited back.
Filter_Blur :: struct {
	radius: f32, // blur radius in pixels
}

Filter_Drop_Shadow :: struct {
	offset_x, offset_y: f32,     // shadow offset in pixels
	blur:               f32,     // blur radius for the shadow
	color:              [4]f32,  // shadow color (including alpha)
}

Filter_Bloom :: struct {
	threshold: f32, // brightness threshold [0,1] for bloom extraction
	intensity: f32, // bloom intensity multiplier
}

Filter_Vignette :: struct {
	radius:   f32, // vignette falloff start radius (0–1 from centre, normalized)
	softness: f32, // feather width
}

// Discriminated union of all built-in image filter types.
Image_Filter :: union { Filter_Blur, Filter_Drop_Shadow, Filter_Bloom, Filter_Vignette }

// Gate 1.5: 2D camera / viewport transform.
// Push with pushCamera2D; all draws until popCamera2D are in camera space.
Camera2D :: struct {
	target:   [2]f32, // World point the camera looks at
	offset:   [2]f32, // Screen position of target (e.g. half screen for centred camera)
	rotation: f32,    // Rotation in radians applied around offset
	zoom:     f32,    // Scale factor (1.0 = no zoom)
}

// Convenience constructor: camera centred on screen, looking at world origin.
camera2d_default :: proc(screen_w, screen_h: f32) -> Camera2D {
	return Camera2D{offset = {screen_w * 0.5, screen_h * 0.5}, zoom = 1.0}
}

// Re-export selection quad type for helpers
Selection_Quad :: txt.Selection_Quad

active_renderer: ^Renderer

// Phase F.3: Global Draw List for deferred rendering
active_draw_list: Draw_List

// Global frame index synchronized by the active renderer implementation (g2d)
frame_index: u32

// Frame index management
setFrameIndex :: proc(fi: u32) {
	frame_index = fi
}

getFrameIndex :: proc() -> u32 {
	return frame_index
}

// Internal accessor for frame index (used by font atlas and other internal modules)
get_frame_index :: proc() -> u32 {
	return frame_index
}

// Global atlas override (pre-init; set BEFORE fonts initialize)
@(private)
atlas_w_override: i32
@(private)
atlas_h_override: i32

//Public APIs mostly for convienience

// Public API: set atlas size override (effective only before atlas_init_if_needed)
SetAtlasSizeOverride :: proc(w, h: i32) {
	if w > 0 && h > 0 {
		atlas_w_override = w
		atlas_h_override = h
	}
}

// Phase DX: Default Asset Render Configuration
defaultAssetRenderConfig :: proc() -> assets.Asset_Render_Config {
	return assets.Asset_Render_Config {
		x                   = 0.0,
		y                   = 0.0,
		width               = 1.0,
		height              = 1.0,
		allow_sync_fallback = true,
		tint_color          = {1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0}, // White, no tint
		opacity             = 1.0,
		preserve_aspect     = true,
	}
}

// Phase DX: Public API Wrappers for new vtable functions

loadAsset :: proc(path: string, type: assets.Asset_Type) -> (assets.LoadingHandle, bool) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		return active_renderer^.loadAsset(path, type)
	}
	return {}, false
}

parseAsset :: proc(loadingHandle: assets.LoadingHandle) -> (assets.RenderHandle, bool) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		return active_renderer^.parseAsset(loadingHandle)
	}
	return {}, false
}

renderAsset :: proc(handle: assets.RenderHandle, config: assets.Asset_Render_Config) -> bool {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		return active_renderer^.renderAsset(handle, config)
	}
	return false
}

drawRenderTarget :: proc(rt: ^Render_Target, dx, dy, dw, dh: f32, color: [4]f32 = {1, 1, 1, 1}) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.drawRenderTarget(rt, dx, dy, dw, dh, color)
	}
}

beginPass :: proc(pass: sg.Pass, pass_type: Renderer_Pass_Type) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.beginPass(pass, pass_type)
	}
}

endPass :: proc() {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.endPass()
	}
}

getArenaUsage :: proc() -> i32 {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		return active_renderer^.getArenaUsage()
	}
	return 0
}

getArenaAllocator :: proc() -> mem.Allocator {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		return active_renderer^.getArenaAllocator()
	}
	// Return a default allocator - this is a fallback and shouldn't normally be used
	return mem.Allocator{}
}

initAssetManager :: proc(config: assets.Asset_Manager_Config) -> bool {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		return active_renderer^.initAssetManager(config)
	}
	return false
}

shutdownAssetManager :: proc() {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.shutdownAssetManager()
	}
}

// Internal: expose current override to other renderer files
getAtlasSizeOverride :: proc() -> (i32, i32) {
	return atlas_w_override, atlas_h_override
}

// Clean API wrappers for renderer configuration (Phase 10D.1)
setLogging :: proc(mode: DoLogging) {
	g2d_set_logging(mode)
}

// Phase 10-01.1: Direct implementation of flushGpuPrePass
flushGpuPrePass :: proc() {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.flushGpuPrePass()
	}
}

// Phase 10-01.2: Direct implementation of setColor
setColor :: proc(r, g, b, a: f32) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.setColor(r, g, b, a)
	}
}

		active_renderer^.drawRect(x, y, w, h, thickness, color, shadow)
	}

	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.fillRect(x, y, w, h, paint_solid(color))
	}

// 	// Removed in favor of Paint struct

// Public API: draw a rounded rectangle (stroke)
drawRectRounded :: proc(x, y, w, h, radius, thickness: f32, color: [4]f32, shadow: Shadow = {}) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.drawRectRounded(x, y, w, h, radius, thickness, color, shadow)
	}
}


// Phase F.2 — Public API: directional drop shadow for rounded rect
// Draw BEFORE the element fill so the card covers the shadow center.
// offset_x/offset_y: light direction (0, 4–8 typical for downward light)
// blur: spread in pixels (2=tight, 12=soft, 24=very diffuse)
dropShadowRect :: proc(x, y, w, h, radius, offset_x, offset_y, blur: f32, color: [4]f32) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.dropShadowRect(x, y, w, h, radius, offset_x, offset_y, blur, color)
	}
}

// Phase F.2 — Public API: directional drop shadow for a circle
dropShadowCircle :: proc(cx, cy, radius, offset_x, offset_y, blur: f32, color: [4]f32) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.dropShadowCircle(cx, cy, radius, offset_x, offset_y, blur, color)
	}
}

// Phase F.2 — Public API: directional drop shadow for an N-gon (hexagon, etc.)
dropShadowNgon :: proc(cx, cy, radius: f32, sides: int, offset_x, offset_y, blur: f32, color: [4]f32) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.dropShadowNgon(cx, cy, radius, sides, offset_x, offset_y, blur, color)
	}
}

// Phase F.2 — Public API: inset shadow for a rect (pressed/recessed look).
// Draw AFTER the element fill and BEFORE content.
// top_depth:  height of dark band at top edge (pixels)
// side_depth: width of dark band at left edge (pixels)
insetShadowRect :: proc(x, y, w, h, top_depth, side_depth: f32, color: [4]f32) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.insetShadowRect(x, y, w, h, top_depth, side_depth, color)
	}
}

fillTriangle :: proc(x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, y3: f32, paint: Paint, shadow: Shadow = {}) {

// Phase 10-01.17: CamelCase wrapper for load_image
loadImage :: proc(path: string) -> Image_Handle {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		return active_renderer^.loadImage(path)
	}
	return Image_Handle_Invalid
}

// Phase 10-01.18: CamelCase wrapper for destroy_image
destroyImage :: proc(img: Image_Handle) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.destroyImage(img)
	}
}

// Phase 10-01.19: CamelCase wrapper for get_image_info
getImageInfo :: proc(img: Image_Handle) -> (width, height: i32) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		return active_renderer^.getImageInfo(img)
	}
	return 0, 0
}

// Phase 10C6.3.b - Clean interface aliases for web-friendly naming
createMaterial :: proc(image: Image_Handle, tint: [4]f32) -> G2D_Material {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		return active_renderer^.createMaterial(image, tint)
	}
	return G2D_Material{image = image, tint = tint}
}

createMaterialSolid :: proc(tint: [4]f32) -> G2D_Material {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		return active_renderer^.createMaterialSolid(tint)
	}
	// For backward compatibility, create a solid material without a specific white image
	// The actual white image will be handled by the renderer implementation
	return G2D_Material{image = Image_Handle_Invalid, tint = tint}
}

// Stress test functions
runLayoutStressTest :: proc(count, min_len, max_len: int, color: [4]f32) {
	// no-op
}


// Helper: draw selection background quads behind text
// This is an additive helper (no vtable churn). It uses the active renderer's fill_rect.
drawTextSelection :: proc(x, y: f32, quads: []Selection_Quad, color: [4]f32) {
	if active_renderer == nil || len(quads) == 0 {
		return
	}
	for q in quads {
		active_renderer^.fillRect(x + q.x, y + q.y, q.w, q.h, paint_solid(color))
	}
}

// ============================================================================
// Phase 10D.3.2 - Perspective Transform Implementation
// ============================================================================

// Default implementation of perspective transform functions
// These will be overridden by specific renderer backends if needed

// Apply perspective projection matrix
default_applyPerspective :: proc(fov: f32, aspect: f32, near: f32, far: f32) {
	// Create perspective projection matrix
	perspective_matrix := createPerspectiveMatrix(fov, aspect, near, far)
	default_applyPerspectiveMatrix(perspective_matrix)
}

// Apply custom perspective matrix
default_applyPerspectiveMatrix :: proc(m: matrix[4, 4]f32) {
	// In a real implementation, this would apply the perspective matrix to the renderer
	// For now, we'll just store it or apply it to the current matrix stack
	// This is a placeholder implementation
	fmt.println("Applying perspective matrix (default implementation)")
}

// Push current perspective state
default_pushPerspective :: proc() {
	// In a real implementation, this would push the current perspective state onto a stack
	fmt.println("Pushing perspective state (default implementation)")
}

// Pop perspective state
default_popPerspective :: proc() {
	// In a real implementation, this would pop the perspective state from the stack
	fmt.println("Popping perspective state (default implementation)")
}

// Create perspective projection matrix
createPerspectiveMatrix :: proc(fov: f32, aspect: f32, near: f32, far: f32) -> matrix[4, 4]f32 {
	// Standard perspective projection matrix
	// Based on: https://www.scratchapixel.com/lessons/3d-basic-rendering/perspective-and-orthographic-projection-matrix/building-basic-perspective-projection-matrix

	tan_fov := math.tan(fov * 0.5)
	scale := 1.0 / tan_fov

	m00 := scale / aspect
	m11 := scale
	m22 := far / (far - near)
	m23 := f32(1.0)
	m32 := -near * far / (far - near)

	// Create matrix using explicit initialization
	result := matrix[4, 4]f32{}
	result[0][0] = m00
	result[1][1] = m11
	result[2][2] = m22
	result[2][3] = m23
	result[3][2] = m32
	return result
}

// Create orthographic projection matrix
createOrthographicMatrix :: proc(left, right, bottom, top, near, far: f32) -> matrix[4, 4]f32 {
	m00 := 2.0 / (right - left)
	m11 := 2.0 / (top - bottom)
	m22 := 1.0 / (far - near)
	m30 := (left + right) / (left - right)
	m31 := (bottom + top) / (bottom - top)
	m32 := near / (near - far)

	// Create matrix using explicit initialization
	result := matrix[4, 4]f32{}
	result[0][0] = m00
	result[1][1] = m11
	result[2][2] = m22
	result[3][0] = m30
	result[3][1] = m31
	result[3][2] = m32
	result[3][3] = 1.0
	return result
}

// Create view matrix (look-at)
createViewMatrix :: proc(eye, center, up: [3]f32) -> matrix[4, 4]f32 {
	f := normalize3(eye - center)
	s := normalize3(cross3(f, up))
	u := cross3(s, f)

	m00 := s[0]
	m01 := u[0]
	m02 := -f[0]
	m10 := s[1]
	m11 := u[1]
	m12 := -f[1]
	m20 := s[2]
	m21 := u[2]
	m22 := -f[2]
	m30 := -dot3(s, eye)
	m31 := -dot3(u, eye)
	m32 := dot3(f, eye)

	// Create matrix using explicit initialization
	result := matrix[4, 4]f32{}
	result[0][0] = m00
	result[0][1] = m01
	result[0][2] = m02
	result[1][0] = m10
	result[1][1] = m11
	result[1][2] = m12
	result[2][0] = m20
	result[2][1] = m21
	result[2][2] = m22
	result[3][0] = m30
	result[3][1] = m31
	result[3][2] = m32
	result[3][3] = 1.0
	return result
}

// Phase 10D.8.4 --- Path Fill Wrapper
pathFill :: proc(paint: Paint) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.pathFill(paint)
	}
}

// Public API: draw a path (stroke)
pathDraw :: proc(thickness: f32, color: [4]f32, join: Line_Join = .Miter, cap: Line_Cap = .Butt) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.pathDraw(thickness, color, join, cap)
	}
}

pathStroke :: proc(stroke: Stroke) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.pathStroke(stroke)
	}
}

fillBezierQuadratic :: proc(x1, y1, cx, cy, x2, y2: f32, paint: Paint) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.fillBezierQuadratic(x1, y1, cx, cy, x2, y2, paint)
	}
}

fillBezierCubic :: proc(x1, y1, c1x, c1y, c2x, c2y, x2, y2: f32, paint: Paint) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.fillBezierCubic(x1, y1, c1x, c1y, c2x, c2y, x2, y2, paint)
	}
}

// --- New Beautification Primitive Wrappers ---

fillNgon :: proc(x, y, radius: f32, sides: int, paint: Paint, shadow: Shadow = {}) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.fillNgon(x, y, radius, sides, paint, shadow)
	}
}

drawNgon :: proc(x, y, radius, thickness: f32, sides: int, color: [4]f32, shadow: Shadow = {}) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.drawNgon(x, y, radius, thickness, sides, color, shadow)
	}
}

fillPie :: proc(x, y, radius, start_angle, end_angle: f32, paint: Paint, shadow: Shadow = {}) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.fillPie(x, y, radius, start_angle, end_angle, paint, shadow)
	}
}

fillStar :: proc(
	x, y: f32,
	num_points: int,
	inner_radius, outer_radius: f32,
	paint: Paint,
	shadow: Shadow = {},
) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		pts := math.max(num_points, 5)
		active_renderer^.fillStar(x, y, pts, inner_radius, outer_radius, paint, shadow)
	}
}

drawStar :: proc(
	x, y: f32,
	num_points: int,
	inner_radius, outer_radius, thickness: f32,
	color: [4]f32,
	shadow: Shadow = {},
) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		pts := math.max(num_points, 5)
		active_renderer^.drawStar(x, y, pts, inner_radius, outer_radius, thickness, color, shadow)
	}
}

fillRing :: proc(x, y, inner_radius, outer_radius: f32, paint: Paint, shadow: Shadow = {}) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.fillRing(x, y, inner_radius, outer_radius, paint, shadow)
	}
}

drawRing :: proc(
	x, y: f32,
	inner_radius, inner_thickness: f32,
	inner_color: [4]f32,
	outer_radius, outer_thickness: f32,
	outer_color: [4]f32,
	shadow: Shadow = {},
) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.drawRing(
			x,
			y,
			inner_radius,
			inner_thickness,
			inner_color,
			outer_radius,
			outer_thickness,
			outer_color,
			shadow,
		)
	}
}

fillWedge :: proc(
	x, y, radius, start_angle, end_angle: f32,
	paint: Paint,
	clockwise: bool = true,
	shadow: Shadow = {},
) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.fillWedge(x, y, radius, start_angle, end_angle, paint, clockwise, shadow)
	}
}

drawWedge :: proc(
	x, y, radius, thickness: f32,
	start_angle, end_angle: f32,
	color: [4]f32,
	clockwise: bool = true,
	shadow: Shadow = {},
) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.drawWedge(
			x,
			y,
			radius,
			thickness,
			start_angle,
			end_angle,
			color,
			clockwise,
			shadow,
		)
	}
}

// Convenience Gradient Wrappers
fillRectGradient :: proc(x, y, w, h: f32, c_tl, c_tr, c_br, c_bl: [4]f32) {
	// Simple map to 4-point linear gradient or solid if same (legacy behavior was 4-corner multicolor)
	// We'll use a linear gradient approximation or solid color for now as Paint system matures
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.fillRect(x, y, w, h, paint_solid(c_tl))
	}
}

fillRectRoundedGradient :: proc(
	x, y, w, h, radius: f32,
	c_top, c_bottom: [4]f32,
	vertical: bool = true,
) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		start := [2]f32{x, y}
		end := vertical ? [2]f32{x, y + h} : [2]f32{x + w, y}
		paint := paint_linear(start, end, {c_top, c_bottom}, {0, 1})
		active_renderer^.fillRectRounded(x, y, w, h, radius, paint)
	}
}

drawTextOnPath :: proc(text: string, font: Font_Handle, color: [4]f32) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.drawTextOnPath(text, font, color)
	}
}

// Public API: replace clip region
setClip :: proc(x, y, w, h: f32) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.setClip(x, y, w, h)
	}
}

// Public API: reset clip region
resetClip :: proc() {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.resetClip()
	}
}

// Gate 1.3: Blend mode public wrappers
setBlendMode :: proc(mode: Blend_Mode) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.setBlendMode(mode)
	}
}
resetBlendMode :: proc() {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.resetBlendMode()
	}
}

// Gate 1.5: Camera2D public wrappers
pushCamera2D :: proc(cam: Camera2D) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.pushCamera2D(cam)
	}
}
popCamera2D :: proc() {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.popCamera2D()
	}
}

// Gate 1.4: Dash pattern public wrappers
setDash :: proc(array: []f32, offset: f32 = 0) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.setDash(array, offset)
	}
}
resetDash :: proc() {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.resetDash()
	}
}

// Public API: hard clip region
pushClip :: proc(x, y, w, h: f32) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.pushClip(x, y, w, h)
	}
}

// Public API: pop clip region
popClip :: proc() {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.popClip()
	}
}

// Phase 10D.2.3 --- Path-Based Clipping APIs ---

// Public API: begin a logical group with transformation and optional opacity
beginGroup :: proc(transform: matrix[3, 3]f32 = linalg.MATRIX3F32_IDENTITY, opacity: f32 = 1.0) {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.beginGroup(transform, opacity)
	}
}

// Public API: end the current group
endGroup :: proc() {
	if active_renderer != nil {
		active_renderer^.endGroup()
	}
}

// Gate 3.2: Public API — saveLayer / group compositing
// Redirect subsequent drawing to an offscreen layer; composite back on popLayer.
pushLayer :: proc(x, y, w, h: f32, opacity: f32 = 1.0, blend_mode: Blend_Mode = .Normal) {
	if active_renderer != nil && active_renderer^.pushLayer != nil {
		active_renderer^.pushLayer(x, y, w, h, opacity, blend_mode)
	}
}

// Gate 3.2: Close the layer opened by pushLayer and composite to parent.
popLayer :: proc() {
	if active_renderer != nil && active_renderer^.popLayer != nil {
		active_renderer^.popLayer()
	}
}

// Gate 3.3: Redirect subsequent draws to an offscreen RT; filter applied on clearFilter.
// x/y/w/h is the destination rect in parent-space (usually full screen).
setFilter :: proc(filter: Image_Filter, x, y, w, h: f32) {
	if active_renderer != nil && active_renderer^.setFilter != nil {
		active_renderer^.setFilter(filter, x, y, w, h)
	}
}

// Gate 3.3: Apply the filter set by setFilter and composite the result back.
clearFilter :: proc() {
	if active_renderer != nil && active_renderer^.clearFilter != nil {
		active_renderer^.clearFilter()
	}
}

import sg "vendor-local:sokol-odin/sokol/gfx"
import "core:mem"
import "core:math/linalg"
import assets "project:assets"

Draw_Layer :: enum {
	Offscreen,  // Shadow silhouettes, blur passes
	Background, // Fills, images behind content
	Content,    // Primitives, shapes (default)
	Overlay,    // Text, tooltips, debug UI
}

Draw_List :: struct {
	commands: [dynamic]Draw_Command,
	layers:   [Draw_Layer][dynamic]int, // indices into commands[], per layer
	arena:    mem.Arena,
}

Draw_Command :: union {
	Cmd_Draw_Render_Target,
	Cmd_Draw_Text_Layout,
	Cmd_Draw_Text_On_Path,
	Cmd_Fill_Rect,
	Cmd_Fill_Rect_Rounded,
	Cmd_Draw_Rect,
	Cmd_Draw_Rect_Rounded,
	Cmd_Draw_Line,
	Cmd_Draw_Circle,
	Cmd_Fill_Circle,
	Cmd_Draw_Ellipse,
	Cmd_Fill_Ellipse,
	Cmd_Fill_Triangle,
	Cmd_Draw_Triangle,
	Cmd_Draw_Polyline,
	Cmd_Fill_Polygon,
	Cmd_Draw_Polygon,
	Cmd_Fill_Pie,
	Cmd_Draw_Arc,
	Cmd_Fill_Arc,
	Cmd_Fill_Star,
	Cmd_Draw_Star,
	Cmd_Fill_Ring,
	Cmd_Draw_Ring,
	Cmd_Fill_Wedge,
	Cmd_Draw_Wedge,
	Cmd_Draw_Point,
	Cmd_Draw_Point_Scale,
	Cmd_Draw_Text,
	Cmd_Draw_Text_Advanced,
	Cmd_Draw_Asset,
	Cmd_Draw_Image,
	Cmd_Draw_Image_Rect,
	Cmd_Draw_Image_Rect_UV_Mat,
	Cmd_Shadow_Rect,
	Cmd_Shadow_Circle,
	Cmd_Shadow_Ngon,
	Cmd_Shadow_Silhouette,
	Cmd_Inset_Shadow_Rect,
	Cmd_Fill_Bezier_Quadratic,
	Cmd_Fill_Bezier_Cubic,
	Cmd_Draw_Bezier_Quadratic,
	Cmd_Draw_Bezier_Cubic,
	Cmd_Begin_Pass,
	Cmd_End_Pass,
	Cmd_Path_Fill,
	Cmd_Path_Draw,
	Cmd_Path_Stroke,
	Cmd_Draw_Ngon,
	Cmd_Stroke_Gradient,
	Cmd_Set_Clip,
	Cmd_Reset_Clip,
	Cmd_Push_Matrix,
	Cmd_Pop_Matrix,
	Cmd_Identity_Matrix,
	Cmd_Translate,
	Cmd_Rotate,
	Cmd_Scale,
	Cmd_Apply_Matrix,
	Cmd_Apply_Matrix4,
	// Phase 12 & 13
	Cmd_Begin_Mask,
	Cmd_End_Mask,
	Cmd_Begin_Masked_Draw,
	Cmd_End_Masked_Draw,
	Cmd_Apply_Box_Blur,
	Cmd_Apply_Bloom,
	Cmd_Apply_Vignette,
	// Gate 3.4: GPU callback — called at correct point in sorted layer stream
	Cmd_GPU_Callback,
	// Gate 3.2: saveLayer / group compositing
	Cmd_Push_Layer,
	Cmd_Pop_Layer,
	// Gate 3.3: image filter API
	Cmd_Set_Filter,
	Cmd_Clear_Filter,
	// Gate 4.1: GPU instanced particles
	Cmd_Draw_Particles_Instanced,
}

Path_Point_Type :: enum {
	Move_To,
	Line_To,
	Arc_To,
	Quad_To,
	Cubic_To,
	Close_Path,
}

Path_Point :: struct {
	type: Path_Point_Type,
	p:    [2]f32,
	c1:   [2]f32, // Control point 1 (Quad/Cubic) or Arc (Radius, StartAngle)
	c2:   [2]f32, // Control point 2 (Cubic) or Arc (EndAngle, Clockwise)
}

Cmd_Draw_Render_Target :: struct {
	common:     Draw_Command_Common,
	rt:         ^Render_Target,
	x, y, w, h: f32,
	color:      [4]f32,
}

// Borrowed slices: lines and y_offsets are backed by the frame arena and are valid
// for the entire frame (until the next g2d_begin_frame). No deep copy needed.
Cmd_Draw_Text_Layout :: struct {
	common:    Draw_Command_Common,
	x, y:      f32,
	lines:     []Visual_Line,
	y_offsets: []f32,
	color:     [4]f32,
	mods:      Text_Modifiers,
}

// points is a copy of ctx.current_path at record time, stored in the draw list arena.
Cmd_Draw_Text_On_Path :: struct {
	common:  Draw_Command_Common,
	text:    string,
	font:    Font_Handle,
	color:   [4]f32,
	points:  []Path_Point,
}

Cmd_Inset_Shadow_Rect :: struct {
	common: Draw_Command_Common,
	x, y, w, h, top_depth, side_depth: f32,
	color: [4]f32,
}

Cmd_Begin_Pass :: struct {
	common: Draw_Command_Common,
	pass: sg.Pass,
	pass_type: Renderer_Pass_Type,
}

Cmd_End_Pass :: struct {
	common: Draw_Command_Common,
}

// Common properties for drawing commands
Draw_Command_Common :: struct {
	layer:         Draw_Layer,
	opacity:       f32,
	target:        rawptr, // Pointer to the render target (or nil for screen)
	flushed:       bool,   // Whether this command has already been executed
	transform:     matrix[3, 3]f32,
	scissor:       [4]f32,
	scissor_on:    bool,
	stencil_level: u8,
	pip_variant:   u8,
}

// Helper to get common fields from any command
draw_command_get_common :: proc(cmd: ^Draw_Command) -> ^Draw_Command_Common {
	switch &c in cmd {
	case Cmd_Draw_Render_Target: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Text_Layout: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Text_On_Path: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Fill_Rect: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Fill_Rect_Rounded: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Rect: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Rect_Rounded: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Line: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Circle: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Fill_Circle: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Ellipse: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Fill_Ellipse: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Fill_Triangle: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Triangle: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Polyline: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Fill_Polygon: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Polygon: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Fill_Pie: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Arc: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Fill_Arc: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Fill_Star: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Star: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Fill_Ring: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Ring: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Fill_Wedge: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Wedge: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Point: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Point_Scale: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Text: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Text_Advanced: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Asset: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Image: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Image_Rect: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Image_Rect_UV_Mat: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Shadow_Rect: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Shadow_Circle: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Shadow_Ngon: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Shadow_Silhouette: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Inset_Shadow_Rect: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Fill_Bezier_Quadratic: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Fill_Bezier_Cubic: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Bezier_Quadratic: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Bezier_Cubic: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Begin_Pass: return &c.common
	case Cmd_End_Pass: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Path_Fill: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Path_Draw: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Path_Stroke: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Ngon: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Stroke_Gradient: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Set_Clip: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Reset_Clip: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Push_Matrix: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Pop_Matrix: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Identity_Matrix: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Translate: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Rotate: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Scale: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Apply_Matrix: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Apply_Matrix4: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Begin_Mask: return &c.common
	case Cmd_End_Mask: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Begin_Masked_Draw: return &c.common
	case Cmd_End_Masked_Draw: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Apply_Box_Blur: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Apply_Bloom: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Apply_Vignette: return &c.common
	case Cmd_GPU_Callback: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Push_Layer: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Pop_Layer: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Set_Filter: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Clear_Filter: return &c.common
	case Cmd_Draw_Particles_Instanced: return &c.common
	}
	return nil
}

Cmd_Fill_Rect :: struct {
	common: Draw_Command_Common,
	x, y, w, h: f32,
	paint: Paint,
}

Cmd_Fill_Rect_Rounded :: struct {
	common: Draw_Command_Common,
	x, y, w, h, radius: f32,
	paint: Paint,
}

Cmd_Draw_Rect :: struct {
	common: Draw_Command_Common,
	x, y, w, h, thickness: f32,
	color: [4]f32,
}

Cmd_Draw_Rect_Rounded :: struct {
	common: Draw_Command_Common,
	x, y, w, h, radius, thickness: f32,
	color: [4]f32,
}

Cmd_Draw_Line :: struct {
	common: Draw_Command_Common,
	x1, y1, x2, y2, thickness: f32,
	color: [4]f32,
	cap: Line_Cap,
}

Cmd_Fill_Circle :: struct {
	common: Draw_Command_Common,
	x, y, radius: f32,
	paint: Paint,
}

Cmd_Draw_Circle :: struct {
	common: Draw_Command_Common,
	x, y, radius, thickness: f32,
	color: [4]f32,

Cmd_Fill_Ellipse :: struct {

}

	radius, softness: f32,

// Gate 3.4: GPU callback command.
// callback is invoked at the correct point in the sorted layer stream,
// enabling custom sokol_gfx draw calls without breaking batching.
// Modelled on ImGui's AddCallback().  The current Sokol render pass is active
// when the callback fires — call sg.apply_pipeline / sg.draw etc. directly.
Cmd_GPU_Callback :: struct {
	common:   Draw_Command_Common,
	callback: proc(userdata: rawptr),
	userdata: rawptr,
}

// Gate 3.2: Capture all subsequent draw commands to an offscreen RT.
// On Cmd_Pop_Layer the RT is blitted back to the parent target with opacity / blend_mode.
// x/y/w/h is the destination rect in parent-space (default: full screen).
Cmd_Push_Layer :: struct {
	common:     Draw_Command_Common,
	x, y, w, h: f32,            // dest rect when compositing back (usually full-screen)
	opacity:    f32,             // [0,1] group opacity applied at composite time
	blend_mode: Blend_Mode,      // compositing mode for the blit-back
}

// Gate 3.2: Close the layer opened by the matching Cmd_Push_Layer.
// No fields beyond common — all parameters are in the matching push, stored in layer_stack.
Cmd_Pop_Layer :: struct {
	common: Draw_Command_Common,
}

// Gate 3.3: Redirect all subsequent draws to a new offscreen RT; filter applied on Cmd_Clear_Filter.
// x/y/w/h is the destination rect for the composite-back (usually full screen).
Cmd_Set_Filter :: struct {
	common:     Draw_Command_Common,
	filter:     Image_Filter,
	x, y, w, h: f32,
}

// Gate 3.3: Apply the filter recorded in the matching Cmd_Set_Filter and composite back.
Cmd_Clear_Filter :: struct {
	common: Draw_Command_Common,
}

// Gate 4.1: Draw N circle particles using a single GPU-instanced draw call.
// `instances` points into frame-arena memory; never freed manually.
// `base_byte_offset` is filled during g2d_flush_gpu_pre_pass (before the screen pass)
// by appending instance data to the instance buffer via sg.append_buffer.
Cmd_Draw_Particles_Instanced :: struct {
	common:           Draw_Command_Common,
	instances:        []Particle_Instance, // frame-allocated slice
	base_byte_offset: u32,                 // byte offset into instance buffer (set pre-pass)
	blend_mode:       Blend_Mode,
}

draw_list_init :: proc(dl: ^Draw_List) {
	dl.commands = make([dynamic]Draw_Command, 0, 1024)
	for layer in Draw_Layer {
		dl.layers[layer] = make([dynamic]int, 0, 256)
	}
	
	// 4MB for path points and other temporary data
	buf := make([]u8, 4 * 1024 * 1024)
	mem.arena_init(&dl.arena, buf)
}

draw_list_clear :: proc(dl: ^Draw_List) {
	clear(&dl.commands)
	for layer in Draw_Layer {
		clear(&dl.layers[layer])
	}
	dl.arena.offset = 0
}

draw_list_push :: proc(dl: ^Draw_List, cmd: Draw_Command) {
	idx := len(dl.commands)
	append(&dl.commands, cmd)
	
	layer: Draw_Layer
	switch c in cmd {
	case Cmd_Draw_Render_Target: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Text_Layout: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Text_On_Path: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Fill_Rect: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Fill_Rect_Rounded: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Rect: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Rect_Rounded: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Line: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Circle: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Fill_Circle: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Ellipse: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Fill_Ellipse: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Fill_Triangle: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Triangle: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Polyline: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Fill_Polygon: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Polygon: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Fill_Pie: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Arc: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Fill_Arc: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Fill_Star: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Star: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Fill_Ring: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Ring: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Fill_Wedge: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Wedge: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Point: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Point_Scale: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Text: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Text_Advanced: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Asset: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Image: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Image_Rect: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Image_Rect_UV_Mat: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Set_Clip: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Reset_Clip: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Push_Matrix: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Pop_Matrix: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Identity_Matrix: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Translate: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Rotate: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Scale: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Apply_Matrix: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Apply_Matrix4: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Shadow_Rect: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Shadow_Circle: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Shadow_Ngon: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Shadow_Silhouette: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Inset_Shadow_Rect: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Fill_Bezier_Quadratic: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Fill_Bezier_Cubic: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Bezier_Quadratic: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Bezier_Cubic: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Begin_Pass: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_End_Pass: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Path_Fill: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Path_Draw: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Ngon: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Stroke_Gradient: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Path_Stroke: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Begin_Mask: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_End_Mask: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Begin_Masked_Draw: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_End_Masked_Draw: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Apply_Box_Blur: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Apply_Bloom: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Apply_Vignette: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_GPU_Callback: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Push_Layer: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Pop_Layer: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Set_Filter: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Clear_Filter: layer = c.common.layer
	case Cmd_Draw_Particles_Instanced: layer = c.common.layer
	}

	append(&dl.layers[layer], idx)
}

draw_list_copy_points :: proc(dl: ^Draw_List, points: [][2]f32) -> [][2]f32 {
	if len(points) == 0 do return nil
	res := make([][2]f32, len(points), draw_list_allocator(dl))
	copy(res, points)
	return res
}

draw_list_copy_path_points :: proc(dl: ^Draw_List, points: []Path_Point) -> []Path_Point {
	if len(points) == 0 do return nil
	res := make([]Path_Point, len(points), draw_list_allocator(dl))
	copy(res, points)
	return res
}

draw_list_allocator :: proc(dl: ^Draw_List) -> mem.Allocator {
	return mem.arena_allocator(&dl.arena)
}

draw_list_destroy :: proc(dl: ^Draw_List) {
	delete(dl.commands)
	for layer in Draw_Layer {
		delete(dl.layers[layer])
	}
	delete(dl.arena.data)
}

DEBUG: Source length: 3348
DEBUG: Source content: package renderer

import log "project:logging"
import fmt "core:fmt"

// Renderer logging shim that delegates to the central logging hub.
// Prefer enabling/disabling via g2d_set_logging, or drive tags directly with:
//   log.enable("renderer.shaping"), log.enable("renderer.fonts"), log.disable(...)

DoLogging :: enum {
    None,
    Fonts,
    Shaping,
    All
}

// Central tag names for this package
RENDER_TAG_SHAPING :: "renderer.shaping"
RENDER_TAG_FONTS   :: "renderer.fonts"

// Remember last requested mode (optional)
g2d_logging_mode: DoLogging = .None

// Public: set renderer logging mode; delegates to central logging tags.
g2d_set_logging :: proc(mode: DoLogging) {
    g2d_logging_mode = mode
    switch mode {
    case .None:
        log.disable(RENDER_TAG_SHAPING)
        log.disable(RENDER_TAG_FONTS)
    case .Fonts:
        log.enable(RENDER_TAG_FONTS)
        log.disable(RENDER_TAG_SHAPING)
    case .Shaping:
        log.enable(RENDER_TAG_SHAPING)
        log.disable(RENDER_TAG_FONTS)
    case .All:
        log.enable(RENDER_TAG_SHAPING)
        log.enable(RENDER_TAG_FONTS)
    }
}

@(private)
map_tag :: proc(kind: DoLogging) -> string {
    tag := ""
    #partial switch kind {
    case .Fonts:
        tag = RENDER_TAG_FONTS
    case .Shaping:
        tag = RENDER_TAG_SHAPING
    case:
        tag = ""
    }
    return tag
}

// Public: query if a renderer logging category should emit output (reads central tags).
g2d_should_log :: proc(kind: DoLogging) -> bool {
    t := map_tag(kind)
    if len(t) == 0 {
        // .All means any of the renderer tags
        if kind == .All {
            return log.should(RENDER_TAG_SHAPING) || log.should(RENDER_TAG_FONTS)
        }
        return false
    }
    return log.should(t)
}

// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Verbose shaping safe logger (throttled + truncation)
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

// Configurable caps (override via -define:SHAPE_LOG_MAX_CHARS=..., etc.)
SHAPE_LOG_MAX_CHARS  :: #config(SHAPE_LOG_MAX_CHARS, 512)
SHAPE_LOG_BURST      :: #config(SHAPE_LOG_BURST, 200)
SHAPE_LOG_BACKOFF    :: #config(SHAPE_LOG_BACKOFF, 50)

@(private)
shaping_total     : i64 = 0
@(private)
shaping_suppressed: i64 = 0

// Internal: truncate long messages and annotate remainder
@(private)
truncate_msg :: proc(msg: string) -> string {
    if len(msg) <= SHAPE_LOG_MAX_CHARS {
        return msg
    }
    extra := len(msg) - SHAPE_LOG_MAX_CHARS
    head := msg[:SHAPE_LOG_MAX_CHARS]
    return fmt.tprintf("%s...(+%d chars)", head, extra)
}

// Public: safe print for verbose shaping
g2d_log_shaping :: proc(msg: string) {
    if !g2d_should_log(.Shaping) {
        return
    }
    shaping_total += 1
    allow := (shaping_total <= SHAPE_LOG_BURST) || (((shaping_total - SHAPE_LOG_BURST) % SHAPE_LOG_BACKOFF) == 0)
    if allow {
        s := truncate_msg(msg)
        fmt.println("[SHAPE] ", s)
        if shaping_suppressed > 0 {
            fmt.println("[SHAPE] (suppressed ", shaping_suppressed, " messages)")
            shaping_suppressed = 0
        }
    } else {
        shaping_suppressed += 1
    }
}

// Public: produce a preview-safe, truncated copy of arbitrary text for logging
g2d_preview :: proc(s: string) -> string {
    return truncate_msg(s)
}

DEBUG: Source content: #+build !js
package renderer

import "core:os"

// Check if a file exists on native platforms using os.stat.
file_exists :: proc(path: string) -> bool {
	file_info, err := os.stat(path, context.temp_allocator)
	if err != nil {
		return false
	}
	return file_info.type != .Directory
}

package renderer

// On WASM, Emscripten's preloaded VFS always provides assets — no file-exists check needed.
file_exists :: proc(path: string) -> bool {
	return true
}

import "core:math"
import "core:math/linalg"
import sg "vendor-local:sokol-odin/sokol/gfx"

// Phase 10D.7.1: Unified Paint System
// Replacing separate fill/gradient functions with a single Paint struct

Paint_Type :: enum {
	Solid,
	Linear_Gradient,
	Radial_Gradient,
	Conical_Gradient,
	Pattern,
	Image,
}

Paint :: struct {
	type:            Paint_Type,
	opacity:         f32, // Defaults to 1.0

	// Solid
	color:           [4]f32,

	// Gradient Shared
	gradient_stops:  []f32,
	gradient_colors: [][4]f32,

	// Linear
	linear_start:    [2]f32,
	linear_end:      [2]f32,

	// Radial, Conical, Pattern, Image
	center:          [2]f32, // Used for radial/conical center, or pattern/image anchor

	// Radial
	radius:          f32,

	// Conical
	start_angle:     f32,

	// Pattern & Image
	image:           Image_Handle, // For texturing (Phase DX: changed from sg.Image)
	uv_transform:    matrix[3, 3]f32, // For scaling/rotating patterns/images
	repeat:          bool, // For tiling

	// Phase 11.3: Fill Rules
	fill_rule:       Fill_Rule, // NonZero or EvenOdd
}

Fill_Rule :: enum {
	NonZero,
	EvenOdd,
}

Shadow :: struct {
	color:  [4]f32,
	offset: [2]f32,
	blur:   f32, // Optional, for soft shadows
}

// --- Stroke System ---

Stroke :: struct {
	width:          f32,
	paint:          Paint,
	cap:            Line_Cap,
	join:           Line_Join,
	miter_limit:    f32,
	dash_array:     []f32,
	dash_offset:    f32,
	// Support for two-color patterns (Phase 11 requested)
	dash_paint:     Paint,
	has_dash_paint: bool,
}

stroke_simple :: proc(width: f32, color: [4]f32) -> Stroke {
	return Stroke {
		width = width,
		paint = paint_solid(color),
		cap = .Butt,
		join = .Miter,
		miter_limit = 4.0,
	}
}

// --- Helper Functions ---

paint_solid :: proc(c: [4]f32) -> Paint {
	return Paint{type = .Solid, color = c, opacity = 1.0}
}

paint_linear :: proc(start, end: [2]f32, colors: [][4]f32, stops: []f32) -> Paint {
	return Paint {
		type = .Linear_Gradient,
		linear_start = start,
		linear_end = end,
		gradient_colors = colors,
		gradient_stops = stops,
		opacity = 1.0,
	}
}

paint_radial :: proc(center: [2]f32, radius: f32, colors: [][4]f32, stops: []f32) -> Paint {
	return Paint {
		type = .Radial_Gradient,
		center = center,
		radius = radius,
		gradient_colors = colors,
		gradient_stops = stops,
		opacity = 1.0,
	}
}

paint_conical :: proc(center: [2]f32, start_angle: f32, colors: [][4]f32, stops: []f32) -> Paint {
	return Paint {
		type = .Conical_Gradient,
		center = center,
		start_angle = start_angle,
		gradient_colors = colors,
		gradient_stops = stops,
		opacity = 1.0,
	}
}

paint_image :: proc(handle: Image_Handle, opacity: f32 = 1.0) -> Paint {
	return Paint {
		type         = .Image,
		image        = handle,
		opacity      = opacity,
		uv_transform = linalg.MATRIX3F32_IDENTITY,
		repeat       = false,
		// fill_rule removed - use default (stencil path) to fix root cause properly
	}
}

paint_pattern :: proc(img: Image_Handle, repeat: bool = true, opacity: f32 = 1.0) -> Paint {
	return Paint {
		type = .Pattern,
		image = img,
		opacity = opacity,
		uv_transform = linalg.MATRIX3F32_IDENTITY,
		repeat = repeat,
	}
}

/**
@documentation: Font Handle
- Opaque handle for font resources that hides FreeType/HarfBuzz implementation details
- Enables clean layer separation between Layer 3 (renderer) and Layer 2 (graphics/text)
- Platform-portable: a future CoreText or browser Canvas backend only needs to implement
  the bridge, not change any Layer 3 or Layer 4 code
- Mirrors Image_Handle pattern (renderer/image.odin)
- Part of Phase G.4 from 260309-RuiShin-2D-Completion-Roadmap3.md
*/

Font_Handle :: struct {
	id: u32,
}

Font_Handle_Invalid :: Font_Handle{id = 0}

// Check if a font handle is valid (not null/invalid)
font_handle_valid :: proc(h: Font_Handle) -> bool {
	return h.id != 0
}

import "core:fmt"

/**
@documentation: Image Handle
- Opaque handle for GPU images that hides Sokol implementation details
- Enables clean layer separation between Layer 3 (renderer) and Layer 1 (Sokol)
- Part of Phase B: Image_Handle + rsd cleanup from 2D Layer Completion Roadmap
*/

Image_Handle :: struct {
	id: u32,
}

Image_Handle_Invalid :: Image_Handle{id = 0}

// Check if an image handle is valid (not null/invalid)
image_handle_valid :: proc(h: Image_Handle) -> bool {
	return h.id != 0
}

import "core:fmt"
import "core:math"
import "core:math/linalg"
import "core:mem"
import "core:time"
import sg "vendor-local:sokol-odin/sokol/gfx"
import logging "project:logging"

// Error Handling System for Missing Resources
// Phase 10D: Added to provide better error messages when fonts or images are missing

// Error types for resource loading
Resource_Error :: enum {
	Font_Missing,
	Image_Missing,
	Font_Load_Failed,
	Image_Load_Failed,
	Unknown,
}

Resource_Error_Info :: struct {
	error_type: Resource_Error,
	resource_path: string,
	details: string,
	timestamp: f64,
}

// Global error queue for display
resource_errors: [dynamic]Resource_Error_Info
// Simple set of errors that have been reported (persistent)
reported_errors: map[string]bool

// Initialize error handling system
init_error_handling :: proc() {
	resource_errors = make([dynamic]Resource_Error_Info, 0, 16)
	reported_errors = make(map[string]bool)
}

// Reset error reporting (call when starting a new frame)
reset_error_flag :: proc() {
	// Clear the map of reported errors
	reported_errors = make(map[string]bool)
}

// Shutdown error handling system
shutdown_error_handling :: proc() {
	if len(resource_errors) > 0 {
		logging.error(.Renderer, fmt.tprintf("Resource Error Summary: %d errors occurred", len(resource_errors)))
		for i in 0..<len(resource_errors) {
			err := resource_errors[i]
			logging.error(.Renderer, fmt.tprintf("  %d: [%s] %s - %s", i+1, resource_error_string(err.error_type), err.resource_path, err.details))
		}
	}
	delete(resource_errors)
}

// Convert error type to string
resource_error_string :: proc(err: Resource_Error) -> string {
	switch err {
	case .Font_Missing: return "FONT_MISSING"
	case .Image_Missing: return "IMAGE_MISSING"
	case .Font_Load_Failed: return "FONT_LOAD_FAILED"
	case .Image_Load_Failed: return "IMAGE_LOAD_FAILED"
	case .Unknown: return "UNKNOWN"
	}
	return "UNKNOWN"
}

// Report a resource error
report_resource_error :: proc(error_type: Resource_Error, path: string, details: string) {
	// Create a unique key for this error
	error_key := fmt.tprintf("%s|%s", resource_error_string(error_type), path)
	
	// If we've already reported this error, don't report again
	if reported_errors[error_key] {
		return
	}
	
	// Mark as reported
	reported_errors[error_key] = true
	
	error_info := Resource_Error_Info{
		error_type = error_type,
		resource_path = path,
		details = details,
		timestamp = 0.0,
	}
	
	// Add to error queue
	append(&resource_errors, error_info)
	
	// Log to console
	logging.error(.Renderer, fmt.tprintf("[RESOURCE ERROR] %s: %s - %s", 
	          resource_error_string(error_type), path, details))
	
	// Phase 10D TODO: Add Sokol debug text rendering here
	// When Sokol debug text is integrated, uncomment and use:
	// debug_draw_text(error_message, 10, y_offset, [4]f32{1, 0, 0, 1})
}

// file_exists is defined in error_handling_platform.odin (#+build !js)
// and error_handling_wasm.odin (#+build js).

// Display error messages using console (fallback when Sokol debug text not available)
display_error_messages_console :: proc() {
	if len(resource_errors) == 0 {
		return
	}
	
	logging.error(.Renderer, "=== RESOURCE LOADING ERRORS ===")
	for i in 0..<len(resource_errors) {
		err := resource_errors[i]
		logging.error(.Renderer, fmt.tprintf("%d. [%s] %s", i+1, resource_error_string(err.error_type), err.resource_path))
		logging.error(.Renderer, fmt.tprintf("   Details: %s", err.details))
	}
	logging.error(.Renderer, "===============================")
}

// Phase 10D TODO: Sokol Debug Text Integration
// When Sokol debug text is properly integrated, implement:
//
// debug_draw_text :: proc(text: string, x, y: f32, color: [4]f32) {
//     // Use Sokol's debug text rendering API
//     // This requires proper Sokol debug text setup
// }
//
// debug_draw_error_overlay :: proc() {
//     y := 10.0
//     for i, err in resource_errors {
//         error_msg := fmt.sprintf("%d. %s: %s", i+1, resource_error_string(err.error_type), err.resource_path)
//         debug_draw_text(error_msg, 10, y, [4]f32{1, 0, 0, 1})
//         y += 20.0
//     }
// }

// Helper function to check font loading and report errors
check_and_report_font_error :: proc(path: string, size: u32, handle: Font_Handle) -> Font_Handle {
	if !font_handle_valid(handle) {
		error_msg := fmt.tprintf("Font failed to load from: %s (size: %d)", path, size)
		report_resource_error(.Font_Load_Failed, path, error_msg)
		return Font_Handle_Invalid
	}
	return handle
}

// Helper function to check image loading and report errors
check_and_report_image_error :: proc(path: string, img: sg.Image) -> sg.Image {
	if img.id == 0 {
		error_msg := fmt.tprintf("Image failed to load from: %s", path)
		report_resource_error(.Image_Load_Failed, path, error_msg)
		return img
	}
	return img
}

DEBUG: Source content: package vfs

import "core:strings"

// Virtual File System (VFS) abstraction for RuiShin
// For now, it's a simple wrapper over core:os, but designed to be extensible
// for web (WASM) assets or mobile bundles in the future.

Asset_Category :: enum {
	Settings, // artifacts/
	Font, // artifacts/fonts/
	Image, // artifacts/resources/
	Theme, // artifacts/themes/
	Log, // artifacts/logs/
}

// Internal path resolution.
// Native: maps to artifacts/ subdirectories.
// WASM: maps to Emscripten preloaded VFS roots (/fonts/, /resources/, etc.).
@(private)
resolve_path :: proc(path: string, cat: Asset_Category) -> string {
	when ODIN_ARCH == .wasm32 || ODIN_ARCH == .wasm64p32 {
		// Emscripten preload: --preload-file artifacts/fonts@/fonts etc.
		// Strip native "artifacts/<dir>/" prefixes and remap to WASM VFS roots.
		if strings.has_prefix(path, "/") do return path
		if strings.has_prefix(path, "artifacts/fonts/") {
			return strings.concatenate({"/fonts/", path[len("artifacts/fonts/"):]}, context.temp_allocator)
		}
		if strings.has_prefix(path, "artifacts/resources/") {
			return strings.concatenate({"/resources/", path[len("artifacts/resources/"):]}, context.temp_allocator)
		}
		if strings.has_prefix(path, "artifacts/themes/") {
			return strings.concatenate({"/themes/", path[len("artifacts/themes/"):]}, context.temp_allocator)
		}
		base := "/"
		#partial switch cat {
		case .Font:  base = "/fonts/"
		case .Image: base = "/resources/"
		case .Theme: base = "/themes/"
		}
		return strings.concatenate({base, path}, context.temp_allocator)
	} else {
		if strings.has_prefix(path, "artifacts/") do return path
		base := "artifacts/"
		#partial switch cat {
		case .Font:  base = "artifacts/fonts/"
		case .Image: base = "artifacts/resources/"
		case .Theme: base = "artifacts/themes/"
		case .Log:   base = "artifacts/logs/"
		}
		return strings.concatenate({base, path}, context.temp_allocator)
	}
}

// loadAsset and save_asset are defined in vfs_platform.odin (#+build !js)
// and vfs_wasm.odin (#+build js).

DEBUG: Source content: #+build js
package vfs

import "core:mem"

// On WASM, files are preloaded into Emscripten's virtual FS by build_web.sh.
// The preload mappings are:
//   artifacts/fonts/     → /fonts/
//   artifacts/resources/ → /resources/
//
// resolve_path() maps native paths to Emscripten VFS paths.
//
// Declare libc I/O directly to avoid core:c/libc (locale.odin is broken for js targets).

foreign import libc "env.o"

SEEK_SET :: i32(0)
SEEK_END :: i32(2)

@(default_calling_convention = "c")
foreign libc {
	fopen  :: proc(filename: cstring, mode: cstring) -> rawptr ---
	fseek  :: proc(stream: rawptr, offset: i32, whence: i32) -> i32 ---
	ftell  :: proc(stream: rawptr) -> i32 ---
	rewind :: proc(stream: rawptr) ---
	fread  :: proc(ptr: rawptr, size: uint, count: uint, stream: rawptr) -> uint ---
	fclose :: proc(stream: rawptr) -> i32 ---
}

loadAsset :: proc(path: string, cat: Asset_Category = .Settings) -> ([]u8, bool) {
	full_path := resolve_path(path, cat)
	cpath := _to_cstring(full_path)
	f := fopen(cpath, "rb")
	if f == nil do return nil, false

	fseek(f, 0, SEEK_END)
	size := int(ftell(f))
	rewind(f)

	data := make([]u8, size, context.allocator)
	n := int(fread(raw_data(data), 1, uint(size), f))
	fclose(f)
	if n != size {
		delete(data)
		return nil, false
	}
	return data, true
}

save_asset :: proc(path: string, data: []u8, cat: Asset_Category = .Settings) -> bool {
	// No persistent file writes on WASM
	return false
}

@(private)
_to_cstring :: proc(s: string) -> cstring {
	// Allocate null-terminated copy for libc calls
	buf := make([]u8, len(s) + 1, context.temp_allocator)
	mem.copy(raw_data(buf), raw_data(s), len(s))
	buf[len(s)] = 0
	return cstring(raw_data(buf))
}

DEBUG: Source content: #+build !js
package vfs

import "core:os"

loadAsset :: proc(path: string, cat: Asset_Category = .Settings) -> ([]u8, bool) {
	full_path := resolve_path(path, cat)
	data, err := os.read_entire_file(full_path, context.allocator)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, false
	}
	return data, true
}

save_asset :: proc(path: string, data: []u8, cat: Asset_Category = .Settings) -> bool {
	full_path := resolve_path(path, cat)
	err := os.write_entire_file(full_path, data)
	return err == nil
}

package main

import "core:strings"

import engine "engine"

main :: proc() {
	load_settings()
	app := engine.new_app()
	win := engine.new_window(
		app,
		engine.Window_Config{
			title        = strings.clone_to_cstring(
				global_settings.window_title,
				context.temp_allocator,
			),
			width        = global_settings.width,
			height       = global_settings.height,
			clear_color  = global_settings.clear_color,
			sample_count = global_settings.use_msaa ? 4 : 1,
		},
	)
	engine.set_init_fn(win,       demo_init)
	engine.set_scene_fn(win,      demo_scene)
	engine.set_post_flush_fn(win, demo_post_flush)
	engine.set_draw_fn(win,       demo_draw)
	engine.set_event_fn(win,      demo_event)
	engine.set_cleanup_fn(win,    demo_cleanup)
	engine.run(app)
}

DEBUG: Source length: 2080
DEBUG: Source content: #+build js
#+feature dynamic-literals
package main

// WASM entry point for Emscripten + Sokol.
//
// With -sINVOKE_RUN=0, Emscripten won't auto-call __main_argc_argv.
// Instead, JS calls wasmExports._start() from onRuntimeInitialized, which runs
// Odin @init procs and then calls main :: proc() below.
// main() sets up the engine and calls sapp.run(), which on Emscripten calls
// emscripten_set_main_loop and returns immediately, handing control to the browser.
//
// The emscripten allocator is required: Odin's default WASM allocator conflicts
// with Emscripten's heap management, causing silent failures during init.

import "base:runtime"
import "core:fmt"
import "core:strings"

import sapp "vendor-local:sokol-odin/sokol/app"
import engine "project:engine"
import web "project:web"

main :: proc() {
	fmt.println("[wasm] main start")
	// Replace Odin's WASM allocator with Emscripten's malloc-based one.
	// Without this, maps, dynamic arrays, and other heap use corrupt memory.
	context.allocator = web.emscripten_allocator()
	runtime.init_global_temporary_allocator(16 * runtime.Megabyte)
	fmt.println("[wasm] allocator ok")

	load_settings()
	fmt.println("[wasm] settings loaded, w=", global_settings.width, "h=", global_settings.height)

	app := engine.new_app()
	win := engine.new_window(
		app,
		engine.Window_Config{
			title        = strings.clone_to_cstring(
				global_settings.window_title,
				context.temp_allocator,
			),
			width        = global_settings.width,
			height       = global_settings.height,
			clear_color  = global_settings.clear_color,
			sample_count = global_settings.use_msaa ? 4 : 1,
		},
	)
	engine.set_init_fn(win,       demo_init)
	engine.set_scene_fn(win,      demo_scene)
	engine.set_post_flush_fn(win, demo_post_flush)
	engine.set_draw_fn(win,       demo_draw)
	engine.set_event_fn(win,      demo_event)
	engine.set_cleanup_fn(win,    demo_cleanup)

	fmt.println("[wasm] calling wasm_prepare")
	desc := engine.wasm_prepare(app)
	fmt.println("[wasm] calling sapp.run")
	sapp.run(desc)
	fmt.println("[wasm] sapp.run returned")
}
