DEBUG: Source length: 3191
DEBUG: Source content: #+build js
package web

// Emscripten-compatible allocator for js_wasm32 builds.
// Odin's default WASM allocator conflicts with Emscripten's heap management.
// Use this as context.allocator at the start of main :: proc() on WASM.
//
// Based on https://github.com/karl-zylinski/odin-sokol-web

import "core:c"
import "base:intrinsics"
import "core:mem"

@(default_calling_convention = "c")
foreign {
	calloc  :: proc(num, size: c.size_t) -> rawptr ---
	free    :: proc(ptr: rawptr) ---
	malloc  :: proc(size: c.size_t) -> rawptr ---
	realloc :: proc(ptr: rawptr, size: c.size_t) -> rawptr ---
}

emscripten_allocator :: proc "contextless" () -> mem.Allocator {
	return mem.Allocator{emscripten_allocator_proc, nil}
}

emscripten_allocator_proc :: proc(
	allocator_data: rawptr,
	mode: mem.Allocator_Mode,
	size, alignment: int,
	old_memory: rawptr,
	old_size: int,
	location := #caller_location,
) -> (data: []byte, err: mem.Allocator_Error) {
	aligned_alloc :: proc(size, alignment: int, zero_memory: bool, old_ptr: rawptr = nil) -> ([]byte, mem.Allocator_Error) {
		a := max(alignment, align_of(rawptr))
		space := size + a - 1

		allocated_mem: rawptr
		if old_ptr != nil {
			original_old_ptr := mem.ptr_offset((^rawptr)(old_ptr), -1)^
			allocated_mem = realloc(original_old_ptr, c.size_t(space + size_of(rawptr)))
		} else if zero_memory {
			allocated_mem = calloc(c.size_t(space + size_of(rawptr)), 1)
		} else {
			allocated_mem = malloc(c.size_t(space + size_of(rawptr)))
		}
		aligned_mem := rawptr(mem.ptr_offset((^u8)(allocated_mem), size_of(rawptr)))
		ptr := uintptr(aligned_mem)
		aligned_ptr := (ptr - 1 + uintptr(a)) & -uintptr(a)
		diff := int(aligned_ptr - ptr)
		if (size + diff) > space || allocated_mem == nil {
			return nil, .Out_Of_Memory
		}
		aligned_mem = rawptr(aligned_ptr)
		mem.ptr_offset((^rawptr)(aligned_mem), -1)^ = allocated_mem
		return mem.byte_slice(aligned_mem, size), nil
	}

	aligned_free :: proc(p: rawptr) {
		if p != nil {
			free(mem.ptr_offset((^rawptr)(p), -1)^)
		}
	}

	aligned_resize :: proc(p: rawptr, old_size: int, new_size: int, new_alignment: int) -> ([]byte, mem.Allocator_Error) {
		if p == nil {
			return nil, nil
		}
		return aligned_alloc(new_size, new_alignment, true, p)
	}

	switch mode {
	case .Alloc:
		return aligned_alloc(size, alignment, true)
	case .Alloc_Non_Zeroed:
		return aligned_alloc(size, alignment, false)
	case .Free:
		aligned_free(old_memory)
		return nil, nil
	case .Resize:
		if old_memory == nil {
			return aligned_alloc(size, alignment, true)
		}
		bytes := aligned_resize(old_memory, old_size, size, alignment) or_return
		if size > old_size {
			new_region := raw_data(bytes[old_size:])
			intrinsics.mem_zero(new_region, size - old_size)
		}
		return bytes, nil
	case .Resize_Non_Zeroed:
		if old_memory == nil {
			return aligned_alloc(size, alignment, false)
		}
		return aligned_resize(old_memory, old_size, size, alignment)
	case .Query_Features:
		set := (^mem.Allocator_Mode_Set)(old_memory)
		if set != nil {
			set^ = {.Alloc, .Free, .Resize, .Query_Features}
		}
		return nil, nil
	case .Free_All, .Query_Info:
		return nil, .Mode_Not_Implemented
	}
	return nil, .Mode_Not_Implemented
}

DEBUG: Source length: 11113
DEBUG: Source content: package layout

import fmt "core:fmt"
import "core:math"
import renderer "project:renderer"
import clay "vendor-local:clay/bindings/odin/clay-odin"
import sg "vendor-local:sokol-odin/sokol/gfx"
import g2d "project:graphics"

// Debug/ordering flags
CLAY_DEBUG_VIS :: false
CLAY_ORDERED_FLUSH :: false
CLAY_SCISSOR_EXPAND :: f32(2.0)
// Asymmetric scissor expansion (Step 3a)
// Asymmetric scissor expansion (Step 3a -> 4B)
CLAY_SCISSOR_EXPAND_L :: f32(8.0)
CLAY_SCISSOR_EXPAND_T :: f32(4.0)
CLAY_SCISSOR_EXPAND_R :: f32(2.0)
CLAY_SCISSOR_EXPAND_B :: f32(2.0)
// Debug: globally disable scissor to isolate first-glyph notch cause (Isolation B)
CLAY_DISABLE_SCISSOR_DEBUG :: false
// Debug: nudge first text in a block away from left edge (Step 3b)
CLAY_DEBUG_FIRSTTEXT_NUDGE :: false
CLAY_FIRSTTEXT_NUDGE_X :: f32(0.5)
// Border debug tweak (Step 4): reduce inward intrusion
CLAY_BORDER_TWEAK_DEBUG :: false
CLAY_BORDER_REDUCE_INWARD :: f32(1.0)

// Helper to draw a single Clay command (used by streaming and ordered flush)
draw_command_impl :: proc(
	r: renderer.Renderer,
	cmd: clay.RenderCommand,
	offset_x: f32,
	offset_y: f32,
	text_debug_count: ^i32,
) {
	switch cmd.commandType {
	case .Rectangle:
		rect := cmd.renderData.rectangle
		r.fillRectRounded(
			cmd.boundingBox.x + offset_x,
			cmd.boundingBox.y + offset_y,
			cmd.boundingBox.width,
			cmd.boundingBox.height,
			rect.cornerRadius.topLeft,
			renderer.paint_solid(rect.backgroundColor),
		)

	case .Text:
		text_data := cmd.renderData.text
		font := font_registry[text_data.fontId]
		if renderer.font_handle_valid(font) {
			str := string(text_data.stringContents.chars[:text_data.stringContents.length])
			font_ptr, _ := g2d.fm_resolve_handle(font)
			ascent := font_ptr != nil ? font_ptr.ascent : 0
			r.drawTextAdvanced(
				cmd.boundingBox.x + offset_x,
				cmd.boundingBox.y + offset_y + ascent,
				str,
				font,
				nil,
				text_data.textColor,
			)
		}
		if CLAY_DEBUG_VIS && text_debug_count != nil && text_debug_count^ < 3 {
			r.drawRectRounded(
				cmd.boundingBox.x + offset_x,
				cmd.boundingBox.y + offset_y,
				cmd.boundingBox.width,
				cmd.boundingBox.height,
				0,
				1.0,
				[4]f32{1, 0, 0, 0.6},
			)
			text_debug_count^ += 1
		}

	case .Image:
		image_data := cmd.renderData.image
		img_handle := sg.Image {
			id = u32(uintptr(image_data.imageData)),
		}
		if img_handle.id != 0 {
			r.drawImage(
				cmd.boundingBox.x + offset_x,
				cmd.boundingBox.y + offset_y,
				cmd.boundingBox.width,
				cmd.boundingBox.height,
				g2d.sg_image_to_handle(img_handle),
				image_data.backgroundColor,
			)
		}

	case .Border:
		border := cmd.renderData.border
		width := f32(
			max(
				border.width.left,
				max(border.width.right, max(border.width.top, border.width.bottom)),
			),
		)
		eff_width := width
		if CLAY_BORDER_TWEAK_DEBUG && width > 0 {
			// Reduce by 1 px to avoid inward overlap with first glyphs near TL radius
			eff_width = max(0.0, width - CLAY_BORDER_REDUCE_INWARD)
			if CLAY_DEBUG_VIS && eff_width != width {
				fmt.printf("[CLAY DBG] BORDER width tweaked: %.1f -> %.1f\n", width, eff_width)
			}
		}
		if eff_width > 0 {
			r.drawRectRounded(
				cmd.boundingBox.x + offset_x,
				cmd.boundingBox.y + offset_y,
				cmd.boundingBox.width,
				cmd.boundingBox.height,
				border.cornerRadius.topLeft,
				eff_width,
				border.color,
			)
		}

	case .None, .ScissorStart, .ScissorEnd, .Custom:
		return
	}
}

render_layout_commands :: proc(
	commands: clay.ClayArray(clay.RenderCommand),
	r: renderer.Renderer,
	offset_x: f32 = 0,
	offset_y: f32 = 0,
) {
	cmds := commands
	// Debug counters/state (Step 0 instrumentation)
	text_debug_count: i32 = 0
	in_scissor: bool = false
	scissor_block_idx: i32 = 0
	ordered_mode := CLAY_ORDERED_FLUSH
	in_block: bool = false
	block_start: i32 = -1

	for i in 0 ..< cmds.length {
		cmd := clay.RenderCommandArray_Get(&cmds, i)

		if CLAY_DEBUG_VIS && in_scissor {
			bb := cmd^.boundingBox
			fmt.printf(
				"[CLAY DBG] SB#%d cmd=%v bb=(%.1f,%.1f,%.1f,%.1f)\n",
				scissor_block_idx,
				cmd^.commandType,
				bb.x + offset_x,
				bb.y + offset_y,
				bb.width,
				bb.height,
			)
		}

		// In ordered mode, while inside a scissor block, skip immediate draws;
		// we will flush in order at ScissorEnd.
		if ordered_mode && in_block {
			if cmd.commandType != .ScissorStart && cmd.commandType != .ScissorEnd {
				continue
			}
		}

		switch cmd^.commandType {
		case .None:
			continue

		case .Rectangle:
			draw_command_impl(r, cmd^, offset_x, offset_y, &text_debug_count)

		case .Text:
			draw_command_impl(r, cmd^, offset_x, offset_y, &text_debug_count)

		case .Image:
			draw_command_impl(r, cmd^, offset_x, offset_y, &text_debug_count)

		case .Border:
			draw_command_impl(r, cmd^, offset_x, offset_y, &text_debug_count)

		case .ScissorStart:
			x := cmd^.boundingBox.x + offset_x
			y := cmd^.boundingBox.y + offset_y
			w := cmd^.boundingBox.width
			h := cmd^.boundingBox.height
			// Asymmetric expansion: more headroom on the left edge
			expand_l := CLAY_SCISSOR_EXPAND_L
			expand_t := CLAY_SCISSOR_EXPAND_T
			expand_r := CLAY_SCISSOR_EXPAND_R
			expand_b := CLAY_SCISSOR_EXPAND_B
			if !CLAY_DISABLE_SCISSOR_DEBUG {
				r.setClip(
					x - expand_l,
					y - expand_t,
					w + expand_l + expand_r,
					h + expand_t + expand_b,
				)
			}
			if CLAY_DEBUG_VIS {
				scissor_block_idx += 1
				in_scissor = true
				if CLAY_DISABLE_SCISSOR_DEBUG {
					fmt.printf(
						"[CLAY DBG] ScissorStart #%d (CLIP DISABLED) at (%.1f,%.1f,%.1f,%.1f) expand(L,T,R,B)=(%.1f,%.1f,%.1f,%.1f)\n",
						scissor_block_idx,
						x,
						y,
						w,
						h,
						expand_l,
						expand_t,
						expand_r,
						expand_b,
					)
				} else {
					fmt.printf(
						"[CLAY DBG] ScissorStart #%d at (%.1f,%.1f,%.1f,%.1f) expand(L,T,R,B)=(%.1f,%.1f,%.1f,%.1f)\n",
						scissor_block_idx,
						x,
						y,
						w,
						h,
						expand_l,
						expand_t,
						expand_r,
						expand_b,
					)
				}
			}
			if ordered_mode {
				in_block = true
				block_start = i + 1
			}

		case .ScissorEnd:
			// Flush buffered commands in order when ordered mode is enabled
			if ordered_mode && in_block && block_start >= 0 {
				// Pass 1: Rect/Image
				for j: i32 = block_start; j < i; j += 1 {
					c := clay.RenderCommandArray_Get(&cmds, j)
					if c^.commandType == .Rectangle || c^.commandType == .Image {
						if CLAY_DEBUG_VIS {
							bb := c^.boundingBox
							fmt.printf(
								"[CLAY DBG] FLUSH#%d pass=1 type=%v bb=(%.1f,%.1f,%.1f,%.1f)\n",
								scissor_block_idx,
								c^.commandType,
								bb.x + offset_x,
								bb.y + offset_y,
								bb.width,
								bb.height,
							)
						}
						draw_command_impl(r, c^, offset_x, offset_y, &text_debug_count)
					}
				}
				// Pass 2: Border
				for j: i32 = block_start; j < i; j += 1 {
					c := clay.RenderCommandArray_Get(&cmds, j)
					if c^.commandType == .Border {
						if CLAY_DEBUG_VIS {
							bb := c^.boundingBox
							fmt.printf(
								"[CLAY DBG] FLUSH#%d pass=2 type=%v bb=(%.1f,%.1f,%.1f,%.1f)\n",
								scissor_block_idx,
								c^.commandType,
								bb.x + offset_x,
								bb.y + offset_y,
								bb.width,
								bb.height,
							)
						}
						draw_command_impl(r, c^, offset_x, offset_y, &text_debug_count)
					}
				}
				// Pass 3: Text
				first_text_done := false
				for j: i32 = block_start; j < i; j += 1 {
					c := clay.RenderCommandArray_Get(&cmds, j)
					if c^.commandType == .Text {
						if CLAY_DEBUG_VIS {
							bb := c^.boundingBox
							fmt.printf(
								"[CLAY DBG] FLUSH#%d pass=3 type=%v bb=(%.1f,%.1f,%.1f,%.1f)\n",
								scissor_block_idx,
								c^.commandType,
								bb.x + offset_x,
								bb.y + offset_y,
								bb.width,
								bb.height,
							)
						}
						offx := offset_x
						if CLAY_DEBUG_FIRSTTEXT_NUDGE && !first_text_done {
							offx = offset_x + CLAY_FIRSTTEXT_NUDGE_X
							first_text_done = true
						}
						// Additional safety inset near rounded left corners: find containing rect and ensure a tiny inset inside its TL radius
						// Compute text top-left in screen coords (approximate for layout check, but verify against font)
						tx := c^.boundingBox.x + offx
						ty := c^.boundingBox.y + offset_y

						// Step 4A: Use true draw Y if possible to check against rounded corner
						text_data := c^.renderData.text
						font := font_registry[text_data.fontId]
						if renderer.font_handle_valid(font) {
							// Text is drawn at y + ascent. To be conservative, we check the Top-Left of the text bounding box.
							// But usually the "Text" command bounding box provided by Clay *is* the layout box (Top-Left origin).
							// However, the visual glyphs might be lower.
							// If we use `ty` (layout top) for `dy` calculation, we get the distance from the top of the container.
							// If the text is centered vertically, `ty` is > `ry`.
							// If we want to check where the glyph *actually* hits the left edge, we should look at the baseline or cap height.
							// Let's stick to the Plan: use bb.y + font.ascent as the "check Y"
							font_ptr, _ := g2d.fm_resolve_handle(font)
							if font_ptr != nil {
								ty = c^.boundingBox.y + offset_y + font_ptr.ascent
							}
						}

						extra := f32(0)
						for k: i32 = block_start; k < i; k += 1 {
							c2 := clay.RenderCommandArray_Get(&cmds, k)
							if c2^.commandType == .Rectangle {
								rbb := c2^.boundingBox
								rx := rbb.x + offset_x; ry := rbb.y + offset_y
								rw := rbb.width; rh := rbb.height
								if tx >= rx && tx <= rx + rw && ty >= ry && ty <= ry + rh {
									rect_data := c2^.renderData.rectangle
									r_tl := rect_data.cornerRadius.topLeft
									aa := f32(0.5)
									// Distance from top edge within TL corner range
									dy := ty - ry
									if dy < 0 {dy = 0}
									if dy > r_tl {dy = r_tl}
									// Compute arc-based safe X so text starts inside the rectangle, not inside rounded arc
									safe_x := rx
									if r_tl > 0 {
										disc := r_tl * r_tl - dy * dy
										if disc > 0 {
											arc_dx := r_tl - f32(math.sqrt(f64(disc)))
											safe_x = rx + arc_dx + aa
										} else {
											// Fallback: small margin near left edge when below the arc
											margin := min(r_tl, 2.0)
											safe_x = rx + margin + aa * 0.5
										}
									}
									if tx < safe_x {
										need := safe_x - tx
										if need > extra {extra = need}
									}
								}
							}
						}
						if extra > 0 {
							offx += extra
							if CLAY_DEBUG_VIS {
								fmt.printf(
									"[CLAY DBG] SAFE_INSET applied: +%.2f px (new offx %.2f)\n",
									extra,
									offx,
								)
							}
						}
						// Call with possibly nudged/safe-inset X offset
						draw_command_impl(r, c^, offx, offset_y, &text_debug_count)
					}
				}
			}

			if !CLAY_DISABLE_SCISSOR_DEBUG {
				r.resetClip()
			}
			if CLAY_DEBUG_VIS && in_scissor {
				fmt.printf("[CLAY DBG] ScissorEnd #%d\n", scissor_block_idx)
			}
			in_scissor = false
			in_block = false
			block_start = -1

		case .Custom:
			continue
		}
	}
}

✅ DEBUG: Parsing successful, tree created
DEBUG: Checking tree language...
✅ DEBUG: Tree language is valid
=== DEBUG: Getting root node ===

import engine "project:engine"
import platform "project:platform"
import clay "vendor-local:clay/bindings/odin/clay-odin"
import renderer "project:renderer"
import "base:runtime"
import "core:fmt"
import "core:mem"

Clay_Context :: struct {
	clay_ctx:     ^clay.Context,
	arena:        mem.Arena,
	arena_buffer: []byte,
}

@(private)
ctx: Clay_Context

// Font Registry for Clay mapping (u16 -> renderer.Font_Handle)
font_registry: map[u16]renderer.Font_Handle

// Hit-test cache for current frame
Layout_HitBox :: struct {
	id:   u32,
	rect: platform.Rect,
	z:    int,
}

current_frame_boxes: [dynamic]Layout_HitBox

// Optional quick lookup; we keep linear scan order for topmost resolution
id_to_rect: map[u32]platform.Rect

measure_text_callback :: proc "c" (
	text: clay.StringSlice,
	config: ^clay.TextElementConfig,
	userData: rawptr,
) -> clay.Dimensions {
	context = runtime.default_context()

	// Fix Phase 10.Z: Use local stack arena for temporary allocations during measurement
	// to avoid leaking into the thread-default temp allocator which may not be reset per-frame.
	arena_buf: [16 * 1024]byte
	arena: mem.Arena
	mem.arena_init(&arena, arena_buf[:])
	context.temp_allocator = mem.arena_allocator(&arena)

	font := font_registry[config.fontId]
	if !renderer.font_handle_valid(font) do return {0, 0}

	// Convert clay string slice to odin string
	str := string(text.chars[:text.length])

	w, h := renderer.active_renderer^.measureText(str, font)
	// Fallback to font size if height is missing
	if h <= 0 {
		h = f32(config.fontSize)
	}
	return {w, h}
}

layout_init :: proc(width, height: f32) {
	// 1. Allocate memory for Clay
	// Clay needs some internal memory. The min size is provided by clay.MinMemorySize()
	min_memory := clay.MinMemorySize()
	// We'll give it 1MB for now, which is plenty for most UI layouts.
	capacity := max(u32(1024 * 1024), min_memory)

	ctx.arena_buffer = make([]byte, capacity, runtime.default_allocator())
	mem.arena_init(&ctx.arena, ctx.arena_buffer)

	clay_arena := clay.CreateArenaWithCapacityAndMemory(
		cast(uint)capacity,
		raw_data(ctx.arena_buffer),
	)

	error_handler := clay.ErrorHandler {
		handler = proc "c" (errorData: clay.ErrorData) {
			context = runtime.default_context()
			fmt.printf("[CLAY ERROR] %s\n", errorData.errorText.chars[:errorData.errorText.length])
		},
	}

	ctx.clay_ctx = clay.Initialize(clay_arena, clay.Dimensions{width, height}, error_handler)

	font_registry = make(map[u16]renderer.Font_Handle, 32, runtime.default_allocator())

	// Initialize per-frame caches once
	current_frame_boxes = make([dynamic]Layout_HitBox, 0, 128, runtime.default_allocator())
	id_to_rect = make(map[u32]platform.Rect, 256, runtime.default_allocator())

	clay.SetMeasureTextFunction(measure_text_callback, nil)
	// Register engine hit-test query hook so Scene can ask for Clay rects
	engine.engineSetLayoutRectQuery(layout_get_rect_for_id)

	fmt.println("[LAYOUT] Clay initialized.")
}

layout_shutdown :: proc() {
	delete(ctx.arena_buffer)
	delete(font_registry)
	delete(current_frame_boxes)
	delete(id_to_rect)
}

layout_register_font :: proc(id: u16, font: renderer.Font_Handle) {
	font_registry[id] = font
}

layout_set_dimensions :: proc(width, height: f32) {
	clay.SetLayoutDimensions({width, height})
}

layout_update_input :: proc(input: platform.Input_State) {
	clay.SetPointerState(
		clay.Vector2{input.mouse_pos.x, input.mouse_pos.y},
		input.mouse_buttons[int(platform.Mouse_Button.Left)],
	)
}

layout_begin :: proc() {
	// Reset per-frame hit-test caches
	clear(&current_frame_boxes)
	clear(&id_to_rect)

	clay.BeginLayout()
}

layout_end :: proc() -> clay.ClayArray(clay.RenderCommand) {
	cmds := clay.EndLayout()

	// Build hit-test rectangles from render commands
	for i: i32 = 0; i < cmds.length; i += 1 {
		rc := clay.RenderCommandArray_Get(&cmds, i)
		// Consider visible element types
		#partial switch rc.commandType {
		case .Rectangle, .Border, .Text, .Image, .Custom:
			rect := platform.Rect {
				x = f32(rc.boundingBox.x),
				y = f32(rc.boundingBox.y),
				w = f32(rc.boundingBox.width),
				h = f32(rc.boundingBox.height),
			}
			append(
				&current_frame_boxes,
				Layout_HitBox{id = rc.id, rect = rect, z = int(rc.zIndex)},
			)
			id_to_rect[rc.id] = rect
		}
	}

	return cmds
}

// --- Sizing Wrappers ---
SizingFixed :: proc(size: f32) -> clay.SizingAxis {
	return clay.SizingFixed(size)
}

SizingGrow :: proc(min_max: clay.SizingConstraintsMinMax = {}) -> clay.SizingAxis {
	return clay.SizingGrow(min_max)
}

SizingFit :: proc(min_max: clay.SizingConstraintsMinMax = {}) -> clay.SizingAxis {
	return clay.SizingFit(min_max)
}

SizingPercent :: proc(percent: f32) -> clay.SizingAxis {
	return clay.SizingPercent(percent)
}

CornerRadiusAll :: proc(radius: f32) -> clay.CornerRadius {
	return clay.CornerRadiusAll(radius)
}

// --- Hit-Testing API (screen-space) ---
layout_hit_test :: proc(x, y: f32) -> (u32, platform.Rect, bool) {
	for i := len(current_frame_boxes) - 1; i >= 0; i -= 1 {
		hb := current_frame_boxes[i]
		if platform.hitTestRect(hb.rect, x, y) {
			return hb.id, hb.rect, true
		}
	}
	return 0, platform.Rect{}, false
}

layout_get_rect_for_id :: proc(id: u32) -> (platform.Rect, bool) {
	for hb in current_frame_boxes {
		if hb.id == id {
			return hb.rect, true
		}
	}
	return platform.Rect{}, false
}

// Bind a NodeUI to a Clay element id for engine-level hit-testing
layout_bind_node :: proc(ui: ^engine.NodeUI, id: u32) {
	if ui == nil do return
	engine.nodeSetLayoutId(&ui.node, id)
}

import "core:math/linalg"

// Input types — shared between engine and graphics.
// This package is backend-agnostic: NO vendor-local imports allowed.
// All Sokol-specific conversion (mapSappEvent) lives in engine/app_sokol.odin.

// ============================================================================
// Key_Code — platform-agnostic key identifiers.
// Numeric values match sapp.Keycode (GLFW-based) so that engine/app_sokol.odin
// can convert with a simple cast: Key_Code(ev.key_code).
// ============================================================================
Key_Code :: enum i32 {
	Invalid        = 0,
	Space          = 32,
	Apostrophe     = 39,
	Comma          = 44,
	Minus          = 45,
	Period         = 46,
	Slash          = 47,
	_0             = 48,
	_1             = 49,
	_2             = 50,
	_3             = 51,
	_4             = 52,
	_5             = 53,
	_6             = 54,
	_7             = 55,
	_8             = 56,
	_9             = 57,
	Semicolon      = 59,
	Equal          = 61,
	A              = 65,
	B              = 66,
	C              = 67,
	D              = 68,
	E              = 69,
	F              = 70,
	G              = 71,
	H              = 72,
	I              = 73,
	J              = 74,
	K              = 75,
	L              = 76,
	M              = 77,
	N              = 78,
	O              = 79,
	P              = 80,
	Q              = 81,
	R              = 82,
	S              = 83,
	T              = 84,
	U              = 85,
	V              = 86,
	W              = 87,
	X              = 88,
	Y              = 89,
	Z              = 90,
	Left_Bracket   = 91,
	Backslash      = 92,
	Right_Bracket  = 93,
	Grave_Accent   = 96,
	World_1        = 161,
	World_2        = 162,
	Escape         = 256,
	Enter          = 257,
	Tab            = 258,
	Backspace      = 259,
	Insert         = 260,
	Delete         = 261,
	Right          = 262,
	Left           = 263,
	Down           = 264,
	Up             = 265,
	Page_Up        = 266,
	Page_Down      = 267,
	Home           = 268,
	End            = 269,
	Caps_Lock      = 280,
	Scroll_Lock    = 281,
	Num_Lock       = 282,
	Print_Screen   = 283,
	Pause          = 284,
	F1             = 290,
	F2             = 291,
	F3             = 292,
	F4             = 293,
	F5             = 294,
	F6             = 295,
	F7             = 296,
	F8             = 297,
	F9             = 298,
	F10            = 299,
	F11            = 300,
	F12            = 301,
	F13            = 302,
	F14            = 303,
	F15            = 304,
	F16            = 305,
	F17            = 306,
	F18            = 307,
	F19            = 308,
	F20            = 309,
	F21            = 310,
	F22            = 311,
	F23            = 312,
	F24            = 313,
	F25            = 314,
	Kp_0           = 320,
	Kp_1           = 321,
	Kp_2           = 322,
	Kp_3           = 323,
	Kp_4           = 324,
	Kp_5           = 325,
	Kp_6           = 326,
	Kp_7           = 327,
	Kp_8           = 328,
	Kp_9           = 329,
	Kp_Decimal     = 330,
	Kp_Divide      = 331,
	Kp_Multiply    = 332,
	Kp_Subtract    = 333,
	Kp_Add         = 334,
	Kp_Enter       = 335,
	Kp_Equal       = 336,
	Left_Shift     = 340,
	Left_Control   = 341,
	Left_Alt       = 342,
	Left_Super     = 343,
	Right_Shift    = 344,
	Right_Control  = 345,
	Right_Alt      = 346,
	Right_Super    = 347,
	Menu           = 348,
}

// ============================================================================
// Modifier flags — same numeric values as sapp.MODIFIER_*
// ============================================================================
MODIFIER_SHIFT :: u32(1)
MODIFIER_CTRL  :: u32(2)
MODIFIER_ALT   :: u32(4)
MODIFIER_SUPER :: u32(8)
MODIFIER_LMB   :: u32(256)
MODIFIER_RMB   :: u32(512)
MODIFIER_MMB   :: u32(1024)

Mouse_Button :: enum {
	Left,
	Right,
	Middle,
}

Input_Event_Type :: enum {
	Invalid,
	// Keyboard
	Key_Down,
	Key_Up,
	// Text input
	Text_Input,    // committed text (renamed from Char for clarity)
	Text_Editing,  // RESERVED — IME composition in progress (not yet fired)
	               // When IME is added, carries composition string + cursor + selection.
	               // See plans/260314-Future-CrossPlatform-Work.md for full design.
	// Mouse
	Mouse_Down,
	Mouse_Up,
	Mouse_Scroll,
	Mouse_Move,
	Mouse_Enter,
	Mouse_Leave,
	// Touch
	Touch_Begin,
	Touch_Move,
	Touch_End,
	Touch_Cancel,
	// Window lifecycle
	Window_Resized,
	Window_Focused,
	Window_Unfocused,
	Window_Minimized,
	Window_Restored,
	// App lifecycle
	App_Suspended,   // mobile/WASM: app going to background
	App_Resumed,     // mobile/WASM: app returning to foreground
	// App control
	Quit_Requested,    // user clicked close — app must call engine.quit() or engine.cancel_quit()
	Files_Dropped,     // user dropped files on window — query engine.dropped_file_count/path
	Clipboard_Pasted,  // Ctrl+V (only when clipboard_enabled = true on App)
}

// Unified input event for the RuiShin framework.
Input_Event :: struct {
	type:        Input_Event_Type,
	key:         Key_Code,
	char:        rune,       // Text_Input: the committed character
	button:      Mouse_Button,
	pos:         [2]f32,     // logical pixels (DPI-divided)
	delta:       [2]f32,
	scroll:      [2]f32,
	modifiers:   u32,        // MODIFIER_* flags
	repeat:      bool,
	num_touches: int,
	touch_id:    uintptr,
	// Window_Resized: new logical size
	window_size: [2]f32,
	// IME reservation (Text_Editing — not yet populated)
	composition:           string,
	composition_cursor:    int,
	composition_selection: int,
	// Aliases for convenience
	x, y: f32,
}

// Tracks the persistent state of input devices.
Input_State :: struct {
	mouse_pos:     [2]f32,
	mouse_buttons: [3]bool,
	keys_down:     [512]bool,
	modifiers:     u32,
	last_event:    Input_Event,
}

// Spatial interaction helpers
Rect :: struct {
	x, y, w, h: f32,
}

hitTestRect :: proc(rect: Rect, x, y: f32) -> bool {
	return x >= rect.x && x <= rect.x + rect.w && y >= rect.y && y <= rect.y + rect.h
}

hitTestCircle :: proc(center_x, center_y, radius, x, y: f32) -> bool {
	dx := x - center_x
	dy := y - center_y
	return (dx * dx + dy * dy) <= (radius * radius)
}

// Interleaved x,y coordinates
hitTestPolygon :: proc(points: []f32, x, y: f32) -> bool {
	if len(points) < 6 do return false // Need at least 3 points
	inside := false
	n := len(points) / 2
	j := n - 1
	for i in 0 ..< n {
		xi, yi := points[i * 2], points[i * 2 + 1]
		xj, yj := points[j * 2], points[j * 2 + 1]
		intersect := ((yi > y) != (yj > y)) && (x < (xj - xi) * (y - yi) / (yj - yi) + xi)
		if intersect do inside = !inside
		j = i
	}
	return inside
}

// Transform coordinate from screen space to local space
screenToLocal :: proc(
	screen_x, screen_y: f32,
	global_transform: matrix[4, 4]f32,
	INV_DPI: f32 = 1.0,
) -> (
	f32,
	f32,
) {
	// Note: global_transform is in logical space
	inv_transform := linalg.inverse(global_transform)
	p := linalg.Vector4f32{screen_x, screen_y, 0, 1}
	local_p := inv_transform * p
	return local_p.x, local_p.y
}

updateInputState :: proc(state: ^Input_State, ev: Input_Event) {
	state.last_event = ev
	state.modifiers = ev.modifiers

	#partial switch ev.type {
	case .Mouse_Move, .Mouse_Down, .Mouse_Up:
		state.mouse_pos = ev.pos
	case .Touch_Move, .Touch_Begin, .Touch_End:
		state.mouse_pos = ev.pos
	}

	#partial switch ev.type {
	case .Mouse_Down:
		state.mouse_buttons[int(ev.button)] = true
	case .Mouse_Up:
		state.mouse_buttons[int(ev.button)] = false
	case .Key_Down:
		idx := int(ev.key)
		if idx >= 0 && idx < len(state.keys_down) {
			state.keys_down[idx] = true
		}
	case .Key_Up:
		idx := int(ev.key)
		if idx >= 0 && idx < len(state.keys_down) {
			state.keys_down[idx] = false
		}
	}
}

DEBUG: Source length: 4689
DEBUG: Source content: package platform

// Platform detection for RuiShin theme system.
// Provides target platform detection to enable platform-specific theme overrides.
//
// IMPORTANT: Uses compile-time ODIN_OS constant which represents the TARGET platform,
// not the build platform. This is correct for cross-compilation scenarios:
// - Building on macOS for Web/WASM: ODIN_OS == .JS (target is Web)
// - Building on macOS for macOS: ODIN_OS == .Darwin (target is macOS)
// - Building on Linux for Windows: ODIN_OS == .Windows (target is Windows)
//
// The theme system will use the target platform's overrides from themespec.yaml.

import "core:fmt"

// Platform_OS represents the detected operating system.
Platform_OS :: enum {
	Unknown,
	MacOS,
	Windows,
	Linux,
	iOS,
	Android,
	Web,
}

// Platform_Family groups platforms by form factor.
Platform_Family :: enum {
	Unknown,
	Desktop,
	Mobile,
	Web,
}

// Platform_Capability represents input/interaction capabilities.
// Uses bit_set for efficient flag checking.
Platform_Capability :: enum {
	Touch,
	Keyboard,
	Mouse,
	Gamepad,
}

Platform_Capabilities :: bit_set[Platform_Capability]

// Platform_Info stores detected platform information.
// Initialized once at startup via detect_platform().
Platform_Info :: struct {
	os:           Platform_OS,
	family:       Platform_Family,
	capabilities: Platform_Capabilities,
}

// Global platform info, initialized on first call to detect_platform().
current_platform: Platform_Info
platform_initialized := false

// detect_platform returns platform information based on the TARGET platform (ODIN_OS).
// This function should be called once at application startup.
// Subsequent calls return the cached result.
//
// Note: ODIN_OS represents the target platform, not the build platform.
// When cross-compiling (e.g., macOS → Web), ODIN_OS will be .JS (the target).
detectPlatform :: proc() -> Platform_Info {
	if platform_initialized {
		return current_platform
	}

	info := Platform_Info{}

	// Detect target OS using compile-time ODIN_OS constant
	// ODIN_OS represents where the compiled binary will RUN, not where it's being built
	when ODIN_OS == .Darwin {
		// macOS or iOS - for now assume macOS (desktop)
		// TODO: Distinguish iOS when building for mobile targets
		info.os = .MacOS
		info.family = .Desktop
		info.capabilities = {.Keyboard, .Mouse}
	} else when ODIN_OS == .Windows {
		info.os = .Windows
		info.family = .Desktop
		info.capabilities = {.Keyboard, .Mouse}
	} else when ODIN_OS == .Linux {
		info.os = .Linux
		info.family = .Desktop
		info.capabilities = {.Keyboard, .Mouse}
	} else when ODIN_OS == .JS {
		// WebAssembly/JavaScript target
		info.os = .Web
		info.family = .Web
		info.capabilities = {.Keyboard, .Mouse, .Touch}
	} else {
		// Unknown platform - default to desktop with keyboard/mouse
		info.os = .Unknown
		info.family = .Desktop
		info.capabilities = {.Keyboard, .Mouse}
	}

	current_platform = info
	platform_initialized = true
	return info
}

// get_platform_name returns the platform name as a string for theme YAML lookup.
// Returns lowercase names matching themespec.yaml platform keys:
// "macos", "windows", "linux", "ios", "android", "web"
get_platform_name :: proc(info: Platform_Info) -> string {
	switch info.os {
	case .MacOS:
		return "macos"
	case .Windows:
		return "windows"
	case .Linux:
		return "linux"
	case .iOS:
		return "ios"
	case .Android:
		return "android"
	case .Web:
		return "web"
	case .Unknown:
		return "unknown"
	}
	return "unknown"
}

// has_capability checks if the platform has a specific capability.
has_capability :: proc(info: Platform_Info, cap: Platform_Capability) -> bool {
	return cap in info.capabilities
}

// Convenience helpers for common platform queries

is_desktop :: proc(info: Platform_Info) -> bool {
	return info.family == .Desktop
}

is_mobile :: proc(info: Platform_Info) -> bool {
	return info.family == .Mobile
}

is_web :: proc(info: Platform_Info) -> bool {
	return info.family == .Web
}

is_macos :: proc(info: Platform_Info) -> bool {
	return info.os == .MacOS
}

is_windows :: proc(info: Platform_Info) -> bool {
	return info.os == .Windows
}

is_linux :: proc(info: Platform_Info) -> bool {
	return info.os == .Linux
}

// has_touch returns true if the platform supports touch input.
has_touch :: proc(info: Platform_Info) -> bool {
	return has_capability(info, .Touch)
}

// has_mouse returns true if the platform supports mouse input.
has_mouse :: proc(info: Platform_Info) -> bool {
	return has_capability(info, .Mouse)
}

// has_keyboard returns true if the platform supports keyboard input.
has_keyboard :: proc(info: Platform_Info) -> bool {
	return has_capability(info, .Keyboard)
}

package main

// ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// demo_stress.odin — STRESS_2D GPU Burn Mode
//
// Contains everything needed when STRESS_2D=true — the compile-time flag that
// completely replaces the 32-tile normal demo with 9 focused GPU burn tiles.
//
// Build usage:
//   STRESS_2D=true odin run build/build.odin -file
//   ./artifacts/ruishin_demo_macARM
//
// Tile grid (3×3, cols 0-23, fills all GRID_ROWS = 32):
//   Row 0 (11 units): S1 Shadow Flood | S2 Stencil Storm | S3 Atlas Hammer
//   Row 1 (11 units): S4 Fill Flood   | S5 Particle Storm | S6 Tween Storm
//   Row 2 (10 units): S7 Glow Orbs    | S8 Particle Demo  | S9 Flying Toasters
//
// Package-level globals (image handles + Plankton types) live in the first
// `when STRESS_2D { }` block below, which is compiled only when STRESS_2D=true.
// Load constants (STRESS_N_*) are defined in main.odin and overridable via
// -define:STRESS_N_SHADOW=200 etc.
// ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

import "core:fmt"
import "core:math"
import "core:math/rand"
import engine "engine"
import renderer "project:renderer"

when STRESS_2D {
stress_toast_img:   renderer.Image_Handle
stress_toaster_img: renderer.Image_Handle

// Plankton particle types — translated from Marco Dell'Anna's JS canvas demo
Plankton_Type :: enum u8 { Larva, LarvaEgg, Egg }
Plankton :: struct {
	type:              Plankton_Type,
	x, y:              f32,
	prog:              f32,  // frame counter driving Lissajous / eye jitter
	radius:            f32,
	angle, speed:      f32,  // Brownian motion state
	blue:              bool, // LarvaEgg colour variant
	vx1, vx2, vy1, vy2: f32, // Lissajous frequency variants (Egg)
	eox, eoy:          f32,  // eye offset (Egg)
	er1, er2, er3:     f32,  // eye radii (Egg)
}
plankton_pool:  [STRESS_N_VORTEX]Plankton
plankton_ready: bool
}

// ── Stress Tiles (STRESS_2D=true only) ───────────────────────────────────
// Six focused burn tiles replace the normal demo: each hammers one subsystem.
// 3×2 grid layout. Each tile has a live FPS / ms HUD at the bottom.
when STRESS_2D {

// ── shared HUD drawn at the bottom of every stress tile ──────────────────
stress_hud :: proc(r: ^renderer.Renderer, x, y, w, h: f32, label: string, n: int) {
	fps     := 1.0 / math.max(f32(engine.frame_duration()), 0.0001)
	ms      := f32(engine.frame_duration()) * 1000.0
	fps_col := [4]f32{0.3, 1, 0.3, 1} if fps >= 58 else ([4]f32{1, 0.8, 0.2, 1} if fps >= 30 else [4]f32{1, 0.3, 0.3, 1})
	r.fillRect(x, y + h - 18, w, 18, renderer.paint_solid({0, 0, 0, 0.6}))
	r.drawText(x + 4, y + h - 14,
		fmt.tprintf("%.0f fps  %.2f ms  |  %s × %d", fps, ms, label, n),
		global_font_small, fps_col)
}

// ── S1 — Shadow Flood ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Stresses shadow composite throughput: N draws reusing a fixed palette of
// 8 cached RTs (one offscreen RT + blur temp per unique size). Using a fixed
// palette avoids exhausting Sokol's image pool — the cost is compositing, not
// cache-miss RT creation.
render_stress_shadow :: proc(node: ^Tile_Node, r: ^renderer.Renderer, x, y, w, h: f32) {
	r.fillRect(x, y, w, h, renderer.paint_solid({0.04, 0.04, 0.06, 1}))
	shadow_cols := [4][4]f32{
		{0.9, 0.3, 0.3, 1}, {0.3, 0.8, 0.9, 1},
		{0.9, 0.8, 0.3, 1}, {0.6, 0.3, 0.9, 1},
	}
	// Fixed palette → only 8 unique shadow cache entries (16 RTs total).
	// Varying sizes would create one RT per unique size and exhaust the pool.
	sizes := [8]f32{8, 10, 13, 16, 20, 24, 28, 32}
	content_h := h - 22
	for i in 0..<STRESS_N_SHADOW {
		fi  := f32(i)
		col := shadow_cols[i % 4]
		sz  := sizes[i % 8]
		sx  := x + 4 + math.mod(fi * (w - 8) / f32(STRESS_N_SHADOW) + now * 14, w - 8)
		sy  := y + 4 + math.mod(fi * 7.3 + now * (5 + math.mod(fi * 0.3, 6)), content_h - 8)
		r.dropShadowRect(sx, sy, sz, sz, 3, 0, 4, 8, col)
		r.fillRectRounded(sx, sy, sz, sz, 3, renderer.paint_solid({col.r*0.4+0.1, col.g*0.4+0.1, col.b*0.4+0.1, 1}))
	}
	stress_hud(r, x, y, w, h, "shadow", STRESS_N_SHADOW)
}

// ── S2 — Stencil Storm ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Each fillBezierCubic = a separate GPU stencil pass (even-odd fill rule).
render_stress_stencil :: proc(node: ^Tile_Node, r: ^renderer.Renderer, x, y, w, h: f32) {
	r.fillRect(x, y, w, h, renderer.paint_solid({0.04, 0.04, 0.06, 1}))
	content_h := h - 22
	for i in 0..<STRESS_N_STENCIL {
		fi  := f32(i)
		t   := now * 0.4 + fi * 0.18
		cx  := x + 4 + math.mod(fi * (w - 8) / f32(STRESS_N_STENCIL) + now * 18, w - 8)
		cy  := y + 4 + math.mod(fi * 7.3 + now * (4 + math.mod(fi * 0.25, 7)), content_h - 8)
		s   := 6 + math.mod(fi * 0.9, 10)
		hue := fi / f32(STRESS_N_STENCIL)
		col := [4]f32{
			0.3 + 0.7 * math.abs(math.sin_f32(hue * math.PI * 2)),
			0.3 + 0.7 * math.abs(math.sin_f32((hue + 0.33) * math.PI * 2)),
			0.3 + 0.7 * math.abs(math.sin_f32((hue + 0.66) * math.PI * 2)),
			0.75,
		}
		r.fillBezierCubic(
			cx - s, cy,
			cx - s*0.3 + math.cos_f32(t)*s*0.5, cy - s + math.sin_f32(t*0.7)*s*0.4,
			cx + s*0.3 + math.sin_f32(t*0.9)*s*0.5, cy - s + math.cos_f32(t*0.6)*s*0.4,
			cx + s, cy,
			renderer.paint_solid(col),
		)
	}
	stress_hud(r, x, y, w, h, "stencil", STRESS_N_STENCIL)
}

// ── S3 — Atlas Hammer ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Many drawText calls at two font sizes — stresses atlas lookup + vertex fill.
render_stress_atlas :: proc(node: ^Tile_Node, r: ^renderer.Renderer, x, y, w, h: f32) {
	r.fillRect(x, y, w, h, renderer.paint_solid({0.04, 0.04, 0.06, 1}))
	content_h := h - 22
	words := [6]string{"RuiShin", "GPU", "Atlas", "Draw", "Text", "2D"}
	for i in 0..<STRESS_N_TEXT {
		fi   := f32(i)
		tx   := x + 2 + math.mod(fi * (w - 4) / f32(STRESS_N_TEXT) + now * 22, w - 4)
		ty   := y + 2 + math.mod(fi * 5.7 + now * (3 + math.mod(fi * 0.2, 8)), content_h - 8)
		col  := [4]f32{0.5 + 0.5*math.sin_f32(fi*0.4), 0.5 + 0.5*math.cos_f32(fi*0.3), 0.6, 0.85}
		font := global_font_small if (i % 2 == 0) else global_font_normal
		r.drawText(tx, ty, words[i % 6], font, col)
	}
	stress_hud(r, x, y, w, h, "text", STRESS_N_TEXT)
}

// ── S4 — Fill Flood ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Raw fillCircle throughput — measures vertex buffer fill rate and batch size.
render_stress_fill :: proc(node: ^Tile_Node, r: ^renderer.Renderer, x, y, w, h: f32) {
	r.fillRect(x, y, w, h, renderer.paint_solid({0.03, 0.03, 0.05, 1}))
	content_h := h - 22
	for i in 0..<STRESS_N_FILL {
		fi  := f32(i)
		t   := now * 1.1 + fi * 0.05
		px  := x + math.mod(fi * (w / f32(STRESS_N_FILL)) + math.sin_f32(t)*6, w)
		py  := y + math.mod(fi * 5.13 + math.cos_f32(t+fi)*4, content_h)
		rad := 1.0 + math.mod(fi * 0.4, 3.0)
		col := [4]f32{
			0.6 + 0.4*math.sin_f32(fi*0.11),
			0.3 + 0.4*math.cos_f32(fi*0.09),
			0.8,
			0.65,
		}
		r.fillCircle(px, py, rad, renderer.paint_solid(col))
	}
	stress_hud(r, x, y, w, h, "circle", STRESS_N_FILL)
}

// ── S5 — Particle Storm ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Simulates N particles across 6 emitters — stresses per-particle draw calls.
render_stress_particle :: proc(node: ^Tile_Node, r: ^renderer.Renderer, x, y, w, h: f32) {
	r.fillRect(x, y, w, h, renderer.paint_solid({0.02, 0.02, 0.04, 1}))
	content_h := h - 22
	n_emitters    := 6
	per_emitter   := STRESS_N_PARTICLE / n_emitters
	for ei in 0..<n_emitters {
		efi    := f32(ei)
		emit_x := x + (efi + 0.5) * (w / f32(n_emitters))
		for i in 0..<per_emitter {
			fi    := f32(i)
			age   := math.mod(fi / f32(per_emitter) + now * 0.6 + efi * 0.4, 1.0)
			alpha := (1.0 - age) * 0.9
			rise  := age * content_h * 0.88
			sway  := math.sin_f32(fi * 0.8 + now * 1.5 + efi) * 14
			px    := emit_x + sway
			py    := y + content_h - rise
			rad   := 1.5 + (1.0 - age) * 3.5
			col   := [4]f32{1.0, 0.4 + age * 0.3, age * 0.2, alpha}
			r.fillCircle(px, py, rad, renderer.paint_solid(col))
		}
	}
	stress_hud(r, x, y, w, h, "particle", STRESS_N_PARTICLE)
}

// ── S6 — Tween Storm ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// N animated balls each running an independent easing curve — draw call storm.
render_stress_tween :: proc(node: ^Tile_Node, r: ^renderer.Renderer, x, y, w, h: f32) {
	r.fillRect(x, y, w, h, renderer.paint_solid({0.05, 0.04, 0.08, 1}))
	content_h := h - 22
	track_pad := f32(6)
	row_h     := (content_h - track_pad * 2) / f32(STRESS_N_TWEEN)
	ball_r    := math.clamp(row_h * 0.35, 1.5, 5.0)
	tx0       := x + track_pad + ball_r
	tx1       := x + w - track_pad - ball_r
	track_w   := tx1 - tx0
	for i in 0..<STRESS_N_TWEEN {
		fi    := f32(i)
		speed := 0.4 + math.mod(fi * 0.07, 0.8)
		phase := fi * 0.19
		t_raw := math.mod(now * speed + phase, 1.0)
		t_ease := math.abs(math.sin_f32(t_raw * math.PI)) // bounce back and forth
		bx    := tx0 + t_ease * track_w
		by    := y + track_pad + fi * row_h + row_h * 0.5
		hue   := fi / f32(STRESS_N_TWEEN)
		col   := [4]f32{
			0.4 + 0.6 * math.abs(math.sin_f32(hue * math.PI * 2 + 0.0)),
			0.4 + 0.6 * math.abs(math.sin_f32(hue * math.PI * 2 + 2.1)),
			0.4 + 0.6 * math.abs(math.sin_f32(hue * math.PI * 2 + 4.2)),
			0.9,
		}
		r.drawLine(tx0, by, tx1, by, 0.5, {1, 1, 1, 0.06})
		r.fillCircle(bx, by, ball_r, renderer.paint_solid(col))
	}
	stress_hud(r, x, y, w, h, "tween", STRESS_N_TWEEN)
}

// ── S7 — Glow Orbs ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// 150 glowing orbs each orbiting its own center at independent speeds.
// 3 layered circles per orb (outer glow, mid, core) = 450 fillCircle/frame.
render_stress_orbs :: proc(node: ^Tile_Node, r: ^renderer.Renderer, x, y, w, h: f32) {
	r.fillRect(x, y, w, h, renderer.paint_solid({0.02, 0.01, 0.05, 1}))
	content_h := h - 22
	for i in 0..<STRESS_N_ORBS {
		fi       := f32(i)
		center_x := x + 10 + math.mod(fi * (w - 20) / f32(STRESS_N_ORBS), w - 20)
		center_y := y + 10 + math.mod(fi * (content_h - 20) * 0.618, content_h - 20)
		orbit_r  := 6 + math.mod(fi * 1.7, 18)
		speed    := 0.4 + math.mod(fi * 0.11, 1.6)
		angle    := now * speed + fi * 2.399 // golden-angle spread
		px       := center_x + math.cos_f32(angle) * orbit_r
		py       := center_y + math.sin_f32(angle) * orbit_r
		hue      := fi / f32(STRESS_N_ORBS)
		cr       := 0.4 + 0.6 * math.abs(math.sin_f32(hue * math.PI * 2 + 0.0))
		cg       := 0.1 + 0.4 * math.abs(math.sin_f32(hue * math.PI * 2 + 1.5))
		cb       := 0.5 + 0.5 * math.abs(math.sin_f32(hue * math.PI * 2 + 3.0))
		core_r   := 2 + math.mod(fi * 0.5, 4)
		r.fillCircle(px, py, core_r * 4, renderer.paint_solid({cr, cg, cb, 0.06}))
		r.fillCircle(px, py, core_r * 2, renderer.paint_solid({cr, cg, cb, 0.18}))
		r.fillCircle(px, py, core_r,     renderer.paint_solid({cr*0.5+0.5, cg*0.3+0.7, cb*0.3+0.7, 0.9}))
	}
	stress_hud(r, x, y, w, h, "orb×3", STRESS_N_ORBS * 3)
}

// ── S8 — Particle Demo (Plankton) ─────────────────────────────────────────
// Odin translation of Marco Dell'Anna's "PLANKTON – Particles Life" JS demo.
// Three particle types swim with Lissajous / Brownian motion on a dark field:
//   Egg       (~3%)  — large cell with translucent body + animated "eyes"
//   Larva     (~27%) — tiny orange dot, random-walk
//   LarvaEgg  (~70%) — tiny blue dot, random-walk

plankton_spawn :: proc(p: ^Plankton, x, y, w, h: f32) {
	rng := rand.float32()
	cx  := x + w * 0.5
	cy  := y + h * 0.5
	p.prog = rand.float32() * 600
	switch {
	case rng > 0.97:
		p.type   = .Egg
		p.x      = cx + (rand.float32()-0.5) * w * 0.65
		p.y      = cy + (rand.float32()-0.5) * h * 0.55
		p.radius = 12 + rand.float32() * 6
		p.vx1    = 1 + rand.float32() * 99; p.vx2 = 1 + rand.float32() * 99
		p.vy1    = 1 + rand.float32() * 99; p.vy2 = 1 + rand.float32() * 99
		p.eox    = (rand.float32()-0.5) * 6; p.eoy = (rand.float32()-0.5) * 6
		p.er1    = 0.5 + rand.float32() * 5
		p.er2    = 0.5 + rand.float32() * 5
		p.er3    = 0.5 + rand.float32() * 5
	case rng > 0.10 && rng < 0.80:
		p.type   = .LarvaEgg
		p.x      = cx + (rand.float32()-0.5) * w * 0.9
		p.y      = cy + (rand.float32()-0.5) * h * 0.9
		p.radius = 0.4 + rand.float32() * 0.8
		p.speed  = rand.float32() * 1.0
		p.angle  = rand.float32() * math.PI * 2
		p.blue   = rand.float32() > 0.2
	case:
		p.type   = .Larva
		p.x      = cx + (rand.float32()-0.5) * w * 0.9
		p.y      = cy + (rand.float32()-0.5) * h * 0.9
		p.radius = 0.3 + rand.float32() * 1.3
		p.speed  = 0.5 + rand.float32() * 1.3
		p.angle  = rand.float32() * math.PI * 2
	}
}

plankton_tick :: proc(r: ^renderer.Renderer, x, y, w, h: f32) {
	// Normalise movement to 60 fps regardless of actual frame rate
	dt := math.clamp(f32(engine.frame_duration()) * 60, 0.5, 3)
	for i in 0..<STRESS_N_VORTEX {
		p := &plankton_pool[i]
		// ── move ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
		switch p.type {
		case .Larva, .LarvaEgg:
			p.x    += math.cos_f32(p.angle) * p.speed * dt
			p.y    += math.sin_f32(p.angle) * p.speed * dt
			p.angle += rand.float32() * 0.8 - 0.4
		case .Egg:
			p.x += (math.sin_f32(p.prog/p.vx1) * math.cos_f32(p.prog/p.vx2)) / 8 * dt
			p.y += (math.sin_f32(p.prog/p.vy1) * math.cos_f32(p.prog/p.vy2)) / 8 * dt
		}
		p.prog += dt
		// respawn when particle drifts out of tile
		if p.x < x || p.x > x+w || p.y < y || p.y > y+h {
			plankton_spawn(p, x, y, w, h)
		}
		// ── draw ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
		switch p.type {
		case .Larva:
			r.fillCircle(p.x, p.y, max(p.radius, 0.5),
				renderer.paint_solid({0.965, 0.604, 0.204, 1}))
		case .LarvaEgg:
			col := [4]f32{0.51, 0.63, 0.77, 1} if p.blue else [4]f32{0.18, 0.28, 0.40, 1}
			r.fillCircle(p.x, p.y, max(p.radius, 0.4), renderer.paint_solid(col))
		case .Egg:
			// Eye halos (original: rgba(241,242,244,0.06) and rgba(255,204,67,0.08))
			r.fillCircle(p.x+p.eox, p.y+p.eoy, p.er2+4,
				renderer.paint_solid({0.95, 0.95, 0.96, 0.06}))
			r.fillCircle(p.x+p.eox, p.y+p.eoy, p.er3+2,
				renderer.paint_solid({1.00, 0.80, 0.26, 0.08}))
			// Red pupil — deterministic jitter replaces Math.random() per frame
			jx := math.sin_f32(p.prog * 0.23) * p.eox * 0.35
			jy := math.cos_f32(p.prog * 0.17) * p.eoy * 0.35
			r.fillCircle(p.x+jx, p.y+jy, max(p.er1, 1),
				renderer.paint_solid({0.60, 0.07, 0.02, 0.22}))
			// Soft body — two layered circles simulate the radial gradient
			// (original: rgba(130,151,180) from 0.09→0 over radius)
			r.fillCircle(p.x, p.y, p.radius,        renderer.paint_solid({0.51, 0.59, 0.71, 0.09}))
			r.fillCircle(p.x, p.y, p.radius * 0.45, renderer.paint_solid({0.60, 0.68, 0.80, 0.06}))
		}
	}
}

render_stress_blackhole :: proc(node: ^Tile_Node, r: ^renderer.Renderer, x, y, w, h: f32) {
	content_h := h - 22
	if !plankton_ready {
		for i in 0..<STRESS_N_VORTEX {
			plankton_spawn(&plankton_pool[i], x, y, w, content_h)
		}
		plankton_ready = true
	}
	// Dark ocean background (original canvas clear: rgba(0,0,20))
	r.fillRect(x, y, w, h, renderer.paint_solid({0.01, 0.01, 0.07, 1}))
	plankton_tick(r, x, y, w, content_h)
	stress_hud(r, x, y, w, h, "plankton", STRESS_N_VORTEX)
}

// ── S9 — Flying Toasters ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Classic screensaver: 40 toasters flying right→left with 5-frame wing
// animation (flyingtoaster.png, 320×64 sprite sheet) + scattered toast.
render_stress_toasters :: proc(node: ^Tile_Node, r: ^renderer.Renderer, x, y, w, h: f32) {
	r.fillRect(x, y, w, h, renderer.paint_solid({0, 0, 0, 1}))
	content_h := h - 22

	// Scattered rotating toast pieces
	if stress_toast_img.id != 0 {
		mat := renderer.active_renderer.createMaterial(stress_toast_img, {1, 1, 1, 1})
		for i in 0..<20 {
			fi  := f32(i)
			tx  := x + math.mod(fi * (w / 20) + math.sin_f32(now * 0.3 + fi) * 5, w - 22)
			ty  := y + math.mod(fi * 7.13 + now * (1.5 + math.mod(fi * 0.3, 2.5)), content_h - 22)
			tsz := 22 + math.mod(fi * 3.1, 14)
			rot := now * 0.25 * (1 if i % 2 == 0 else -1) + fi * 0.8
			r.pushMatrix()
			r.translate(tx + tsz * 0.5, ty + tsz * 0.5)
			r.rotate(rot)
			r.drawImageRectUvMat({-tsz*0.5, -tsz*0.5, tsz, tsz}, {0, 0, 1, 1}, mat)
			r.popMatrix()
		}
	}

	// Flying toasters — right-to-left with parallax depth
	if stress_toaster_img.id != 0 {
		for i in 0..<STRESS_N_TOASTER {
			fi     := f32(i)
			period := 5.0 + math.mod(fi * 1.37, 7.0)
			phase  := fi / f32(STRESS_N_TOASTER)
			t_raw  := math.mod(now / period + phase, 1.0)
			// enter from right, exit left; drift slightly downward
			px     := x + w + 48 - t_raw * (w + 96)
			entry_y := y + math.mod(fi * content_h / f32(STRESS_N_TOASTER), content_h * 0.78)
			py     := entry_y + t_raw * content_h * 0.18
			// size varies for parallax depth
			scale  := 0.45 + math.mod(fi * 0.19, 0.85)
			tsz    := f32(52) * scale
			// wing animation: 10 fps, each toaster offset by phase
			wing   := int(now * 10 + fi * 1.6) % 5
			u0     := f32(wing) / 5.0
			u1     := f32(wing + 1) / 5.0
			mat    := renderer.active_renderer.createMaterial(stress_toaster_img, {1, 1, 1, 1})
			r.drawImageRectUvMat({px, py, tsz, tsz}, {u0, 0, u1, 1}, mat)
		}
	}

	stress_hud(r, x, y, w, h, "toaster", STRESS_N_TOASTER)
}

} // when STRESS_2D

/**
@documentation: Central Configuration Hub
vision: configpkg is the single source of truth for platform-specific paths,
global engine settings, and the future theme system.
*/

// Import logging config for DEBUG flag
import logging "project:logging"

//System behaviour flags
BATCH_MERGING_ENABLED :: true  // Enable batch merging for performance optimization (can be disabled for debugging)

// Memory Management Configuration
BATCH_SAFETY_LIMIT :: #config(BATCH_SAFETY_LIMIT, 1000) // Maximum batches to process per frame (safety limit)
FRAME_ARENA_SIZE :: #config(FRAME_ARENA_SIZE, 12 * 1024 * 1024) // 12MB frame arena size
PATH_ARENA_SIZE :: #config(PATH_ARENA_SIZE, 6 * 1024 * 1024) // 6MB path arena size
ARENA_STATS :: #config(ARENA_STATS, false) // Enable arena memory statistics logging
ARENA_OVERFLOW_CHECKS :: #config(ARENA_OVERFLOW_CHECKS, logging.DEBUG) // Enable arena overflow checking

// --- Build Type Detection ---
// Check if this is a static build (set by build script)
STATIC_BUILD :: #config(STATIC_BUILD, false)

// --- Platform-specific Paths ---
// Use static libraries for static builds, dynamic libraries for dynamic builds
when ODIN_OS == .Windows {
	when STATIC_BUILD {
		HB_PATH :: "extern/odin-harfbuzz/libs/harfbuzz.lib"
	} else {
		HB_PATH :: "system:harfbuzz"
	}
} else when ODIN_OS == .Linux {
	when STATIC_BUILD {
		HB_PATH :: "extern/odin-harfbuzz/libs/libharfbuzz.a"
	} else {
		HB_PATH :: "system:harfbuzz"
	}
} else when ODIN_OS == .Darwin {
	when STATIC_BUILD {
		HB_PATH :: "extern/odin-harfbuzz/libs/libharfbuzz.a"
	} else {
		HB_PATH :: "system:/opt/homebrew/lib/libharfbuzz.dylib"
	}
} else {
	when STATIC_BUILD {
		HB_PATH :: "extern/odin-harfbuzz/libs/libharfbuzz.a"
	} else {
		HB_PATH :: "system:harfbuzz"
	}
}

// --- Future Theme System Placeholders ---
// Moved to settings.json (use_msaa) or handled by the theme system (design tokens).

// --- Font Path Configuration ---
// vision: Centralized font paths for easy asset management and cross-platform overrides.

FONT_PATH_DEFAULT :: "liberation-mono/LiberationMono-Regular.ttf"

FONT_FALLBACKS_ARABIC :: []string {
	"Parastoo/static/Parastoo-Regular.ttf",
	"noto/NotoSansArabic-VariableFont_wdth,wght.ttf",
	"noto/Arabic-Regular.ttf",
	"noto/NotoNaskhArabic-Regular.ttf",
}

FONT_FALLBACKS_HEBREW :: []string{"noto/NotoSansHebrew-Regular.ttf", "noto/Hebrew-Regular.ttf"}

FONT_FALLBACKS_CJK :: []string {
	"noto/NotoSansSC-VariableFont_wght.ttf",
	"noto/NotoSansCJKsc-Regular.ttf",
	"noto/NotoSansCJK-Regular.ttc",
	"noto/NotoSansCJK-Regular.otf",
}

// Persian specific (though often covered by Arabic fonts)
FONT_PATH_PERSIAN :: "Parastoo/static/Parastoo-Regular.ttf"
