DEBUG: Source length: 29964
DEBUG: Source content: package graphics

import vfs "project:vfs"
import txt "project:graphics/text"
import renderer "project:renderer"
import "base:runtime"
import c "core:c"
import "core:fmt"
import "core:mem"
import ft "vendor-local:odin-freetype"
import hb "vendor-local:odin-harfbuzz/harfbuzz"
import hb_ot "vendor-local:odin-harfbuzz/harfbuzz/ot"
import sg "vendor-local:sokol-odin/sokol/gfx"

// using txt (Removed as file-scope 'using' is deprecated in Odin)

// Toggle HarfBuzz shaping (false => disable HB and use naive FT-based shaping)
HB_ENABLED :: true

// Constants
MAX_ATLAS_ROWS :: #config(MAX_ATLAS_ROWS, 512)
MAX_ATLAS_PAGES :: #config(MAX_ATLAS_PAGES, 8)
ATLAS_RING_MODE :: #config(ATLAS_RING_MODE, true)
ATLAS_RECENCY_WINDOW :: #config(ATLAS_RECENCY_WINDOW, 24)
ATLAS_ALLOCATOR_OPTION :: #config(ATLAS_ALLOCATOR_OPTION, 2) // 1=Ring, 2=Shelves (Opt 2)

// Gate 3.1: Enable SDF glyph baking via FT_RENDER_MODE_SDF.
// Requires pip_text_sdf to be active during text rendering (see g2d_text.odin).
// Default: true (SDF gives crisp text at all sizes; disable with -define:ATLAS_SDF_MODE=false).
ATLAS_SDF_MODE :: #config(ATLAS_SDF_MODE, true)

LOG_ATLAS_DEBUG :: #config(LOG_ATLAS_DEBUG, false)
LOG_ATLAS_WRAP :: #config(LOG_ATLAS_WRAP, false)
LOG_GI_VALIDATE :: #config(LOG_GI_VALIDATE, false)
LOG_UV_STABILITY :: #config(LOG_UV_STABILITY, false)
LOG_CACHE_STABILITY :: #config(LOG_CACHE_STABILITY, false)
LOG_GI_FENCE :: #config(LOG_GI_FENCE, false)

// Global FreeType library
@(private)
ft_lib: ft.Library
@(private)
ft_lib_inited: bool

// One Font_File per unique font file path — shared across all sizes.
@(private)
font_file_cache: map[string]^txt.Font_File

ft_lib_init_if_needed :: proc() {
	if ft_lib_inited do return
	ft.init_free_type(&ft_lib)
	ft_lib_inited = true
}

// ---------------- Types ----------------
// ---------------- Global State ----------------

shared_atlas: txt.Shared_Atlas
@(private)
shared_atlas_dirty: bool

// Redundancy removed.

// ---------------- Core Management ----------------

atlas_init_if_needed :: proc() {
	if shared_atlas.initialized do return
	ft_lib_init_if_needed()

	ow, oh := renderer.getAtlasSizeOverride()
	atlas_w, atlas_h := 2048, 2048
	if ow > 0 && oh > 0 {
		atlas_w, atlas_h = int(ow), int(oh)
	}
	shared_atlas.atlas_w = i32(atlas_w)
	shared_atlas.atlas_h = i32(atlas_h)

	shared_atlas.pages = make(
		[dynamic]txt.Atlas_Page,
		0,
		MAX_ATLAS_PAGES,
		runtime.default_allocator(),
	)
	shared_atlas.uv_track = make(map[u32]txt.Tracked_UV, 0, runtime.default_allocator())

	_ = atlas_add_page()
	shared_atlas.initialized = true
}

atlas_frame_prepare :: proc() {
	log_cache_stats_if_due()

	if renderer.get_frame_index() % 60 == 0 {
		atlas_maintenance()
	}

	for i in 0 ..< len(shared_atlas.pages) {
		page := &shared_atlas.pages[i]
		page.write_barrier_y = 0
		page.did_wrap_this_frame = false
		if page.pinned_rows != nil {
			clear(&page.pinned_rows)
		}
		page.defers_this_frame = 0
		page.miss_this_frame = 0
		page.miss_suppressed_this_frame = 0
	}

	shared_atlas.defers_this_frame = 0
	shared_atlas.evictions_this_frame = 0
	shared_atlas.miss_this_frame = 0
	shared_atlas.miss_suppressed_this_frame = 0
	shared_atlas.upload_this_frame = 0
	shared_atlas.preflight_uploaded_this_frame = false
	shared_atlas.did_postdraw_upload = false
	shared_atlas.repacks_needed_this_frame = 0
	shared_atlas.max_y_filled = 0

	// Phase 10.Z: Memory profiling - track atlas data structure growth
	// (Unconditional for leak investigation)
	when #config(LOG_MEMORY, false) {
		frame := renderer.get_frame_index()
		if frame % 120 == 0 {
			total_purge_entries := 0
			total_rows := 0
			for i in 0 ..< len(shared_atlas.pages) {
				page := &shared_atlas.pages[i]
				total_rows += len(page.rows)
				for _, refs in page.purge_index {
					total_purge_entries += len(refs)
				}
			}
			// Also poll text cache
			fonts_cached, glyphs_cached := txt.get_cache_stats()

			fmt.printf(
				"[MEM_PROFILE] frame=%d | Atlas: pages=%d rows=%d purge_entries=%d uv_track=%d | Text: fonts=%d glyphs=%d\n",
				frame,
				len(shared_atlas.pages),
				total_rows,
				total_purge_entries,
				len(shared_atlas.uv_track),
				fonts_cached,
				glyphs_cached,
			)
		}
	}
}

atlas_update_gpu_if_dirty :: proc() {
	if !shared_atlas.initialized do return
	if !shared_atlas_dirty do return

	// Sokol allows only one sg_update_image per image per frame.
	// If flushGpuPrePass is called multiple times in the same frame (e.g. for
	// secondary offscreen RT population in demo_post_flush), skip subsequent
	// atlas uploads. Glyphs first encountered in secondary flushes will be
	// uploaded on the next frame.
	if shared_atlas.last_update_frame == renderer.get_frame_index() do return

	for i in 0 ..< len(shared_atlas.pages) {
		page := &shared_atlas.pages[i]
		if !page.dirty do continue

		data := sg.Image_Data{}
		data.mip_levels[0] = sg.Range {
			ptr  = &page.rgba_pixels[0],
			size = uint(len(page.rgba_pixels)),
		}
		sg.update_image(page.image, data)
		page.dirty = false
	}

	shared_atlas.last_update_frame = renderer.get_frame_index()
	shared_atlas_dirty = false
}

atlas_try_early_upload_for_text_preflight :: proc() {
	// Phase 4.4.1: Deprecated. Early uploads often violate backend constraints (e.g. Metal).
	// Updates now happen exclusively in g2d_end_frame.
}

// ---------------- Page Helpers ----------------

atlas_active_page :: proc() -> ^txt.Atlas_Page {
	if len(shared_atlas.pages) == 0 do return nil
	return &shared_atlas.pages[len(shared_atlas.pages) - 1]
}

atlas_find_row_idx_by_y :: proc(page: ^txt.Atlas_Page, y: i32) -> i32 {
	for i in 0 ..< i32(len(page.rows)) {
		if page.rows[i].y == y do return i
	}
	return -1
}

atlas_maintenance :: proc() {
	if !shared_atlas.initialized do return

	// Threshold for "stale" rows (e.g. 10 seconds at 60fps)
	STALE_THRESHOLD :: 600

	for i in 0 ..< len(shared_atlas.pages) {
		page := &shared_atlas.pages[i]
		purged_in_page := u32(0)

		for j in 0 ..< i32(len(page.rows)) {
			row := &page.rows[j]
			if row.active && (renderer.get_frame_index() - row.last_used_frame > STALE_THRESHOLD) {
				// Proactively reclaim stale row
				row.active = false
				row.h = 0
				row.epoch += 1
				atlas_purge_row_glyphs_page(page, j)
				purged_in_page += 1
			}
		}

		if purged_in_page > 0 && LOG_ATLAS_DEBUG {
			if LOG_ATLAS_DEBUG do fmt.println("[ATLAS_MAINTENANCE] page=", page.id, " purged_rows=", purged_in_page)
		}
	}
}

atlas_purge_entire_page :: proc(page_id: int) {
	if page_id < 0 || page_id >= len(shared_atlas.pages) do return
	page := &shared_atlas.pages[page_id]

	// 1. Purge all rows
	for i in 0 ..< i32(len(page.rows)) {
		atlas_purge_row_glyphs_page(page, i)
		page.rows[i].active = false
		page.rows[i].h = 0
		page.rows[i].epoch += 1
	}

	// 2. Reset packing state
	page.curr_x, page.curr_y = 1, 1
	page.max_row_h = 0
	page.write_barrier_y = 0

	// 3. Clear shelves
	clear(&page.shelves)
	for i in 0 ..< 8 {
		clear(&page.shelf_buckets[i])
		page.next_fit_idx[i] = -1
	}

	// 4. Zero pixels
	for i in 0 ..< len(page.rgba_pixels) {
		page.rgba_pixels[i] = 0
	}
	page.dirty = true
	shared_atlas_dirty = true
}

atlas_add_page :: proc() -> ^txt.Atlas_Page {
	if len(shared_atlas.pages) >= MAX_ATLAS_PAGES {
		// Emergency: Atlas is at page capacity. Find an unpinned page to recycle.
		target_idx := -1
		for i in 0 ..< len(shared_atlas.pages) {
			p := &shared_atlas.pages[i]
			if p.pinned_rows == nil || len(p.pinned_rows) == 0 {
				target_idx = i
				break
			}
		}

		if target_idx != -1 {
			when LOG_ATLAS_DEBUG {
				if LOG_ATLAS_DEBUG do fmt.println("[ATLAS_EMERGENCY_PURGE] recycling page=", target_idx)
			}
			atlas_purge_entire_page(target_idx)
			return &shared_atlas.pages[target_idx]
		}

		return nil
	}

	page := txt.Atlas_Page{}
	page.id = i16(len(shared_atlas.pages))

	w, h := int(shared_atlas.atlas_w), int(shared_atlas.atlas_h)
	page.rgba_pixels = make([]u8, w * h * 4)

	desc := sg.Image_Desc {
		width        = i32(w),
		height       = i32(h),
		pixel_format = .RGBA8,
	}
	desc.usage.dynamic_update = true
	page.image = sg.make_image(desc)

	page.curr_x, page.curr_y = 1, 1
	// Performance: Pre-allocate with reasonable capacities to avoid immediate reallocation
	page.rows = make([dynamic]txt.Atlas_Row, 0, 32, runtime.default_allocator())
	page.pinned_rows = make(map[i32]bool, 16, runtime.default_allocator())
	page.purge_index = make(map[i32][dynamic]txt.Glyph_Row_Ref, 16, runtime.default_allocator())

	// Init shelves with reasonable capacities
	page.shelves = make([dynamic]txt.Shelf, 0, 16, runtime.default_allocator())
	for i in 0 ..< 8 {
		page.shelf_buckets[i] = make([dynamic]i32, 0, 8, runtime.default_allocator())
		page.next_fit_idx[i] = -1
	}

	append(&shared_atlas.pages, page)

	when LOG_ATLAS_DEBUG {
		if LOG_ATLAS_DEBUG do fmt.println("[ATLAS_PAGE_ADD] id=", page.id, " total=", len(shared_atlas.pages))
	}
	return &shared_atlas.pages[len(shared_atlas.pages) - 1]
}

// ---------------- Option 2: txt.Shelf Allocator ----------------

atlas_opt2_try_place_page :: proc(page: ^txt.Atlas_Page, w, h: i32) -> (i32, i32, i32, bool) {
	if ATLAS_ALLOCATOR_OPTION != 2 do return 0, 0, -1, false

	class := atlas_classify_height(h)

	// 1. Try existing shelves in this class
	for shelf_idx in page.shelf_buckets[class] {
		s := &page.shelves[shelf_idx]

		// Liveness check: txt.Shelf is only valid if its row is still active and has same epoch
		row := &page.rows[s.row_idx]
		if !row.active || row.epoch != s.row_epoch do continue

		if s.curr_x + w <= shared_atlas.atlas_w {
			res_x := s.curr_x
			res_y := s.y
			s.curr_x += w + 1
			when LOG_ATLAS_DEBUG {
				if LOG_ATLAS_DEBUG do fmt.println("[SHELF_HIT] page=", page.id, " x=", res_x, " y=", res_y, " w=", w)
			}
			return res_x, res_y, s.row_idx, true
		}
	}

	return 0, 0, -1, false
}

atlas_opt2_create_shelf_page :: proc(page: ^txt.Atlas_Page, h: i32, y: i32) -> bool {
	row_idx := atlas_find_row_idx_by_y(page, y)
	if row_idx < 0 do return false
	row := &page.rows[row_idx]

	class := atlas_classify_height(h)
	shelf_h := i32(16)
	if class == 1 do shelf_h = 32
	if class == 2 do shelf_h = 48
	if class == 3 do shelf_h = 64
	if class == 4 do shelf_h = h // custom size

	if y + shelf_h > shared_atlas.atlas_h do return false

	if row.shelf_idx != -1 {
		// Reuse existing shelf slot. Must remove from old bucket first.
		old_shelf := &page.shelves[row.shelf_idx]
		old_class := old_shelf.size_class

		bucket := &page.shelf_buckets[old_class]
		for i in 0 ..< len(bucket) {
			if bucket[i] == row.shelf_idx {
				unordered_remove(bucket, i)
				break
			}
		}

		old_shelf.y = y
		old_shelf.h = shelf_h
		// Promotion: if we are making a shelf from the current active row,
		// we MUST preserve the curr_x so we don't overwrite glyphs.
		if y == page.curr_y {
			old_shelf.curr_x = page.curr_x
		} else {
			old_shelf.curr_x = 1
		}
		old_shelf.size_class = class
		old_shelf.row_epoch = row.epoch

		append(&page.shelf_buckets[class], row.shelf_idx)
	} else {
		s := txt.Shelf {
			y          = y,
			h          = shelf_h,
			curr_x     = (y == page.curr_y) ? page.curr_x : 1,
			size_class = class,
			row_idx    = row_idx,
			row_epoch  = row.epoch,
		}
		append(&page.shelves, s)
		row.shelf_idx = i32(len(page.shelves) - 1)
		append(&page.shelf_buckets[class], row.shelf_idx)
	}

	when LOG_ATLAS_DEBUG {
		fmt.println(
			"[SHELF_ADD] page=",
			page.id,
			" class=",
			class,
			" y=",
			y,
			" h=",
			shelf_h,
			" rx=",
			row_idx,
		)
	}
	return true
}

// ---------------- Allocation and Packing ----------------

atlas_begin_new_row_ring_for_page :: proc(page: ^txt.Atlas_Page, required_h: i32) -> bool {
	if required_h <= 0 do return true

	// Before moving, if Option 2 is enabled, the current "finished" row can become a shelf
	if ATLAS_ALLOCATOR_OPTION == 2 && page.max_row_h > 0 {
		atlas_opt2_create_shelf_page(page, page.max_row_h, page.curr_y)
	}

	found_next := false
	test_y := page.curr_y + page.max_row_h + 1

	// Scan for a non-pinned slot in the ring
	// We scan forward from test_y, wrapping around if needed.
	MAX_SCAN_STEPS :: 100
	scan_found := false
	for step in 0 ..< MAX_SCAN_STEPS {
		if test_y + required_h > shared_atlas.atlas_h {
			test_y = 1
			page.did_wrap_this_frame = true
			page.wrap_count += 1
			page.last_wrap_frame = renderer.get_frame_index()
			when LOG_ATLAS_WRAP {
				if LOG_ATLAS_DEBUG do fmt.println("[ATLAS_WRAP] page=", page.id, " barrier=", page.write_barrier_y)
			}
		}

		if !atlas_is_region_pinned_page(page, test_y, required_h) {
			scan_found = true
			break
		}

		// Skip forward. We use 16 as a reasonable grain, but we could be more precise.
		test_y += 16
	}

	if !scan_found {
		when LOG_ATLAS_DEBUG {
			fmt.println("[ATLAS_FAIL] Could not find unpinned region for height=", required_h)
		}
		return false
	}

	found_next = true

	if !found_next do return false

	page.curr_y = test_y
	page.curr_x = 1
	page.max_row_h = 0

	_ = atlas_acquire_row_page(page, required_h, page.curr_y)
	return true
}

atlas_is_region_pinned_page :: proc(page: ^txt.Atlas_Page, y, h: i32) -> bool {
	if page.pinned_rows == nil do return false
	y1 := y + h
	for i in 0 ..< i32(len(page.rows)) {
		r := &page.rows[i]
		if r.y + r.h > y && r.y < y1 {
			if i in page.pinned_rows && page.pinned_rows[i] {
				return true
			}
		}
	}
	return false
}

atlas_purge_row_glyphs_page :: proc(page: ^txt.Atlas_Page, row_idx: i32) {
	refs, ok := &page.purge_index[row_idx]
	if !ok do return

	for ref in refs {
		if ref.font != nil {
			delete_key(&ref.font.glyphs, ref.glyph_key)
		}
	}
	clear(refs)
}

atlas_acquire_row_page :: proc(page: ^txt.Atlas_Page, required_h: i32, y: i32) -> i32 {
	y1 := y + required_h
	reuse_idx := -1

	// Scan for ANY rows that overlap the new region [y, y+required_h)
	// and purge them. This is critical for ring buffer mode to avoid aliasing.
	for i in 0 ..< i32(len(page.rows)) {
		r := &page.rows[i]
		if !r.active do continue

		// Intersection check: r.y < y1 && r.y+r.h > y
		if r.y < y1 && r.y + r.h > y {
			// This row is being partially or fully overwritten. Purge it.
			atlas_purge_row_glyphs_page(page, i)
			r.active = false

			// If it happens to start exactly at 'y', we can reuse this slot
			if r.y == y {
				reuse_idx = int(i)
			}
		}
	}

	if reuse_idx != -1 {
		idx := i32(reuse_idx)
		page.rows[idx].active = true
		page.rows[idx].epoch += 1
		page.rows[idx].h = required_h // Initialize height
		page.rows[idx].last_used_frame = renderer.get_frame_index()

		// Clear the pixel memory for this row to prevent "ghosting" or corruption
		// from previous occupants of this space.
		// Add 1px padding above and below to prevent bilinear filtering artifacts.
		atlas_w := int(shared_atlas.atlas_w)
		clear_y := int(max(0, y - 1))
		clear_h := int(required_h) + 2
		start_byte := clear_y * atlas_w * 4
		byte_count := clear_h * atlas_w * 4

		if start_byte + byte_count <= len(page.rgba_pixels) {
			mem.set(&page.rgba_pixels[start_byte], 0, byte_count)
		}

		return idx
	}

	append(
		&page.rows,
		txt.Atlas_Row {
			y               = y,
			h               = required_h, // Initialize height
			active          = true,
			last_used_frame = renderer.get_frame_index(),
			epoch           = 1,
			shelf_idx       = -1,
		},
	)

	// Also clear memory for new rows (though usually appends are fresh,
	// in ring mode 'y' might wrap to existing memory space)
	atlas_w := int(shared_atlas.atlas_w)
	clear_y := int(max(0, y - 1))
	clear_h := int(required_h) + 2
	start_byte := clear_y * atlas_w * 4
	byte_count := clear_h * atlas_w * 4
	if start_byte + byte_count <= len(page.rgba_pixels) {
		mem.set(&page.rgba_pixels[start_byte], 0, byte_count)
	}

	return i32(len(page.rows) - 1)
}

atlas_grow_row_height :: proc(page: ^txt.Atlas_Page, row_idx: i32, new_h: i32) {
	if row_idx < 0 || row_idx >= i32(len(page.rows)) do return

	row := &page.rows[row_idx]
	if new_h <= row.h do return

	old_h := row.h
	y := row.y

	// We are expanding into [y + old_h, y + new_h).
	// We must ensure this new band is clear of other rows and pixels.

	// 1. Purge overlapping rows in the delta region
	y_start_check := y + old_h
	y_end_check := y + new_h

	for i in 0 ..< i32(len(page.rows)) {
		r := &page.rows[i]
		if !r.active || i == row_idx do continue

		// Intersection check: r.y < y_end_check && r.y + r.h > y_start_check
		if r.y < y_end_check && r.y + r.h > y_start_check {
			atlas_purge_row_glyphs_page(page, i)
			r.active = false
		}
	}

	// 2. Clear pixel memory for the extension (with padding safety)
	// We clear from old_h (start of new region) to new_h + 1
	atlas_w := int(shared_atlas.atlas_w)

	clear_y_start := int(y + old_h)
	clear_y_end := int(y + new_h + 1)

	start_byte := clear_y_start * atlas_w * 4
	byte_count := (clear_y_end - clear_y_start) * atlas_w * 4

	if start_byte + byte_count <= len(page.rgba_pixels) {
		mem.set(&page.rgba_pixels[start_byte], 0, byte_count)
	}

	// 3. Update height
	row.h = new_h
}

atlas_active_row_index_page :: proc(page: ^txt.Atlas_Page) -> i32 {
	for i in 0 ..< i32(len(page.rows)) {
		if page.rows[i].active && page.rows[i].y == page.curr_y {
			return i
		}
	}
	return -1
}

ensure_glyph_in_atlas :: proc(
	font: ^txt.Font,
	glyph_index: u32,
	style_bits: u32 = 0,
) -> (
	txt.Glyph_Info,
	bool,
) {
	glyph_key := (glyph_index << 2) | style_bits
	gi, found := font.glyphs[glyph_key]
	if found && gi_is_live(gi) {
		if int(gi.page_id) >= 0 && int(gi.page_id) < len(shared_atlas.pages) {
			page := &shared_atlas.pages[gi.page_id]
			if gi.row_index >= 0 && gi.row_index < i32(len(page.rows)) {
				page.pinned_rows[gi.row_index] = true
				page.rows[gi.row_index].last_used_frame = renderer.get_frame_index()
				atlas_update_write_barrier_page(page, gi.y1_px)
			}
		}
		gi.last_used_frame = renderer.get_frame_index()
		font.glyphs[glyph_key] = gi
		return gi, true
	}

	// Phase 10.Z: Track Atlas Misses
	LOG_ATLAS_MISS :: #config(LOG_ATLAS_MISS, false)
	if !found && LOG_ATLAS_MISS {
		fmt.printf("[ATLAS_MISS] font=%p idx=%d bits=%d\n", font, glyph_index, style_bits)
	}

	active_page := atlas_active_page()
	if active_page == nil {
		active_page = atlas_add_page()
		if active_page == nil do return txt.Glyph_Info{}, false
	}

	if font.file == nil do return txt.Glyph_Info{}, false

	// Shared face: set the size for this handle before any FreeType rasterization call.
	ft.set_pixel_sizes(font.file.face, 0, font.size_px)

	// Gate 3.1: in SDF mode, always load outline (no inline render) then render as SDF.
	// In normal mode, unstyled glyphs use .Render flag for inline rasterization.
	load_flags: ft.Load_Flags
	when ATLAS_SDF_MODE {
		load_flags = {}
	} else {
		load_flags = {.Render}
	}
	if style_bits != 0 {
		load_flags = {} // Must load as outline to apply bold/italic transforms
	}

	if err := ft.load_glyph(font.file.face, glyph_index, load_flags); err != ft.Error.Ok {
		return txt.Glyph_Info{}, false
	}

	// Apply style transforms on the outline (before rasterization).
	if style_bits != 0 {
		if (style_bits & txt.FONT_STYLE_BOLD) != 0 {
			ft.glyphslot_embolden(font.file.face.glyph)
		}
		if (style_bits & txt.FONT_STYLE_ITALIC) != 0 {
			shear := ft.Fixed(0.25 * 65536.0)
			mat := ft.Matrix {
				xx = 1 << 16,
				xy = shear,
				yx = 0,
				yy = 1 << 16,
			}
			ft.outline_transform(&font.file.face.glyph.outline, &mat)
		}
	}

	// Rasterize: SDF mode always calls render_glyph(.SDF).
	// Normal mode only needs render_glyph for styled glyphs (unstyled was rendered inline).
	when ATLAS_SDF_MODE {
		if err := ft.render_glyph(font.file.face.glyph, .SDF); err != ft.Error.Ok {
			return txt.Glyph_Info{}, false
		}
	} else {
		if style_bits != 0 {
			if err := ft.render_glyph(font.file.face.glyph, .Normal); err != ft.Error.Ok {
				return txt.Glyph_Info{}, false
			}
		}
	}

	glyph := font.file.face.glyph
	bmp := glyph.bitmap

	// ---------------- Allocation Stage ----------------
	x, y: i32
	row_idx: i32 = -1
	found_slot := false

	// Attempt Option 2 (Shelves)
	if ATLAS_ALLOCATOR_OPTION == 2 {
		if sx, sy, srx, sok := atlas_opt2_try_place_page(
			active_page,
			i32(bmp.width),
			i32(bmp.rows),
		); sok {
			x, y = sx, sy
			row_idx = srx
			found_slot = true
		}
	}

	if !found_slot {
		// Fallback to Ring Mode
		if active_page.curr_x + i32(bmp.width) >= shared_atlas.atlas_w {
			if !atlas_begin_new_row_ring_for_page(active_page, i32(bmp.rows)) {
				if len(shared_atlas.pages) < MAX_ATLAS_PAGES {
					active_page = atlas_add_page()
					if active_page == nil do return txt.Glyph_Info{}, false
				} else {
					return txt.Glyph_Info{}, false
				}
			}
		}

		if active_page.curr_y + i32(bmp.rows) >= shared_atlas.atlas_h {
			if !atlas_begin_new_row_ring_for_page(active_page, i32(bmp.rows)) {
				if len(shared_atlas.pages) < MAX_ATLAS_PAGES {
					active_page = atlas_add_page()
					if active_page == nil do return txt.Glyph_Info{}, false
				} else {
					return txt.Glyph_Info{}, false
				}
			}
		}

		x, y = active_page.curr_x, active_page.curr_y
		row_idx = atlas_active_row_index_page(active_page)
	}

	// FIXED: Ensure row height accommodates the new glyph BEFORE blitting,
	// because growing clears the pixel memory (and would wipe the blitted glyph).
	if row_idx >= 0 && i32(bmp.rows) > active_page.rows[row_idx].h {
		atlas_grow_row_height(active_page, row_idx, i32(bmp.rows))
	}

	// ---------------- Blit Stage ----------------
	atlas_w := int(shared_atlas.atlas_w)
	for yy in 0 ..< int(bmp.rows) {
		src_row := yy * int(bmp.pitch)
		dst_row := ((int(y) + yy) * atlas_w + int(x)) * 4
		for xx in 0 ..< int(bmp.width) {
			val := bmp.buffer[src_row + xx]
			di := dst_row + xx * 4
			if di + 3 < len(active_page.rgba_pixels) {
				active_page.rgba_pixels[di + 0] = 255
				active_page.rgba_pixels[di + 1] = 255
				active_page.rgba_pixels[di + 2] = 255
				active_page.rgba_pixels[di + 3] = val
			}
		}
	}

	active_page.dirty = true
	shared_atlas_dirty = true

	// Apply exact UV mapping for 1:1 pixel mapping
	aw := f32(shared_atlas.atlas_w)
	ah := f32(shared_atlas.atlas_h)
	u0f := f32(x) / aw
	v0f := f32(y) / ah
	u1f := f32(x + i32(bmp.width)) / aw
	v1f := f32(y + i32(bmp.rows)) / ah
	// Clamp in case of very small glyphs
	if u1f < u0f {u1f = u0f}
	if v1f < v0f {v1f = v0f}

	ginfo := txt.Glyph_Info {
		page_id         = active_page.id,
		u0              = u0f,
		v0              = v0f,
		u1              = u1f,
		v1              = v1f,
		width           = f32(bmp.width),
		height          = f32(bmp.rows),
		bearing_x       = f32(glyph.bitmap_left),
		bearing_y       = f32(glyph.bitmap_top),
		advance         = f32(glyph.advance.x >> 6),
		last_used_frame = renderer.get_frame_index(),
		row_index       = row_idx,
		y0_px           = y,
		y1_px           = y + i32(bmp.rows),
	}

	if ginfo.row_index >= 0 {
		ginfo.row_epoch = active_page.rows[ginfo.row_index].epoch

		active_page.rows[ginfo.row_index].last_used_frame = renderer.get_frame_index()
		if active_page.pinned_rows ==
		   nil {active_page.pinned_rows = make(map[i32]bool, 0, runtime.default_allocator())}
		active_page.pinned_rows[ginfo.row_index] = true

		// Phase 4.4: Register for purge tracking
		// Create new bucket entry for this row using persistent allocator if needed
		if ginfo.row_index not_in active_page.purge_index {
			active_page.purge_index[ginfo.row_index] = make(
				[dynamic]txt.Glyph_Row_Ref,
				0,
				0,
				runtime.default_allocator(),
			)
		}
		append(&active_page.purge_index[ginfo.row_index], txt.Glyph_Row_Ref{font, glyph_key})
	}

	font.glyphs[glyph_key] = ginfo

	if !found_slot {
		active_page.curr_x += i32(bmp.width) + 1
		if i32(bmp.rows) > active_page.max_row_h {
			active_page.max_row_h = i32(bmp.rows)
		}
	}

	atlas_update_write_barrier_page(active_page, ginfo.y1_px)

	return ginfo, true
}

// get_or_create_font_file returns the shared Font_File for path, loading the file
// and initialising FreeType + HarfBuzz exactly once per unique path.
@(private)
get_or_create_font_file :: proc(path: string, data: []u8) -> ^txt.Font_File {
	if ff, ok := font_file_cache[path]; ok {
		ff.ref_count += 1
		return ff
	}

	ff := new(txt.Font_File, runtime.default_allocator())
	ff.font_data = data

	ft_lib_init_if_needed()
	if err := ft.new_memory_face(ft_lib, &data[0], c.long(len(data)), 0, &ff.face);
	   err != ft.Error.Ok {
		free(ff)
		return nil
	}

	if HB_ENABLED {
		hb_blob := hb.blob_create(
			cast([^]byte)&data[0],
			c.uint(len(data)),
			hb.memory_mode_t.MEMORY_MODE_READONLY,
			nil,
			nil,
		)
		if hb_blob != nil {
			ff.hb_blob = hb_blob
			hb_face := hb.face_create(hb_blob, 0)
			if hb_face != nil {
				ff.hb_face = hb_face
			}
		}
	}

	ff.ref_count = 1
	font_file_cache[path] = ff
	return ff
}

create_font_atlas :: proc(path: string, size: u32) -> (txt.Font, bool) {
	font: txt.Font
	font.glyphs = make(map[u32]txt.Glyph_Info, 128, runtime.default_allocator())
	font.size_px = size

	// Load raw bytes (or reuse existing; Font_File owns them)
	data, ok := vfs.loadAsset(path, .Font)
	if !ok do return font, false

	ff := get_or_create_font_file(path, data)
	if ff == nil {
		delete(data)
		return font, false
	}
	// If the file was already cached, get_or_create returned the existing ff and
	// incremented ref_count; we no longer own 'data' — free it.
	if raw_data(ff.font_data) != raw_data(data) {
		delete(data)
	}
	font.file = ff

	// Set size on the shared face so metrics are valid for this size.
	ft.set_pixel_sizes(ff.face, 0, size)

	if HB_ENABLED && ff.hb_face != nil {
		hb_font := hb.font_create(ff.hb_face)
		if hb_font != nil {
			font.hb_font = hb_font
			hb_ot.font_set_funcs(font.hb_font)
			hb.font_set_scale(font.hb_font, c.int(size) * 64, c.int(size) * 64)
			hb.font_set_ppem(font.hb_font, c.uint(size), c.uint(size))
		}
	}

	metrics := ff.face.size.metrics
	font.ascent = f32(metrics.ascender) / 64.0
	font.descent = f32(metrics.descender) / 64.0
	font.line_height = f32(metrics.height) / 64.0

	if font.line_height <= 0 { font.line_height = f32(size) * 1.2 }
	if font.ascent <= 0      { font.ascent      = f32(size) * 0.8 }

	atlas_init_if_needed()
	if len(shared_atlas.pages) > 0 {
		font.atlas_image = shared_atlas.pages[0].image
		font.rgba_pixels = shared_atlas.pages[0].rgba_pixels
	}

	font.atlas_w = shared_atlas.atlas_w
	font.atlas_h = shared_atlas.atlas_h
	font.atlas_dirty = true
	shared_atlas_dirty = true

	return font, true
}

// ---------------- Support Helpers ----------------

atlas_update_write_barrier_page :: proc(page: ^txt.Atlas_Page, y1: i32) {
	if y1 > page.write_barrier_y {
		page.write_barrier_y = y1
	}
}

ensure_glyph_in_atlas_preflight :: proc(
	font: ^txt.Font,
	glyph_index: u32,
	style_bits: u32 = 0,
) -> bool {
	glyph_key := (glyph_index << 2) | style_bits
	gi, found := font.glyphs[glyph_key]
	if found && gi_is_live(gi) {
		if int(gi.page_id) >= 0 && int(gi.page_id) < len(shared_atlas.pages) {
			page := &shared_atlas.pages[gi.page_id]
			if gi.row_index >= 0 && gi.row_index < i32(len(page.rows)) {
				if page.pinned_rows == nil {page.pinned_rows = make(map[i32]bool)}
				page.pinned_rows[gi.row_index] = true
				atlas_update_write_barrier_page(page, gi.y1_px)
			}
		}
		return true
	}
	return false
}

gi_is_live :: proc(gi: txt.Glyph_Info) -> bool {
	if gi.width == 0 || gi.height == 0 do return true
	if int(gi.page_id) < 0 || int(gi.page_id) >= len(shared_atlas.pages) do return false
	page := &shared_atlas.pages[gi.page_id]
	if gi.row_index < 0 || gi.row_index >= i32(len(page.rows)) do return false
	r := page.rows[gi.row_index]
	// A glyph is live if its row is active AND the epoch matches.
	// (Epoch mismatch means the row space was reclaimed for something else).
	return r.active && r.epoch == gi.row_epoch
}


atlas_pin_glyph_if_cached :: proc(
	font: ^txt.Font,
	glyph_index: u32,
	style_bits: u32,
	frame_index: u32,
) {
	glyph_key := (glyph_index << 2) | style_bits
	gi, ok := font.glyphs[glyph_key]
	if !ok || !gi_is_live(gi) do return

	if int(gi.page_id) >= 0 && int(gi.page_id) < len(shared_atlas.pages) {
		page := &shared_atlas.pages[gi.page_id]
		if gi.row_index >= 0 && gi.row_index < i32(len(page.rows)) {
			if page.pinned_rows == nil {page.pinned_rows = make(map[i32]bool)}
			page.pinned_rows[gi.row_index] = true
			page.rows[gi.row_index].last_used_frame = frame_index
			atlas_update_write_barrier_page(page, gi.y1_px)
		}
	}
}

atlas_classify_height :: proc(h: i32) -> u8 {
	if h <= 16 do return 0
	if h <= 32 do return 1
	if h <= 48 do return 2
	if h <= 64 do return 3
	return 4
}

// ---------------- Diagnostics ----------------

uv_stability_track :: proc(gid: u32, gi: txt.Glyph_Info, was_repack: bool) {
	when LOG_UV_STABILITY {
		// Minimal track logic
	}
}

log_cache_stats_if_due :: proc() {
	when LOG_CACHE_STABILITY {
		// Minimal log logic
	}
}

g2d_debug_get_shared_atlas_image :: proc(page_id: i16 = 0) -> (sg.Image, i32, i32) {
	if int(page_id) >= 0 && int(page_id) < len(shared_atlas.pages) {
		p := shared_atlas.pages[page_id]
		return p.image, shared_atlas.atlas_w, shared_atlas.atlas_h
	}
	return sg.Image{}, 0, 0
}

// ---------------- Cleanup ----------------

destroy_font :: proc(font: ^txt.Font) {
	if font == nil do return
	if font.hb_font != nil {hb.font_destroy(font.hb_font); font.hb_font = nil}
	delete(font.glyphs)
	// Decrement shared file ref; destroy when last reference drops
	if font.file != nil {
		font.file.ref_count -= 1
		if font.file.ref_count <= 0 {
			if font.file.hb_face != nil {hb.face_destroy(font.file.hb_face); font.file.hb_face = nil}
			if font.file.hb_blob != nil {hb.blob_destroy(font.file.hb_blob); font.file.hb_blob = nil}
			if font.file.face != nil {_ = ft.done_face(font.file.face); font.file.face = ft.Face{}}
			if font.file.font_data != nil {delete(font.file.font_data)}
			for k, v in font_file_cache {
				if v == font.file {delete_key(&font_file_cache, k); break}
			}
			free(font.file)
		}
		font.file = nil
	}
	font.atlas_dirty = false
}

@(private)
font_files_shutdown :: proc() {
	delete(font_file_cache)
	font_file_cache = nil
}

DEBUG: Source length: 18143
DEBUG: Source content: package graphics

import "base:runtime"
import "core:fmt"
import "core:math"
import "core:math/linalg"
import "core:mem"
import renderer "project:renderer"
import logging "project:logging"
import txt "project:graphics/text"
import sg "vendor-local:sokol-odin/sokol/gfx"

// ============================================================================
// TEXT RENDERING SYSTEM
// ============================================================================

// Text rendering script-specific offsets (dx, dy) in pixels
Text_Offsets :: struct {
	latin:  [2]f32,
	arabic: [2]f32,
	hebrew: [2]f32,
	cjk:    [2]f32,
	emoji:  [2]f32,
	symbol: [2]f32,
	other:  [2]f32,
}

// Glyph quad for batched text rendering
Glyph_Quad :: struct {
	dest:  [4]f32,
	uv:    [4]f32,
	color: [4]f32,
}

// Convert text modifiers to style bits for rendering
modifiers_to_style_bits :: proc(mods: renderer.Text_Modifiers) -> u32 {
	bits: u32 = 0
	if .Bold in mods do bits |= renderer.FONT_STYLE_BOLD
	if .Italic in mods do bits |= renderer.FONT_STYLE_ITALIC
	return bits
}

@(private)
draw_decorations :: proc(
	x, y, width: f32,
	font: ^txt.Font,
	color: [4]f32,
	mods: renderer.Text_Modifiers,
) {
	if mods == {} do return
	// Phase 22: Target-aware DPI scaling for text layout
	dpi_to_use := ctx.active_rt.dpi_scale if ctx.active_rt != nil else ctx.dpi_scale
	inv_dpi := 1.0 / dpi_to_use
	if .Underline in mods {
		thick := math.max(font.line_height / 16.0, 1.0) * inv_dpi
		pos := font.descent * 0.5 * inv_dpi
		g2d_fill_rect(x, y - pos, width, thick, renderer.paint_solid(color))
	}
	if .Strikethrough in mods {
		thick := math.max(font.line_height / 16.0, 1.0) * inv_dpi
		pos := font.ascent * 0.4 * inv_dpi
		g2d_fill_rect(x, y - pos, width, thick, renderer.paint_solid(color))
	}
}

@(private)
_emit_text_bucket_page :: proc(page_idx: int, quads: []Glyph_Quad) {
	if len(quads) == 0 do return
	page := &shared_atlas.pages[page_idx]
	old_img := ctx.curr_image
	ctx.curr_image = page.image
	b := _g2d_get_text_sdf_batch() when ATLAS_SDF_MODE else get_batch()
	for q in quads {
		p1 := apply_transform(q.dest.x, q.dest.y)
		p2 := apply_transform(q.dest.x + q.dest.z, q.dest.y)
		p3 := apply_transform(q.dest.x + q.dest.z, q.dest.y + q.dest.w)
		p4 := apply_transform(q.dest.x, q.dest.y + q.dest.w)

		v1 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p1, {q.uv.x, q.uv.y}, q.color}
		v2 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p2, {q.uv.z, q.uv.y}, q.color}
		v3 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p3, {q.uv.z, q.uv.w}, q.color}
		v4 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p4, {q.uv.x, q.uv.w}, q.color}

		add_vertices(b, v1, v2, v3, v1, v3, v4)
	}
	ctx.curr_image = old_img
}

@(private)
g2d_draw_shaped_spans :: proc(
	x, y: f32,
	spans: []txt.Shaped_Span,
	color: [4]f32 = {0, 0, 0, 0},
	mods: renderer.Text_Modifiers = {},
) {
	if len(spans) == 0 do return
	c := resolve_color(color)
	first_glyph_done := false

	// Phase 6.1: Use frame arena for per-frame temporary data
	frame_scratch := g2d_get_frame_scratch()
	widths := make([dynamic]f32, 0, len(spans), frame_scratch)
	total_line_w: f32 = 0
	dpi_to_use := (ctx.active_rt != nil) ? ctx.active_rt.dpi_scale : ctx.dpi_scale
	inv_dpi := 1.0 / dpi_to_use
	for s in spans {
		run_adv: f32 = 0
		for i in 0 ..< len(s.glyphs) {run_adv += math.abs(s.glyphs[i].advance_x)}
		run_adv *= inv_dpi
		append(&widths, run_adv)
		total_line_w += run_adv
	}
	para_dir: txt.Direction = .LTR
	if len(spans) > 0 {para_dir = spans[0].dir}

	pen_x := x
	pen_edge := x + total_line_w // used when para_dir==RTL (right-anchored)
	first_span := true
	prev_dir: txt.Direction
	prev_script: txt.Script_Kind

	// Pass 1: Preflight and PIN every glyph for all spans in the entire line.
	style_bits := modifiers_to_style_bits(mods)
	for s in spans {
		use_font := cast(^txt.Font)s.font
		for i in 0 ..< len(s.glyphs) {
			g := s.glyphs[i]
			if g.id == 0 do continue
			atlas_pin_glyph_if_cached(use_font, g.id, style_bits, ctx.frame_index)
			_, _ = ensure_glyph_in_atlas(use_font, g.id, style_bits)
		}
	}
	// Ensure any pending atlas updates are uploaded before the render pass begins
	atlas_try_early_upload_for_text_preflight()

	// Pass 2: Fill buckets
	for idx_s in 0 ..< len(spans) {
		s := spans[idx_s]
		width := widths[idx_s]
		use_font := cast(^txt.Font)s.font


		origin: f32 = (para_dir == .RTL) ? (pen_edge - width) : pen_x
		acc := f32(0)

		for i in 0 ..< len(s.glyphs) {
			g := s.glyphs[i]
			adv := math.abs(g.advance_x)
			if g.id == 0 {
				acc += adv
				continue
			}

			atlas_pin_glyph_if_cached(use_font, g.id, style_bits, ctx.frame_index)
			gi, ok := ensure_glyph_in_atlas(use_font, g.id, style_bits)

			if !ok || !gi_is_live(gi) {
				if !ok do acc += adv
				else do ctx.gi_stale_skips_this_frame += 1
				continue
			}

			ofx, ofy: f32
			#partial switch s.script {
			case .Latin:
				ofx, ofy = ctx.text_offsets.latin[0], ctx.text_offsets.latin[1]
			case .Arabic:
				ofx, ofy = ctx.text_offsets.arabic[0], ctx.text_offsets.arabic[1]
			case .Hebrew:
				ofx, ofy = ctx.text_offsets.hebrew[0], ctx.text_offsets.hebrew[1]
			case .CJK:
				ofx, ofy = ctx.text_offsets.cjk[0], ctx.text_offsets.cjk[1]
			case .Emoji:
				ofx, ofy = ctx.text_offsets.emoji[0], ctx.text_offsets.emoji[1]
			case .Symbol:
				ofx, ofy = ctx.text_offsets.symbol[0], ctx.text_offsets.symbol[1]
			case:
				ofx, ofy = ctx.text_offsets.other[0], ctx.text_offsets.other[1]
			}

			dpi_to_use := (ctx.active_rt != nil) ? ctx.active_rt.dpi_scale : ctx.dpi_scale
	inv_dpi := 1.0 / dpi_to_use
		render_x := (origin + acc + (gi.bearing_x + g.offset_x + ofx) * inv_dpi)
		render_y := (y - (gi.bearing_y + g.offset_y - ofy) * inv_dpi)

		dest := [4]f32{render_x, render_y, gi.width * inv_dpi, gi.height * inv_dpi}
		if !first_glyph_done {
			pad := G2D_FIRST_GLYPH_PAD_X * inv_dpi
			dest.x -= pad
			dest.z += pad
			first_glyph_done = true
		}
		uv := [4]f32{gi.u0, gi.v0, gi.u1, gi.v1}

		if int(gi.page_id) >= 0 && int(gi.page_id) < MAX_ATLAS_PAGES {
			append(&ctx.text_buckets[gi.page_id], Glyph_Quad{dest, uv, c})
		}
		acc += adv * inv_dpi
		}

		if para_dir == .RTL {pen_edge -= width} else {pen_x += width}
		prev_dir = s.dir
		prev_script = s.script
		if first_span {first_span = false}
	}

	if mods != {} && len(spans) > 0 {
		draw_decorations(x, y, total_line_w, cast(^txt.Font)spans[0].font, c, mods)
	}

	// Pass 3: Emit buckets and CLEAR
	for p in 0 ..< len(shared_atlas.pages) {
		bucket := ctx.text_buckets[p]
		if len(bucket) == 0 do continue
		_emit_text_bucket_page(p, bucket[:])
		clear(&ctx.text_buckets[p]) // CRITICAL: Fix O(N^2) double-drawing
	}
}

g2d_draw_text_immediate :: proc(
	x, y: f32,
	text: string,
	font: renderer.Font_Handle,
	color: [4]f32 = {0, 0, 0, 0},
	mods: renderer.Text_Modifiers = {},
) {
	font_ptr, font_ok := fm_resolve_handle(font)
	// Performance: Removed redundant runtime.default_allocator() assignment
	if !font_ok || font_ptr == nil || len(text) == 0 {
		return
	}

	// Use normalized UVs; no per-call image size queries needed
	c := resolve_color(color)
	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		if color == {0, 0, 0, 0} {
			logging.debug(
				.G2D,
				fmt.tprintf("[TEXT_COLOR] default-param used; resolved curr_color=%v", c),
				)
		}
	}

	// If we have an hb_font, use shaping (ligatures, kerning, features)
	// Use itemized shaping for full script support
	spans := renderer.shape_text_itemized(font_ptr, nil, nil, text)
	style_bits := modifiers_to_style_bits(mods)
	g2d_draw_shaped_spans(x, y, spans, color, mods)
}

g2d_draw_text_advanced_immediate :: proc(
	x, y: f32,
	text: string,
	primary: renderer.Font_Handle,
	fallbacks: []renderer.Font_Handle,
	color: [4]f32 = {0, 0, 0, 0},
	mods: renderer.Text_Modifiers = {},
) {
	primary_ptr, primary_ok := fm_resolve_handle(primary)
	// Performance: Removed redundant runtime.default_allocator() assignment
	if !primary_ok || primary_ptr == nil || len(text) == 0 {
		return
	}

	c := resolve_color(color)
	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		if color == {0, 0, 0, 0} {
			logging.debug(
				.G2D,
				fmt.tprintf("[TEXT_COLOR] default-param used; resolved curr_color=%v", c),
				)
		}
	}

	// Preserve BiDi controls for FriBidi/HB analysis
	clean := text

	// Resolve fallback handles to concrete pointers
	fb_ptrs: [8]^txt.Font
	n_fb := min(len(fallbacks), 8)
	for i in 0 ..< n_fb {
		if p, ok := fm_resolve_handle(fallbacks[i]); ok {
			fb_ptrs[i] = p
		}
	}

	// Resolve default chain for the itemizer
	size := renderer.get_font_size(primary_ptr)
	defs := fm_get_default_fallbacks(size)

	spans := renderer.shape_text_itemized(primary_ptr, fb_ptrs[:n_fb], defs, clean)
	g2d_draw_shaped_spans(x, y, spans[:], color, mods)
}

@(private)
g2d_draw_shaped_spans_styled :: proc(
	x, y: f32,
	spans: []txt.Shaped_Span,
	styles: []txt.TextSpan,
	default_color: [4]f32 = {0, 0, 0, 0},
	mods: renderer.Text_Modifiers = {},
) {
	if len(spans) == 0 do return
	base_color := resolve_color(default_color)

	// Helper to find style for a byte cluster (styles assumed sorted, non-overlapping)
	find_style :: proc(styles: []txt.TextSpan, cluster: i32) -> (txt.TextSpan, bool) {
		for i in 0 ..< len(styles) {
			s := styles[i]
			if cluster >= s.start && cluster < s.end {
				return s, true
			}
		}
		return txt.TextSpan{}, false
	}

	// Phase 6.1: Use frame arena for per-frame temporary data
	frame_scratch := g2d_get_frame_scratch()
	widths := make([dynamic]f32, 0, len(spans), frame_scratch)
	total_line_w: f32 = 0
	dpi_to_use := (ctx.active_rt != nil) ? ctx.active_rt.dpi_scale : ctx.dpi_scale
	inv_dpi := 1.0 / dpi_to_use
	for s in spans {
		run_adv: f32 = 0
		for i in 0 ..< len(s.glyphs) {
			g := s.glyphs[i]
			adv := math.abs(g.advance_x)
			st, ok := find_style(styles, g.cluster)
			sc := ok && st.scale > 0 ? st.scale : 1.0
			run_adv += adv * sc
		}
		run_adv *= inv_dpi
		append(&widths, run_adv)
		total_line_w += run_adv
	}
	para_dir: txt.Direction = .LTR
	if len(spans) > 0 {para_dir = spans[0].dir}

	pen_x := x
	pen_edge := x + total_line_w
	first_span := true
	prev_dir: txt.Direction
	prev_script: txt.Script_Kind

	// Pass 1: Preflight for all glyphs
	style_bits := modifiers_to_style_bits(mods)
	for s in spans {
		use_font := cast(^txt.Font)s.font
		for i in 0 ..< len(s.glyphs) {
			g := s.glyphs[i]
			if g.id == 0 do continue
			atlas_pin_glyph_if_cached(use_font, g.id, style_bits, ctx.frame_index)
			_, _ = ensure_glyph_in_atlas(use_font, g.id, style_bits)
		}
	}
	// Ensure any pending atlas uploads are committed prior to drawing
	atlas_try_early_upload_for_text_preflight()

	// Pass 2: Fill buckets with styled geometry
	for idx_s in 0 ..< len(spans) {
		s := spans[idx_s]
		width := widths[idx_s]
		use_font := cast(^txt.Font)s.font


		origin: f32 = (para_dir == .RTL) ? (pen_edge - width) : pen_x
		acc := f32(0)

		for i in 0 ..< len(s.glyphs) {
			g := s.glyphs[i]
			adv := math.abs(g.advance_x)
			if g.id == 0 {
				// Advance still scales to preserve spacing of missing glyphs
				st1, ok1 := find_style(styles, g.cluster)
				sc := ok1 && st1.scale > 0 ? st1.scale : 1.0
				acc += adv * sc
				continue
			}

			gi, ok := ensure_glyph_in_atlas(use_font, g.id, style_bits)
			if !ok || !gi_is_live(gi) {
				st2, ok2 := find_style(styles, g.cluster)
				sc := ok2 && st2.scale > 0 ? st2.scale : 1.0
				if !ok {
					acc += adv * sc
				} else {
					ctx.gi_stale_skips_this_frame += 1
				}
				continue
			}

			// Resolve per-script offset
			ofx, ofy: f32
			#partial switch s.script {
			case .Latin:
				ofx, ofy = ctx.text_offsets.latin[0], ctx.text_offsets.latin[1]
			case .Arabic:
				ofx, ofy = ctx.text_offsets.arabic[0], ctx.text_offsets.arabic[1]
			case .Hebrew:
				ofx, ofy = ctx.text_offsets.hebrew[0], ctx.text_offsets.hebrew[1]
			case .CJK:
				ofx, ofy = ctx.text_offsets.cjk[0], ctx.text_offsets.cjk[1]
			case .Emoji:
				ofx, ofy = ctx.text_offsets.emoji[0], ctx.text_offsets.emoji[1]
			case .Symbol:
				ofx, ofy = ctx.text_offsets.symbol[0], ctx.text_offsets.symbol[1]
			case:
				ofx, ofy = ctx.text_offsets.other[0], ctx.text_offsets.other[1]
			}

			// Resolve style attributes
			st3, ok3 := find_style(styles, g.cluster)
			col := base_color
			shift := f32(0)
			sc := f32(1)
			if ok3 {
				if st3.color != ([4]f32{0, 0, 0, 0}) {col = st3.color}
				if st3.baseline_shift != 0 {shift = st3.baseline_shift}
				if st3.scale > 0 {sc = st3.scale}
			}

			render_x := math.floor(origin + acc + (gi.bearing_x + g.offset_x + ofx) * sc + 0.5)
			// Positive baseline_shift moves text up; our Y grows down, so subtract
			render_y := math.floor(y - (gi.bearing_y + g.offset_y) * sc + ofy - shift + 0.5)

			dest := [4]f32{render_x, render_y, gi.width * sc, gi.height * sc}
			uv := [4]f32{gi.u0, gi.v0, gi.u1, gi.v1}

			if int(gi.page_id) >= 0 && int(gi.page_id) < MAX_ATLAS_PAGES {
				append(&ctx.text_buckets[gi.page_id], Glyph_Quad{dest, uv, col})
			}

			acc += adv * sc
		}

		if para_dir == .RTL {pen_edge -= width} else {pen_x += width}
		prev_dir = s.dir
		prev_script = s.script
		if first_span {first_span = false}
	}

	if mods != {} && len(spans) > 0 {
		draw_decorations(x, y, total_line_w, cast(^txt.Font)spans[0].font, base_color, mods)
	}

	// Emit buckets
	for p in 0 ..< len(shared_atlas.pages) {
		bucket := ctx.text_buckets[p]
		if len(bucket) == 0 do continue
		_emit_text_bucket_page(p, bucket[:])
		clear(&ctx.text_buckets[p])
	}
}

g2d_draw_text_rich :: proc(
	x, y: f32,
	rich: txt.RichText,
	primary: renderer.Font_Handle,
	fallbacks: []renderer.Font_Handle,
	default_color: [4]f32 = {0, 0, 0, 0},
) {
	primary_ptr, primary_ok := fm_resolve_handle(primary)
	if !primary_ok || primary_ptr == nil || len(rich.text) == 0 {return}
	// Resolve fallback handles
	fb_ptrs: [8]^txt.Font
	n_fb := min(len(fallbacks), 8)
	for i in 0 ..< n_fb {
		if p, ok := fm_resolve_handle(fallbacks[i]); ok {
			fb_ptrs[i] = p
		}
	}
	// Resolve default chain for the itemizer
	size := renderer.get_font_size(primary_ptr)
	defs := fm_get_default_fallbacks(size)
	spans := txt.shape_rich_text(rich, primary_ptr, fb_ptrs[:n_fb], defs)
	g2d_draw_shaped_spans_styled(x, y, spans, rich.spans, default_color)
}

g2d_draw_text_layout_immediate :: proc(
	x, y: f32,
	lines: []txt.Visual_Line,
	y_offsets: []f32,
	color: [4]f32 = {0, 0, 0, 0},
	mods: renderer.Text_Modifiers = {},
) {
	// Performance: Removed redundant runtime.default_allocator() assignment
	if len(lines) == 0 do return

	// Pass 1: Global Preflight for the entire layout block.
	// This ensures all glyphs for all lines are both allocated and pinned
	// before the single shared GPU upload.
	style_bits := modifiers_to_style_bits(mods)
	for line in lines {
		for s in line.spans {
			use_font := cast(^txt.Font)s.font
			for g in s.glyphs {
				if g.id == 0 do continue
				atlas_pin_glyph_if_cached(use_font, g.id, style_bits, ctx.frame_index)
				_, _ = ensure_glyph_in_atlas(use_font, g.id, style_bits)
			}
		}
	}

	// Phase 4.4: Single Shared GPU Upload for all lines (Phase 4.4: Moved to end_frame)

	// Pass 2: Concurrent-style drawing of lines
	for i in 0 ..< len(lines) {
		line := lines[i]
		yo := y_offsets[i]
		g2d_draw_shaped_spans(x, y + yo, line.spans, color, mods)
	}
}

@(private)
_g2d_draw_shaped_glyph :: proc(font: ^txt.Font, g: txt.Shaped_Glyph, color: [4]f32) {
	if g.id == 0 do return

	info, ok := ensure_glyph_in_atlas(font, g.id, 0)
	if !ok do return

	// Setup quad vertices in local space based on glyph metrics
	dpi_to_use := (ctx.active_rt != nil) ? ctx.active_rt.dpi_scale : ctx.dpi_scale
	inv_dpi := 1.0 / dpi_to_use
	w, h := info.width * inv_dpi, info.height * inv_dpi
	ox, oy := (info.bearing_x + g.offset_x) * inv_dpi, (-info.bearing_y + g.offset_y) * inv_dpi

	p1 := apply_transform(ox, oy)
	p2 := apply_transform(ox + w, oy)
	p3 := apply_transform(ox + w, oy + h)
	p4 := apply_transform(ox, oy + h)

	c := color
	page := info.page_id

	// Note: For rotated text (TextPath), we use direct primitive batching
	// instead of bucketed axis-aligned quads.
	ctx.curr_image = shared_atlas.pages[page].image
	b := _g2d_get_text_sdf_batch() when ATLAS_SDF_MODE else get_batch()

	v1 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p1, {info.u0, info.v0}, c}
	v2 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p2, {info.u1, info.v0}, c}
	v3 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p3, {info.u1, info.v1}, c}
	v4 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p4, {info.u0, info.v1}, c}

	add_vertices(b, v1, v2, v3, v1, v3, v4)
}

g2d_draw_text_on_path :: proc(text: string, font: renderer.Font_Handle, color: [4]f32) {
	font_ptr, font_ok := fm_resolve_handle(font)
	if len(text) == 0 || !font_ok || font_ptr == nil || len(ctx.current_path) < 2 do return
	c := resolve_color(color)
	points := _g2d_get_flat_points()
	if len(points) < 2 do return

	// 1. Calculate path segment lengths and cumulative distance
	dists := make([]f32, len(points), mem.arena_allocator(&ctx.arena))
	total_len: f32 = 0
	for i in 1 ..< len(points) {
		d := linalg.distance(points[i - 1], points[i])
		total_len += d
		dists[i] = total_len
	}

	// 2. Shape text
	spans := txt.shape_text_itemized(font_ptr, nil, nil, text)

	// 3. Place glyphs
	curr_dist: f32 = 0
	for span in spans {
		for g in span.glyphs {
			// Center the glyph on its advance?
			// For TextPath, we usually treat 'curr_dist' as the center or start of the glyph.
			// Let's use start + half advance for the sample point.
			sample_dist := curr_dist + g.advance_x * 0.5
			if sample_dist > total_len do break

			// Find segment
			seg_idx := 0
			for i in 1 ..< len(points) {
				if dists[i] >= sample_dist {
					seg_idx = i
					break
				}
			}
			if seg_idx == 0 do break

			// Interpolate
			p1, p2 := points[seg_idx - 1], points[seg_idx]
			seg_start_dist := dists[seg_idx - 1]
			seg_end_dist := dists[seg_idx]
			t := (sample_dist - seg_start_dist) / (seg_end_dist - seg_start_dist)
			pos := linalg.lerp(p1, p2, t)
			tangent := linalg.normalize(p2 - p1)
			angle := math.atan2(tangent.y, tangent.x)

			dpi_to_use := (ctx.active_rt != nil) ? ctx.active_rt.dpi_scale : ctx.dpi_scale
	inv_dpi := 1.0 / dpi_to_use
			// Draw glyph with rotation
			g2d_push_matrix()
			g2d_translate(pos.x, pos.y)
			g2d_rotate(angle)
			// Adjust for glyph offset and center it
			_g2d_draw_shaped_glyph(span.font, g, c)
			g2d_pop_matrix()

			curr_dist += g.advance_x * inv_dpi
		}
	}
}

DEBUG: Source content: package graphics

import "core:math"
import "core:fmt"
import "core:time"
import "base:runtime"

// ============================================================================
// Phase 10D.3.2 - Advanced Animation System
// ============================================================================
// Intent: Provide a comprehensive keyframe animation system with timeline support
// for complex animations, supporting interpolation, easing, and event callbacks.
// ============================================================================

// Animation Interpolation Types
Animation_Interpolation :: enum {
    Linear,
    Step,
    Bezier,
    CatmullRom,
}

// Animation Playback State
Animation_State :: enum {
    Stopped,
    Playing,
    Paused,
    Completed,
}

// Keyframe Data Structure
Keyframe :: struct {
    time:       f32,       // Time in milliseconds
    value:      f32,       // Keyframe value
    in_tangent: f32,       // In tangent for smooth interpolation
    out_tangent: f32,      // Out tangent for smooth interpolation
    interpolation: Animation_Interpolation,
}

// Animation Track for a single property
Animation_Track :: struct {
    name:           string,
    keyframes:      []Keyframe,
    current_value:  f32,
    start_value:    f32,
    end_value:      f32,
    duration:       f32,
    loop:           bool,
    ping_pong:      bool,
    speed:          f32,
    state:          Animation_State,
    elapsed_time:   f32,
    playback_dir:   f32,  // 1.0 for forward, -1.0 for reverse
}

// Animation Timeline containing multiple tracks
Animation_Timeline :: struct {
    name:           string,
    tracks:         []Animation_Track,
    duration:       f32,
    state:          Animation_State,
    elapsed_time:   f32,
    loop:           bool,
    ping_pong:      bool,
    speed:          f32,
    playback_dir:   f32,
    on_complete:    proc(timeline: ^Animation_Timeline),
    on_start:       proc(timeline: ^Animation_Timeline),
    on_stop:        proc(timeline: ^Animation_Timeline),
}

// Animation Controller for managing multiple timelines
Animation_Controller :: struct {
    timelines:      []^Animation_Timeline,
    active_timeline: ^Animation_Timeline,
    global_time:    f32,
    is_paused:      bool,
}

// ============================================================================
// Keyframe Management
// ============================================================================

// Create a new keyframe
create_keyframe :: proc(time, value: f32, interpolation: Animation_Interpolation = .Linear) -> Keyframe {
    kf := Keyframe{}
    kf.time = time
    kf.value = value
    kf.in_tangent = 0.0
    kf.out_tangent = 0.0
    kf.interpolation = interpolation
    return kf
}

// Add a keyframe to a track
add_keyframe :: proc(track: ^Animation_Track, time, value: f32, interpolation: Animation_Interpolation = .Linear) {
    keyframe := create_keyframe(time, value, interpolation)
    
    // For now, just append the keyframe
    // We'll implement proper sorted insertion later
    old_len := len(track.keyframes)
    new_len := old_len + 1
    
    // Create a new array with the new element
    new_array := make([]Keyframe, new_len)
    for i := 0; i < old_len; i += 1 {
        new_array[i] = track.keyframes[i]
    }
    new_array[old_len] = keyframe
    track.keyframes = new_array
    
    // Update track duration
    if time > track.duration {
        track.duration = time
    }
}

// ============================================================================
// Animation Track Management
// ============================================================================

// Create a new animation track
create_animation_track :: proc(name: string) -> Animation_Track {
    track := Animation_Track{}
    track.name = name
    track.keyframes = []Keyframe{}
    track.current_value = 0.0
    track.start_value = 0.0
    track.end_value = 0.0
    track.duration = 0.0
    track.loop = false
    track.ping_pong = false
    track.speed = 1.0
    track.state = .Stopped
    track.elapsed_time = 0.0
    track.playback_dir = 1.0
    return track
}

// Start playing an animation track
start_animation_track :: proc(track: ^Animation_Track) {
    if track == nil do return
    
    track.state = .Playing
    track.elapsed_time = 0.0
    track.playback_dir = 1.0
    
    // Initialize values
    if len(track.keyframes) > 0 {
        track.start_value = track.keyframes[0].value
        track.end_value = track.keyframes[len(track.keyframes) - 1].value
        track.current_value = track.start_value
    }
}

// Stop an animation track
stop_animation_track :: proc(track: ^Animation_Track) {
    if track == nil do return
    track.state = .Stopped
    track.elapsed_time = 0.0
}

// Pause an animation track
pause_animation_track :: proc(track: ^Animation_Track) {
    if track == nil do return
    if track.state == .Playing {
        track.state = .Paused
    }
}

// Resume a paused animation track
resume_animation_track :: proc(track: ^Animation_Track) {
    if track == nil do return
    if track.state == .Paused {
        track.state = .Playing
    }
}

// Update an animation track
update_animation_track :: proc(track: ^Animation_Track, dt_ms: f32) -> (f32, bool) {
    if track == nil || track.state != .Playing do return track.current_value, false
    
    // Apply speed and direction
    track.elapsed_time += dt_ms * track.speed * track.playback_dir
    
    // Handle completion
    completed := false
    if track.elapsed_time >= track.duration {
        if track.loop {
            if track.ping_pong {
                track.playback_dir *= -1.0
                track.elapsed_time = track.duration - (track.elapsed_time - track.duration)
            } else {
                track.elapsed_time = 0.0
            }
        } else {
            track.elapsed_time = track.duration
            track.state = .Completed
            completed = true
        }
    } else if track.elapsed_time < 0.0 {
        if track.loop {
            if track.ping_pong {
                track.playback_dir *= -1.0
                track.elapsed_time = -track.elapsed_time
            } else {
                track.elapsed_time = track.duration
            }
        } else {
            track.elapsed_time = 0.0
            track.state = .Completed
            completed = true
        }
    }
    
    // Calculate current value through interpolation
    track.current_value = interpolate_track(track)
    
    return track.current_value, completed
}

// Interpolate track value based on current time
interpolate_track :: proc(track: ^Animation_Track) -> f32 {
    if len(track.keyframes) == 0 do return 0.0
    if len(track.keyframes) == 1 do return track.keyframes[0].value
    
    // Find the keyframe segment we're in
    prev_kf := &track.keyframes[0]
    next_kf := &track.keyframes[1]
    
    for i in 1..<len(track.keyframes) {
        if track.elapsed_time <= track.keyframes[i].time {
            next_kf = &track.keyframes[i]
            prev_kf = &track.keyframes[i - 1]
            break
        }
    }
    
    // Calculate interpolation factor
    segment_duration := next_kf.time - prev_kf.time
    segment_progress := track.elapsed_time - prev_kf.time
    t := segment_progress / segment_duration
    
    // Clamp t to [0, 1]
    t = math.clamp(t, 0.0, 1.0)
    
    // Apply interpolation based on type
    switch prev_kf.interpolation {
        case .Linear:
            return linear_interpolate(prev_kf.value, next_kf.value, t)
        case .Step:
            return prev_kf.value
        case .Bezier:
            return bezier_interpolate(prev_kf.value, next_kf.value, 
                                    prev_kf.out_tangent, next_kf.in_tangent, t)
        case .CatmullRom:
            return catmull_rom_interpolate(track, prev_kf, next_kf, t)
    }
    
    return prev_kf.value
}

// ============================================================================
// Animation Timeline Management
// ============================================================================

// Create a new animation timeline
create_animation_timeline :: proc(name: string) -> ^Animation_Timeline {
    timeline := new(Animation_Timeline)
    timeline.name = name
    timeline.tracks = []Animation_Track{}
    timeline.duration = 0.0
    timeline.state = .Stopped
    timeline.elapsed_time = 0.0
    timeline.loop = false
    timeline.ping_pong = false
    timeline.speed = 1.0
    timeline.playback_dir = 1.0
    return timeline
}

// Add a track to a timeline
add_track_to_timeline :: proc(timeline: ^Animation_Timeline, track: Animation_Track) {
    if timeline == nil do return
    
    // Update timeline duration
    if track.duration > timeline.duration {
        timeline.duration = track.duration
    }
    
    old_len := len(timeline.tracks)
    new_len := old_len + 1
    
    // Create a new array with the new element
    new_array := make([]Animation_Track, new_len)
    for i := 0; i < old_len; i += 1 {
        new_array[i] = timeline.tracks[i]
    }
    new_array[old_len] = track
    timeline.tracks = new_array
}

// Start playing a timeline
start_animation_timeline :: proc(timeline: ^Animation_Timeline) {
    if timeline == nil do return
    
    timeline.state = .Playing
    timeline.elapsed_time = 0.0
    timeline.playback_dir = 1.0
    
    // Start all tracks
    for i in 0..<len(timeline.tracks) {
        start_animation_track(&timeline.tracks[i])
    }
    
    // Call on_start callback
    if timeline.on_start != nil {
        timeline.on_start(timeline)
    }
}

// Stop a timeline
stop_animation_timeline :: proc(timeline: ^Animation_Timeline) {
    if timeline == nil do return
    
    timeline.state = .Stopped
    timeline.elapsed_time = 0.0
    
    // Stop all tracks
    for i in 0..<len(timeline.tracks) {
        stop_animation_track(&timeline.tracks[i])
    }
    
    // Call on_stop callback
    if timeline.on_stop != nil {
        timeline.on_stop(timeline)
    }
}

// Pause a timeline
pause_animation_timeline :: proc(timeline: ^Animation_Timeline) {
    if timeline == nil do return
    if timeline.state == .Playing {
        timeline.state = .Paused
        
        // Pause all tracks
        for i in 0..<len(timeline.tracks) {
            pause_animation_track(&timeline.tracks[i])
        }
    }
}

// Resume a paused timeline
resume_animation_timeline :: proc(timeline: ^Animation_Timeline) {
    if timeline == nil do return
    if timeline.state == .Paused {
        timeline.state = .Playing
        
        // Resume all tracks
        for i in 0..<len(timeline.tracks) {
            resume_animation_track(&timeline.tracks[i])
        }
    }
}

// Update a timeline
update_animation_timeline :: proc(timeline: ^Animation_Timeline, dt_ms: f32) -> bool {
    if timeline == nil || timeline.state != .Playing do return false
    
    // Apply speed and direction
    adjusted_dt := dt_ms * timeline.speed * timeline.playback_dir
    timeline.elapsed_time += adjusted_dt
    
    // Handle completion
    completed := false
    if timeline.elapsed_time >= timeline.duration {
        if timeline.loop {
            if timeline.ping_pong {
                timeline.playback_dir *= -1.0
                timeline.elapsed_time = timeline.duration - (timeline.elapsed_time - timeline.duration)
            } else {
                timeline.elapsed_time = 0.0
            }
        } else {
            timeline.elapsed_time = timeline.duration
            timeline.state = .Completed
            completed = true
            
            // Call on_complete callback
            if timeline.on_complete != nil {
                timeline.on_complete(timeline)
            }
        }
    } else if timeline.elapsed_time < 0.0 {
        if timeline.loop {
            if timeline.ping_pong {
                timeline.playback_dir *= -1.0
                timeline.elapsed_time = -timeline.elapsed_time
            } else {
                timeline.elapsed_time = timeline.duration
            }
        } else {
            timeline.elapsed_time = 0.0
            timeline.state = .Completed
            completed = true
            
            // Call on_complete callback
            if timeline.on_complete != nil {
                timeline.on_complete(timeline)
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Update all tracks
    for i in 0..<len(timeline.tracks) {
        update_animation_track(&timeline.tracks[i], adjusted_dt)
    }
    
    return completed
}

// ============================================================================
// Animation Controller Management
// ============================================================================

// Create a new animation controller
create_animation_controller :: proc() -> ^Animation_Controller {
    controller := new(Animation_Controller)
    controller.timelines = []^Animation_Timeline{}
    controller.global_time = 0.0
    controller.is_paused = false
    return controller
}

// Add a timeline to the controller
add_timeline_to_controller :: proc(controller: ^Animation_Controller, timeline: ^Animation_Timeline) {
    if controller == nil || timeline == nil do return
    old_len := len(controller.timelines)
    new_len := old_len + 1
    
    // Create a new array with the new element
    new_array := make([]^Animation_Timeline, new_len)
    for i := 0; i < old_len; i += 1 {
        new_array[i] = controller.timelines[i]
    }
    new_array[old_len] = timeline
    controller.timelines = new_array
}

// Set the active timeline
set_active_timeline :: proc(controller: ^Animation_Controller, timeline: ^Animation_Timeline) {
    if controller == nil do return
    controller.active_timeline = timeline
}

// Update the animation controller
update_animation_controller :: proc(controller: ^Animation_Controller, dt_ms: f32) {
    if controller == nil || controller.is_paused do return
    
    controller.global_time += dt_ms
    
    // Update all timelines
    for i in 0..<len(controller.timelines) {
        timeline := controller.timelines[i]
        if timeline.state == .Playing {
            update_animation_timeline(timeline, dt_ms)
        }
    }
}

// ============================================================================
// Interpolation Functions
// ============================================================================

// Linear interpolation
linear_interpolate :: proc(a, b, t: f32) -> f32 {
    return a + (b - a) * t
}

// Bezier interpolation
bezier_interpolate :: proc(p0, p1, c0, c1, t: f32) -> f32 {
    // Cubic bezier interpolation
    t2 := t * t
    t3 := t2 * t
    mt := 1.0 - t
    mt2 := mt * mt
    mt3 := mt2 * mt
    
    return mt3 * p0 + 3.0 * mt2 * t * c0 + 3.0 * mt * t2 * c1 + t3 * p1
}

// Catmull-Rom interpolation
catmull_rom_interpolate :: proc(track: ^Animation_Track, prev_kf, next_kf: ^Keyframe, t: f32) -> f32 {
    // Find previous and next keyframes for Catmull-Rom
    prev_prev_kf := prev_kf
    next_next_kf := next_kf
    
    // Get previous keyframe
    for i in 0..<len(track.keyframes) {
        if &track.keyframes[i] == prev_kf && i > 0 {
            prev_prev_kf = &track.keyframes[i - 1]
            break
        }
    }
    
    // Get next keyframe
    for i in 0..<len(track.keyframes) {
        if &track.keyframes[i] == next_kf && i < len(track.keyframes) - 1 {
            next_next_kf = &track.keyframes[i + 1]
            break
        }
    }
    
    // Catmull-Rom interpolation
    return catmull_rom(prev_prev_kf.value, prev_kf.value, next_kf.value, next_next_kf.value, t)
}

// Catmull-Rom interpolation (4 points)
catmull_rom :: proc(p0, p1, p2, p3, t: f32) -> f32 {
    // Catmull-Rom spline interpolation
    t2 := t * t
    t3 := t2 * t
    
    return 0.5 * (
        (2.0 * p1) +
        (-p0 + p2) * t +
        (2.0 * p0 - 5.0 * p1 + 4.0 * p2 - p3) * t2 +
        (-p0 + 3.0 * p1 - 3.0 * p2 + p3) * t3
    )
}

// ============================================================================
// Utility Functions
// ============================================================================

// Get track by name
get_track_by_name :: proc(timeline: ^Animation_Timeline, name: string) -> ^Animation_Track {
    if timeline == nil do return nil
    
    for i in 0..<len(timeline.tracks) {
        if timeline.tracks[i].name == name {
            return &timeline.tracks[i]
        }
    }
    
    return nil
}

// Get track value by name
get_track_value :: proc(timeline: ^Animation_Timeline, name: string) -> (f32, bool) {
    track := get_track_by_name(timeline, name)
    if track == nil do return 0.0, false
    return track.current_value, true
}

// Check if timeline is playing
is_timeline_playing :: proc(timeline: ^Animation_Timeline) -> bool {
    return timeline != nil && timeline.state == .Playing
}

// Check if timeline is completed
is_timeline_completed :: proc(timeline: ^Animation_Timeline) -> bool {
    return timeline != nil && timeline.state == .Completed
}

//import core services
import "base:runtime"
import fmt "core:fmt"
import "core:math"
import "core:math/linalg"

import "core:reflect"
import "core:strings"

//import sokol ressources
import ft "vendor-local:odin-freetype"
import sg "vendor-local:sokol-odin/sokol/gfx"

//import library internal modules
import "core:mem"
import assets "project:assets"
import config "project:configpkg"
import logging "project:logging"//
import renderer "project:renderer"
import shaders "project:graphics/shaders"
import txt "project:graphics/text"
import platform "project:platform"


G2D_FIRST_GLYPH_PAD_X :: #config(G2D_FIRST_GLYPH_PAD_X, 0.0)

// Gate 1.1: Linear-light blending flag.
// Phase 1 (this commit): bloom shaders always gamma-correct (linearise → blend → encode).
// Phase 2 (future): when LINEAR_BLENDING=true, the screen pass routes through an intermediate
// RGBA8 RT so all alpha blending happens in linear light; a final gamma-blit encodes to sRGB.
// Enable Phase 2 with: -define:LINEAR_BLENDING=true
LINEAR_BLENDING :: #config(LINEAR_BLENDING, false)

// Debug: pad the first glyph quad slightly to the left to safeguard negative side-bearing

// Global sampler filter kinds — alias to renderer.Sampler_Filter (single source of truth)
G2D_Sampler_Filter :: renderer.Sampler_Filter


G2D_Batch :: struct {
	v_offset:                   i32,
	v_count:                    i32,
	image:                      sg.Image,
	// SDF shape params (used when pipeline == pip_sdf)
	use_sdf_params:             bool,
	sdf_params:                 shaders.Sdf_Fs_Params,

	// Phase 10.4.b: capture sampler per-batch to allow advanced sampling without affecting draw order
	sampler:                    sg.Sampler,
	// Hard clip (GPU scissor) captured at batch-record time
	scissor_on:                 bool,
	scissor:                    [4]f32, // x,y,w,h in pixels
	target:                     ^renderer.Render_Target, // nil for default

	// Phase 11: Pipeline per batch (for Stencil)
	pipeline:                   sg.Pipeline,
	stencil_ref:                u8,

	// Phase 11.1: Stencil batch identification for batched stencil operations
	is_stencil_batch:           bool, // Identifies batches that require stencil operations
	stencil_fill_rule:          renderer.Fill_Rule, // Fill rule for stencil batches
	stencil_paint:              renderer.Paint, // Paint information for stencil cover quad coloring

	// Phase 11.2: Stencil Cover Quad (Pre-calculated to avoid appending during render pass)
	cover_v_offset:             i32,
	cover_v_count:              i32,

	// Phase 11.3: Stencil/Gradient Params pre-calculated
	min_x, max_x, min_y, max_y: f32,
	dx, dy, size:               f32,
	flushed:                    bool,
	gpu_v_offset:               i32, // Phase 6: absolute byte offset in GPU streaming buffer
	gpu_offset_assigned:        bool, // Phase 6: true once gpu_v_offset has been written

	// Gate 4.1: GPU instanced particles — v_count is 0 for these; use instance_count instead.
	is_instanced:               bool,
	instance_count:             i32,
	inst_buf_offset:            i32, // byte offset into ctx.particle_inst_buf
}

// Render Pass Types for enhanced context tracking
Pass_Type :: enum {
	None, // No active render pass
	Screen, // Direct screen rendering pass
	Offscreen, // Offscreen render target pass
	Compute, // Compute pass (future use)
	Custom, // Custom/other pass type
	Stencil, // Stencil pass for fill rules
}

// Pipeline set identifier - replaces magic numbers [0] and [1]
G2D_Pipeline_Set :: enum {
	Screen, // Main window/swapchain rendering
	Offscreen, // Offscreen render targets with depth/stencil
	OffscreenNoDepth, // Offscreen render targets without depth/stencil (shadows)
}

// Pipeline variants (keep existing from canvas_core.odin)
G2D_Pipeline_Variant :: enum {
	Normal,
	StencilWriteNonZero,
	StencilWriteEvenOdd,
	StencilCover,
	Clippable,
	StencilCoverNested,
	Blit, // Specialized pipeline for offscreen-to-screen blitting
	MaskWrite, // REPLACE op with level ref — winding-independent mask write
	// Gate 1.3: Blend modes
	BlendAdd,      // Additive: particles, glows
	BlendMultiply, // Multiply: darken overlays
	BlendScreen,   // Screen: lighten, highlights
}

// Pipeline collection structure (keep existing from canvas_core.odin)
G2D_Pipelines :: struct {
	pip:                       sg.Pipeline,
	pip_stencil_write_nonzero: sg.Pipeline,
	pip_stencil_write_evenodd: sg.Pipeline,
	pip_stencil_cover:         sg.Pipeline,
	pips_clippable:            [4]sg.Pipeline,
	pips_stencil_cover_nested: [4]sg.Pipeline,
	pip_blit:                  sg.Pipeline, // Blit pipeline for offscreen-to-screen
	pips_mask_write:           [4]sg.Pipeline, // REPLACE op, ref=level — winding-independent
	pip_sdf:                   sg.Pipeline, // SDF AA pipeline for circles and rounded rects
	pip_text_sdf:              sg.Pipeline, // Gate 3.1: SDF text pipeline
	// Gate 1.3: Blend mode pipelines
	pip_blend_add:             sg.Pipeline, // Additive blending
	pip_blend_multiply:        sg.Pipeline, // Multiply blending
	pip_blend_screen:          sg.Pipeline, // Screen blending
}

// Pipeline manager - replaces all_pips[2] array
// Note: uses a fixed array indexed by enum value to avoid map[enum] call_indirect
// crashes on WASM (Odin map hashers use proc pointers with mismatched WASM types).
G2D_Pipeline_Manager :: struct {
	sets:        [G2D_Pipeline_Set]G2D_Pipelines,
	active_set:  G2D_Pipeline_Set,
	initialized: bool,
}

G2D_Scissor_State :: struct {
	rect:   [4]f32,
	target: rawptr,
}

G2D_Context :: struct {
	vertices:                  [dynamic]renderer.G2D_Vertex,
	batches:                   [dynamic]G2D_Batch,
	curr_image:                sg.Image,
	white_image:               sg.Image,
	white_view:                sg.View,
	pip:                       sg.Pipeline,

	// Phase 11: Current pipeline state
	curr_pipeline:             sg.Pipeline,
	sdf_shader:                sg.Shader, // SDF shape shader (distinct from solid_color shader)
	text_sdf_shader:           sg.Shader, // Gate 3.1: SDF text shader
	bind:                      sg.Bindings,
	smp:                       sg.Sampler,
	curr_color:                [4]f32,
	screen_w:                  f32,
	screen_h:                  f32,
	prev_screen_w:             f32, // for resize-triggered shadow cache invalidation
	prev_screen_h:             f32,
	matrix_stack:              [dynamic]matrix[3, 3]f32,
	curr_matrix:               matrix[3, 3]f32,
	screen_matrix_save:        matrix[3, 3]f32, // Phase 22: Isolated screen-space matrix
	screen_matrix_stack_save:  [dynamic]matrix[3, 3]f32, // Phase 22: Isolated screen-space stack
	image_views:               map[u32]sg.View, // Cache to store views per image (keyed by sg.Image.id to avoid map[struct] WASM crash)
	// Phase 4.2: per-frame bookkeeping and atlas updates
	frame_index:               u32,
	dirty_fonts:               [dynamic]renderer.Font_Handle,
	text_offsets:              Text_Offsets,

	// Per-frame stale-guard diagnostics
	gi_stale_skips_this_frame: u32,

	// Default fallback chains per pixel size (computed lazily)
	default_fallbacks:         map[u32][dynamic]renderer.Font_Handle,

	// Current scissor state (captured per batch)
	curr_scissor_on:           bool,
	curr_scissor:              [4]f32,
	curr_scissor_target:       rawptr, // Pointer to the target this scissor belongs to

	// BugFixM3 [2026-01-10]: streaming vertex buffer tracking to prevent heap corruption from buffer over-append across mid-frame flushes.
	// Rationale: sg.append_buffer may be called multiple times per frame (e.g. on scissor flush); we guard against exceeding buffer capacity.
	vbuf_capacity:             i32,
	debug_appended_bytes:      i32,

	// Phase 4.4: Bucketed text rendering to minimize texture swaps
	text_buckets:              [MAX_ATLAS_PAGES][dynamic]Glyph_Quad,

	// Phase 5: Input and DPI
	input:                     platform.Input_State,
	dpi_scale:                 f32,

	// Phase 6 Stability: Transient Arena for per-frame scratch allocations
	arena:                     mem.Arena,
	arena_buffer:              []byte,

	// --- ALLOCATOR ARCHITECTURE REDESIGN ---
	// Three owned arenas, never exposed to application context

	// Per-frame scratch: lives from g2d_new_frame to g2d_end_frame
	// Reset once per frame. Holds batch lists, text buckets, matrix scratch.
	frame_arena:               mem.Arena,
	frame_arena_buf:           []byte,
	frame_arena_peak:          i32, // Track peak usage for diagnostics

	// Per-path scratch: reset after EVERY g2d_path_fill / g2d_path_stroke call
	// Holds contours, tessellation indices, stencil vertex temp buffers.
	// These never need to outlive a single fill/stroke operation.
	path_arena:                mem.Arena,
	path_arena_buf:            []byte,
	path_arena_peak:           i32, // Track peak usage for diagnostics

	// Phase 6B: Path API State
	current_path:              [dynamic]renderer.Path_Point,

	// Phase 10.1: Offscreen Rendering
	active_rt:                 ^renderer.Render_Target,
	// Store main window format for cross-platform compatibility
	main_window_color_format:  sg.Pixel_Format,
	main_window_depth_format:  sg.Pixel_Format,


	// Phase 10.4.b: Sampler management (cache + current)
	sampler_cache:             map[u32]sg.Sampler,
	curr_sampler:              sg.Sampler,
	// Logical sampler settings — captured into deferred commands at record time
	curr_sampler_filter:       renderer.Sampler_Filter,
	curr_sampler_repeat:       bool,
	curr_sampler_mipmap:       bool,

	// Phase 10.6: Hardware Clip Stack (Nested Scissoring)
	clip_stack:                [dynamic]G2D_Scissor_State,

	// Phase 11: Stencil Buffer Pipelines (Self-Intersecting Poly)
	// OLD: all_pips: [2]G2D_Pipelines, // 0=Main, 1=Offscreen
	// NEW: Clean pipeline management system
	pipeline_mgr:              G2D_Pipeline_Manager,
	curr_pip_variant:          G2D_Pipeline_Variant, // Logical pipeline type
	// Phase 6: Sync/Pink Flush Fix - redundant all_v_offset removed
	vertices_flushed:          bool,
	vbuf_appended_count:       i32, // Phase 6: Sync/Pink Rendering Debug (Bug 6) - Track how many vertices were already uploaded
	immediate_mode:            bool, // Phase 6: Sync/Pink Rendering Debug (Bug 6)

	// Phase 11: Hierarchical Grouping State
	opacity_stack:             [dynamic]f32,
	curr_opacity:              f32,
	screen_opacity_save:       f32, // Phase 22: Isolated screen-space opacity
	screen_opacity_stack_save: [dynamic]f32, // Phase 22: Isolated screen-space stack

	// Phase 10.6: Hardware Clip Stack save (Nested Scissoring)
	screen_clip_stack_save:    [dynamic]G2D_Scissor_State, // Phase 22: Isolated screen-space stack

	// Phase 12: Stencil Clipping State
	stencil_clip_level:        u8, // Current nesting level (0 = none)
	curr_stencil_ref:          u8, // Cached reference for current level

	// === Enhanced Render Pass Tracking ===
	current_pass_active:       bool, // Whether we're in an active render pass
	current_pass_type:         Pass_Type, // Type of current render pass (Screen/Offscreen/None)
	pass_nesting_depth:        int, // Track nested pass depth

	// Phase 12: Offscreen Stencil Target
	offscreen_target:          renderer.Render_Target,

	// Phase E: Gaussian Blur
	blur_shader:               sg.Shader,
	blur_pipeline:             sg.Pipeline,
	vertex_buffer:             sg.Buffer, // Full-screen quad for blur operations

	// Phase 13: Post-Effects
	threshold_shader:          sg.Shader,
	threshold_pipeline:        sg.Pipeline,
	blend_shader:              sg.Shader,
	blend_pipeline:            sg.Pipeline,
	vignette_shader:           sg.Shader,
	vignette_pipeline:         sg.Pipeline,
	box_blur_shader:           sg.Shader,
	box_blur_pipeline:         sg.Pipeline,

	// Phase F.2.5: Offscreen Shadow Cache
	// Entries are heap-allocated so pointers remain stable across map growth.
	shadow_cache:              map[u64]^renderer.Shadow_Cache_Entry, // keyed by _shadow_key_hash to avoid map[struct] WASM crash
	pending_blurs:             [dynamic]renderer.Pending_Blur,
	// Phase F.3: Shadow cache management
	shadow_cache_keys:         [dynamic]renderer.Shadow_Key,
	max_shadow_cache_entries:  i32,

	// Gate 1.4: Context-level dash state (overlay applied to all stroke calls)
	curr_dash_array:           []f32, // nil = no dash; references user-owned or ctx-owned memory
	curr_dash_offset:          f32,

	// Gate 3.2: saveLayer RT pool + layer stack
	layer_rt_pool:             [dynamic]Layer_RT_Pool_Entry,
	layer_stack:               [dynamic]Layer_Stack_Entry,

	// Gate 3.3: image filter stack + pending filter list
	filter_stack:              [dynamic]Filter_Stack_Entry,
	pending_filters:           [dynamic]Pending_Filter_Entry,

	// Gate 3.3/3.2 scissor save stack: layer/filter pushes disable the screen-space scissor so
	// that shapes recorded inside a filter/layer don't get clipped by the screen-space scissor
	// rect when they are replayed into the pool RT (which is much smaller than the screen).
	layer_scissor_stack:       [dynamic]Layer_Scissor_Save,

	// Gate 4.1: GPU instanced particles
	particle_shader:           sg.Shader,
	particle_pip_screen:       sg.Pipeline,  // normal alpha-blend, screen target
	particle_pip_offscr:       sg.Pipeline,  // normal alpha-blend, offscreen target
	particle_pip_add_screen:   sg.Pipeline,  // additive blend, screen
	particle_pip_add_offscr:   sg.Pipeline,  // additive blend, offscreen
	particle_template_buf:     sg.Buffer,    // static 6-vert unit quad [-1,1]
	particle_inst_buf:         sg.Buffer,    // per-frame dynamic instance data
	particle_inst_cap:         i32,          // capacity in bytes
}

// Gate 3.2: One slot in the RT pool.  RTs are never destroyed mid-session; in_use is reset
// at the start of each frame's draw-list replay so the same RT can be reused next frame.
@(private = "file")
Layer_RT_Pool_Entry :: struct {
	rt:     renderer.Render_Target,
	in_use: bool,
	w, h:   i32,
}

// Gate 3.2: Layer stack entry — saved when Cmd_Push_Layer is processed during replay.
// Note: matrix/scissor state is NOT stored here.  Coordinate offset is baked into
// each command's common.transform at recording time by g2d_push_layer/g2d_pop_layer.
@(private = "file")
Layer_Stack_Entry :: struct {
	pool_idx:   int,                    // index into ctx.layer_rt_pool
	parent_rt:  ^renderer.Render_Target, // saved ctx.active_rt before the push
	opacity:    f32,
	blend_mode: renderer.Blend_Mode,
	x, y, w, h: f32,                   // dest rect for the composite-back blit (screen space)
}

// Gate 3.2/3.3: Scissor save for layer/filter recording-time push.
// Shapes inside a push_layer / set_filter are rendered into a pool RT.  The pool RT is
