g2d_draw_rect_immediate :: proc(
	x, y, w, h, thickness: f32,
	color: [4]f32,
	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	c := resolve_color(color)
	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		if color == {0, 0, 0, 0} {
			logging.debug(
				.G2D,
				fmt.tprintf("[TEXT_COLOR] default-param used; resolved curr_color=%v", c),
			)
		}
	}
	g2d_draw_line_immediate(x, y, x + w, y, thickness, c, .Square)
	g2d_draw_line_immediate(x + w, y, x + w, y + h, thickness, c, .Square)
	g2d_draw_line_immediate(x + w, y + h, x, y + h, thickness, c, .Square)
	g2d_draw_line_immediate(x, y + h, x, y, thickness, c, .Square)
}

// Gate 1.2: SDF pipeline for solid opaque fills in normal pipeline mode (perfect AA corners).
// Gradient/image/pattern paints and any special pipeline mode fall back to tessellation.
g2d_fill_rect_rounded_immediate :: proc(
	x, y, w, h, radius: f32,
	paint: renderer.Paint,
	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	r := math.min(radius, math.min(w, h) * 0.5)
	if r <= 0 {
		g2d_fill_rect(x, y, w, h, paint, shadow)
		return
	}

	// SDF path: solid paint only, and only in normal (non-stencil/mask) pipeline mode.
	if paint.type == .Solid && ctx.curr_pip_variant == .Normal {
		cx, cy := x + w * 0.5, y + h * 0.5
		c := g2d_resolve_paint_color(cx, cy, paint)
		b := _g2d_get_sdf_batch(
			shaders.Sdf_Fs_Params{half_size = {w * 0.5, h * 0.5}, corner_radius = r},
		)
		tl := apply_transform(x,     y)
		tr := apply_transform(x + w, y)
		br := apply_transform(x + w, y + h)
		bl := apply_transform(x,     y + h)
		add_vertices(
			b,
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{tl, {-1, -1}, c},
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{tr, {+1, -1}, c},
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{br, {+1, +1}, c},
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{tl, {-1, -1}, c},
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{br, {+1, +1}, c},
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{bl, {-1, +1}, c},
		)
		return
	}

	// Fallback: fan tessellation — handles gradients, images, patterns, and stencil modes.
	if paint.type == .Image || paint.type == .Pattern {
		ctx.curr_image = handle_to_sg_image(paint.image)
	} else {
		ctx.curr_image = ctx.white_image
	}
	b := get_batch()
	segments_per_corner := 8
	cx, cy := x + w * 0.5, y + h * 0.5
	center_pos := apply_transform(cx, cy)
	center_color := g2d_resolve_paint_color(cx, cy, paint)
	corners := [4][2]f32{
		{x + w - r, y + r},
		{x + w - r, y + h - r},
		{x + r, y + h - r},
		{x + r, y + r},
	}
	start_angles := [4]f32{1.5 * math.PI, 0, 0.5 * math.PI, math.PI}
	last_p, first_p := [2]f32{0, 0}, [2]f32{0, 0}
	last_c, first_c := [4]f32{0, 0, 0, 0}, [4]f32{0, 0, 0, 0}
	first := true
	for i in 0 ..< 4 {
		ccx, ccy := corners[i].x, corners[i].y
		sa := start_angles[i]
		for j in 0 ..= segments_per_corner {
			angle := sa + (f32(j) / f32(segments_per_corner)) * (math.PI * 0.5)
			px := ccx + math.cos(angle) * r
			py := ccy + math.sin(angle) * r
			curr_p := apply_transform(px, py)
			curr_c := g2d_resolve_paint_color(px, py, paint)
			if first {
				first_p = curr_p; first_c = curr_c; first = false
			} else {
				add_vertices(b,
					renderer.G2D_Vertex{center_pos, {0.5, 0.5}, center_color},
					renderer.G2D_Vertex{last_p, {0, 0}, last_c},
					renderer.G2D_Vertex{curr_p, {0, 0}, curr_c},
				)
			}
			last_p = curr_p; last_c = curr_c
		}
	}
	add_vertices(b,
		renderer.G2D_Vertex{center_pos, {0.5, 0.5}, center_color},
		renderer.G2D_Vertex{last_p, {0, 0}, last_c},
		renderer.G2D_Vertex{first_p, {0, 0}, first_c},
	)
}

g2d_fill_rect_rounded_multi :: proc(x, y, w, h: f32, radii: [4]f32, color: [4]f32 = {0, 0, 0, 0}) {
	// Replaced by non-multi version with Paint?
	// Keeping for now if needed by other code but the interface removed it.
	// If obsolete, we can remove or leave as helper.
}

@(private)
draw_corner_arc_paint :: proc(cx, cy, r: f32, start_angle: f32, paint: renderer.Paint) {
	if paint.type == .Image || paint.type == .Pattern {
		ctx.curr_image = handle_to_sg_image(paint.image)
	} else {
		ctx.curr_image = ctx.white_image
	}
	b := get_batch()
	segments := 24
	center := apply_transform(cx, cy)
	c_center := g2d_resolve_paint_color(cx, cy, paint)

	for i in 0 ..< segments {
		t1 := start_angle + (f32(i) / f32(segments)) * (math.PI / 2.0)
		t2 := start_angle + (f32(i + 1) / f32(segments)) * (math.PI / 2.0)

		px1 := cx + math.cos(t1) * r
		py1 := cy + math.sin(t1) * r
		px2 := cx + math.cos(t2) * r
		py2 := cy + math.sin(t2) * r

		p1 := apply_transform(px1, py1)
		p2 := apply_transform(px2, py2)

		c1 := g2d_resolve_paint_color(px1, py1, paint)
		c2 := g2d_resolve_paint_color(px2, py2, paint)

		v1 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{center, {0.5, 0.5}, c_center}
		v2 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p1, {0, 0}, c1}
		v3 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p2, {0, 0}, c2}

		add_vertices(b, v1, v2, v3)
	}
}

@(private)
draw_corner_stroke_arc :: proc(cx, cy, r, thickness: f32, start_angle: f32, color: [4]f32) {
	ctx.curr_image = ctx.white_image
	b := get_batch()
	segments := 24

	half_thick := thickness * 0.5
	r_inner := r - half_thick
	r_outer := r + half_thick

	for i in 0 ..< segments {
		t1 := start_angle + (f32(i) / f32(segments)) * (math.PI / 2.0)
		t2 := start_angle + (f32(i + 1) / f32(segments)) * (math.PI / 2.0)

		// Calculate inner and outer points for each segment edge
		p_in1 := apply_transform(cx + math.cos(t1) * r_inner, cy + math.sin(t1) * r_inner)
		p_out1 := apply_transform(cx + math.cos(t1) * r_outer, cy + math.sin(t1) * r_outer)
		p_in2 := apply_transform(cx + math.cos(t2) * r_inner, cy + math.sin(t2) * r_inner)
		p_out2 := apply_transform(cx + math.cos(t2) * r_outer, cy + math.sin(t2) * r_outer)

		// Triangle 1 (p_in1, p_out1, p_out2)
		v1 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p_in1, {0, 0}, color}
		v2 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p_out1, {1, 0}, color}
		v3 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p_out2, {1, 1}, color}
		// Triangle 2 (p_in1, p_out2, p_in2)
		v4 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p_in1, {0, 0}, color}
		v5 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p_out2, {1, 1}, color}
		v6 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p_in2, {0, 1}, color}

		add_vertices(b, v1, v2, v3, v4, v5, v6)
	}
}

g2d_draw_rect_rounded_immediate :: proc(
	x, y, w, h, radius, thickness: f32,
	color: [4]f32,
	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	c := resolve_color(color)
	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		if color == {0, 0, 0, 0} {
			logging.debug(
				.G2D,
				fmt.tprintf("[TEXT_COLOR] default-param used; resolved curr_color=%v", c),
			)
		}
	}
	r := math.min(radius, math.min(w, h) * 0.5)

	g2d_draw_line_immediate(x + r, y, x + w - r, y, thickness, c, .Butt)
	g2d_draw_line_immediate(x + w, y + r, x + w, y + h - r, thickness, c, .Butt)
	g2d_draw_line_immediate(x + r, y + h, x + w - r, y + h, thickness, c, .Butt)
	g2d_draw_line_immediate(x, y + r, x, y + h - r, thickness, c, .Butt)

	draw_corner_stroke_arc(x + r, y + r, r, thickness, math.PI, c)
	draw_corner_stroke_arc(x + w - r, y + r, r, thickness, 3.0 * math.PI / 2.0, c)
	draw_corner_stroke_arc(x + w - r, y + h - r, r, thickness, 0, c)
	draw_corner_stroke_arc(x + r, y + h - r, r, thickness, math.PI / 2.0, c)
}

//Pahse 0: Trinagle primitives
//Pahse 0: Trinagle primitives
g2d_fill_triangle_immediate :: proc(
	x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, y3: f32,
	paint: renderer.Paint,
	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	if paint.type == .Image || paint.type == .Pattern {
		ctx.curr_image = handle_to_sg_image(paint.image)
	} else {
		ctx.curr_image = ctx.white_image
	}
	b := get_batch()

	p1 := apply_transform(x1, y1)
	p2 := apply_transform(x2, y2)
	p3 := apply_transform(x3, y3)

	c1 := g2d_resolve_paint_color(x1, y1, paint)
	c2 := g2d_resolve_paint_color(x2, y2, paint)
	c3 := g2d_resolve_paint_color(x3, y3, paint)

	add_vertices(
		b,
		renderer.G2D_Vertex{p1, {0, 0}, c1},
		renderer.G2D_Vertex{p2, {1, 0}, c2},
		renderer.G2D_Vertex{p3, {0, 1}, c3},
	)
}

g2d_draw_triangle_immediate :: proc(
	x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, y3, thickness: f32,
	color: [4]f32,
	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	c := resolve_color(color)
	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		if color == {0, 0, 0, 0} {
			logging.debug(
				.G2D,
				fmt.tprintf("[TEXT_COLOR] default-param used; resolved curr_color=%v", c),
			)
		}
	}

	g2d_draw_line_immediate(x1, y1, x2, y2, thickness, c, .Butt)
	g2d_draw_line_immediate(x2, y2, x3, y3, thickness, c, .Butt)
	g2d_draw_line_immediate(x3, y3, x1, y1, thickness, c, .Butt)

	normalize_vec2 :: proc(v: [2]f32) -> [2]f32 {
		mag := math.sqrt(v.x * v.x + v.y * v.y)
		if mag == 0 do return {0, 0}
		return {v.x / mag, v.y / mag}
	}

	fill_miter_corner :: proc(
		px, py, x_prev, y_prev, x_next, y_next, thick: f32,
		col: [4]f32,
		normalize_vec2: proc(_: [2]f32) -> [2]f32,
	) {
		dir1 := [2]f32{x_prev - px, y_prev - py}
		dir2 := [2]f32{x_next - px, y_next - py}
		v1 := normalize_vec2(dir1)
		v2 := normalize_vec2(dir2)
		t := normalize_vec2(v1 + v2)
		n1 := [2]f32{-v1.y, v1.x}
		cos_theta := t.x * n1.x + t.y * n1.y
		if math.abs(cos_theta) < 0.1 do return
		miter_len := (thick * 0.5) / cos_theta
		cross := v1.x * v2.y - v1.y * v2.x
		if cross > 0 do miter_len = -miter_len
		miter_pt := [2]f32{px, py} + t * miter_len
		p1, p2: [2]f32
		if cross <= 0 {
			p1 = [2]f32{px, py} + n1 * (thick * 0.5)
			p2 = [2]f32{px, py} + [2]f32{v2.y, -v2.x} * (thick * 0.5)
		} else {
			p1 = [2]f32{px, py} - n1 * (thick * 0.5)
			p2 = [2]f32{px, py} - [2]f32{v2.y, -v2.x} * (thick * 0.5)
		}
		tp_center := apply_transform(px, py)
		tp_miter := apply_transform(miter_pt.x, miter_pt.y)
		tp_out1 := apply_transform(p1.x, p1.y)
		tp_out2 := apply_transform(p2.x, p2.y)
		b := get_batch()
		add_vertices(
			b,
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{tp_center, {0.5, 0.5}, col},
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{tp_out1, {0, 0}, col},
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{tp_miter, {0, 0}, col},
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{tp_center, {0.5, 0.5}, col},
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{tp_out2, {0, 0}, col},
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{tp_miter, {0, 0}, col},
		)
	}
	fill_miter_corner(x1, y1, x3, y3, x2, y2, thickness, c, normalize_vec2)
	fill_miter_corner(x2, y2, x1, y1, x3, y3, thickness, c, normalize_vec2)
	fill_miter_corner(x3, y3, x2, y2, x1, y1, thickness, c, normalize_vec2)
}

//Phase 0: Circle primitves
// Gate 1.2: SDF pipeline for solid opaque fills in normal pipeline mode (analytically AA, 1 quad).
// Gradient/image/pattern paints and any special pipeline mode (stencil, mask, etc.)
// fall back to fan tessellation so colour sampling and GPU state are correct.
g2d_fill_circle_immediate :: proc(
	x, y, radius: f32,
	paint: renderer.Paint,
	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	// SDF path: solid paint only, and only in normal (non-stencil/mask) pipeline mode.
	if paint.type == .Solid && ctx.curr_pip_variant == .Normal {
		c := g2d_resolve_paint_color(x, y, paint)
		b := _g2d_get_sdf_batch(
			shaders.Sdf_Fs_Params{half_size = {radius, radius}, corner_radius = radius},
		)
		// Quad corners: uv carries local coords in -1..+1 range.
		tl := apply_transform(x - radius, y - radius)
		tr := apply_transform(x + radius, y - radius)
		br := apply_transform(x + radius, y + radius)
		bl := apply_transform(x - radius, y + radius)
		add_vertices(
			b,
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{tl, {-1, -1}, c},
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{tr, {+1, -1}, c},
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{br, {+1, +1}, c},
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{tl, {-1, -1}, c},
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{br, {+1, +1}, c},
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{bl, {-1, +1}, c},
		)
		return
	}

	// Fallback: fan tessellation — handles gradients, images, patterns, and stencil modes.
	if paint.type == .Image || paint.type == .Pattern {
		ctx.curr_image = handle_to_sg_image(paint.image)
	} else {
		ctx.curr_image = ctx.white_image
	}
	b := get_batch()
	segs := 64
	center := apply_transform(x, y)
	c_center := g2d_resolve_paint_color(x, y, paint)
	for i in 0 ..< segs {
		t1 := f32(i) * 2 * math.PI / f32(segs)
		t2 := f32(i + 1) * 2 * math.PI / f32(segs)
		px1 := x + radius * math.cos(t1)
		py1 := y + radius * math.sin(t1)
		px2 := x + radius * math.cos(t2)
		py2 := y + radius * math.sin(t2)
		p1 := apply_transform(px1, py1)
		p2 := apply_transform(px2, py2)
		c1 := g2d_resolve_paint_color(px1, py1, paint)
		c2 := g2d_resolve_paint_color(px2, py2, paint)
		add_vertices(
			b,
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{center, {0.5, 0.5}, c_center},
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{p1, {0, 0}, c1},
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{p2, {0, 0}, c2},
		)
	}
}

// Circle Gradient functions removed in favor of Paint struct

g2d_draw_circle_immediate :: proc(
	x, y, radius, thickness: f32,
	color: [4]f32,
	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	c := resolve_color(color)
	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		if color == {0, 0, 0, 0} {
			logging.debug(
				.G2D,
				fmt.tprintf("[TEXT_COLOR] default-param used; resolved curr_color=%v", c),
			)
		}
	}
	segs := 40
	for i in 0 ..< segs {
		t1 := f32(i) * 2 * math.PI / f32(segs)
		t2 := f32(i + 1) * 2 * math.PI / f32(segs)
		g2d_draw_line(
			x + radius * math.cos(t1),
			y + radius * math.sin(t1),
			x + radius * math.cos(t2),
			y + radius * math.sin(t2),
			thickness,
			c,
		)
	}
}

g2d_fill_ellipse_immediate :: proc(
	x, y, rx, ry: f32,
	paint: renderer.Paint,
	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	if paint.type == .Image || paint.type == .Pattern {
		ctx.curr_image = handle_to_sg_image(paint.image)
	} else {
		ctx.curr_image = ctx.white_image
	}
	b := get_batch()

	segs := 64
	center := apply_transform(x, y)

	c_center := g2d_resolve_paint_color(x, y, paint)

	for i in 0 ..< segs {
		t1 := f32(i) * 2 * math.PI / f32(segs)
		t2 := f32(i + 1) * 2 * math.PI / f32(segs)

		px1 := x + rx * math.cos(t1)
		py1 := y + ry * math.sin(t1)
		px2 := x + rx * math.cos(t2)
		py2 := y + ry * math.sin(t2)

		p1 := apply_transform(px1, py1)
		p2 := apply_transform(px2, py2)

		c1 := g2d_resolve_paint_color(px1, py1, paint)
		c2 := g2d_resolve_paint_color(px2, py2, paint)

		add_vertices(
			b,
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{center, {0.5, 0.5}, c_center},
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{p1, {0, 0}, c1},
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{p2, {0, 0}, c2},
		)
	}
}

g2d_draw_ellipse_immediate :: proc(
	x, y, rx, ry, thickness: f32,
	color: [4]f32,
	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	c := resolve_color(color)
	segs := 40
	for i in 0 ..< segs {
		t1 := f32(i) * 2 * math.PI / f32(segs)
		t2 := f32(i + 1) * 2 * math.PI / f32(segs)
		g2d_draw_line(
			x + rx * math.cos(t1),
			y + ry * math.sin(t1),
			x + rx * math.cos(t2),
			y + ry * math.sin(t2),
			thickness,
			c,
		)
	}
}

// Phase 3.1 Image loading and destroying
// Create a global map or a simple array to cache views if you don't want to change the struct

g2d_draw_texture_simple :: proc(x, y, w, h: f32, img: sg.Image, color: [4]f32 = {1, 1, 1, 1}) {
	g2d_draw_image_immediate(x, y, w, h, img, color)
}

// --- Phase 3.3: Soft Clipping (UV Mapping) ---
g2d_draw_image_rect_immediate :: proc(
	dest: [4]f32,
	src: [4]f32,
	img: sg.Image,
	color: [4]f32 = {1, 1, 1, 1},
) {
	old_image := ctx.curr_image
	ctx.curr_image = img
	b := get_batch()

	// Phase 3.4: Use direct width/height queries to avoid dependency on Image_Desc
	iw := f32(sg.query_image_width(img))
	ih := f32(sg.query_image_height(img))

	// 1. Normalize pixel coordinates to 0.0-1.0 UV range
	u0, v0 := src.x / iw, src.y / ih
	u1, v1_uv := (src.x + src.z) / iw, (src.y + src.w) / ih // Renamed v1 to v1_uv

	// 2. Transform the destination coordinates
	p1 := apply_transform(dest.x, dest.y)
	p2 := apply_transform(dest.x + dest.z, dest.y)
	p3 := apply_transform(dest.x + dest.z, dest.y + dest.w)
	p4 := apply_transform(dest.x, dest.y + dest.w)

	// 3. Create unique vertex names
	v_a := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p1, {u0, v0}, color}
	v_b := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p2, {u1, v0}, color}
	v_c := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p3, {u1, v1_uv}, color}
	v_d := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p4, {u0, v1_uv}, color}

	// 4. Pass those unique names to the helper
	add_vertices(b, v_a, v_b, v_c, v_a, v_c, v_d)

	ctx.curr_image = old_image
}

// New: draw with normalized UVs to avoid per-call width/height queries
g2d_draw_image_rect_uv :: proc(
	dest: [4]f32,
	uvs: [4]f32,
	img: sg.Image,
	color: [4]f32 = {1, 1, 1, 1},
) {
	old_image := ctx.curr_image
	ctx.curr_image = img
	b := get_batch()

	// 1. Use provided normalized UVs directly
	u0, v0 := uvs.x, uvs.y
	u1, v1_uv := uvs.z, uvs.w

	// 2. Transform the destination coordinates
	p1 := apply_transform(dest.x, dest.y)
	p2 := apply_transform(dest.x + dest.z, dest.y)
	p3 := apply_transform(dest.x + dest.z, dest.y + dest.w)
	p4 := apply_transform(dest.x, dest.y + dest.w)

	// 3. Create vertices
	v_a := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p1, {u0, v0}, color}
	v_b := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p2, {u1, v0}, color}
	v_c := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p3, {u1, v1_uv}, color}
	v_d := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p4, {u0, v1_uv}, color}

	add_vertices(b, v_a, v_b, v_c, v_a, v_c, v_d)

	ctx.curr_image = old_image
}

// Phase 3.2 : --- Hard Clipping (GPU Scissor) ---
// Phase 10.6 : Updated for nested clip support (intersection + stack)

//Phase 3.3 gert inmage x,y
g2d_get_image_info :: proc(img: sg.Image) -> (width, height: i32) {
	// Phase 3.4: Use direct query functions for portability and to avoid unresolved symbols
	w := sg.query_image_width(img)
	h := sg.query_image_height(img)
	return i32(w), i32(h)
}

// Phase B: Image_Handle bridge functions for Layer 3 separation
// Convert Image_Handle to sg.Image for internal use
@(private)
_image_handle_to_sg :: proc(h: renderer.Image_Handle) -> sg.Image {
	return sg.Image{id = h.id}
}

// Convert sg.Image to Image_Handle for Layer 3 exposure
@(private)
_sg_to_image_handle :: proc(img: sg.Image) -> renderer.Image_Handle {
	return renderer.Image_Handle{id = img.id}
}

// Get image dimensions from Image_Handle (Layer 3 safe)
g2d_get_image_handle_info :: proc(handle: renderer.Image_Handle) -> (width, height: i32) {
	img := _image_handle_to_sg(handle)
	return g2d_get_image_info(img)
}

// Draw image using Image_Handle (Layer 3 safe)
g2d_draw_image_rect_handle :: proc(
	dest: [4]f32,
	src: [4]f32,
	handle: renderer.Image_Handle,
	color: [4]f32 = {1, 1, 1, 1},
) {
	img := _image_handle_to_sg(handle)
	g2d_draw_image_rect_immediate(dest, src, img, color)
}

@(private)
g2d_stroke_rect_immediate :: proc(
	x, y, w, h: f32,
	stroke: renderer.Stroke,
	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	s := _g2d_merge_dash(stroke)
	if len(s.dash_array) > 0 {
		g2d_stroke_rect(x, y, w, h, s, shadow)
		return
	}
	g2d_draw_rect_immediate(x, y, w, h, s.width, resolve_paint_color(s.paint), shadow)
}

@(private)
g2d_stroke_rect_rounded_immediate :: proc(
	x, y, w, h, radius: f32,
	stroke: renderer.Stroke,
	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	s := _g2d_merge_dash(stroke)
	if len(s.dash_array) > 0 {
		g2d_stroke_rect_rounded(x, y, w, h, radius, s, shadow)
		return
	}
	g2d_draw_rect_rounded_immediate(
		x,
		y,
		w,
		h,
		radius,
		s.width,
		resolve_paint_color(s.paint),
		shadow,
	)
}

@(private)
g2d_stroke_line_immediate :: proc(x1, y1, x2, y2: f32, stroke: renderer.Stroke) {
	s := _g2d_merge_dash(stroke)
	if len(s.dash_array) > 0 {
		g2d_stroke_line(x1, y1, x2, y2, s)
		return
	}
	g2d_draw_line_immediate(
		x1,
		y1,
		x2,
		y2,
		s.width,
		resolve_paint_color(s.paint),
		s.cap,
	)
}

@(private)
g2d_stroke_polyline_immediate :: proc(points: [][2]f32, stroke: renderer.Stroke) {
	s := _g2d_merge_dash(stroke)
	if len(s.dash_array) > 0 {
		g2d_stroke_polyline(points, s)
		return
	}
	g2d_draw_polyline_immediate(
		points,
		s.width,
		resolve_paint_color(s.paint),
		s.cap,
	)
}

@(private)
g2d_stroke_polygon_immediate :: proc(
	points: [][2]f32,

	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	s := _g2d_merge_dash(stroke)
	if len(s.dash_array) > 0 {

		return

}

g2d_stroke_circle_immediate :: proc(

g2d_stroke_ellipse_immediate :: proc(

@(private)
g2d_stroke_arc_immediate :: proc(
	x, y, radius, start_angle, end_angle: f32,

	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	s := _g2d_merge_dash(stroke)
	if len(s.dash_array) > 0 {

g2d_stroke_star_immediate :: proc(
	x, y: f32,
	num_points: int,
	inner_radius, outer_radius: f32,

) {

		x,

g2d_stroke_bezier_quadratic_immediate :: proc(
	x1, y1, cx, cy, x2, y2: f32,
	stroke: renderer.Stroke,
) {
	g2d_draw_bezier_quadratic_immediate(
		x1,
		y1,
		cx,
		cy,
		x2,
		y2,

		resolve_paint_color(stroke.paint),
	)

g2d_stroke_bezier_cubic_immediate :: proc(
	x1, y1, c1x, c1y, c2x, c2y, x2, y2: f32,
	stroke: renderer.Stroke,
) {
	g2d_draw_bezier_cubic_immediate(
		x1,
		y1,
		c1x,
		c1y,
		c2x,
		c2y,
		x2,
		y2,
		stroke.width,
		resolve_paint_color(stroke.paint),
	)
}

// Utility: measure text width and height in pixels for given font (uses HB if available)
g2d_measure_text :: proc(text: string, font: renderer.Font_Handle) -> (width, height: f32) {
	font_ptr, ok := fm_resolve_handle(font)
	if !ok || font_ptr == nil || len(text) == 0 {
		return 0, 0
	}

	shaped := renderer.shape_text_basic(font_ptr, text)
	// Phase 22: Target-aware DPI scaling for text measurement
	dpi_to_use := ctx.active_rt != nil ? ctx.active_rt.dpi_scale : ctx.dpi_scale
	inv_dpi := 1.0 / dpi_to_use
	width = 0
	for i in 0 ..< len(shaped) {
		width += shaped[i].advance_x * inv_dpi
	}

	height = font_ptr.line_height * inv_dpi
	return width, height
}

@(private)
g2d_replay_post_effects :: proc(dl: ^renderer.Draw_List) {
	if dl == nil do return

	// Post-effects usually target the screen (nil target) or are transition steps.
	// We replay them in recording order across all layers (usually they are in .Content).
	for cmd_idx in 0 ..< len(dl.commands) {
		cmd := &dl.commands[cmd_idx]
		#partial switch c in cmd^ {
		case renderer.Cmd_Apply_Box_Blur:
			g2d_apply_box_blur(c.source, c.output, c.temp, c.radius)
		case renderer.Cmd_Apply_Bloom:
			g2d_apply_bloom(c.source, c.output, c.temp, c.threshold, c.intensity)
		case renderer.Cmd_Apply_Vignette:
			g2d_apply_vignette(c.source, c.output, c.radius, c.softness)
		}
	}
}

// Phase 6.1: Path-based clipping support logic
g2d_begin_clip_path :: proc() {
	if ctx.stencil_clip_level >= 3 {
		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.debug(.G2D, "Maximum stencil clip depth reached (4)")
		}
		return
	}
	ctx.stencil_clip_level += 1
	ctx.curr_stencil_ref = ctx.stencil_clip_level

	// Validate pipeline before using
	pipeline := pipeline_mgr_get(&ctx.pipeline_mgr, .Clippable, ctx.stencil_clip_level)
	if pipeline.id == 0 {
		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.debug(
				.G2D,
				fmt.tprintf(
					"Invalid clippable pipeline at level %d, using fallback",
					ctx.stencil_clip_level,
				),
			)
		}
		ctx.curr_pipeline = pipeline_mgr_get(&ctx.pipeline_mgr, .Normal) // Fallback to normal pipeline
	} else {
		ctx.curr_pipeline = pipeline
	}
}

g2d_end_clip_path :: proc() {
	if ctx.stencil_clip_level > 0 {
		ctx.stencil_clip_level -= 1
		ctx.curr_stencil_ref = ctx.stencil_clip_level

		// Validate pipeline before using
		pipeline_mgr_activate(&ctx.pipeline_mgr, .Screen)
		pipeline := pipeline_mgr_get(&ctx.pipeline_mgr, .Clippable, ctx.stencil_clip_level)
		if pipeline.id == 0 {
			when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
				logging.debug(
					.G2D,
					fmt.tprintf(
						"Invalid clippable pipeline at level %d in end_clip_path, using fallback",
						ctx.stencil_clip_level,
					),
				)
			}
			ctx.curr_pipeline = pipeline_mgr_get(&ctx.pipeline_mgr, .Normal) // Fallback to normal pipeline
		} else {
			ctx.curr_pipeline = pipeline
		}
	}
}

g2d_clip_path :: proc(path: ^renderer.Path) {
	if path == nil || len(path.commands) == 0 do return

	// 1. Temporarily switch to Stencil Write pipeline for the current level
	old_pip := ctx.curr_pipeline

	// Use the appropriate pipeline set
	pipeline_mgr_activate(&ctx.pipeline_mgr, .Screen)
	// We use Non-Zero by default for clips unless we add an option
	ctx.curr_pipeline = pipeline_mgr_get(&ctx.pipeline_mgr, .StencilWriteNonZero)

	// Start clipping setup
	get_batch()

	// Save current path to restore later
	old_path := ctx.current_path
	ctx.current_path = make([dynamic]renderer.Path_Point, 0, 16, mem.arena_allocator(&ctx.arena))

	// Convert renderer.Path to internal Path_Point representation
	for cmd in path.commands {
		switch data in cmd.data {
		case renderer.MoveTo_Data:
			g2d_path_move_to(data.x, data.y)
		case renderer.LineTo_Data:
			g2d_path_line_to(data.x, data.y)
		case renderer.CubicBezier_Data:
			g2d_path_cubic_to(data.cx1, data.cy1, data.cx2, data.cy2, data.x, data.y)
		case renderer.QuadraticBezier_Data:
			g2d_path_quad_to(data.cx, data.cy, data.x, data.y)
		case renderer.Arc_Data:
			// Note: g2d_path_arc_to uses (rx, ry, x_axis_rot, start, end, clockwise)
			g2d_path_arc_to(
				data.rx,
				data.ry,
				data.x_axis_rotation,
				data.start_angle,
				data.end_angle,
				data.clockwise,
			)
		case renderer.ClosePath_Data:
			g2d_path_close()
		}
	}

	// Prepare for batch creation
	contours := _g2d_get_contours()
	if len(contours) == 0 {
		ctx.current_path = old_path
		ctx.curr_pipeline = old_pip
		return
	}

	// Calculate bounds
	min_x, max_x, min_y, max_y: f32
	first_pt := true
	for points in contours {
		for p in points {
			if first_pt {min_x, max_x = p.x, p.x; min_y, max_y = p.y, p.y; first_pt = false} else {
				if p.x < min_x do min_x = p.x; if p.x > max_x do max_x = p.x
				if p.y < min_y do min_y = p.y; if p.y > max_y do max_y = p.y
			}
		}
	}

	// Create a stencil batch with NO cover quad (clipping setup only)
	paint := renderer.paint_solid({0, 0, 0, 0})
	paint.fill_rule = .NonZero
	_g2d_create_stencil_batch(contours, paint, min_x, min_y, max_x, max_y, 0, 0, 1)

	// Mark batch as NOT having a cover pass (crucial for clips)
	b := &ctx.batches[len(ctx.batches) - 1]
	b.cover_v_count = 0

	// Increment clip level for future batches
	ctx.stencil_clip_level += 1

	// Restore path and pipeline
	ctx.current_path = old_path
	ctx.curr_pipeline = old_pip
}

// Phase 12: Stencil Masking (Procedural API)
g2d_begin_mask :: proc() {
	if ctx.stencil_clip_level >= 3 {
		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.debug(.G2D, "Maximum stencil mask depth reached")
		}
		return
	}

	ctx.stencil_clip_level += 1
	ctx.curr_stencil_ref = ctx.stencil_clip_level
	
	// Use MaskWrite pipeline for the defining phase.
	// MaskWrite uses REPLACE op on both front+back faces, writing exactly `level` to stencil.
	// This fixes the Metal NDC Y-flip issue where all 2D geometry is back-face, causing
	// StencilWriteNonZero's DECR_WRAP to write 255 instead of 1, breaking Clippable EQUAL test.
	ctx.curr_pip_variant = .MaskWrite
	ctx.curr_pipeline = pipeline_mgr_get(&ctx.pipeline_mgr, .MaskWrite, ctx.stencil_clip_level)
}

g2d_end_mask :: proc() {
	// Stop writing. Switch back to normal pipeline (or previous clippable)
	if ctx.stencil_clip_level == 0 {
		ctx.curr_pip_variant = .Normal
		ctx.curr_pipeline = pipeline_mgr_get(&ctx.pipeline_mgr, .Normal)
	} else {
		ctx.curr_pip_variant = .Clippable
		ctx.curr_pipeline = pipeline_mgr_get(&ctx.pipeline_mgr, .Clippable, ctx.stencil_clip_level - 1)
	}
}

g2d_begin_masked_draw :: proc() {
	// Test against the stencil we just wrote at the current level
	ctx.curr_pip_variant = .Clippable
	ctx.curr_pipeline = pipeline_mgr_get(&ctx.pipeline_mgr, .Clippable, ctx.stencil_clip_level)
}

g2d_end_masked_draw :: proc() {
	if ctx.stencil_clip_level > 0 {
		ctx.stencil_clip_level -= 1
		ctx.curr_stencil_ref = ctx.stencil_clip_level
	}
	
	if ctx.stencil_clip_level == 0 {
		ctx.curr_pip_variant = .Normal
		ctx.curr_pipeline = pipeline_mgr_get(&ctx.pipeline_mgr, .Normal)
	} else {
		ctx.curr_pip_variant = .Clippable
		ctx.curr_pipeline = pipeline_mgr_get(&ctx.pipeline_mgr, .Clippable, ctx.stencil_clip_level)
	}
}

// Phase 12: --- Recursive SVG Drawing Helper ---
// This allows direct rendering of parsed SVG trees for verification.
g2d_draw_svg_element :: proc(el: SVG_Element) {
	g2d_draw_svg_element_with_style(el, default_svg_style())
}

// Internal helper that handles style inheritance
g2d_draw_svg_element_with_style :: proc(el: SVG_Element, parent_style: SVG_Style) {
	// Inherit styles from parent if not explicitly set
	effective_style := el.style

	// Inherit fill from parent if current element has "none" fill
	if _, is_none := effective_style.fill.(SVG_None); is_none {
		effective_style.fill = parent_style.fill
	}

	// Inherit stroke from parent if current element has "none" stroke
	if _, is_none := effective_style.stroke.(SVG_None); is_none {
		effective_style.stroke = parent_style.stroke
		effective_style.stroke_width = parent_style.stroke_width
	}

	// 1. Group/Root: handle transforms and children
	if el.type == .Root || el.type == .Group {
		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.debug(
				.G2D,
				fmt.tprintf("[SVG_RENDER] Visiting %s, children=%d", el.type, len(el.children)),
			)
		}
		g2d_begin_group(el.transform, el.style.opacity)

		// If element has a clipPath, we should apply it here
		// [TODO] Resolve clipPath ID to SVG_Element and call g2d_clip_path

		for child in el.children {
			g2d_draw_svg_element_with_style(child, effective_style)
		}

		g2d_end_group()
		return
	}

	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		logging.debug(.SVG, fmt.tprintf("[SVG_RENDER] Visiting %s", el.type))
	}

	// 2. Path
	if el.type == .Path {
		data, has_data := el.data.(SVG_Path_Data)
		if !has_data || !data.has_parsed {
			logging.debug(
				.SVG,
				fmt.tprintf(
					"[SVG_RENDER] Path skipped: has_data=%v, has_parsed=%v",
					has_data,
					data.has_parsed,
				),
			)
			return
		}

		if len(data.parsed.commands) == 0 {
			logging.debug(.SVG, "[SVG_RENDER] Path skipped: 0 commands")
			return
		}

		logging.debug(
			.SVG,
			fmt.tprintf("[SVG_RENDER] Rendering path with %d commands", len(data.parsed.commands)),
		)

		g2d_path_begin()
		// Track current position for H/V line commands
		current_x: f32 = 0
		current_y: f32 = 0
		// Track subpath start for Close_Path
		subpath_start_x: f32 = 0
		subpath_start_y: f32 = 0

		for cmd in data.parsed.commands {
			#partial switch cmd.type {
			case .Move_To:
				d := cmd.data.(Move_To_Data)
				g2d_path_move_to(d.point.x, d.point.y)
				current_x = d.point.x
				current_y = d.point.y
				// Update subpath start
				subpath_start_x = current_x
				subpath_start_y = current_y
			case .Line_To:
				d := cmd.data.(Line_To_Data)
				g2d_path_line_to(d.point.x, d.point.y)
				current_x = d.point.x
				current_y = d.point.y
			case .Horizontal_Line_To:
				d := cmd.data.(Horizontal_Line_To_Data)
				g2d_path_line_to(d.x, current_y) // Use current Y coordinate
				current_x = d.x
			case .Vertical_Line_To:
				d := cmd.data.(Vertical_Line_To_Data)
				g2d_path_line_to(current_x, d.y) // Use current X coordinate
				current_y = d.y
			case .Cubic_Bezier:
				d := cmd.data.(Cubic_Bezier_Data)
				g2d_path_cubic_to(d.cp1.x, d.cp1.y, d.cp2.x, d.cp2.y, d.end.x, d.end.y)
				current_x = d.end.x
				current_y = d.end.y
			case .Quadratic_Bezier:
				d := cmd.data.(Quadratic_Bezier_Data)
				g2d_path_quad_to(d.cp.x, d.cp.y, d.end.x, d.end.y)
				current_x = d.end.x
				current_y = d.end.y
			case .Close_Path:
				g2d_path_close()
				// Reset current position to subpath start
				current_x = subpath_start_x
				current_y = subpath_start_y
			}
		}

		// Resolve paint
		paint: renderer.Paint
		paint.fill_rule = (renderer.Fill_Rule)(effective_style.fill_rule)

		// Fill
		if fill_solid, ok := effective_style.fill.(SVG_Solid_Color); ok {
			paint.type = .Solid
			paint.color = fill_solid
			g2d_path_fill(paint)
		}

		// Stroke
		if stroke_solid, ok := effective_style.stroke.(SVG_Solid_Color); ok {
			when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
				logging.debug(
					.G2D,
					fmt.tprintf(
						"[SVG_DRAW] Stroking path with color: %v, width: %v",
						stroke_solid,
						effective_style.stroke_width,
					),
				)
			}
			g2d_path_stroke(
				{
					width = effective_style.stroke_width,
					paint = {type = .Solid, color = stroke_solid},
				},
			)
		}
	}

	// Handle other simple shapes if needed for Tiger
}
@(private)
is_bidi_control :: proc(cp: rune) -> bool {
	// Bidi control characters that should be invisible; some fonts render tofu
	// LRM/RLM, ALM, Embedding/Override, isolates
	if cp == 0x200E || cp == 0x200F || cp == 0x061C do return true
	if cp >= 0x202A && cp <= 0x202E do return true
	if cp >= 0x2066 && cp <= 0x2069 do return true
	return false
}

@(private)
sanitize_bidi_controls :: proc(s: string) -> string {
	out := make([dynamic]u8, 0, len(s))
	i := i32(0)
	for i < i32(len(s)) {
		b0 := s[i]
		cp: rune; w: i32
		if (b0 & 0x80) == 0 {
			cp, w = rune(b0), 1
		} else if (b0 & 0xE0) == 0xC0 && i + 1 < i32(len(s)) {
			cp = (rune(b0 & 0x1F) << 6) | rune(s[i + 1] & 0x3F); w = 2
		} else if (b0 & 0xF0) == 0xE0 && i + 2 < i32(len(s)) {
			cp =
				(rune(b0 & 0x0F) << 12) |
				(rune(s[i + 1] & 0x3F) << 6) |
				rune(s[i + 2] & 0x3F); w = 3
		} else if (b0 & 0xF8) == 0xF0 && i + 3 < i32(len(s)) {
			cp =
				(rune(b0 & 0x07) << 18) |
				(rune(s[i + 1] & 0x3F) << 12) |
				(rune(s[i + 2] & 0x3F) << 6) |
				rune(s[i + 3] & 0x3F); w = 4
		} else {
			cp, w = rune(b0), 1
		}
		// Keep ZWJ (U+200D) as it affects shaping, but drop bidi controls
		if !is_bidi_control(cp) {
			// copy bytes
			for k in 0 ..< w {
				_, _ = runtime.append_elem(&out, s[i + k])
			}
		}
		i += w
	}
	return strings.clone(string(out[:]))
}

// Configure per-script text offset in pixels (dx, dy)
g2d_set_text_offset :: proc(kind: txt.Script_Kind, dx, dy: f32) {
	offs := [2]f32{dx, dy}
	switch kind {
	case .Latin:
		ctx.text_offsets.latin = offs
	case .Arabic:
		ctx.text_offsets.arabic = offs
	case .Hebrew:
		ctx.text_offsets.hebrew = offs
	case .CJK:
		ctx.text_offsets.cjk = offs
	case .Emoji:
		ctx.text_offsets.emoji = offs
	case .Symbol:
		ctx.text_offsets.symbol = offs
	case .Other:
		ctx.text_offsets.other = offs
	}
}

// Track which fonts actually packed glyphs this frame (dedup by pointer)


// Phase 4.3: High-level drawing of pre-calculated layout lines.

@(private)
g2d_validate_atlas_coherency :: proc(
	font: ^txt.Font,
	gid: u32,
	style_bits: u32,
	frame: u32,
) -> bool {
	if font == nil do return false

	glyph_key := (gid << 2) | style_bits
	gi, found := font.glyphs[glyph_key]
	if !found {
		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.debug(.Textures, fmt.tprintf("frame=%v gid=%v NOT_IN_CACHE", frame, gid))
		}
		return false
	}
	if font.atlas_image.id == 0 {
		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.debug(
				.Textures,
				fmt.tprintf("frame=%v gid=%v INVALID_ATLAS_IMAGE", frame, gid),
			)
		}
		return false
	}
	if !gi_is_live(gi) {
		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.debug(
				.Textures,
				fmt.tprintf(
					"[COHERENCY] frame=%v gid=%v STALE_ROW row=%v epoch=%v",
					frame,
					gid,
					gi.row_index,
					gi.row_epoch,
				),
			)
		}
		return false
	}
	if gi.u0 < 0 ||
	   gi.u0 > 1 ||
	   gi.u1 < 0 ||
	   gi.u1 > 1 ||
	   gi.v0 < 0 ||
	   gi.v0 > 1 ||
	   gi.v1 < 0 ||
	   gi.v1 > 1 {
		return false
	}
	if gi.width < 0 || gi.width > 512 || gi.height < 0 || gi.height > 512 {
		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.debug(
				.Textures,
				fmt.tprintf(
					"[COHERENCY] frame=%v gid=%v INVALID_DIMS w=%v h=%v",
					frame,
					gid,
					gi.width,
					gi.height,
				),
			)
		}
		return false
	}
	return true
}

// Helper to resolve color per vertex
@(private)
_g2d_resolve_vertex_color :: proc(
	px, py: f32,
	paint: renderer.Paint,
	min_x, min_y, dx, dy, size: f32,
) -> [4]f32 {
	switch paint.type {
	case .Solid:
		return resolve_color(paint.color)
	case .Linear_Gradient:
		t := ((px - paint.linear_start.x) * dx + (py - paint.linear_start.y) * dy) / size
		return resolve_color(sample_gradient(t, paint.gradient_colors, paint.gradient_stops))
	case .Radial_Gradient:
		dist := math.sqrt_f32(
			(px - paint.center.x) * (px - paint.center.x) +
			(py - paint.center.y) * (py - paint.center.y),
		)
		t := dist / paint.radius
		return resolve_color(sample_gradient(t, paint.gradient_colors, paint.gradient_stops))
	case .Conical_Gradient:
		angle_from_center := math.atan2_f32(py - paint.center.y, px - paint.center.x)
		normalized_angle := (angle_from_center - paint.start_angle) / (math.PI * 2)
		t := normalized_angle - math.floor_f32(normalized_angle)
		return resolve_color(sample_gradient(t, paint.gradient_colors, paint.gradient_stops))
	case .Pattern, .Image:
		// Tint logic: use white with group opacity
		return resolve_color({1, 1, 1, paint.opacity})
	}
	return resolve_color({1, 1, 1, 1})
}

//Renderer definition
make_g2d :: proc() -> renderer.Renderer {
	g2d_setup()

	return persistent_renderer
}

// --- Phase 6B: Path API Implementation ---


// Helper: Tesselate a cubic bezier into flat points
@(private)
tesselate_cubic :: proc(points: ^[dynamic][2]f32, p0, c1, c2, p1: [2]f32, segments: int = 32) {
	for i in 1 ..= segments {
		t := f32(i) / f32(segments)
		inv_t := 1.0 - t
		p :=
			inv_t * inv_t * inv_t * p0 +
			3.0 * inv_t * inv_t * t * c1 +
			3.0 * inv_t * t * t * c2 +
			t * t * t * p1
		append(points, p)
	}
}

// Helper function to format pixel format for debugging
format_pixel_format :: proc(pixel_fmt: sg.Pixel_Format) -> string {
	#partial switch pixel_fmt {
	case .NONE:
		return "NONE"
	case .RGBA8:
		return "RGBA8"
	case .BGRA8:
		return "BGRA8"
	case .RGBA16:
		return "RGBA16"
	case .DEPTH:
		return "DEPTH"
	case .DEPTH_STENCIL:
		return "DEPTH_STENCIL"
	case .BC1_RGBA:
		return "BC1_RGBA"
	case .BC2_RGBA:
		return "BC2_RGBA"
	case .BC3_RGBA:
		return "BC3_RGBA"
	case .BC4_R:
		return "BC4_R"
	case .BC5_RG:
		return "BC5_RG"
	case .BC7_RGBA:
		return "BC7_RGBA"
	}
	return fmt.tprintf("UNKNOWN(%d)", pixel_fmt)
}

// Helper: Tesselate an arc into flat points
@(private)
tesselate_arc :: proc(
	points: ^[dynamic][2]f32,
	cx, cy, r, start_angle, end_angle: f32,
	clockwise: bool,
	segments: int = 32,
) {
	diff := end_angle - start_angle
	if clockwise && diff < 0 do diff += 2.0 * math.PI
	if !clockwise && diff > 0 do diff -= 2.0 * math.PI

	for i in 1 ..= segments {
		t := f32(i) / f32(segments)
		angle := start_angle + diff * t
		p := [2]f32{cx + math.cos(angle) * r, cy + math.sin(angle) * r}
		append(points, p)
	}
}

// Phase 11: Stencil Buffer Fill Implementation
@(private)
_g2d_fill_path_stencil :: proc(
	contours: [dynamic][dynamic][2]f32,
	paint: renderer.Paint,
	min_x, min_y, dx, dy, size: f32,
) {
	if len(contours) == 0 {
		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.debug(.G2D, "[STENCIL] No contours to fill")
		}
		return
	}

	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		logging.debug(.SVG, "[STENCIL] === Starting stencil fill ===")
		logging.debug(
			.SVG,
			fmt.tprintf("[STENCIL] Fill rule: %v, contours: %d", paint.fill_rule, len(contours)),
		)
	}

	// ========================================================================
	// STEP 1: FLUSH PENDING BATCHES
	// ========================================================================
	// Critical: Render everything batched so far before we change pipelines
	// and do immediate draws

	if len(ctx.batches) > 0 {
		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.debug(
				.G2D,
				fmt.tprintf(
					"[STENCIL] Flushing %d batches before stencil operation",
					len(ctx.batches),
				),
			)
		}

		screen_w := ctx.screen_w
		screen_h := ctx.screen_h

		_g2d_render_batches(ctx.batches[:], screen_w, screen_h)
		clear(&ctx.batches)
		clear(&ctx.vertices)
	}

	// ========================================================================
	// STEP 2: SAVE STATE
	// ========================================================================

	old_pipeline := ctx.curr_pipeline
	old_image := ctx.curr_image

	// ========================================================================
	// STEP 3: STENCIL WRITE PASS (IMMEDIATE RENDERING)
	// ========================================================================
	// We're already in a render pass - just switch pipeline and draw

	// Select appropriate stencil write pipeline
	stencil_variant: G2D_Pipeline_Variant
	if paint.fill_rule == .EvenOdd {
		stencil_variant = .StencilWriteEvenOdd
	} else {
		stencil_variant = .StencilWriteNonZero
	}

	stencil_pip := pipeline_mgr_get(&ctx.pipeline_mgr, stencil_variant)

	if stencil_pip.id == 0 {
		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.error(.G2D, "[STENCIL] ERROR: Invalid stencil write pipeline!")
		}
		return
	}

	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		logging.debug(
			.SVG,
			fmt.tprintf(
				"[STENCIL] Stencil write pipeline: id=%d, variant=%v",
				stencil_pip.id,
				stencil_variant,
			),
		)
	}

	// Apply stencil write pipeline
	sg.apply_pipeline(stencil_pip)

	// CRITICAL FIX: Declare vs_params for apply_uniforms
	vs_params := shaders.Vs_Params {
		screen_size = {ctx.screen_w, ctx.screen_h},
	}

	// Build triangle fans for all contours
	// Use path arena - these vertices are only needed for this operation
	path_scratch := g2d_get_path_scratch()
	stencil_verts := make([dynamic]renderer.G2D_Vertex, 0, 2048, path_scratch)
	// No defer delete needed - path_arena resets at next fill operation

	dummy_color := [4]f32{0, 0, 0, 0} // Masked out by pipeline
	dummy_uv := [2]f32{0, 0}

	// Tessellate each contour into triangles
	for points in contours {
		if len(points) < 3 do continue

		// CRITICAL FIX: Triangle fan from fixed origin {0, 0} instead of points[0]
		// This ensures correct winding number calculation for NonZero fill rule
		// The origin {0, 0} is in NDC space and outside visible area after transform
		origin := renderer.G2D_Vertex {
			pos   = {0, 0},
			uv    = dummy_uv,
			color = dummy_color,
		}

		for i in 0 ..< len(points) - 1 {
			p1_world := points[i]
			p2_world := points[i + 1]

			// Skip degenerate edges (but not origin checks - origin is always safe)
			if linalg.distance(p1_world, p2_world) < 0.001 do continue

			p1 := apply_transform(p1_world.x, p1_world.y)
			p2 := apply_transform(p2_world.x, p2_world.y)

			append(
				&stencil_verts,
				origin,
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{pos = p1, uv = dummy_uv, color = dummy_color},
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{pos = p2, uv = dummy_uv, color = dummy_color},
			)
		}
	}

	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		logging.debug(
			.SVG,
			fmt.tprintf(
				"[STENCIL] Generated %d stencil vertices from %d contours",
				len(stencil_verts),
				len(contours),
			),
		)
	}

	// Upload and draw stencil vertices IMMEDIATELY (not batched)
	if len(stencil_verts) > 0 {
		vertex_data_size := uint(len(stencil_verts) * size_of(renderer.G2D_Vertex))

		offset := sg.append_buffer(
			ctx.bind.vertex_buffers[0],
			sg.Range{ptr = raw_data(stencil_verts), size = vertex_data_size},
		)

		// Bind vertex buffer and dummy texture
		ctx.bind.vertex_buffers[0] = ctx.bind.vertex_buffers[0]
		ctx.bind.views[shaders.VIEW_tex] = ctx.white_view
		ctx.bind.samplers[shaders.SMP_smp] = ctx.smp
		ctx.bind.vertex_buffer_offsets[0] = offset // CRITICAL FIX: Use byte offset in bindings
		sg.apply_bindings(ctx.bind)
		// CRITICAL FIX: Add missing apply_uniforms after apply_pipeline
		vs_params := shaders.Vs_Params {
			screen_size = {ctx.screen_w, ctx.screen_h},
		}
		sg.apply_uniforms(0, sg.Range{ptr = &vs_params, size = size_of(shaders.Vs_Params)})

		// Draw NOW (immediate, not batched)
		sg.draw(0, i32(len(stencil_verts)), 1) // CRITICAL FIX: Draw from vertex 0

		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.debug(
				.SVG,
				fmt.tprintf(
					"[STENCIL] Drew %d stencil vertices at offset %d",
					len(stencil_verts),
					offset,
				),
			)
		}
	}

	// ========================================================================
	// STEP 4: STENCIL COVER PASS (IMMEDIATE RENDERING)
	// ========================================================================
	// Now draw where stencil != 0

	cover_pip := pipeline_mgr_get(&ctx.pipeline_mgr, .StencilCover)

	if cover_pip.id == 0 {
		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.error(.G2D, "[STENCIL] ERROR: Invalid stencil cover pipeline!")
		}
		ctx.curr_pipeline = old_pipeline
		ctx.curr_image = old_image
		return
	}

	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		logging.debug(.SVG, fmt.tprintf("[STENCIL] Stencil cover pipeline: id=%d", cover_pip.id))
	}

	// Apply cover pipeline
	sg.apply_pipeline(cover_pip)

	// Calculate tight bounds around all contours
	real_min_x, real_max_x := min_x, min_x
	real_min_y, real_max_y := min_y, min_y
	first := true

	for points in contours {
		for p in points {
			if first {
				real_min_x = p.x
				real_max_x = p.x
				real_min_y = p.y
				real_max_y = p.y
				first = false
			} else {
				real_min_x = min(real_min_x, p.x)
				real_max_x = max(real_max_x, p.x)
				real_min_y = min(real_min_y, p.y)
				real_max_y = max(real_max_y, p.y)
			}
		}
	}

	// Add small padding to ensure full coverage
	padding := f32(1.0)
	real_min_x -= padding
	real_min_y -= padding
	real_max_x += padding
	real_max_y += padding

	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		logging.debug(
			.SVG,
			fmt.tprintf(
				"[STENCIL] Cover bounds: [%.1f, %.1f] to [%.1f, %.1f]",
				real_min_x,
				real_min_y,
				real_max_x,
				real_max_y,
			),
		)
	}

	// Transform cover quad corners
	q0 := apply_transform(real_min_x, real_min_y)
	q1 := apply_transform(real_max_x, real_min_y)
	q2 := apply_transform(real_max_x, real_max_y)
	q3 := apply_transform(real_min_x, real_max_y)

	// Resolve colors based on paint type
	c0 := _g2d_resolve_vertex_color(real_min_x, real_min_y, paint, min_x, min_y, dx, dy, size)
	c1 := _g2d_resolve_vertex_color(real_max_x, real_min_y, paint, min_x, min_y, dx, dy, size)
	c2 := _g2d_resolve_vertex_color(real_max_x, real_max_y, paint, min_x, min_y, dx, dy, size)
	c3 := _g2d_resolve_vertex_color(real_min_x, real_max_y, paint, min_x, min_y, dx, dy, size)

	// UV coordinates for image/pattern paints
	uv0 := [2]f32{0, 0}
	uv1 := [2]f32{1, 0}
	uv2 := [2]f32{1, 1}
	uv3 := [2]f32{0, 1}

	// Create cover quad (2 triangles)
	cover_verts := [6]renderer.G2D_Vertex {
		// Triangle 1
		{pos = q0, uv = uv0, color = c0},
		{pos = q1, uv = uv1, color = c1},
		{pos = q2, uv = uv2, color = c2},
		// Triangle 2
		{pos = q0, uv = uv0, color = c0},
		{pos = q2, uv = uv2, color = c2},
		{pos = q3, uv = uv3, color = c3},
	}

	// Upload cover quad
	offset := sg.append_buffer(
		ctx.bind.vertex_buffers[0],
		sg.Range{ptr = &cover_verts[0], size = size_of(cover_verts)},
	)

	// Select appropriate image based on paint type
	cover_image := ctx.white_image
	if paint.type == .Image || paint.type == .Pattern {
		cover_image = handle_to_sg_image(paint.image)
	}

	// Bind resources for cover quad
	ctx.bind.vertex_buffers[0] = ctx.bind.vertex_buffers[0]
	// For cover image, we need to get the appropriate view
	view_for_cover := ctx.white_view
	if cover_image.id != ctx.white_image.id {
		if v, ok := ctx.image_views[cover_image.id]; ok && v.id != 0 {
			view_for_cover = v
		} else {
			view_for_cover = sg.make_view({texture = {image = cover_image}})
			ctx.image_views[cover_image.id] = view_for_cover
		}
	}
	ctx.bind.views[shaders.VIEW_tex] = view_for_cover
	ctx.bind.samplers[shaders.SMP_smp] = ctx.smp
	ctx.bind.vertex_buffer_offsets[0] = offset // CRITICAL FIX: Use byte offset in bindings
	sg.apply_bindings(ctx.bind)
	// CRITICAL FIX: Add missing apply_uniforms after apply_pipeline
	sg.apply_uniforms(0, sg.Range{ptr = &vs_params, size = size_of(shaders.Vs_Params)})

	// Draw cover quad NOW (immediate, not batched)
	sg.draw(0, 6, 1) // CRITICAL FIX: Draw from vertex 0

	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		logging.debug(.SVG, "[STENCIL] Drew cover quad")
	}

	// ========================================================================
	// STEP 5: RESTORE STATE
	// ========================================================================
	// Restore original state so normal batching can continue
	ctx.curr_pipeline = old_pipeline
	ctx.curr_image = old_image
	// CRITICAL FIX: Restore pipeline and uniforms after stencil operations
	if ctx.curr_pipeline.id != 0 {
		sg.apply_pipeline(ctx.curr_pipeline)
		vs_params := shaders.Vs_Params {
			screen_size = {ctx.screen_w, ctx.screen_h},
		}
		sg.apply_uniforms(0, sg.Range{ptr = &vs_params, size = size_of(shaders.Vs_Params)})
	}

	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		logging.debug(.SVG, "[STENCIL] === Stencil fill complete ===")
	}
} //end _g2d_fill_path_stencil

// Unified Path Fill
g2d_path_fill_immediate :: proc(paint: renderer.Paint) {
	// Phase 6.1: Reset path arena for this fill operation
	mem.arena_free_all(&ctx.path_arena)

	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		logging.debug(.G2D, fmt.tprintf("g2d_path_fill called with fill_rule=%v", paint.fill_rule))
	}
	if len(ctx.current_path) < 3 do return

	// Use paint image if applicable, otherwise white image for solid/gradients
	if paint.type == .Image || paint.type == .Pattern {
		ctx.curr_image = handle_to_sg_image(paint.image)
	} else {
		ctx.curr_image = ctx.white_image
	}

	contours := _g2d_get_contours()
	if len(contours) == 0 do return

	// Calculate LOCAL bounds from Contours for paint resolution
	min_x, max_x, min_y, max_y: f32
	first_pt := true

	for points in contours {
		for p in points {
			if first_pt {
				min_x, max_x = p.x, p.x
				min_y, max_y = p.y, p.y
				first_pt = false
			} else {
				if p.x < min_x do min_x = p.x
				if p.x > max_x do max_x = p.x
				if p.y < min_y do min_y = p.y
				if p.y > max_y do max_y = p.y
			}
		}
	}

	if first_pt do return // No points found


	width := max_x - min_x
	height := max_y - min_y
	size := math.sqrt(width * width + height * height)

	// Pre-calc linear gradient params
	dx, dy: f32
	if paint.type == .Linear_Gradient {
		v := paint.linear_end - paint.linear_start
		len_v := linalg.length(v)
		if len_v > 0.0001 {
			dx = v.x / len_v
			dy = v.y / len_v
			size = len_v // Gradient length
		}
	}

	inv_w: f32 = width > 0 ? 1.0 / width : 0
	inv_h: f32 = height > 0 ? 1.0 / height : 0

	// Phase 11: Stencil Buffer Path for Self-Intersecting Polygons
	// Use batched stencil operations instead of immediate execution
	if (paint.fill_rule == .NonZero || paint.fill_rule == .EvenOdd) {

		// Create a stencil batch instead of executing immediately
		// This resolves the timing issue where pathFill is called outside render pass
		_g2d_create_stencil_batch(contours, paint, min_x, min_y, max_x, max_y, dx, dy, size)
		return
	}

	// Always use tessellation for now to avoid sample count mismatches

	// Fallback: Get flat points for tessellator
	points := _g2d_get_flat_points()
	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		logging.info(
			.G2D,
			fmt.tprintf(
				"Tessellating polygon with %d points, paint type: %v",
				len(points),
				paint.type,
			),
		)
	}
	// Phase 6.1: Use path arena for tessellation
	path_scratch := g2d_get_path_scratch()
	indices, ok := tessellate_polygon(points[:], paint.fill_rule, path_scratch)
	if !ok {
		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.info(
				.G2D,
				fmt.tprintf(
					"Tessellation failed for %d points with fill rule %v",
					len(points),
					paint.fill_rule,
				),
			)
			logging.info(.G2D, "Using fallback: rendering bounding box")
		}

		// Fallback strategy for complex polygons - works for ALL paint types
		// For image patterns, if tessellation fails, render a bounding box with the image
		// This ensures the image is visible even if the shape isn't perfect

		// Calculate bounding box
		bb_min_x := min_x
		bb_min_y := min_y
		bb_max_x := max_x
		bb_max_y := max_y

		// Create a simple quad that covers the bounding box
		bb_points := [][2]f32 {
			{bb_min_x, bb_min_y},
			{bb_max_x, bb_min_y},
			{bb_max_x, bb_max_y},
			{bb_min_x, bb_max_y},
		}

		// Tessellate the bounding box (should always work for convex quad)
		// Phase 6.1: Use path arena for tessellation
		path_scratch := g2d_get_path_scratch()
		bb_indices, bb_ok := tessellate_polygon(bb_points[:], paint.fill_rule, path_scratch)
		if bb_ok {
			when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
				logging.info(.G2D, "Fallback successful: rendering bounding box")
			}

			b := get_batch()

			// Render the bounding box with the original paint
			for i := 0; i < len(bb_indices); i += 3 {
				idx0 := bb_indices[i]
				idx1 := bb_indices[i + 1]
				idx2 := bb_indices[i + 2]

				p0_world := bb_points[idx0]
				p1_world := bb_points[idx1]
				p2_world := bb_points[idx2]

				p0 := apply_transform(p0_world.x, p0_world.y)
				p1 := apply_transform(p1_world.x, p1_world.y)
				p2 := apply_transform(p2_world.x, p2_world.y)

				c0 := _g2d_resolve_vertex_color(
					p0_world.x,
					p0_world.y,
					paint,
					min_x,
					min_y,
					dx,
					dy,
					size,
				)
				c1 := _g2d_resolve_vertex_color(
					p1_world.x,
					p1_world.y,
					paint,
					min_x,
					min_y,
					dx,
					dy,
					size,
				)
				c2 := _g2d_resolve_vertex_color(
					p2_world.x,
					p2_world.y,
					paint,
					min_x,
					min_y,
					dx,
					dy,
					size,
				)

				// Handle UV coordinates based on paint type
				uv0, uv1, uv2 := [2]f32{0.0, 0.0}, [2]f32{1.0, 0.0}, [2]f32{1.0, 1.0}
				if i + 3 == len(bb_indices) { 	// Last triangle
					uv2 = [2]f32{0.0, 1.0}
				}

				// For non-image paints, UV coordinates don't matter but we still need valid values
				if paint.type != .Image && paint.type != .Pattern {
					uv0 = [2]f32{0.0, 0.0}
					uv1 = [2]f32{0.0, 0.0}
					uv2 = [2]f32{0.0, 0.0}
				}

				add_vertices(
					b,
					renderer.G2D_Vertex{p0, uv0, c0},
					renderer.G2D_Vertex{p1, uv1, c1},
					renderer.G2D_Vertex{p2, uv2, c2},
				)
			}
			return
		}

		// If no fallback worked, just return
		return
	}

	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		logging.info(
			.G2D,
			fmt.tprintf(
				"Tessellation succeeded: %d indices (%d triangles), image: %v",
				len(indices),
				len(indices) / 3,
				paint.type == .Image || paint.type == .Pattern,
			),
		)
	}

	b := get_batch()

	for i := 0; i < len(indices); i += 3 {
		idx0 := indices[i]
		idx1 := indices[i + 1]
		idx2 := indices[i + 2]

		p0_world := points[idx0]
		p1_world := points[idx1]
		p2_world := points[idx2]

		p0 := apply_transform(p0_world.x, p0_world.y)
		p1 := apply_transform(p1_world.x, p1_world.y)
		p2 := apply_transform(p2_world.x, p2_world.y)

		c0 := _g2d_resolve_vertex_color(p0_world.x, p0_world.y, paint, min_x, min_y, dx, dy, size)
		c1 := _g2d_resolve_vertex_color(p1_world.x, p1_world.y, paint, min_x, min_y, dx, dy, size)
		c2 := _g2d_resolve_vertex_color(p2_world.x, p2_world.y, paint, min_x, min_y, dx, dy, size)

		uv0 := [2]f32{0.5, 0.5}
		uv1 := [2]f32{0.5, 0.5}
		uv2 := [2]f32{0.5, 0.5}

		if paint.type == .Image || paint.type == .Pattern {
			// For image patterns in tessellated polygons, we need to ensure the entire
			// texture is visible. The current approach maps polygon bounds to [0,1],
			// but this can result in empty areas if the UV mapping is incorrect.

			// Simple UV mapping: map polygon bounds to [0,1] texture range
			uv0 = {(p0_world.x - min_x) * inv_w, (p0_world.y - min_y) * inv_h}
			uv1 = {(p1_world.x - min_x) * inv_w, (p1_world.y - min_y) * inv_h}
			uv2 = {(p2_world.x - min_x) * inv_w, (p2_world.y - min_y) * inv_h}

			// Debug: Ensure UV coordinates are in valid range
			logging.info(
				.G2D,
				fmt.tprintf(
					"UV0: (%.3f, %.3f), UV1: (%.3f, %.3f), UV2: (%.3f, %.3f)",
					uv0.x,
					uv0.y,
					uv1.x,
					uv1.y,
					uv2.x,
					uv2.y,
				),
			)

			// Clamp UV coordinates to [0,1] range to prevent sampling outside texture
			uv0.x = math.clamp(uv0.x, 0.0, 1.0)
			uv0.y = math.clamp(uv0.y, 0.0, 1.0)
			uv1.x = math.clamp(uv1.x, 0.0, 1.0)
			uv1.y = math.clamp(uv1.y, 0.0, 1.0)
			uv2.x = math.clamp(uv2.x, 0.0, 1.0)
			uv2.y = math.clamp(uv2.y, 0.0, 1.0)

			if paint.uv_transform != linalg.MATRIX3F32_IDENTITY {
				uv0 = (paint.uv_transform * [3]f32{uv0.x, uv0.y, 1.0}).xy
				uv1 = (paint.uv_transform * [3]f32{uv1.x, uv1.y, 1.0}).xy
				uv2 = (paint.uv_transform * [3]f32{uv2.x, uv2.y, 1.0}).xy
			}
		}

		add_vertices(
			b,
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{p0, uv0, c0},
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{p1, uv1, c1},
			renderer.G2D_Vertex{p2, uv2, c2},
		)
	}
} //end of g2d_fill_path

// Helper: Sample gradient
sample_gradient :: proc(t_in: f32, colors: [][4]f32, stops: []f32) -> [4]f32 {
	t := clamp(t_in, 0, 1)
	if len(colors) == 0 do return {0, 0, 0, 0}
	if len(colors) == 1 do return colors[0]

	if len(stops) == 0 {
		index := int(t * f32(len(colors) - 1))
		return colors[index]
	}

	if t <= stops[0] do return colors[0]
	if t >= stops[len(stops) - 1] do return colors[len(colors) - 1]

	for i in 0 ..< len(stops) - 1 {
		if t >= stops[i] && t <= stops[i + 1] {
			local_t := (t - stops[i]) / (stops[i + 1] - stops[i])
			return linalg.lerp(colors[i], colors[i + 1], local_t)
		}
	}
	return colors[len(colors) - 1]
}

resolve_paint_color :: proc(p: renderer.Paint) -> [4]f32 {
	#partial switch p.type {
	case .Solid:
		return p.color
	case .Linear_Gradient, .Radial_Gradient, .Conical_Gradient:
		if len(p.gradient_colors) > 0 {
			return p.gradient_colors[0]
		}
	case .Pattern, .Image:
	// Default to white for images if just using for color tint
	}
	return {1, 1, 1, 1}
} //end of resolve_paint_color

g2d_path_draw_immediate :: proc(
	thickness: f32,
	color: [4]f32,
	join: renderer.Line_Join = .Miter,
	cap: renderer.Line_Cap = .Butt,
) {
	if len(ctx.current_path) < 2 do return
	points := _g2d_get_flat_points()
	if len(points) < 2 do return

	paint := renderer.paint_solid(color)
	stroke := renderer.Stroke {
		width       = thickness,
		paint       = paint,
		join        = join,
		cap         = cap,
		miter_limit = 4.0,
	}
	_g2d_stroke_raw(points[:], stroke)
} // end of g2d_path_draw


g2d_draw_bezier_quadratic_immediate :: proc(
	x1, y1, cx, cy, x2, y2, thickness: f32,
	color: [4]f32,
) {
	g2d_path_begin()
	g2d_path_move_to(x1, y1)
	g2d_path_quad_to(cx, cy, x2, y2)
	g2d_path_draw(thickness, color)
}

g2d_draw_bezier_cubic :: proc(x1, y1, c1x, c1y, c2x, c2y, x2, y2, thickness: f32, color: [4]f32) {
	g2d_path_begin()
	g2d_path_move_to(x1, y1)
	g2d_path_cubic_to(c1x, c1y, c2x, c2y, x2, y2)
	g2d_path_draw(thickness, color)
}

g2d_get_input_state :: proc() -> rawptr {
	return &ctx.input
}

// --- Phase 9B: Beautification Implementation ---

g2d_fill_rect_gradient :: proc(x, y, w, h: f32, c_tl, c_tr, c_br, c_bl: [4]f32) {
	g2d_fill_rect_multi_color(x, y, w, h, c_tl, c_tr, c_br, c_bl)
}

g2d_fill_rect_rounded_gradient_immediate :: proc(
	x, y, w, h, radius: f32,
	c_top, c_bottom: [4]f32,
	vertical: bool = true,
) {
	// Simplified implementation:
	// Split into top/center/bottom parts for vertical gradient.
	// For now, we will just use the bounding box gradient linear logic
	// but applying it to the rounded rect is tricky without a shader or complex tesselation.
	//
	// Alternative: Use the existing logic but manually interpolate colors for the parts.
	// A full gradient on a rounded rect requires either a fragment shader or many vertices.
	//
	// Approximation for vertical gradient:
	// Top Round Part: Solid c_top or Gradient(c_top -> c_mid_top)
	// Vertically, t goes from 0 at y to 1 at y+h.

	// Let's implement a high-quality vertical gradient by tesselating.
	// Actually, `g2d_fill_rect_linear_gradient` tesselates as a single quad (2 triangles).
	// This won't work for rounded corners.

	// Fallback to "Good Enough" for Demo:
	// We will draw a normal rounded rect with the Top color,
	// and overlay a linear gradient quad in the center? No.

	// Better approach:
	// Just use `g2d_fill_rect_rounded` with the average color? No, user wants gradients.
	//
	// OK, Manual Construction:
	// r = radius
	// Top Left Corner: Fan. Center=(x+r, y+r). Pixels y..y+r. Color near top.
	// Top Right Corner: Fan. Center=(x+w-r, y+r).
	// Top Edge: Rect.
	// Center: Rect.
	// Bottom Edge: Rect.
	// Bottom Corners...

	// To support `c_top` to `c_bottom` vertically:
	// We need to calculate the color at any Y.
	// lerp_y(y_pos) = lerp(c_top, c_bottom, (y_pos - y) / h)

	r := math.min(radius, math.min(w, h) * 0.5)

	get_col :: proc(p_y, start_y, h: f32, c1, c2: [4]f32) -> [4]f32 {
		t := clamp((p_y - start_y) / h, 0, 1)
		return linalg.lerp(c1, c2, t)
	}

	// 1. Top Section (y to y+r)
	c_0 := get_col(y, y, h, c_top, c_bottom) // Top edge
	c_r := get_col(y + r, y, h, c_top, c_bottom) // Top-corner bottom edge

	// Draw Top Corners (Fans)
	// We cheat and use solid color for corners to save triangles, or average?
	// High quality: Vertex colors.
	// Center of TL fan is at (x+r, y+r). UVs?
	// `draw_corner_vertex_gradient` helper needed.

	// Simpler Hack for time:
	// Use the g2d_fill_rect_linear_gradient logic but just on the center, and solid colors for ends?
	// No, that looks bad.

	// Let's defer "Perfect" rounded gradients and implement "Center Rect Gradient + Solid Corners" for now?
	// No, that looks bad.

	// We will implement `g2d_fill_rect_rounded` but manually set the colors of the vertices.
	// We need access to `draw_corner_arc` logic but with per-vertex color.
	// I'll re-implement the geometry generation here.

	ctx.curr_image = ctx.white_image
	b := get_batch()

	// Helper to add vertex with vertical gradient calc
	// BUT, adding vertices one by one is slow if we do logic per vertex.
	// Optimization: Pre-calculate colors at key Y positions (y, y+r, y+h-r, y+h).

	c_y0 := get_col(y, y, h, c_top, c_bottom)
	c_yr := get_col(y + r, y, h, c_top, c_bottom)
	c_yhr := get_col(y + h - r, y, h, c_top, c_bottom)
	c_yh := get_col(y + h, y, h, c_top, c_bottom)

	// 1. Center Rect (solid gradient vertical)
	// x, y+r, w, h-2r
	// Top color: c_yr, Bottom color: c_yhr
	g2d_fill_rect_multi_color(x, y + r, w, h - 2 * r, c_yr, c_yr, c_yhr, c_yhr)

	// 2. Top Rect (between corners)
	// x+r, y, w-2r, r
	// Top: c_y0, Bottom: c_yr
	g2d_fill_rect_multi_color(x + r, y, w - 2 * r, r, c_y0, c_y0, c_yr, c_yr)

	// 3. Bottom Rect (between corners)
	// x+r, y+h-r, w-2r, r
	// Top: c_yhr, Bottom: c_yh
	g2d_fill_rect_multi_color(x + r, y + h - r, w - 2 * r, r, c_yhr, c_yhr, c_yh, c_yh)

	// 4. Corners
	// We need a version of draw_corner_arc that takes INNER color and OUTER color?
	// For vertical gradient:
	// Top-Left: Center=(x+r, y+r). Inner Color = c_yr. Outer Loop colors go from c_y0 (at -90/top) to c_yr (at 180/left).
	// This requires angular interpolation of colors?
	// Approximate: Use c_yr for center, and interpolate outer ring?
	// Actually, Y varies in the corner.
	// Correct way: Calculate color for each vertex based on its Y.

	draw_gradient_corner :: proc(
		cx, cy, r: f32,
		start_angle, end_angle: f32,
		y_start, h: f32,
		c_top, c_bottom: [4]f32,
	) {
		segments := 12
		center_pos := apply_transform(cx, cy)
		// Center color is determined by cy
		c_center := linalg.lerp(c_top, c_bottom, clamp((cy - y_start) / h, 0, 1))

		delta := (end_angle - start_angle)

		for i in 0 ..< segments {
			t1 := start_angle + (f32(i) / f32(segments)) * delta
			t2 := start_angle + (f32(i + 1) / f32(segments)) * delta

			px1 := cx + math.cos(t1) * r
			py1 := cy + math.sin(t1) * r
			px2 := cx + math.cos(t2) * r
			py2 := cy + math.sin(t2) * r

			c1 := linalg.lerp(c_top, c_bottom, clamp((py1 - y_start) / h, 0, 1))
			c2 := linalg.lerp(c_top, c_bottom, clamp((py2 - y_start) / h, 0, 1))

			tp1 := apply_transform(px1, py1)
			tp2 := apply_transform(px2, py2)

			add_vertices(
				get_batch(),
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{center_pos, {0.5, 0.5}, c_center},
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{tp1, {0, 0}, c1},
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{tp2, {0, 0}, c2},
			)
		}
	}

	// TL: PI to 3PI/2. (But wait, `draw_corner_arc` uses different angles?)
	// draw_corner_arc uses:
	// TL: PI
	// TR: 3PI/2
	// BR: 0
	// BL: PI/2
	// AND it loops 0..segments -> quarter circle.

	draw_gradient_corner(x + r, y + r, r, math.PI, math.PI * 1.5, y, h, c_top, c_bottom) // TL? No, 180 to 270 is Top Left.
	draw_gradient_corner(x + w - r, y + r, r, math.PI * 1.5, math.PI * 2.0, y, h, c_top, c_bottom) // TR
	draw_gradient_corner(x + w - r, y + h - r, r, 0, math.PI * 0.5, y, h, c_top, c_bottom) // BR
	draw_gradient_corner(x + r, y + h - r, r, math.PI * 0.5, math.PI, y, h, c_top, c_bottom) // BL
}

// ============================================================================
// Phase F.2.5 — Offscreen Blur Drop Shadow Pipeline
// ============================================================================
// Draws the shape silhouette into an offscreen render target, queues it for
// Gaussian blur during the GPU pre-pass, and draws the blurred result to the
// screen. Results are cached per shape+color+blur combination.

// Drop shadow for rounded rectangles
g2d_drop_shadow_rect_immediate :: proc(
	x, y, w, h, radius: f32,
	offset_x, offset_y: f32, // light direction offset
	blur: f32, // spread width in pixels
	color: [4]f32,
) {
	if blur <= 0 || color.w <= 0 do return

	key := renderer.Shadow_Key {
		kind      = 0,
		_padding1 = {0, 0, 0},
		w         = w,
		h         = h,
		r         = radius,
		sides     = 0,
		offset_x  = offset_x,
		offset_y  = offset_y,
		blur      = blur,
	}
	entry := _g2d_request_drop_shadow_rt(key)
	if entry != nil {
		// Cache hit: draw the blurred shadow RT behind where the shape will be, tinted by color
		g2d_draw_render_target(
			&entry.rt_shadow,
			x - blur * 2 + offset_x,
			y - blur * 2 + offset_y,
			entry.width,
			entry.height,
			color,
		)
	} else {
		logging.debug(.G2D, "[SHADOW] Cache miss for rect shadow. Queueing silhouette for offscreen pass.")
		// Cache miss: create stable heap entry and queue silhouette rendering for offscreen pass.
		// This ensures all shadow rendering happens before main pass begins, preventing interleaved batches.
		entry = _g2d_create_drop_shadow_rt(key)

		// Render silhouette as white into shadow RT — color is applied as tint at draw time,
		// so the RT can be reused for any color of the same shape.
		// Use g2d_set_render_target so the correct OffscreenNoDepth pipeline is
		// activated — otherwise get_batch() stores the Screen pipeline in the batch,
		// causing a pipeline/pass format mismatch when the batch is rendered.
		old_rt := ctx.active_rt
		old_matrix := ctx.curr_matrix
		old_scissor_on := ctx.curr_scissor_on
		old_scissor := ctx.curr_scissor
		old_opacity := ctx.curr_opacity
		g2d_set_render_target(&entry.rt_shadow)
		ctx.curr_matrix = linalg.MATRIX3F32_IDENTITY
		ctx.curr_opacity = 1.0
		ctx.curr_scissor_on = true
		ctx.curr_scissor = [4]f32{0, 0, f32(entry.rt_shadow.width), f32(entry.rt_shadow.height)}
		g2d_fill_rect_rounded_immediate(blur * 2, blur * 2, w, h, radius, renderer.paint_solid({1, 1, 1, 1}))
		g2d_set_render_target(old_rt)
		ctx.curr_matrix = old_matrix
		ctx.curr_opacity = old_opacity
		ctx.curr_scissor_on = old_scissor_on
		ctx.curr_scissor = old_scissor

		// Queue the blur operation for this new shadow RT
		blur_entry := renderer.Pending_Blur {
			target = entry.rt_shadow,
			temp   = entry.rt_temp,
			radius = blur,
		}
		append(&ctx.pending_blurs, blur_entry)
	}
}

// Drop shadow for circles
g2d_drop_shadow_circle_immediate :: proc(
	cx, cy, radius: f32,
	offset_x, offset_y: f32,
	blur: f32,
	color: [4]f32,
) {
	if blur <= 0 || color.w <= 0 do return

	key := renderer.Shadow_Key {
		kind      = 1,
		_padding1 = {0, 0, 0},
		w         = radius * 2, // Map to w/h for cache consistency
		h         = radius * 2,
		r         = radius,
		sides     = 0,
		offset_x  = offset_x,
		offset_y  = offset_y,
		blur      = blur,
	}
	entry := _g2d_request_drop_shadow_rt(key)
	if entry != nil {
		// Cache hit: draw the blurred shadow RT behind where the shape will be, tinted by color
		g2d_draw_render_target(
			&entry.rt_shadow,
			cx - radius - blur * 2 + offset_x,
			cy - radius - blur * 2 + offset_y,
			entry.width,
			entry.height,
			color,
		)
	} else {
		logging.debug(.G2D, "[SHADOW] Cache miss for circle shadow. Queueing silhouette for offscreen pass.")
		// Cache miss: create stable heap entry and queue silhouette rendering for offscreen pass.
		// This ensures all shadow rendering happens before main pass begins, preventing interleaved batches.
		entry = _g2d_create_drop_shadow_rt(key)

		// Render silhouette as white into shadow RT — color is applied as tint at draw time
		old_rt := ctx.active_rt
		old_matrix := ctx.curr_matrix
		old_scissor_on := ctx.curr_scissor_on
		old_scissor := ctx.curr_scissor
		old_opacity := ctx.curr_opacity
		g2d_set_render_target(&entry.rt_shadow)
		ctx.curr_matrix = linalg.MATRIX3F32_IDENTITY
		ctx.curr_opacity = 1.0
		ctx.curr_scissor_on = true
		ctx.curr_scissor = [4]f32{0, 0, f32(entry.rt_shadow.width), f32(entry.rt_shadow.height)}
		g2d_fill_circle_immediate(blur * 2 + radius, blur * 2 + radius, radius, renderer.paint_solid({1, 1, 1, 1}))
		g2d_set_render_target(old_rt)
		ctx.curr_matrix = old_matrix
		ctx.curr_opacity = old_opacity
		ctx.curr_scissor_on = old_scissor_on
		ctx.curr_scissor = old_scissor

		// Queue the blur operation for this new shadow RT
		blur_entry := renderer.Pending_Blur {
			target = entry.rt_shadow,
			temp   = entry.rt_temp,
			radius = blur,
		}
		append(&ctx.pending_blurs, blur_entry)
	}
}

// Drop shadow for N-gons (convex polygon icon buttons etc.)
g2d_drop_shadow_ngon_immediate :: proc(
	cx, cy, radius: f32,
	sides: int,
	offset_x, offset_y: f32,
	blur: f32,
	color: [4]f32,
) {
	if blur <= 0 || color.w <= 0 do return

	key := renderer.Shadow_Key {
		kind      = 2,
		_padding1 = {0, 0, 0},
		w         = radius * 2, // Map to w/h for cache consistency
		h         = radius * 2,
		r         = radius,
		sides     = i32(sides),
		offset_x  = offset_x,
		offset_y  = offset_y,
		blur      = blur,
	}
	entry := _g2d_request_drop_shadow_rt(key)
	if entry != nil {
		// Cache hit: draw the blurred shadow RT behind where the shape will be, tinted by color
		g2d_draw_render_target(
			&entry.rt_shadow,
			cx - radius - blur * 2 + offset_x,
			cy - radius - blur * 2 + offset_y,
			entry.width,
			entry.height,
			color,
		)
	} else {
		logging.debug(.G2D, "[SHADOW] Cache miss for ngon shadow. Queueing silhouette for offscreen pass.")
		// Cache miss: create stable heap entry and queue silhouette rendering for offscreen pass.
		// This ensures all shadow rendering happens before main pass begins, preventing interleaved batches.
		entry = _g2d_create_drop_shadow_rt(key)

		// Render silhouette as white into shadow RT — color is applied as tint at draw time
		old_rt := ctx.active_rt
		old_matrix := ctx.curr_matrix
		old_scissor_on := ctx.curr_scissor_on
		old_scissor := ctx.curr_scissor
		old_opacity := ctx.curr_opacity
		g2d_set_render_target(&entry.rt_shadow)
		ctx.curr_matrix = linalg.MATRIX3F32_IDENTITY
		ctx.curr_opacity = 1.0
		ctx.curr_scissor_on = true
		ctx.curr_scissor = [4]f32{0, 0, f32(entry.rt_shadow.width), f32(entry.rt_shadow.height)}
		g2d_fill_ngon_immediate(blur * 2 + radius, blur * 2 + radius, radius, sides, renderer.paint_solid({1, 1, 1, 1}))
		g2d_set_render_target(old_rt)
		ctx.curr_matrix = old_matrix
		ctx.curr_opacity = old_opacity
		ctx.curr_scissor_on = old_scissor_on
		ctx.curr_scissor = old_scissor

		// Queue the blur operation for this new shadow RT
		blur_entry := renderer.Pending_Blur {
			target = entry.rt_shadow,
			temp   = entry.rt_temp,
			radius = blur,
		}
		append(&ctx.pending_blurs, blur_entry)
	}
}

// FNV-1a hash over Shadow_Key bytes to avoid map[struct] call_indirect crash on WASM.
// Odin maps with struct keys use a generic hasher stored as a function pointer; on WASM
// the call_indirect type doesn't match → function signature mismatch.
// Collision risk is negligible for typical shadow cache sizes (< 32 entries).
@(private)
_shadow_key_hash :: proc(k: renderer.Shadow_Key) -> u64 {
	bytes := transmute([size_of(renderer.Shadow_Key)]u8)k
	h: u64 = 14695981039346656037 // FNV offset basis
	for b in bytes {
		h ~= u64(b)
		h *= 1099511628211 // FNV prime
	}
	return h
}

// Internal helper: checks cache and queues blur if missing
// Returns the cached shadow entry for this key, or nil on miss.
// Always safe: the returned pointer is heap-allocated and stable.
// Destroys all cached shadow RTs and frees their entries.
// Called on resize so stale per-tile-size RTs don't exhaust the image pool.
_g2d_clear_shadow_cache :: proc() {
	for _, entry in ctx.shadow_cache {
		g2d_destroy_render_target(entry.rt_shadow)
		g2d_destroy_render_target(entry.rt_temp)
		free(entry)
	}
	clear(&ctx.shadow_cache)
	clear(&ctx.shadow_cache_keys)
}

_g2d_request_drop_shadow_rt :: proc(key: renderer.Shadow_Key) -> ^renderer.Shadow_Cache_Entry {
	if _shadow_key_hash(key) in ctx.shadow_cache {
		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.debug(
				.G2D,
				fmt.tprintf(
					"[SHADOW_CACHE] Hit for key: w=%.1f h=%.1f r=%.1f blur=%.1f",
					key.w,
					key.h,
					key.r,
					key.blur,
				),
			)
		}
		return ctx.shadow_cache[_shadow_key_hash(key)]
	}
	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		logging.debug(
			.G2D,
			fmt.tprintf(
				"[SHADOW_CACHE] Miss for key: w=%.1f h=%.1f r=%.1f blur=%.1f cache_size=%d",
				key.w,
				key.h,
				key.r,
				key.blur,
				len(ctx.shadow_cache),
			),
		)
	}
	return nil
}

// Creates a new heap-allocated cache entry for this key, queues its blur.
// Caller is responsible for drawing the silhouette into entry.rt_shadow
// via g2d_set_render_target before returning control to the frame.
_g2d_create_drop_shadow_rt :: proc(key: renderer.Shadow_Key) -> ^renderer.Shadow_Cache_Entry {
	// Enforce cap: evict oldest entry (FIFO via shadow_cache_keys) before adding a new one.
	for i32(len(ctx.shadow_cache)) >= ctx.max_shadow_cache_entries && len(ctx.shadow_cache_keys) > 0 {
		oldest := ctx.shadow_cache_keys[0]
		ordered_remove(&ctx.shadow_cache_keys, 0)
		if old_entry, ok := ctx.shadow_cache[_shadow_key_hash(oldest)]; ok {
			g2d_destroy_render_target(old_entry.rt_shadow)
			g2d_destroy_render_target(old_entry.rt_temp)
			free(old_entry)
			delete_key(&ctx.shadow_cache, _shadow_key_hash(oldest))
		}
	}

	// 2× blur padding on each side prevents falloff clipping at RT edges.
	padding := key.blur * 2.0
	rt_w := key.w + (padding * 2)
	rt_h := key.h + (padding * 2)

	shadow_rt := g2d_create_render_target(i32(rt_w), i32(rt_h), false)
	temp_rt := g2d_create_render_target(i32(rt_w), i32(rt_h), false)

	// Heap-allocate the entry so its address is stable even as the map grows.
	entry := new(renderer.Shadow_Cache_Entry, runtime.default_allocator())
	entry^ = renderer.Shadow_Cache_Entry {
		rt_shadow = shadow_rt,
		rt_temp   = temp_rt,
		width     = rt_w,
		height    = rt_h,
		offset_x  = key.offset_x,
		offset_y  = key.offset_y,
	}
	ctx.shadow_cache[_shadow_key_hash(key)] = entry
	append(&ctx.shadow_cache_keys, key) // track insertion order for FIFO eviction
	// The Gaussian blur will be queued by the caller (e.g. g2d_drop_shadow_rect_immediate)
	// after it pushes the silhouette rendering command.

	return entry
}
// ============================================================================
// Phase F.2 — Inset Shadow
// ============================================================================
// Draws a dark gradient band inside the element, simulating a pressed/recessed look.
// Call AFTER the element fill and BEFORE content.
// top_depth:  height of the dark band at the top edge
// side_depth: width at each side edge
// color:      typically {0, 0, 0, 0.20}

g2d_inset_shadow_rect_immediate :: proc(
	x, y, w, h: f32,
	top_depth, side_depth: f32,
	color: [4]f32,
) {
	NUM_LAYERS :: 4
	// Top strip: dark at y → transparent at y+top_depth
	step_h := top_depth / f32(NUM_LAYERS)
	for i in 0 ..< NUM_LAYERS {
		frac := f32(i) / f32(NUM_LAYERS)
		frac_next := f32(i + 1) / f32(NUM_LAYERS)
		strip_y := y + frac * top_depth
		c_top := color; c_top.w = color.w * (1.0 - frac)
		c_bot := color; c_bot.w = color.w * (1.0 - frac_next)
		g2d_fill_rect_multi_color(x, strip_y, w, step_h, c_top, c_top, c_bot, c_bot)
	}
	// Left strip: dark at x → transparent at x+side_depth
	step_w := side_depth / f32(NUM_LAYERS)
	for i in 0 ..< NUM_LAYERS {
		frac := f32(i) / f32(NUM_LAYERS)
		frac_next := f32(i + 1) / f32(NUM_LAYERS)
		strip_x := x + frac * side_depth
		c_left := color; c_left.w = color.w * (1.0 - frac)
		c_right := color; c_right.w = color.w * (1.0 - frac_next)
		g2d_fill_rect_multi_color(strip_x, y, step_w, h, c_left, c_right, c_right, c_left)
	}
}

// ============================================================================
g2d_fill_ngon_immediate :: proc(
	x, y, radius: f32,
	sides: int,
	paint: renderer.Paint,
	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	if sides < 3 do return

	// Set the current image based on paint type
	if paint.type == .Image {
		ctx.curr_image = handle_to_sg_image(paint.image)
	} else if paint.type == .Pattern {
		ctx.curr_image = handle_to_sg_image(paint.image)
	} else {
		ctx.curr_image = ctx.white_image
	}

	b := get_batch()

	c_center := g2d_resolve_paint_color(x, y, paint)
	center := apply_transform(x, y)

	for i in 0 ..< sides {
		t1 := f32(i) * 2 * math.PI / f32(sides) - (math.PI / 2.0)
		t2 := f32(i + 1) * 2 * math.PI / f32(sides) - (math.PI / 2.0)

		px1, py1 := x + radius * math.cos(t1), y + radius * math.sin(t1)
		px2, py2 := x + radius * math.cos(t2), y + radius * math.sin(t2)

		p1 := apply_transform(px1, py1)
		p2 := apply_transform(px2, py2)

		c1 := g2d_resolve_paint_color(px1, py1, paint)
		c2 := g2d_resolve_paint_color(px2, py2, paint)

		// Calculate proper UV coordinates for image patterns
		// For image patterns, map the angular position to UV coordinates
		if paint.type == .Image || paint.type == .Pattern {
			// Map angle to UV coordinates (0-1 range around the circle)
			u1 := 0.5 + 0.5 * math.cos(t1)
			v1 := 0.5 + 0.5 * math.sin(t1)
			u2 := 0.5 + 0.5 * math.cos(t2)
			v2 := 0.5 + 0.5 * math.sin(t2)

			add_vertices(
				b,
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{center, {0.5, 0.5}, c_center},
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{p1, {u1, v1}, c1},
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{p2, {u2, v2}, c2},
			)
		} else {
			// For non-image patterns, use simple UV coordinates
			add_vertices(
				b,
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{center, {0.5, 0.5}, c_center},
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{p1, {0, 0}, c1},
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{p2, {0, 0}, c2},
			)
		}
	}
}

g2d_draw_ngon_immediate :: proc(
	x, y, radius, thickness: f32,
	sides: int,
	color: [4]f32,
	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	if sides < 3 do return
	g2d_path_begin()
	for i in 0 ..= sides {
		angle := f32(i) * 2.0 * math.PI / f32(sides) - math.PI / 2.0
		px := x + math.cos(angle) * radius
		py := y + math.sin(angle) * radius
		if i == 0 {
			g2d_path_move_to(px, py)
		} else {
			g2d_path_line_to(px, py)
		}
	}
	g2d_path_draw(thickness, color)
}
g2d_draw_polyline_immediate :: proc(
	points: [][2]f32,
	thickness: f32,
	color: [4]f32,
	lineCap: renderer.Line_Cap = .Butt,
) {
	if len(points) < 2 do return
	g2d_path_begin()
	g2d_path_move_to(points[0].x, points[0].y)
	for i in 1 ..< len(points) {
		g2d_path_line_to(points[i].x, points[i].y)
	}
	g2d_path_draw(thickness, color, .Miter, lineCap)
}

g2d_fill_polygon_immediate :: proc(
	points: [][2]f32,
	paint: renderer.Paint,
	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	if len(points) < 3 do return
	g2d_path_begin()
	g2d_path_move_to(points[0].x, points[0].y)
	for i in 1 ..< len(points) {
		g2d_path_line_to(points[i].x, points[i].y)
	}
	g2d_path_close()
	g2d_path_fill(paint)
}

g2d_draw_polygon_immediate :: proc(
	points: [][2]f32,
	thickness: f32,
	color: [4]f32,
	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	if len(points) < 3 do return
	g2d_path_begin()
	g2d_path_move_to(points[0].x, points[0].y)
	for i in 1 ..< len(points) {
		g2d_path_line_to(points[i].x, points[i].y)
	}
	g2d_path_close()
	g2d_path_draw(thickness, color)
}

g2d_fill_bezier_quadratic_immediate :: proc(x1, y1, cx, cy, x2, y2: f32, paint: renderer.Paint) {
	g2d_path_begin()
	g2d_path_move_to(x1, y1)
	g2d_path_quad_to(cx, cy, x2, y2)
	g2d_path_close()
	g2d_path_fill_immediate(paint)
}

g2d_fill_bezier_cubic_immediate :: proc(
	x1, y1, c1x, c1y, c2x, c2y, x2, y2: f32,
	paint: renderer.Paint,
) {
	g2d_path_begin()
	g2d_path_move_to(x1, y1)
	g2d_path_cubic_to(c1x, c1y, c2x, c2y, x2, y2)
	g2d_path_close()
	g2d_path_fill_immediate(paint)
}


g2d_draw_bezier_cubic_immediate :: proc(
	x1, y1, c1x, c1y, c2x, c2y, x2, y2, thickness: f32,
	color: [4]f32,
) {
	g2d_path_begin()
	g2d_path_move_to(x1, y1)
	g2d_path_cubic_to(c1x, c1y, c2x, c2y, x2, y2)
	g2d_path_draw_immediate(thickness, color)
}


g2d_fill_pie_immediate :: proc(
	x, y, radius, start_angle, end_angle: f32,
	paint: renderer.Paint,
	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	ctx.curr_image = ctx.white_image
	b := get_batch()
	c := resolve_paint_color(paint)

	center := apply_transform(x, y)
	segments := 32
	delta := end_angle - start_angle

	for i in 0 ..< segments {
		t1 := start_angle + (f32(i) / f32(segments)) * delta
		t2 := start_angle + (f32(i + 1) / f32(segments)) * delta

		p1 := apply_transform(x + radius * math.cos(t1), y + radius * math.sin(t1))
