@(private)
g2d_record_render_asset :: proc(
	handle: assets.RenderHandle,
	config: assets.Asset_Render_Config,
) -> bool {
	renderer.draw_list_push(
		&renderer.active_draw_list,
		renderer.Cmd_Draw_Asset {
			common = _g2d_make_common(.Content),
			handle = handle,
			config = config,
		},
	)
	return true
}

@(private)
_g2d_copy_paint :: proc(p: renderer.Paint) -> renderer.Paint {
	allocator := renderer.draw_list_allocator(&renderer.active_draw_list)
	res := p
	if len(p.gradient_colors) > 0 {
		c := make([][4]f32, len(p.gradient_colors), allocator)
		copy(c, p.gradient_colors[:])
		res.gradient_colors = c
	}
	if len(p.gradient_stops) > 0 {
		s := make([]f32, len(p.gradient_stops), allocator)
		copy(s, p.gradient_stops[:])
		res.gradient_stops = s
	}
	return res
}

@(private)
_g2d_copy_stroke :: proc(s: renderer.Stroke) -> renderer.Stroke {
	allocator := renderer.draw_list_allocator(&renderer.active_draw_list)
	res := s
	res.paint = _g2d_copy_paint(s.paint)
	if s.has_dash_paint {
		res.dash_paint = _g2d_copy_paint(s.dash_paint)
	}
	if len(s.dash_array) > 0 {
		d := make([]f32, len(s.dash_array), allocator)
		copy(d, s.dash_array[:])
		res.dash_array = d
	}
	return res
}

@(private)
_g2d_copy_string :: proc(s: string) -> string {
	if len(s) == 0 do return ""
	allocator := renderer.draw_list_allocator(&renderer.active_draw_list)
	res := make([]u8, len(s), allocator)
	copy(res, s)
	return string(res)
}

@(private)
_g2d_copy_points :: proc(points: [][2]f32) -> [][2]f32 {
	if len(points) == 0 do return nil
	allocator := renderer.draw_list_allocator(&renderer.active_draw_list)
	res := make([][2]f32, len(points), allocator)
	copy(res, points)
	return res
}

@(private)
g2d_record_path_fill :: proc(paint: renderer.Paint) {
	// Copy current path points using draw list arena
	allocator := renderer.draw_list_allocator(&renderer.active_draw_list)
	points := make([]renderer.Path_Point, len(ctx.current_path), allocator)
	copy(points, ctx.current_path[:])

	renderer.draw_list_push(
		&renderer.active_draw_list,
		renderer.Cmd_Path_Fill {
			common = _g2d_make_common(.Content),
			points = points,
			paint = _g2d_copy_paint(paint),
		},
	)
}

@(private)
g2d_record_path_draw :: proc(
	thickness: f32,
	color: [4]f32,
	join: renderer.Line_Join = .Miter,
	cap: renderer.Line_Cap = .Butt,
) {
	// Copy current path points using draw list arena
	allocator := renderer.draw_list_allocator(&renderer.active_draw_list)
	points := make([]renderer.Path_Point, len(ctx.current_path), allocator)
	copy(points, ctx.current_path[:])

	renderer.draw_list_push(
		&renderer.active_draw_list,
		renderer.Cmd_Path_Draw {
			common = _g2d_make_common(.Content),
			points = points,
			thickness = thickness,
			color = color,
			join = join,
			cap = cap,
		},
	)
}

@(private)
g2d_record_path_stroke :: proc(stroke: renderer.Stroke) {
	// Copy current path points using draw list arena
	allocator := renderer.draw_list_allocator(&renderer.active_draw_list)
	points := make([]renderer.Path_Point, len(ctx.current_path), allocator)
	copy(points, ctx.current_path[:])

	renderer.draw_list_push(
		&renderer.active_draw_list,
		renderer.Cmd_Path_Stroke {
			common = _g2d_make_common(.Content),
			points = points,
			stroke = _g2d_copy_stroke(stroke),
		},
	)
}

// --- REPLAY CORE ---
g2d_record_begin_mask :: proc() {
	renderer.draw_list_push(&renderer.active_draw_list, renderer.Cmd_Begin_Mask{common = _g2d_make_common(.Content)})
	// Phase 12 Fix: Still update the recording state so subsequent commands capture correct implicit state
	g2d_begin_mask()
}

g2d_record_end_mask :: proc() {
	renderer.draw_list_push(&renderer.active_draw_list, renderer.Cmd_End_Mask{common = _g2d_make_common(.Content)})
	g2d_end_mask()
}

g2d_record_begin_masked_draw :: proc() {
	renderer.draw_list_push(&renderer.active_draw_list, renderer.Cmd_Begin_Masked_Draw{common = _g2d_make_common(.Content)})
	g2d_begin_masked_draw()
}

g2d_record_end_masked_draw :: proc() {
	renderer.draw_list_push(&renderer.active_draw_list, renderer.Cmd_End_Masked_Draw{common = _g2d_make_common(.Content)})
	g2d_end_masked_draw()
}

g2d_record_apply_box_blur :: proc(source, output, temp: ^renderer.Render_Target, radius: f32) {
	renderer.draw_list_push(&renderer.active_draw_list, renderer.Cmd_Apply_Box_Blur{
		common = _g2d_make_common(.Content),
		source = source, output = output, temp = temp,
		radius = radius,
	})
}

g2d_record_apply_bloom :: proc(source, output, temp: ^renderer.Render_Target, threshold, intensity: f32) {
	renderer.draw_list_push(&renderer.active_draw_list, renderer.Cmd_Apply_Bloom{
		common = _g2d_make_common(.Content),
		source = source, output = output, temp = temp,
		threshold = threshold, intensity = intensity,
	})
}

g2d_record_apply_vignette :: proc(source, output: ^renderer.Render_Target, radius, softness: f32) {
	renderer.draw_list_push(&renderer.active_draw_list, renderer.Cmd_Apply_Vignette{
		common = _g2d_make_common(.Content),
		source = source, output = output,
		radius = radius, softness = softness,
	})
}

@(private)
g2d_draw_list_flush_layer :: proc(dl: ^renderer.Draw_List, layer: renderer.Draw_Layer, target_filter: rawptr = nil) {
	if dl == nil do return

	old_rt := ctx.active_rt
	g2d_set_render_target(cast(^renderer.Render_Target)target_filter)
	defer g2d_set_render_target(old_rt) // Ensures pipeline sets are updated correctly

	// Set active renderer to immediate for the duration of the flush
	// This ensures any nested drawing (like rendering an asset) happens immediately
	old_renderer := renderer.active_renderer
	renderer.active_renderer = &immediate_renderer
	defer renderer.active_renderer = old_renderer

	for cmd_idx in dl.layers[layer] {
		cmd := &dl.commands[cmd_idx]
		common := renderer.draw_command_get_common(cmd)
		if common == nil || common.flushed || common.target != target_filter do continue

		// Gate 1.6: Scroll culling — skip commands clipped to a zero-area scissor rect.
		// When a widget is scrolled fully out of a scroll container, Clay intersects its
		// scissor rect to zero (or negative) dimensions.  No vertices need to be generated.
		if common.scissor_on && (common.scissor[2] <= 0 || common.scissor[3] <= 0) {
			common.flushed = true
			continue
		}

		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.debug(
					.G2D,
					fmt.tprintf("[FLUSH_CMD] layer=%v target=%p", layer, common.target),
				)
			}
			common.flushed = true

			// --- STATELESS DEFERRED REPLAY ---
			// Apply the recorded state for this specific command directly to the context
			ctx.curr_matrix = common.transform
			ctx.curr_opacity = common.opacity
			ctx.curr_scissor_on = common.scissor_on
			if common.scissor_on {
				ctx.curr_scissor = common.scissor
			}
			ctx.stencil_clip_level = common.stencil_level
			ctx.curr_pip_variant = G2D_Pipeline_Variant(common.pip_variant)
			switch c in cmd^ {
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Render_Target:
				g2d_draw_render_target(c.rt, c.x, c.y, c.w, c.h, c.color)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Text_Layout:
				g2d_draw_text_layout_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.lines, c.y_offsets, c.color, c.mods)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Text_On_Path:
				old_path := ctx.current_path
				ctx.current_path = make([dynamic]renderer.Path_Point, len(c.points), context.temp_allocator)
				copy(ctx.current_path[:], c.points)
				g2d_draw_text_on_path(c.text, c.font, c.color)
				ctx.current_path = old_path
			case renderer.Cmd_Fill_Rect:
				g2d_fill_rect_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.w, c.h, c.paint)
			case renderer.Cmd_Fill_Rect_Rounded:
				g2d_fill_rect_rounded_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.w, c.h, c.radius, c.paint)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Rect:
				g2d_draw_rect_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.w, c.h, c.thickness, c.color)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Rect_Rounded:
				g2d_draw_rect_rounded_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.w, c.h, c.radius, c.thickness, c.color)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Line:
				g2d_draw_line_immediate(c.x1, c.y1, c.x2, c.y2, c.thickness, c.color, c.cap)
			case renderer.Cmd_Fill_Circle:
				g2d_fill_circle_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.radius, c.paint)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Circle:
				g2d_draw_circle_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.radius, c.thickness, c.color)
			case renderer.Cmd_Fill_Ellipse:
				g2d_fill_ellipse_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.rx, c.ry, c.paint)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Ellipse:
				g2d_draw_ellipse_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.rx, c.ry, c.thickness, c.color)
			case renderer.Cmd_Fill_Triangle:
				g2d_fill_triangle_immediate(c.x1, c.y1, c.x2, c.y2, c.x3, c.y3, c.paint)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Triangle:
				g2d_draw_triangle_immediate(c.x1, c.y1, c.x2, c.y2, c.x3, c.y3, c.thickness, c.color)
			case renderer.Cmd_Fill_Polygon:
				g2d_fill_polygon_immediate(c.points, c.paint)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Polygon:
				g2d_draw_polygon_immediate(c.points, c.thickness, c.color)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Polyline:
				g2d_draw_polyline_immediate(c.points, c.thickness, c.color, c.lineCap)
			case renderer.Cmd_Fill_Pie:
				g2d_fill_pie_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.radius, c.start_angle, c.end_angle, c.paint)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Arc:
				g2d_draw_arc_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.radius, c.start_angle, c.end_angle, c.thickness, c.color, c.cap)
			case renderer.Cmd_Fill_Arc:
				g2d_fill_arc_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.radius, c.start_angle, c.end_angle, c.paint)
			case renderer.Cmd_Fill_Star:
				g2d_fill_star_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.num_points, c.inner_radius, c.outer_radius, c.paint)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Star:
				g2d_draw_star_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.num_points, c.inner_radius, c.outer_radius, c.thickness, c.color)
			case renderer.Cmd_Fill_Ring:
				g2d_fill_ring_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.inner_radius, c.outer_radius, c.paint)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Ring:
				g2d_draw_ring_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.inner_radius, c.inner_thickness, c.inner_color, c.outer_radius, c.outer_thickness, c.outer_color)
			case renderer.Cmd_Fill_Wedge:
				g2d_fill_wedge_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.radius, c.start_angle, c.end_angle, c.paint, c.clockwise)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Wedge:
				g2d_draw_wedge_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.radius, c.thickness, c.start_angle, c.end_angle, c.color, c.clockwise)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Point:
				g2d_draw_point_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.color)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Point_Scale:
				g2d_draw_point_scale_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.color)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Text:
				g2d_draw_text_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.text, c.font, c.color, c.mods)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Text_Advanced:
				fallback_arr := c.fallbacks
				fbs := fallback_arr[:c.n_fallbacks]
				g2d_draw_text_advanced_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.text, c.font, fbs, c.color, c.mods)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Image:
				g2d_draw_image_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.w, c.h, cast(sg.Image)c.img, c.color)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Image_Rect:
				g2d_draw_image_rect_immediate(c.dest, c.src, cast(sg.Image)c.img, c.color)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Image_Rect_UV_Mat:
				// Ensure sampling is set before drawing if it differs from default
				g2d_set_sampling(c.filter, c.repeat, c.mipmap)
				g2d_draw_image_rect_uv_mat(c.dest, c.uvs, c.mat)
				g2d_reset_sampling()
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Asset:
				g2d_render_asset_immediate(c.handle, c.config)
			case renderer.Cmd_Fill_Bezier_Quadratic:
				g2d_fill_bezier_quadratic_immediate(c.x1, c.y1, c.cx, c.cy, c.x2, c.y2, c.paint)
			case renderer.Cmd_Fill_Bezier_Cubic:
				g2d_fill_bezier_cubic_immediate(c.x1, c.y1, c.c1x, c.c1y, c.c2x, c.c2y, c.x2, c.y2, c.paint)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Bezier_Quadratic:
				g2d_draw_bezier_quadratic_immediate(c.x1, c.y1, c.cx, c.cy, c.x2, c.y2, c.thickness, c.color)
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Bezier_Cubic:
				g2d_draw_bezier_cubic_immediate(c.x1, c.y1, c.c1x, c.c1y, c.c2x, c.c2y, c.x2, c.y2, c.thickness, c.color)
			case renderer.Cmd_Path_Fill:
				old_path := ctx.current_path
				ctx.current_path = make([dynamic]renderer.Path_Point, len(c.points), context.temp_allocator)
				copy(ctx.current_path[:], c.points)
				g2d_path_fill_immediate(c.paint)
				ctx.current_path = old_path
			case renderer.Cmd_Path_Draw:
				old_path := ctx.current_path
				ctx.current_path = make([dynamic]renderer.Path_Point, len(c.points), context.temp_allocator)
				copy(ctx.current_path[:], c.points)
				g2d_path_draw_immediate(c.thickness, c.color, c.join, c.cap)
				ctx.current_path = old_path
			case renderer.Cmd_Path_Stroke:
				old_path := ctx.current_path
				ctx.current_path = make([dynamic]renderer.Path_Point, len(c.points), context.temp_allocator)
				copy(ctx.current_path[:], c.points)
				g2d_path_stroke_immediate(c.stroke)
				ctx.current_path = old_path
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Ngon:
				g2d_draw_ngon_immediate(c.cx, c.cy, c.radius, 1.0, c.sides, c.color)
			case renderer.Cmd_Stroke_Gradient:
				g2d_stroke_gradient_immediate(c.points, c.paint, c.width, c.options)
			case renderer.Cmd_Shadow_Rect:
				g2d_drop_shadow_rect_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.w, c.h, c.radius, c.offset_x, c.offset_y, c.blur, c.color)
			case renderer.Cmd_Shadow_Circle:
				g2d_drop_shadow_circle_immediate(c.cx, c.cy, c.radius, c.offset_x, c.offset_y, c.blur, c.color)
			case renderer.Cmd_Shadow_Ngon:
				g2d_drop_shadow_ngon_immediate(c.cx, c.cy, c.radius, c.sides, c.offset_x, c.offset_y, c.blur, c.color)
			case renderer.Cmd_Inset_Shadow_Rect:
				g2d_inset_shadow_rect_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.w, c.h, c.top_depth, c.side_depth, c.color)
			case renderer.Cmd_Begin_Mask:
				g2d_begin_mask()
			case renderer.Cmd_End_Mask:
				g2d_end_mask()
			case renderer.Cmd_Begin_Masked_Draw:
				g2d_begin_masked_draw()
			case renderer.Cmd_End_Masked_Draw:
				g2d_end_masked_draw()
			case renderer.Cmd_Apply_Box_Blur, renderer.Cmd_Apply_Bloom, renderer.Cmd_Apply_Vignette:
				// Skip post-effects in the main flush pass; they are replayed at the end of pre-pass
				// to ensure offscreen geometry is fully rendered first.
				continue
			case renderer.Cmd_GPU_Callback:
				// Gate 3.4: invoke user-supplied sokol_gfx callback at the correct layer stream point.
				if c.callback != nil {
					c.callback(c.userdata)
				}
			case renderer.Cmd_Draw_Particles_Instanced:
				// Gate 4.1: upload instance data; push a sentinel batch for the render pass.
				if len(c.instances) > 0 {
					n   := len(c.instances)
					bsz := uint(n * size_of(renderer.Particle_Instance))
					off := i32(sg.append_buffer(ctx.particle_inst_buf, sg.Range{ptr = &c.instances[0], size = bsz}))
					use_add := c.blend_mode == .Add
					pip: sg.Pipeline
					if ctx.active_rt == nil {
						pip = ctx.particle_pip_add_screen if use_add else ctx.particle_pip_screen
					} else {
						pip = ctx.particle_pip_add_offscr if use_add else ctx.particle_pip_offscr
					}
					append(&ctx.batches, G2D_Batch{
						target          = ctx.active_rt,
						pipeline        = pip,
						scissor_on      = common.scissor_on,
						scissor         = common.scissor,
						is_instanced    = true,
						instance_count  = i32(n),
						inst_buf_offset = off,
					})
				}
			// Gate 3.2: saveLayer — redirect subsequent commands to a new offscreen RT.
			case renderer.Cmd_Push_Layer:
				rt_w := i32(f32(c.w) * ctx.dpi_scale + 0.5)
				rt_h := i32(f32(c.h) * ctx.dpi_scale + 0.5)
				pool_idx := _g2d_layer_rt_acquire(rt_w, rt_h)
				if pool_idx >= 0 {
					entry := Layer_Stack_Entry {
						pool_idx   = pool_idx,
						parent_rt  = ctx.active_rt,
						opacity    = c.opacity,
						blend_mode = c.blend_mode,
						x          = c.x,
						y          = c.y,
						w          = c.w,
						h          = c.h,
					}
					append(&ctx.layer_stack, entry)
					ctx.active_rt = &ctx.layer_rt_pool[pool_idx].rt
					// Layer RTs are always no-depth; activate matching pipeline set so
					// get_batch() records the correct pipeline for this target.
					// Coordinate offset is already baked into each command's transform
					// at recording time by g2d_push_layer.
					pipeline_mgr_activate(&ctx.pipeline_mgr, .OffscreenNoDepth)
				}
			// Gate 3.2: popLayer — composite the layer RT back onto the parent.
			case renderer.Cmd_Pop_Layer:
				if len(ctx.layer_stack) > 0 {
					top := pop(&ctx.layer_stack)
					layer_rt := &ctx.layer_rt_pool[top.pool_idx].rt
					// Restore active_rt to parent and update pipeline to match.
					// ctx.curr_matrix is already set to the parent matrix by the stateless
					// replay (Cmd_Pop_Layer.common.transform was recorded after matrix restore).
					ctx.active_rt = top.parent_rt
					_g2d_activate_pipeline_for_rt(top.parent_rt)
					// Composite: blit layer RT to parent using the recorded opacity/blend_mode
					old_variant := ctx.curr_pip_variant
					if top.blend_mode == .Add {
						ctx.curr_pip_variant = .BlendAdd
					} else if top.blend_mode == .Multiply {
						ctx.curr_pip_variant = .BlendMultiply
					} else if top.blend_mode == .Screen {
						ctx.curr_pip_variant = .BlendScreen
					}
					composite_color := [4]f32{1, 1, 1, top.opacity}
					g2d_draw_render_target(layer_rt, top.x, top.y, top.w, top.h, composite_color)
					ctx.curr_pip_variant = old_variant
				}
			// Gate 3.3: setFilter — redirect subsequent commands to a new offscreen RT.
			case renderer.Cmd_Set_Filter:
				rt_w := i32(f32(c.w) * ctx.dpi_scale + 0.5)
				rt_h := i32(f32(c.h) * ctx.dpi_scale + 0.5)
				pool_idx := _g2d_layer_rt_acquire(rt_w, rt_h)
				if pool_idx >= 0 {
					entry := Filter_Stack_Entry {
						pool_idx  = pool_idx,
						parent_rt = ctx.active_rt,
						filter    = c.filter,
						x         = c.x,
						y         = c.y,
						w         = c.w,
						h         = c.h,
					}
					append(&ctx.filter_stack, entry)
					ctx.active_rt = &ctx.layer_rt_pool[pool_idx].rt
					// Coordinate offset is already baked into each command's transform
					// at recording time by g2d_set_filter.
					// Filter RTs are always no-depth; activate matching pipeline set.
					pipeline_mgr_activate(&ctx.pipeline_mgr, .OffscreenNoDepth)
				}
			// Gate 3.3: clearFilter — restore parent RT, enqueue composite-back, save pending filter.
			case renderer.Cmd_Clear_Filter:
				if len(ctx.filter_stack) > 0 {
					top := pop(&ctx.filter_stack)
					filter_rt := &ctx.layer_rt_pool[top.pool_idx].rt
					ctx.active_rt = top.parent_rt
					// ctx.curr_matrix is already set to the parent matrix by the stateless
					// replay (Cmd_Clear_Filter.common.transform was recorded after matrix restore).
					// Restore pipeline to match parent RT depth capabilities.
					_g2d_activate_pipeline_for_rt(top.parent_rt)

					pending := Pending_Filter_Entry {
						filter          = top.filter,
						filter_pool_idx = top.pool_idx,
						temp_pool_idx   = -1,
						output_pool_idx = -1,
						shadow_pool_idx = -1,
						x               = top.x,
						y               = top.y,
						w               = top.w,
						h               = top.h,
					}

					// Temp/output/shadow RTs must match the filter RT's physical dimensions.
					phys_w := i32(f32(top.w) * ctx.dpi_scale + 0.5)
					phys_h := i32(f32(top.h) * ctx.dpi_scale + 0.5)

					switch f in top.filter {
					case renderer.Filter_Blur:
						temp_idx := _g2d_layer_rt_acquire(phys_w, phys_h)
						if temp_idx >= 0 {
							pending.temp_pool_idx = temp_idx
							// In-place blur: composite back the (soon-to-be-blurred) filter_rt
							g2d_draw_render_target(filter_rt, top.x, top.y, top.w, top.h, {1, 1, 1, 1})
						}

					case renderer.Filter_Vignette:
						temp_idx := _g2d_layer_rt_acquire(phys_w, phys_h)
						if temp_idx >= 0 {
							pending.output_pool_idx = temp_idx
							// Composite back the vignette output RT
							output_rt := &ctx.layer_rt_pool[temp_idx].rt
							g2d_draw_render_target(output_rt, top.x, top.y, top.w, top.h, {1, 1, 1, 1})
						}

					case renderer.Filter_Bloom:
						output_idx  := _g2d_layer_rt_acquire(phys_w, phys_h)
						scratch_idx := _g2d_layer_rt_acquire(phys_w, phys_h)
						if output_idx >= 0 && scratch_idx >= 0 {
							pending.output_pool_idx = output_idx
							pending.temp_pool_idx   = scratch_idx
							output_rt := &ctx.layer_rt_pool[output_idx].rt
							g2d_draw_render_target(output_rt, top.x, top.y, top.w, top.h, {1, 1, 1, 1})
						}

					case renderer.Filter_Drop_Shadow:
						shadow_idx := _g2d_layer_rt_acquire(phys_w, phys_h)
						temp_idx   := _g2d_layer_rt_acquire(phys_w, phys_h)
						if shadow_idx >= 0 && temp_idx >= 0 {
							pending.shadow_pool_idx = shadow_idx
							pending.temp_pool_idx   = temp_idx
							shadow_rt := &ctx.layer_rt_pool[shadow_idx].rt
							// Shadow layer at offset (rendered before original)
							g2d_draw_render_target(shadow_rt, top.x + f.offset_x, top.y + f.offset_y, top.w, top.h, f.color)
							// Original content on top
							g2d_draw_render_target(filter_rt, top.x, top.y, top.w, top.h, {1, 1, 1, 1})
						}
					}

					append(&ctx.pending_filters, pending)
				}
			case renderer.Cmd_Begin_Pass:
				g2d_begin_pass_immediate(c.pass, _g2d_convert_pass_type(c.pass_type))
			case renderer.Cmd_End_Pass:
				g2d_end_pass_immediate()
			case renderer.Cmd_Set_Clip:
				g2d_set_clip_immediate(c.x, c.y, c.w, c.h)
			case renderer.Cmd_Reset_Clip:
				g2d_reset_clip_immediate()
			case renderer.Cmd_Push_Matrix:
				g2d_push_matrix_immediate()
			case renderer.Cmd_Pop_Matrix:
				g2d_pop_matrix_immediate()
			case renderer.Cmd_Identity_Matrix:
				g2d_identity_matrix_immediate()
			case renderer.Cmd_Translate:
				g2d_translate_immediate(c.dx, c.dy)
			case renderer.Cmd_Rotate:
				g2d_rotate_immediate(c.angle)
			case renderer.Cmd_Scale:
				g2d_scale_immediate(c.sx, c.sy)
			case renderer.Cmd_Apply_Matrix:
				g2d_apply_matrix_immediate(c.m)
			case renderer.Cmd_Apply_Matrix4:
				g2d_apply_matrix4_immediate(c.m)
			case renderer.Cmd_Shadow_Silhouette:
				// Look up the shadow cache entry using the stable key
				entry, ok := ctx.shadow_cache[_shadow_key_hash(c.key)]
				if !ok {
					when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
						logging.debug(.G2D, "[SHADOW_SILHOUETTE] Cache entry not found for key")
					}
					break // Skip this command if entry not found
				}

				// Store current state
				old_matrix := ctx.curr_matrix
				old_scissor_on := ctx.curr_scissor_on
				old_scissor := ctx.curr_scissor
				old_opacity := ctx.curr_opacity

				// Setup minimal state for silhouette rendering
				ctx.curr_matrix = linalg.MATRIX3F32_IDENTITY
				ctx.curr_opacity = 1.0
				ctx.curr_scissor_on = true
				ctx.curr_scissor = [4]f32 {
					0,
					0,
					f32(entry.rt_shadow.width),
					f32(entry.rt_shadow.height),
				}

				// Direct vertex generation - bypass batching for performance
				b := get_batch()
				color := resolve_color(c.color)
				r := math.min(c.radius, math.min(c.w, c.h) * 0.5)

				if r <= 0 {
					// Simple rectangle for zero radius
					p1 := apply_transform(c.x, c.y)
					p2 := apply_transform(c.x + c.w, c.y)
					p3 := apply_transform(c.x + c.w, c.y + c.h)
					p4 := apply_transform(c.x, c.y + c.h)

					add_vertices(
						b,
						renderer.G2D_Vertex{p1, {0, 0}, color},
						renderer.G2D_Vertex{p2, {1, 0}, color},
						renderer.G2D_Vertex{p3, {1, 1}, color},
						renderer.G2D_Vertex{p1, {0, 0}, color},
						renderer.G2D_Vertex{p3, {1, 1}, color},
						renderer.G2D_Vertex{p4, {0, 1}, color},
					)
				} else {
					// Rounded rectangle - optimized vertex generation
					segments_per_corner := 4 // Reduced for performance
					center_x, center_y := c.x + c.w * 0.5, c.y + c.h * 0.5
					center_pos := apply_transform(center_x, center_y)

					// Four corners
					corners := [4][2]f32 {
						{c.x + c.w - r, c.y + r}, // TR
						{c.x + c.w - r, c.y + c.h - r}, // BR
						{c.x + r, c.y + c.h - r}, // BL
						{c.x + r, c.y + r}, // TL
					}
					start_angles := [4]f32{1.5 * math.PI, 0, 0.5 * math.PI, math.PI}

					for i in 0 ..< 4 {
						ccx, ccy := corners[i].x, corners[i].y
						sa := start_angles[i]

						for j in 0 ..< segments_per_corner {
							t1 := sa + (f32(j) / f32(segments_per_corner)) * (math.PI * 0.5)
							t2 := sa + (f32(j + 1) / f32(segments_per_corner)) * (math.PI * 0.5)

							px1 := ccx + math.cos(t1) * r
							py1 := ccy + math.sin(t1) * r
							px2 := ccx + math.cos(t2) * r
							py2 := ccy + math.sin(t2) * r

							p1 := apply_transform(px1, py1)
							p2 := apply_transform(px2, py2)

							add_vertices(
								b,
								renderer.G2D_Vertex{center_pos, {0.5, 0.5}, color},
								renderer.G2D_Vertex{p1, {0, 0}, color},
								renderer.G2D_Vertex{p2, {0, 0}, color},
							)
						}
					}
				}

				// Restore state
				ctx.curr_matrix = old_matrix
				ctx.curr_opacity = old_opacity
				ctx.curr_scissor_on = old_scissor_on
				ctx.curr_scissor = old_scissor
			}
		}
}

// --- Public API Wrappers (point to recording functions for deferred rendering) ---

g2d_push_matrix :: g2d_record_push_matrix
g2d_pop_matrix :: g2d_record_pop_matrix
g2d_translate :: g2d_record_translate
g2d_rotate :: g2d_record_rotate
g2d_scale :: g2d_record_scale
g2d_fill_rect :: g2d_record_fill_rect
g2d_draw_rect :: g2d_record_draw_rect
g2d_draw_line :: g2d_record_draw_line
g2d_path_fill :: g2d_record_path_fill
g2d_path_draw :: g2d_record_path_draw
g2d_path_stroke :: g2d_record_path_stroke
g2d_draw_image :: proc(x, y, w, h: f32, img: any, color: [4]f32 = {1, 1, 1, 1}) {
	handle: renderer.Image_Handle
	switch i in img {
	case sg.Image:
		handle = transmute(renderer.Image_Handle)i
	case renderer.Image_Handle:
		handle = i
	case:
		return
	}
	g2d_record_draw_image(x, y, w, h, handle, color)
}
g2d_fill_circle :: g2d_record_fill_circle
g2d_draw_circle :: g2d_record_draw_circle
g2d_fill_rect_rounded :: g2d_record_fill_rect_rounded
g2d_draw_rect_rounded :: g2d_record_draw_rect_rounded
g2d_drop_shadow_rect :: g2d_record_drop_shadow_rect
g2d_drop_shadow_circle :: g2d_record_drop_shadow_circle
g2d_drop_shadow_ngon :: g2d_record_drop_shadow_ngon
g2d_fill_ngon :: g2d_record_fill_ngon
g2d_draw_text :: proc(
	x, y: f32,
	text: string,
	font: renderer.Font_Handle = renderer.Font_Handle_Invalid,
	color: [4]f32 = {1, 1, 1, 1},
	mods: renderer.Text_Modifiers = {},
) {
	g2d_record_draw_text(x, y, text, font, color, mods)
}

g2d_begin_pass :: proc(
	pass: sg.Pass,
	type: renderer.Renderer_Pass_Type = renderer.Renderer_Pass_Type.Screen,
) {
	g2d_begin_pass_immediate(pass, _g2d_convert_pass_type(type))
}

g2d_end_pass :: proc() {
	g2d_end_pass_immediate()
}

g2d_draw_image_rect :: proc(
	dest, src: [4]f32,
	img: renderer.Image_Handle,
	color: [4]f32 = {1, 1, 1, 1},
) {
	g2d_record_draw_image_rect(dest, src, img, color)
}

renderAssetNew :: proc(handle: GPU_RenderHandle, config: GPU_Asset_Render_Config) -> bool {
	ah := assets.RenderHandle {
		id         = handle.id,
		asset_type = cast(assets.Asset_Type)handle.asset_type,
		timestamp  = handle.timestamp,
	}

	// Set the raw_parsed field based on asset type to preserve parsed data for deferred rendering
	switch handle.asset_type {
	case .SVG:
		ah.raw_parsed = rawptr(handle.svg_element)
	case .RSD:
		ah.raw_parsed = rawptr(handle.rsd_drawing)
	case .Image, .Font:
	// Not yet implemented - raw_parsed will remain nil
	}

	ac := assets.Asset_Render_Config {
		x                   = config.x,
		y                   = config.y,
		width               = config.width,
		height              = config.height,
		allow_sync_fallback = config.allow_sync_fallback,
		tint_color          = config.tint_color,
		opacity             = config.opacity,
		preserve_aspect     = config.preserve_aspect,
	}
	return g2d_record_render_asset(ah, ac)
}

g2d_set_clip :: g2d_record_set_clip
g2d_reset_clip :: g2d_record_reset_clip

package graphics

import "core:os"

rsd_write_file :: proc(path: string, data: []u8) -> bool {
	return os.write_entire_file(path, data) == nil
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Path Parser (Phase 10C.4)
- Robust tokenizer for SVG path data with proper float/whitespace/comma handling
- Supports all SVG 1.1 path commands: M, L, H, V, C, S, Q, T, A, Z (absolute and relative)
- Implements implicit command continuation (e.g., multiple coordinates after M = multiple line segments)
- Elliptical arc endpoint to center parameterization conversion (A command)
- No global state: all parsing is local to the parse function
- Conservative design with arena allocator for temporary buffers

Technical Details:
- SVG path data grammar: https://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/paths.html#PathDataBNF
- Arc conversion uses the algorithm from SVG 1.1 Appendix F.6
- Implicit command handling: M/m followed by extra pairs implies lineto commands
- Whitespace handling: commas and whitespace are interchangeable in most contexts
- Number format: supports integers, decimals, exponents (e.g., 1e-3, .5, -3.14)
*/

import "core:math"
import "core:mem"
import "core:strconv"
import "core:strings"

// ============================================================================
// Path Command Types
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: Path Command Type
- Represents the type of SVG path command
- Commands can be absolute (uppercase) or relative (lowercase)
*/
Path_Command_Type :: enum {
	Move_To, // M/m
	Line_To, // L/l
	Horizontal_Line_To, // H/h
	Vertical_Line_To, // V/v
	Cubic_Bezier, // C/c
	Cubic_Bezier_Smooth, // S/s
	Quadratic_Bezier, // Q/q
	Quadratic_Bezier_Smooth, // T/t
	Arc, // A/a
	Close_Path, // Z/z
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Point 2D
- Single point in path data with x,y coordinates
- Distinct from Path_Point in canvas_core.odin to avoid conflicts
*/
SVG_Point2D :: [2]f32

/**
@documentation: SVG Path Command
- Single parsed SVG path command with type and parameters
- Parameters are stored in command-specific structs via union
- Renamed from Path_Command to avoid conflict with RSD Path_Command enum
*/
SVG_Path_Command :: struct {
	type:     Path_Command_Type,
	absolute: bool, // true for uppercase (absolute), false for lowercase (relative)
	data:     Path_Command_Data,
}

/**
@documentation: Path Command Data Union
- Union containing parameters for each command type
*/
Path_Command_Data :: union {
	Move_To_Data,
	Line_To_Data,
	Horizontal_Line_To_Data,
	Vertical_Line_To_Data,
	Cubic_Bezier_Data,
	Cubic_Bezier_Smooth_Data,
	Quadratic_Bezier_Data,
	Quadratic_Bezier_Smooth_Data,
	Arc_Data,
	Close_Path_Data,
}

Move_To_Data :: struct {
	point: SVG_Point2D,
}

Line_To_Data :: struct {
	point: SVG_Point2D,
}

Horizontal_Line_To_Data :: struct {
	x: f32,
}

Vertical_Line_To_Data :: struct {
	y: f32,
}

Cubic_Bezier_Data :: struct {
	cp1: SVG_Point2D, // First control point
	cp2: SVG_Point2D, // Second control point
	end: SVG_Point2D, // End point
}

Cubic_Bezier_Smooth_Data :: struct {
	cp2: SVG_Point2D, // Second control point (first is implicit reflection)
	end: SVG_Point2D, // End point
}

Quadratic_Bezier_Data :: struct {
	cp:  SVG_Point2D, // Control point
	end: SVG_Point2D, // End point
}

Quadratic_Bezier_Smooth_Data :: struct {
	end: SVG_Point2D, // End point (control point is implicit reflection)
}

Arc_Data :: struct {
	rx:        f32, // X radius
	ry:        f32, // Y radius
	rotation:  f32, // X-axis rotation (degrees)
	large_arc: bool, // Large arc flag
	sweep:     bool, // Sweep flag
	end:       SVG_Point2D, // End point
}

Close_Path_Data :: struct {
	// No data needed for close path
}

// ============================================================================
// Parsed Path Structure
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: Parsed SVG Path
- Result of parsing an SVG path data string
- Commands are stored in a dynamic array
- Current point and previous control point are tracked for relative/smooth commands
*/
SVG_Parsed_Path :: struct {
	commands:      [dynamic]SVG_Path_Command,
	// Parser state (not part of output, used during parsing)
	current_point: SVG_Point2D,
	start_point:   SVG_Point2D, // For close path
	last_cp:       SVG_Point2D, // Last control point (for smooth curves)
	has_last_cp:   bool, // Whether last_cp is valid
}

// ============================================================================
// Path Tokenizer
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: Path Token Type
- Types of tokens in SVG path data
*/
Path_Token_Type :: enum {
	Command, // M, L, H, V, C, S, Q, T, A, Z (case insensitive for detection)
	Number, // Float value
	End, // End of input
}

/**
@documentation: Path Token
- Single token from the path data tokenizer
*/
Path_Token :: struct {
	type:    Path_Token_Type,
	command: u8, // For Command type: 'M', 'm', 'L', etc.
	value:   f32, // For Number type
}

/**
@documentation: Path Tokenizer State
- State for the path data tokenizer
*/
Path_Tokenizer :: struct {
	data: string,
	pos:  int,
}

/**
@documentation: Initialize Path Tokenizer
- Set up tokenizer with path data string
*/
@(private)
path_tokenizer_init :: proc(t: ^Path_Tokenizer, data: string) {
	t.data = data
	t.pos = 0
}

/**
@documentation: Skip Whitespace and Commas
- Advance position past whitespace and optional commas
- In SVG path data, commas and whitespace are interchangeable delimiters
*/
@(private)
path_skip_whitespace :: proc(t: ^Path_Tokenizer) {
	for t.pos < len(t.data) {
		c := t.data[t.pos]
		if c == ' ' || c == '\t' || c == '\n' || c == '\r' || c == ',' {
			t.pos += 1
		} else {
			break
		}
	}
}

/**
@documentation: Check if Character is Command Letter
- Returns true for valid SVG path command letters
*/
@(private)
is_path_command :: proc(c: u8) -> bool {
	switch c {
	case 'M',
	     'm',
	     'L',
	     'l',
	     'H',
	     'h',
	     'V',
	     'v',
	     'C',
	     'c',
	     'S',
	     's',
	     'Q',
	     'q',
	     'T',
	     't',
	     'A',
	     'a',
	     'Z',
	     'z':
		return true
	}
	return false
}

/**
@documentation: Check if Character is Sign
- Returns true for '+' or '-'
*/
@(private)
is_sign :: proc(c: u8) -> bool {
	return c == '+' || c == '-'
}

/**
@documentation: Check if Character is Digit
- Returns true for '0' through '9'
*/
@(private)
is_digit :: proc(c: u8) -> bool {
	return c >= '0' && c <= '9'
}

/**
@documentation: Get Next Path Token
- Returns the next token from the path data
- Handles implicit command continuation (returns previous command type when numbers follow without explicit command)
- Number format: supports integers, decimals, exponents
*/
@(private)
path_tokenizer_next :: proc(t: ^Path_Tokenizer, last_was_command: bool) -> Path_Token {
	path_skip_whitespace(t)

	if t.pos >= len(t.data) {
		return Path_Token{type = .End}
	}

	c := t.data[t.pos]

	// Check for command letter (only at start or after whitespace/comma)
	if is_path_command(c) {
		t.pos += 1
		return Path_Token{type = .Command, command = c}
	}

	// Must be a number
	// Number format: [sign] [digits] [. digits] [e|E [sign] digits]
	start_pos := t.pos

	// Optional sign
	if is_sign(c) {
		t.pos += 1
		if t.pos >= len(t.data) {
			// Just a sign with no number - invalid, but return 0
			return Path_Token{type = .Number, value = 0}
		}
		c = t.data[t.pos]
	}

	// Integer part
	for t.pos < len(t.data) && is_digit(t.data[t.pos]) {
		t.pos += 1
	}

	// Decimal part
	if t.pos < len(t.data) && t.data[t.pos] == '.' {
		t.pos += 1
		for t.pos < len(t.data) && is_digit(t.data[t.pos]) {
			t.pos += 1
		}
	}

	// Exponent part
	if t.pos < len(t.data) && (t.data[t.pos] == 'e' || t.data[t.pos] == 'E') {
		t.pos += 1
		if t.pos < len(t.data) && is_sign(t.data[t.pos]) {
			t.pos += 1
		}
		for t.pos < len(t.data) && is_digit(t.data[t.pos]) {
			t.pos += 1
		}
	}

	// Parse the number
	num_str := t.data[start_pos:t.pos]
	val, ok := strconv.parse_f64(num_str)
	if !ok {
		// Failed to parse - return 0 but advance to avoid infinite loop
		return Path_Token{type = .Number, value = 0}
	}

	return Path_Token{type = .Number, value = f32(val)}
}

// ============================================================================
// Path Parser
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: Parse SVG Path Data String
- Main entry point for parsing SVG path data
- Returns parsed path with all commands
- Handles implicit command continuation per SVG spec
*/
@(private)
svg_parse_path_data :: proc(data: string, allocator: mem.Allocator) -> SVG_Parsed_Path {
	path: SVG_Parsed_Path
	path.commands = make([dynamic]SVG_Path_Command, allocator)
	path.current_point = {0, 0}
	path.start_point = {0, 0}
	path.last_cp = {0, 0}
	path.has_last_cp = false

	if data == "" {
		return path
	}

	tokenizer: Path_Tokenizer
	path_tokenizer_init(&tokenizer, data)

	// Track current command type for implicit continuation
	current_cmd: u8 = 0
	implicit_mode := false

	for {
		// Capture position before parsing token so we can rewind if needed (for implicit commands)
		token_start_pos := tokenizer.pos
		token := path_tokenizer_next(&tokenizer, current_cmd == 0)

		if token.type == .End {
			break
		}

		if token.type == .Command {
			current_cmd = token.command
			implicit_mode = false

			// Parse based on command
			switch token.command {
			case 'M', 'm':
				if !parse_move_to(&tokenizer, &path, token.command == 'M') {
					continue
				}
				// After first M/m pair, subsequent pairs are treated as lineto
				current_cmd = 'L' if token.command == 'M' else 'l'
				implicit_mode = true

			case 'L', 'l':
				parse_line_to(&tokenizer, &path, token.command == 'L')
				implicit_mode = true

			case 'H', 'h':
				parse_horizontal_line_to(&tokenizer, &path, token.command == 'H')
				implicit_mode = true

			case 'V', 'v':
				parse_vertical_line_to(&tokenizer, &path, token.command == 'V')
				implicit_mode = true

			case 'C', 'c':
				parse_cubic_bezier(&tokenizer, &path, token.command == 'C')
				implicit_mode = true

			case 'S', 's':
				parse_cubic_bezier_smooth(&tokenizer, &path, token.command == 'S')
				implicit_mode = true

			case 'Q', 'q':
				parse_quadratic_bezier(&tokenizer, &path, token.command == 'Q')
				implicit_mode = true

			case 'T', 't':
				parse_quadratic_bezier_smooth(&tokenizer, &path, token.command == 'T')
				implicit_mode = true

			case 'A', 'a':
				parse_arc(&tokenizer, &path, token.command == 'A')
				implicit_mode = true

			case 'Z', 'z':
				parse_close_path(&path)
				// Z doesn't have implicit continuation
				implicit_mode = false
			}
		} else if token.type == .Number && implicit_mode {
			// Implicit command continuation - put the number back and continue with current command
			// We need to re-parse this number as part of the next command of the same type
			tokenizer.pos = token_start_pos // Rewind to start of number (including whitespace)

			switch current_cmd {
			case 'L', 'l':
				parse_line_to(&tokenizer, &path, current_cmd == 'L')
			case 'H', 'h':
				parse_horizontal_line_to(&tokenizer, &path, current_cmd == 'H')
			case 'V', 'v':
				parse_vertical_line_to(&tokenizer, &path, current_cmd == 'V')
			case 'C', 'c':
				parse_cubic_bezier(&tokenizer, &path, current_cmd == 'C')
			case 'S', 's':
				parse_cubic_bezier_smooth(&tokenizer, &path, current_cmd == 'S')
			case 'Q', 'q':
				parse_quadratic_bezier(&tokenizer, &path, current_cmd == 'Q')
			case 'T', 't':
				parse_quadratic_bezier_smooth(&tokenizer, &path, current_cmd == 'T')
			case 'A', 'a':
				parse_arc(&tokenizer, &path, current_cmd == 'A')
			}
		}
	}

	return path
}

/**
@documentation: Parse Move To Command (M/m)
- Parses coordinate pair(s) and moves current point
- First pair establishes new current point and start point for subpath
- Subsequent pairs are treated as implicit lineto commands
*/
@(private)
parse_move_to :: proc(t: ^Path_Tokenizer, path: ^SVG_Parsed_Path, absolute: bool) -> bool {
	// Parse first coordinate pair
	x := parse_number(t)
	y := parse_number(t)

	if absolute {
		path.current_point = {x, y}
	} else {
		path.current_point[0] += x
		path.current_point[1] += y
	}

	// Update start point for this subpath (used by close path)
	path.start_point = path.current_point

	cmd := SVG_Path_Command {
		type = .Move_To,
		absolute = absolute,
		data = Move_To_Data{point = path.current_point},
	}
	append(&path.commands, cmd)

	// Reset control point state for smooth curves
	path.has_last_cp = false

	return true
}

/**
@documentation: Parse Line To Command (L/l)
- Parses coordinate pair and draws line from current point to new point
*/
@(private)
parse_line_to :: proc(t: ^Path_Tokenizer, path: ^SVG_Parsed_Path, absolute: bool) {
	x := parse_number(t)
	y := parse_number(t)

	point: SVG_Point2D
	if absolute {
		point = {x, y}
	} else {
		point = {path.current_point[0] + x, path.current_point[1] + y}
	}

	cmd := SVG_Path_Command {
		type = .Line_To,
		absolute = absolute,
		data = Line_To_Data{point = point},
	}
	append(&path.commands, cmd)

	path.current_point = point
	path.has_last_cp = false
}

/**
@documentation: Parse Horizontal Line To Command (H/h)
- Parses x coordinate and draws horizontal line from current point
*/
@(private)
parse_horizontal_line_to :: proc(t: ^Path_Tokenizer, path: ^SVG_Parsed_Path, absolute: bool) {
	x := parse_number(t)

	new_x: f32
	if absolute {
		new_x = x
	} else {
		new_x = path.current_point[0] + x
	}

	cmd := SVG_Path_Command {
		type = .Horizontal_Line_To,
		absolute = absolute,
		data = Horizontal_Line_To_Data{x = new_x},
	}
	append(&path.commands, cmd)

	path.current_point[0] = new_x
	path.has_last_cp = false
}

/**
@documentation: Parse Vertical Line To Command (V/v)
- Parses y coordinate and draws vertical line from current point
*/
@(private)
parse_vertical_line_to :: proc(t: ^Path_Tokenizer, path: ^SVG_Parsed_Path, absolute: bool) {
	y := parse_number(t)

	new_y: f32
	if absolute {
		new_y = y
	} else {
		new_y = path.current_point[1] + y
	}

	cmd := SVG_Path_Command {
		type = .Vertical_Line_To,
		absolute = absolute,
		data = Vertical_Line_To_Data{y = new_y},
	}
	append(&path.commands, cmd)

	path.current_point[1] = new_y
	path.has_last_cp = false
}

/**
@documentation: Parse Cubic Bezier Command (C/c)
- Parses two control points and end point for cubic Bezier curve
*/
@(private)
parse_cubic_bezier :: proc(t: ^Path_Tokenizer, path: ^SVG_Parsed_Path, absolute: bool) {
	x1 := parse_number(t)
	y1 := parse_number(t)
	x2 := parse_number(t)
	y2 := parse_number(t)
	x := parse_number(t)
	y := parse_number(t)

	// Filter out degenerate curves (all zeros in relative mode, or all points equal)
	// This helps avoid rendering artifacts with some tessellators (div by zero in normal calc)
	if !absolute && x1 == 0 && y1 == 0 && x2 == 0 && y2 == 0 && x == 0 && y == 0 {
		return
	}

	cp1, cp2, end: SVG_Point2D
	if absolute {
		cp1 = {x1, y1}
		cp2 = {x2, y2}
		end = {x, y}
	} else {
		cx := path.current_point[0]
		cy := path.current_point[1]
		cp1 = {cx + x1, cy + y1}
		cp2 = {cx + x2, cy + y2}
		end = {cx + x, cy + y}
	}

	cmd := SVG_Path_Command {
		type = .Cubic_Bezier,
		absolute = absolute,
		data = Cubic_Bezier_Data{cp1 = cp1, cp2 = cp2, end = end},
	}
	append(&path.commands, cmd)

	// Save control point for smooth continuation
	path.last_cp = cp2
	path.has_last_cp = true
	path.current_point = end
}

/**
@documentation: Parse Smooth Cubic Bezier Command (S/s)
- Parses second control point and end point, first control point is reflection of previous
*/
@(private)
parse_cubic_bezier_smooth :: proc(t: ^Path_Tokenizer, path: ^SVG_Parsed_Path, absolute: bool) {
	x2 := parse_number(t)
	y2 := parse_number(t)
	x := parse_number(t)
	y := parse_number(t)

	// First control point is reflection of last control point
	cp1: SVG_Point2D
	if path.has_last_cp {
		// Reflect: cp1 = current_point + (current_point - last_cp)
		cp1[0] = 2 * path.current_point[0] - path.last_cp[0]
		cp1[1] = 2 * path.current_point[1] - path.last_cp[1]
	} else {
		// No previous control point, use current point
		cp1 = path.current_point
	}

	cp2, end: SVG_Point2D
	if absolute {
		cp2 = {x2, y2}
		end = {x, y}
	} else {
		cx := path.current_point[0]
		cy := path.current_point[1]
		cp2 = {cx + x2, cy + y2}
		end = {cx + x, cy + y}
	}

	cmd := SVG_Path_Command {
		type = .Cubic_Bezier_Smooth,
		absolute = absolute,
		data = Cubic_Bezier_Smooth_Data{cp2 = cp2, end = end},
	}
	append(&path.commands, cmd)

	// Save control point for smooth continuation
	path.last_cp = cp2
	path.has_last_cp = true
	path.current_point = end
}

/**
@documentation: Parse Quadratic Bezier Command (Q/q)
- Parses control point and end point for quadratic Bezier curve
*/
@(private)
parse_quadratic_bezier :: proc(t: ^Path_Tokenizer, path: ^SVG_Parsed_Path, absolute: bool) {
	x1 := parse_number(t)
	y1 := parse_number(t)
	x := parse_number(t)
	y := parse_number(t)

	cp, end: SVG_Point2D
	if absolute {
		cp = {x1, y1}
		end = {x, y}
	} else {
		cx := path.current_point[0]
		cy := path.current_point[1]
		cp = {cx + x1, cy + y1}
		end = {cx + x, cy + y}
	}

	cmd := SVG_Path_Command {
		type = .Quadratic_Bezier,
		absolute = absolute,
		data = Quadratic_Bezier_Data{cp = cp, end = end},
	}
	append(&path.commands, cmd)

	// Save control point for smooth continuation
	path.last_cp = cp
	path.has_last_cp = true
	path.current_point = end
}

/**
@documentation: Parse Smooth Quadratic Bezier Command (T/t)
- Parses end point, control point is reflection of previous
*/
@(private)
parse_quadratic_bezier_smooth :: proc(t: ^Path_Tokenizer, path: ^SVG_Parsed_Path, absolute: bool) {
	x := parse_number(t)
	y := parse_number(t)

	// Control point is reflection of last control point
	cp: SVG_Point2D
	if path.has_last_cp {
		cp[0] = 2 * path.current_point[0] - path.last_cp[0]
		cp[1] = 2 * path.current_point[1] - path.last_cp[1]
	} else {
		cp = path.current_point
	}

	end: SVG_Point2D
	if absolute {
		end = {x, y}
	} else {
		end = {path.current_point[0] + x, path.current_point[1] + y}
	}

	cmd := SVG_Path_Command {
		type = .Quadratic_Bezier_Smooth,
		absolute = absolute,
		data = Quadratic_Bezier_Smooth_Data{end = end},
	}
	append(&path.commands, cmd)

	// Save control point for smooth continuation
	path.last_cp = cp
	path.has_last_cp = true
	path.current_point = end
}

/**
@documentation: Parse Arc Command (A/a)
- Parses elliptical arc parameters and end point
- Arc is converted to center parameterization for easier rendering
*/
@(private)
parse_arc :: proc(t: ^Path_Tokenizer, path: ^SVG_Parsed_Path, absolute: bool) {
	rx := parse_number(t)
	ry := parse_number(t)
	rotation := parse_number(t)
	large_arc_flag := parse_number(t) != 0
	sweep_flag := parse_number(t) != 0
	x := parse_number(t)
	y := parse_number(t)

	end: SVG_Point2D
	if absolute {
		end = {x, y}
	} else {
		end = {path.current_point[0] + x, path.current_point[1] + y}
	}

	cmd := SVG_Path_Command {
		type = .Arc,
		absolute = absolute,
		data = Arc_Data {
			rx = rx,
			ry = ry,
			rotation = rotation,
			large_arc = large_arc_flag,
			sweep = sweep_flag,
			end = end,
		},
	}
	append(&path.commands, cmd)

	path.current_point = end
	path.has_last_cp = false
}

/**
@documentation: Parse Close Path Command (Z/z)
- Closes the current subpath by drawing line to start point
*/
@(private)
parse_close_path :: proc(path: ^SVG_Parsed_Path) {
	cmd := SVG_Path_Command {
		type     = .Close_Path,
		absolute = true,
		data     = Close_Path_Data{},
	}
	append(&path.commands, cmd)

	// Move current point to start of subpath
	path.current_point = path.start_point
	path.has_last_cp = false
}

/**
@documentation: Parse Number from Tokenizer
- Parses a number, skipping whitespace first
- Returns 0 if no number is found
*/
@(private)
parse_number :: proc(t: ^Path_Tokenizer) -> f32 {
	path_skip_whitespace(t)

	if t.pos >= len(t.data) {
		return 0
	}

	start_pos := t.pos
	c := t.data[t.pos]

	// Optional sign
	if is_sign(c) {
		t.pos += 1
		if t.pos >= len(t.data) {
			return 0
		}
		c = t.data[t.pos]
	}

	// Integer part
	for t.pos < len(t.data) && is_digit(t.data[t.pos]) {
		t.pos += 1
	}

	// Decimal part
	if t.pos < len(t.data) && t.data[t.pos] == '.' {
		t.pos += 1
		for t.pos < len(t.data) && is_digit(t.data[t.pos]) {
			t.pos += 1
		}
	}

	// Exponent part
	if t.pos < len(t.data) && (t.data[t.pos] == 'e' || t.data[t.pos] == 'E') {
		t.pos += 1
		if t.pos < len(t.data) && is_sign(t.data[t.pos]) {
			t.pos += 1
		}
		for t.pos < len(t.data) && is_digit(t.data[t.pos]) {
			t.pos += 1
		}
	}

	if start_pos == t.pos {
		// Parse the number
		num_str := t.data[start_pos:t.pos]
		val, ok := strconv.parse_f64(num_str)
		if !ok {
			return 0
		}

		return f32(val)
	}

	num_str := t.data[start_pos:t.pos]
	val, ok := strconv.parse_f64(num_str)
	if !ok {
		return 0
	}

	return f32(val)
}

// ============================================================================
// Elliptical Arc Conversion
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: Arc Center Parameterization
- Result of converting endpoint parameterization to center parameterization
- Used for rendering elliptical arcs
*/
Arc_Center_Params :: struct {
	cx:          f32, // Center x
	cy:          f32, // Center y
	rx:          f32, // X radius (may be corrected)
	ry:          f32, // Y radius (may be corrected)
	phi:         f32, // Rotation angle (radians)
	theta1:      f32, // Start angle (radians)
	delta_theta: f32, // Sweep angle (radians, can be negative)
}

/**
@documentation: Convert Arc from Endpoint to Center Parameterization
- Implements the algorithm from SVG 1.1 Appendix F.6
- Input: current point, arc parameters from A command
- Output: center point, radii, rotation, start angle, sweep angle
- This conversion is necessary for rendering arcs using most graphics APIs
*/
@(private)
arc_endpoint_to_center :: proc(current: SVG_Point2D, params: Arc_Data) -> Arc_Center_Params {
	// Extract parameters
	x1 := current[0]
	y1 := current[1]
	x2 := params.end[0]
	y2 := params.end[1]
	phi := math.to_radians(params.rotation)

	// Step 1: Compute (x1', y1')
	dx := (x1 - x2) / 2
	dy := (y1 - y2) / 2
	x1p := math.cos(phi) * dx + math.sin(phi) * dy
	y1p := -math.sin(phi) * dx + math.cos(phi) * dy

	// Step 2: Compute (cx', cy')
	// Correct radii if necessary
	rx := params.rx
	ry := params.ry

	// Ensure radii are positive
	if rx < 0 {rx = -rx}
	if ry < 0 {ry = -ry}

	// Check if radii need correction
	lambda := (x1p * x1p) / (rx * rx) + (y1p * y1p) / (ry * ry)
	if lambda > 1 {
		sqrt_lambda := math.sqrt_f32(lambda)
		rx *= sqrt_lambda
		ry *= sqrt_lambda
	}

	// Calculate (cx', cy')
	s := math.sqrt_f32(
		math.max(
			f32(0),
			(rx * rx * ry * ry - rx * rx * y1p * y1p - ry * ry * x1p * x1p) /
			(rx * rx * y1p * y1p + ry * ry * x1p * x1p),
		),
	)

	// Adjust sign of s based on large_arc and sweep flags
	if params.large_arc == params.sweep {
		s = -s
	}

	cxp := s * rx * y1p / ry
	cyp := -s * ry * x1p / rx

	// Step 3: Compute (cx, cy) from (cx', cy')
	cx := math.cos(phi) * cxp - math.sin(phi) * cyp + (x1 + x2) / 2
	cy := math.sin(phi) * cxp + math.cos(phi) * cyp + (y1 + y2) / 2

	// Step 4: Compute theta1 and delta_theta
	// theta1 is the angle from the center to the start point
	theta1_vec_x := (x1p - cxp) / rx
	theta1_vec_y := (y1p - cyp) / ry
	theta1 := math.atan2_f32(theta1_vec_y, theta1_vec_x)

	// delta_theta is the sweep from start to end
	delta_vec_x := (-x1p - cxp) / rx
	delta_vec_y := (-y1p - cyp) / ry
	delta_theta_unnorm := math.atan2_f32(delta_vec_y, delta_vec_x) - theta1

	// Normalize delta_theta to the correct range based on sweep flag
	delta_theta := delta_theta_unnorm
	if params.sweep {
		// Positive sweep
		for delta_theta < 0 {
			delta_theta += 2 * math.PI
		}
		if delta_theta > 2 * math.PI {
			delta_theta -= 2 * math.PI
		}
	} else {
		// Negative sweep
		for delta_theta > 0 {
			delta_theta -= 2 * math.PI
		}
		if delta_theta < -2 * math.PI {
			delta_theta += 2 * math.PI
		}
	}

	return Arc_Center_Params {
		cx = cx,
		cy = cy,
		rx = rx,
		ry = ry,
		phi = phi,
		theta1 = theta1,
		delta_theta = delta_theta,
	}
}

/**
@documentation: Flatten Arc to Cubic Bezier Curves
- Approximates an elliptical arc using cubic Bezier segments
- Returns array of cubic Bezier control points [cp1, cp2, end, cp1, cp2, end, ...]
- Tolerance controls the approximation quality (default: 0.5)
- This is useful for renderers that don't natively support arcs
*/
@(private)
arc_to_cubic_beziers :: proc(
	center: Arc_Center_Params,
	tolerance: f32 = 0.5,
	allocator: mem.Allocator,
) -> []f32 {
	// Maximum angle for each segment to meet tolerance
	// For a unit circle, the maximum deviation from a cubic Bezier
	// occurs at the midpoint. For angle theta, max error is approximately:
	// error = (4/3) * tan(theta/4) - sin(theta/2)
	// We solve for theta given the tolerance

	// Simplification: use fixed angle steps based on tolerance
	// For tolerance 0.5, angle step is about PI/4 (45 degrees)
	// For tolerance 0.1, angle step is about PI/8 (22.5 degrees)
	// For tolerance 1.0, angle step is about PI/2 (90 degrees)

	max_segment_angle: f32 = math.PI / 4 // Default 45 degrees
	if tolerance <= 0.1 {
		max_segment_angle = math.PI / 8
	} else if tolerance >= 1.0 {
		max_segment_angle = math.PI / 2
	}

	// Calculate number of segments needed
	abs_delta := math.abs(center.delta_theta)
	num_segments := int(math.ceil_f32(abs_delta / max_segment_angle))
	if num_segments < 1 {
		num_segments = 1
	}

	segment_angle := center.delta_theta / f32(num_segments)

	// Precompute rotation matrix
	cos_phi := math.cos(center.phi)
	sin_phi := math.sin(center.phi)

	// Generate Bezier segments
	bezier_points := make([dynamic]f32, 0, num_segments * 6)

	current_theta := center.theta1

	for i := 0; i < num_segments; i += 1 {
		theta_start := current_theta
		theta_end := current_theta + segment_angle

		// Calculate control points using the arc-to-Bezier formula
		// kappa is the magic constant: (4/3) * tan(angle/4)
		kappa: f32 = 4.0 / 3.0 * math.tan_f32(segment_angle / 4.0)

		// Start point on ellipse
		cos_start := math.cos(theta_start)
		sin_start := math.sin(theta_start)
		x1 := center.cx + cos_phi * center.rx * cos_start - sin_phi * center.ry * sin_start
		y1 := center.cy + sin_phi * center.rx * cos_start + cos_phi * center.ry * sin_start

		// End point on ellipse
		cos_end := math.cos(theta_end)
		sin_end := math.sin(theta_end)
		x4 := center.cx + cos_phi * center.rx * cos_end - sin_phi * center.ry * sin_end
		y4 := center.cy + sin_phi * center.rx * cos_end + cos_phi * center.ry * sin_end

		// Tangent vectors at start and end
		dx1 := -center.rx * cos_phi * sin_start - center.ry * sin_phi * cos_start
		dy1 := -center.rx * sin_phi * sin_start + center.ry * cos_phi * cos_start
		dx4 := -center.rx * cos_phi * sin_end - center.ry * sin_phi * cos_end
		dy4 := -center.rx * sin_phi * sin_end + center.ry * cos_phi * cos_end

		// Control points
		x2 := x1 + kappa * dx1
		y2 := y1 + kappa * dy1
		x3 := x4 - kappa * dx4
		y3 := y4 - kappa * dy4

		// Append to results: cp1, cp2, end
		append(&bezier_points, x2, y2)
		append(&bezier_points, x3, y3)
		append(&bezier_points, x4, y4)

		current_theta = theta_end
	}

	return bezier_points[:]
}

/**
@documentation: Destroy Parsed Path
- Free memory associated with parsed path
*/
@(private)
destroy_parsed_path :: proc(path: ^SVG_Parsed_Path) {
	if path.commands != nil {
		delete(path.commands)
		path.commands = nil
	}
}

/**
@documentation: Theme Validation (Phase 10C2.12)
- Validates theme token references in RSD drawings.
- Checks that referenced tokens exist in the theme store.
- Reports missing or invalid references with severity levels.
*/

import "core:fmt"
import "core:mem"
import "core:strings"
import theme "project:ui/theme"

// Validation severity levels
Validation_Severity :: enum {
    Info,    // Informational (e.g., token usage count)
    Warning, // Non-critical issues (e.g., unused fallback)
    Error,   // Critical issues (e.g., missing token)
}

// Individual validation issue
Validation_Issue :: struct {
    severity:  Validation_Severity,
    layer:     string,          // Layer name (empty for global)
    item_id:   u32,             // Path item ID (0 for global)
    token:     string,          // Theme token that was validated
    message:   string,          // Human-readable message
    path:      string,          // Property path (e.g., "fill", "stroke.color")
}

// Validation result summary
Validation_Result :: struct {
    issues:        [dynamic]Validation_Issue,
    valid_tokens:  u32,         // Count of valid token references
    invalid_tokens: u32,        // Count of invalid token references
    warnings:      u32,         // Total warnings
    errors:        u32,         // Total errors (critical)
}

// Theme token reference found during traversal
Token_Reference :: struct {
    token:     string,
    kind:      Token_Kind,
    path:      string,          // Property path (fill, stroke.color, etc.)
    layer:     string,
    item_id:   u32,
}

// Kind of theme token being referenced
Token_Kind :: enum {
    Color,
    Gradient,
}

// create_validation_result creates a new validation result with allocated issues array.
create_validation_result :: proc(allocator: mem.Allocator = context.allocator) -> Validation_Result {
    return Validation_Result {
        issues = make([dynamic]Validation_Issue, allocator),
    }
}

// destroy_validation_result frees resources in a validation result.
destroy_validation_result :: proc(result: ^Validation_Result) {
    delete(result.issues)
}

// add_issue adds a validation issue to the result and updates counters.
add_issue :: proc(result: ^Validation_Result, issue: Validation_Issue) {
    append(&result.issues, issue)
    
    #partial switch issue.severity {
    case .Info:
        // No counter update for info
    case .Warning:
        result.warnings += 1
    case .Error:
        result.errors += 1
    }
}

// add_info adds an info-level validation issue.
add_info :: proc(result: ^Validation_Result, layer_name: string, item_id: u32, token, path, message: string, allocator: mem.Allocator) {
    issue := Validation_Issue {
        severity = .Info,
        layer = strings.clone(layer_name, allocator),
        item_id = item_id,
        token = strings.clone(token, allocator),
        message = strings.clone(message, allocator),
        path = strings.clone(path, allocator),
    }
    add_issue(result, issue)
}

// add_warning adds a warning-level validation issue.
add_warning :: proc(result: ^Validation_Result, layer_name: string, item_id: u32, token, path, message: string, allocator: mem.Allocator) {
    issue := Validation_Issue {
        severity = .Warning,
        layer = strings.clone(layer_name, allocator),
        item_id = item_id,
        token = strings.clone(token, allocator),
        message = strings.clone(message, allocator),
        path = strings.clone(path, allocator),
    }
    add_issue(result, issue)
}

// add_error adds an error-level validation issue.
add_error :: proc(result: ^Validation_Result, layer_name: string, item_id: u32, token, path, message: string, allocator: mem.Allocator) {
    issue := Validation_Issue {
        severity = .Error,
        layer = strings.clone(layer_name, allocator),
        item_id = item_id,
        token = strings.clone(token, allocator),
        message = strings.clone(message, allocator),
        path = strings.clone(path, allocator),
    }
    add_issue(result, issue)
}

/**
@procedure: validate_theme_token
Validates if a theme token exists and matches the expected kind.

@param token: The theme token path (e.g., "colors.primary", "gradients.button")
@param kind: The expected kind of token (Color or Gradient)
@param theme: The theme token store to validate against

@returns: (exists: bool, is_valid: bool)
- exists: true if token is found in theme
- is_valid: true if token exists AND matches expected kind
*/
validate_theme_token :: proc(token: string, kind: Token_Kind, theme: ^theme.Token_Store) -> (exists: bool, is_valid: bool) {
    if theme == nil {
        return false, false
    }
    
    #partial switch kind {
    case .Color:
        // Check direct lookup
        if _, ok := theme.colors[token]; ok {
            return true, true
        }
        // Check with "colors." prefix if not already present
        if !strings.has_prefix(token, "colors.") {
            prefixed_builder := strings.builder_make()
            strings.write_string(&prefixed_builder, "colors.")
            strings.write_string(&prefixed_builder, token)
            prefixed := strings.to_string(prefixed_builder)
            if _, ok := theme.colors[prefixed]; ok {
                return true, true
            }
        }
        
    case .Gradient:
        // Check direct lookup
        if _, ok := theme.gradients[token]; ok {
            return true, true
        }
        // Check with "gradients." prefix if not already present
        if !strings.has_prefix(token, "gradients.") {
            prefixed_builder := strings.builder_make()
            strings.write_string(&prefixed_builder, "gradients.")
            strings.write_string(&prefixed_builder, token)
            prefixed := strings.to_string(prefixed_builder)
            if _, ok := theme.gradients[prefixed]; ok {
                return true, true
            }
        }
    }
    
    return false, false
}

/**
@procedure: collect_theme_references
Collects all theme token references from a drawing.

@param drawing: The RSD drawing to scan
@param allocator: Memory allocator for collecting references

@returns: Dynamic array of Token_Reference objects
*/
collect_theme_references :: proc(drawing: ^Drawing, allocator: mem.Allocator = context.allocator) -> [dynamic]Token_Reference {
    references := make([dynamic]Token_Reference, allocator)
    
    if drawing == nil {
        return references
    }
    
    for layer, layer_idx in drawing.layers {
        for item, item_idx in layer.items {
            collect_item_theme_refs(item, layer.name, u32(item_idx), &references, allocator)
        }
    }
    
    return references
}

// collect_item_theme_refs recursively collects theme references from an item.
collect_item_theme_refs :: proc(item: Item, layer_name: string, item_id: u32, references: ^[dynamic]Token_Reference, allocator: mem.Allocator) {
    #partial switch &it in item {
    case Path_Item:
        // Check fill style for theme references
        check_fill_theme_ref(it.fill, layer_name, item_id, "fill", references, allocator)
        
        // Check stroke style for theme token
        if it.stroke.theme_token != "" {
            ref := Token_Reference {
                token = strings.clone(it.stroke.theme_token, allocator),
                kind = .Color,
                path = strings.clone("stroke.color", allocator),
                layer = strings.clone(layer_name, allocator),
                item_id = item_id,
            }
            append(references, ref)
        }
        
    case Group_Item:
        for sub_item, sub_idx in it.items {
            collect_item_theme_refs(sub_item, layer_name, item_id, references, allocator)
        }
    }
}

// check_fill_theme_ref checks a fill style for theme references.
check_fill_theme_ref :: proc(fill: Fill_Style, layer_name: string, item_id: u32, path: string, references: ^[dynamic]Token_Reference, allocator: mem.Allocator) {
    #partial switch &f in fill {
    case Theme_Ref:
        // Color theme reference
        ref := Token_Reference {
            token = strings.clone(f.token, allocator),
            kind = .Color,
            path = strings.clone(path, allocator),
            layer = strings.clone(layer_name, allocator),
            item_id = item_id,
        }
        append(references, ref)
        
    case Theme_Gradient_Ref:
        // Gradient theme reference
        ref := Token_Reference {
            token = strings.clone(f.token, allocator),
            kind = .Gradient,
            path = strings.clone(path, allocator),
            layer = strings.clone(layer_name, allocator),
            item_id = item_id,
        }
        append(references, ref)
    }
}

/**
@procedure: validate_drawing_theme_refs
Validates all theme token references in a drawing against a theme store.

@param drawing: The RSD drawing to validate
@param theme: The theme token store to validate against (nil for no validation)
@param allocator: Memory allocator for validation result

@returns: Validation_Result with all issues found
*/
validate_drawing_theme_refs :: proc(
    drawing:   ^Drawing,
    theme:     ^theme.Token_Store,
    allocator: mem.Allocator = context.allocator,
) -> Validation_Result {
    result := create_validation_result(allocator)
    
    if drawing == nil {
        add_error(&result, "", 0, "", "", "Nil drawing provided for validation", allocator)
        return result
    }
    
    // Collect all theme references from the drawing
    references := collect_theme_references(drawing, allocator)
    defer delete(references)
    
    // Validate each reference
    for ref in references {
        exists, is_valid := validate_theme_token(ref.token, ref.kind, theme)
        
        if exists && is_valid {
            // Token exists and matches expected kind - valid reference
            result.valid_tokens += 1
            msg := "Theme token resolved successfully"
            add_info(&result, ref.layer, ref.item_id, ref.token, ref.path, msg, allocator)
        } else if exists && !is_valid {
            // Token exists but kind mismatch (e.g., color token used for gradient)
            result.invalid_tokens += 1
            expected := ref.kind == .Color ? "color" : "gradient"
            found := ref.kind == .Color ? "gradient" : "color"
            msg := fmt.tprintf("Token type mismatch: expected %s, found %s", expected, found)
            add_error(&result, ref.layer, ref.item_id, ref.token, ref.path, msg, allocator)
        } else {
            // Token not found in theme
            result.invalid_tokens += 1
            msg := fmt.tprintf("Theme token not found: '%s' (fallback value will be used)", ref.token)
            add_warning(&result, ref.layer, ref.item_id, ref.token, ref.path, msg, allocator)
        }
    }
    
    // If no references found, add an info message
    if len(references) == 0 {
        add_info(&result, "", 0, "", "", "No theme references found in drawing", allocator)
    }
    
    return result
}

/**
@procedure: validate_theme_token_exists
Quick check if a specific token exists in the theme.

@param token: The theme token path
@param theme: The theme token store

@returns: true if token exists in theme (any kind)
*/
validate_theme_token_exists :: proc(token: string, theme: ^theme.Token_Store) -> bool {
    if theme == nil {
        return false
    }
    
    // Check colors
    if _, ok := theme.colors[token]; ok {
        return true
    }
    if !strings.has_prefix(token, "colors.") {
        prefixed_builder := strings.builder_make()
        strings.write_string(&prefixed_builder, "colors.")
        strings.write_string(&prefixed_builder, token)
        prefixed := strings.to_string(prefixed_builder)
        if _, ok := theme.colors[prefixed]; ok {
            return true
        }
    }
    
    // Check gradients
    if _, ok := theme.gradients[token]; ok {
        return true
    }
    if !strings.has_prefix(token, "gradients.") {
        prefixed_builder := strings.builder_make()
        strings.write_string(&prefixed_builder, "gradients.")
        strings.write_string(&prefixed_builder, token)
        prefixed := strings.to_string(prefixed_builder)
        if _, ok := theme.gradients[prefixed]; ok {
            return true
        }
    }
    
    return false
}

/**
@procedure: format_validation_report
Creates a human-readable report of validation results.

@param result: The validation result to format
@param allocator: Memory allocator for the report string

@returns: Formatted string with all issues and summary
*/
format_validation_report :: proc(result: ^Validation_Result, allocator: mem.Allocator = context.allocator) -> string {
    builder := strings.builder_make(allocator)
    defer strings.builder_destroy(&builder)
    
    // Summary header
    strings.write_string(&builder, "=== Theme Validation Report ===\n")
    fmt.sbprintf(&builder, "Valid tokens:   %d\n", result.valid_tokens)
    fmt.sbprintf(&builder, "Invalid tokens: %d\n", result.invalid_tokens)
    fmt.sbprintf(&builder, "Warnings:       %d\n", result.warnings)
    fmt.sbprintf(&builder, "Errors:         %d\n", result.errors)
    strings.write_string(&builder, "\n")
    
    if len(result.issues) == 0 {
        strings.write_string(&builder, "No validation issues found.\n")
        return strings.to_string(builder)
    }
    
    // Group issues by severity
    strings.write_string(&builder, "=== Issues ===\n")
    
    for issue in result.issues {
        severity_str := "INFO"
        #partial switch issue.severity {
        case .Warning:
            severity_str = "WARN"
        case .Error:
            severity_str = "ERROR"
        }
        
        prefix := fmt.tprintf("[%s]", severity_str)
        if issue.layer != "" {
            fmt.sbprintf(&builder, "%s Layer='%s' Item=%d", prefix, issue.layer, issue.item_id)
        } else {
            fmt.sbprintf(&builder, "%s", prefix)
        }
        
        if issue.token != "" {
            fmt.sbprintf(&builder, " Token='%s'", issue.token)
        }
        if issue.path != "" {
            fmt.sbprintf(&builder, " Path='%s'", issue.path)
        }
        fmt.sbprintf(&builder, " - %s\n", issue.message)
    }
    
    return strings.to_string(builder)
}
