DEBUG: Source length: 27156
DEBUG: Source content: package graphics

import "base:runtime"
import "core:fmt"
import "core:math"
import "core:mem"
import "core:strings"
import "core:time"
import assets "project:assets"

import logging "project:logging"
import renderer "project:renderer"
import vfs "project:vfs"
// asset_types is automatically available in the same package

// Debug flags for asset management
DEBUG_ASSET_RENDERING :: false

// Asset Manager Configuration
// Configuration options for the asset management system
Asset_Manager_Config :: struct {
	max_assets:                int, // Maximum number of assets to cache
	max_memory_mb:             int, // Maximum memory usage in MB
	enable_background_loading: bool, // Enable background loading
	cache_directory:           string, // Directory for cached assets
}

// Default Asset Manager Configuration
// Returns a sensible default configuration for the asset manager
defaultAssetManagerConfig :: proc() -> Asset_Manager_Config {
	return Asset_Manager_Config {
		max_assets = 1024,
		max_memory_mb = 512,
		enable_background_loading = true,
		cache_directory = "artifacts/cache/",
	}
}

// Asset Manager main structure
// Manages the lifecycle of all assets in the system
Asset_Manager :: struct {
	config:       Asset_Manager_Config,
	assets:       map[u32]_Asset_Buffer, // Map of asset buffers by ID
	path_to_id:   map[string]u32, // Map of paths to asset IDs
	next_id:      u32, // Next available asset ID
	allocator:    mem.Allocator, // Allocator for persistent data
	temp_alloc:   mem.Allocator, // Allocator for temporary operations
	total_memory: u64, // Total memory used by all assets
	stats:        Asset_Manager_Stats, // Usage statistics
}

// Asset Manager Statistics
// Tracks usage and performance metrics
Asset_Manager_Stats :: struct {
	assets_loaded:    int,
	assets_parsed:    int,
	assets_errored:   int,
	memory_used:      u64,
	memory_peak:      u64,
	load_time_total:  f64,
	parse_time_total: f64,
}

// Private Asset Buffer structure
// Contains the actual data and state for a managed asset
@(private)
_Asset_Buffer :: struct {
	id:           u32,
	path:         string,
	asset_type:   GPU_Asset_Type,
	data:         []u8,
	state:        GPU_Asset_State,
	parse_result: assets.Asset_Parse_Result,
	timestamp:    i64,
	ref_count:    int,
	data_size:    u64,
	last_used:    i64,
	load_time:    f64,
	parse_time:   f64,
	arena_buffer: []u8, // Persistent storage for parsed data (SVG/RSD)
}

// Global Asset Manager instance
// The single instance of the asset manager for the application
asset_manager: ^Asset_Manager

// Initialize Asset Manager
// Creates and initializes the asset management system
initAssetManager :: proc(config: Asset_Manager_Config) -> bool {
	if asset_manager != nil {
		logging.warn(.Asset, "Asset manager already initialized")
		return false
	}

	logging.info(.Lifecycle, "Initializing asset manager")

	// Create asset manager instance
	asset_manager = new(Asset_Manager)
	asset_manager.config = config
	asset_manager.next_id = 1
	asset_manager.allocator = runtime.default_allocator()
	asset_manager.temp_alloc = runtime.default_allocator()

	// Initialize maps
	asset_manager.assets = make(map[u32]_Asset_Buffer, asset_manager.allocator)
	asset_manager.path_to_id = make(map[string]u32, asset_manager.allocator)

	// Create cache directory if it doesn't exist
	gpu_asset_ensure_cache_dir(config.cache_directory)

	logging.info(.Lifecycle, "Asset manager initialized successfully")
	return true
}

// Shutdown Asset Manager
// Cleans up all resources used by the asset management system
shutdownAssetManager :: proc() {
	if asset_manager == nil {
		return
	}

	logging.info(.Lifecycle, "Shutting down asset manager")

	// Clean up all assets
	for key in asset_manager.assets {
		asset := &asset_manager.assets[key]
		_cleanupAssetBuffer(asset)
	}

	// Clear maps
	asset_manager.assets = make(map[u32]_Asset_Buffer, asset_manager.allocator)
	asset_manager.path_to_id = make(map[string]u32, asset_manager.allocator)

	// Free manager instance
	free(asset_manager)
	asset_manager = nil

	logging.info(.Lifecycle, "Asset manager shutdown complete")
}

// Private: Cleanup Asset Buffer
// Cleans up resources associated with a single asset buffer
@(private)
_cleanupAssetBuffer :: proc(asset: ^_Asset_Buffer) {
	// Free asset data
	if len(asset.data) > 0 {
		delete(asset.data)
		asset.data = nil
	}

	// Free arena buffer
	if len(asset.arena_buffer) > 0 {
		delete(asset.arena_buffer)
		asset.arena_buffer = nil
	}

	// Clean up parse results based on type
	switch result in asset.parse_result {
	case rawptr:
		// Determine type based on asset_type and free appropriately
		switch asset.asset_type {
		case .SVG:
			svg_ptr := transmute(^SVG_Element)(result)
			free(svg_ptr, asset_manager.allocator)
		case .RSD:
			// RSD drawings are managed by their allocator, no need to free
		case .Image, .Font:
			// Not yet implemented
		}
	case string:
		// Error strings are temporary, no cleanup needed
		break
	}

	// Reset state
	asset.state = .Raw
	// Initialize parse_result as empty (will be set when parsed)
	asset.parse_result = rawptr(nil)
}


// Find Loaded Asset
// Finds an asset by path and returns its handle and found status
findLoadedAsset :: proc(path: string) -> (Asset_Handle, bool) {
	if asset_manager == nil {
		return {}, false
	}

	if asset_id, exists := asset_manager.path_to_id[path]; exists {
		if asset, found := asset_manager.assets[asset_id]; found {
			return Asset_Handle{id = asset_id, valid = true}, true
		}
	}

	return {}, false
}

// Get Asset Parse Result
// Safely returns the parsed result (e.g., ^SVG_Element) for a given handle
getAssetParseResult :: proc(handle: Asset_Handle) -> assets.Asset_Parse_Result {
	if asset_manager == nil || !handle.valid do return nil
	if asset, found := asset_manager.assets[handle.id]; found {
		return asset.parse_result
	}
	return nil
}

// Render Loading Placeholder
// Renders a placeholder for an asset that is still loading
renderLoadingPlaceholder :: proc(config: GPU_Asset_Render_Config) -> bool {
	// Draw a simple loading indicator
	// This is a temporary implementation
	g2d_fill_rect(
		config.x,
		config.y,
		config.width,
		config.height,
		renderer.paint_solid([4]f32{0.2, 0.2, 0.2, 1.0}),
	)

	// Draw loading text
	g2d_draw_text(
		config.x + 10,
		config.y + 20,
		"Loading...",
		renderer.Font_Handle_Invalid, // TODO: Use proper font
		{1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0},
	)

	return true
}

// Render Error Placeholder
// Renders a placeholder for an asset that failed to load
renderErrorPlaceholder :: proc(config: GPU_Asset_Render_Config, error_msg: string) -> bool {
	// Draw error background
	g2d_fill_rect(
		config.x,
		config.y,
		config.width,
		config.height,
		renderer.paint_solid([4]f32{0.8, 0.2, 0.2, 1.0}),
	)

	// Draw error text
	g2d_draw_text(
		config.x + 10,
		config.y + 20,
		fmt.tprintf("Error: %s", error_msg),
		renderer.Font_Handle_Invalid, // TODO: Use proper font
		{1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0},
	)

	return true
}

// Trigger Background Loading
// Triggers background loading of an asset (stub for now)
triggerBackgroundLoading :: proc(path: string, asset_type: GPU_Asset_Type) {
	if !asset_manager.config.enable_background_loading {
		return
	}

	// TODO: Implement actual background loading
	logging.info(.Asset, fmt.tprintf("Background loading triggered for: %s", path))

	// For now, just load it synchronously in the background
	// In a real implementation, this would use a worker thread/queue
	// TODO: Implement actual background loading
	logging.info(.Asset, fmt.tprintf("Background loading triggered for: %s", path))
	// Note: Background loading not yet implemented
}

// Get Asset Statistics
// Returns statistics about the asset management system
getAssetStatistics :: proc() -> Asset_Manager_Stats {
	if asset_manager == nil {
		return Asset_Manager_Stats{}
	}
	return asset_manager.stats
}

// Log Asset Statistics
// Logs current asset management statistics
logAssetStatistics :: proc() {
	if asset_manager == nil {
		return
	}

	stats := asset_manager.stats

	logging.info(
		.Asset,
		fmt.tprintf(
			"Asset Manager Stats: Loaded=%d, Parsed=%d, Errored=%d, " + "Memory=%.2f/%.2f MB",
			stats.assets_loaded,
			stats.assets_parsed,
			stats.assets_errored,
			f64(stats.memory_used) / (1024 * 1024),
			f64(stats.memory_peak) / (1024 * 1024),
		),
	)
}

// Load Asset (New Architecture)
// Loads raw asset data without parsing
// Returns LoadingHandle or error message
loadAssetNew :: proc(path: string, asset_type: GPU_Asset_Type) -> (GPU_LoadingHandle, string) {
	if asset_manager == nil {
		return GPU_LoadingHandle{}, "Asset manager not initialized"
	}

	// Check if asset is already loaded
	if asset_id, exists := asset_manager.path_to_id[path]; exists {
		if asset, found := asset_manager.assets[asset_id]; found {
			// Return error if already loaded (should use existing handle)
			return GPU_LoadingHandle{}, fmt.tprintf("Asset already loaded: %s", path)
		}
	}

	// Check if we've reached maximum assets
	if len(asset_manager.assets) >= asset_manager.config.max_assets {
		return GPU_LoadingHandle{}, "Maximum asset count reached"
	}

	// Generate new asset ID
	asset_id := asset_manager.next_id
	asset_manager.next_id += 1

	// Load asset data using VFS
	data, ok := vfs.loadAsset(path)
	if !ok {
		// Fall back to direct file system (native only)
		data, ok = gpu_asset_read_file(path, asset_manager.allocator)
		if !ok {
			return GPU_LoadingHandle{}, fmt.tprintf("Failed to load asset data: %s", path)
		}
	}

	// Create asset buffer and add to asset manager
	asset := _Asset_Buffer {
		id         = asset_id,
		path       = path,
		asset_type = asset_type,
		data       = data,
		state      = .Raw,
		timestamp  = time.to_unix_nanoseconds(time.now()),
		ref_count  = 1,
		data_size  = u64(len(data)),
	}

	// Add to asset manager
	asset_manager.assets[asset_id] = asset
	asset_manager.path_to_id[path] = asset_id
	asset_manager.total_memory += u64(len(data))
	asset_manager.stats.assets_loaded += 1

	// Create loading handle
	loading_handle := GPU_LoadingHandle {
		id         = asset_id,
		data       = data,
		path       = path,
		asset_type = asset_type,
		timestamp  = time.to_unix_nanoseconds(time.now()),
	}

	logging.info(.Asset, fmt.tprintf("Loaded asset %d: %s (%d bytes)", asset_id, path, len(data)))
	return loading_handle, ""
}

// Parse Asset (New Architecture)
// Converts LoadingHandle to RenderHandle
// Returns RenderHandle or error message
parseAssetNew :: proc(loading_handle: GPU_LoadingHandle) -> (GPU_RenderHandle, string) {
	if asset_manager == nil {
		return GPU_RenderHandle{}, "Asset manager not initialized"
	}

	// Validate loading handle
	if loading_handle.id == 0 {
		return GPU_RenderHandle{}, "Invalid loading handle (ID=0)"
	}

	// Check if asset exists
	asset_id := loading_handle.id
	asset, found := asset_manager.assets[asset_id]
	if !found {
		return GPU_RenderHandle{}, fmt.tprintf("Asset not found: %d", asset_id)
	}

	// Check current state
	if asset.state != .Raw {
		return GPU_RenderHandle{}, fmt.tprintf("Asset not in Raw state (state: %s)", assetStateToString(asset.state))
	}

	// Mark as parsing
	asset.state = .Parsing
	asset.asset_type = loading_handle.asset_type
	asset.timestamp = time.to_unix_nanoseconds(time.now())
	asset_manager.assets[asset_id] = asset

	// Parse based on asset type
	start_time := time.to_unix_nanoseconds(time.now())
	parse_success := false
	parse_result: assets.Asset_Parse_Result

	switch loading_handle.asset_type {
	case .SVG:
		// Create persistent arena for SVG parsing
		arena_size := 4 * 1024 * 1024 // 4MB persistent arena
		asset.arena_buffer = make([]byte, arena_size, asset_manager.allocator)

		arena: mem.Arena
		mem.arena_init(&arena, asset.arena_buffer)

		// Parse SVG
		svg_element, warnings := parse_svg(loading_handle.data, arena)

		if len(warnings) > 0 {
			for warning in warnings {
				logging.warn(.SVG, fmt.tprintf("[ASSET_%d] SVG Warning: %s", asset_id, warning))
			}
		}

		// Check if SVG parsing failed
		if svg_element.type == .None {
			error_result: string = "Failed to parse SVG: invalid root element"
			parse_result = error_result
		} else {
			// Allocate the element on the heap so we can have a stable pointer
			heap_el := new(SVG_Element, asset_manager.allocator)
			heap_el^ = svg_element
			svg_result: ^SVG_Element = heap_el
			parse_result = rawptr(svg_result)
			parse_success = true
		}

	case .RSD:
		// Parse RSD
		rsd_drawing, ok := decode_bytes(loading_handle.data, asset_manager.allocator)

		if !ok {
			error_result: string = "Failed to parse RSD: invalid format"
			parse_result = error_result
		} else {
			// Debug: Log RSD structure
			when logging.DEBUG_RSD {
				paths_count := 0
				groups_count := 0
				for layer in rsd_drawing.layers {
					for item in layer.items {
						if _, ok := item.(Path_Item); ok {
							paths_count += 1
						} else if _, ok := item.(Group_Item); ok {
							groups_count += 1
						}
					}
				}
				logging.info(
					.RSD,
					fmt.tprintf(
						"[RSD_LOAD] Loaded RSD: %d layers, %d paths, %d groups, %d gradients",
						len(rsd_drawing.layers),
						paths_count,
						groups_count,
						len(rsd_drawing.gradients),
					),
					key = "rsd_load_stats",
				)
			}
			drawing_result: ^Drawing = rsd_drawing
			parse_result = rawptr(drawing_result)
			parse_success = true
		}

	case .Image, .Font:
		parse_result = fmt.tprintf(
			"Asset type not yet supported: %s",
			assetTypeToString(loading_handle.asset_type),
		)
	}

	asset.parse_time = f64(time.to_unix_nanoseconds(time.now()) - start_time) / 1e9 // Convert to seconds

	if !parse_success {
		asset.state = .Error
		asset.parse_result = parse_result
		asset_manager.assets[asset_id] = asset
		logging.error(.Asset, fmt.tprintf("Parse failed for asset %d: %s", asset_id, parse_result))
		return GPU_RenderHandle{}, fmt.tprintf("Parse failed: %s", parse_result)
	}

	// Create render handle
	render_handle := GPU_RenderHandle {
		id         = asset_id,
		asset_type = loading_handle.asset_type,
		timestamp  = time.to_unix_nanoseconds(time.now()),
		parse_time = asset.parse_time,
	}

	// Store parse result based on type
	switch v in parse_result {
	case rawptr:
		// Determine type based on asset_type
		switch loading_handle.asset_type {
		case .SVG:
			render_handle.svg_element = transmute(^SVG_Element)(v)
		case .RSD:
			render_handle.rsd_drawing = transmute(^Drawing)(v)
		case .Image, .Font:
			asset.state = .Error
			asset.parse_result = parse_result
			return GPU_RenderHandle{}, fmt.tprintf("Unsupported asset type: %v", loading_handle.asset_type)
		}
	case string:
		asset.state = .Error
		asset.parse_result = parse_result
		return GPU_RenderHandle{}, fmt.tprintf("Unexpected parse result: %s", v)
	}

	// Update asset state
	asset.state = .Parsed
	asset.parse_result = parse_result
	asset.ref_count = 1
	asset_manager.assets[asset_id] = asset

	// Store parse result in render handle by extracting from assets parse result
	#partial switch v in asset.parse_result {
	case rawptr:
		// We need to determine the type based on the asset_type
		switch loading_handle.asset_type {
		case .SVG:
			render_handle.svg_element = transmute(^SVG_Element)( v)
		case .RSD:
			render_handle.rsd_drawing = transmute(^Drawing)( v)
		case .Image, .Font:
			// Not yet implemented
			return GPU_RenderHandle{}, fmt.tprintf("Unsupported asset type: %v", loading_handle.asset_type)
		}
	case string:
		// This shouldn't happen since we already checked parse_success
		return GPU_RenderHandle{}, fmt.tprintf("Unexpected parse result in asset buffer: %s", v)
	}

	logging.info(
		.Asset,
		fmt.tprintf(
			"Parsed asset %d successfully (%.2fms)",
			asset_id,
			render_handle.parse_time * 1000,
		),
	)
	return render_handle, ""
}

gpu_render_asset_immediate :: proc(render_handle: GPU_RenderHandle, config: GPU_Asset_Render_Config) -> bool {
	// Validate render handle
	if render_handle.id == 0 {
		logging.error(.Asset, "Invalid render handle (ID=0)")
		return false
	}

	// Check asset manager state
	if asset_manager == nil {
		logging.error(.Asset, "Asset manager not initialized")
		return false
	}

	// Validate asset existence and state
	asset, found := asset_manager.assets[render_handle.id]
	if !found {
		logging.error(.Asset, fmt.tprintf("Asset not found: %d", render_handle.id))
		return false
	}

	if asset.state != .Parsed {
		logging.error(
			.Asset,
			fmt.tprintf(
				"Asset not in Parsed state: %d (state: %s)",
				render_handle.id,
				assetStateToString(asset.state),
			),
		)
		return false
	}

	// Render based on asset type with proper error handling
	if render_handle.svg_element != nil {
		when DEBUG_ASSET_RENDERING {
			logging.debug(.SVG, fmt.tprintf("Rendering SVG asset %d", render_handle.id))
		}
		return _renderSvgAssetNew(render_handle.svg_element, config)
	}

	if render_handle.rsd_drawing != nil {
		when DEBUG_ASSET_RENDERING {
			logging.debug(.RSD, fmt.tprintf("Rendering RSD asset %d", render_handle.id))
		}
		return _renderRsdAssetNew(render_handle.rsd_drawing, config)
	}

	// Handle error case
	error_msg := ""
	#partial switch &v in asset.parse_result {
	case string:
		error_msg = v
	}

	logging.error(.Asset, fmt.tprintf("Asset parse error: %d - %s", render_handle.id, error_msg))
	return false
}

// Private: Render SVG Asset (New Architecture)
// Renders an SVG element using the provided configuration
@(private)
_renderSvgAssetNew :: proc(element: ^SVG_Element, config: GPU_Asset_Render_Config) -> bool {
	// Save current matrix
	g2d_push_matrix()
	defer g2d_pop_matrix()

	// Apply transformation
	g2d_translate(config.x, config.y)

	// Apply viewBox normalization if the root has viewBox data
	if root_data, ok := element.data.(SVG_Root_Data);
	   ok && root_data.viewbox[2] > 0 && root_data.viewbox[3] > 0 {
		vb := root_data.viewbox

		// 1. Map viewport dimensions to unit scale
		scale_x := config.width / vb[2]
		scale_y := config.height / vb[3]

		// Use uniform scaling (meet) if requested or by default
		if config.preserve_aspect {
			s := math.min(scale_x, scale_y)
			scale_x = s
			scale_y = s
		}

		g2d_scale(scale_x, scale_y)

		// 2. Translate to account for viewBox origin
		g2d_translate(-vb[0], -vb[1])
		when logging.DEBUG {
			logging.debug(
				.SVG,
				fmt.tprintf(
					"[ASSET]Applied viewBox transform: scale_x=%f, scale_y=%f, origin=[%f, %f]",
					scale_x,
					scale_y,
					vb[0],
					vb[1],
				),
			)
		} //end LOGGING.DEBUG	
	} else {
		// Legacy behavior: just scale by raw width/height (risky)
		g2d_scale(config.width, config.height)
	}

	// Apply tint if specified
	if config.tint_color[0] != 1.0 ||
	   config.tint_color[1] != 1.0 ||
	   config.tint_color[2] != 1.0 ||
	   config.tint_color[3] != 1.0 ||
	   config.opacity != 1.0 {
		// TODO: Implement tinting and opacity for SVG
		logging.warn(.Renderer, "SVG tinting/opacity not yet implemented")
	}

	// Draw the SVG element
	g2d_draw_svg_element(element^)

	return true
}

// Render Asset Smart (New Architecture)
// Intelligent rendering with automatic fallback and background loading
renderAssetSmart :: proc(
	path: string,
	asset_type: GPU_Asset_Type,
	config: GPU_Asset_Render_Config,
) -> bool {
	if asset_manager == nil {
		logging.error(.Asset, "Asset manager not initialized")
		return false
	}

	// 1. Check if asset is already loaded and parsed
	if asset_id, exists := asset_manager.path_to_id[path]; exists {
		if asset, found := asset_manager.assets[asset_id]; found {
			switch asset.state {
			case .Parsed:
				// Asset is ready - create render handle and render
				render_handle := GPU_RenderHandle {
					id         = asset_id,
					asset_type = asset_type,
					timestamp  = time.to_unix_nanoseconds(time.now()),
				}

				// Copy parse result based on type
				#partial switch v in asset.parse_result {
				case string:
					logging.error(.Asset, fmt.tprintf("Asset in Error state: %s", v))
					if config.allow_sync_fallback {
						return renderErrorPlaceholderNew(config, v)
					}
					return false
				}

				return renderAssetNew(render_handle, config)

			case .Parsing:
				// Asset is currently being parsed - show loading placeholder
				logging.info(
					.Asset,
					fmt.tprintf("Asset %d is still parsing, showing placeholder", asset_id),
				)
				return renderLoadingPlaceholderNew(config)

			case .Error:
				// Asset failed to parse - show error placeholder
				error_msg := ""
				#partial switch &v in asset.parse_result {
				case string:
					error_msg = v
				}

				logging.error(.Asset, fmt.tprintf("Asset in Error state: %s", error_msg))
				if config.allow_sync_fallback {
					return renderErrorPlaceholderNew(config, error_msg)
				}
				return false

			case .Raw:
				// Asset loaded but not parsed - parse it now if allowed
				logging.info(.Asset, fmt.tprintf("Asset %d is raw, parsing now", asset_id))

				if config.allow_sync_fallback {
					// Try to parse synchronously
					gpu_loading_handle := GPU_LoadingHandle {
						id         = asset_id,
						data       = asset.data,
						path       = path,
						asset_type = asset_type,
						timestamp  = time.to_unix_nanoseconds(time.now()),
					}

					render_handle, parse_error := parseAssetNew(gpu_loading_handle)
					if parse_error == "" {
						return renderAssetNew(render_handle, config)
					} else {
						logging.error(.Asset, fmt.tprintf("Sync parse failed: %s", parse_error))
						return renderErrorPlaceholderNew(config, parse_error)
					}
				} else {
					// Trigger background parsing and show loading placeholder
					triggerBackgroundLoadingNew(path, asset_type)
					return renderLoadingPlaceholder(config)
				}
			}
		}
	}

	// 2. Not loaded - trigger background loading
	logging.info(.Asset, fmt.tprintf("Asset not found, triggering background loading: %s", path))
	triggerBackgroundLoadingNew(path, asset_type)

	// 3. For immediate needs, do synchronous load/parse if allowed
	if config.allow_sync_fallback {
		logging.info(.Asset, "Synchronous fallback enabled, loading and parsing now")

		// Load the asset
		loading_handle, load_error := loadAssetNew(path, asset_type)
		if load_error != "" {
			logging.error(.Asset, fmt.tprintf("Sync load failed: %s", load_error))
			return renderErrorPlaceholderNew(config, load_error)
		}

		// Parse the asset
		render_handle, parse_error := parseAssetNew(loading_handle)
		if parse_error != "" {
			logging.error(.Asset, fmt.tprintf("Sync parse failed: %s", parse_error))
			return renderErrorPlaceholderNew(config, parse_error)
		}

		// Render the asset
		return renderAssetNew(render_handle, config)
	}

	// 4. If async-only, show loading placeholder
	logging.info(.Asset, "Async-only mode, showing loading placeholder")
	return renderLoadingPlaceholderNew(config)
}

// Trigger Background Loading (New Architecture)
// Triggers background loading of an asset (stub for now)
triggerBackgroundLoadingNew :: proc(path: string, asset_type: GPU_Asset_Type) {
	if !asset_manager.config.enable_background_loading {
		return
	}

	// TODO: Implement actual background loading
	logging.info(.Asset, fmt.tprintf("Background loading triggered for: %s", path))

	// For now, just load it synchronously in the background
	// In a real implementation, this would use a worker thread/queue
	loading_handle, load_error := loadAssetNew(path, asset_type)
	if load_error != "" {
		logging.error(.Asset, fmt.tprintf("Background load failed: %s", load_error))
		return
	}

	// Parse it in the background
	_, parse_error := parseAssetNew(loading_handle)
	if parse_error != "" {
		logging.error(.Asset, fmt.tprintf("Background parse failed: %s", parse_error))
	}
}

// Render Loading Placeholder (New Architecture)
// Renders a placeholder for an asset that is still loading
renderLoadingPlaceholderNew :: proc(config: GPU_Asset_Render_Config) -> bool {
	// Draw a simple loading indicator
	g2d_fill_rect(
		config.x,
		config.y,
		config.width,
		config.height,
		renderer.paint_solid([4]f32{0.2, 0.2, 0.2, 1.0}),
	)

	// Draw loading text
	g2d_draw_text(
		config.x + 10,
		config.y + 20,
		"Loading...",
		renderer.Font_Handle_Invalid, // TODO: Use proper font
		[4]f32{1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0},
	)

	return true
}

// Render Error Placeholder (New Architecture)
// Renders a placeholder for an asset that failed to load
renderErrorPlaceholderNew :: proc(config: GPU_Asset_Render_Config, error_msg: string) -> bool {
	// Draw error background
	g2d_fill_rect(
		config.x,
		config.y,
		config.width,
		config.height,
		renderer.paint_solid([4]f32{0.8, 0.2, 0.2, 1.0}),
	)

	// Draw error text
	g2d_draw_text(
		config.x + 10,
		config.y + 20,
		fmt.tprintf("Error: %s", error_msg),
		renderer.Font_Handle_Invalid, // TODO: Use proper font
		[4]f32{1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0},
	)

	return true
}

// Private: Render RSD Asset (New Architecture)
// Renders an RSD drawing using the provided configuration
@(private)
_renderRsdAssetNew :: proc(drawing: ^Drawing, config: GPU_Asset_Render_Config) -> bool {
	// Create render config
	// Note: resolve_image and resolve_font are left nil intentionally.
	// Calling proc literals stored in struct fields via call_indirect on WASM causes
	// "function signature mismatch" due to multi-return ABI. The nil guards at the
	// call sites (cfg.resolve_font != nil, cfg.resolve_image != nil) handle this safely.
	rsd_config: Render_Config

	// Save current matrix
	g2d_push_matrix()
	defer g2d_pop_matrix()

	// Apply transformation
	g2d_translate(config.x, config.y)

	// Normalize RSD coordinate space to target width/height
	vw := drawing.width
	vh := drawing.height
	vx, vy: f32 = 0, 0

	if drawing.view_box.z > 0 && drawing.view_box.w > 0 {
		vx = drawing.view_box.x
		vy = drawing.view_box.y
		vw = drawing.view_box.z
		vh = drawing.view_box.w
	}

	if vw > 0 && vh > 0 {
		scale_x := config.width / vw
		scale_y := config.height / vh

		if config.preserve_aspect {
			s := math.min(scale_x, scale_y)
			scale_x = s
			scale_y = s
		}

		g2d_scale(scale_x, scale_y)

		// Translate based on viewBox origin and center if aspect ratio preserved
		if config.preserve_aspect {
			ox := (config.width - vw * scale_x) * 0.5
			oy := (config.height - vh * scale_y) * 0.5
			g2d_translate(ox / scale_x - vx, oy / scale_y - vy)
		} else {
			g2d_translate(-vx, -vy)
		}
	} else {
		g2d_scale(config.width, config.height)
		logging.warn(
			.Renderer,
			fmt.tprintf(
				"[RSD_RENDER] Normalized scaling failed: drawing.width=%.1f, using direct config scale=%.1f",
				drawing.width,
				config.width,
			),
		)
	}

	// Apply tint if specified
	if config.tint_color[0] != 1.0 ||
	   config.tint_color[1] != 1.0 ||
	   config.tint_color[2] != 1.0 ||
	   config.tint_color[3] != 1.0 ||
	   config.opacity != 1.0 {
		// TODO: Implement tinting and opacity for RSD
		logging.warn(.Renderer, "RSD tinting/opacity not yet implemented")
	}

	// Draw the RSD drawing using unified rendering
	when logging.DEBUG_RSD {
		logging.info(.RSD, "[RSD_RENDER] Starting RSD drawing rendering", key = "rsd_render_start")
	}
	draw_drawing_unified(drawing, rsd_config)

	when logging.DEBUG_RSD {
		logging.info(.RSD, "[RSD_RENDER] Completed RSD drawing rendering", key = "rsd_render_complete")
	}

	return true
}

DEBUG: Source length: 22467
DEBUG: Source content: package graphics

/**
@documentation: RSD to SVG Export (Phase 10C.12)
- Serializes RSD (RuiShin Drawing) model back to SVG XML format
- Limited subset: basic shapes, paths, text, gradients, styles, transforms
- Conservative design: no global state, pure functions
- Uses string builder for SVG XML generation (no external XML writer dependency)

Export Mapping:
- RSD Drawing → <svg> root element
- RSD Layer → <g> group element
- RSD Path_Item → <path> or shape elements (<rect>, <circle>, <ellipse>, <line>)
- RSD Text_Item → <text> element
- RSD Group_Item → nested <g> element
- RSD Gradient → <linearGradient> or <radialGradient> in <defs>

Style Mapping:
- Fill_Style → fill attribute (solid color or url(#gradient-id))
- Stroke_Style → stroke, stroke-width, stroke-linecap, stroke-linejoin, stroke-dasharray, stroke-dashoffset
- Fill_Rule → fill-rule attribute
- matrix[3,3]f32 → transform attribute

Known Limitations:
- Image_Item not yet exported (deferred to later phase)
- Gradient spread methods (pad, reflect, repeat) not yet exported (pad only)
- Complex path commands (H, V, S, T) not yet optimized
- No viewBox/preserveAspectRatio export (uses width/height only)
- No meta data export
*/

import "core:strings"
import "core:fmt"
import "core:math"
import "core:math/linalg"
import "core:mem"
import "project:renderer"

// ============================================================================
// Export Context
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: RSD to SVG Export Context
- Holds state during RSD to SVG conversion
- Tracks gradient IDs for generating unique references
- Collects warnings during export
*/
RSD_To_SVG_Context :: struct {
	allocator: mem.Allocator,
	warnings:  [dynamic]string,
	gradient_id_counter: u32,
}

// ============================================================================
// Main Entry Point
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: Convert RSD Drawing to SVG String
- Main entry point for RSD to SVG export
- Takes RSD Drawing structure and converts to SVG XML string
- Returns SVG string and any warnings
- Phase 10C.12: Export skeleton implementation
*/
rsd_to_svg_string :: proc(drawing: Drawing, allocator: mem.Allocator) -> (string, [dynamic]string) {
	ctx: RSD_To_SVG_Context
	ctx.allocator = allocator
	ctx.warnings = make([dynamic]string)
	ctx.gradient_id_counter = 0

	builder := strings.builder_make(allocator)
	defer strings.builder_destroy(&builder)

	// Serialize drawing to SVG
	serialize_drawing(&builder, drawing, &ctx)

	svg_string := strings.to_string(builder)
	return svg_string, ctx.warnings
}

// ============================================================================
// Drawing Serialization
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: Serialize RSD Drawing to SVG <svg> Element
- Wraps all content in <svg> root element
- Includes width, height, and xmlns attributes
- Collects gradients in <defs> section
- Serializes layers as groups
*/
serialize_drawing :: proc(builder: ^strings.Builder, drawing: Drawing, ctx: ^RSD_To_SVG_Context) {
	// Start <svg> element
	fmt.sbprintf(builder, `<svg width="%.2f" height="%.2f" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">`, drawing.width, drawing.height)

	// Serialize gradients in <defs> if any exist
	if len(drawing.gradients) > 0 {
		fmt.sbprint(builder, "\n  <defs>")
		for i := 0; i < len(drawing.gradients); i += 1 {
			serialize_gradient(builder, drawing.gradients[i], ctx)
		}
		fmt.sbprint(builder, "\n  </defs>")
	}

	// Serialize layers
	for i := 0; i < len(drawing.layers); i += 1 {
		layer := drawing.layers[i]
		if layer.visible {
			serialize_layer(builder, layer, ctx, 1)
		}
	}

	// End <svg> element
	fmt.sbprint(builder, "\n</svg>")
}

// ============================================================================
// Layer Serialization
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: Serialize RSD Layer to SVG <g> Element
- Converts layer to SVG group element
- Includes opacity and transform attributes
- Serializes all items in the layer
- indent parameter controls XML indentation
*/
serialize_layer :: proc(builder: ^strings.Builder, layer: Layer, ctx: ^RSD_To_SVG_Context, indent: int) {
	indent_str := make_indent_string(indent)

	// Start <g> element
	fmt.sbprintf(builder, "\n%s<g", indent_str)

	// Add opacity if not 1.0
	if layer.opacity != 1.0 {
		fmt.sbprintf(builder, ` opacity="%.3f"`, layer.opacity)
	}

	// Add transform if not identity
	if !is_identity_matrix(layer.transform) {
		transform_str := serialize_transform(layer.transform)
		fmt.sbprintf(builder, ` transform="%s"`, transform_str)
	}

	fmt.sbprint(builder, ">")

	// Serialize items
	for i := 0; i < len(layer.items); i += 1 {
		serialize_item(builder, layer.items[i], ctx, indent + 1)
	}

	// End <g> element
	fmt.sbprintf(builder, "\n%s</g>", indent_str)
}

// ============================================================================
// Item Serialization
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: Serialize RSD Item to SVG Element
- Dispatches to item-specific serialization based on type
- Handles Path_Item, Text_Item, Group_Item
- Image_Item not yet supported (logged as warning)
*/
serialize_item :: proc(builder: ^strings.Builder, item: Item, ctx: ^RSD_To_SVG_Context, indent: int) {
	switch v in item {
	case Path_Item:
		serialize_path_item(builder, v, ctx, indent)
	case Text_Item:
		serialize_text_item(builder, v, ctx, indent)
	case Group_Item:
		serialize_group_item(builder, v, ctx, indent)
	case Image_Item:
		append(&ctx.warnings, "rsd_to_svg: Image_Item export not yet supported")
	}
}

// ============================================================================
// Path Item Serialization
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: Serialize RSD Path_Item to SVG Element
- Converts Path_Item to <path> element with d attribute
- Includes fill, stroke, fill-rule, and transform attributes
- Handles all path commands: Move, Line, Quad, Cubic, Arc, Close
*/
serialize_path_item :: proc(builder: ^strings.Builder, path_item: Path_Item, ctx: ^RSD_To_SVG_Context, indent: int) {
	indent_str := make_indent_string(indent)

	// Start <path> element
	fmt.sbprintf(builder, "\n%s<path", indent_str)

	// Serialize path data to d attribute
	path_data := serialize_path_commands(path_item.commands, path_item.points)
	fmt.sbprintf(builder, ` d="%s"`, path_data)

	// Serialize fill style
	fill_attr := serialize_fill_style(path_item.fill, ctx)
	if len(fill_attr) > 0 {
		fmt.sbprintf(builder, ` %s`, fill_attr)
	}

	// Serialize stroke style
	stroke_attrs := serialize_stroke_style(path_item.stroke)
	if len(stroke_attrs) > 0 {
		fmt.sbprintf(builder, ` %s`, stroke_attrs)
	}

	// Serialize fill-rule
	if path_item.fill_rule != .NonZero {
		fill_rule_str := serialize_fill_rule(path_item.fill_rule)
		fmt.sbprintf(builder, ` %s`, fill_rule_str)
	}

	// Serialize transform if not identity
	if !is_identity_matrix(path_item.transform) {
		transform_str := serialize_transform(path_item.transform)
		fmt.sbprintf(builder, ` transform="%s"`, transform_str)
	}

	// End <path> element (self-closing)
	fmt.sbprint(builder, "/>")
}

// ============================================================================
// Text Item Serialization
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: Serialize RSD Text_Item to SVG <text> Element
- Converts Text_Item to <text> element
- Includes x, y, font-size, fill, text-anchor, and transform attributes
- Text content is escaped for XML safety
*/
serialize_text_item :: proc(builder: ^strings.Builder, text_item: Text_Item, ctx: ^RSD_To_SVG_Context, indent: int) {
	indent_str := make_indent_string(indent)

	// Extract position from transform (translation component)
	x, y := extract_translation_from_transform(text_item.transform)

	// Start <text> element
	fmt.sbprintf(builder, "\n%s<text", indent_str)

	// Add position
	fmt.sbprintf(builder, ` x="%.2f" y="%.2f"`, x, y)

	// Phase 10D.4.2: Add comprehensive font attributes
	
	// Add font-size
	fmt.sbprintf(builder, ` font-size="%.2f"`, text_item.font.size)
	
	// Add font-family
	if text_item.font.name != "" && text_item.font.name != "default" {
		fmt.sbprintf(builder, ` font-family="%s"`, text_item.font.name)
	}
	
	// Add font-weight
	if text_item.font.weight == .Bold {
		fmt.sbprintf(builder, ` font-weight="bold"`)
	}
	
	// Add font-style
	if text_item.font.style == .Italic {
		fmt.sbprintf(builder, ` font-style="italic"`)
	}

	// Add fill color
	fill_color_str := serialize_color(text_item.color)
	fmt.sbprintf(builder, ` fill="%s"`, fill_color_str)
	
	// Add opacity if not default
	if text_item.opacity != 1.0 {
		fmt.sbprintf(builder, ` opacity="%.2f"`, text_item.opacity)
	}

	// Add text-anchor
	if text_item.anchor != .Start {
		anchor_str := serialize_text_anchor(text_item.anchor)
		fmt.sbprintf(builder, ` %s`, anchor_str)
	}

	// Serialize transform if not identity (excluding translation which is already applied)
	if !is_identity_matrix(text_item.transform) {
		transform_str := serialize_transform(text_item.transform)
		fmt.sbprintf(builder, ` transform="%s"`, transform_str)
	}

	fmt.sbprint(builder, ">")

	// Add text content (escaped for XML)
	escaped_text := escape_xml_text(text_item.text)
	fmt.sbprint(builder, escaped_text)

	// End <text> element
	fmt.sbprint(builder, "</text>")
}

// ============================================================================
// Group Item Serialization
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: Serialize RSD Group_Item to SVG <g> Element
- Converts Group_Item to nested SVG group element
- Includes transform attribute
- Serializes all child items recursively
*/
serialize_group_item :: proc(builder: ^strings.Builder, group_item: Group_Item, ctx: ^RSD_To_SVG_Context, indent: int) {
	indent_str := make_indent_string(indent)

	// Start <g> element
	fmt.sbprintf(builder, "\n%s<g", indent_str)

	// Add transform if not identity
	if !is_identity_matrix(group_item.transform) {
		transform_str := serialize_transform(group_item.transform)
		fmt.sbprintf(builder, ` transform="%s"`, transform_str)
	}

	fmt.sbprint(builder, ">")

	// Serialize child items
	for i := 0; i < len(group_item.items); i += 1 {
		serialize_item(builder, group_item.items[i], ctx, indent + 1)
	}

	// End <g> element
	fmt.sbprintf(builder, "\n%s</g>", indent_str)
}

// ============================================================================
// Gradient Serialization
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: Serialize RSD Gradient to SVG Gradient Element
- Converts Gradient to <linearGradient> or <radialGradient>
- Includes id, gradientUnits, and gradientTransform attributes
- Serializes all stops as <stop> elements
- Generates unique gradient ID for referencing
*/
serialize_gradient :: proc(builder: ^strings.Builder, gradient: Gradient, ctx: ^RSD_To_SVG_Context) {
	// Generate gradient ID
	grad_id := fmt.tprintf("grad%d", ctx.gradient_id_counter)
	ctx.gradient_id_counter += 1

	indent_str := "    "

	// Start gradient element
	if gradient.type == .Linear {
		fmt.sbprintf(builder, "\n%s<linearGradient id=\"%s\"", indent_str, grad_id)
	} else {
		fmt.sbprintf(builder, "\n%s<radialGradient id=\"%s\"", indent_str, grad_id)
	}

	// Add gradientUnits
	if gradient.units != .UserSpaceOnUse {
		units_str := serialize_gradient_units(gradient.units)
		fmt.sbprintf(builder, ` %s`, units_str)
	}

	// Add gradientTransform if not identity
	if !is_identity_matrix(gradient.transform) {
		transform_str := serialize_transform(gradient.transform)
		fmt.sbprintf(builder, ` gradientTransform="%s"`, transform_str)
	}

	fmt.sbprint(builder, ">")

	// Add gradient-specific attributes
	if gradient.type == .Linear {
		fmt.sbprintf(builder, ` x1="%.2f" y1="%.2f" x2="%.2f" y2="%.2f"`,
			gradient.p0[0], gradient.p0[1], gradient.p1[0], gradient.p1[1])
	} else {
		// Radial gradient: p0 is center, p1 is focal point (x,y) and radius (stored in p1[1])
		fmt.sbprintf(builder, ` cx="%.2f" cy="%.2f" r="%.2f" fx="%.2f" fy="%.2f"`,
			gradient.p0[0], gradient.p0[1], gradient.p1[1], gradient.p1[0], gradient.p1[1])
	}

	// Serialize stops
	for i := 0; i < len(gradient.stops); i += 1 {
		stop := gradient.stops[i]
		stop_color_str := serialize_color(stop.color)
		fmt.sbprintf(builder, "\n%s  <stop offset=\"%.3f\" stop-color=\"%s\" />", indent_str, stop.t, stop_color_str)
	}

	// End gradient element
	if gradient.type == .Linear {
		fmt.sbprint(builder, "\n    </linearGradient>")
	} else {
		fmt.sbprint(builder, "\n    </radialGradient>")
	}
}

// ============================================================================
// Path Command Serialization
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: Serialize Path Commands to SVG Path Data String
- Converts RSD SVG_Path_Command array to SVG path data (d attribute)
- Supports: M (Move), L (Line), Q (Quad), C (Cubic), A (Arc), Z (Close)
- Uses absolute coordinates for simplicity
*/
serialize_path_commands :: proc(commands: []Path_Command, points: [][2]f32) -> string {
	builder := strings.builder_make()
	defer strings.builder_destroy(&builder)

	point_index := 0

	for i := 0; i < len(commands); i += 1 {
		cmd := commands[i]

		switch cmd {
		case .Move:
			if point_index + 1 < len(points) {
				fmt.sbprintf(&builder, "M %.2f %.2f", points[point_index][0], points[point_index][1])
				point_index += 1
			}
		case .Line:
			if point_index + 1 < len(points) {
				fmt.sbprintf(&builder, " L %.2f %.2f", points[point_index][0], points[point_index][1])
				point_index += 1
			}
		case .Quad:
			if point_index + 2 < len(points) {
				fmt.sbprintf(&builder, " Q %.2f %.2f %.2f %.2f",
					points[point_index][0], points[point_index][1],
					points[point_index + 1][0], points[point_index + 1][1])
				point_index += 2
			}
		case .Cubic:
			if point_index + 3 < len(points) {
				fmt.sbprintf(&builder, " C %.2f %.2f %.2f %.2f %.2f %.2f",
					points[point_index][0], points[point_index][1],
					points[point_index + 1][0], points[point_index + 1][1],
					points[point_index + 2][0], points[point_index + 2][1])
				point_index += 3
			}
		case .Arc:
			if point_index + 3 < len(points) {
				// Arc command: rx, ry, x-axis-rotation, large-arc-flag, sweep-flag, x, y
				// RSD stores arc parameters in points array
				fmt.sbprintf(&builder, " A %.2f %.2f 0 0 1 %.2f %.2f",
					points[point_index][0], points[point_index][1], // rx, ry
					points[point_index + 2][0], points[point_index + 2][1]) // x, y
				point_index += 3
			}
		case .Close:
			fmt.sbprint(&builder, " Z")
		}
	}

	return strings.to_string(builder)
}

// ============================================================================
// Style Serialization Helpers
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: Serialize Fill_Style to SVG fill Attribute
- Converts Solid_Color to hex color string
- Converts Gradient_Ref to url(#gradient-id) reference
- Returns empty string for None (no fill)
*/
serialize_fill_style :: proc(fill: Fill_Style, ctx: ^RSD_To_SVG_Context) -> string {
	switch v in fill {
	case Solid_Color:
		color_str := serialize_color(v)
		return fmt.tprintf(`fill="%s"`, color_str)
	case Gradient_Ref:
		grad_id := fmt.tprintf("grad%d", v.id)
		return fmt.tprintf(`fill="url(#%s)"`, grad_id)
	case Theme_Ref:  // Phase 10C2: Export using fallback color (SVG doesn't support theme refs)
		color_str := serialize_color(v.fallback)
		return fmt.tprintf(`fill="%s"`, color_str)
	case Theme_Gradient_Ref:  // Phase 10C2.2: Export using fallback gradient
		// Reference the fallback gradient by ID
		grad_id := fmt.tprintf("grad%d", v.fallback.id)
		return fmt.tprintf(`fill="url(#%s)"`, grad_id)
	case None:
		return `fill="none"`
	}
	return ""
}

/**
@documentation: Serialize Stroke_Style to SVG Stroke Attributes
- Converts stroke color, width, linecap, linejoin, dasharray, dashoffset
- Returns empty string if stroke width is 0
*/
serialize_stroke_style :: proc(stroke: Stroke_Style) -> string {
	if stroke.width <= 0.0 {
		return ""
	}

	builder := strings.builder_make()
	defer strings.builder_destroy(&builder)

	// Stroke color
	color_str := serialize_color(stroke.color)
	fmt.sbprintf(&builder, `stroke="%s"`, color_str)

	// Stroke width
	fmt.sbprintf(&builder, ` stroke-width="%.2f"`, stroke.width)

	// Stroke linecap
	cap_str := serialize_line_cap(stroke.cap)
	fmt.sbprintf(&builder, ` %s`, cap_str)

	// Stroke linejoin
	join_str := serialize_line_join(stroke.join)
	fmt.sbprintf(&builder, ` %s`, join_str)

	// Stroke dasharray
	if len(stroke.dash_array) > 0 {
		dasharray_str := serialize_dasharray(stroke.dash_array)
		fmt.sbprintf(&builder, ` %s`, dasharray_str)
	}

	// Stroke dashoffset
	if stroke.dash_offset != 0.0 {
		fmt.sbprintf(&builder, ` stroke-dashoffset="%.2f"`, stroke.dash_offset)
	}

	return strings.to_string(builder)
}

/**
@documentation: Serialize RGBA Color to Hex String
- Converts [4]f32 color to #RRGGBB hex format
- Ignores alpha channel for MVP (SVG 1.1 limited alpha support)
*/
serialize_color :: proc(color: [4]f32) -> string {
	r := u8(color[0] * 255.0)
	g := u8(color[1] * 255.0)
	b := u8(color[2] * 255.0)
	return fmt.tprintf("#%02X%02X%02X", r, g, b)
}

/**
@documentation: Serialize Fill_Rule to SVG fill-rule Attribute
- Converts Fill_Rule enum to SVG fill-rule string
*/
serialize_fill_rule :: proc(fill_rule: Fill_Rule) -> string {
	switch fill_rule {
	case .NonZero:
		return `fill-rule="nonzero"`
	case .EvenOdd:
		return `fill-rule="evenodd"`
	}
	return ""
}

/**
@documentation: Serialize Text_Anchor to SVG text-anchor Attribute
- Converts Text_Anchor enum to SVG text-anchor string
*/
serialize_text_anchor :: proc(anchor: Text_Anchor) -> string {
	switch anchor {
	case .Start:
		return `text-anchor="start"`
	case .Middle:
		return `text-anchor="middle"`
	case .End:
		return `text-anchor="end"`
	}
	return ""
}

/**
@documentation: Serialize Line_Cap to SVG stroke-linecap Attribute
- Converts Line_Cap enum to SVG stroke-linecap string
*/
serialize_line_cap :: proc(cap: renderer.Line_Cap) -> string {
	switch cap {
	case .Butt:
		return `stroke-linecap="butt"`
	case .Round:
		return `stroke-linecap="round"`
	case .Square:
		return `stroke-linecap="square"`
	}
	return ""
}

/**
@documentation: Serialize Line_Join to SVG stroke-linejoin Attribute
- Converts Line_Join enum to SVG stroke-linejoin string
*/
serialize_line_join :: proc(join: renderer.Line_Join) -> string {
	switch join {
	case .Miter:
		return `stroke-linejoin="miter"`
	case .Round:
		return `stroke-linejoin="round"`
	case .Bevel:
		return `stroke-linejoin="bevel"`
	}
	return ""
}

/**
@documentation: Serialize Dash Array to SVG stroke-dasharray Attribute
- Converts []f32 dash array to space-separated string
*/
serialize_dasharray :: proc(dasharray: []f32) -> string {
	if len(dasharray) == 0 {
		return ""
	}

	builder := strings.builder_make()
	defer strings.builder_destroy(&builder)

	for i := 0; i < len(dasharray); i += 1 {
		if i > 0 {
			fmt.sbprint(&builder, " ")
		}
		fmt.sbprintf(&builder, "%.2f", dasharray[i])
	}

	return fmt.tprintf(`stroke-dasharray="%s"`, strings.to_string(builder))
}

/**
@documentation: Serialize Gradient_Units to SVG gradientUnits Attribute
- Converts Gradient_Units enum to SVG gradientUnits string
*/
serialize_gradient_units :: proc(units: Gradient_Units) -> string {
	switch units {
	case .UserSpaceOnUse:
		return `gradientUnits="userSpaceOnUse"`
	case .ObjectBoundingBox:
		return `gradientUnits="objectBoundingBox"`
	}
	return ""
}

// ============================================================================
// Transform Serialization
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: Serialize Matrix to SVG transform Attribute
- Converts matrix[3,3]f32 to SVG transform matrix() function
- Format: matrix(a,b,c,d,e,f) where a-f are matrix elements
*/
serialize_transform :: proc(transform: matrix[3,3]f32) -> string {
	// SVG transform matrix uses column-major order: matrix(a,b,c,d,e,f)
	// where the matrix is:
	// [a c e]
	// [b d f]
	// [0 0 1]
	return fmt.tprintf("matrix(%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f)",
		transform[0][0], transform[1][0], // a, b
		transform[0][1], transform[1][1], // c, d
		transform[0][2], transform[1][2]) // e, f
}

// ============================================================================
// Utility Functions
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: Check if Matrix is Identity
- Returns true if matrix is identity (no transform)
*/
is_identity_matrix :: proc(m: matrix[3,3]f32) -> bool {
	identity := linalg.MATRIX3F32_IDENTITY
	return m == identity
}

/**
@documentation: Extract Translation from Transform Matrix
- Extracts x, y translation from matrix[3,3]f32
- Used for text positioning
*/
extract_translation_from_transform :: proc(transform: matrix[3,3]f32) -> (f32, f32) {
	x := transform[0][2]
	y := transform[1][2]
	return x, y
}

/**
@documentation: Make Indentation String
- Creates a string of spaces for XML indentation
- indent parameter specifies number of indentation levels (2 spaces per level)
*/
make_indent_string :: proc(indent: int) -> string {
	spaces := make([]byte, indent * 2)
	for i := 0; i < len(spaces); i += 1 {
		spaces[i] = ' '
	}
	return string(spaces)
}

/**
@documentation: Escape Text for XML Safety
- Escapes special XML characters: <, >, &, ", '
- Returns escaped string safe for XML content
*/
escape_xml_text :: proc(text: string) -> string {
	builder := strings.builder_make()
	defer strings.builder_destroy(&builder)

	for i := 0; i < len(text); i += 1 {
		r := text[i]
		switch r {
		case '<':
			fmt.sbprint(&builder, "&lt;")
		case '>':
			fmt.sbprint(&builder, "&gt;")
		case '&':
			fmt.sbprint(&builder, "&amp;")
		case '"':
			fmt.sbprint(&builder, "&quot;")
		case '\'':
			fmt.sbprint(&builder, "&apos;")
		case:
			fmt.sbprintf(&builder, "%c", r)
		}
	}

	return strings.to_string(builder)
}

import "project:renderer"


/**
@documentation: RSD/Model
- In-memory representation of the RuiShin Drawing (RSD) format.
- Aligned with g2d API for direct rendering.
- Coordinates are stored as f32 (pixels).
- Colors are stored as [4]f32 (normalized RGBA).
*/

Drawing :: struct {
	width:           f32,
	height:          f32,
	dpi:             f32,
	units:           Units,
	background:      [4]f32,
	view_box:        [4]f32, // x, y, width, height (Phase 11.3)
	layers:          []Layer,
	gradients:       []Gradient,
	images:          []Image_Blob,
	meta:            []Meta_Entry,
	clip_paths:      []Clip_Path_Def,
	masks:           []Mask_Def,
	format_version:  u16,
	warnings:        []string,
	transform_cache: Transform_Cache, // Phase 10D.3.2: Transform caching
}

Units :: enum u8 {
	Pixels = 0,
}

// Phase 10D.3.2: Transform Cache for efficient reuse
Transform_Cache :: struct {
	transforms: map[u32]matrix[3, 3]f32, // ID -> Transform matrix
}

Layer :: struct {
	name:      string,
	visible:   bool,
	opacity:   f32,
	transform: matrix[3, 3]f32,
	items:     []Item,
}

Item :: union {
	Path_Item,
	Text_Item,
	Image_Item,
	Group_Item,
}

Path_Item :: struct {
	commands:     []Path_Command,
	points:       [][2]f32,
	fill_rule:    Fill_Rule,
	fill:         Fill_Style,
	stroke:       Stroke_Style,
	transform:    matrix[3, 3]f32,
	clip_path_id: u32, // 0 = none
	mask_id:      u32, // 0 = none
}

Path_Command :: enum u8 {
	Move  = 0x01,
	Line  = 0x02,
	Quad  = 0x03,
	Cubic = 0x04,
	Arc   = 0x05,
	Close = 0x06,
}

Fill_Rule :: enum u8 {
	NonZero = 0,
	EvenOdd = 1,
}

Fill_Style :: union {
	Solid_Color,
	Gradient_Ref,
	Theme_Ref, // Phase 10C2: Theme color reference
	Theme_Gradient_Ref, // Phase 10C2.2: Theme gradient reference
	None,
}

None :: struct {}

Solid_Color :: [4]f32

Gradient_Ref :: struct {
	id: u32,
}

/**
@documentation: Theme Reference (Phase 10C2)
- References a theme color token by path.
- Used in RSD to enable runtime theme switching.
- Fallback color used if token not found or invalid.
*/
Theme_Ref :: struct {
	token:    string, // Theme token path (e.g., "colors.primary", "button.background")
	fallback: [4]f32, // Fallback RGBA color if token invalid or not found
}

/**
@documentation: Theme Gradient Reference (Phase 10C2.2)
- References a theme gradient preset by token path.
- Enables theme-defined gradients for reuse across assets.
- Fallback gradient used if token not found.
*/
Theme_Gradient_Ref :: struct {
	token:    string, // Theme gradient token (e.g., "gradients.primary_button")
	fallback: Gradient, // Fallback gradient if token invalid
}

Stroke_Style :: struct {
	color:       [4]f32, // Resolved color (fallback if theme_token not set)
	theme_token: string, // Theme token path for runtime resolution (10C2.8)
	width:       f32,
	join:        renderer.Line_Join,
	cap:         renderer.Line_Cap,
	miter_limit: f32,
	dash_array:  []f32,
	dash_offset: f32,
}

Text_Item :: struct {
	text:         string,
	font:         Font_Info,
	color:        [4]f32,
	anchor:       Text_Anchor,
	line_height:  f32,
	transform:    matrix[3, 3]f32,
	opacity:      f32,
	clip_path_id: u32,
	mask_id:      u32,
}

// Phase 10D.4.2: Font weight and style enums
Font_Weight :: enum u8 {
	Normal = 0,
	Bold   = 1,
}

Font_Style :: enum u8 {
	Normal = 0,
	Italic = 1,
}

Font_Info :: struct {
	name:   string,
	size:   f32,
	weight: Font_Weight,
	style:  Font_Style,
}

Text_Anchor :: enum u8 {
	Start  = 0,
	Middle = 1,
	End    = 2,
}

Image_Item :: struct {
	source:       Image_Source,
	dest_rect:    [4]f32, // x, y, w, h
	src_rect:     [4]f32, // optional, all zeros if not used
	tint:         [4]f32,
	transform:    matrix[3, 3]f32,
	clip_path_id: u32,
	mask_id:      u32,
}

Image_Source :: union {
	Embedded_Image,
	External_Path,
}

Embedded_Image :: struct {
	id: u32,
}

External_Path :: string

Group_Item :: struct {
	transform:    matrix[3, 3]f32,
	items:        []Item,
	clip_path_id: u32,
	mask_id:      u32,
}

/**
@documentation: Gradient (Phase 10A)
- Linear or radial gradient definition
- Phase 10C2.2: Also used as fallback in Theme_Gradient_Ref
*/
Gradient :: struct {
	id:        u32,
	type:      Gradient_Type,
	units:     Gradient_Units,
	p0, p1:    [2]f32,
	fx, fy:    f32, // Phase 10D.3.1: Focal point for radial gradients
	stops:     []Gradient_Stop,
	transform: matrix[3, 3]f32,
}

Gradient_Type :: enum u8 {
	Linear = 0,
	Radial = 1,
}

Gradient_Units :: enum u8 {
	UserSpaceOnUse    = 0,
	ObjectBoundingBox = 1,
}

Gradient_Stop :: struct {
	t:     f32,
	color: [4]f32,
}

// Phase 12: Clip and Mask Definitions
Clip_Path_Def :: struct {
	id:    u32,
	items: []Item,
}

Mask_Def :: struct {
	id:    u32,
	items: []Item,
}

Meta_Entry :: struct {
	key:   string,
	value: string,
}

Image_Blob :: struct {
	id:     u32,
	kind:   u8, // Format: 0=RAW, 1=PNG, 2=JPEG (matching fmt in Chunk_Imag)
	data:   []u8,
	width:  u32,
	height: u32,
}
