import "base:runtime"
import "core:bytes"
import "core:compress/zlib"
import "core:encoding/endian"
import "core:fmt"
import "core:io"
import "core:math"
import "core:math/linalg"
import "core:mem"
import "core:slice"
import "core:strings"
import logging "project:logging"
import "project:renderer"
import "project:ui/theme"

/**
@documentation: RSD/Decoder
- Implementation of the RSD deserialization.
- Handles chunk walking, validation, and reconstruction of the in-memory model.
- Uses strict bounds checks and handles endianness via header flags.
*/

// Phase 10D.3.2: Check if transform is valid (non-singular)
is_valid_transform :: proc(m: matrix[3, 3]f32) -> bool {
	det := m[0][0] * m[1][1] - m[0][1] * m[1][0]
	return math.abs(det) > 1e-6
}

decode_bytes :: proc(data: []u8, allocator := context.allocator) -> (d: ^Drawing, ok: bool) {
	if len(data) < size_of(File_Header) {
		fmt.printf("[RSD] Data too short: %d (min %d)\n", len(data), size_of(File_Header))
		return nil, false
	}

	// Check for ZLIB magic (0x78 approx)
	// Typical ZLIB header: CMF(1) + FLG(1). CMF=0x78 for Deflate/32k window.
	is_compressed := false
	if data[0] == 0x78 {
		is_compressed = true
	}

	decode_data := data
	inflated_buf: bytes.Buffer

	if is_compressed {
		// Inflate
		// Phase 6.1: Use heap allocator for file I/O operations
		bytes.buffer_init_allocator(&inflated_buf, 0, len(data) * 4, runtime.default_allocator())
		// No need to defer destroy for temp allocator, but harmless

		if err := zlib.inflate(data, &inflated_buf); err != nil {
			fmt.printf("[RSD] Inflate failed: %v\n", err)
			return nil, false
		}
		decode_data = bytes.buffer_to_bytes(&inflated_buf)
	}

	r: bytes.Reader
	bytes.reader_init(&r, decode_data)
	return decode_stream(bytes.reader_to_stream(&r), allocator)
}

decode_stream :: proc(s: io.Stream, allocator := context.allocator) -> (d: ^Drawing, ok: bool) {
	// 1. Read File Header
	header_bytes: [size_of(File_Header)]u8
	if _, err := io.read(s, header_bytes[:]); err != nil {
		fmt.printf("[RSD] Header read failed: %v\n", err)
		return nil, false
	}
	header := (^File_Header)(raw_data(header_bytes[:]))^

	magic := [4]u8{'R', 'S', 'D', '0'}
	if mem.compare(header.magic[:], magic[:]) != 0 {
		fmt.printf("[RSD] Magic mismatch: %s\n", string(header.magic[:]))
		return nil, false
	}

	// RSD is little-endian by spec/default, bit 0 in flags confirms it
	is_little := (header.flags & u16(File_Flags.Little_Endian)) != 0
	if !is_little {
		fmt.printf("[RSD] Endian mismatch (flags: 0x%X)\n", header.flags)
		return nil, false
	}

	d = new(Drawing, allocator)
	defer if !ok do free(d, allocator)

	d.format_version = header.version
	warning_list: [dynamic]string
	defer if !ok {
		for s in warning_list do delete(s, allocator)
		delete(warning_list)
	}

	if header.version > VERSION_V1 {
		msg := fmt.tprintf(
			"Format version %v is newer than supported %v",
			header.version,
			VERSION_V1,
		)
		w_str, _ := strings.clone(msg, allocator)
		append(&warning_list, w_str)
	}

	layer_list: [dynamic]Layer
	gradient_list: [dynamic]Gradient
	image_list: [dynamic]Image_Blob
	meta_list: [dynamic]Meta_Entry
	clip_path_list: [dynamic]Clip_Path_Def
	mask_list: [dynamic]Mask_Def

	// 2. Chunk Loop
	for {
		chunk_bytes: [size_of(Chunk_Header)]u8
		if _, err := io.read(s, chunk_bytes[:]); err != nil {
			// End of stream is normal if we read exactly to the end
			break
		}
		chunk := (^Chunk_Header)(raw_data(chunk_bytes[:]))^

		// Phase 6.1: Use heap allocator for file I/O operations
		payload := make([]u8, chunk.length, runtime.default_allocator())
		if _, err := io.read(s, payload); err != nil {
			fmt.printf("[RSD] Payload read failed: %v (chunk: %s)\n", err, string(chunk.id[:]))
			return nil, false
		}

		// Handle alignment padding (4 bytes)
		if over := chunk.length % 4; over != 0 {
			pad := 4 - over
			for _ in 0 ..< pad {
				if _, err := io.read_byte(s); err != nil do return nil, false
			}
		}

		switch chunk.id {
		case CHUNK_ID_HEAD:
			if len(payload) < size_of(Chunk_Head) do return nil, false
			h := (^Chunk_Head)(raw_data(payload))^
			d.format_version = h.version
			d.units = Units(h.units)
			d.dpi = h.dpi
			d.width = h.width
			d.height = h.height
			d.background = h.background
			d.view_box = h.view_box

		case CHUNK_ID_GRAD:
			if grad, g_ok := parse_grad_chunk(payload, allocator); g_ok {
				logging.debug(
					.RSD,
					fmt.tprintf(
						"[RSD_DECODE] Decoded gradient id=%d with %d stops",
						grad.id,
						len(grad.stops),
					),
				)
				append(&gradient_list, grad)
			} else {
				fmt.printf("[RSD] GRAD chunk parse failed\n")
				return nil, false
			}

		case CHUNK_ID_THGR:
			// Phase 10C2.2: Theme Gradient Reference
			if thgr, t_ok := parse_thgr_chunk(payload, allocator); t_ok {
				// Add the fallback gradient to the gradient list
				append(&gradient_list, thgr.fallback)
				// Note: The Theme_Gradient_Ref itself is stored in a separate structure
				// For now, we'll just add the fallback gradient and log a warning
			} else {
				fmt.printf("[RSD] THGR chunk parse failed\n")
				return nil, false
			}

		case CHUNK_ID_LAYR:
			if layer, l_ok := parse_layr_chunk(payload, d, &warning_list, allocator); l_ok {
				append(&layer_list, layer)
			} else {
				fmt.printf("[RSD] LAYR chunk parse failed\n")
				return nil, false
			}

		case CHUNK_ID_META:
			if meta, m_ok := parse_meta_chunk(payload, allocator); m_ok {
				append(&meta_list, meta)
			} else {
				fmt.printf("[RSD] META chunk parse failed\n")
				return nil, false
			}

		case CHUNK_ID_TSTK:
			// Phase 10D.3.2: Transform Stack
			if tstk, ts_ok := parse_tstk_chunk(payload, allocator); ts_ok {
				// Store transform stack in drawing
				d.transform_cache = tstk
			} else {
				fmt.printf("[RSD] TSTK chunk parse failed\n")
				return nil, false
			}

		case CHUNK_ID_IMAG:
			// If we hit IMAG at top level, treat it as a blob registration
			if blob, i_ok := parse_image_blob(payload, allocator); i_ok {
				append(&image_list, blob)
			} else {
				fmt.printf("[RSD] IMAG chunk parse failed\n")
				return nil, false
			}

		case CHUNK_ID_CDEF:
			if cdef, c_ok := parse_cdef_chunk(payload, d, &warning_list, allocator); c_ok {
				append(&clip_path_list, cdef)
			} else {
				fmt.printf("[RSD] CDEF chunk parse failed\n")
				return nil, false
			}

		case CHUNK_ID_MDEF:
			if mdef, m_ok := parse_mdef_chunk(payload, d, &warning_list, allocator); m_ok {
				append(&mask_list, mdef)
			} else {
				fmt.printf("[RSD] MDEF chunk parse failed\n")
				return nil, false
			}

		case:
			msg := fmt.tprintf("Unknown top-level chunk: %s", string(chunk.id[:]))
			w_str, _ := strings.clone(msg, allocator)
			append(&warning_list, w_str)
		}
	}

	d.layers = slice.clone(layer_list[:], allocator)
	d.gradients = slice.clone(gradient_list[:], allocator)
	d.images = slice.clone(image_list[:], allocator)
	d.meta = slice.clone(meta_list[:], allocator)
	d.clip_paths = slice.clone(clip_path_list[:], allocator)
	d.masks = slice.clone(mask_list[:], allocator)
	d.warnings = slice.clone(warning_list[:], allocator)

	if logging.STRESS_TEST {
		fmt.printf(
			"[RSD_DECODE] Drawing decoded: version=%d, layers=%d\n",
			d.format_version,
			len(d.layers),
		)
		for layer in d.layers {
			fmt.printf("[RSD_DECODE]   Layer %q: items=%d\n", layer.name, len(layer.items))
		}
	}

	return d, true
}

// --- Specific Parsers ---

// Phase 10D.3.2: Parse Transform Stack Chunk
parse_tstk_chunk :: proc(
	payload: []u8,
	allocator: mem.Allocator,
) -> (
	cache: Transform_Cache,
	ok: bool,
) {
	if len(payload) < size_of(Chunk_Tstk) do return {}, false

	schema := (^Chunk_Tstk)(raw_data(payload))^
	offset := size_of(Chunk_Tstk)

	// Create transform cache
	cache.transforms = make(map[u32]matrix[3, 3]f32, allocator)

	// Parse each transform
	for i in 0 ..< schema.transform_count {
		if offset + size_of(Chunk_Matr) > len(payload) do return {}, false

		mat := (^Chunk_Matr)(raw_data(payload[offset:]))^
		transform := transmute(matrix[3, 3]f32)mat.m

		// Validate transform
		if !is_valid_transform(transform) {
			fmt.eprintln("Warning: Invalid transform in TSTK chunk at index", i)
			transform = linalg.MATRIX3F32_IDENTITY
		}

		cache.transforms[u32(i + 1)] = transform // 1-based indexing
		offset += size_of(Chunk_Matr)
	}

	return cache, true
}

// Phase 10D.3.1: Extended gradient parsing with transforms and focal points
parse_grad_chunk :: proc(payload: []u8, allocator: mem.Allocator) -> (g: Gradient, ok: bool) {
	if len(payload) < size_of(Chunk_Grad) do return {}, false

	schema := (^Chunk_Grad)(raw_data(payload))^
	g.id = schema.id
	g.type = Gradient_Type(schema.type)
	g.units = Gradient_Units(schema.units)
	g.p0 = schema.p0
	g.p1 = schema.p1
	g.fx = schema.fx // Phase 10D.3.1: Focal point for radial gradients
	g.fy = schema.fy // Phase 10D.3.1: Focal point for radial gradients

	offset := size_of(Chunk_Grad)

	// Phase 10D.3.1: Parse transform matrix if present
	if schema.has_transform == 1 {
		if offset + 9 * 4 > len(payload) do return {}, false

		for i in 0 ..< 3 {
			for j in 0 ..< 3 {
				g.transform[i][j], _ = endian.get_f32(payload[offset:], .Little)
				offset += 4
			}
		}
	} else {
		g.transform = linalg.MATRIX3F32_IDENTITY
	}

	// Parse gradient stops
	// Phase 6.1: Use heap allocator for file I/O operations
	stops := make([dynamic]Gradient_Stop, runtime.default_allocator())
	for _ in 0 ..< schema.stops_count {
		if offset + 4 + 4 > len(payload) do return {}, false

		t, _ := endian.get_f32(payload[offset:], .Little)
		offset += 4

		color := rgba8_to_f32(payload[offset:offset + 4])
		offset += 4

		append(&stops, Gradient_Stop{t = t, color = color})
	}
	g.stops = slice.clone(stops[:], allocator)

	return g, true
}

/**
@documentation: Parse Theme Gradient Reference Chunk (Phase 10C2.2)
- Parses THGR chunk to create Theme_Gradient_Ref
- Token string follows the fixed header
- Fallback gradient is parsed and returned
*/
parse_thgr_chunk :: proc(
	payload: []u8,
	allocator: mem.Allocator,
) -> (
	g: Theme_Gradient_Ref,
	ok: bool,
) {
	if len(payload) < size_of(Chunk_Thgr) do return {}, false

	schema := (^Chunk_Thgr)(raw_data(payload))^

	offset := size_of(Chunk_Thgr)
	if offset + int(schema.token_len) > len(payload) do return {}, false

	token := string(payload[offset:offset + int(schema.token_len)])
	offset += int(schema.token_len)

	// Parse fallback gradient from the remaining payload
	fallback: Gradient
	if offset < len(payload) {
		// Try to parse inline gradient as fallback
		fallback_chunk := payload[offset:]
		if len(fallback_chunk) >= size_of(Chunk_Grad) {
			grad_schema := (^Chunk_Grad)(raw_data(fallback_chunk))^
			fallback.id = grad_schema.id
			fallback.type = Gradient_Type(grad_schema.type)
			fallback.units = Gradient_Units(grad_schema.units)
			fallback.p0 = grad_schema.p0
			fallback.p1 = grad_schema.p1

			grad_offset := size_of(Chunk_Grad)
			// Phase 6.1: Use heap allocator for file I/O operations
			stops := make([dynamic]Gradient_Stop, runtime.default_allocator())
			for _ in 0 ..< grad_schema.stops_count {
				if grad_offset + 4 + 4 > len(fallback_chunk) do break

				t, _ := endian.get_f32(fallback_chunk[grad_offset:], .Little)
				grad_offset += 4

				color := rgba8_to_f32(fallback_chunk[grad_offset:grad_offset + 4])
				grad_offset += 4

				append(&stops, Gradient_Stop{t = t, color = color})
			}
			fallback.stops = slice.clone(stops[:], allocator)
			fallback.transform = 1
		} else {
			// Use default fallback gradient
			fallback.id = schema.fallback_id
			fallback.type = .Linear
			fallback.units = .ObjectBoundingBox
			fallback.p0 = {0, 0}
			fallback.p1 = {1, 0}
			fallback.stops = make([]Gradient_Stop, 2, allocator)
			fallback.stops[0] = Gradient_Stop {
				t     = 0.0,
				color = {0, 0, 0, 1},
			}
			fallback.stops[1] = Gradient_Stop {
				t     = 1.0,
				color = {1, 1, 1, 1},
			}
			fallback.transform = 1
		}
	} else {
		// Use default fallback gradient
		fallback.id = schema.fallback_id
		fallback.type = .Linear
		fallback.units = .ObjectBoundingBox
		fallback.p0 = {0, 0}
		fallback.p1 = {1, 0}
		fallback.stops = make([]Gradient_Stop, 2, allocator)
		fallback.stops[0] = Gradient_Stop {
			t     = 0.0,
			color = {0, 0, 0, 1},
		}
		fallback.stops[1] = Gradient_Stop {
			t     = 1.0,
			color = {1, 1, 1, 1},
		}
		fallback.transform = 1
	}

	return Theme_Gradient_Ref{token = token, fallback = fallback}, true
}

parse_layr_chunk :: proc(
	payload: []u8,
	d: ^Drawing,
	warnings: ^[dynamic]string,
	allocator: mem.Allocator,
) -> (
	l: Layer,
	ok: bool,
) {
	if len(payload) < size_of(Chunk_Layr) do return {}, false

	schema := (^Chunk_Layr)(raw_data(payload))^
	l.visible = schema.visible != 0
	l.opacity = schema.opacity
	l.transform = 1

	offset := size_of(Chunk_Layr)
	if offset + int(schema.name_len) > len(payload) do return {}, false

	l.name = string(slice.clone(payload[offset:offset + int(schema.name_len)], allocator))
	offset += int(schema.name_len)

	// Sub-chunks (MATR, PATH, TEXT, IMAG)
	items: [dynamic]Item
	for offset < len(payload) {
		// Respect 4-byte alignment for sub-chunks
		if offset % 4 != 0 do offset += (4 - (offset % 4))
		if offset + size_of(Chunk_Header) > len(payload) do break

		chunk := (^Chunk_Header)(raw_data(payload[offset:]))^
		offset += size_of(Chunk_Header)

		if offset + int(chunk.length) > len(payload) {
			fmt.printf(
				"[RSD] LAYR sub-chunk overflow: id=%s len=%d offset=%d total=%d\n",
				string(chunk.id[:]),
				chunk.length,
				offset,
				len(payload),
			)
			return {}, false
		}
		item_payload := payload[offset:offset + int(chunk.length)]
		offset += int(chunk.length)

		switch chunk.id {
		case CHUNK_ID_MATR:
			if len(item_payload) < size_of(Chunk_Matr) do return {}, false
			m := (^Chunk_Matr)(raw_data(item_payload))^
			l.transform = transmute(matrix[3, 3]f32)m.m

		case CHUNK_ID_PATH:
			if path, p_ok := parse_path_item(item_payload, warnings, allocator); p_ok {
				append(&items, path)
			} else {
				fmt.printf("[RSD] PATH item parse failed\n")
				return {}, false
			}

		case CHUNK_ID_TEXT:
			if text, t_ok := parse_text_item(item_payload, warnings, allocator); t_ok {
				append(&items, text)
			} else {
				return {}, false
			}

		case CHUNK_ID_IMAG:
			if img, i_ok := parse_image_item(item_payload, d, warnings, allocator); i_ok {
				append(&items, img)
			} else {
				return {}, false
			}

		case CHUNK_ID_GRUP:
			// Recursive Group
			if group, g_ok := parse_group_item(item_payload, d, warnings, allocator); g_ok {
				append(&items, group)
			} else {
				return {}, false
			}

		case:
			msg := fmt.tprintf("Unknown item-level chunk: %s", string(chunk.id[:]))
			w_str, _ := strings.clone(msg, allocator)
			append(warnings, w_str)
		}
	}

	l.items = slice.clone(items[:], allocator)
	return l, true
}

parse_path_item :: proc(
	payload: []u8,
	warnings: ^[dynamic]string,
	allocator: mem.Allocator,
) -> (
	p: Path_Item,
	ok: bool,
) {
	if len(payload) < size_of(Chunk_Path) {
		fmt.printf("[RSD] PATH chunk too small: %d < %d\n", len(payload), size_of(Chunk_Path))
		return {}, false
	}

	schema := (^Chunk_Path)(raw_data(payload))^
	p.fill_rule = Fill_Rule(schema.fill_rule)
	p.transform = 1

	offset := size_of(Chunk_Path)

	// Commands
	if offset + int(schema.n_cmd) > len(payload) {
		fmt.printf(
			"[RSD] PATH commands overflow: %d > %d\n",
			offset + int(schema.n_cmd),
			len(payload),
		)
		return {}, false
	}
	p.commands = make([]Path_Command, schema.n_cmd, allocator)
	for i in 0 ..< schema.n_cmd {
		p.commands[i] = Path_Command(payload[offset + int(i)])
	}
	offset += int(schema.n_cmd)

	// Align for points
	if offset % 4 != 0 do offset += (4 - (offset % 4))

	// Points
	if offset + int(schema.n_pt) * 8 > len(payload) {
		fmt.printf(
			"[RSD] PATH points overflow: %d > %d\n",
			offset + int(schema.n_pt) * 8,
			len(payload),
		)
		return {}, false
	}
	p.points = make([][2]f32, schema.n_pt, allocator)
	for i in 0 ..< schema.n_pt {
		p.points[i].x, _ = endian.get_f32(payload[offset + int(i) * 8:], .Little)
		p.points[i].y, _ = endian.get_f32(payload[offset + int(i) * 8 + 4:], .Little)
	}
	offset += int(schema.n_pt) * 8

	// Sub-chunks (STYL, MATR)
	when logging.DEBUG_RSD {
		logging.debug(
			.RSD,
			fmt.tprintf(
				"[RSD_DECODER] PATH chunk: starting sub-chunk processing at offset %d, remaining %d bytes",
				offset,
				len(payload) - offset,
			),
			key = "rsd_path_subchunks_start",
		)
	}

	found_subchunks := false
	for offset < len(payload) {
		found_subchunks = true
		if offset % 4 != 0 do offset += (4 - (offset % 4))
		if offset + size_of(Chunk_Header) > len(payload) do break

		chunk := (^Chunk_Header)(raw_data(payload[offset:]))^
		chunk_id_str := fmt.tprintf("%c%c%c%c", chunk.id[0], chunk.id[1], chunk.id[2], chunk.id[3])
		when logging.DEBUG_RSD {
			logging.debug(
				.RSD,
				fmt.tprintf(
					"[RSD_DECODER] Found sub-chunk %s at offset %d, length %d",
					chunk_id_str,
					offset,
					chunk.length,
				),
				key = "rsd_path_subchunk_found",
			)
		}
		offset += size_of(Chunk_Header)

		if offset + int(chunk.length) > len(payload) do return {}, false
		sub_payload := payload[offset:offset + int(chunk.length)]
		offset += int(chunk.length)

		switch chunk.id {
		case CHUNK_ID_STYL:
			when logging.DEBUG_RSD {
				logging.debug(
					.RSD,
					fmt.tprintf(
						"[RSD_DECODER] Processing STYL sub-chunk with %d bytes",
						len(sub_payload),
					),
					key = "rsd_styl_processing",
				)
			}
			if len(sub_payload) < size_of(Chunk_Styl) do return {}, false
			styl := (^Chunk_Styl)(raw_data(sub_payload))^
			p.stroke.color = rgba8_to_f32(styl.stroke_rgba[:])
			p.stroke.width = styl.stroke_width
			p.stroke.join = renderer.Line_Join(styl.join)
			p.stroke.cap = renderer.Line_Cap(styl.cap)
			p.stroke.miter_limit = styl.miter_limit
			p.stroke.dash_offset = styl.dash_offset

			// Debug: Log the extracted colors
			when logging.DEBUG_RSD {
				fill_color := "None"
				if styl.fill_kind == 1 { 	// Solid
					fill_color = fmt.tprintf(
						"Solid(%f,%f,%f,%f)",
						styl.fill_rgba[0] / 255.0,
						styl.fill_rgba[1] / 255.0,
						styl.fill_rgba[2] / 255.0,
						styl.fill_rgba[3] / 255.0,
					)
				} else if styl.fill_kind == 2 { 	// Gradient
					fill_color = fmt.tprintf("Gradient(%d)", styl.fill_grad_id)
				} else if styl.fill_kind == 3 { 	// Theme
					fill_color = "Theme"
				}
				stroke_color := fmt.tprintf(
					"RGBA(%d,%d,%d,%d)",
					styl.stroke_rgba[0],
					styl.stroke_rgba[1],
					styl.stroke_rgba[2],
					styl.stroke_rgba[3],
				)
				logging.debug(
					.RSD,
					fmt.tprintf(
						"[RSD_DECODER] Extracted STYL: fill_kind=%d, fill=%s, stroke=%s, width=%f",
						styl.fill_kind,
						fill_color,
						stroke_color,
						styl.stroke_width,
					),
					key = "rsd_styl_extracted",
				)
			}

			// Dashes
			if styl.dash_count > 0 {
				dash_offset := size_of(Chunk_Styl)
				expected_size := dash_offset + int(styl.dash_count) * 4

				// Validate dash array size to prevent overflow/corruption
				if styl.dash_count > 255 {
					logging.error(
						.RSD,
						fmt.tprintf(
							"Dash count overflow: %d (max 255), truncating",
							styl.dash_count,
						),
						key = "rsd_dash_overflow",
					)
					// Truncate to maximum safe value
					styl.dash_count = 255
					expected_size = dash_offset + 255 * 4
				}

				if expected_size <= len(sub_payload) {
					p.stroke.dash_array = make([]f32, styl.dash_count, allocator)
					for i in 0 ..< styl.dash_count {
						p.stroke.dash_array[i], _ = endian.get_f32(
							sub_payload[dash_offset + int(i) * 4:],
							.Little,
						)
					}
				} else {
					logging.error(
						.RSD,
						fmt.tprintf(
							"Dash array size mismatch: expected %d, got %d",
							expected_size,
							len(sub_payload),
						),
						key = "rsd_dash_size_mismatch",
					)
				}
			}

			switch styl.fill_kind {
			case 0:
				p.fill = None{}
			case 1:
				p.fill = rgba8_to_f32(styl.fill_rgba[:])
			case 2:
				// Gradient reference - ID will be validated during rendering
				p.fill = Gradient_Ref {
					id = styl.fill_grad_id,
				}
			case 3:
				// Phase 10C2: Theme color/gradient reference
				// Read variable-length Theme_Ref/Theme_Gradient_Ref data after fixed header
				data_offset := size_of(Chunk_Styl) + int(styl.dash_count) * 4
				if data_offset + 2 <= len(sub_payload) {
					// Read token length
					token_len_val, _ := endian.get_u16(sub_payload[data_offset:], .Little)
					token_len := int(token_len_val)
					data_offset += 2
					// Read token string
					if data_offset + int(token_len) + 1 <= len(sub_payload) {
						token := string(sub_payload[data_offset:data_offset + int(token_len)])
						data_offset += int(token_len)
						// Peek at next byte to determine if this is color or gradient ref
						if data_offset + 4 <= len(sub_payload) {
							// Check if it looks like a gradient type (0=Linear, 1=Radial) or RGBA
							next_byte := sub_payload[data_offset]
							if next_byte == 0 || next_byte == 1 {
								// This is a Theme_Gradient_Ref - decode fallback gradient
								fallback_type := Gradient_Type(next_byte)
								data_offset += 1
								if data_offset + 1 < len(sub_payload) {
									fallback_units := Gradient_Units(sub_payload[data_offset])
									data_offset += 1
									if data_offset + 4 <= len(sub_payload) {
										fallback_id, _ := endian.get_u32(
											sub_payload[data_offset:],
											.Little,
										)
										data_offset += 4
										// Read stops count
										if data_offset + 2 <= len(sub_payload) {
											stops_count_val, _ := endian.get_u16(
												sub_payload[data_offset:],
												.Little,
											)
											data_offset += 2
											stops := make([dynamic]Gradient_Stop, allocator)
											for _ in 0 ..< int(stops_count_val) {
												if data_offset + 4 + 4 > len(sub_payload) do break
												t, _ := endian.get_f32(
													sub_payload[data_offset:],
													.Little,
												)
												data_offset += 4
												fallback_color := rgba8_to_f32(
													sub_payload[data_offset:data_offset + 4],
												)
												data_offset += 4
												append(
													&stops,
													Gradient_Stop{t = t, color = fallback_color},
												)
											}
											p.fill = Theme_Gradient_Ref {
												token = token,
												fallback = Gradient {
													id = fallback_id,
													type = fallback_type,
													units = fallback_units,
													stops = slice.clone(stops[:], allocator),
													transform = 1,
												},
											}
										} else {
											p.fill = None{}
											msg := fmt.tprintf(
												"rsd: Theme_Gradient_Ref stops count extends beyond payload",
											)
											append(warnings, msg)
										}
									} else {
										p.fill = None{}
										msg := fmt.tprintf(
											"rsd: Theme_Gradient_Ref fallback id extends beyond payload",
										)
										append(warnings, msg)
									}
								} else {
									p.fill = None{}
									msg := fmt.tprintf(
										"rsd: Theme_Gradient_Ref units extends beyond payload",
									)
									append(warnings, msg)
								}
							} else {
								p.fill = None{}
								msg := fmt.tprintf(
									"rsd: Theme_Gradient_Ref type extends beyond payload",
								)
								append(warnings, msg)
							}
						} else {
							// This is a Theme_Ref - decode fallback color
							if data_offset + 4 <= len(sub_payload) {
								fallback := rgba8_to_f32(sub_payload[data_offset:data_offset + 4])
								p.fill = Theme_Ref {
									token    = token,
									fallback = fallback,
								}
							} else {
								p.fill = None{}
								msg := fmt.tprintf(
									"rsd: Theme_Ref fallback color extends beyond payload",
								)
								append(warnings, msg)
							}
						}
					} else {
						p.fill = None{}
						msg := fmt.tprintf(
							"rsd: Theme_Ref/Theme_Gradient_Ref token extends beyond payload",
						)
						append(warnings, msg)
					}
				} else {
					p.fill = None{}
					msg := fmt.tprintf(
						"rsd: Theme_Ref/Theme_Gradient_Ref data incomplete in STYL chunk",
					)
					append(warnings, msg)
				}
			}

		case CHUNK_ID_MATR:
			if len(sub_payload) < size_of(Chunk_Matr) do return {}, false
			m := (^Chunk_Matr)(raw_data(sub_payload))^
			p.transform = transmute(matrix[3, 3]f32)m.m

		case CHUNK_ID_CLPR:
			if len(sub_payload) < size_of(Chunk_Clip_Ref) do return {}, false
			ref := (^Chunk_Clip_Ref)(raw_data(sub_payload))^
			p.clip_path_id = ref.id

		case CHUNK_ID_MSKR:
			if len(sub_payload) < size_of(Chunk_Mask_Ref) do return {}, false
			ref := (^Chunk_Mask_Ref)(raw_data(sub_payload))^
			p.mask_id = ref.id

		case:
			msg := fmt.tprintf("Unknown path-sub-chunk: %s", string(chunk.id[:]))
			w_str, _ := strings.clone(msg, allocator)
			append(warnings, w_str)
		}
	}

	when logging.DEBUG_RSD {
		if !found_subchunks {
			logging.debug(
				.RSD,
				fmt.tprintf("[RSD_DECODER] PATH chunk: no sub-chunks found after processing"),
				key = "rsd_path_no_subchunks",
			)
		}
	}

	return p, true
}

parse_text_item :: proc(
	payload: []u8,
	warnings: ^[dynamic]string,
	allocator: mem.Allocator,
) -> (
	t: Text_Item,
	ok: bool,
) {
	if len(payload) < size_of(Chunk_Text) do return {}, false

	schema := (^Chunk_Text)(raw_data(payload))^
	t.font.name = "" // Will be set below
	t.font.size = schema.size
	t.color = rgba8_to_f32(schema.color_rgba[:])
	t.anchor = Text_Anchor(schema.anchor)
	t.line_height = schema.line_height
	t.transform = 1

	offset := size_of(Chunk_Text)
	if offset + int(schema.text_len) > len(payload) do return {}, false
	t.text = string(slice.clone(payload[offset:offset + int(schema.text_len)], allocator))
	offset += int(schema.text_len)

	// Font name follows text bytes (non-aligned strings)
	if offset < len(payload) {
		if s, s_ok := parse_string(payload, &offset, allocator); s_ok {
			t.font.name = s
		}
	}

	// Sub-chunks (MATR, etc.)
	for offset < len(payload) {
		if offset % 4 != 0 do offset += (4 - (offset % 4))
		if offset + size_of(Chunk_Header) > len(payload) do break

		chunk := (^Chunk_Header)(raw_data(payload[offset:]))^
		offset += size_of(Chunk_Header)

		if offset + int(chunk.length) > len(payload) do break
		sub_payload := payload[offset:offset + int(chunk.length)]
		offset += int(chunk.length)

		switch chunk.id {
		case CHUNK_ID_MATR:
			if len(sub_payload) >= size_of(Chunk_Matr) {
				m := (^Chunk_Matr)(raw_data(sub_payload))^
				t.transform = transmute(matrix[3, 3]f32)m.m
			}

		case CHUNK_ID_CLPR:
			if len(sub_payload) >= size_of(Chunk_Clip_Ref) {
				ref := (^Chunk_Clip_Ref)(raw_data(sub_payload))^
				t.clip_path_id = ref.id
			}

		case CHUNK_ID_MSKR:
			if len(sub_payload) >= size_of(Chunk_Mask_Ref) {
				ref := (^Chunk_Mask_Ref)(raw_data(sub_payload))^
				t.mask_id = ref.id
			}

		case:
			msg := fmt.tprintf("Unknown text-sub-chunk: %s", string(chunk.id[:]))
			w_str, _ := strings.clone(msg, allocator)
			append(warnings, w_str)
		}
	}

	return t, true
}

parse_image_item :: proc(
	payload: []u8,
	d: ^Drawing,
	warnings: ^[dynamic]string,
	allocator: mem.Allocator,
) -> (
	img: Image_Item,
	ok: bool,
) {
	if len(payload) < size_of(Chunk_Imag) do return {}, false

	schema := (^Chunk_Imag)(raw_data(payload))^
	img.dest_rect = {0, 0, f32(schema.w), f32(schema.h)}
	img.tint = {1, 1, 1, 1}
	img.transform = 1

	offset := size_of(Chunk_Imag)
	if schema.kind == 0 { 	// External
		if offset < len(payload) {
			img.source = External_Path(string(payload[offset:]))
		}
	} else { 	// Embedded
		msg := fmt.tprintf("rsd: Embedded images not yet fully supported")
		append(warnings, msg)
	}

	// Sub-chunks (CLPR, MSKR, MATR)
	for offset < len(payload) {
		if offset % 4 != 0 do offset += (4 - (offset % 4))
		if offset + size_of(Chunk_Header) > len(payload) do break

		chunk := (^Chunk_Header)(raw_data(payload[offset:]))^
		offset += size_of(Chunk_Header)

		if offset + int(chunk.length) > len(payload) do break
		sub_payload := payload[offset:offset + int(chunk.length)]
		offset += int(chunk.length)

		switch chunk.id {
		case CHUNK_ID_MATR:
			if len(sub_payload) >= size_of(Chunk_Matr) {
				m := (^Chunk_Matr)(raw_data(sub_payload))^
				img.transform = transmute(matrix[3, 3]f32)m.m
			}
		case CHUNK_ID_CLPR:
			if len(sub_payload) >= size_of(Chunk_Clip_Ref) {
				ref := (^Chunk_Clip_Ref)(raw_data(sub_payload))^
				img.clip_path_id = ref.id
			}
		case CHUNK_ID_MSKR:
			if len(sub_payload) >= size_of(Chunk_Mask_Ref) {
				ref := (^Chunk_Mask_Ref)(raw_data(sub_payload))^
				img.mask_id = ref.id
			}
		}
	}

	return img, true
}

parse_meta_chunk :: proc(payload: []u8, allocator: mem.Allocator) -> (m: Meta_Entry, ok: bool) {
	if len(payload) < size_of(Chunk_Meta) do return {}, false

	schema := (^Chunk_Meta)(raw_data(payload))^
	offset := size_of(Chunk_Meta)

	if offset + int(schema.key_len) > len(payload) do return {}, false
	m.key = string(slice.clone(payload[offset:offset + int(schema.key_len)], allocator))
	offset += int(schema.key_len)

	if offset + int(schema.value_len) > len(payload) do return {}, false
	m.value = string(slice.clone(payload[offset:offset + int(schema.value_len)], allocator))

	return m, true
}

parse_image_blob :: proc(payload: []u8, allocator: mem.Allocator) -> (img: Image_Blob, ok: bool) {
	if len(payload) < size_of(Chunk_Imag) do return {}, false

	schema := (^Chunk_Imag)(raw_data(payload))^
	offset := size_of(Chunk_Imag)

	img.kind = schema.fmt // Reuse fmt field for image format
	img.width = schema.w
	img.height = schema.h

	data_len := int(schema.data_len)
	if offset + data_len > len(payload) do return {}, false
	img.data = slice.clone(payload[offset:offset + data_len], allocator)

	return img, true
}

rgba8_to_f32 :: proc(rgba: []u8) -> [4]f32 {
	assert(len(rgba) >= 4)
	return {f32(rgba[0]) / 255.0, f32(rgba[1]) / 255.0, f32(rgba[2]) / 255.0, f32(rgba[3]) / 255.0}
}

// Phase 12: Clip and Mask Parsers
parse_cdef_chunk :: proc(
	payload: []u8,
	d: ^Drawing,
	warnings: ^[dynamic]string,
	allocator: mem.Allocator,
) -> (
	cp: Clip_Path_Def,
	ok: bool,
) {
	if len(payload) < size_of(Chunk_Cdef) do return {}, false
	schema := (^Chunk_Cdef)(raw_data(payload))^
	cp.id = schema.id

	offset := size_of(Chunk_Cdef)
	items: [dynamic]Item
	for offset < len(payload) {
		if offset % 4 != 0 do offset += (4 - (offset % 4))
		if offset + size_of(Chunk_Header) > len(payload) do break

		chunk := (^Chunk_Header)(raw_data(payload[offset:]))^
		offset += size_of(Chunk_Header)

		item_payload := payload[offset:offset + int(chunk.length)]
		offset += int(chunk.length)

		switch chunk.id {
		case CHUNK_ID_PATH:
			if path, p_ok := parse_path_item(item_payload, warnings, allocator); p_ok {
				append(&items, path)
			}
		case CHUNK_ID_TEXT:
			if text, t_ok := parse_text_item(item_payload, warnings, allocator); t_ok {
				append(&items, text)
			}
		case CHUNK_ID_IMAG:
			if img, i_ok := parse_image_item(item_payload, d, warnings, allocator); i_ok {
				append(&items, img)
			}
		}
	}
	cp.items = slice.clone(items[:], allocator)
	return cp, true
}

parse_mdef_chunk :: proc(
	payload: []u8,
	d: ^Drawing,
	warnings: ^[dynamic]string,
	allocator: mem.Allocator,
) -> (
	m: Mask_Def,
	ok: bool,
) {
	if len(payload) < size_of(Chunk_Mdef) do return {}, false
	schema := (^Chunk_Mdef)(raw_data(payload))^
	m.id = schema.id

	offset := size_of(Chunk_Mdef)
	items: [dynamic]Item
	for offset < len(payload) {
		if offset % 4 != 0 do offset += (4 - (offset % 4))
		if offset + size_of(Chunk_Header) > len(payload) do break

		chunk := (^Chunk_Header)(raw_data(payload[offset:]))^
		offset += size_of(Chunk_Header)

		item_payload := payload[offset:offset + int(chunk.length)]
		offset += int(chunk.length)

		switch chunk.id {
		case CHUNK_ID_PATH:
			if path, p_ok := parse_path_item(item_payload, warnings, allocator); p_ok {
				append(&items, path)
			}
		case CHUNK_ID_TEXT:
			if text, t_ok := parse_text_item(item_payload, warnings, allocator); t_ok {
				append(&items, text)
			}
		case CHUNK_ID_IMAG:
			if img, i_ok := parse_image_item(item_payload, d, warnings, allocator); i_ok {
				append(&items, img)
			}
		case CHUNK_ID_LAYR:
			// Recursive Group
			if group, g_ok := parse_group_item(item_payload, d, warnings, allocator); g_ok {
				append(&items, group)
			}
		}
	}
	m.items = slice.clone(items[:], allocator)
	return m, true
}

parse_string :: proc(data: []u8, offset: ^int, allocator: mem.Allocator) -> (s: string, ok: bool) {
	if offset^ + 2 > len(data) do return "", false

	length_val, _ := endian.get_u16(data[offset^:], .Little)
	length := int(length_val)
	offset^ += 2

	if offset^ + length > len(data) do return "", false

	s = string(slice.clone(data[offset^:offset^ + length], allocator))
	offset^ += length

	return s, true
}

// --- Theme Resolution (Phase 10C2.4) ---

// theme_get_color looks up a color token in the theme.
// Supports token formats: "colors.<name>", "<name>", or full path like "tokens.colors.primary"
theme_get_color :: proc(theme: ^theme.Theme_Data, token: string) -> (color: [4]f32, ok: bool) {
	if theme == nil do return {}, false

	// Direct map lookup in theme.resolved.colors
	// theme.resolved.colors is map[string][4]f32
	token_key := token

	// Handle full path format "tokens.colors.xxx" -> extract "xxx"
	if strings.has_prefix(token, "tokens.") {
		parts := strings.split(token, ".")
		defer delete(parts)
		if len(parts) >= 3 {
			token_key = parts[2]
		}
	}

	// Try direct lookup with the token as-is
	if c, found := theme.resolved.colors[token_key]; found {
		return c, true
	}

	// Try with "colors." prefix stripped
	if strings.has_prefix(token_key, "colors.") {
		color_key := token_key[7:]
		if c, found := theme.resolved.colors[color_key]; found {
			return c, true
		}
	}

	return {}, false
}

// theme_get_gradient looks up a gradient token in the theme.
// Returns the gradient definition if found.
// Phase 10C2.10: Uses theme.resolved.gradients for lookup.
theme_get_gradient :: proc(theme: ^theme.Theme_Data, token: string) -> (grad: Gradient, ok: bool) {
	if theme == nil do return {}, false

	// Try direct lookup with the token as-is
	token_key := token

	// Handle full path format "gradients.xxx" -> extract "xxx"
	if strings.has_prefix(token, "gradients.") {
		token_key = token[10:] // Remove "gradients." prefix
	}

	// Try direct lookup with the token as-is
	if spec, found := theme.resolved.gradients[token_key]; found {
		return gradient_spec_to_gradient(spec, context.allocator), true
	}

	// Try with "gradients." prefix
	if !strings.has_prefix(token, "gradients.") {
		prefixed_builder := strings.builder_make()
		strings.write_string(&prefixed_builder, "gradients.")
		strings.write_string(&prefixed_builder, token)
		prefixed := strings.to_string(prefixed_builder)
		if spec, found := theme.resolved.gradients[prefixed]; found {
			return gradient_spec_to_gradient(spec, context.allocator), true
		}
	}

	return {}, false
}

// gradient_spec_to_gradient converts a Gradient_Spec to a Gradient.
// Phase 10C2.10: Helper for converting theme gradient spec to RSD gradient.
gradient_spec_to_gradient :: proc(
	spec: theme.Gradient_Spec,
	allocator: mem.Allocator,
) -> Gradient {
	grad: Gradient
	grad.type = Gradient_Type(spec.type)
	grad.units = .ObjectBoundingBox
	grad.p0 = {0, 0}
	grad.p1 = {1, 0}
	// Manually copy stops since Gradient_Stop types differ between theme and rsd packages
	grad.stops = make([]Gradient_Stop, len(spec.stops), allocator)
	for i := 0; i < len(spec.stops); i += 1 {
		ts := spec.stops[i]
		grad.stops[i] = Gradient_Stop {
			t     = ts.t,
			color = ts.color,
		}
	}
	grad.transform = spec.transform
	return grad
}

// decode_fill_with_theme resolves a Theme_Ref or Theme_Gradient_Ref using the theme system.
// If theme is nil or token not found, returns the fallback value.
decode_fill_with_theme :: proc(fill: Fill_Style, theme: ^theme.Theme_Data) -> Fill_Style {
	switch f in fill {
	case Theme_Ref:
		if theme != nil {
			if color, ok := theme_get_color(theme, f.token); ok {
				return Theme_Ref{token = f.token, fallback = color}
			}
		}
		return f
	case Theme_Gradient_Ref:
		if theme != nil {
			if grad, ok := theme_get_gradient(theme, f.token); ok {
				return Theme_Gradient_Ref{token = f.token, fallback = grad}
			}
		}
		return f
	case None, Solid_Color, Gradient_Ref:
		return fill
	}
	return fill
}

// parse_group_item parses a GRUP chunk (nested items).
// Recursive: calls into item switch logic.
parse_group_item :: proc(
	payload: []u8,
	d: ^Drawing,
	warnings: ^[dynamic]string,
	allocator: mem.Allocator,
) -> (
	g: Group_Item,
	ok: bool,
) {
	g.transform = 1 // Identity

	items: [dynamic]Item
	offset := 0
	for offset < len(payload) {
		if offset % 4 != 0 do offset += (4 - (offset % 4))
		if offset + size_of(Chunk_Header) > len(payload) do break

		chunk := (^Chunk_Header)(raw_data(payload[offset:]))^
		offset += size_of(Chunk_Header)

		if offset + int(chunk.length) > len(payload) do return {}, false
		item_payload := payload[offset:offset + int(chunk.length)]

		switch chunk.id {
		case CHUNK_ID_MATR:
			if len(item_payload) < size_of(Chunk_Matr) do return {}, false
			m := (^Chunk_Matr)(raw_data(item_payload))^
			g.transform = transmute(matrix[3, 3]f32)m.m

		case CHUNK_ID_PATH:
			if path, p_ok := parse_path_item(item_payload, warnings, allocator); p_ok {
				append(&items, path)
			} else {
				return {}, false
			}

		case CHUNK_ID_TEXT:
			if text, t_ok := parse_text_item(item_payload, warnings, allocator); t_ok {
				append(&items, text)
			} else {
				return {}, false
			}

		case CHUNK_ID_IMAG:
			if img, i_ok := parse_image_item(item_payload, d, warnings, allocator); i_ok {
				append(&items, img)
			} else {
				return {}, false
			}

		case CHUNK_ID_GRUP:
			if grp, g_ok := parse_group_item(item_payload, d, warnings, allocator); g_ok {
				append(&items, grp)
			} else {
				return {}, false
			}

		case CHUNK_ID_CLPR:
			if len(item_payload) < size_of(Chunk_Clip_Ref) do return {}, false
			ref := (^Chunk_Clip_Ref)(raw_data(item_payload))^
			g.clip_path_id = ref.id

		case CHUNK_ID_MSKR:
			if len(item_payload) < size_of(Chunk_Mask_Ref) do return {}, false
			ref := (^Chunk_Mask_Ref)(raw_data(item_payload))^
			g.mask_id = ref.id
		}

		offset += int(chunk.length)
	}

	g.items = items[:]
	return g, true
}

DEBUG: Source content: package graphics

import "base:runtime"
import "core:fmt"
import "core:math"
import "core:math/linalg"
import "core:mem"
import "core:time"
import "project:renderer"

/**
@documentation: SVG Path Caching System (Phase 10D.5.1)
- Implements simple cache for frequently used SVG paths
- Improves rendering performance by avoiding repeated path parsing
- Provides basic cache statistics

@documentation: SVG LOD System (Phase 10D.5.2)
- Implements Level of Detail system for complex SVG rendering
- Automatically adjusts rendering quality based on performance
- Provides manual LOD control for debugging and optimization
*/

// Simple Path Cache using a map (no LRU for initial implementation)
SVG_Path_Cache :: struct {
    entries: map[string]SVG_Parsed_Path,  // Simple map-based cache
    hit_count: i64,                       // Cache hit counter
    miss_count: i64,                      // Cache miss counter
    max_size: int,                        // Maximum number of entries
    allocator: mem.Allocator,             // Allocator for cache
}

// Cache Statistics
SVG_Path_Cache_Stats :: struct {
    hit_rate: f32,              // Cache hit rate (0.0 - 1.0)
    current_size: int,          // Current cache size
    max_size: int,              // Maximum cache size
}

// Initialize SVG Path Cache
svg_path_cache_init :: proc(cache: ^SVG_Path_Cache, max_size: int, allocator: mem.Allocator) {
    cache.entries = make(map[string]SVG_Parsed_Path, allocator)
    cache.hit_count = 0
    cache.miss_count = 0
    cache.max_size = max_size
    cache.allocator = allocator
}

// Cleanup SVG Path Cache
svg_path_cache_cleanup :: proc(cache: ^SVG_Path_Cache) {
    // Clear the cache map by creating a new empty map
    cache.entries = make(map[string]SVG_Parsed_Path, cache.allocator)
    cache.hit_count = 0
    cache.miss_count = 0
}

// Get path from cache (or parse if not found)
svg_path_cache_get :: proc(cache: ^SVG_Path_Cache, path_data: string) -> (SVG_Parsed_Path, bool) {
    // Check cache
    if parsed_path := cache.entries[path_data]; len(parsed_path.commands) > 0 {
        // Cache hit
        cache.hit_count += 1
        return parsed_path, true
    }
    
    // Cache miss
    cache.miss_count += 1
    return SVG_Parsed_Path{}, false
}

// Add path to cache
svg_path_cache_add :: proc(cache: ^SVG_Path_Cache, path_data: string, parsed_path: SVG_Parsed_Path) {
    // Only add if we have space and the path is valid
    if len(parsed_path.commands) > 0 && len(cache.entries) < cache.max_size {
        cache.entries[path_data] = parsed_path
    }
}

// Get current cache statistics
svg_path_cache_get_stats :: proc(cache: ^SVG_Path_Cache) -> SVG_Path_Cache_Stats {
    stats := SVG_Path_Cache_Stats{}
    
    total_accesses := cache.hit_count + cache.miss_count
    if total_accesses > 0 {
        stats.hit_rate = f32(cache.hit_count) / f32(total_accesses)
    } else {
        stats.hit_rate = 0.0
    }
    
    stats.current_size = len(cache.entries)
    stats.max_size = cache.max_size
    
    return stats
}

// Phase 10D.5.1: Cache-aware path parsing function
svg_parse_path_cached :: proc(cache: ^SVG_Path_Cache, path_data: string, allocator: mem.Allocator) -> SVG_Parsed_Path {
    // Try cache first
    if cached_path, found := svg_path_cache_get(cache, path_data); found {
        return cached_path
    }
    
    // Parse the path
    parsed_path := svg_parse_path_data(path_data, allocator)
    
    // Add to cache if successful
    if len(parsed_path.commands) > 0 {
        svg_path_cache_add(cache, path_data, parsed_path)
    }
    
    return parsed_path
}

// Phase 10D.5.1: Global path cache for SVG rendering
svg_global_path_cache: ^SVG_Path_Cache
svg_global_path_cache_enabled: bool = false

// Initialize global path cache
svg_init_global_path_cache :: proc(max_size: int, allocator: mem.Allocator) {
    if svg_global_path_cache == nil {
        svg_global_path_cache = new(SVG_Path_Cache)
        svg_path_cache_init(svg_global_path_cache, max_size, allocator)
        svg_global_path_cache_enabled = true
    }
}

// Cleanup global path cache
svg_cleanup_global_path_cache :: proc() {
    if svg_global_path_cache != nil {
        svg_path_cache_cleanup(svg_global_path_cache)
        svg_global_path_cache = nil
        svg_global_path_cache_enabled = false
    }
}

// Get global cache statistics
svg_get_global_cache_stats :: proc() -> SVG_Path_Cache_Stats {
    if svg_global_path_cache != nil {
        return svg_path_cache_get_stats(svg_global_path_cache)
    }
    return SVG_Path_Cache_Stats{}
}

// ============================================================================
// Phase 10D.5.2: Level of Detail (LOD) System for SVG Rendering
// ============================================================================

// LOD Levels
SVG_LOD_Level :: enum {
    High,    // Full quality - all details, no simplification
    Medium,  // Balanced quality - some simplification
    Low,     // Performance mode - significant simplification
    Minimal, // Minimum quality - basic shapes only
}

// LOD Configuration
SVG_LOD_Config :: struct {
    // Automatic LOD selection based on performance
    auto_lod_enabled: bool,
    
    // Performance thresholds (in milliseconds per frame)
    high_lod_threshold: f32,    // > threshold: use High LOD
    medium_lod_threshold: f32,  // > threshold: use Medium LOD
    low_lod_threshold: f32,     // > threshold: use Low LOD
    
    // Manual LOD override
    manual_lod_enabled: bool,
    manual_lod_level: SVG_LOD_Level,
    
    // Current LOD level
    current_lod_level: SVG_LOD_Level,
    
    // Debug visualization
    debug_show_lod: bool,
    debug_show_stats: bool,
}

// Global LOD configuration
svg_global_lod_config: SVG_LOD_Config

// Initialize LOD system with default settings
svg_init_lod_system :: proc() {
    svg_global_lod_config = SVG_LOD_Config{
        auto_lod_enabled = true,
        high_lod_threshold = 16.0,    // ~60 FPS
        medium_lod_threshold = 33.0,  // ~30 FPS
        low_lod_threshold = 50.0,     // ~20 FPS
        manual_lod_enabled = false,
        manual_lod_level = .High,
        current_lod_level = .High,
        debug_show_lod = false,
        debug_show_stats = false,
    }
}

// Update LOD level based on current frame time
svg_update_lod_level :: proc(frame_time_ms: f32) {
    if svg_global_lod_config.manual_lod_enabled {
        // Manual override - use specified level
        svg_global_lod_config.current_lod_level = svg_global_lod_config.manual_lod_level
        return
    }
    
    if !svg_global_lod_config.auto_lod_enabled {
        // Auto LOD disabled - use high quality
        svg_global_lod_config.current_lod_level = .High
        return
    }
    
    // Automatic LOD selection based on frame time
    if frame_time_ms > svg_global_lod_config.low_lod_threshold {
        svg_global_lod_config.current_lod_level = .Low
    } else if frame_time_ms > svg_global_lod_config.medium_lod_threshold {
        svg_global_lod_config.current_lod_level = .Medium
    } else if frame_time_ms > svg_global_lod_config.high_lod_threshold {
        svg_global_lod_config.current_lod_level = .High
    } else {
        svg_global_lod_config.current_lod_level = .High
    }
}

// Get current LOD level
svg_get_current_lod_level :: proc() -> SVG_LOD_Level {
    return svg_global_lod_config.current_lod_level
}

// Set manual LOD override
svg_set_manual_lod :: proc(level: SVG_LOD_Level) {
    svg_global_lod_config.manual_lod_enabled = true
    svg_global_lod_config.manual_lod_level = level
    svg_global_lod_config.current_lod_level = level
}

// Disable manual LOD override
svg_disable_manual_lod :: proc() {
    svg_global_lod_config.manual_lod_enabled = false
}

// Toggle auto LOD
svg_toggle_auto_lod :: proc(enabled: bool) {
    svg_global_lod_config.auto_lod_enabled = enabled
    if enabled {
        svg_global_lod_config.manual_lod_enabled = false
    }
}

// Get LOD level name for display
svg_lod_level_name :: proc(level: SVG_LOD_Level) -> string {
    switch level {
    case .High: return "High"
    case .Medium: return "Medium"
    case .Low: return "Low"
    case .Minimal: return "Minimal"
    }
    return "Unknown"
}

// Apply LOD to path rendering (simplification based on LOD level)
svg_apply_lod_to_path :: proc(path: SVG_Parsed_Path, lod_level: SVG_LOD_Level) -> SVG_Parsed_Path {
    // For now, return the original path (simplification to be implemented)
    // This is a placeholder for future LOD simplification algorithms
    return path
}

// Get LOD statistics for display
svg_get_lod_stats :: proc(frame_time_ms: f32) -> string {
    lod_level := svg_get_current_lod_level()
    
    auto_str := "Manual"
    if svg_global_lod_config.auto_lod_enabled {
        auto_str = "Auto"
    }
    
    return fmt.tprintf("LOD: %s (%s) | %.1fms",
        svg_lod_level_name(lod_level),
        auto_str,
        frame_time_ms
    )
}

// Phase 10D.5.2: LOD-aware path rendering function
svg_render_path_with_lod :: proc(path: SVG_Parsed_Path, render_func: proc(path: SVG_Parsed_Path)) {
    lod_level := svg_get_current_lod_level()
    
    // Apply LOD simplification (placeholder for future implementation)
    lod_path := svg_apply_lod_to_path(path, lod_level)
    
    // Render the LOD-adjusted path
    render_func(lod_path)
}
