import "base:runtime"
import "core:fmt"
import "core:math"
import "core:math/linalg"
import "core:mem"
import assets "project:assets"
import logging "project:logging"
import renderer "project:renderer"
import txt "project:graphics/text"
import sg "vendor-local:sokol-odin/sokol/gfx"

// ============================================================================
// DRAWING PROCEDURES
// ============================================================================

// Matrix and Transform Operations
// ============================================================================

g2d_push_matrix_immediate :: proc() {
	append(&ctx.matrix_stack, ctx.curr_matrix)
}

g2d_pop_matrix_immediate :: proc() {
	if len(ctx.matrix_stack) > 0 {
		ctx.curr_matrix = pop(&ctx.matrix_stack)
	} else {
		ctx.curr_matrix = linalg.MATRIX3F32_IDENTITY
	}
}

g2d_begin_group :: proc(transform: matrix[3, 3]f32, opacity: f32) {
	g2d_push_matrix_immediate()
	ctx.curr_matrix = ctx.curr_matrix * transform

	append(&ctx.opacity_stack, ctx.curr_opacity)
	ctx.curr_opacity *= opacity
}

g2d_end_group :: proc() {
	g2d_pop_matrix_immediate()
	if len(ctx.opacity_stack) > 0 {
		ctx.curr_opacity = pop(&ctx.opacity_stack)
	} else {
		ctx.curr_opacity = 1.0
	}
}

// Gate 3.2: Deferred pushLayer — records Cmd_Push_Layer to the draw list.
// All subsequent draw commands land on an offscreen RT until g2d_pop_layer.
// opacity and blend_mode are applied when the layer is composited back on pop.
//
// IMPORTANT: pushLayer mutates ctx.curr_matrix at RECORDING time so that all
// subsequent draw commands bake translate(-x,-y) into their common.transform.
// The stateless replay loop restores each command's recorded transform, which
// automatically places shapes into RT-local coordinates [0,w]×[0,h].
// popLayer restores ctx.curr_matrix before recording Cmd_Pop_Layer, so the
// composite-back blit is recorded with the parent (screen-space) transform.
g2d_push_layer :: proc(x, y, w, h: f32, opacity: f32 = 1.0, blend_mode: renderer.Blend_Mode = .Normal) {
	renderer.draw_list_push(
		&renderer.active_draw_list,
		renderer.Cmd_Push_Layer {
			common     = _g2d_make_common(.Content),
			x          = x,
			y          = y,
			w          = w,
			h          = h,
			opacity    = opacity,
			blend_mode = blend_mode,
		},
	)
	// Bake coordinate offset into all subsequent commands.
	// Also save+clear the screen-space scissor: shapes are rendered into a pool RT
	// (w×h px), not the screen.  The screen-space scissor would clip everything.
	append(&ctx.matrix_stack, ctx.curr_matrix)
	ctx.curr_matrix = matrix[3, 3]f32{
		1, 0, -x,
		0, 1, -y,
		0, 0, 1,
	}
	append(&ctx.layer_scissor_stack, Layer_Scissor_Save{scissor_on = ctx.curr_scissor_on, scissor = ctx.curr_scissor})
	ctx.curr_scissor_on = false
}

// Gate 3.2: Deferred popLayer — records Cmd_Pop_Layer to the draw list.
g2d_pop_layer :: proc() {
	// Restore parent matrix BEFORE recording Cmd_Pop_Layer so the composite-back
	// blit (emitted during replay) uses the correct screen-space transform.
	if len(ctx.matrix_stack) > 0 {
		ctx.curr_matrix = pop(&ctx.matrix_stack)
	}
	// Restore the screen-space scissor saved on push.
	if len(ctx.layer_scissor_stack) > 0 {
		saved := pop(&ctx.layer_scissor_stack)
		ctx.curr_scissor_on = saved.scissor_on
		ctx.curr_scissor    = saved.scissor
	}
	renderer.draw_list_push(
		&renderer.active_draw_list,
		renderer.Cmd_Pop_Layer{common = _g2d_make_common(.Content)},
	)
}

// Gate 3.3: Deferred setFilter — records Cmd_Set_Filter to the draw list.
// All subsequent draw commands land in an offscreen RT until g2d_clear_filter.
// x/y/w/h is the destination rect for compositing back (usually full screen).
//
// Same coordinate-baking strategy as pushLayer (see comment above).
g2d_set_filter :: proc(filter: renderer.Image_Filter, x, y, w, h: f32) {
	renderer.draw_list_push(
		&renderer.active_draw_list,
		renderer.Cmd_Set_Filter {
			common = _g2d_make_common(.Content),
			filter = filter,
			x      = x,
			y      = y,
			w      = w,
			h      = h,
		},
	)
	// Bake coordinate offset into all subsequent commands.
	// Also save+clear the screen-space scissor: shapes are rendered into a pool RT
	// (w×h px), not the screen.  The screen-space scissor would clip everything.
	append(&ctx.matrix_stack, ctx.curr_matrix)
	ctx.curr_matrix = matrix[3, 3]f32{
		1, 0, -x,
		0, 1, -y,
		0, 0, 1,
	}
	append(&ctx.layer_scissor_stack, Layer_Scissor_Save{scissor_on = ctx.curr_scissor_on, scissor = ctx.curr_scissor})
	ctx.curr_scissor_on = false
}

// Gate 3.3: Deferred clearFilter — records Cmd_Clear_Filter to the draw list.
g2d_clear_filter :: proc() {
	// Restore parent matrix BEFORE recording Cmd_Clear_Filter.
	if len(ctx.matrix_stack) > 0 {
		ctx.curr_matrix = pop(&ctx.matrix_stack)
	}
	// Restore the screen-space scissor saved on push.
	if len(ctx.layer_scissor_stack) > 0 {
		saved := pop(&ctx.layer_scissor_stack)
		ctx.curr_scissor_on = saved.scissor_on
		ctx.curr_scissor    = saved.scissor
	}
	renderer.draw_list_push(
		&renderer.active_draw_list,
		renderer.Cmd_Clear_Filter{common = _g2d_make_common(.Content)},
	)
}

g2d_translate_immediate :: proc(x, y: f32) {
	m := matrix[3, 3]f32{
		1, 0, x,
		0, 1, y,
		0, 0, 1,
	}
	ctx.curr_matrix = ctx.curr_matrix * m
}

g2d_rotate_immediate :: proc(angle_radians: f32) {
	c := math.cos(angle_radians)
	s := math.sin(angle_radians)
	m := matrix[3, 3]f32{
		c, -s, 0,
		s, c, 0,
		0, 0, 1,
	}
	ctx.curr_matrix = ctx.curr_matrix * m
}

g2d_scale_immediate :: proc(sx, sy: f32) {
	m := matrix[3, 3]f32{
		sx, 0, 0,
		0, sy, 0,
		0, 0, 1,
	}
	ctx.curr_matrix = ctx.curr_matrix * m
}

// Aliases for main.odin compatibility
pushMatrix :: g2d_push_matrix
popMatrix :: g2d_pop_matrix
translate :: g2d_translate
scale :: g2d_scale
applyMatrix :: g2d_apply_matrix
applyMatrix4 :: g2d_apply_matrix4

g2d_apply_matrix4 :: proc(m4: matrix[4, 4]f32) {
	g2d_record_apply_matrix4(m4)
}

g2d_apply_matrix4_immediate :: proc(m4: matrix[4, 4]f32) {
	// Map 4x4 -> 2D affine; our linalg matrices use column 3 for translation
	// Keep column mapping so 2D receives the correct translation in logical units

	m3 := matrix[3, 3]f32{
		m4[0, 0], m4[0, 1], m4[0, 3],
		m4[1, 0], m4[1, 1], m4[1, 3],
		0, 0, 1,
	}
	ctx.curr_matrix = ctx.curr_matrix * m3
}

g2d_apply_matrix :: proc(m: matrix[3, 3]f32) {
	g2d_record_apply_matrix(m)
}

g2d_apply_matrix_immediate :: proc(m: matrix[3, 3]f32) {
	ctx.curr_matrix = ctx.curr_matrix * m
}

@(private)
apply_transform :: proc(x, y: f32) -> [2]f32 {
	m := ctx.curr_matrix
	nx := m[0, 0] * x + m[0, 1] * y + m[0, 2]
	ny := m[1, 0] * x + m[1, 1] * y + m[1, 2]

	res := [2]f32{nx * ctx.dpi_scale, ny * ctx.dpi_scale}
	// [TRANSFORM_DIAG] log if needed
	// Apply DPI scaling at the end of the transform chain
	// This makes all coordinate inputs "logical pixels"
	return res
}

// Paint and Gradient Helpers
// ============================================================================

g2d_sample_gradient :: proc(t_in: f32, colors: [][4]f32, stops: []f32) -> [4]f32 {
	t := clamp(t_in, 0, 1)
	if len(colors) == 0 do return {0, 0, 0, 0}
	if len(colors) == 1 do return colors[0]

	if len(stops) == 0 {
		index := int(t * f32(len(colors) - 1))
		return colors[index]
	}

	if t <= stops[0] do return colors[0]
	if t >= stops[len(stops) - 1] do return colors[len(colors) - 1]

	for i in 0 ..< len(stops) - 1 {
		if t >= stops[i] && t <= stops[i + 1] {
			local_t := (t - stops[i]) / (stops[i + 1] - stops[i])
			return linalg.lerp(colors[i], colors[i + 1], local_t)
		}
	}
	return colors[len(colors) - 1]
}

g2d_resolve_paint_color :: proc(px, py: f32, paint: renderer.Paint) -> [4]f32 {
	switch paint.type {
	case .Solid:
		return resolve_color(paint.color)
	case .Linear_Gradient:
		v := paint.linear_end - paint.linear_start
		len_sq := linalg.dot(v, v)
		if len_sq < 0.0001 do return resolve_color(paint.gradient_colors[0] if len(paint.gradient_colors) > 0 else paint.color)

		dx, dy := v.x, v.y
		// Project point onto gradient line
		t := ((px - paint.linear_start.x) * dx + (py - paint.linear_start.y) * dy) / len_sq
		return g2d_sample_gradient(t, paint.gradient_colors, paint.gradient_stops)
	case .Radial_Gradient:
		dist := math.sqrt_f32(
			(px - paint.center.x) * (px - paint.center.x) +
			(py - paint.center.y) * (py - paint.center.y),
		)
		t := dist / paint.radius
		return g2d_sample_gradient(t, paint.gradient_colors, paint.gradient_stops)
	case .Conical_Gradient:
		angle_from_center := math.atan2_f32(py - paint.center.y, px - paint.center.x)
		normalized_angle := (angle_from_center - paint.start_angle) / (math.PI * 2)
		// Wrap to 0-1
		t := normalized_angle - math.floor_f32(normalized_angle)
		return g2d_sample_gradient(t, paint.gradient_colors, paint.gradient_stops)
	case .Pattern, .Image:
		// For patterns/images, the color is typically a tint.
		// Detailed UV mapping logic is handled in the Loop or RenderState,
		// but here we return the tint (usually white or paint.color).
		return {1, 1, 1, paint.opacity}
	}
	return {1, 1, 1, 1}
}

// Primitive Drawing Functions
// ============================================================================

g2d_draw_point_immediate :: proc(x, y: f32, color: [4]f32 = {0, 0, 0, 0}) {
	b := get_batch()
	c := resolve_color(color)
	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		if color == {0, 0, 0, 0} {
			logging.debug(
				.G2D,
				fmt.tprintf("[TEXT_COLOR] default-param used; resolved curr_color=%v", c),
			)
		}
	}
	p := apply_transform(x, y)
	sz: f32 = 1.0

	v1 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{{p.x - sz, p.y - sz}, {0, 0}, c}
	v2 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{{p.x + sz, p.y - sz}, {1, 0}, c}
	v3 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{{p.x + sz, p.y + sz}, {1, 1}, c}
	v4 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{{p.x - sz, p.y + sz}, {0, 1}, c}

	add_vertices(b, v1, v2, v3, v1, v3, v4)
}

g2d_draw_point_scale_immediate :: proc(x, y: f32, color: [4]f32 = {0, 0, 0, 0}) {
	b := get_batch()
	c := resolve_color(color)
	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		if color == {0, 0, 0, 0} {
			logging.debug(
				.G2D,
				fmt.tprintf("[TEXT_COLOR] default-param used; resolved curr_color=%v", c),
			)
		}
	}
	sz: f32 = 0.5

	p1 := apply_transform(x - sz, y - sz)
	p2 := apply_transform(x + sz, y - sz)
	p3 := apply_transform(x + sz, y + sz)
	p4 := apply_transform(x - sz, y + sz)

	v1 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p1, {0, 0}, c}
	v2 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p2, {1, 0}, c}
	v3 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p3, {1, 1}, c}
	v4 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p4, {0, 1}, c}

	add_vertices(b, v1, v2, v3, v1, v3, v4)
}

//Phase 1.4 Updated Line primitves with linc caps
g2d_draw_line_simple :: proc(
	x1, y1, x2, y2: f32,
	color: [4]f32 = {0, 0, 0, 0},
	thickness: f32 = 1.0,
) {
	g2d_draw_line(x1, y1, x2, y2, thickness, color, .Butt)
}

g2d_fill_rect_simple :: proc(x, y, w, h: f32, color: [4]f32) {
	g2d_fill_rect(x, y, w, h, renderer.paint_solid(color))
}

// Image Loading and Drawing
// ============================================================================

// TEMPORARY LAYER VIOLATION — exposes sg.Image in signature
// Resolution: Phase G.1 — replace callers with Image_Handle path
// See: 260309-RuiShin-2D-Completion-Roadmap3.md Phase G
@(private)
g2d_load_image_from_file :: proc(path: string) -> sg.Image {
	img_data, ok := assets.decode_image_from_file(path)
	if !ok do return ctx.white_image

	defer delete(img_data.pixels)

	desc := sg.Image_Desc {
		width        = i32(img_data.width),
		height       = i32(img_data.height),
		pixel_format = .RGBA8,
	}

	// Phase 3.4: Use an explicitly-typed sg.Range to define initial image data
	desc.data.mip_levels[0] = sg.Range {
		ptr  = &img_data.pixels[0],
		size = uint(len(img_data.pixels)),
	}

	// Create the view here once!
	img := sg.make_image(desc)

	// Store the view in our cache map so g2d_end_frame can find it
	// Revert to implicit nested-literal style (as used elsewhere) to match sg.make_view expectations
	ctx.image_views[img.id] = sg.make_view({texture = {image = img}})

	// In a production app, you'd store this view in a table mapped to the Image ID
	return img
}

@(private)
g2d_destroy_image :: proc(img: sg.Image) {
	// 1. Check if we have a view for this image
	if view, ok := ctx.image_views[img.id]; ok {
		sg.destroy_view(view) // Tell the GPU to delete the view
		delete_key(&ctx.image_views, img.id) // Remove the entry from the Odin map
	}

	// 2. Tell the GPU to delete the image itself
	sg.destroy_image(img)
}

//Phase 3.2 draw image
@(private)
g2d_draw_image_immediate :: proc(x, y, w, h: f32, img: sg.Image, color: [4]f32 = {1, 1, 1, 1}) {
	// 1. Switch texture if necessary to maintain batching
	old_image := ctx.curr_image
	ctx.curr_image = img
	b := get_batch()

	// 2. Resolve tint (Architecture: tint multiplies fragment color)
	c := color

	// 3. Transform the 4 corners using our matrix stack
	p1 := apply_transform(x, y)
	p2 := apply_transform(x + w, y)
	p3 := apply_transform(x + w, y + h)
	p4 := apply_transform(x, y + h)

	// 4. Map UVs (0,0 top-left to 1,1 bottom-right)
	v1 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p1, {0, 0}, c}
	v2 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p2, {1, 0}, c}
	v3 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p3, {1, 1}, c}
	v4 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p4, {0, 1}, c}

	// 5. Add to batch (using the "intelligent" helper)
	add_vertices(b, v1, v2, v3, v1, v3, v4)

	// Reset current image so subsequent solid shapes use white_image
	ctx.curr_image = old_image
}

// Clipping Functions
// ============================================================================

g2d_set_clip_immediate :: proc(x, y, w, h: f32) {
	// Apply current transformations to the logical boundaries
	p_min := apply_transform(x, y)
	p_max := apply_transform(x + w, y + h)

	// Construct AABB in physical pixels
	rx := math.min(p_min.x, p_max.x)
	ry := math.min(p_min.y, p_max.y)
	rw := math.abs(p_max.x - p_min.x)
	rh := math.abs(p_max.y - p_min.y)

	new_rect := [4]f32{rx, ry, rw, rh}

	// Intersection with parent clip
	if len(ctx.clip_stack) > 0 {
		parent := ctx.clip_stack[len(ctx.clip_stack) - 1].rect

		ix := math.max(new_rect[0], parent[0])
		iy := math.max(new_rect[1], parent[1])
		iw := math.min(new_rect[0] + new_rect[2], parent[0] + parent[2]) - ix
		ih := math.min(new_rect[1] + new_rect[3], parent[1] + parent[3]) - iy

		if iw < 0 do iw = 0
		if ih < 0 do ih = 0

		new_rect = {ix, iy, iw, ih}
	}

	when logging.DEBUG_G2D 
	{
		fmt.printf(
			"[CLIP_DIAG] push depth=%d rect=%f %f %f %f\n",
			len(ctx.clip_stack) + 1,
			new_rect[0],
			new_rect[1],
			new_rect[2],
			new_rect[3],
		)
	}

	append(&ctx.clip_stack, G2D_Scissor_State{rect = new_rect, target = rawptr(ctx.active_rt)})
	ctx.curr_scissor_on = true
	ctx.curr_scissor = new_rect
}

g2d_reset_clip_immediate :: proc() {
	if len(ctx.clip_stack) > 0 {
		pop(&ctx.clip_stack)
	}

	if len(ctx.clip_stack) > 0 {
		ctx.curr_scissor_on = true
		ctx.curr_scissor = ctx.clip_stack[len(ctx.clip_stack) - 1].rect
	} else {
		ctx.curr_scissor_on = false
	}

	when logging.DEBUG_G2D 
	{
		fmt.printf("[CLIP_DIAG] pop depth=%d active=%v\n", len(ctx.clip_stack), ctx.curr_scissor_on)
	}
}

// Phase 10-X: Clear all clips from stack (for 3D rendering without 2D clipping)
g2d_clear_all_clips :: proc() {
	clear(&ctx.clip_stack)
	ctx.curr_scissor_on = false
}

// Path API
// ============================================================================

g2d_path_begin :: proc() {
	clear(&ctx.current_path)
}

g2d_path_move_to :: proc(x, y: f32) {
	append(&ctx.current_path, renderer.Path_Point{type = .Move_To, p = {x, y}})
}

g2d_path_line_to :: proc(x, y: f32) {
	append(&ctx.current_path, renderer.Path_Point{type = .Line_To, p = {x, y}})
}

g2d_path_arc_to :: proc(x, y, r, start_angle, end_angle: f32, clockwise: bool = true) {
	append(
		&ctx.current_path,
		renderer.Path_Point {
			type = .Arc_To,
			p = {x, y},
			c1 = {r, start_angle},
			c2 = {end_angle, clockwise ? 1.0 : -1.0},
		},
	)
}

g2d_path_quad_to :: proc(cx, cy, x, y: f32) {
	append(&ctx.current_path, renderer.Path_Point{type = .Quad_To, p = {x, y}, c1 = {cx, cy}})
}

g2d_path_cubic_to :: proc(c1x, c1y, c2x, c2y, x, y: f32) {
	append(
		&ctx.current_path,
		renderer.Path_Point{type = .Cubic_To, p = {x, y}, c1 = {c1x, c1y}, c2 = {c2x, c2y}},
	)
}

g2d_path_close :: proc() {
	if len(ctx.current_path) == 0 do return
	append(&ctx.current_path, renderer.Path_Point{type = .Close_Path})
}

// Helper: Tesselate a quadratic bezier into flat points
@(private)
tesselate_quad :: proc(points: ^[dynamic][2]f32, p0, c, p1: [2]f32, segments: int = 24) {
	for i in 1 ..= segments {
		t := f32(i) / f32(segments)
		inv_t := 1.0 - t
		p := inv_t * inv_t * p0 + 2.0 * inv_t * t * c + t * t * p1
		append(points, p)
	}
}

// ============================================================================
// TESSELLATION (Polygon Triangulation)
// ============================================================================

// Tessellate a polygon into triangles using ear clipping algorithm
// Returns indices into the points array forming triangles
tessellate_polygon :: proc(
	points: [][2]f32,
	fill_rule: renderer.Fill_Rule = .NonZero,
	allocator := context.allocator,
) -> (
	indices: []u32,
	ok: bool,
) {
	when logging.DEBUG {
		logging.info(
			.G2D,
			fmt.tprintf("tessellate_polygon: %d points, fill_rule %v", len(points), fill_rule),
		)
	}

	n := len(points)
	if n < 3 {
		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.info(.G2D, "Early return: less than 3 points")
		}
		return nil, false
	}

	// For simple polygons, ear clipping works regardless of fill rule
	// The fill rule affects how we handle self-intersecting paths,
	// but for now we'll implement robust basic ear clipping

	indices_list := make([dynamic]u32, allocator)
	defer if !ok do delete(indices_list)

	// Create index list of remaining vertices
	remaining := make([dynamic]int, allocator)
	defer delete(remaining)

	// Helper checks for valid polygon area to detect degeneracy early
	area: f32 = 0
	for i in 0 ..< n {
		p1 := points[i]
		p2 := points[(i + 1) % n]
		area += (p1.x * p2.y) - (p1.y * p2.x)
	}

	// If area is very small, it's degenerate
	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		logging.info(.G2D, fmt.tprintf("Polygon area: %f", area))
	}
	if math.abs(area) < 0.0001 {
		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.info(.G2D, "Degenerate polygon: area too small")
		}
		return nil, false
	}

	if area > 0 {
		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.info(.G2D, "Polygon winding: CCW")
		}
	} else {
		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.info(.G2D, "Polygon winding: CW")
		}
	}

	// Standardize winding order?
	// Ear clipping (as implemented) technically assumes a specific winding or checks angles relative to it.
	// Actually, standard ear clipping works if we just ensure we pick ears that are convex.
	// The `is_ear` function tests convexity based on the cross product.
	// If the polygon is wound clockwise, all "convex" corners might look reflex or vice versa depending on the check.
	// However, `is_ear` typically checks if the vertex is convex *relative to the polygon interior*.

	// Populate remaining list
	for i in 0 ..< n {
		append(&remaining, i)
	}

	// Ear clipping main loop
	// We iterate until only 3 vertices remain
	params := Ear_Clip_Params {
		points    = points,
		remaining = &remaining,
	}

	stuck_counter := 0
	max_stuck := n * 2 // Safety break

	for len(remaining) > 3 {
		ear_found := false
		n_rem := len(remaining)

		for i in 0 ..< n_rem {
			prev_idx := (i - 1 + n_rem) % n_rem
			next_idx := (i + 1) % n_rem

			prev := remaining[prev_idx]
			curr := remaining[i]
			next := remaining[next_idx]

			// Check if this is an ear
			if is_ear(params, prev, curr, next, area) {
				// Add triangle
				append(&indices_list, u32(prev))
				append(&indices_list, u32(curr))
				append(&indices_list, u32(next))

				// Remove current vertex from remaining list
				ordered_remove(&remaining, i)
				ear_found = true
				stuck_counter = 0
				break
			}
		}

		if !ear_found {
			stuck_counter += 1
			when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
				logging.info(.G2D, fmt.tprintf("No ear found, stuck_counter: %d", stuck_counter))
			}
			if stuck_counter > 2 {
				when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
					logging.info(.G2D, "Failed to find ears after multiple attempts")
				}
				// If we can't find an ear, the polygon might be self-intersecting or twisted
				// in a way that basic ear clipping fails.
				// In a robust implementation, we might try to split it or just triangulate
				// the remaining separately, but for now, fail gracefully.
				return nil, false
			}
		}
	}

	// Add final triangle
	if len(remaining) == 3 {
		append(&indices_list, u32(remaining[0]))
		append(&indices_list, u32(remaining[1]))
		append(&indices_list, u32(remaining[2]))
	}

	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		logging.info(
			.G2D,
			fmt.tprintf(
				"Tessellation successful: %d indices (%d triangles)",
				len(indices_list),
				len(indices_list) / 3,
			),
		)
	}
	return indices_list[:], true
}

Ear_Clip_Params :: struct {
	points:    [][2]f32,
	remaining: ^[dynamic]int,
}

// Check if vertex at curr_idx is an ear
is_ear :: proc(params: Ear_Clip_Params, prev_idx, curr_idx, next_idx: int, area: f32) -> bool {
	p0 := params.points[prev_idx]
	p1 := params.points[curr_idx]
	p2 := params.points[next_idx]

	// Debug logging
	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		logging.info(
			.G2D,
			fmt.tprintf(
				"is_ear check: prev_idx=%d, curr_idx=%d, next_idx=%d",
				prev_idx,
				curr_idx,
				next_idx,
			),
		)
	}
	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		logging.info(
			.G2D,
			fmt.tprintf(
				"  Points: p0=(%.2f, %.2f), p1=(%.2f, %.2f), p2=(%.2f, %.2f)",
				p0.x,
				p0.y,
				p1.x,
				p1.y,
				p2.x,
				p2.y,
			),
		)
	}

	// 1. Check if triangle is convex
	// Cross product z-component: (b.x - a.x)*(c.y - a.y) - (b.y - a.y)*(c.x - a.x)
	// For counter-clockwise winding, convex vertices have a positive cross product (if y-up) or negative (if y-down)?
	// It depends on the coordinate system.
	// Let's assume the standard `is_convex` definition for the polygon's winding.
	// NOTE: We don't enforce winding direction here, which might be an issue if the input is CW.
	// However, valid simple polygons usually have a consistent winding.

	// Robust cross product check
	cross := (p1.x - p0.x) * (p2.y - p0.y) - (p1.y - p0.y) * (p2.x - p0.x)
	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		logging.info(.G2D, fmt.tprintf("  Cross product: %.6f", cross))
	}

	// Determine winding direction from area sign
	// If area is negative, polygon is clockwise wound, so invert the convexity check
	winding_ccw := area > 0
	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		logging.info(.G2D, fmt.tprintf("  Winding CCW: %v", winding_ccw))
	}

	// Epsilon for flatness
	if winding_ccw {
		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.info(
				.G2D,
				fmt.tprintf(
					"  CCW convexity check: cross (%.6f) <= 0.00001? %v",
					cross,
					cross <= 0.00001,
				),
			)
		}
		if cross <= 0.00001 {return false} 	// CCW: convex vertices have positive cross product
	} else {
		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.info(
				.G2D,
				fmt.tprintf(
					"  CW convexity check: cross (%.6f) >= -0.00001? %v",
					cross,
					cross >= -0.00001,
				),
			)
		}
		if cross >= -0.00001 {return false} 	// CW: convex vertices have negative cross product
	}

	// 2. Check if any other vertex is inside this triangle
	// We only need to check reflex vertices of the remaining polygon,
	// but checking all remaining is safer/simpler O(N^2) total.
	for idx in params.remaining {
		if idx == prev_idx || idx == curr_idx || idx == next_idx do continue

		pt := params.points[idx]
		if point_in_triangle(pt, p0, p1, p2) {
			when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
				logging.info(
					.G2D,
					fmt.tprintf(
						"  Point (%.2f, %.2f) is inside triangle - not an ear",
						pt.x,
						pt.y,
					),
				)
			}
			return false
		}
	}

	when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
		logging.info(.G2D, fmt.tprintf("  No points inside triangle - this is an ear!"))
	}
	return true
}

// Check if point p is inside triangle (a, b, c) using barycentric coords
point_in_triangle :: proc(p, a, b, c: [2]f32) -> bool {
	v0 := c - a
	v1 := b - a
	v2 := p - a

	dot00 := linalg.dot(v0, v0)
	dot01 := linalg.dot(v0, v1)
	dot02 := linalg.dot(v0, v2)
	dot11 := linalg.dot(v1, v1)
	dot12 := linalg.dot(v1, v2)

	inv_denom := 1.0 / (dot00 * dot11 - dot01 * dot01)
	// Check for degenerate triangle (collinear vertices in a-b-c)
	if math.is_inf(inv_denom) || math.is_nan(inv_denom) do return false

	u := (dot11 * dot02 - dot01 * dot12) * inv_denom
	v := (dot00 * dot12 - dot01 * dot02) * inv_denom

	// Use epsilon to include points ON the edge, or exclude?
	// Usually for ear clipping "inside" means strict interior, or on-edge counts as intersection?
	// If a vertex lies ON the edge of the potential ear triangle, it breaks the ear.
	// So we should be inclusive with a small epsilon.
	return (u >= -0.0001) && (v >= -0.0001) && (u + v <= 1.0001)
}


// ============================================================================
// Stroke Functions
// ============================================================================

// --- Internal Stroke Helpers ---
@(private)
_g2d_stroke_raw :: proc(points: [][2]f32, stroke: renderer.Stroke) {
	if len(points) < 2 do return
	ctx.curr_image = ctx.white_image
	// Resolve base color for solid fallback/tinting
	c := resolve_paint_color(stroke.paint)

	// Phase 6.1: Use path arena for stroke operations
	path_scratch := g2d_get_path_scratch()
	half_thick := stroke.width * 0.5
	b := get_batch()

	// 1. Calculate extrusions for each point
	extrusions := make([][2]f32, len(points), path_scratch)
	if len(extrusions) == 0 {
		when logging.DEBUG_VERTICES {
			logging.debug(
				.G2D,
				fmt.tprintf(
					"Failed to allocate extrusions. Points: %d, Arena offset: %d",
					len(points),
					ctx.arena.offset,
				),
			)
		}
		return
	}

	for i in 0 ..< len(points) {
		p := points[i]
		v_prev, v_next: [2]f32
		if i > 0 do v_prev = linalg.normalize(p - points[i - 1])
		if i < len(points) - 1 do v_next = linalg.normalize(points[i + 1] - p)

		if i == 0 {
			extrusions[i] = [2]f32{-v_next.y, v_next.x} * half_thick
		} else if i == len(points) - 1 {
			extrusions[i] = [2]f32{-v_prev.y, v_prev.x} * half_thick
		} else {
			// Join logic (Miter)
			n_prev := [2]f32{-v_prev.y, v_prev.x}
			n_next := [2]f32{-v_next.y, v_next.x}
			miter_sum := n_prev + n_next
			miter_dir: [2]f32
			if linalg.length(miter_sum) > 0.001 {
				miter_dir = linalg.normalize(miter_sum)
			} else {
				miter_dir = n_prev
			}
			k := linalg.dot(miter_dir, n_prev)

			if k > 0.001 {
				miter_len := half_thick / k

				// SVG Miter Limit: miter_length / stroke_width > miterlimit
				// miter_length = half_thick / k
				// stroke_width = 2 * half_thick
				// So: (half_thick / k) / (2 * half_thick) = 1 / (2 * k)
				miter_ratio := 1.0 / (2.0 * k)

				if miter_ratio > stroke.miter_limit {
					// Bevel join: use the bisector but clamp to half_thick
					// This creates a flat edge between the two segments
					bevel_dir := linalg.normalize(n_prev + n_next)
					extrusions[i] = bevel_dir * half_thick
				} else {
					extrusions[i] = miter_dir * miter_len
				}
			} else {
				extrusions[i] = n_prev * half_thick
			}
		}
	}

	// 2. Emit vertices for triangle strips
	for i in 0 ..< len(points) - 1 {
		p1, p2 := points[i], points[i + 1]
		ext1, ext2 := extrusions[i], extrusions[i + 1]

		tp1_a := apply_transform(p1.x + ext1.x, p1.y + ext1.y)
		tp1_b := apply_transform(p1.x - ext1.x, p1.y - ext1.y)
		tp2_a := apply_transform(p2.x + ext2.x, p2.y + ext2.y)
		tp2_b := apply_transform(p2.x - ext2.x, p2.y - ext2.y)

		// For now, resolve color per-segment using start point
		cur_c := _g2d_resolve_vertex_color(
			p1.x,
			p1.y,
			stroke.paint,
			p1.x,
			p1.y,
			0,
			0,
			stroke.width,
		)

		v1 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{tp1_a, {0, 0}, cur_c}
		v2 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{tp2_a, {1, 0}, cur_c}
		v3 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{tp2_b, {1, 1}, cur_c}
		v4 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{tp1_b, {0, 1}, cur_c}

		add_vertices(b, v1, v2, v3, v1, v3, v4)
	}

	// 3. Handle Caps
	if stroke.cap == .Round {
		for i in ([]int{0, len(points) - 1}) {
			p := points[i]
			ext := extrusions[i]
			angle := math.atan2(ext.y, ext.x)
			if i == 0 {
				_g2d_stroke_draw_corner_arc(p.x, p.y, half_thick, angle, c)
			} else {
				_g2d_stroke_draw_corner_arc(p.x, p.y, half_thick, angle + math.PI, c)
			}
		}
	} else if stroke.cap == .Square {
		if len(points) >= 2 {
			v_start := linalg.normalize(points[1] - points[0])
			v_end := linalg.normalize(points[len(points) - 1] - points[len(points) - 2])
			ext_start := extrusions[0]
			ext_end := extrusions[len(points) - 1]
			p_start := points[0]
			p_end := points[len(points) - 1]

			// Start cap
			s1 := apply_transform(p_start.x + ext_start.x, p_start.y + ext_start.y)
			s2 := apply_transform(p_start.x - ext_start.x, p_start.y - ext_start.y)
			s3 := apply_transform(
				p_start.x - ext_start.x - v_start.x * half_thick,
				p_start.y - ext_start.y - v_start.y * half_thick,
			)
			s4 := apply_transform(
				p_start.x + ext_start.x - v_start.x * half_thick,
				p_start.y + ext_start.y - v_start.y * half_thick,
			)
			add_vertices(
				b,
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{s1, {0, 0}, c},
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{s2, {1, 0}, c},
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{s3, {1, 1}, c},
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{s1, {0, 0}, c},
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{s3, {1, 1}, c},
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{s4, {0, 1}, c},
			)

			// End cap
			e1 := apply_transform(p_end.x + ext_end.x, p_end.y + ext_end.y)
			e2 := apply_transform(p_end.x - ext_end.x, p_end.y - ext_end.y)
			e3 := apply_transform(
				p_end.x - ext_end.x + v_end.x * half_thick,
				p_end.y - ext_end.y + v_end.y * half_thick,
			)
			e4 := apply_transform(
				p_end.x + ext_end.x + v_end.x * half_thick,
				p_end.y + ext_end.y + v_end.y * half_thick,
			)
			add_vertices(
				b,
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{e1, {0, 0}, c},
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{e2, {1, 0}, c},
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{e3, {1, 1}, c},
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{e1, {0, 0}, c},
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{e3, {1, 1}, c},
				renderer.G2D_Vertex{e4, {0, 1}, c},
			)
		}
	}
} //end of _g2d_stroke_raw

@(private)
_g2d_stroke_dashed :: proc(points: [][2]f32, stroke: renderer.Stroke) {
	if len(points) < 2 do return
	if len(stroke.dash_array) == 0 {
		_g2d_stroke_raw(points, stroke)
		return
	}

	arena_alloc := mem.arena_allocator(&ctx.arena)

	// Implementation: Flattened path distance-based segmentation
	total_dist: f32 = 0
	dash_idx := 0
	dash_offset := stroke.dash_offset
	// Correct for initial dash offset
	pattern_len: f32 = 0
	for d in stroke.dash_array do pattern_len += d
	if pattern_len == 0 do return

	dash_offset = math.mod(dash_offset, pattern_len)
	if dash_offset < 0 do dash_offset += pattern_len

	curr_dist := -dash_offset
	active_points := make([dynamic][2]f32, arena_alloc)

	for i in 0 ..< len(points) - 1 {
		p1, p2 := points[i], points[i + 1]
		seg_vec := p2 - p1
		seg_len := linalg.length(seg_vec)
		if seg_len < 0.001 do continue

		seg_dir := seg_vec / seg_len
		pos: f32 = 0

		for pos < seg_len {
			// Find current dash state using modulo phase
			at := curr_dist + pos
			phase := math.mod(at, pattern_len)
			if phase < 0 do phase += pattern_len

			d_sum: f32 = 0
			dash_idx = 0
			for i in 0 ..< len(stroke.dash_array) {
				dash_idx = i
				d := stroke.dash_array[i]
				if phase < d_sum + d {
					break
				}
				d_sum += d
			}

			is_on := (dash_idx % 2 == 0)
			remaining_in_dash := (d_sum + stroke.dash_array[dash_idx]) - phase
			// Safety: Ensure we always make progress to avoid infinite loops
			step := math.max(0.001, math.min(seg_len - pos, remaining_in_dash))

			if is_on || stroke.has_dash_paint {
				paint := is_on ? stroke.paint : stroke.dash_paint

				// Add points to current dash segment
				if len(active_points) == 0 {
					append(&active_points, p1 + seg_dir * pos)
				}
				append(&active_points, p1 + seg_dir * (pos + step))

				// If we finished a dash part, flush it
				if step == remaining_in_dash || pos + step == seg_len {
					if len(active_points) >= 2 {
						_g2d_stroke_raw(active_points[:], stroke)
					}
					clear(&active_points)
				}
			} else {
				// Off dash, just clear active points
				clear(&active_points)
			}

			pos += step
		}
		curr_dist += seg_len
	}
} // end of _g2d_stroke_dashed

//Phase 1.4 rounded corner rectangles
@(private)
_g2d_stroke_draw_corner_arc :: proc(cx, cy, r: f32, start_angle: f32, color: [4]f32) {
	ctx.curr_image = ctx.white_image
	b := get_batch()
	segments := 24
	center := apply_transform(cx, cy)

	for i in 0 ..< segments {
		t1 := start_angle + (f32(i) / f32(segments)) * (math.PI / 2.0)
		t2 := start_angle + (f32(i + 1) / f32(segments)) * (math.PI / 2.0)
		p1 := apply_transform(cx + math.cos(t1) * r, cy + math.sin(t1) * r)
		p2 := apply_transform(cx + math.cos(t2) * r, cy + math.sin(t2) * r)

		v1 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{center, {0.5, 0.5}, color}
		v2 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p1, {0, 0}, color}
		v3 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p2, {0, 0}, color}

		add_vertices(b, v1, v2, v3)
	}
} //end _g2d_stroke_draw_corner_arc


g2d_path_stroke_immediate :: proc(stroke: renderer.Stroke) {
	if len(ctx.current_path) < 2 do return
	points := _g2d_get_flat_points()
	if len(points) < 2 do return

	if len(stroke.dash_array) == 0 {
		_g2d_stroke_raw(points[:], stroke)
	} else {
		_g2d_stroke_dashed(points[:], stroke)
	}
} // end of g2d_path_stroke

// --- Phase 11: Advanced Stroke Implementation ---

g2d_stroke_rect :: proc(x, y, w, h: f32, stroke: renderer.Stroke, shadow: renderer.Shadow = {}) {
	g2d_path_begin()
	g2d_path_move_to(x, y)
	g2d_path_line_to(x + w, y)
	g2d_path_line_to(x + w, y + h)
	g2d_path_line_to(x, y + h)
	g2d_path_close()
	g2d_path_stroke(stroke)
} //end of g2d_stroke_rect

g2d_stroke_rect_rounded :: proc(
	x, y, w, h, radius: f32,
	stroke: renderer.Stroke,
	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	g2d_path_begin()
	g2d_path_move_to(x + radius, y)
	g2d_path_line_to(x + w - radius, y)
	g2d_path_arc_to(x + w - radius, y + radius, radius, -math.PI * 0.5, 0, true)
	g2d_path_line_to(x + w, y + h - radius)
	g2d_path_arc_to(x + w - radius, y + h - radius, radius, 0, math.PI * 0.5, true)
	g2d_path_line_to(x + radius, y + h - radius)
	g2d_path_arc_to(x + radius, y + h - radius, radius, math.PI * 0.5, math.PI, true)
	g2d_path_line_to(x, y + radius)
	g2d_path_arc_to(x + radius, y + radius, radius, math.PI, math.PI * 1.5, true)
	g2d_path_close()
	g2d_path_stroke(stroke)
} //end of g2d_stroke_rect_rounded

g2d_stroke_line :: proc(x1, y1, x2, y2: f32, stroke: renderer.Stroke) {
	g2d_path_begin()
	g2d_path_move_to(x1, y1)
	g2d_path_line_to(x2, y2)
	g2d_path_stroke(stroke)
} //end of g2d_stroke_line

g2d_stroke_polyline :: proc(points: [][2]f32, stroke: renderer.Stroke) {
	if len(points) < 2 do return
	g2d_path_begin()
	g2d_path_move_to(points[0].x, points[0].y)
	for i in 1 ..< len(points) {
		g2d_path_line_to(points[i].x, points[i].y)
	}
	g2d_path_stroke(stroke)
} //end of g2d_stroke_polyline

g2d_stroke_polygon :: proc(
	points: [][2]f32,
	stroke: renderer.Stroke,
	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	if len(points) < 3 do return
	g2d_path_begin()
	g2d_path_move_to(points[0].x, points[0].y)
	for i in 1 ..< len(points) {
		g2d_path_line_to(points[i].x, points[i].y)
	}
	g2d_path_close()
	g2d_path_stroke(stroke)
} //end of g2d_stroke_polygon

g2d_stroke_circle :: proc(
	x, y, radius: f32,
	stroke: renderer.Stroke,
	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	g2d_path_begin()
	g2d_path_move_to(x + radius, y)
	g2d_path_arc_to(x, y, radius, 0, math.PI * 2.0, true)
	g2d_path_close()
	g2d_path_stroke(stroke)
} //end of g2d_stroke_circle

g2d_stroke_ellipse :: proc(
	x, y, rx, ry: f32,
	stroke: renderer.Stroke,
	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	// Ellipse using 4 Bezier curves
	kappa :: 0.55228475
	ox := rx * kappa
	oy := ry * kappa

	g2d_path_begin()
	g2d_path_move_to(x - rx, y)
	g2d_path_cubic_to(x - rx, y - oy, x - ox, y - ry, x, y - ry)
	g2d_path_cubic_to(x + ox, y - ry, x + rx, y - oy, x + rx, y)
	g2d_path_cubic_to(x + rx, y + oy, x + ox, y + ry, x, y + ry)
	g2d_path_cubic_to(x - ox, y + ry, x - rx, y + oy, x - rx, y)
	g2d_path_close()
	g2d_path_stroke(stroke)
} //end of g2d_stroke_ellipse

g2d_stroke_arc :: proc(
	x, y, radius, start_angle, end_angle: f32,
	stroke: renderer.Stroke,
	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	g2d_path_begin()
	sx := x + math.cos(start_angle) * radius
	sy := y + math.sin(start_angle) * radius
	g2d_path_move_to(sx, sy)
	g2d_path_arc_to(x, y, radius, start_angle, end_angle, end_angle > start_angle)
	g2d_path_stroke(stroke)
} //end of g2d_stroke_arc

g2d_stroke_star :: proc(
	x, y: f32,
	num_points: int,
	inner_radius, outer_radius: f32,
	stroke: renderer.Stroke,
	shadow: renderer.Shadow = {},
) {
	pts := math.max(num_points, 5)
	g2d_path_begin()
	angle_step := math.PI / f32(pts)

	for i in 0 ..< pts * 2 {
		r := (i % 2 == 0) ? outer_radius : inner_radius
		angle := f32(i) * angle_step - math.PI * 0.5
		curr_x := x + math.cos(angle) * r
		curr_y := y + math.sin(angle) * r
		if i == 0 {
			g2d_path_move_to(curr_x, curr_y)
		} else {
			g2d_path_line_to(curr_x, curr_y)
		}
	}
	g2d_path_close()
	g2d_path_stroke(stroke)
} //end of g2d_stroke_star

g2d_stroke_bezier_quadratic :: proc(x1, y1, cx, cy, x2, y2: f32, stroke: renderer.Stroke) {
	g2d_path_begin()
	g2d_path_move_to(x1, y1)
	g2d_path_quad_to(cx, cy, x2, y2)
	g2d_path_stroke(stroke)
} //end of g2d_stroke_bezier_quadratic

g2d_stroke_bezier_cubic :: proc(x1, y1, c1x, c1y, c2x, c2y, x2, y2: f32, stroke: renderer.Stroke) {
	g2d_path_begin()
	g2d_path_move_to(x1, y1)
	g2d_path_cubic_to(c1x, c1y, c2x, c2y, x2, y2)
	g2d_path_stroke(stroke)
} //end of g2d_stroke_bezier_cubic

// Phase 11: Gradient Stroke Implementation

g2d_stroke_gradient_immediate :: proc(
	points: [][2]f32,
	paint: renderer.Paint,
	width: f32,
	options: renderer.Gradient_Stroke_Options = {},
) {
	if len(points) < 2 do return

	// 1. Calculate path length and cumulative distances
	// Phase 6.1: Use path arena for stroke operations
	path_scratch := g2d_get_path_scratch()
	dists := make([dynamic]f32, 0, len(points), path_scratch)

	// 1. Calculate extrusions
	is_closed := linalg.distance(points[0], points[len(points) - 1]) < 0.001
	extrusions := make([][2]f32, len(points), path_scratch)
	total_len: f32 = 0
	append(&dists, 0)
	for i in 1 ..< len(points) {
		d := linalg.distance(points[i], points[i - 1])
		total_len += d
		append(&dists, total_len)
	}

	// 2. Stroke the path with gradient colors
	_g2d_stroke_gradient_impl(points, dists, total_len, paint, width)

	// 3. Draw edge lines if requested
	if options.edge_lines {
		left_edge := make([dynamic][2]f32, 0, len(points), path_scratch)
		right_edge := make([dynamic][2]f32, 0, len(points), path_scratch)

		half_thick := width * 0.5

		for i in 0 ..< len(points) {
			p := points[i]
			v_prev, v_next: [2]f32
			if i > 0 do v_prev = linalg.normalize(p - points[i - 1])
			if i < len(points) - 1 do v_next = linalg.normalize(points[i + 1] - p)

			normal: [2]f32
			if i == 0 {
				normal = {-v_next.y, v_next.x}
			} else if i == len(points) - 1 {
				normal = {-v_prev.y, v_prev.x}
			} else {
				n_prev := [2]f32{-v_prev.y, v_prev.x}
				n_next := [2]f32{-v_next.y, v_next.x}
				sum := n_prev + n_next
				if linalg.length(sum) > 0.001 {
					normal = linalg.normalize(sum)
					k := linalg.dot(normal, n_prev)
					if k > 0.1 {
						normal = normal * (1.0 / k)
					}
				} else {
					normal = n_prev
				}
			}

			append(&left_edge, p + normal * half_thick)
			append(&right_edge, p - normal * half_thick)
		}

		edge_stroke := renderer.Stroke {
			width = options.edge_width > 0 ? options.edge_width : 1.0,
			paint = {type = .Solid, color = options.edge_color},
			cap = .Round,
			join = .Round,
		}

		g2d_stroke_polyline(left_edge[:], edge_stroke)
		g2d_stroke_polyline(right_edge[:], edge_stroke)
	}
} //end of g2d_stroke_gradient

@(private)
_g2d_stroke_gradient_impl :: proc(
	points: [][2]f32,
	dists: [dynamic]f32,
	total_len: f32,
	paint: renderer.Paint,
	width: f32,
) {
	// Phase 6.1: Use path arena for stroke operations
	path_scratch := g2d_get_path_scratch()
	b := get_batch()
	half_thick := width * 0.5

	// Helper to sample gradient
	// Helper to sample gradient from parallel arrays
	sample_grad :: proc(t: f32, stops: []f32, colors: [][4]f32) -> [4]f32 {
		if len(stops) == 0 || len(colors) == 0 do return {1, 1, 1, 1}
		if len(stops) != len(colors) do return {1, 0, 1, 1} // Mismatch error color

		if t <= stops[0] do return colors[0]
		if t >= stops[len(stops) - 1] do return colors[len(stops) - 1]

		for i in 0 ..< len(stops) - 1 {
			p1 := stops[i]
			p2 := stops[i + 1]
			if t >= p1 && t <= p2 {
				c1 := colors[i]
				c2 := colors[i + 1]
				factor := (t - p1) / (p2 - p1)
				return c1 + (c2 - c1) * factor
			}
		}
		return colors[len(stops) - 1]
	}

	// 1. Calculate extrusions
	is_closed := linalg.distance(points[0], points[len(points) - 1]) < 0.001
	extrusions := make([][2]f32, len(points), path_scratch)
	for i in 0 ..< len(points) {
		p := points[i]
		v_prev, v_next: [2]f32
		if i > 0 {
			v_prev = linalg.normalize(p - points[i - 1])
		} else if is_closed {
			v_prev = linalg.normalize(p - points[len(points) - 2])
		}

		if i < len(points) - 1 {
			v_next = linalg.normalize(points[i + 1] - p)
		} else if is_closed {
			v_next = linalg.normalize(points[1] - p)
		}

		if i == 0 && !is_closed {
			extrusions[i] = [2]f32{-v_next.y, v_next.x} * half_thick
		} else if i == len(points) - 1 && !is_closed {
			extrusions[i] = [2]f32{-v_prev.y, v_prev.x} * half_thick
		} else {
			n_prev := [2]f32{-v_prev.y, v_prev.x}
			n_next := [2]f32{-v_next.y, v_next.x}
			miter_sum := n_prev + n_next
			miter_dir: [2]f32
			if linalg.length(miter_sum) > 0.001 {
				miter_dir = linalg.normalize(miter_sum)
			} else {
				miter_dir = n_prev
			}
			k := linalg.dot(miter_dir, n_prev)
			if k > 0.001 {
				extrusions[i] = miter_dir * (half_thick / k)
			} else {
				extrusions[i] = n_prev * half_thick
			}
		}
	}

	// 2. Emit vertices
	inv_len := total_len > 0 ? 1.0 / total_len : 0

	for i in 0 ..< len(points) - 1 {
		p1, p2 := points[i], points[i + 1]
		ext1, ext2 := extrusions[i], extrusions[i + 1]

		tp1_a := apply_transform(p1.x + ext1.x, p1.y + ext1.y)
		tp1_b := apply_transform(p1.x - ext1.x, p1.y - ext1.y)
		tp2_a := apply_transform(p2.x + ext2.x, p2.y + ext2.y)
		tp2_b := apply_transform(p2.x - ext2.x, p2.y - ext2.y)

		t1 := dists[i] * inv_len
		t2 := dists[i + 1] * inv_len

		c1 := sample_grad(t1, paint.gradient_stops[:], paint.gradient_colors[:])
		c2 := sample_grad(t2, paint.gradient_stops[:], paint.gradient_colors[:])

		v1 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{tp1_a, {0, 0}, c1}
		v2 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{tp2_a, {1, 0}, c2}
		v3 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{tp2_b, {1, 1}, c2}
		v4 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{tp1_b, {0, 1}, c1}

		add_vertices(b, v1, v2, v3, v1, v3, v4)
	}
} //end proc _g2d_stroke_gradient_impl

// Recording is not supported on WASM (no file write access).
_Rec_File_Handle :: rawptr

_rec_file_open :: proc(path: string) -> (_Rec_File_Handle, bool) {
	return nil, false
