/**
@documentation: SVG Parser
- Conservative XML reader for SVG parsing using Odin's core XML parser
- Simple DOM approach with arena allocators for temporary buffers
- No global state: all parsing state is local to the parse function
- Designed to map SVG elements to RSD (RuiShin Drawing) format
- Phase 10C.1: XML reader selection/integration
- 
- NOTE: This is a minimal implementation for Phase 10C.1.
- Full XML parser integration will be completed in later tasks.
- The current Odin XML parser structure requires further investigation.
*/

import "core:encoding/xml"
import "core:fmt"
import "core:math"
import "core:math/linalg"
import "core:mem"
import "core:strconv"
import "core:strings"
import renderer "project:renderer"
import logging "project:logging"

// SVG element types (subset for MVP)
SVG_Element_Type :: enum {
	None,
	Root, // <svg>
	Group, // <g>
	Defs, // <defs>
	Rect, // <rect>
	Circle, // <circle>
	Ellipse, // <ellipse>
	Line, // <line>
	Polyline, // <polyline>
	Polygon, // <polygon>
	Path, // <path>
	Text, // <text> (for later)
	LinearGradient, // <linearGradient> (10C.10)
	RadialGradient, // <radialGradient> (10C.10)
	ClipPath, // <clipPath> (Phase 12)
	Mask, // <mask> (Phase 12)
}

// SVG paint types (fill and stroke)
SVG_Paint :: union {
	SVG_Solid_Color,
	SVG_None,
	SVG_Gradient_Ref, // 10C.10: Gradient reference
}

SVG_Solid_Color :: [4]f32 // RGBA
SVG_None :: struct {}

// SVG style attributes (MVP subset)
SVG_Style :: struct {
	fill:              SVG_Paint,
	stroke:            SVG_Paint,
	stroke_width:      f32,
	stroke_linecap:    renderer.Line_Cap,
	stroke_linejoin:   renderer.Line_Join,
	fill_rule:         u8, // 0=NonZero, 1=EvenOdd (mapped from rsd.Fill_Rule)
	opacity:           f32,
	stroke_dasharray:  []f32,
	stroke_dashoffset: f32,
	stroke_miterlimit: f32,
	// 10C2.6: Original color string values for theme lookup
	fill_color_name:   string,
	stroke_color_name: string,
	// Phase 12: Clipping and Masking
	clip_path:         string, // url(#id)
	mask:              string, // url(#id)
	clip_rule:         u8, // 0=NonZero, 1=EvenOdd
}

// Element-specific data (union for different element types)
SVG_Element_Data :: union {
	SVG_Rect_Data,
	SVG_Circle_Data,
	SVG_Ellipse_Data,
	SVG_Line_Data,
	SVG_Polyline_Data,
	SVG_Polygon_Data,
	SVG_Path_Data,
	SVG_Text_Data,
	SVG_Gradient, // 10C.10: Gradient definition (linear or radial)
	SVG_Root_Data, // Store viewBox and mapping info
}

SVG_Rect_Data :: struct {
	x:      f32,
	y:      f32,
	width:  f32,
	height: f32,
	rx:     f32, // corner radius x
	ry:     f32, // corner radius y
}

SVG_Circle_Data :: struct {
	cx: f32,
	cy: f32,
	r:  f32,
}

SVG_Ellipse_Data :: struct {
	cx: f32,
	cy: f32,
	rx: f32,
	ry: f32,
}

SVG_Line_Data :: struct {
	x1: f32,
	y1: f32,
	x2: f32,
	y2: f32,
}

SVG_Polyline_Data :: struct {
	points: [][2]f32,
}

SVG_Polygon_Data :: struct {
	points: [][2]f32,
}

SVG_Path_Data :: struct {
	d:          string, // Original path data string
	parsed:     SVG_Parsed_Path, // Parsed path commands (10C.4)
	has_parsed: bool, // True if parsing succeeded
}

SVG_Text_Data :: struct {
	x:               f32,
	y:               f32,
	text:            string,
	font_size:       f32,
	anchor:          u8, // 0=Start, 1=Middle, 2=End (mapped from rsd.Text_Anchor)
	// Phase 10D.4.2: Extended text attributes
	font_family:     string,
	font_weight:     u8, // 0=Normal, 1=Bold (mapped from rsd.Font_Weight)
	font_style:      u8, // 0=Normal, 1=Italic (mapped from rsd.Font_Style)
	text_decoration: u8, // Bit flags: 1=Underline, 2=Overline, 4=LineThrough
	fill:            SVG_Paint,
	stroke:          SVG_Paint,
	stroke_width:    f32,
	opacity:         f32,
}

SVG_Root_Data :: struct {
	viewbox: [4]f32,
}

// ============================================================================
// Phase 10C.10: Gradient Structures
// ============================================================================

// SVG gradient types
SVG_Gradient_Type :: enum {
	Linear,
	Radial,
}

// SVG gradient units
SVG_Gradient_Units :: enum {
	UserSpaceOnUse,
	ObjectBoundingBox,
}

// SVG gradient stop
SVG_Gradient_Stop :: struct {
	offset: f32, // 0.0 to 1.0
	color:  [4]f32, // RGBA
}

// SVG linear gradient data
SVG_Linear_Gradient_Data :: struct {
	x1: f32, // Start x
	y1: f32, // Start y
	x2: f32, // End x
	y2: f32, // End y
}

// SVG radial gradient data
SVG_Radial_Gradient_Data :: struct {
	cx: f32, // Center x
	cy: f32, // Center y
	r:  f32, // Radius
	fx: f32, // Focal point x
	fy: f32, // Focal point y
}

// SVG gradient definition (stored in <defs>)
SVG_Gradient :: struct {
	id:          string,
	type:        SVG_Gradient_Type,
	units:       SVG_Gradient_Units,
	transform:   matrix[3, 3]f32,
	stops:       []SVG_Gradient_Stop,
	// Gradient-specific data (union)
	linear_data: SVG_Linear_Gradient_Data,
	radial_data: SVG_Radial_Gradient_Data,
}

// SVG gradient reference (used in fill/stroke)
SVG_Gradient_Ref :: struct {
	id: string, // Reference to gradient in <defs>
}

// Base SVG element structure
SVG_Element :: struct {
	type:      SVG_Element_Type,
	id:        string,
	transform: matrix[3, 3]f32,
	style:     SVG_Style,
	data:      SVG_Element_Data,
	children:  []SVG_Element, // for container elements (svg, g, defs)
}

// Transform stack for hierarchical elements
Transform_Stack :: struct {
	stack: [dynamic]matrix[3, 3]f32,
}

// Parser context (no global state)
SVG_Parser_Context :: struct {
	allocator:             mem.Arena, // For temporary allocations
	root:                  SVG_Element,
	warnings:              [dynamic]string,
	// ViewBox and preserveAspectRatio for <svg> root
	viewbox:               [4]f32, // x, y, width, height
	preserve_aspect_ratio: string,
	transform_stack:       Transform_Stack,
	id_map:                map[string]^SVG_Element, // For ID resolution (Phase 12)
	// Phase 12: Style inheritance stack for fill/stroke properties
	style_stack:           [dynamic]SVG_Style, // Stack of parent styles for inheritance
}

// Default SVG style values (function to avoid compile-time constant issues with unions)
default_svg_style :: proc() -> SVG_Style {
	style: SVG_Style

	// Initialize fill paint to "none" so elements inherit from parents
	// Per SVG spec, if fill is not specified, it should inherit from parent
	style.fill = SVG_None{}

	// Initialize stroke paint (none)
	style.stroke = SVG_None{}

	style.stroke_width = 1.0
	style.stroke_linecap = .Butt
	style.stroke_linejoin = .Miter
	style.fill_rule = 0 // NonZero
	style.opacity = 1.0
	style.stroke_dasharray = {}
	style.stroke_dashoffset = 0.0
	style.clip_path = ""
	style.mask = ""
	style.clip_rule = 0 // NonZero
	return style
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Main Entry Point
- Parse SVG data from byte array
- Returns root SVG element and list of warnings
- Uses arena allocator for temporary allocations
- All allocations are freed when arena is reset
- 
- NOTE: This is a minimal implementation for Phase 10C.1.
- Full XML parser integration will be completed in later tasks.
*/
parse_svg :: proc(data: []byte, allocator: mem.Arena) -> (SVG_Element, [dynamic]string) {
	ctx: SVG_Parser_Context
	ctx.allocator = allocator
	ctx.warnings = make([dynamic]string)
	ctx.viewbox = {0, 0, 0, 0}
	ctx.preserve_aspect_ratio = "xMidYMid meet"

	// Initialize transform stack with identity matrix
	transform_stack_init(&ctx.transform_stack)
	
	// Phase 12: Initialize style inheritance stack
	ctx.style_stack = make([dynamic]SVG_Style, 0, 16)

	// Parse XML document
	doc, err := xml.parse_bytes(data)
	if err != nil {
		append(&ctx.warnings, fmt.tprintf("XML parse error: %v", err))
		return {}, ctx.warnings
	}

	// Find the root <svg> element (usually the first element in the document)
	root_node: ^xml.Element
	for i := 0; i < len(doc.elements); i += 1 {
		if doc.elements[i].ident == "svg" {
			root_node = &doc.elements[i]
			break
		}
	}

	if root_node == nil {
		append(&ctx.warnings, "No <svg> root element found")
		return {}, ctx.warnings
	}

	// Parse root attributes
	root_attrs := make(map[string]string)
	for attr in root_node.attribs {
		root_attrs[attr.key] = attr.val
	}

	if val, ok := root_attrs["viewBox"]; ok {
		// Simple viewbox parsing: four floats
		parts := strings.split(val, " ")
		if len(parts) == 4 {
			ctx.viewbox[0] = parse_float_attr(parts[0])
			ctx.viewbox[1] = parse_float_attr(parts[1])
			ctx.viewbox[2] = parse_float_attr(parts[2])
			ctx.viewbox[3] = parse_float_attr(parts[3])
		}
	}

	// Walk XML tree recursively
	root_el := parse_xml_node_to_svg_element(&ctx, doc, root_node)

	return root_el, ctx.warnings
}

@(private)
parse_xml_node_to_svg_element :: proc(
	ctx: ^SVG_Parser_Context,
	doc: ^xml.Document,
	node: ^xml.Element,
) -> SVG_Element {
	el: SVG_Element
	el.id = ""

	// Map tag to element type
	switch node.ident {
	case "svg":
		el.type = .Root
	case "g":
		el.type = .Group
	case "path":
		el.type = .Path
	case "rect":
		el.type = .Rect
	case "circle":
		el.type = .Circle
	case "ellipse":
		el.type = .Ellipse
	case "line":
		el.type = .Line
	case "polyline":
		el.type = .Polyline
	case "polygon":
		el.type = .Polygon
	case "text":
		el.type = .Text
	case "defs":
		el.type = .Defs
	case "clipPath":
		el.type = .ClipPath
	case "mask":
		el.type = .Mask
	case:
		el.type = .None
	}

	// Parse attributes
	attrs := make(map[string]string)
	for attr in node.attribs {
		attrs[attr.key] = attr.val
		if attr.key == "id" do el.id = attr.val
	}

	// Handle transforms
	el.transform = identity_matrix()
	if transform_str, ok := attrs["transform"]; ok {
		el.transform = parse_transform_attr(transform_str)
	}

	// Push transform for children
	old_transform := transform_stack_get_current(&ctx.transform_stack)
	append(&ctx.transform_stack.stack, old_transform * el.transform)

	// Parse style from attributes
	el.style = parse_element_style(attrs)

	// Phase 12: Inherit fill/stroke from parent if not explicitly set
	// This is needed for Tiger SVG where paths inherit colors from <g> elements
	// Must be done BEFORE pushing to stack so we can access parent styles
	if el.type != .Root && el.type != .Group {
		// Check if stroke is not set (still default "none")
		if none_paint, is_none := el.style.stroke.(SVG_None); is_none {
			// Inherit stroke from parent stack (search from top down)
			for i := len(ctx.style_stack) - 1; i >= 0; i -= 1 {
				if parent_stroke, ok := ctx.style_stack[i].stroke.(SVG_Solid_Color); ok {
					el.style.stroke = parent_stroke
					break
				}
			}
		}
		
		// Check if fill is not set (still default "none")
		if none_paint, is_none := el.style.fill.(SVG_None); is_none {
			// Inherit fill from parent stack (search from top down)
			for i := len(ctx.style_stack) - 1; i >= 0; i -= 1 {
				if parent_fill, ok := ctx.style_stack[i].fill.(SVG_Solid_Color); ok {
					el.style.fill = parent_fill
					break
				}
			}
		}
	}

	// Phase 12: Style inheritance - push current style to stack for children
	// Push current style to inheritance stack (for children to inherit from)
	append(&ctx.style_stack, el.style)

	// Phase 12: Clipping and Masking attributes
	if val, ok := attrs["clip-path"]; ok do el.style.clip_path = val
	if val, ok := attrs["mask"]; ok do el.style.mask = val
	if val, ok := attrs["clip-rule"]; ok {
		if val == "evenodd" do el.style.clip_rule = 1
		else do el.style.clip_rule = 0
	}
	// Also check in 'style' attribute if present (simplified)
	if style_attr, ok := attrs["style"]; ok {
		// Basic semicolon parsing
		parts := strings.split(style_attr, ";")
		for part in parts {
			kv := strings.split(part, ":")
			if len(kv) == 2 {
				k := strings.trim_space(kv[0])
				v := strings.trim_space(kv[1])
				if k == "clip-path" do el.style.clip_path = v
				else if k == "mask" do el.style.mask = v
				else if k == "clip-rule" {
					if v == "evenodd" do el.style.clip_rule = 1
					else do el.style.clip_rule = 0
				}
			}
		}
	}

	// Parse element-specific data
	arena_alloc := mem.arena_allocator(&ctx.allocator)
	#partial switch el.type {
	case .Root:
		el.data = SVG_Root_Data {
			viewbox = ctx.viewbox,
		}
	case .Path:
		data := parse_path_attrs(attrs, arena_alloc)
		el.data = data
	case .Rect:
		data := parse_rect_attrs(attrs)
		mem.copy(rawptr(&el.data), rawptr(&data), size_of(SVG_Rect_Data))
	case .Circle:
		data := parse_circle_attrs(attrs)
		mem.copy(rawptr(&el.data), rawptr(&data), size_of(SVG_Circle_Data))
	case .Ellipse:
		data := parse_ellipse_attrs(attrs)
		mem.copy(rawptr(&el.data), rawptr(&data), size_of(SVG_Ellipse_Data))
	case .Line:
		data := parse_line_attrs(attrs)
		mem.copy(rawptr(&el.data), rawptr(&data), size_of(SVG_Line_Data))
	case .Polyline:
		data := parse_polyline_attrs(attrs, arena_alloc)
		mem.copy(rawptr(&el.data), rawptr(&data), size_of(SVG_Polyline_Data))
	case .Polygon:
		data := parse_polygon_attrs(attrs, arena_alloc)
		mem.copy(rawptr(&el.data), rawptr(&data), size_of(SVG_Polygon_Data))
	case .Text:
		data := parse_text_attrs(attrs)
		mem.copy(rawptr(&el.data), rawptr(&data), size_of(SVG_Text_Data))
	case .LinearGradient:
		data: SVG_Gradient
		data.id = el.id
		data.type = .Linear
		data.units = parse_gradient_units(attrs["gradientUnits"])
		data.transform =
			parse_transform_attr(attrs["gradientTransform"]) if "gradientTransform" in attrs else identity_matrix()
		data.linear_data = parse_linear_gradient_attrs(attrs)
		data.stops = {} // Stops will be added during children parsing
		mem.copy(rawptr(&el.data), rawptr(&data), size_of(SVG_Gradient))
	case .RadialGradient:
		data: SVG_Gradient
		data.id = el.id
		data.type = .Radial
		data.units = parse_gradient_units(attrs["gradientUnits"])
		data.transform =
			parse_transform_attr(attrs["gradientTransform"]) if "gradientTransform" in attrs else identity_matrix()
		data.radial_data = parse_radial_gradient_attrs(attrs)
		data.stops = {} // Stops will be added during children parsing
		mem.copy(rawptr(&el.data), rawptr(&data), size_of(SVG_Gradient))
	}

	// Parse children
	children := make([dynamic]SVG_Element, arena_alloc)
	for val in node.value {
		switch v in val {
		case xml.Element_ID:
			child_node := &doc.elements[v]
			if child_node.ident != "" {
				child_el := parse_xml_node_to_svg_element(ctx, doc, child_node)
				if child_el.type != .None {
					append(&children, child_el)
				}
			}
		case string:
		// Text content - could handle for <text> elements later
		}
	}
	el.children = children[:]

	// Pop transform
	pop(&ctx.transform_stack.stack)
	
	// Phase 12: Pop style from inheritance stack
	if len(ctx.style_stack) > 0 {
		pop(&ctx.style_stack)
	}

	return el
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Identity Matrix
- Return 3x3 identity matrix
*/
identity_matrix :: proc() -> matrix[3, 3]f32 {
	m: matrix[3, 3]f32
	m[0, 0] = 1
	m[0, 1] = 0
	m[0, 2] = 0
	m[1, 0] = 0
	m[1, 1] = 1
	m[1, 2] = 0
	m[2, 0] = 0
	m[2, 1] = 0
	m[2, 2] = 1
	return m
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Transform Stack Initialization
- Initialize transform stack with identity matrix
*/
transform_stack_init :: proc(stack: ^Transform_Stack) {
	stack.stack = make([dynamic]matrix[3, 3]f32)
	append(&stack.stack, identity_matrix())
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Transform Stack Get Current
- Get current transform matrix from stack
*/
transform_stack_get_current :: proc(stack: ^Transform_Stack) -> matrix[3, 3]f32 {
	if len(stack.stack) > 0 {
		return stack.stack[len(stack.stack) - 1]
	}
	return identity_matrix()
}

// ============================================================================
// Phase 10C.2: SVG Element Attribute Parsing
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Float Attribute
- Parse a float value from an attribute string
- Returns default value if parsing fails
*/
parse_float_attr :: proc(attr: string, default_val: f32 = 0) -> f32 {
	if attr == "" {
		return default_val
	}
	val, ok := strconv.parse_f32(attr)
	if !ok {
		return default_val
	}
	return val
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Length Attribute
- Parse a length value (number with optional unit)
- MVP: only supports px units (or unitless, treated as px)
- Returns default value if parsing fails
*/
parse_length_attr :: proc(attr: string, default_val: f32 = 0) -> f32 {
	if attr == "" {
		return default_val
	}
	// For MVP, strip any trailing units and parse as float
	// Full unit support (em, ex, px, pt, pc, cm, mm, in, %) deferred to later phase
	trimmed := strings.trim_space(attr)
	if len(trimmed) == 0 {
		return default_val
	}

	// Find where the numeric part ends
	num_end := 0
	for i := 0; i < len(trimmed); i += 1 {
		c := trimmed[i]
		if (c >= '0' && c <= '9') || c == '.' || c == '-' || c == '+' || c == 'e' || c == 'E' {
			num_end = i + 1
		} else {
			break
		}
	}

	if num_end == 0 {
		return default_val
	}

	num_str := trimmed[:num_end]
	val, ok := strconv.parse_f32(num_str)
	if !ok {
		return default_val
	}
	return val
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Rect Attributes
- Parse <rect> element attributes: x, y, width, height, rx, ry
- rx/ry handling: if only rx specified, ry=rx; if only ry, rx=ry
*/
parse_rect_attrs :: proc(attrs: map[string]string) -> SVG_Rect_Data {
	data: SVG_Rect_Data
	data.x = parse_length_attr(attrs["x"])
	data.y = parse_length_attr(attrs["y"])
	data.width = parse_length_attr(attrs["width"])
	data.height = parse_length_attr(attrs["height"])

	// Handle rx/ry corner radius
	rx_str := attrs["rx"]
	ry_str := attrs["ry"]

	if rx_str != "" && ry_str == "" {
		data.rx = parse_length_attr(rx_str)
		data.ry = data.rx
	} else if rx_str == "" && ry_str != "" {
		data.ry = parse_length_attr(ry_str)
		data.rx = data.ry
	} else {
		data.rx = parse_length_attr(rx_str)
		data.ry = parse_length_attr(ry_str)
	}

	// Clamp radii to half the rect dimensions per SVG spec
	max_rx := data.width / 2
	max_ry := data.height / 2
	if data.rx > max_rx {data.rx = max_rx}
	if data.ry > max_ry {data.ry = max_ry}

	return data
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Circle Attributes
- Parse <circle> element attributes: cx, cy, r
*/
parse_circle_attrs :: proc(attrs: map[string]string) -> SVG_Circle_Data {
	data: SVG_Circle_Data
	data.cx = parse_length_attr(attrs["cx"])
	data.cy = parse_length_attr(attrs["cy"])
	data.r = parse_length_attr(attrs["r"])
	if data.r < 0 {data.r = 0}
	return data
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Ellipse Attributes
- Parse <ellipse> element attributes: cx, cy, rx, ry
*/
parse_ellipse_attrs :: proc(attrs: map[string]string) -> SVG_Ellipse_Data {
	data: SVG_Ellipse_Data
	data.cx = parse_length_attr(attrs["cx"])
	data.cy = parse_length_attr(attrs["cy"])
	data.rx = parse_length_attr(attrs["rx"])
	data.ry = parse_length_attr(attrs["ry"])
	if data.rx < 0 {data.rx = 0}
	if data.ry < 0 {data.ry = 0}
	return data
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Line Attributes
- Parse <line> element attributes: x1, y1, x2, y2
*/
parse_line_attrs :: proc(attrs: map[string]string) -> SVG_Line_Data {
	data: SVG_Line_Data
	data.x1 = parse_length_attr(attrs["x1"])
	data.y1 = parse_length_attr(attrs["y1"])
	data.x2 = parse_length_attr(attrs["x2"])
	data.y2 = parse_length_attr(attrs["y2"])
	return data
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Points Attribute
- Parse points attribute for polyline and polygon
- Format: "x1,y1 x2,y2 x3,y3" or "x1 y1, x2 y2"
- Returns slice of [2]f32 points
*/
parse_points_attr :: proc(attr: string, allocator: mem.Allocator) -> [][2]f32 {
	if attr == "" {
		return nil
	}

	points := make([dynamic][2]f32, allocator)

	// Split by whitespace and/or commas
	// Use a scanner approach for robust parsing
	s := attr
	i := 0

	for i < len(s) {
		// Skip whitespace and commas
		for i < len(s) &&
		    (s[i] == ' ' || s[i] == '\t' || s[i] == '\n' || s[i] == '\r' || s[i] == ',') {
			i += 1
		}
		if i >= len(s) {break}

		// Parse x coordinate
		x_start := i
		for i < len(s) &&
		    !(s[i] == ' ' || s[i] == '\t' || s[i] == '\n' || s[i] == '\r' || s[i] == ',') {
			i += 1
		}
		x_str := strings.trim_space(s[x_start:i])
		x, x_ok := strconv.parse_f32(x_str)
		if !x_ok {continue}

		// Skip separator
		for i < len(s) &&
		    (s[i] == ' ' || s[i] == '\t' || s[i] == '\n' || s[i] == '\r' || s[i] == ',') {
			i += 1
		}
		if i >= len(s) {break}

		// Parse y coordinate
		y_start := i
		for i < len(s) &&
		    !(s[i] == ' ' || s[i] == '\t' || s[i] == '\n' || s[i] == '\r' || s[i] == ',') {
			i += 1
		}
		y_str := strings.trim_space(s[y_start:i])
		y, y_ok := strconv.parse_f32(y_str)
		if !y_ok {continue}

		append(&points, [2]f32{x, y})
	}

	return points[:]
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Polyline Attributes
- Parse <polyline> element attributes: points
*/
parse_polyline_attrs :: proc(
	attrs: map[string]string,
	allocator: mem.Allocator,
) -> SVG_Polyline_Data {
	data: SVG_Polyline_Data
	data.points = parse_points_attr(attrs["points"], allocator)
	return data
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Polygon Attributes
- Parse <polygon> element attributes: points
*/
parse_polygon_attrs :: proc(
	attrs: map[string]string,
	allocator: mem.Allocator,
) -> SVG_Polygon_Data {
	data: SVG_Polygon_Data
	data.points = parse_points_attr(attrs["points"], allocator)
	return data
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Path Attributes
- Parse <path> element attributes: d (path data)
- Uses path data parser from svg_path.odin (10C.4)
- Supports all SVG 1.1 path commands: M, L, H, V, C, S, Q, T, A, Z
*/
parse_path_attrs :: proc(attrs: map[string]string, allocator: mem.Allocator) -> SVG_Path_Data {
	data: SVG_Path_Data
	data.d = attrs["d"] if "d" in attrs else ""

	if data.d != "" {
		when logging.DEBUG_SVG {
			logging.debug(.SVG, fmt.tprintf("[PATH_PARSE] Parsing path, d length: %d", len(data.d)))
		}
		data.parsed = svg_parse_path_data(data.d, allocator)
		data.has_parsed = len(data.parsed.commands) > 0
		when logging.DEBUG_SVG {
			logging.debug(.SVG, fmt.tprintf("[PATH_PARSE] Parsed commands: %d", len(data.parsed.commands)))
		}
	} else {
		when logging.DEBUG_SVG {
			logging.debug(.SVG, fmt.tprintf("[PATH_PARSE] No 'd' attribute found"))
		}
	}

	return data
}


/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Text Attributes
- Parse <text> element attributes: x, y, font-size, text-anchor
- 10C.11: Text element attribute parsing
*/
parse_text_attrs :: proc(attrs: map[string]string) -> SVG_Text_Data {
	data: SVG_Text_Data
	data.x = parse_length_attr(attrs["x"])
	data.y = parse_length_attr(attrs["y"])
	data.font_size = parse_length_attr(attrs["font-size"])
	if data.font_size == 0 {
		data.font_size = 16 // Default font size per SVG spec
	}

	// Parse text-anchor attribute
	anchor_str := attrs["text-anchor"]
	if anchor_str != "" {
		switch anchor_str {
		case "start":
			data.anchor = 0
		case "middle":
			data.anchor = 1
		case "end":
			data.anchor = 2
		case:
			data.anchor = 0 // Default to start
		}
	} else {
		data.anchor = 0 // Default to start
	}

	// Phase 10D.4.2: Parse extended text attributes

	// Font family
	if "font-family" in attrs {
		data.font_family = attrs["font-family"]
	} else {
		data.font_family = "sans-serif" // Default font family
	}

	// Font weight
	if "font-weight" in attrs {
		weight_str := attrs["font-weight"]
		switch weight_str {
		case "bold", "700", "800", "900":
			data.font_weight = 1 // Bold
		case "normal", "400", "500":
			data.font_weight = 0 // Normal
		case:
			data.font_weight = 0 // Default to normal
		}
	} else {
		data.font_weight = 0 // Default to normal
	}

	// Font style
	if "font-style" in attrs {
		style_str := attrs["font-style"]
		switch style_str {
		case "italic", "oblique":
			data.font_style = 1 // Italic
		case "normal":
			data.font_style = 0 // Normal
		case:
			data.font_style = 0 // Default to normal
		}
	} else {
		data.font_style = 0 // Default to normal
	}

	// Text decoration
	if "text-decoration" in attrs {
		dec_str := attrs["text-decoration"]
		data.text_decoration = 0
		if strings.contains(dec_str, "underline") {
			data.text_decoration |= 1
		}
		if strings.contains(dec_str, "overline") {
			data.text_decoration |= 2
		}
		if strings.contains(dec_str, "line-through") {
			data.text_decoration |= 4
		}
	} else {
		data.text_decoration = 0 // No decoration
	}

	// Fill and stroke
	if "fill" in attrs {
		fill_paint, ok := parse_color(attrs["fill"])
		if ok {
			if fill_color, color_ok := fill_paint.(SVG_Solid_Color); color_ok {
				data.fill = fill_color
			}
		}
	} else {
		data.fill = [4]f32{0, 0, 0, 1} // Default black fill
	}

	if "stroke" in attrs {
		stroke_paint, ok := parse_color(attrs["stroke"])
		if ok {
			if stroke_color, color_ok := stroke_paint.(SVG_Solid_Color); color_ok {
				data.stroke = stroke_color
			} else if _, is_none := stroke_paint.(SVG_None); is_none {
				// stroke="none" - set to transparent stroke
				data.stroke = [4]f32{0, 0, 0, 0}
			} else {
				// Gradient or other paint type - use default black for now
				data.stroke = [4]f32{0, 0, 0, 1}
			}
		} else {
			// If stroke parsing failed, use default black stroke
			data.stroke = [4]f32{0, 0, 0, 1}
		}
	} else {
		data.stroke = [4]f32{0, 0, 0, 1} // Default black stroke
	}

	// Stroke width
	if "stroke-width" in attrs {
		data.stroke_width = parse_length_attr(attrs["stroke-width"])
	} else {
		data.stroke_width = 1.0 // Default stroke width
	}

	// Opacity
	if "opacity" in attrs {
		data.opacity = parse_float_attr(attrs["opacity"], 1.0)
	} else {
		data.opacity = 1.0 // Default opacity
	}

	// Text content will be extracted from XML element during parsing
	data.text = ""

	return data
}

// ============================================================================
// Phase 10C.10: Gradient Parsing
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Gradient Units
- Parse gradientUnits attribute: userSpaceOnUse or objectBoundingBox
- Returns SVG_Gradient_Units enum
*/
parse_gradient_units :: proc(attr: string) -> SVG_Gradient_Units {
	if attr == "userSpaceOnUse" {
		return .UserSpaceOnUse
	}
	// Default is objectBoundingBox per SVG spec
	return .ObjectBoundingBox
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Gradient Stop
- Parse <stop> element attributes: offset, stop-color, stop-opacity
- Returns SVG_Gradient_Stop struct
*/
parse_gradient_stop :: proc(attrs: map[string]string) -> SVG_Gradient_Stop {
	stop: SVG_Gradient_Stop

	// Parse offset (0.0 to 1.0)
	offset_str := attrs["offset"]
	if offset_str != "" {
		trimmed := strings.trim_space(offset_str)
		if strings.has_suffix(trimmed, "%") {
			pct_str := trimmed[:len(trimmed) - 1]
			pct, ok := strconv.parse_f32(pct_str)
			if ok {
				stop.offset = clamp(pct / 100.0, 0, 1)
			}
		} else {
			val, ok := strconv.parse_f32(trimmed)
			if ok {
				stop.offset = clamp(val, 0, 1)
			}
		}
	}

	// Parse stop-color
	color_str := attrs["stop-color"]
	if color_str != "" {
		paint, ok := parse_color(color_str)
		if ok {
			switch p in paint {
			case SVG_Solid_Color:
				stop.color = p
			case SVG_None:
				stop.color = [4]f32{0, 0, 0, 1} // Default black
			case SVG_Gradient_Ref:
				stop.color = [4]f32{0, 0, 0, 1} // Default black (gradients not supported in stops)
			}
		}
	} else {
		stop.color = [4]f32{0, 0, 0, 1} // Default black
	}

	// Parse stop-opacity (multiplies with color alpha)
	opacity_str := attrs["stop-opacity"]
	if opacity_str != "" {
		opacity, ok := strconv.parse_f32(opacity_str)
		if ok {
			stop.color[3] *= clamp(opacity, 0, 1)
		}
	}

	return stop
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Linear Gradient Attributes
- Parse <linearGradient> element attributes: x1, y1, x2, y2, gradientUnits, gradientTransform
- Returns SVG_Linear_Gradient_Data struct
*/
parse_linear_gradient_attrs :: proc(attrs: map[string]string) -> SVG_Linear_Gradient_Data {
	data: SVG_Linear_Gradient_Data

	// Parse coordinates (default to 0% or 0 depending on gradientUnits)
	data.x1 = parse_length_attr(attrs["x1"], 0)
	data.y1 = parse_length_attr(attrs["y1"], 0)
	data.x2 = parse_length_attr(attrs["x2"], 100)
	data.y2 = parse_length_attr(attrs["y2"], 0)

	return data
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Radial Gradient Attributes
- Parse <radialGradient> element attributes: cx, cy, r, fx, fy, gradientUnits, gradientTransform
- Returns SVG_Radial_Gradient_Data struct
*/
parse_radial_gradient_attrs :: proc(attrs: map[string]string) -> SVG_Radial_Gradient_Data {
	data: SVG_Radial_Gradient_Data

	// Parse coordinates (defaults per SVG spec)
	data.cx = parse_length_attr(attrs["cx"], 50)
	data.cy = parse_length_attr(attrs["cy"], 50)
	data.r = parse_length_attr(attrs["r"], 50)
	data.fx = parse_length_attr(attrs["fx"], data.cx) // Focal point defaults to center
	data.fy = parse_length_attr(attrs["fy"], data.cy)

	return data
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Gradient Reference
- Parse fill or stroke attribute that references a gradient
- Format: url(#gradient-id)
- Returns SVG_Gradient_Ref struct
*/
parse_gradient_ref :: proc(attr: string) -> (SVG_Gradient_Ref, bool) {
	if attr == "" {
		return {}, false
	}

	trimmed := strings.trim_space(attr)

	// Check for url(#id) format
	if strings.has_prefix(trimmed, "url(") && strings.has_suffix(trimmed, ")") {
		inner := trimmed[4:len(trimmed) - 1]
		inner = strings.trim_space(inner)

		// Remove # prefix if present
		if strings.has_prefix(inner, "#") {
			ref: SVG_Gradient_Ref
			ref.id = inner[1:]
			return ref, true
		}
	}

	return {}, false
}

// ============================================================================
// Phase 10C.2: Style Parsing
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Color
- Parse SVG color values: hex (#RGB, #RRGGBB), rgb(), named colors, gradient references
- MVP: supports hex, rgb(), named colors, and url(#gradient-id) for gradients (10C.10)
- Returns SVG_Paint union
*/
parse_color :: proc(color_str: string) -> (SVG_Paint, bool) {
	s := strings.trim_space(color_str)

	// Handle "none"
	if s == "none" || s == "" {
		none: SVG_None
		return none, true
	}

	// Handle gradient references: url(#gradient-id) (10C.10)
	if strings.has_prefix(s, "url(") {
		ref, ok := parse_gradient_ref(s)
		if ok {
			return ref, true
		}
	}

	// Handle hex colors: #RGB or #RRGGBB
	if strings.has_prefix(s, "#") {
		hex := s[1:]
		r, g, b: f32

		if len(hex) == 3 {
			// #RGB format
			r =
				f32(
					hex[0] - '0' if hex[0] >= '0' && hex[0] <= '9' else (hex[0] - 'a' + 10 if hex[0] >= 'a' && hex[0] <= 'f' else hex[0] - 'A' + 10),
				) /
				15.0
			g =
				f32(
					hex[1] - '0' if hex[1] >= '0' && hex[1] <= '9' else (hex[1] - 'a' + 10 if hex[1] >= 'a' && hex[1] <= 'f' else hex[1] - 'A' + 10),
				) /
				15.0
			b =
				f32(
					hex[2] - '0' if hex[2] >= '0' && hex[2] <= '9' else (hex[2] - 'a' + 10 if hex[2] >= 'a' && hex[2] <= 'f' else hex[2] - 'A' + 10),
				) /
				15.0
		} else if len(hex) == 6 {
			// #RRGGBB format
			r_val := hex_value(hex[0]) * 16 + hex_value(hex[1])
			g_val := hex_value(hex[2]) * 16 + hex_value(hex[3])
			b_val := hex_value(hex[4]) * 16 + hex_value(hex[5])
			r = f32(r_val) / 255.0
			g = f32(g_val) / 255.0
			b = f32(b_val) / 255.0
		} else {
			// Invalid hex format
			return {}, false
		}

		return SVG_Solid_Color{r, g, b, 1.0}, true
	}

	// Handle rgb() format: rgb(r,g,b) or rgb(r, g, b)
	if strings.has_prefix(s, "rgb(") && strings.has_suffix(s, ")") {
		inner := s[4:len(s) - 1]
		parts := strings.split(inner, ",")
		if len(parts) == 3 {
			r := parse_color_component(strings.trim_space(parts[0]))
			g := parse_color_component(strings.trim_space(parts[1]))
			b := parse_color_component(strings.trim_space(parts[2]))
			return SVG_Solid_Color{r, g, b, 1.0}, true
		}
	}

	// Handle named colors (MVP subset)
	color := parse_named_color(s)
	if color[3] > 0 { 	// Alpha > 0 indicates valid color
		return color, true
	}

	return {}, false
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Hex Value Helper
- Convert hex character to numeric value
*/
hex_value :: proc(c: byte) -> int {
	if c >= '0' && c <= '9' {return int(c - '0')}
	if c >= 'a' && c <= 'f' {return int(c - 'a' + 10)}
	if c >= 'A' && c <= 'F' {return int(c - 'A' + 10)}
	return 0
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Color Component
- Parse a color component value (0-255 or 0%-100%)
*/
parse_color_component :: proc(s: string) -> f32 {
	trimmed := strings.trim_space(s)
	if strings.has_suffix(trimmed, "%") {
		pct_str := trimmed[:len(trimmed) - 1]
		pct, ok := strconv.parse_f32(pct_str)
		if ok {
			return clamp(pct / 100.0, 0, 1)
		}
	} else {
		val, ok := strconv.parse_f32(trimmed)
		if ok {
			return clamp(val / 255.0, 0, 1)
		}
	}
	return 0
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Named Color
- Parse named SVG colors (MVP subset of common colors)
- Returns RGBA color with alpha=0 if not recognized
*/
parse_named_color :: proc(name: string) -> SVG_Solid_Color {
	// Common named colors (MVP subset)
	switch name {
	case "black":
		return {0, 0, 0, 1}
	case "white":
		return {1, 1, 1, 1}
	case "red":
		return {1, 0, 0, 1}
	case "green":
		return {0, 0.5, 0, 1}
	case "blue":
		return {0, 0, 1, 1}
	case "yellow":
		return {1, 1, 0, 1}
	case "cyan":
		return {0, 1, 1, 1}
	case "magenta":
		return {1, 0, 1, 1}
	case "silver":
		return {0.75, 0.75, 0.75, 1}
	case "gray", "grey":
		return {0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 1}
	case "maroon":
		return {0.5, 0, 0, 1}
	case "olive":
		return {0.5, 0.5, 0, 1}
	case "lime":
		return {0, 1, 0, 1}
	case "aqua":
		return {0, 1, 1, 1}
	case "teal":
		return {0, 0.5, 0.5, 1}
	case "navy":
		return {0, 0, 0.5, 1}
	case "fuchsia":
		return {1, 0, 1, 1}
	case "purple":
		return {0.5, 0, 0.5, 1}
	case "orange":
		return {1, 0.65, 0, 1}
	}
	return {0, 0, 0, 1} // Default to black if color name not found
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Clamp Helper
- Clamp value to [min, max] range
*/
clamp :: proc(v, min, max: f32) -> f32 {
	if v < min {return min}
	if v > max {return max}
	return v
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Style Attribute
- Parse inline style="..." attribute
- Format: "property1: value1; property2: value2"
- Applies parsed values to SVG_Style struct
*/
parse_style_attr :: proc(style_str: string, style: ^SVG_Style) {
	if style_str == "" {
		return
	}

	// Split by semicolons to get individual declarations
	declarations := strings.split(style_str, ";")
	defer delete(declarations)

	for decl in declarations {
		decl_trimmed := strings.trim_space(decl)
		if decl_trimmed == "" {continue}

		// Split by colon to get property and value
		parts := strings.split(decl_trimmed, ":")
		if len(parts) != 2 {continue}

		prop := strings.trim_space(parts[0])
		value := strings.trim_space(parts[1])

		apply_style_property(prop, value, style)
	}
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Apply Style Property
- Apply a single CSS property to SVG_Style
- Supports: fill, stroke, stroke-width, stroke-linecap, stroke-linejoin, fill-rule, opacity, stroke-dasharray, stroke-dashoffset
*/
apply_style_property :: proc(prop, value: string, style: ^SVG_Style) {
	switch prop {
	case "fill":
		if paint, ok := parse_color(value); ok {
			style.fill = paint
			// 10C2.6: Store original color string for theme lookup
			style.fill_color_name = value
		}
	case "stroke":
		if paint, ok := parse_color(value); ok {
			style.stroke = paint
			// 10C2.6: Store original color string for theme lookup
			style.stroke_color_name = value
		}
	case "stroke-width":
		style.stroke_width = parse_length_attr(value, 1.0)
	case "stroke-linecap":
		switch value {
		case "butt":
			style.stroke_linecap = .Butt
		case "round":
			style.stroke_linecap = .Round
		case "square":
			style.stroke_linecap = .Square
		}
	case "stroke-linejoin":
		switch value {
		case "miter":
			style.stroke_linejoin = .Miter
		case "round":
			style.stroke_linejoin = .Round
		case "bevel":
			style.stroke_linejoin = .Bevel
		}
	case "fill-rule":
		switch value {
		case "nonzero":
			style.fill_rule = 0
		case "evenodd":
			style.fill_rule = 1
		}
	case "opacity":
		style.opacity = clamp(parse_float_attr(value), 0, 1)
	case "stroke-dasharray":
		style.stroke_dasharray = parse_dasharray(value)
	case "stroke-dashoffset":
		style.stroke_dashoffset = parse_length_attr(value)
	case "stroke-miterlimit":
		style.stroke_miterlimit = parse_float_attr(value, 4.0)
	}
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Dash Array
- Parse stroke-dasharray value
- Format: "dashlen,gaplen" or "dashlen gaplen"
- Returns slice of floats
- 10C.9: Fixed to use heap allocator instead of non-existent context.temp_allocator
*/
parse_dasharray :: proc(value: string) -> []f32 {
	if value == "none" || value == "" {
		return nil
	}

	// Simple parsing: split by comma or space
	// Use heap allocator for dynamic array
	dashes := make([dynamic]f32)

	// Split by comma first, then by space
	parts := strings.split(value, ",")
	defer delete(parts)

	for part in parts {
		part_trimmed := strings.trim_space(part)
		if part_trimmed == "" {continue}

		// Handle space-separated values within comma parts
		subparts := strings.split(part_trimmed, " ")
		defer delete(subparts)

		for subpart in subparts {
			subpart_trimmed := strings.trim_space(subpart)
			if subpart_trimmed == "" {continue}

			val, ok := strconv.parse_f32(subpart_trimmed)
			if ok && val >= 0 {
				append(&dashes, val)
			}
		}
	}

	if len(dashes) == 0 {
		return nil
	}

	// Clone to regular slice for return
	result := make([]f32, len(dashes))
	copy(result, dashes[:])
	return result
}

// ============================================================================
// Phase 10C.2: Transform Parsing
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Transform Attribute
- Parse transform="..." attribute
- Supports: matrix(a,b,c,d,e,f), translate(x,y), scale(x,y), rotate(angle,cx,cy), skewX(angle), skewY(angle)
- Returns 3x3 transformation matrix
*/
parse_transform_attr :: proc(transform_str: string) -> matrix[3, 3]f32 {
	if transform_str == "" {
		return identity_matrix()
	}

	result := identity_matrix()
	s := strings.trim_space(transform_str)

	// Parse each transform function
	i := 0
	for i < len(s) {
		// Skip whitespace
		for i < len(s) &&
		    (s[i] == ' ' || s[i] == '\t' || s[i] == '\n' || s[i] == '\r' || s[i] == ',') {
			i += 1
		}
		if i >= len(s) {break}

		// Find function name
		func_start := i
		for i < len(s) && s[i] != '(' {
			i += 1
		}
		if i >= len(s) {break}

		func_name := strings.trim_space(s[func_start:i])
		i += 1 // Skip '('

		// Find closing parenthesis
		paren_depth := 1
		args_start := i
		for i < len(s) && paren_depth > 0 {
			if s[i] == '(' {paren_depth += 1}
			if s[i] == ')' {paren_depth -= 1}
			i += 1
		}
		if paren_depth > 0 {break} 	// Unclosed parenthesis

		args_str := s[args_start:i - 1]

		// Parse the transform function
		transform := parse_transform_function(func_name, args_str)
		result = linalg.matrix_mul(result, transform)
	}

	return result
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Transform Function
- Parse individual transform function
- matrix(a,b,c,d,e,f): 2x3 matrix as 3x3
- translate(x,y): translation
- scale(x,y): scaling
- rotate(angle,cx,cy): rotation (optional center)
- skewX(angle): horizontal skew
- skewY(angle): vertical skew
*/
parse_transform_function :: proc(func_name, args_str: string) -> matrix[3, 3]f32 {
	args := parse_transform_args(args_str)
	defer delete(args)

	switch func_name {
	case "matrix":
		if len(args) >= 6 {
			a, b, c := args[0], args[1], args[2]
			d, e, f := args[3], args[4], args[5]
			m: matrix[3, 3]f32
			// Layout:
			// [ a c e ]
			// [ b d f ]
			// [ 0 0 1 ]
			m[0, 0] = a; m[0, 1] = c; m[0, 2] = e
			m[1, 0] = b; m[1, 1] = d; m[1, 2] = f
			m[2, 0] = 0; m[2, 1] = 0; m[2, 2] = 1
			return m
		}

	case "translate":
		if len(args) >= 1 {
			tx := args[0]
			ty := args[1] if len(args) >= 2 else 0
			return translation_matrix(tx, ty)
		}

	case "scale":
		if len(args) >= 1 {
			sx := args[0]
			sy := args[1] if len(args) >= 2 else sx
			return scale_matrix(sx, sy)
		}

	case "rotate":
		if len(args) >= 1 {
			angle := args[0] * linalg.RAD_PER_DEG
			cx := args[1] if len(args) >= 2 else 0
			cy := args[2] if len(args) >= 3 else 0

			// rotate(angle, cx, cy) = translate(cx,cy) * rotate(angle) * translate(-cx,-cy)
			if len(args) >= 3 {
				t1 := translation_matrix(cx, cy)
				r := rotation_matrix(angle)
				t2 := translation_matrix(-cx, -cy)
				return linalg.matrix_mul(linalg.matrix_mul(t1, r), t2)
			} else {
				return rotation_matrix(angle)
			}
		}

	case "skewX":
		if len(args) >= 1 {
			angle := args[0] * linalg.RAD_PER_DEG
			return skew_x_matrix(angle)
		}

	case "skewY":
		if len(args) >= 1 {
			angle := args[0] * linalg.RAD_PER_DEG
			return skew_y_matrix(angle)
		}
	}

	return identity_matrix()
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Transform Arguments
- Parse comma or space separated float arguments
*/
parse_transform_args :: proc(args_str: string) -> []f32 {
	args := make([dynamic]f32)

	// Split by comma or whitespace
	// Manual parsing for robustness
	s := args_str
	i := 0

	for i < len(s) {
		// Skip separators
		for i < len(s) &&
		    (s[i] == ' ' || s[i] == '\t' || s[i] == '\n' || s[i] == '\r' || s[i] == ',') {
			i += 1
		}
		if i >= len(s) {break}

		// Find number
		num_start := i
		for i < len(s) &&
		    !(s[i] == ' ' || s[i] == '\t' || s[i] == '\n' || s[i] == '\r' || s[i] == ',') {
			i += 1
		}

		num_str := strings.trim_space(s[num_start:i])
		val, ok := strconv.parse_f32(num_str)
		if ok {
			append(&args, val)
		}
	}

	return args[:]
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Translation Matrix
- Create 3x3 translation matrix
*/
translation_matrix :: proc(tx, ty: f32) -> matrix[3, 3]f32 {
	m := identity_matrix()
	m[0][2] = tx
	m[1][2] = ty
	return m
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Scale Matrix
- Create 3x3 scale matrix
*/
scale_matrix :: proc(sx, sy: f32) -> matrix[3, 3]f32 {
	m := identity_matrix()
	m[0][0] = sx
	m[1][1] = sy
	return m
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Rotation Matrix
- Create 3x3 rotation matrix (angle in radians)
*/
rotation_matrix :: proc(angle: f32) -> matrix[3, 3]f32 {
	c := math.cos(angle)
	s := math.sin(angle)
	m := identity_matrix()
	m[0][0] = c; m[0][1] = -s
	m[1][0] = s; m[1][1] = c
	return m
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Skew X Matrix
- Create 3x3 horizontal skew matrix (angle in radians)
*/
skew_x_matrix :: proc(angle: f32) -> matrix[3, 3]f32 {
	m := identity_matrix()
	m[0][1] = math.tan(angle)
	return m
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Skew Y Matrix
- Create 3x3 vertical skew matrix (angle in radians)
*/
skew_y_matrix :: proc(angle: f32) -> matrix[3, 3]f32 {
	m := identity_matrix()
	m[1][0] = math.tan(angle)
	return m
}

// ============================================================================
// Phase 10C.3: Comprehensive Style Parsing (Inline Attributes + style="...")
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse Element Style
- Parse both presentation attributes (inline) and style="..." attribute
- SVG precedence: presentation attributes < style attribute (CSS specificity)
- This function applies them in correct order for proper cascade
- Supports: fill, stroke, stroke-width, stroke-linecap, stroke-linejoin, fill-rule, opacity
- 10C.3: Style parsing integration layer
*/
parse_element_style :: proc(attrs: map[string]string) -> SVG_Style {
	style := default_svg_style()

	// Step 1: Apply presentation attributes (lower precedence)
	// These are inline attributes like fill="red", stroke="blue", etc.
	if fill_attr, has_fill := attrs["fill"]; has_fill {
		if paint, ok := parse_color(fill_attr); ok {
			style.fill = paint
			// 10C2.6: Store original color string for theme lookup
			style.fill_color_name = fill_attr
		}
	}

	if stroke_attr, has_stroke := attrs["stroke"]; has_stroke {
		if paint, ok := parse_color(stroke_attr); ok {
			style.stroke = paint
			// 10C2.6: Store original color string for theme lookup
			style.stroke_color_name = stroke_attr
		}
	}

	if stroke_width_attr, has_stroke_width := attrs["stroke-width"]; has_stroke_width {
		style.stroke_width = parse_length_attr(stroke_width_attr, 1.0)
	}

	if stroke_linecap_attr, has_linecap := attrs["stroke-linecap"]; has_linecap {
		switch stroke_linecap_attr {
		case "butt":
			style.stroke_linecap = .Butt
		case "round":
			style.stroke_linecap = .Round
		case "square":
			style.stroke_linecap = .Square
		}
	}

	if stroke_linejoin_attr, has_linejoin := attrs["stroke-linejoin"]; has_linejoin {
		switch stroke_linejoin_attr {
		case "miter":
			style.stroke_linejoin = .Miter
		case "round":
			style.stroke_linejoin = .Round
		case "bevel":
			style.stroke_linejoin = .Bevel
		}
	}

	if fill_rule_attr, has_fill_rule := attrs["fill-rule"]; has_fill_rule {
		switch fill_rule_attr {
		case "nonzero":
			style.fill_rule = 0
		case "evenodd":
			style.fill_rule = 1
		}
	}

	if opacity_attr, has_opacity := attrs["opacity"]; has_opacity {
		style.opacity = clamp(parse_float_attr(opacity_attr), 0, 1)
	}

	if dasharray_attr, has_dasharray := attrs["stroke-dasharray"]; has_dasharray {
		style.stroke_dasharray = parse_dasharray(dasharray_attr)
	}

	if dashoffset_attr, has_dashoffset := attrs["stroke-dashoffset"]; has_dashoffset {
		style.stroke_dashoffset = parse_length_attr(dashoffset_attr)
	}

	// Step 2: Apply style="..." attribute (higher precedence, overrides presentation attrs)
	// Per CSS specificity rules, style attribute has higher specificity than presentation attributes
	if style_attr, has_style := attrs["style"]; has_style {
		parse_style_attr(style_attr, &style)
	}

	return style
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Create SVG Element from XML Node
- Create a fully parsed SVG element from XML attributes
- Combines geometry parsing (from 10C.2) with style parsing (10C.3)
- Handles transform attribute as well
- 10C.3: Integration of attribute parsing
*/
create_svg_element :: proc(
	type: SVG_Element_Type,
	attrs: map[string]string,
	allocator: mem.Allocator,
) -> SVG_Element {
	elem: SVG_Element
	elem.type = type
	elem.id = attrs["id"] if "id" in attrs else ""
	elem.style = parse_element_style(attrs)
	elem.transform =
		parse_transform_attr(attrs["transform"]) if "transform" in attrs else identity_matrix()

	// Parse element-specific data based on type
	#partial switch type {
	case .Rect:
		data := parse_rect_attrs(attrs)
		mem.copy(rawptr(&elem.data), rawptr(&data), size_of(SVG_Rect_Data))
	case .Circle:
		data := parse_circle_attrs(attrs)
		mem.copy(rawptr(&elem.data), rawptr(&data), size_of(SVG_Circle_Data))
	case .Ellipse:
		data := parse_ellipse_attrs(attrs)
		mem.copy(rawptr(&elem.data), rawptr(&data), size_of(SVG_Ellipse_Data))
	case .Line:
		data := parse_line_attrs(attrs)
		mem.copy(rawptr(&elem.data), rawptr(&data), size_of(SVG_Line_Data))
	case .Polyline:
		data := parse_polyline_attrs(attrs, allocator)
		mem.copy(rawptr(&elem.data), rawptr(&data), size_of(SVG_Polyline_Data))
	case .Polygon:
		data := parse_polygon_attrs(attrs, allocator)
		mem.copy(rawptr(&elem.data), rawptr(&data), size_of(SVG_Polygon_Data))
	case .Path:
		data := parse_path_attrs(attrs, allocator)
		mem.copy(rawptr(&elem.data), rawptr(&data), size_of(SVG_Path_Data))
	case .LinearGradient:
		// 10C.10: Parse linear gradient attributes
		data: SVG_Gradient
		data.id = attrs["id"] if "id" in attrs else ""
		data.type = .Linear
		data.units = parse_gradient_units(attrs["gradientUnits"])
		data.transform =
			parse_transform_attr(attrs["gradientTransform"]) if "gradientTransform" in attrs else identity_matrix()
		data.linear_data = parse_linear_gradient_attrs(attrs)
		data.stops = {} // Stops will be added during XML parsing
		mem.copy(rawptr(&elem.data), rawptr(&data), size_of(SVG_Gradient))
	case .RadialGradient:
		// 10C.10: Parse radial gradient attributes
		data: SVG_Gradient
		data.id = attrs["id"] if "id" in attrs else ""
		data.type = .Radial
		data.units = parse_gradient_units(attrs["gradientUnits"])
		data.transform =
			parse_transform_attr(attrs["gradientTransform"]) if "gradientTransform" in attrs else identity_matrix()
		data.radial_data = parse_radial_gradient_attrs(attrs)
		data.stops = {} // Stops will be added during XML parsing
		mem.copy(rawptr(&elem.data), rawptr(&data), size_of(SVG_Gradient))
	case .Text:
		// 10C.11: Parse text attributes
		data := parse_text_attrs(attrs)
		mem.copy(rawptr(&elem.data), rawptr(&data), size_of(SVG_Text_Data))
	case .Root, .Group, .Defs:
		// Container elements - no specific data to parse yet
		// Children will be added during tree construction
		break
	}

	return elem
}

// ============================================================================
// Phase 10C.6: Transform Stack, ViewBox, and preserveAspectRatio
// ============================================================================

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse ViewBox Attribute
- Parse viewBox="min-x min-y width height" attribute
- Returns [4]f32 with {min_x, min_y, width, height}
- Per SVG spec: negative width/height are invalid (treated as 0)
- 10C.6: ViewBox parsing for root SVG element
*/
parse_viewbox_attr :: proc(attr: string) -> [4]f32 {
	result: [4]f32 = {0, 0, 0, 0}
	if attr == "" {
		return result
	}

	// Parse four numbers separated by whitespace and/or commas
	s := strings.trim_space(attr)
	i := 0
	num_count := 0

	for i < len(s) && num_count < 4 {
		// Skip whitespace and commas
		for i < len(s) &&
		    (s[i] == ' ' || s[i] == '\t' || s[i] == '\n' || s[i] == '\r' || s[i] == ',') {
			i += 1
		}
		if i >= len(s) {break}

		// Find number end
		num_start := i
		for i < len(s) &&
		    !(s[i] == ' ' || s[i] == '\t' || s[i] == '\n' || s[i] == '\r' || s[i] == ',') {
			i += 1
		}

		num_str := strings.trim_space(s[num_start:i])
		val, ok := strconv.parse_f32(num_str)
		if ok {
			result[num_count] = val
			num_count += 1
		}
	}

	// Per SVG spec: negative width/height are invalid
	if result[2] < 0 {result[2] = 0}
	if result[3] < 0 {result[3] = 0}

	return result
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse preserveAspectRatio Attribute
- Parse preserveAspectRatio="alignment [meet|slice]"
- Returns struct with alignment and meet/slice flag
- Default: xMidYMid meet
- Alignments: none, xMinYMin, xMidYMin, xMaxYMin, xMinYMid, xMidYMid, xMaxYMid, xMinYMax, xMidYMax, xMaxYMax
- 10C.6: preserveAspectRatio parsing for viewBox scaling
*/
SVG_Alignment :: enum {
	None,
	XMinYMin,
	XMidYMin,
	XMaxYMin,
	XMinYMid,
	XMidYMid,
	XMaxYMid,
	XMinYMax,
	XMidYMax,
	XMaxYMax,
}

SVG_Meet_Slice :: enum {
	Meet,
	Slice,
}

SVG_Preserve_Aspect_Ratio :: struct {
	alignment:     SVG_Alignment,
	meet_or_slice: SVG_Meet_Slice,
}

parse_preserve_aspect_ratio_attr :: proc(attr: string) -> SVG_Preserve_Aspect_Ratio {
	result := SVG_Preserve_Aspect_Ratio {
		alignment     = .XMidYMid,
		meet_or_slice = .Meet,
	}
	if attr == "" {
		return result
	}

	s := strings.trim_space(attr)
	parts := strings.split(s, " ")
	defer delete(parts)

	// First part is alignment
	if len(parts) >= 1 {
		align_str := strings.trim_space(parts[0])
		switch align_str {
		case "none":
			result.alignment = .None
		case "xMinYMin":
			result.alignment = .XMinYMin
		case "xMidYMin":
			result.alignment = .XMidYMin
		case "xMaxYMin":
			result.alignment = .XMaxYMin
		case "xMinYMid":
			result.alignment = .XMinYMid
		case "xMidYMid":
			result.alignment = .XMidYMid
		case "xMaxYMid":
			result.alignment = .XMaxYMid
		case "xMinYMax":
			result.alignment = .XMinYMax
		case "xMidYMax":
			result.alignment = .XMidYMax
		case "xMaxYMax":
			result.alignment = .XMaxYMax
		}
	}

	// Second part is meet or slice (optional, default meet)
	if len(parts) >= 2 {
		meet_slice_str := strings.trim_space(parts[1])
		switch meet_slice_str {
		case "meet":
			result.meet_or_slice = .Meet
		case "slice":
			result.meet_or_slice = .Slice
		}
	}

	return result
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Transform Stack Push
- Push a new transform matrix onto the stack
- Used when entering a group (<g>) element to accumulate transforms
- 10C.6: Transform stack management for hierarchical elements
*/
transform_stack_push :: proc(stack: ^Transform_Stack, transform: matrix[3, 3]f32) {
	// New transform = current * local (matrix multiplication order for SVG)
	current := transform_stack_get_current(stack)
	new_transform := linalg.matrix_mul(current, transform)
	append(&stack.stack, new_transform)
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Transform Stack Pop
- Pop the current transform matrix from the stack
- Used when exiting a group (<g>) element
- 10C.6: Transform stack management for hierarchical elements
*/
transform_stack_pop :: proc(stack: ^Transform_Stack) {
	if len(stack.stack) > 1 {
		// Never pop the last element (identity)
		pop(&stack.stack)
	}
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Compute ViewBox Transform
- Compute the transformation matrix from viewBox to viewport
- Implements SVG 1.1 viewBox/preserveAspectRatio algorithm
- Returns matrix that maps viewBox coordinates to viewport coordinates
- 10C.6: Core algorithm for viewBox to canvas transform
*/
compute_viewbox_transform :: proc(
	viewbox: [4]f32, // {min_x, min_y, width, height}
	viewport_width: f32,
	viewport_height: f32,
	par: SVG_Preserve_Aspect_Ratio,
) -> matrix[3, 3]f32 {
	// Start with identity
	result := identity_matrix()

	// Extract viewBox values
	vb_min_x := viewbox[0]
	vb_min_y := viewbox[1]
	vb_width := viewbox[2]
	vb_height := viewbox[3]

	// If viewBox has zero width or height, treat as identity (no transform)
	if vb_width <= 0 || vb_height <= 0 {
		return result
	}

	// If viewport has zero width or height, treat as identity
	if viewport_width <= 0 || viewport_height <= 0 {
		return result
	}

	// If alignment is "none", scale non-uniformly to fill viewport
	if par.alignment == .None {
		scale_x := viewport_width / vb_width
		scale_y := viewport_height / vb_height

		// translate(-min_x, -min_y) * scale(scale_x, scale_y) * translate(0, 0)
		// But matrix multiplication is right-to-left for column vectors
		result = translation_matrix(-vb_min_x * scale_x, -vb_min_y * scale_y)
		result = linalg.matrix_mul(result, scale_matrix(scale_x, scale_y))
		return result
	}

	// Compute scale factor (uniform scaling)
	scale_x := viewport_width / vb_width
	scale_y := viewport_height / vb_height

	scale: f32
	if par.meet_or_slice == .Meet {
		// meet: scale to fit entirely within viewport (smaller scale)
		scale = min(scale_x, scale_y)
	} else {
		// slice: scale to cover entire viewport (larger scale)
		scale = max(scale_x, scale_y)
	}

	// Compute translate based on alignment
	tx, ty: f32

	// X alignment
	switch par.alignment {
	case .XMinYMin, .XMinYMid, .XMinYMax:
		// align viewBox.minX with viewport left edge
		tx = 0
	case .XMidYMin, .XMidYMid, .XMidYMax:
		// center viewBox in viewport horizontally
		tx = (viewport_width - vb_width * scale) / 2.0
	case .XMaxYMin, .XMaxYMid, .XMaxYMax:
		// align viewBox.maxX with viewport right edge
		tx = viewport_width - vb_width * scale
	case .None:
		tx = 0
	}

	// Y alignment
	switch par.alignment {
	case .XMinYMin, .XMidYMin, .XMaxYMin:
		// align viewBox.minY with viewport top edge
		ty = 0
	case .XMinYMid, .XMidYMid, .XMaxYMid:
		// center viewBox in viewport vertically
		ty = (viewport_height - vb_height * scale) / 2.0
	case .XMinYMax, .XMidYMax, .XMaxYMax:
		// align viewBox.maxY with viewport bottom edge
		ty = viewport_height - vb_height * scale
	case .None:
		ty = 0
	}

	// Build transform: translate(tx, ty) * scale(scale) * translate(-min_x, -min_y)
	// In matrix multiplication order (right to left for column vectors)
	result = translation_matrix(tx, ty)
	result = linalg.matrix_mul(result, scale_matrix(scale, scale))
	result = linalg.matrix_mul(result, translation_matrix(-vb_min_x, -vb_min_y))

	return result
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/SVG Root Attributes
- Structure to hold parsed <svg> element attributes
- 10C.6: Container for width, height, viewBox, and preserveAspectRatio
*/
SVG_Root_Attributes :: struct {
	width:                 f32,
	height:                f32,
	viewbox:               [4]f32,
	preserve_aspect_ratio: SVG_Preserve_Aspect_Ratio,
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Parse SVG Root Attributes
- Parse <svg> element attributes: width, height, viewBox, preserveAspectRatio
- Returns SVG_Root_Attributes with parsed values
- Default width/height: 100% (treated as viewport size, caller must provide)
- Default viewBox: none (uses 0,0,width,height if not specified)
- 10C.6: Root SVG element parsing for viewport setup
*/
parse_svg_root_attrs :: proc(attrs: map[string]string) -> SVG_Root_Attributes {
	result: SVG_Root_Attributes

	// Parse width and height (default to 0, caller should provide actual viewport size)
	result.width = parse_length_attr(attrs["width"])
	result.height = parse_length_attr(attrs["height"])

	// Parse viewBox
	if viewbox_attr, has_viewbox := attrs["viewBox"]; has_viewbox {
		result.viewbox = parse_viewbox_attr(viewbox_attr)
	} else {
		// No viewBox specified: use implicit viewBox of 0 0 width height
		result.viewbox = {0, 0, result.width, result.height}
	}

	// Parse preserveAspectRatio
	if par_attr, has_par := attrs["preserveAspectRatio"]; has_par {
		result.preserve_aspect_ratio = parse_preserve_aspect_ratio_attr(par_attr)
	} else {
		// Default: xMidYMid meet
		result.preserve_aspect_ratio = SVG_Preserve_Aspect_Ratio {
			alignment     = .XMidYMid,
			meet_or_slice = .Meet,
		}
	}

	return result
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Get Element Transform with Stack
- Get the full accumulated transform for an element
- Combines the current transform stack with the element's local transform
- Returns matrix that maps from element local coordinates to viewport coordinates
- 10C.6: Transform composition for hierarchical elements
*/
get_element_transform :: proc(
	stack: ^Transform_Stack,
	local_transform: matrix[3, 3]f32,
) -> matrix[3, 3]f32 {
	current := transform_stack_get_current(stack)
	return linalg.matrix_mul(current, local_transform)
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Transform Stack Reset
- Reset transform stack to identity
- Used when starting to process a new SVG document
- 10C.6: Transform stack initialization
*/
transform_stack_reset :: proc(stack: ^Transform_Stack) {
	clear(&stack.stack)
	append(&stack.stack, identity_matrix())
}

/**
@documentation: SVG Parser/Get Root Transform
- Compute the initial transform for an SVG document
- Combines viewBox transform with any root-level transform attribute
- Returns matrix that maps from SVG user coordinates to viewport coordinates
- 10C.6: Entry point for establishing SVG coordinate system
*/
get_root_transform :: proc(
	root_attrs: SVG_Root_Attributes,
	viewport_width: f32,
	viewport_height: f32,
	root_transform_attr: matrix[3, 3]f32,
) -> matrix[3, 3]f32 {
	// Compute viewBox transform (maps viewBox to viewport)
	viewbox_transform := compute_viewbox_transform(
		root_attrs.viewbox,
		viewport_width,
		viewport_height,
		root_attrs.preserve_aspect_ratio,
	)

	// Apply root transform attribute (if any) on top of viewBox transform
	return linalg.matrix_mul(viewbox_transform, root_transform_attr)
}

basic_3d_shader_desc :: proc (backend: sg.Backend) -> sg.Shader_Desc {
    desc: sg.Shader_Desc
    desc.label = "basic_3d_shader"
    #partial switch backend {
    case .GLES3:
        desc.vertex_func.source = transmute(cstring)&vs_source_glsl300es
        desc.vertex_func.entry = "main"
        desc.fragment_func.source = transmute(cstring)&fs_source_glsl300es
        desc.fragment_func.entry = "main"
        desc.attrs[0].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[0].glsl_name = "position"
        desc.attrs[1].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[1].glsl_name = "color0"
        desc.attrs[2].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[2].glsl_name = "texcoord0"
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].stage = .VERTEX
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].layout = .STD140
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].size = 64
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].glsl_uniforms[0].type = .FLOAT4
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].glsl_uniforms[0].array_count = 4
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].glsl_uniforms[0].glsl_name = "vs_params_3d"
    case .METAL_MACOS:
        desc.vertex_func.source = transmute(cstring)&vs_source_metal_macos
        desc.vertex_func.entry = "main0"
        desc.fragment_func.source = transmute(cstring)&fs_source_metal_macos
        desc.fragment_func.entry = "main0"
        desc.attrs[0].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[1].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[2].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].stage = .VERTEX
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].layout = .STD140
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].size = 64
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].msl_buffer_n = 0
    }
    return desc
}

c2d_blur_shader_desc :: proc (backend: sg.Backend) -> sg.Shader_Desc {
    desc: sg.Shader_Desc
    desc.label = "c2d_blur_shader"
    #partial switch backend {
    case .GLES3:
        desc.vertex_func.source = transmute(cstring)&blur_vs_source_glsl300es
        desc.vertex_func.entry = "main"
        desc.fragment_func.source = transmute(cstring)&blur_fs_source_glsl300es
        desc.fragment_func.entry = "main"
        desc.attrs[0].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[0].glsl_name = "position"
        desc.attrs[1].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[1].glsl_name = "texcoord"
        desc.uniform_blocks[1].stage = .FRAGMENT
        desc.uniform_blocks[1].layout = .STD140
        desc.uniform_blocks[1].size = 16
        desc.uniform_blocks[1].glsl_uniforms[0].type = .FLOAT4
        desc.uniform_blocks[1].glsl_uniforms[0].array_count = 1
        desc.uniform_blocks[1].glsl_uniforms[0].glsl_name = "fs_params_blur"
        desc.views[0].texture.stage = .FRAGMENT
        desc.views[0].texture.image_type = ._2D
        desc.views[0].texture.sample_type = .FLOAT
        desc.views[0].texture.multisampled = false
        desc.samplers[0].stage = .FRAGMENT
        desc.samplers[0].sampler_type = .FILTERING
        desc.texture_sampler_pairs[0].stage = .FRAGMENT
        desc.texture_sampler_pairs[0].view_slot = 0
        desc.texture_sampler_pairs[0].sampler_slot = 0
        desc.texture_sampler_pairs[0].glsl_name = "tex_blur_smp_blur"
    case .METAL_MACOS:
        desc.vertex_func.source = transmute(cstring)&blur_vs_source_metal_macos
        desc.vertex_func.entry = "main0"
        desc.fragment_func.source = transmute(cstring)&blur_fs_source_metal_macos
        desc.fragment_func.entry = "main0"
        desc.attrs[0].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[1].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.uniform_blocks[1].stage = .FRAGMENT
        desc.uniform_blocks[1].layout = .STD140
        desc.uniform_blocks[1].size = 16
        desc.uniform_blocks[1].msl_buffer_n = 1
        desc.views[0].texture.stage = .FRAGMENT
        desc.views[0].texture.image_type = ._2D
        desc.views[0].texture.sample_type = .FLOAT
        desc.views[0].texture.multisampled = false
        desc.views[0].texture.msl_texture_n = 0
        desc.samplers[0].stage = .FRAGMENT
        desc.samplers[0].sampler_type = .FILTERING
        desc.samplers[0].msl_sampler_n = 0
        desc.texture_sampler_pairs[0].stage = .FRAGMENT
        desc.texture_sampler_pairs[0].view_slot = 0
        desc.texture_sampler_pairs[0].sampler_slot = 0
    }
    return desc
}

    using namespace metal;

        float2 screen_size;

        float4 color [[user(locn0)]];

        float2 position [[attribute(0)]];

        float4 color0 [[attribute(2)]];

    vertex main0_out main0(main0_in in [[stage_in]], constant vs_params& _19 [[buffer(0)]])

        main0_out out = {};
        out.gl_Position = float4(((in.position.x / _19.screen_size.x) * 2.0) - 1.0, 1.0 - ((in.position.y / _19.screen_size.y) * 2.0), 0.0, 1.0);
        out.color = in.color0;
        out.uv = in.texcoord;
        return out;
    }

c2d_solid_shader_desc :: proc (backend: sg.Backend) -> sg.Shader_Desc {
    desc: sg.Shader_Desc
    desc.label = "c2d_solid_shader"
    #partial switch backend {
    case .GLES3:
        desc.vertex_func.source = transmute(cstring)&vs_source_glsl300es
        desc.vertex_func.entry = "main"
        desc.fragment_func.source = transmute(cstring)&fs_source_glsl300es
        desc.fragment_func.entry = "main"
        desc.attrs[0].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[0].glsl_name = "position"
        desc.attrs[1].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[1].glsl_name = "texcoord"
        desc.attrs[2].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[2].glsl_name = "color0"
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].stage = .VERTEX
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].layout = .STD140
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].size = 16
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].glsl_uniforms[0].type = .FLOAT4
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].glsl_uniforms[0].array_count = 1
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].glsl_uniforms[0].glsl_name = "vs_params"
        desc.views[0].texture.stage = .FRAGMENT
        desc.views[0].texture.image_type = ._2D
        desc.views[0].texture.sample_type = .FLOAT
        desc.views[0].texture.multisampled = false
        desc.samplers[0].stage = .FRAGMENT
        desc.samplers[0].sampler_type = .FILTERING
        desc.texture_sampler_pairs[0].stage = .FRAGMENT
        desc.texture_sampler_pairs[0].view_slot = 0
        desc.texture_sampler_pairs[0].sampler_slot = 0
        desc.texture_sampler_pairs[0].glsl_name = "tex_smp"
    case .METAL_MACOS:
        desc.vertex_func.source = transmute(cstring)&vs_source_metal_macos
        desc.vertex_func.entry = "main0"
        desc.fragment_func.source = transmute(cstring)&fs_source_metal_macos
        desc.fragment_func.entry = "main0"
        desc.attrs[0].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[1].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[2].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].stage = .VERTEX
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].layout = .STD140
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].size = 16
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].msl_buffer_n = 0
        desc.views[0].texture.stage = .FRAGMENT
        desc.views[0].texture.image_type = ._2D
        desc.views[0].texture.sample_type = .FLOAT
        desc.views[0].texture.multisampled = false
        desc.views[0].texture.msl_texture_n = 0
        desc.samplers[0].stage = .FRAGMENT
        desc.samplers[0].sampler_type = .FILTERING
        desc.samplers[0].msl_sampler_n = 0
        desc.texture_sampler_pairs[0].stage = .FRAGMENT
        desc.texture_sampler_pairs[0].view_slot = 0
        desc.texture_sampler_pairs[0].sampler_slot = 0
    }
    return desc
}

        desc.attrs[0].glsl_name = "position"
        desc.attrs[1].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[1].glsl_name = "texcoord"
        desc.attrs[2].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[2].glsl_name = "color0"
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].stage = .VERTEX
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].layout = .STD140
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].size = 16
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].glsl_uniforms[0].type = .FLOAT4
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].glsl_uniforms[0].array_count = 1
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].glsl_uniforms[0].glsl_name = "vs_text_sdf_params"
        desc.views[0].texture.stage = .FRAGMENT
        desc.views[0].texture.image_type = ._2D
        desc.views[0].texture.sample_type = .FLOAT
        desc.views[0].texture.multisampled = false
        desc.samplers[0].stage = .FRAGMENT
        desc.samplers[0].sampler_type = .FILTERING
        desc.texture_sampler_pairs[0].stage = .FRAGMENT
        desc.texture_sampler_pairs[0].view_slot = 0
        desc.texture_sampler_pairs[0].sampler_slot = 0
        desc.texture_sampler_pairs[0].glsl_name = "tex_sdf_text_smp_sdf_text"
    case .METAL_MACOS:
        desc.vertex_func.source = transmute(cstring)&vs_text_sdf_source_metal_macos
        desc.vertex_func.entry = "main0"
        desc.fragment_func.source = transmute(cstring)&fs_text_sdf_source_metal_macos
        desc.fragment_func.entry = "main0"

    #version 300 es
    precision mediump float;
    precision highp int;

    uniform highp vec4 fs_params_threshold[1];
    uniform highp sampler2D tex_postfx_smp_postfx;

    in highp vec2 v_texcoord;
    layout(location = 0) out highp vec4 frag_color;

    highp vec3 srgb_to_linear(highp vec3 c)
    {
        return pow(max(c, vec3(0.0)), vec3(2.2000000476837158203125));
    }

    void main()

        highp vec3 _44 = srgb_to_linear(param);

        {
            frag_color = vec4(_44, _39.w);

particle_instanced_shader_desc :: proc (backend: sg.Backend) -> sg.Shader_Desc {
    desc: sg.Shader_Desc
    desc.label = "particle_instanced_shader"
    #partial switch backend {
    case .GLES3:
        desc.vertex_func.source = transmute(cstring)&particle_vs_source_glsl300es
        desc.vertex_func.entry = "main"
        desc.fragment_func.source = transmute(cstring)&particle_fs_source_glsl300es
        desc.fragment_func.entry = "main"
        desc.attrs[0].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[0].glsl_name = "vert_local"
        desc.attrs[1].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[1].glsl_name = "inst_pos_size"
        desc.attrs[2].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[2].glsl_name = "inst_color"
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].stage = .VERTEX
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].layout = .STD140
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].size = 16
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].glsl_uniforms[0].type = .FLOAT4
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].glsl_uniforms[0].array_count = 1
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].glsl_uniforms[0].glsl_name = "vs_params_particle"
    case .METAL_MACOS:
        desc.vertex_func.source = transmute(cstring)&particle_vs_source_metal_macos
        desc.vertex_func.entry = "main0"
        desc.fragment_func.source = transmute(cstring)&particle_fs_source_metal_macos
        desc.fragment_func.entry = "main0"
        desc.attrs[0].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[1].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[2].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].stage = .VERTEX
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].layout = .STD140
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].size = 16
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].msl_buffer_n = 0
    }
    return desc
}

    0x5f,0x36,0x37,0x29,0x3b,0x0a,0x20,0x20,0x20,0x20,0x6f,0x75,0x74,0x2e,0x66,0x72,
    0x61,0x67,0x5f,0x63,0x6f,0x6c,0x6f,0x72,0x20,0x3d,0x20,0x69,0x6e,0x2e,0x76,0x5f,
    0x63,0x6f,0x6c,0x6f,0x72,0x20,0x2a,0x20,0x28,0x31,0x2e,0x30,0x20,0x2d,0x20,0x73,
    0x6d,0x6f,0x6f,0x74,0x68,0x73,0x74,0x65,0x70,0x28,0x2d,0x5f,0x37,0x30,0x2c,0x20,
    0x5f,0x37,0x30,0x2c,0x20,0x5f,0x36,0x37,0x29,0x29,0x3b,0x0a,0x20,0x20,0x20,0x20,
    0x72,0x65,0x74,0x75,0x72,0x6e,0x20,0x6f,0x75,0x74,0x3b,0x0a,0x7d,0x0a,0x0a,0x00,

}
c2d_sdf_shader_desc :: proc (backend: sg.Backend) -> sg.Shader_Desc {
    desc: sg.Shader_Desc
    desc.label = "c2d_sdf_shader"
    #partial switch backend {
    case .GLES3:
        desc.vertex_func.source = transmute(cstring)&sdf_vs_source_glsl300es
        desc.vertex_func.entry = "main"
        desc.fragment_func.source = transmute(cstring)&sdf_fs_source_glsl300es
        desc.fragment_func.entry = "main"
        desc.attrs[0].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[0].glsl_name = "position"
        desc.attrs[1].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[1].glsl_name = "texcoord"
        desc.attrs[2].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[2].glsl_name = "color0"
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].stage = .VERTEX
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].layout = .STD140
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].size = 16
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].glsl_uniforms[0].type = .FLOAT4
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].glsl_uniforms[0].array_count = 1
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].glsl_uniforms[0].glsl_name = "sdf_vs_params"
        desc.uniform_blocks[1].stage = .FRAGMENT
        desc.uniform_blocks[1].layout = .STD140
        desc.uniform_blocks[1].size = 16
        desc.uniform_blocks[1].glsl_uniforms[0].type = .FLOAT4
        desc.uniform_blocks[1].glsl_uniforms[0].array_count = 1
        desc.uniform_blocks[1].glsl_uniforms[0].glsl_name = "sdf_fs_params"
    case .METAL_MACOS:
        desc.vertex_func.source = transmute(cstring)&sdf_vs_source_metal_macos
        desc.vertex_func.entry = "main0"
        desc.fragment_func.source = transmute(cstring)&sdf_fs_source_metal_macos
        desc.fragment_func.entry = "main0"
        desc.attrs[0].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[1].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.attrs[2].base_type = .FLOAT
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].stage = .VERTEX
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].layout = .STD140
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].size = 16
        desc.uniform_blocks[0].msl_buffer_n = 0
        desc.uniform_blocks[1].stage = .FRAGMENT
        desc.uniform_blocks[1].layout = .STD140
        desc.uniform_blocks[1].size = 16
        desc.uniform_blocks[1].msl_buffer_n = 1
    }
    return desc
}

package graphics

import "core:fmt"
import "core:mem"
import "core:os"
import logging "project:logging"

gpu_asset_ensure_cache_dir :: proc(dir: string) {
	if !os.exists(dir) {
		err := os.make_directory(dir)
		if err != nil {
			logging.error(.IO, fmt.tprintf("Failed to create cache directory: %s - %v", dir, err))
		}
	}
}

gpu_asset_read_file :: proc(path: string, allocator: mem.Allocator) -> ([]u8, bool) {
	data, err := os.read_entire_file(path, allocator)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, false
	}
	return data, true
}

DEBUG: Source content: package graphics

import config "project:configpkg"
import txt "project:graphics/text"
import renderer "project:renderer"
import "base:runtime"
import fmt "core:fmt"

// using txt (Removed as file-scope 'using' is deprecated in Odin)

// Simple font manager: caches fonts per (path,size) to avoid reloading.
// Usage:
//   fm_init()
//   handle, ok := fm_get_handle("path.ttf", 18)
//   fm_shutdown()

Font_Manager :: struct {
	cache:          map[string]map[u32]^txt.Font, // path -> size_px -> ^Font
	fallback_cache: map[u32][dynamic]^txt.Font,   // size_px -> cached font chain
}

// --- renderer.Font_Handle bridge (G.4) ---
// Maps opaque renderer.Font_Handle IDs to concrete ^txt.Font pointers.
// Only the graphics/ layer (Layer 2) should call fm_resolve_handle.
@(private)
font_handle_next_id: u32 = 1

@(private)
// Keyed by Font_Handle.id (u32) to avoid map[struct] call_indirect crash on WASM.
// Odin maps with struct keys use a generic hasher stored as a proc pointer; on WASM
// the call_indirect type doesn't match → function signature mismatch at runtime.
font_handle_map: map[u32]^txt.Font

@(private)
font_ptr_to_handle: map[uintptr]u32 // reverse map for idempotent registration; value is Font_Handle.id

// Register a concrete ^Font and return its opaque handle.
// Idempotent: calling twice with the same pointer returns the same handle.
fm_register_handle :: proc(f: ^txt.Font) -> renderer.Font_Handle {
	if f == nil do return renderer.Font_Handle_Invalid
	key := uintptr(f)
	if id, ok := font_ptr_to_handle[key]; ok {
		return renderer.Font_Handle{id = id}
	}
	h := renderer.Font_Handle{id = font_handle_next_id}
	font_handle_next_id += 1
	font_handle_map[h.id] = f
	font_ptr_to_handle[key] = h.id
	return h
}

// Resolve a renderer.Font_Handle back to its concrete ^Font.
// Returns (nil, false) for invalid or unknown handles.
fm_resolve_handle :: proc(h: renderer.Font_Handle) -> (^txt.Font, bool) {
	if h.id == 0 do return nil, false
	f, ok := font_handle_map[h.id]
	return f, ok
}

// Unregister a handle (call before destroying the underlying Font).
fm_unregister_handle :: proc(h: renderer.Font_Handle) {
	if h.id == 0 do return
	if f, ok := font_handle_map[h.id]; ok {
		delete_key(&font_ptr_to_handle, uintptr(f))
	}
	delete_key(&font_handle_map, h.id)
}

// Load a font and return its opaque renderer.Font_Handle.
// If already cached, returns the existing handle without reloading.
fm_get_handle :: proc(path: string, size_px: u32) -> (renderer.Font_Handle, bool) {
	f, ok := fm_get(path, size_px)
	if !ok || f == nil do return renderer.Font_Handle_Invalid, false
	return fm_register_handle(f), true
}

@(private)

font_mgr: Font_Manager

// User-registered custom fallback fonts (global list)
custom_sys_fallbacks: [dynamic]string

// M3B: iterate all cached fonts (path -> size -> ^Font)
fm_each_font :: proc(visitor: proc(f: ^txt.Font)) {
	for _, size_map in font_mgr.cache {
		for _, fptr in size_map {
			if fptr != nil {
				visitor(fptr)
			}
		}
	}
}

// M3B: iterate all cached fonts (path -> size -> ^Font)

fm_init :: proc() {
	font_mgr.cache = make(map[string]map[u32]^txt.Font)
	font_mgr.fallback_cache = make(map[u32][dynamic]^txt.Font, 0, runtime.default_allocator())
	txt.font_paths = make(map[^txt.Font]string)
	txt.font_sizes = make(map[^txt.Font]u32)
	font_handle_map = make(map[u32]^txt.Font, 64, runtime.default_allocator())
	font_ptr_to_handle = make(map[uintptr]u32, 64, runtime.default_allocator())
	font_handle_next_id = 1
}

fm_get :: proc(path: string, size_px: u32) -> (^txt.Font, bool) {
	// Level-1: path
	size_map, ok := font_mgr.cache[path]
	if !ok {
		// Use system allocator for persistent cache metadata
		size_map = make(map[u32]^txt.Font, runtime.default_allocator())
		font_mgr.cache[path] = size_map
	}
	// Level-2: size
	if f, ok2 := size_map[size_px]; ok2 {
		return f, f != nil
	}

	// Not cached: create new atlas at requested size
	f_val, ok3 := create_font_atlas(path, size_px)
	if !ok3 {
		size_map[size_px] = nil // Cache the failure
		return nil, false
	}
	// Heap-allocate to keep a stable pointer in cache using system allocator
	f_ptr := new(txt.Font, runtime.default_allocator())
	if f_ptr == nil do return nil, false
	f_ptr^ = f_val
	size_map[size_px] = f_ptr
	// Track debug metadata (persistent)
	if txt.font_paths == nil {txt.font_paths = make(map[^txt.Font]string, runtime.default_allocator())}
	if txt.font_sizes == nil {txt.font_sizes = make(map[^txt.Font]u32, runtime.default_allocator())}
	txt.font_paths[f_ptr] = path
	txt.font_sizes[f_ptr] = size_px
	return f_ptr, true
}

fm_shutdown :: proc() {
	// Destroy all cached fonts and clear maps
	for _, size_map in font_mgr.cache {
		for _, fptr in size_map {
			if fptr != nil {
				destroy_font(fptr)
			}
		}
		delete(size_map)
	}
	delete(font_mgr.cache)
	for _, chain in font_mgr.fallback_cache {
		delete(chain)
	}
	delete(font_mgr.fallback_cache)
	if custom_sys_fallbacks != nil {
		delete(custom_sys_fallbacks)
	}
	if txt.font_paths != nil {delete(txt.font_paths)}
	if txt.font_sizes != nil {delete(txt.font_sizes)}
	delete(font_handle_map)
	delete(font_ptr_to_handle)
	txt.shaper_shutdown()
	font_files_shutdown()
}


// Register a custom font path to be used as a high-priority fallback.
// This allows users to support languages not covered by default Noto fonts.
fm_add_fallback_font :: proc(path: string) {
	if custom_sys_fallbacks == nil {
		custom_sys_fallbacks = make([dynamic]string)
	}
	append(&custom_sys_fallbacks, path)
}
// Return a default fallback chain for a given pixel size.
// Attempts to load common Noto fonts for Arabic, Hebrew, and CJK.
// Any font that fails to load is skipped.
// Return a default fallback chain for a given pixel size (CACHED).
fm_get_default_fallbacks :: proc(size_px: u32) -> []^txt.Font {
	if chain, ok := font_mgr.fallback_cache[size_px]; ok {
		return chain[:]
	}

	out := make([dynamic]^txt.Font, 0, 4, runtime.default_allocator())

	// 0. Custom user overrides
	if custom_sys_fallbacks != nil {
		for path in custom_sys_fallbacks {
			f, ok := fm_get(path, size_px)
			if ok && f != nil {
				append(&out, f)
			}
		}
	}

	// 1. Arabic & Hebrew
	{
		for p in config.FONT_FALLBACKS_ARABIC {
			f, ok := fm_get(p, size_px)
			if ok && f != nil {
				append(&out, f)
				break
			}
		}
		for p in config.FONT_FALLBACKS_HEBREW {
			f, ok := fm_get(p, size_px)
			if ok && f != nil {
				append(&out, f)
				break
			}
		}
	}

	// 2. CJK
	{
		for p in config.FONT_FALLBACKS_CJK {
			f, ok := fm_get(p, size_px)
			if ok && f != nil {
				append(&out, f)
				break
			}
		}
	}

	font_mgr.fallback_cache[size_px] = out
	return out[:]
}
