DEBUG: Source length: 10588
DEBUG: Source content: package text

import runtime "base:runtime"
import c "core:c"
import fmt "core:fmt"
import strings "core:strings"

// Optional logs for bidi decisions
LOG_BIDI :: #config(LOG_BIDI, false)
LOG_BIDI_RAW :: #config(LOG_BIDI_RAW, false)

// M2 scaffold: Bidirectional analysis (FriBidi integration placeholder)
// Final goal:
// - Convert UTF-8 to UTF-32 codepoints
// - Use FriBidi to compute embedding levels and split directional runs
// - Map even levels -> LTR, odd -> RTL

@(private)
fribidi_base_dir_cache: int = -1
// - Return logical runs for shaper to shape with matching hb_direction_t

when ODIN_OS == .Windows {
	foreign import fribidi "fribidi.lib"
} else when ODIN_OS == .Linux {
	foreign import fribidi "system:fribidi"
} else when ODIN_OS == .Darwin {
	foreign import fribidi "system:/opt/homebrew/lib/libfribidi.dylib"
} else when ODIN_ARCH == .wasm32 || ODIN_ARCH == .wasm64p32 {
	// libfribidi_wasm.a is passed to emcc explicitly in scripts/build_web.sh
	foreign import fribidi "env.o"
}

@(default_calling_convention = "c")
foreign fribidi {
	@(link_name = "fribidi_get_bidi_types")
	fribidi_get_bidi_types :: proc(str: [^]u32, len: c.int, types: [^]u32) ---
	@(link_name = "fribidi_get_bracket_types")
	fribidi_get_bracket_types :: proc(str: [^]u32, len: c.int, types: [^]u32, btypes: [^]u32) ---
	@(link_name = "fribidi_get_par_embedding_levels_ex")
	fribidi_get_par_embedding_levels_ex :: proc(types: [^]u32, btypes: [^]u32, len: c.int, pbase_dir: ^c.int, levels: [^]c.uchar) -> c.int ---
}


// Bidi run over [start, end) indices (byte indices into the original UTF-8 string for now)
BidiRun :: struct {
	start:     i32,
	end:       i32,
	level:     u8,
	direction: Direction,
}

@(private)
utf8_decode_at :: proc(s: string, i: i32) -> (rune, i32) {
	n := i32(len(s))
	if i >= n {
		return rune(0), 0
	}
	b0 := s[i]
	if (b0 & 0x80) == 0 { 	// 1-byte
		return rune(b0), 1
	} else if (b0 & 0xE0) == 0xC0 && i + 1 < n { 	// 2-byte
		b1 := s[i + 1]
		r := (rune(b0 & 0x1F) << 6) | rune(b1 & 0x3F)
		return r, 2
	} else if (b0 & 0xF0) == 0xE0 && i + 2 < n { 	// 3-byte
		b1 := s[i + 1]; b2 := s[i + 2]
		r := (rune(b0 & 0x0F) << 12) | (rune(b1 & 0x3F) << 6) | rune(b2 & 0x3F)
		return r, 3
	} else if (b0 & 0xF8) == 0xF0 && i + 3 < n { 	// 4-byte
		b1 := s[i + 1]; b2 := s[i + 2]; b3 := s[i + 3]
		r :=
			(rune(b0 & 0x07) << 18) |
			(rune(b1 & 0x3F) << 12) |
			(rune(b2 & 0x3F) << 6) |
			rune(b3 & 0x3F)
		return r, 4
	}
	return rune(b0), 1
}

// [Deleted unused is_rtl_cp helper]

/**
FriBidi-based bidi analysis: compute embedding levels and split into directional runs.
- Converts UTF-8 to UTF-32 codepoints
- Uses fribidi_get_bidi_types and fribidi_get_par_embedding_levels
- Maps even levels -> LTR, odd levels -> RTL
- Returns visual-ordered runs (Unicode BiDi reordering on runs)
*/
fribidi_analyze_paragraph :: proc(text_utf8: string) -> []BidiRun {
	// CRITICAL: Use temp allocator for per-frame temporary data
	context.allocator = context.temp_allocator
	runs := make([dynamic]BidiRun, 0, 4)
	n_bytes := i32(len(text_utf8))
	if n_bytes == 0 {
		return runs[:]
	}

	// Convert to UTF-32 codepoints and keep byte-position mapping
	cps := make([dynamic]u32, 0, n_bytes)
	cp_bytepos := make([dynamic]i32, 0, n_bytes)
	{
		i := i32(0)
		for i < n_bytes {
			cp, w := utf8_decode_at(text_utf8, i)
			if w <= 0 {
				break
			}
			_, _ = runtime.append_elem(&cps, u32(cp))
			_, _ = runtime.append_elem(&cp_bytepos, i)
			i += w
		}
	}
	n := i32(len(cps))
	if n == 0 {
		return runs[:]
	}

	// Query types and embedding levels from FriBidi
	types := make([dynamic]u32, n)
	btypes := make([dynamic]u32, n)
	levels := make([dynamic]c.uchar, n)
	// Optimization: Cache environment variable lookup to avoid allocations per-paragraph
	if fribidi_base_dir_cache == -1 {
		fribidi_base_dir_cache = 2 // Default: AUTO

		when #config(FRIBIDI_BASE_DIR_LTR, false) {fribidi_base_dir_cache = 0}
		when #config(FRIBIDI_BASE_DIR_RTL, false) {fribidi_base_dir_cache = 1}

		// Runtime override
		dir_env := strings.trim_space(strings.to_lower(bidi_get_env("FRIBIDI_BASE_DIR")))
		if len(dir_env) > 0 {
			if dir_env == "ltr" {
				fribidi_base_dir_cache = 0
			} else if dir_env == "rtl" {
				fribidi_base_dir_cache = 1
			} else if dir_env == "auto" {
				fribidi_base_dir_cache = 2
			}
		}
	}
	base_dir: c.int = c.int(fribidi_base_dir_cache)

	// DIAGNOSTIC M2: Capture base_dir before call
	base_dir_in := base_dir

	{
		raw_cps: [^]u32 = cast([^]u32)&cps[0]
		raw_types: [^]u32 = cast([^]u32)&types[0]
		raw_btypes: [^]u32 = cast([^]u32)&btypes[0]
		raw_levels: [^]c.uchar = cast([^]c.uchar)&levels[0]
		fribidi_get_bidi_types(raw_cps, c.int(n), raw_types)
		fribidi_get_bracket_types(raw_cps, c.int(n), raw_types, raw_btypes)
		_ = fribidi_get_par_embedding_levels_ex(
			raw_types,
			raw_btypes,
			c.int(n),
			&base_dir,
			raw_levels,
		)
	}

	when #config(LOG_BIDI, false) {
		bd := "AUTO"
		if base_dir == 0 {bd = "LTR"} else if base_dir == 1 {bd = "RTL"}
		fmt.println(
			"[BIDI] base_dir_in=",
			base_dir_in,
			" base_dir_out=",
			base_dir,
			" (",
			bd,
			") cps=",
			n,
		)

		// Dump types and levels to debug weak/neutral resolution
		fmt.print("[BIDI_DUMP] types=[")
		for i in 0 ..< n {fmt.printf("%X ", types[i])}
		fmt.print("] levels=[")
		for i in 0 ..< n {fmt.printf("%d ", levels[i])}
		fmt.println("]")

		// Dump input hex
		fmt.print("[BIDI_INPUT] hex=")
		for i in 0 ..< n_bytes {fmt.printf("%02X", text_utf8[i])}
		fmt.println("")
	}

	// Low-noise, compact raw diagnostics for compactor
	when #config(LOG_BIDI_RAW, false) {
		// Re-read env to report parsed value
		dir_env_raw := strings.trim_space(strings.to_lower(bidi_get_env("FRIBIDI_BASE_DIR")))
		bd_sym := "AUTO"
		if base_dir == 0 {bd_sym = "LTR"} else if base_dir == 1 {bd_sym = "RTL"}
		// Parity summary
		odd := 0; even := 0
		for i in 0 ..< n {if (levels[i] & 1) != 0 {odd += 1} else {even += 1}}
		// Truncate dumps
		m := n
		if m > 64 {m = 64}
		mb := n_bytes
		if mb > 128 {mb = 128}
		// Header line
		fmt.println(
			"[BIDI_RAW] env=",
			dir_env_raw,
			" base_in=",
			base_dir_in,
			" base_out=",
			base_dir,
			" sym=",
			bd_sym,
			" cps=",
			n,
			" bytes=",
			n_bytes,
			" parity=",
			odd,
			"/",
			even,
		)
		// Types slice
		fmt.print("[BIDI_RAW] types=")
		for i in 0 ..< m {fmt.printf("%X ", types[i])}
		fmt.println("")
		// Levels slice
		fmt.print("[BIDI_RAW] levels=")
		for i in 0 ..< m {fmt.printf("%d ", levels[i])}
		fmt.println("")
		// Input hex slice
		fmt.print("[BIDI_RAW] hex=")
		for i in 0 ..< mb {fmt.printf("%02X", text_utf8[i])}
		if mb < n_bytes {fmt.print("...")}
		fmt.println("")
	}

	// Build logical runs by level parity changes
	run_start := i32(0)
	curr_level: u8 = u8(levels[0])
	for idx in 1 ..< n {
		if u8(levels[idx]) != curr_level {
			dir := Direction.LTR
			if (curr_level & 1) != 0 {
				dir = Direction.RTL
			}
			start_b := cp_bytepos[run_start]
			end_b := cp_bytepos[idx]
			_, _ = runtime.append_elem(
				&runs,
				BidiRun{start = start_b, end = end_b, level = u8(curr_level), direction = dir},
			)
			run_start = idx
			curr_level = levels[idx]
		}
	}
	// Tail run
	{
		dir := Direction.LTR
		if (curr_level & 1) != 0 {
			dir = Direction.RTL
		}
		start_b := cp_bytepos[run_start]
		end_b := n_bytes
		_, _ = runtime.append_elem(
			&runs,
			BidiRun{start = start_b, end = end_b, level = u8(curr_level), direction = dir},
		)
	}

	// Reorder runs to visual order per Unicode BiDi (implicit base direction)
	// Visual reordering algorithm: for levels from max..1, reverse consecutive runs with level >= current.
	max_level: u8 = 0
	for i in 0 ..< len(runs) {
		if runs[i].level > max_level {max_level = runs[i].level}
	}
	lvl := i32(max_level)
	for lvl >= 1 {
		i := 0
		for i < len(runs) {
			if runs[i].level >= u8(lvl) {
				j := i
				for j < len(runs) && runs[j].level >= u8(lvl) {
					j += 1
				}
				// reverse runs[i:j]
				l := i; r := j - 1
				for l < r {
					tmp := runs[l]; runs[l] = runs[r]; runs[r] = tmp
					l += 1; r -= 1
				}
				i = j
			} else {
				i += 1
			}
		}
		lvl -= 1
	}

	when #config(LOG_BIDI, false) {
		for i in 0 ..< len(runs) {
			dr := "LTR"
			if runs[i].direction == Direction.RTL {dr = "RTL"}
			fmt.println(
				"[BIDI_RUN] i=",
				i,
				" s=",
				runs[i].start,
				" e=",
				runs[i].end,
				" lvl=",
				runs[i].level,
				" dr=",
				dr,
			)
		}
	}
	return runs[:]
}

// Helper: query paragraph base direction using FriBidi; LTR fallback for AUTO
get_paragraph_base_dir :: proc(text_utf8: string) -> Direction {
	// CRITICAL: Use temp allocator for per-frame temporary data
	context.allocator = context.temp_allocator
	n_bytes := i32(len(text_utf8))
	when #config(LOG_BIDI, false) {
		fmt.println("[BIDI] PAR_DIR_PROBE start len=", n_bytes)
	}
	if n_bytes == 0 {return Direction.LTR}
	// Convert to UTF-32
	cps := make([dynamic]u32, 0, n_bytes)
	{
		i := i32(0)
		for i < n_bytes {
			cp, w := utf8_decode_at(text_utf8, i)
			if w <= 0 {break}
			_, _ = runtime.append_elem(&cps, u32(cp))
			i += w
		}
	}
	n := i32(len(cps))
	if n == 0 {return Direction.LTR}
	types := make([dynamic]u32, n)
	btypes := make([dynamic]u32, n)
	levels := make([dynamic]c.uchar, n)
	base_dir: c.int = 2 // AUTO
	when #config(FRIBIDI_BASE_DIR_LTR, false) {base_dir = 0}
	when #config(FRIBIDI_BASE_DIR_RTL, false) {base_dir = 1}
	{
		dir_env := strings.trim_space(strings.to_lower(bidi_get_env("FRIBIDI_BASE_DIR")))
		if len(dir_env) > 0 {
			if dir_env ==
			   "ltr" {base_dir = 0} else if dir_env == "rtl" {base_dir = 1} else if dir_env == "auto" {base_dir = 2}
		}
	}
	raw_cps: [^]u32 = cast([^]u32)&cps[0]
	raw_types: [^]u32 = cast([^]u32)&types[0]
	raw_btypes: [^]u32 = cast([^]u32)&btypes[0]
	raw_levels: [^]c.uchar = cast([^]c.uchar)&levels[0]
	fribidi_get_bidi_types(raw_cps, c.int(n), raw_types)
	fribidi_get_bracket_types(raw_cps, c.int(n), raw_types, raw_btypes)
	// Duplicates removed

	base_dir_in := base_dir
	_ = fribidi_get_par_embedding_levels_ex(raw_types, raw_btypes, c.int(n), &base_dir, raw_levels)

	// Debug logging for detection failure
	when #config(LOG_BIDI, false) {
		bd_in := "AUTO"; bd_out := "LTR"
		if base_dir_in == 0 {bd_in = "LTR"} else if base_dir_in == 1 {bd_in = "RTL"}
		if base_dir == 1 {bd_out = "RTL"} else if base_dir == 2 {bd_out = "AUTO"}
		fmt.println(
			"[BIDI] PAR_DIR_PROBE in=",
			bd_in,
			"(",
			base_dir_in,
			") out=",
			bd_out,
			"(",
			base_dir,
			") n=",
			n,
		)
	}

	// Parity check: odd = RTL, even = LTR (handles 273 vs 272)
	if (base_dir & 1) != 0 {return Direction.RTL}
	return Direction.LTR
}

import ft "vendor-local:odin-freetype"
import hb "vendor-local:odin-harfbuzz/harfbuzz"
import sg "vendor-local:sokol-odin/sokol/gfx"

// Direction of a Bidi run or text flow

// Direction of a Bidi run or text flow
Direction :: enum {
	LTR,
	RTL,
}

// Unicode script classification for font selection/shaping heuristics
Script_Kind :: enum {
	Latin,
	Arabic,
	Hebrew,
	CJK,
	Emoji,
	Symbol,
	Other,
}

Text_Modifier :: enum {
	Bold,
	Italic,
	Underline,
	Strikethrough,
}

Text_Modifiers :: bit_set[Text_Modifier]

// Internal style bits for atlas keying
FONT_STYLE_BOLD :: 0x01
FONT_STYLE_ITALIC :: 0x02

// Shaped glyph data in pixel units (after hb_font scale)
Shaped_Glyph :: struct {
	id:        u32, // glyph id in the font
	advance_x: f32, // x advance in pixels
	advance_y: f32, // y advance in pixels
	offset_x:  f32, // x offset from pen in pixels
	offset_y:  f32, // y offset from pen in pixels
	cluster:   i32, // source text cluster index
}

// Itemized sub-run with a concrete font assignment over [start, end) byte range
ItemizedRun :: struct {
	start: i32,
	end:   i32,
	dir:   Direction,
	font:  ^Font,
}

// Generic shaped span
Shaped_Span :: struct {
	font:   ^Font,
	dir:    Direction,
	script: Script_Kind,
	start:  i32,
	end:    i32,
	glyphs: []Shaped_Glyph,
}

// Selection quad for rendering text selection backgrounds
Selection_Quad :: struct {
	x, y, w, h: f32,
	line_index: i32,
}

// --- Moved from renderer/font_atlas.odin for package consolidation ---

// Debug labeling for fonts used in logs (persistent, allocated with system allocator)
font_paths: map[^Font]string
font_sizes: map[^Font]u32

get_font_path :: proc(font: ^Font) -> string {
	if font_paths != nil {
		if p, ok := font_paths[font]; ok {
			return p
		}
	}
	return ""
}

get_font_size :: proc(font: ^Font) -> u32 {
	if font_sizes != nil {
		if s, ok := font_sizes[font]; ok {
			return s
		}
	}
	return 0
}

Glyph_Info :: struct {
	u0, v0, u1, v1:  f32,
	width, height:   f32,
	bearing_x:       f32,
	bearing_y:       f32,
	advance:         f32,
	last_used_frame: u32,
	row_index:       i32,
	row_epoch:       u32,
	y0_px:           i32,
	y1_px:           i32,
	page_id:         i16,
}

Atlas_Row :: struct {
	y:                  i32,
	h:                  i32,
	last_used_frame:    u32,
	active:             bool,
	size_class:         u8,
	tallest_glyph_px:   i32,
	eviction_count:     u32,
	epoch:              u32,
	hot_score:          i32,
	hot_until_frame:    u32,
	last_touched_frame: u32,
	shelf_idx:          i32, // associated shelf index (-1 if none)
}

Shelf :: struct {
	y:          i32,
	h:          i32,
	curr_x:     i32,
	size_class: u8,
	row_idx:    i32,
	row_epoch:  u32,
}

// Font_File holds data that is identical across all pixel sizes of the same font file.
// One instance per unique file path; shared by all Font size-handles via ref_count.
Font_File :: struct {
	font_data: []u8,       // Raw file bytes — kept alive for FT and HB raw-pointer APIs
	face:      ft.Face,    // FreeType face (vector outlines); call set_pixel_sizes before rasterizing
	hb_blob:   ^hb.blob_t,
	hb_face:   ^hb.face_t,
	ref_count: int,
}

Font :: struct {
	file:                    ^Font_File, // Shared per-file data (face, raw bytes, hb_face)
	size_px:                 u32,        // Pixel size this handle was created at
	atlas_image:             sg.Image,
	glyphs:                  map[u32]Glyph_Info,
	hb_font:                 ^hb.font_t, // Per-size HB font wrapping file.hb_face
	ascent:                  f32,
	descent:                 f32,
	line_height:             f32,
	atlas_w:                 i32,
	atlas_h:                 i32,
	rgba_pixels:             []u8,
	curr_x:                  i32,
	curr_y:                  i32,
	max_row_h:               i32,
	atlas_dirty:             bool,
	atlas_last_update_frame: u32,
}

Atlas_Page :: struct {
	id:                         i16,
	image:                      sg.Image,
	rgba_pixels:                []u8,
	curr_x:                     i32,
	curr_y:                     i32,
	max_row_h:                  i32,
	rows:                       [dynamic]Atlas_Row,
	purge_index:                map[i32][dynamic]Glyph_Row_Ref,
	pinned_rows:                map[i32]bool,
	write_barrier_y:            i32,
	did_wrap_this_frame:        bool,
	wrap_count:                 u32,
	last_wrap_frame:            u32,
	defers_this_frame:          u32,
	miss_this_frame:            u32,
	miss_suppressed_this_frame: u32,
	dirty:                      bool,

	// Option 2: Shelves per page
	shelves:                    [dynamic]Shelf,
	shelf_buckets:              [8][dynamic]i32,
	next_fit_idx:               [8]i32,
}

Shared_Atlas :: struct {
	pages:                         [dynamic]Atlas_Page,
	atlas_w:                       i32,
	atlas_h:                       i32,
	last_update_frame:             u32,
	initialized:                   bool,
	preflight_uploaded_this_frame: bool,
	did_postdraw_upload:           bool,
	repacks_needed_this_frame:     u32,
	uv_track:                      map[u32]Tracked_UV,
	cache_stats_last_frame:        u32,
	// Aggregated per-frame counters
	defers_this_frame:             u32,
	evictions_this_frame:          u32,
	miss_this_frame:               u32,
	miss_suppressed_this_frame:    u32,
	upload_this_frame:             u32,
	max_y_filled:                  i32,
}

Glyph_Row_Ref :: struct {
	font:      ^Font,
	glyph_key: u32, // (glyph_index << 2) | style_bits
}

Tracked_UV :: struct {
	row_index:         i32,
	row_epoch:         u32,
	u0, v0, u1, v1:    f32,
	last_repack_frame: u32,
	last_logged_frame: u32,
}

import "base:runtime"
import c "core:c"
import ft "vendor-local:odin-freetype"
import hb "vendor-local:odin-harfbuzz/harfbuzz"

// Cache for nominal-glyph support probes per font
Glyph_Support :: struct {
	present:  bool,
	glyph_id: u32,
}

@(private)
glyph_support_cache: map[^Font]map[rune]Glyph_Support

@(private)
ensure_font_cache :: proc(font: ^Font) -> ^map[rune]Glyph_Support {
	if glyph_support_cache == nil {
		glyph_support_cache = make(map[^Font]map[rune]Glyph_Support, runtime.default_allocator())
	}
	table, ok := &glyph_support_cache[font]
	if !ok {
		glyph_support_cache[font] = make(map[rune]Glyph_Support, runtime.default_allocator())
		table = &glyph_support_cache[font]
	}
	return table
}


// Phase 10.Z: Debug helper to track cache size
get_cache_stats :: proc() -> (int, int) {
	if glyph_support_cache == nil do return 0, 0
	total_entries := 0
	for _, table in glyph_support_cache {
		total_entries += len(table)
	}
	return len(glyph_support_cache), total_entries
}

// has_glyph: query font for nominal glyph for a Unicode codepoint and cache the result
has_glyph :: proc(font: ^Font, cp: rune) -> (bool, u32) {
	if font == nil do return false, 0
	table := ensure_font_cache(font)
	if gs, ok := table^[cp]; ok do return gs.present, gs.glyph_id

	present: bool
	gid_u32: u32 = 0
	if font.hb_font != nil {
		gid: hb.codepoint_t
		found := hb.font_get_nominal_glyph(font.hb_font, hb.codepoint_t(cp), &gid)
		present = found != 0
		gid_u32 = u32(gid)
	} else if font.file != nil && font.file.face != nil {
		gi := ft.get_char_index(font.file.face, c.ulong(cp))
		present = gi != 0
		gid_u32 = u32(gi)
	}

	table^[cp] = Glyph_Support {
		present  = present,
		glyph_id = gid_u32,
	}
	return present, gid_u32
}


// utf8_decode_at (Removed duplicated declaration; using the one in bidi.odin)

@(private)
is_cluster_linker :: proc(cp: rune) -> bool {
	return cp == 0x200D || cp == 0xFE0E || cp == 0xFE0F
}

@(private)
is_combining_mark :: proc(cp: rune) -> bool {
	if cp >= 0x0591 && cp <= 0x05C7 do return true
	if (cp >= 0x0610 && cp <= 0x061A) || (cp >= 0x064B && cp <= 0x065F) || (cp == 0x0670) || (cp >= 0x06D6 && cp <= 0x06ED) do return true
	return false
}

@(private)
is_arabic_cp :: proc(cp: rune) -> bool {
	return(
		(cp >= 0x0600 && cp <= 0x06FF) ||
		(cp >= 0x0750 && cp <= 0x077F) ||
		(cp >= 0x08A0 && cp <= 0x08FF) \
	)
}

@(private)
is_hebrew_cp :: proc(cp: rune) -> bool {
	return cp >= 0x0590 && cp <= 0x05FF
}

itemize_by_font :: proc(
	text: string,
	run: BidiRun,
	primary: ^Font,
	fallbacks: []^Font,
	default_fallbacks: []^Font,
) -> []ItemizedRun {
	context.allocator = context.temp_allocator
	items := make([dynamic]ItemizedRun, 0, 8)
	if len(text) == 0 || run.end <= run.start do return items[:]

	i := run.start
	curr_font: ^Font = nil
	sub_start := i32(-1)

	for i < run.end {
		cp, w := utf8_decode_at(text, i)
		if w <= 0 do break

		j := i + w
		for j < run.end {
			next_cp, w2 := utf8_decode_at(text, j)
			if w2 <= 0 do break
			if is_cluster_linker(next_cp) || is_combining_mark(next_cp) {
				j += w2
				continue
			}
			break
		}

		selected: ^Font = nil
		if is_arabic_cp(cp) || is_hebrew_cp(cp) {
			for f in fallbacks {
				if ok, _ := has_glyph(f, cp); ok {
					selected = f
					break
				}
			}
			if selected == nil {
				for f in default_fallbacks {
					if ok, _ := has_glyph(f, cp); ok {
						selected = f
						break
					}
				}
			}
			if selected == nil {
				if ok, _ := has_glyph(primary, cp); ok do selected = primary
			}
		} else {
			if ok, _ := has_glyph(primary, cp); ok {
				selected = primary
			} else {
				for f in fallbacks {
					if ok, _ := has_glyph(f, cp); ok {
						selected = f
						break
					}
				}
				if selected == nil {
					for f in default_fallbacks {
						if ok, _ := has_glyph(f, cp); ok {
							selected = f
							break
						}
					}
				}
			}
		}

		if selected == nil do selected = primary

		if curr_font == nil {
			curr_font = selected
			sub_start = i
		} else if selected != curr_font {
			if sub_start >= 0 && curr_font != nil && i > sub_start {
				append(
					&items,
					ItemizedRun{start = sub_start, end = i, dir = run.direction, font = curr_font},
				)
			}
			curr_font = selected
			sub_start = i
		}
		i = j
	}

	if sub_start >= 0 && curr_font != nil && i > sub_start {
		append(
			&items,
			ItemizedRun{start = sub_start, end = i, dir = run.direction, font = curr_font},
		)
	}
	return items[:]
}

// TextBlockLayout: a lightweight, reusable wrapper around shaping, breaking and alignment
// Intended for per-frame usage with context.temp_allocator; persist only when needed.

TextBlockLayout :: struct {
    text:         string,
    shaped_spans: []Shaped_Span,
    lines:        []Visual_Line,
    metrics:      Text_Metrics,
    x_offsets:    []f32,
    y_offsets:    []f32,
    max_width:    f32,
    max_lines:    i32,
    use_ellipsis: bool,
    h_align:      Horizontal_Align,
    v_align:      Vertical_Align,
    line_height:  f32,
    // Optional: link hit regions built from RichText spans (id -> selection quads)
    link_quads_by_id: map[u32][dynamic]Selection_Quad,
}

// Prepare or re-prepare the layout for current text and configuration.
text_block_prepare :: proc(
    blk: ^TextBlockLayout,
    primary: ^Font,
    fallbacks: []^Font,
    default_fallbacks: []^Font,
) {
    // Use temp allocator for all intermediate work
    context.allocator = context.temp_allocator

    // 1) Shape with itemization and font fallback
    blk.shaped_spans = shape_text_itemized(primary, fallbacks, default_fallbacks, blk.text)

    // 2) Break and align
    blk.lines = break_lines_greedy(blk.text, blk.shaped_spans, blk.max_width, blk.max_lines, blk.use_ellipsis)
    blk.metrics, blk.x_offsets, blk.y_offsets = align_text_layout(
        blk.lines,
        blk.max_width,
        blk.h_align,
        blk.v_align,
        blk.line_height,
    )
}

// Convenience: create a new TextBlockLayout with defaults
text_block_make :: proc(text: string, max_width: f32, h: Horizontal_Align = .Left, v: Vertical_Align = .Top, line_height_mult: f32 = 1.0, max_lines: i32 = 0, use_ellipsis: bool = false) -> TextBlockLayout {
    return TextBlockLayout{
        text = text,
        max_width = max_width,
        max_lines = max_lines,
        use_ellipsis = use_ellipsis,
        h_align = h,
        v_align = v,
        line_height = line_height_mult,
    }
}

// Hit-test: map local (x,y) to UTF-8 byte index
text_block_hit_test :: proc(blk: ^TextBlockLayout, x, y: f32) -> int {
    return txt_coordinate_to_index(blk.text, blk.metrics, blk.lines, blk.x_offsets, blk.y_offsets, x, y)
}

// Map UTF-8 byte index to local (x,y) at the line baseline
text_block_index_to_xy :: proc(blk: ^TextBlockLayout, index: int) -> (f32, f32) {
    return txt_index_to_coordinate(blk.metrics, blk.lines, blk.x_offsets, blk.y_offsets, index)
}

// Build selection rectangles for [start,end) in local coordinates
text_block_build_selection :: proc(blk: ^TextBlockLayout, start: int, end_: int) -> []Selection_Quad {
    return build_selection_quads(blk.text, blk.lines, blk.x_offsets, blk.y_offsets, start, end_)
}

// Rich Text support: data model and shaping/drawing stubs (M4a)
// NOTE: Keep this file free of imports to renderer to avoid circular deps.

// A styled span over logical byte range [start, end)
TextSpan :: struct {
    start:          i32,         // inclusive byte offset in RichText.text
    end:            i32,         // exclusive byte offset in RichText.text
    font:           ^Font,       // optional override; nil -> use primary
    color:          [4]f32,      // RGBA per-span color (0,0,0,0 -> use default)
    link_id:        u32,         // optional link identifier (0 = none)
    baseline_shift: f32,         // pixels; positive moves up visually
    scale:          f32,         // 1.0 = normal; optional per-span scale
}

// Declarative rich text payload
RichText :: struct {
    text:  string,
    spans: []TextSpan, // Spans are expected to be non-overlapping and sorted by start
}

// Convenience type for link bookkeeping
LinkRange :: struct {
    start: i32,
    end:   i32,
    id:    u32,
}

// M4: Shaping across style spans (placeholder for M4a)
// For scaffolding, we delegate to existing itemized shaping over the full text.
// M4b will split by style boundaries and carry metadata alongside shaped spans.
shape_rich_text :: proc(
    rich: RichText,
    primary: ^Font,
    fallbacks: []^Font,
    default_fallbacks: []^Font = nil,
) -> []Shaped_Span {
    if primary == nil || len(rich.text) == 0 {
        return make([dynamic]Shaped_Span, 0, 0)[:]
    }

    // If no explicit style spans are provided, shape the entire text with the primary font.
    if len(rich.spans) == 0 {
        return shape_text_itemized(primary, fallbacks, default_fallbacks, rich.text)
    }

    out := make([dynamic]Shaped_Span, 0, len(rich.spans))

    // Iterate declared style spans, shape per segment, and normalize byte indices
    for i in 0 ..< len(rich.spans) {
        rs := rich.spans[i]
        // Clamp and validate
        s := rs.start
        e := rs.end
        if s < 0 do s = 0
        if e < s do e = s
        if e > i32(len(rich.text)) do e = i32(len(rich.text))
        if s >= e do continue

        seg_text := rich.text[s:e]
        use_primary := rs.font != nil ? rs.font : primary

        shaped := shape_text_itemized(use_primary, fallbacks, default_fallbacks, seg_text)
        if len(shaped) == 0 do continue

        // Offset clusters and span byte ranges to global coordinates
        for j in 0 ..< len(shaped) {
            sp := shaped[j]
            sp.start += s
            sp.end += s
            // Normalize clusters to absolute byte offsets
            for k in 0 ..< len(sp.glyphs) {sp.glyphs[k].cluster += s}
            append(&out, sp)
        }
    }

    return out[:]
}

// Produce a cached TextBlockLayout from a RichText payload
// Note: Does not draw; callers can render via g2d_draw_text_layout
rich_to_layout :: proc(
    rich: RichText,
    primary: ^Font,
    fallbacks: []^Font,
    default_fallbacks: []^Font,
    max_width: f32,
    max_lines: i32,
    use_ellipsis: bool,
    h_align: Horizontal_Align = .Left,
    v_align: Vertical_Align = .Top,
    line_height_mult: f32 = 1.0,
) -> TextBlockLayout {
    blk := text_block_make(rich.text, max_width, h_align, v_align, line_height_mult, max_lines, use_ellipsis)
    // Prepare using existing itemized shaping for now (M4a scaffold)
    text_block_prepare(&blk, primary, fallbacks, default_fallbacks)
    return blk
}

// M5: Link hit-test helper placeholder. Will be implemented when link regions are built.
txt_hit_link :: proc(blk: ^TextBlockLayout, x, y: f32) -> (u32, bool) {
    // Placeholder (M5b will populate link regions and implement this)
    return 0, false
}

DEBUG: Source content: package text

import "core:fmt"
import "core:unicode"


// UAX#14 Line Break Class subsets
Line_Break_Class :: enum {
	AL, // Alphabetic
	BA, // Break After
	BB, // Break Before
	BK, // Mandatory Break
	CM, // Combining Mark
	EX, // Exclamation/Interrogation
	GL, // Glue
	HL, // Hebrew Letter
	ID, // Ideographic
	IN, // Inseparable
	IS, // Infix Numeric Separator
	LF, // Line Feed
	NS, // Nonstarter
	NU, // Numeric
	OP, // Open Punctuation
	QU, // Quotation
	SP, // Space
	ZW, // Zero Width Space
	// ... add more as needed
	OTHER,
}

// Map a rune to its approximate LB class (simplified subset)
get_lb_class :: proc(cp: rune) -> Line_Break_Class {
	if cp == '\n' || cp == '\r' do return .LF
	if cp == 0x00A0 do return .GL // NBSP (no break)
	if cp == ' ' || cp == '\t' do return .SP
	if cp == 0x200B do return .ZW // ZWSP

	if cp >= 0x4E00 && cp <= 0x9FFF do return .ID // CJK Ideographs
	if (cp >= 0x3040 && cp <= 0x309F) || (cp >= 0x30A0 && cp <= 0x30FF) do return .ID // Hiragana/Katakana

	if unicode.is_alpha(cp) do return .AL
	if unicode.is_number(cp) do return .NU

	switch cp {
	case '(', '[', '{':
		return .OP
	case ')', ']', '}':
		return .HL // Actually NS/CL but HL is fine for now
	case '!', '?', ';', ':':
		return .EX
	case '-', '/':
		return .BA
	case '.', ',':
		return .IS
	}

	return .OTHER
}

// Layout metrics for a block of text
Text_Metrics :: struct {
	width:      f32,
	height:     f32,
	ascent:     f32, // maximum ascent across all lines
	descent:    f32, // maximum descent across all lines
	line_count: i32,
}

Horizontal_Align :: enum {
	Left,
	Center,
	Right,
	Justify,
}

Vertical_Align :: enum {
	Top,
	Middle,
	Bottom,
	Baseline,
}

// A single line of shaped text
Visual_Line :: struct {
	spans:   []Shaped_Span, // Spans belonging to this line (may be sub-spans of original)
	width:   f32,
	ascent:  f32,
	descent: f32,
}

// Greedy line breaker: splits shaped spans into lines based on a maximum width.
// Returns a slice of Visual_Lines. Each Visual_Line contains sub-spans of the original input.
// If max_lines > 0, stops after reaching the limit.
break_lines_greedy :: proc(
	text: string,
	spans: []Shaped_Span,
	max_width: f32,
	max_lines: i32 = 0,
	use_ellipsis: bool = false,
) -> []Visual_Line {
	// CRITICAL: Use temp allocator for per-frame temporary data
	context.allocator = context.temp_allocator
	lines := make([dynamic]Visual_Line, 0, 4)
	if len(spans) == 0 do return lines[:]

	curr_line_spans := make([dynamic]Shaped_Span, 0, 8)
	curr_w: f32 = 0
	curr_line_ascent: f32 = 0
	curr_line_descent: f32 = 0

	// Pending spans that haven't been processed yet
	pending_spans := make([dynamic]Shaped_Span, 0, len(spans))
	append(&pending_spans, ..spans)

	for len(pending_spans) > 0 {
		s := pending_spans[0]
		ordered_remove(&pending_spans, 0)

		last_break_glyph_idx := -1
		last_break_w: f32 = 0

		span_w: f32 = 0
		broken := false

		for g_idx in 0 ..< len(s.glyphs) {
			g := s.glyphs[g_idx]

			// Check for break opportunity AFTER this glyph
			if g.cluster >= 0 && int(g.cluster) < len(text) {
				cp, _ := utf8_decode_at(text, g.cluster)
				lbc := get_lb_class(cp)
				if lbc == .SP || lbc == .ZW {
					last_break_glyph_idx = g_idx + 1
					last_break_w = curr_w + span_w + g.advance_x
				}
			}

			if curr_w + span_w + g.advance_x > max_width && max_width > 0 {
				// We must break. Use last opportunity if it was on THIS line.
				break_idx := -1

				if last_break_glyph_idx != -1 {
					break_idx = last_break_glyph_idx
					curr_w = last_break_w
				} else if len(curr_line_spans) > 0 {
					// No break in this span, but previous spans exist: break at start of this span
					break_idx = 0
				} else {
					// Force break at current glyph (hard wrap)
					break_idx = g_idx > 0 ? g_idx : 1
					curr_w += span_w // current accumulated span width
				}

				if break_idx == 0 {
					// Break at span boundary
					inject_at(&pending_spans, 0, s)
				} else if break_idx < len(s.glyphs) {
					// Split span
					head := s
					head.glyphs = s.glyphs[:break_idx]
					head.end = s.glyphs[break_idx].cluster

					tail := s
					tail.glyphs = s.glyphs[break_idx:]
					tail.start = s.glyphs[break_idx].cluster

					append(&curr_line_spans, head)
					if head.font != nil {
						if head.font.ascent > curr_line_ascent do curr_line_ascent = head.font.ascent
						if head.font.descent > curr_line_descent do curr_line_descent = head.font.descent
					}
					inject_at(&pending_spans, 0, tail)

					// Re-calculate curr_w for the head if it wasn't set by last_break_w
					if last_break_glyph_idx == -1 {
						curr_w = 0
						for hg in head.glyphs do curr_w += hg.advance_x
					}
				} else {
					// Break at end of span (exactly fits)
					append(&curr_line_spans, s)
					if s.font != nil {
						if s.font.ascent > curr_line_ascent do curr_line_ascent = s.font.ascent
						if s.font.descent > curr_line_descent do curr_line_descent = s.font.descent
					}
				}

				// Flush line
				append(
					&lines,
					Visual_Line {
						spans = curr_line_spans[:],
						width = curr_w,
						ascent = curr_line_ascent,
						descent = curr_line_descent,
					},
				)
				curr_line_spans = make([dynamic]Shaped_Span, 0, 8)
				curr_w = 0
				curr_line_ascent = 0
				curr_line_descent = 0
				broken = true

				if max_lines > 0 && i32(len(lines)) >= max_lines {
					// We've reached the line limit.
					// If requested, inject an ellipsis into the last line by trimming glyphs until it fits.
					if use_ellipsis && len(lines) > 0 && max_width > 0 {
						li := len(lines) - 1
						l := &lines[li]

						// Choose font and direction from the last non-empty span in the line (fallbacks if needed)
						ell_font: ^Font
						dir := Direction.LTR
						last_cluster: i32 = 0

						// Scan from end for a non-empty span
						for si := len(l.spans) - 1; si >= 0; si -= 1 {
							sp := l.spans[si]
							if len(sp.glyphs) > 0 {
								ell_font = sp.font
								dir = sp.dir
								last_cluster = sp.glyphs[len(sp.glyphs) - 1].cluster
								break
							}
						}
						if ell_font == nil {
							// Try to fall back to the original input spans (if any)
							if len(spans) > 0 {
								ell_font = spans[0].font
								dir = spans[0].dir
							}
						}

						ellipsis_g := shape_ellipsis_glyphs(ell_font, dir)
						// Measure ellipsis width
						ellipsis_w: f32 = 0
						for eg in ellipsis_g do ellipsis_w += eg.advance_x

						// Ensure positive width contribution for naive RTL negative advances
						if ellipsis_w < 0 do ellipsis_w = -ellipsis_w

						// Trim from tail for LTR, from head for RTL
						space_needed := ellipsis_w
						if l.width + space_needed > max_width {
							if dir == .RTL {
								// Trim from line start
								for len(l.spans) > 0 && l.width + space_needed > max_width {
									sp := &l.spans[0]
									for len(sp.glyphs) > 0 && l.width + space_needed > max_width {
										g0 := sp.glyphs[0]
										adv := g0.advance_x
										if adv < 0 do adv = -adv
										// remove first glyph
										sp.glyphs = sp.glyphs[1:]
										l.width -= adv
										// Update span start to the new first cluster if any
										if len(sp.glyphs) > 0 {
											sp.start = sp.glyphs[0].cluster
										}
									}
									// Drop empty span
									if len(sp.glyphs) == 0 {
										// remove first span by slicing
										l.spans = l.spans[1:]
									} else {
										break
									}
								}
							} else {
								// LTR: Trim from line end
								for len(l.spans) > 0 && l.width + space_needed > max_width {
									sp := &l.spans[len(l.spans) - 1]
									for len(sp.glyphs) > 0 && l.width + space_needed > max_width {
										gl := sp.glyphs[len(sp.glyphs) - 1]
										adv := gl.advance_x
										if adv < 0 do adv = -adv
										// remove last glyph
										sp.glyphs = sp.glyphs[:len(sp.glyphs) - 1]
										l.width -= adv
										// Update span end to the new last cluster if any
										if len(sp.glyphs) > 0 {
											sp.end = sp.glyphs[len(sp.glyphs) - 1].cluster
										}
									}
									// Drop empty span
									if len(sp.glyphs) == 0 {
										// remove last span by slicing
										l.spans = l.spans[:len(l.spans) - 1]
									} else {
										break
									}
								}
							}
						}

						// Recompute last_cluster after trimming
						last_cluster = 0
						if len(l.spans) > 0 {
							ls := l.spans[len(l.spans) - 1]
							if len(ls.glyphs) > 0 {
								last_cluster = ls.glyphs[len(ls.glyphs) - 1].cluster
							} else {
								last_cluster = ls.end
							}
						} else {
							// If line became empty, map ellipsis to end of text
							last_cluster = i32(len(text))
						}

						// Build ellipsis span and normalize clusters to last_cluster for stable caret mapping
						e_span := Shaped_Span {
							font   = ell_font,
							dir    = dir,
							script = .Latin,
							start  = last_cluster,
							end    = last_cluster,
							glyphs = ellipsis_g,
						}
						for i := 0;
						    i < len(e_span.glyphs);
						    i += 1 {e_span.glyphs[i].cluster = last_cluster}

						if dir == .RTL {
							new_spans := make([dynamic]Shaped_Span, 0, len(l.spans) + 1)
							append(&new_spans, e_span)
							append(&new_spans, ..l.spans)
							l.spans = new_spans[:]
						} else {
							// l.spans is a slice, so create a dynamic array to append
							new_spans := make([dynamic]Shaped_Span, 0, len(l.spans) + 1)
							append(&new_spans, ..l.spans)
							append(&new_spans, e_span)
							l.spans = new_spans[:]
						}

						l.width += ellipsis_w
						if ell_font != nil {
							if ell_font.ascent > l.ascent do l.ascent = ell_font.ascent
							if ell_font.descent > l.descent do l.descent = ell_font.descent
						}
					}

					delete(pending_spans)
					return lines[:]
				}
				break
			}
			span_w += g.advance_x
		}

		if !broken {
			append(&curr_line_spans, s)
			if s.font != nil {
				if s.font.ascent > curr_line_ascent do curr_line_ascent = s.font.ascent
				if s.font.descent > curr_line_descent do curr_line_descent = s.font.descent
			}
			curr_w += span_w
		}
	}

	// Final line flush
	if len(curr_line_spans) > 0 {
		append(
			&lines,
			Visual_Line {
				spans = curr_line_spans[:],
				width = curr_w,
				ascent = curr_line_ascent,
				descent = curr_line_descent,
			},
		)
	}

	return lines[:]
}

// Compute vertical metrics and alignment offsets for a set of lines
align_text_layout :: proc(
	lines: []Visual_Line,
	max_width: f32,
	h_align: Horizontal_Align,
	v_align: Vertical_Align,
	line_height_mult: f32 = 1.0,
) -> (
	metrics: Text_Metrics,
	x_offsets: []f32,
	y_offsets: []f32,
) {
	// CRITICAL: Use temp allocator for per-frame temporary data
	context.allocator = context.temp_allocator
	metrics = Text_Metrics {
		line_count = i32(len(lines)),
	}

	x_offsets = make([]f32, len(lines))
	y_offsets = make([]f32, len(lines))

	if len(lines) == 0 do return

	// 1. Calculate line heights and max width
	total_h: f32 = 0
	max_ascent: f32 = 0
	max_descent: f32 = 0

	for i in 0 ..< len(lines) {
		l := &lines[i]
		if l.width > metrics.width do metrics.width = l.width

		// Use line-specific metrics if available, otherwise fallback
		line_ascent := l.ascent > 0 ? l.ascent : 16.0
		line_descent := l.descent > 0 ? l.descent : 4.0

		if line_ascent > max_ascent do max_ascent = line_ascent
		if line_descent > max_descent do max_descent = line_descent

		line_h := (line_ascent + line_descent) * line_height_mult
		y_offsets[i] = total_h + line_ascent // Store as baseline offset from block top
		total_h += line_h
	}

	metrics.height = total_h
	metrics.ascent = max_ascent
	metrics.descent = max_descent

	// 2. Vertical alignment adjustment
	v_shift: f32 = 0
	switch v_align {
	case .Top:
		v_shift = 0
	case .Middle:
		v_shift = -metrics.height * 0.5
	case .Bottom:
		v_shift = -metrics.height
	case .Baseline:
		v_shift = -y_offsets[0] // align first baseline to zero
	}

	for i in 0 ..< len(lines) {
		y_offsets[i] += v_shift
	}

	// 3. Horizontal alignment
	for i in 0 ..< len(lines) {
		l := &lines[i]
		switch h_align {
		case .Left:
			x_offsets[i] = 0
		case .Center:
			x_offsets[i] = (max_width - l.width) * 0.5
		case .Right:
			x_offsets[i] = max_width - l.width
		case .Justify:
			// TODO: Inter-word spacing adjustment
			x_offsets[i] = 0
		}
	}

	return
}

// Public API for measuring and laying out text without drawing.
// This is used by high-level UI controls to determine size before calling draw procedures.
measure_text_layout :: proc(
	text: string,
	max_width: f32,
	h_align: Horizontal_Align,
	v_align: Vertical_Align,
	line_height_mult: f32,
	shaped_spans: []Shaped_Span, // Pre-computed shaped spans
	max_lines: i32 = 0,
	use_ellipsis: bool = false,
) -> (
	metrics: Text_Metrics,
	lines: []Visual_Line,
	x_offsets: []f32,
	y_offsets: []f32,
) {
	lines = break_lines_greedy(text, shaped_spans, max_width, max_lines, use_ellipsis)
	metrics, x_offsets, y_offsets = align_text_layout(
		lines,
		max_width,
		h_align,
		v_align,
		line_height_mult,
	)
	return
}

// Find the character index (utf8 offset) at a given local (x, y) coordinate.
txt_coordinate_to_index :: proc(
	text: string,
	metrics: Text_Metrics,
	lines: []Visual_Line,
	x_offsets: []f32,
	y_offsets: []f32,
	target_x, target_y: f32,
) -> int {
	if len(lines) == 0 do return 0

	// 1. Find line
	line_idx := 0
	for i in 0 ..< len(lines) {
		// Lines are stored by baseline; check boundaries
		// Since y increases downwards, check if target_y is between line top and bottom
		line_top := y_offsets[i] - lines[i].ascent
		line_bottom := y_offsets[i] + lines[i].descent
		if target_y >= line_top && target_y <= line_bottom {
			line_idx = i
			break
		}
		if target_y < line_top {
			line_idx = i
			break
		}
		line_idx = i
	}

	l := &lines[line_idx]
	lx := x_offsets[line_idx]
	curr_x := lx

	// 2. Find glyph in line
	last_cluster: i32 = -1
	for s in l.spans {
		for g in s.glyphs {
			if target_x >= curr_x && target_x <= curr_x + g.advance_x {
				// Clicked this glyph. Check if it's closer to the start or end of the glyph for precise cursor placement.
				if target_x > curr_x + g.advance_x * 0.5 {
					// Closer to the end; move to next UTF-8 codepoint boundary
					_, next := utf8_decode_at(text, g.cluster)
					return int(next)
				}
				return int(g.cluster)
			}
			curr_x += g.advance_x
			last_cluster = g.cluster
		}
	}

	// Fallback to end of line or start of line
	if target_x < lx do return int(l.spans[0].start)
	if last_cluster >= 0 {
		_, next := utf8_decode_at(text, last_cluster)
		return int(next)
	}
	return int(l.spans[0].start)
}

// Find the local (x, y) coordinate for a given character index.
txt_index_to_coordinate :: proc(
	metrics: Text_Metrics,
	lines: []Visual_Line,
	x_offsets: []f32,
	y_offsets: []f32,
	index: int,
) -> (
	f32,
	f32,
) {
	for i in 0 ..< len(lines) {
		l := &lines[i]
		lx := x_offsets[i]
		curr_x := lx

		for s in l.spans {
			// Check if index is within this span's range
			if index >= int(s.start) && index <= int(s.end) {
				for g in s.glyphs {
					if int(g.cluster) == index {
						return curr_x, y_offsets[i]
					}
					curr_x += g.advance_x
				}
			} else {
				// Skip span but accumulate width
				for g in s.glyphs do curr_x += g.advance_x
			}
		}
	}

	return 0, 0
}

// Build selection background quads for a logical UTF-8 byte range [sel_start, sel_end)
// Returns rectangles in local coordinates relative to the provided layout origin.
build_selection_quads :: proc(
    text: string,
    lines: []Visual_Line,
    x_offsets: []f32,
    y_offsets: []f32,
    sel_start: int,
    sel_end: int,
) -> []Selection_Quad {
    // Use temp allocator for per-frame allocations
    context.allocator = context.temp_allocator
    out := make([dynamic]Selection_Quad, 0, 8)

    if len(lines) == 0 do return out[:]
    s0 := i32(sel_start)
    s1 := i32(sel_end)
    if s1 < s0 { s0, s1 = s1, s0 }

    for i in 0 ..< len(lines) {
        l := &lines[i]
        if len(l.spans) == 0 do continue

        base_y := y_offsets[i]
        line_top := base_y - l.ascent
        line_h := l.ascent + l.descent
        lx := x_offsets[i]
        curr_x := lx

        in_run := false
        run_x0: f32 = 0
        run_x1: f32 = 0

        // Iterate glyphs in visual order within the line
        for si in 0 ..< len(l.spans) {
            sp := l.spans[si]
            for gi in 0 ..< len(sp.glyphs) {
                g := sp.glyphs[gi]
                // Compute left/right taking into account potential negative advances
                left := curr_x
                right := curr_x + g.advance_x
                if right < left {
                    t := left; left = right; right = t
                }

                // Select glyphs whose cluster falls within [s0, s1)
                if g.cluster >= s0 && g.cluster < s1 {
                    if !in_run {
                        in_run = true
                        run_x0 = left
                        run_x1 = right
                    } else {
                        // Extend run
                        if right > run_x1 do run_x1 = right
                    }
                } else {
                    if in_run {
                        // Flush current run
                        if run_x1 > run_x0 {
                            append(&out, Selection_Quad{x = run_x0, y = line_top, w = run_x1 - run_x0, h = line_h, line_index = i32(i)})
                        }
                        in_run = false
                    }
                }

                curr_x += g.advance_x
            }
        }

        // Flush any trailing run
        if in_run && run_x1 > run_x0 {
            append(&out, Selection_Quad{x = run_x0, y = line_top, w = run_x1 - run_x0, h = line_h, line_index = i32(i)})
        }
    }

    return out[:]
}

import "base:runtime"
import c "core:c"
import fmt "core:fmt"
import "core:mem"
import ft "vendor-local:odin-freetype"
import hb "vendor-local:odin-harfbuzz/harfbuzz"

// Optional shaping caches (disabled by default to avoid per-frame string allocs)
SHAPER_CACHE :: #config(SHAPER_CACHE, false)
LOG_SHAPER_MEM :: #config(LOG_SHAPER_MEM, false)

@(private)
persistent_hb_buf: ^hb.buffer_t

@(private)
get_recycled_hb_buffer :: proc() -> ^hb.buffer_t {
	if persistent_hb_buf == nil {
		persistent_hb_buf = hb.buffer_create()
	} else {
		hb.buffer_reset(persistent_hb_buf)
	}
	return persistent_hb_buf
}

shaper_shutdown :: proc() {
	if persistent_hb_buf != nil {
		hb.buffer_destroy(persistent_hb_buf)
		persistent_hb_buf = nil
	}
}

when #config(SHAPER_CACHE, false) {
	@(private)
	shaped_basic_cache: map[string][]Shaped_Glyph
	@(private)
	shaped_adv_cache: map[string][]Shaped_Span

	@(private)
	shaper_init_if_needed :: proc() {
		if shaped_basic_cache ==
		   nil {shaped_basic_cache = make(map[string][]Shaped_Glyph, runtime.default_allocator())}
		if shaped_adv_cache ==
		   nil {shaped_adv_cache = make(map[string][]Shaped_Span, runtime.default_allocator())}
	}

	@(private)
	basic_key :: proc(font: ^Font, text: string) -> string {
		return fmt.tprintf("%p|%u|%s", font, get_font_size(font), text)
	}

	@(private)
	adv_key :: proc(primary: ^Font, fallbacks: []^Font, text: string) -> string {
		return fmt.tprintf("p=%p|ps=%u|%s", primary, get_font_size(primary), text)
	}
}

LOG_VERBOSE_SHAPING :: #config(LOG_VERBOSE_SHAPING, false)
ALLOW_HB_DOWNGRADE_LATIN :: #config(ALLOW_HB_DOWNGRADE_LATIN, false)
LOG_HB_GLYPHS :: #config(LOG_HB_GLYPHS, false)
LOG_HB_WARN_NAIVE :: #config(LOG_HB_WARN_NAIVE, false)
FORCE_HEBREW_LTR :: #config(FORCE_HEBREW_LTR, false)
SHAPER_TRIM_RUN_SPACES :: #config(SHAPER_TRIM_RUN_SPACES, false)

// M1 basic shaper: Latin LTR shaping with default features.
shape_text_basic :: proc(font: ^Font, text: string) -> []Shaped_Glyph {
	context.allocator = context.temp_allocator
	shaped := make([dynamic]Shaped_Glyph, 0, len(text))
	if font == nil || len(text) == 0 {
		return shaped[:]
	}
	when #config(SHAPER_CACHE, false) {
		shaper_init_if_needed()
		k := basic_key(font, text)
		if cached, ok := shaped_basic_cache[k]; ok {
			return cached[:]
		}
	}
	if font.hb_font == nil {
		out := naive_shape_substring(font, .LTR, text)
		when #config(SHAPER_CACHE, false) {
			shaper_init_if_needed()
			k := basic_key(font, text)
			shaped_basic_cache[k] = out
		}
		return out[:]
	}

	buf := get_recycled_hb_buffer()
	hb.buffer_set_direction(buf, .DIRECTION_LTR)
	hb.buffer_set_script(buf, .SCRIPT_LATIN)
	hb.buffer_set_language(buf, hb.language_from_string("en", -1))
	hb.buffer_set_cluster_level(buf, .BUFFER_CLUSTER_LEVEL_MONOTONE_GRAPHEMES)

	raw: [^]u8 = raw_data(text)
	text_len := c.int(len(text))
	hb.buffer_add_utf8(buf, raw, text_len, 0, text_len)
	hb.shape(font.hb_font, buf, nil, 0)

	glyph_count: c.uint
	infos: [^]hb.glyph_info_t = hb.buffer_get_glyph_infos(buf, &glyph_count)
	poss: [^]hb.glyph_position_t = hb.buffer_get_glyph_positions(buf, &glyph_count)

	inv64 := 1.0 / 64.0
	for i in 0 ..< int(glyph_count) {
		info := infos[i]
		pos := poss[i]
		append(
			&shaped,
			Shaped_Glyph {
				id = u32(info.codepoint),
				advance_x = f32(pos.x_advance) * f32(inv64),
				advance_y = f32(pos.y_advance) * f32(inv64),
				offset_x = f32(pos.x_offset) * f32(inv64),
				offset_y = f32(pos.y_offset) * f32(inv64),
				cluster = i32(info.cluster),
			},
		)
	}

	// Recycled buffer; no individual destroy needed
	when #config(SHAPER_CACHE, false) {
		k := basic_key(font, text)
		shaped_basic_cache[k] = shaped[:]
	}
	return shaped[:]
}

// Naive per-codepoint shaping as a last-resort fallback.
// DECUPLED: Does not call ensure_glyph_in_atlas; gets metrics from FT directly.
naive_shape_substring :: proc(font: ^Font, dir: Direction, text: string) -> []Shaped_Glyph {
	context.allocator = context.temp_allocator
	out := make([dynamic]Shaped_Glyph, 0, len(text))
	if font == nil || font.file == nil || font.file.face == nil || len(text) == 0 {
		return out[:]
	}
	ft.set_pixel_sizes(font.file.face, 0, font.size_px)

	i := i32(0)
	cluster := i32(0)
	for i < i32(len(text)) {
		b0 := text[i]
		cp: rune; w: i32
		if (b0 & 0x80) == 0 {
			cp, w = rune(b0), 1
		} else if (b0 & 0xE0) == 0xC0 && i + 1 < i32(len(text)) {
			cp = (rune(b0 & 0x1F) << 6) | rune(text[i + 1] & 0x3F); w = 2
		} else if (b0 & 0xF0) == 0xE0 && i + 2 < i32(len(text)) {
			cp =
				(rune(b0 & 0x0F) << 12) |
				(rune(text[i + 1] & 0x3F) << 6) |
				rune(text[i + 2] & 0x3F); w = 3
		} else if (b0 & 0xF8) == 0xF0 && i + 3 < i32(len(text)) {
			cp =
				(rune(b0 & 0x07) << 18) |
				(rune(text[i + 1] & 0x3F) << 12) |
				(rune(text[i + 2] & 0x3F) << 6) |
				rune(text[i + 3] & 0x3F); w = 4
		} else {
			cp, w = rune(b0), 1
		}

		gid := u32(ft.get_char_index(font.file.face, c.ulong(cp)))
		adv: f32 = 0
		if gid != 0 {
			// Get metrics directly from FreeType to avoid circular package dependency
			if ft.load_glyph(font.file.face, gid, {}) == .Ok {
				adv = f32(font.file.face.glyph.advance.x) / 64.0
			}
		}
		if dir == .RTL {
			adv = -adv
		}

		append(
			&out,
			Shaped_Glyph {
				id = gid,
				advance_x = adv,
				advance_y = 0,
				offset_x = 0,
				offset_y = 0,
				cluster = cluster,
			},
		)

		i += w
		cluster += 1
	}
	return out[:]
}

detect_script_kind :: proc(text: string) -> (hb.script_t, hb.language_t, Script_Kind) {
	script: hb.script_t = .SCRIPT_LATIN
	lang := hb.language_from_string(fmt.ctprint("en"), -1)
	kind: Script_Kind = .Latin

	i := i32(0)
	max_scan := i32(32)
	for i < i32(len(text)) && i < max_scan {
		b0 := text[i]
		cp: rune; w: i32
		if (b0 & 0x80) == 0 {
			cp, w = rune(b0), 1
		} else if (b0 & 0xE0) == 0xC0 && i + 1 < i32(len(text)) {
			b1 := text[i + 1]
			cp = (rune(b0 & 0x1F) << 6) | rune(b1 & 0x3F); w = 2
		} else if (b0 & 0xF0) == 0xE0 && i + 2 < i32(len(text)) {
			b1 := text[i + 1]; b2 := text[i + 2]
			cp = (rune(b0 & 0x0F) << 12) | (rune(b1 & 0x3F) << 6) | rune(b2 & 0x3F); w = 3
		} else if (b0 & 0xF8) == 0xF0 && i + 3 < i32(len(text)) {
			b1 := text[i + 1]; b2 := text[i + 2]; b3 := text[i + 3]
			cp =
				(rune(b0 & 0x07) << 18) |
				(rune(b1 & 0x3F) << 12) |
				(rune(b2 & 0x3F) << 6) |
				rune(b3 & 0x3F); w = 4
		} else {
			cp, w = rune(b0), 1
		}

		if (cp >= 0x0600 && cp <= 0x06FF) ||
		   (cp >= 0x0750 && cp <= 0x077F) ||
		   (cp >= 0x08A0 && cp <= 0x08FF) {
			script = .SCRIPT_ARABIC
			lang = hb.language_from_string(fmt.ctprint("ar"), -1)
			kind = .Arabic
			break
		}
		if (cp >= 0x0590 && cp <= 0x05FF) {
			script = .SCRIPT_HEBREW
			lang = hb.language_from_string(fmt.ctprint("he"), -1)
			kind = .Hebrew
			break
		}
		i += w
	}
	return script, lang, kind
}

@(private)
trim_span_spaces :: proc(text: string, start, end: i32) -> (i32, i32) {
	s, e := start, end
	for s < e && text[s] == ' ' do s += 1
	for e > s && text[e - 1] == ' ' do e -= 1
	return s, e
}

shape_substring :: proc(font: ^Font, dir: Direction, text: string) -> []Shaped_Glyph {
	context.allocator = context.temp_allocator
	shaped := make([dynamic]Shaped_Glyph, 0, len(text))
	if font == nil || len(text) == 0 do return shaped[:]

	if font.hb_font == nil {
		return naive_shape_substring(font, dir, text)
	}

	buf := get_recycled_hb_buffer()
	if dir == .RTL do hb.buffer_set_direction(buf, .DIRECTION_RTL)
	else do hb.buffer_set_direction(buf, .DIRECTION_LTR)

	script, lang, _ := detect_script_kind(text)
	hb.buffer_set_script(buf, script)
	hb.buffer_set_language(buf, lang)
	hb.buffer_set_cluster_level(buf, .BUFFER_CLUSTER_LEVEL_MONOTONE_GRAPHEMES)

	raw := raw_data(text)
	text_len := c.int(len(text))
	hb.buffer_add_utf8(buf, raw, text_len, 0, text_len)
	hb.shape(font.hb_font, buf, nil, 0)

	glyph_count: c.uint
	infos: [^]hb.glyph_info_t = hb.buffer_get_glyph_infos(buf, &glyph_count)
	poss: [^]hb.glyph_position_t = hb.buffer_get_glyph_positions(buf, &glyph_count)

	inv64 := 1.0 / 64.0
	for k in 0 ..< int(glyph_count) {
		info, pos := infos[k], poss[k]
		append(
			&shaped,
			Shaped_Glyph {
				id = u32(info.codepoint),
				advance_x = f32(pos.x_advance) * f32(inv64),
				advance_y = f32(pos.y_advance) * f32(inv64),
				offset_x = f32(pos.x_offset) * f32(inv64),
				offset_y = f32(pos.y_offset) * f32(inv64),
				cluster = i32(info.cluster),
			},
		)
	}
	// Recycled buffer; no individual destroy needed
	return shaped[:]
}

// Shape an ellipsis with fallback to three ASCII dots if the ellipsis glyph is missing.
shape_ellipsis_glyphs :: proc(font: ^Font, dir: Direction) -> []Shaped_Glyph {
	context.allocator = context.temp_allocator
	out := make([dynamic]Shaped_Glyph, 0, 3)
	if font == nil {
		return out[:]
	}
	// Try single Unicode ellipsis first
	g := shape_substring(font, dir, "…")
	missing := 0
	for gg in g do if gg.id == 0 do missing += 1
	if len(g) == 0 || missing > 0 {
		g2 := shape_substring(font, dir, "...")
		miss2 := 0
		for gg in g2 do if gg.id == 0 do miss2 += 1
		if len(g2) > 0 && (len(g) == 0 || miss2 <= missing) {
			g = g2
		}
	}
	return g[:]
}

// Shape full text using bidi runs and font fallback itemization
shape_text_itemized :: proc(
	primary: ^Font,
	fallbacks: []^Font,
	default_fallbacks: []^Font,
	text: string,
) -> []Shaped_Span {
	// CRITICAL: Use temp allocator for per-frame temporary data
	context.allocator = context.temp_allocator
	spans := make([dynamic]Shaped_Span, 0, 8)
	if primary == nil || len(text) == 0 {
		return spans[:]
	}
	// Optional cache (disabled by default)
	when #config(SHAPER_CACHE, false) {
		shaper_init_if_needed()
		k := adv_key(primary, fallbacks, text)
		if cached, ok := shaped_adv_cache[k]; ok {
			return cached[:]
		}
	}

	runs := fribidi_analyze_paragraph(text)
	for r in runs {
		subs := itemize_by_font(text, r, primary, fallbacks, default_fallbacks)
		if r.direction == .RTL {
			for i := 0; i < len(subs) / 2; i += 1 {
				j := len(subs) - 1 - i
				subs[i], subs[j] = subs[j], subs[i]
			}
		}
		for sub in subs {
			sstart, send := sub.start, sub.end
			when #config(SHAPER_TRIM_RUN_SPACES, false) {
				sstart, send = trim_span_spaces(text, sstart, send)
			}
			substr := text[sstart:send]
			if len(substr) == 0 do continue
			_, _, sk := detect_script_kind(substr)
			eff_dir := sub.dir
			if FORCE_HEBREW_LTR && sk == .Hebrew do eff_dir = .LTR

			use_font := sub.font
			glyphs := shape_substring(use_font, eff_dir, substr)

			if len(glyphs) == 0 do glyphs = naive_shape_substring(use_font, eff_dir, substr)

			// Fallback logic
			if len(glyphs) > 0 {
				missing := 0
				for g in glyphs do if g.id == 0 do missing += 1
				if missing > 0 {
					best_g, best_missing, best_font := glyphs, missing, use_font
					for f in fallbacks {
						if f == use_font do continue
						g2 := shape_substring(f, sub.dir, substr)
						miss2 := 0
						for g in g2 do if g.id == 0 do miss2 += 1
						if len(g2) > 0 && miss2 < best_missing {
							best_g, best_missing, best_font = g2, miss2, f
							if best_missing == 0 do break
						}
					}
					if best_missing < missing {
						glyphs, use_font = best_g, best_font
					}
				}
			}

			append(
				&spans,
				Shaped_Span {
					font = use_font,
					dir = eff_dir,
					script = sk,
					start = sstart,
					end = send,
					glyphs = glyphs,
				},
			)
		}
	}
	when #config(SHAPER_CACHE, false) {
		k := adv_key(primary, fallbacks, text)
		shaped_adv_cache[k] = spans[:]
	}
	return spans[:]
}

/**
@documentation: RSD/Format
- Binary container specification for RuiShin Drawing (RSD).
- RIFF-inspired chunked layout for forward/backward compatibility.
- Header defines magic, version, and endianness.
*/

MAGIC :: "RSD0"
VERSION_V1 :: 0x0001

File_Header :: struct #packed {
	magic:    [4]u8, // "RSD0"
	version:  u16, // 0x0001
	flags:    u16, // bit0: little-endian=1
	reserved: u32,
}

File_Flags :: enum u16 {
	Little_Endian = 1 << 0,
}

Chunk_Header :: struct #packed {
	id:     [4]u8, // 4B ASCII (e.g., "HEAD", "LAYR")
	length: u32, // Payload size in bytes
	crc:    u32, // CRC32 of payload (optional, 0 if not used)
}

// Chunk IDs (4B ASCII)
CHUNK_ID_HEAD :: [4]u8{'H', 'E', 'A', 'D'}
CHUNK_ID_CANV :: [4]u8{'C', 'A', 'N', 'V'}
CHUNK_ID_LAYR :: [4]u8{'L', 'A', 'Y', 'R'}
CHUNK_ID_GRUP :: [4]u8{'G', 'R', 'U', 'P'}
CHUNK_ID_PATH :: [4]u8{'P', 'A', 'T', 'H'}
CHUNK_ID_STYL :: [4]u8{'S', 'T', 'Y', 'L'}
CHUNK_ID_GRAD :: [4]u8{'G', 'R', 'A', 'D'}
CHUNK_ID_THGR :: [4]u8{'T', 'H', 'G', 'R'} // Phase 10C2.2: Theme Gradient Reference
CHUNK_ID_TEXT :: [4]u8{'T', 'E', 'X', 'T'}
CHUNK_ID_IMAG :: [4]u8{'I', 'M', 'A', 'G'}
CHUNK_ID_MATR :: [4]u8{'M', 'A', 'T', 'R'}
CHUNK_ID_CDEF :: [4]u8{'C', 'D', 'E', 'F'} // Clip Definition
CHUNK_ID_MDEF :: [4]u8{'M', 'D', 'E', 'F'} // Mask Definition
CHUNK_ID_CLPR :: [4]u8{'C', 'L', 'P', 'R'} // Clip Reference
CHUNK_ID_MSKR :: [4]u8{'M', 'S', 'K', 'R'} // Mask Reference
CHUNK_ID_META :: [4]u8{'M', 'E', 'T', 'A'}

// --- Fill Kind Enum (Phase 10C2) ---
Fill_Kind :: enum u8 {
	None      = 0, // No fill
	Solid     = 1, // Solid color
	Gradient  = 2, // Gradient reference
	Theme_Ref = 3, // Theme color reference (variable-length encoding)
}

// --- Binary Chunk Schemas (Strict spec alignment) ---

// HEAD chunk (required, follows header)
Chunk_Head :: struct #packed {
	version:    u16,
	flags:      u16,
	units:      u8, // 0=px
	_pad:       u8,
	dpi:        f32,
	width:      f32,
	height:     f32,
	background: [4]f32, // Added for Phase 11.3 persistence
	view_box:   [4]f32, // Added for Phase 11.3 persistence
}

// LAYR chunk (header)
Chunk_Layr :: struct #packed {
	name_len: u16,
	visible:  u8,
	_pad:     u8,
	opacity:  f32,
}

// PATH chunk (header)
Chunk_Path :: struct #packed {
	fill_rule: u8,
	_pad:      [3]u8,
	n_cmd:     u32,
	n_pt:      u32,
}

// STYL chunk (applied to paths)
// For fill_kind 0-2: fixed 32-byte structure
// For fill_kind 3 (Theme_Ref): variable-length following this header
Chunk_Styl :: struct #packed {
	stroke_rgba:  [4]u8,
	stroke_width: f32,
	join:         u8,
	cap:          u8,
	dash_count:   u8,
	_pad:         u8,
	miter_limit:  f32,
	dash_offset:  f32,
	fill_kind:    u8, // 0=None, 1=Solid, 2=GradRef, 3=Theme_Ref
	_pad2:        [3]u8,
	fill_rgba:    [4]u8, // Used for fill_kind 1 (Solid)
	fill_grad_id: u32, // Used for fill_kind 2 (Gradient Ref)
}

// GRAD chunk (gradient definition - inline gradient)
// Phase 10D.3.1: Extended for multi-stop gradients with transforms
Chunk_Grad :: struct #packed {
	id:            u32,
	type:          u8, // 0=Linear, 1=Radial
	units:         u8, // 0=UserSpace, 1=ObjectBoundingBox
	stops_count:   u16,
	p0:            [2]f32, // For linear: start point, for radial: center
	p1:            [2]f32, // For linear: end point, for radial: radius vector
	fx:            f32, // For radial: focal point x (0 if unused)
	fy:            f32, // For radial: focal point y (0 if unused)
	has_transform: u8, // 0=No transform, 1=Has transform matrix
	_pad:          [3]u8, // Padding for alignment
}
// If has_transform=1, followed by 9 f32 values (3x3 matrix in row-major order)
// Followed by stops_count entries of (t:f32, color:[4]u8)

// THGR chunk (Phase 10C2.2: Theme Gradient Reference)
// References a theme gradient preset by token path with inline fallback
Chunk_Thgr :: struct #packed {
	token_len:   u16, // Length of theme token string
	fallback_id: u32, // Fallback gradient ID if theme lookup fails
	_pad:        [2]u8, // Padding for alignment
}
// Followed by: token_len bytes (UTF-8 theme token string)

// TEXT chunk
Chunk_Text :: struct #packed {
	font_ref:    u32,
	size:        f32,
	color_rgba:  [4]u8,
	anchor:      u8,
	_pad:        [3]u8,
	line_height: f32,
	text_len:    u32,
}

// IMAG chunk
Chunk_Imag :: struct #packed {
	kind:     u8, // 0=external, 1=embedded
	fmt:      u8, // reserved/fmt
	_pad:     u16,
	w, h:     u32,
	data_len: u32,
}

// MATR chunk (3x3 matrix)
Chunk_Matr :: struct #packed {
	m: [3][3]f32,
}

// META chunk (key/value)
Chunk_Meta :: struct #packed {
	key_len:   u16,
	value_len: u16,
}

// Phase 12: Clip and Mask Chunks
Chunk_Clip_Ref :: struct #packed {
	id: u32,
}

Chunk_Mask_Ref :: struct #packed {
	id: u32,
}

Chunk_Cdef :: struct #packed {
	id: u32,
}

Chunk_Mdef :: struct #packed {
	id: u32,
}

// Phase 10D.3.2: Transform Stack Chunk
CHUNK_ID_TSTK :: [4]u8{'T', 'S', 'T', 'K'} // Transform Stack

Chunk_Tstk :: struct #packed {
	transform_count: u16,
	current_index:   u16,
}
// Followed by transform_count entries of Chunk_Matr

import "core:fmt"
import "core:mem"
import "core:time"
import assets "project:assets"
import logging "project:logging"
import rsd "project:graphics"

// GPU-specific Asset Type enumeration
// This matches assets.Asset_Type but is defined in graphics package for type safety
GPU_Asset_Type :: enum {
	SVG, // SVG vector graphics
	RSD, // RuiShin Drawing format
	Image, // Image assets (for future unification)
	Font, // Font assets (for future unification)
}

// GPU-specific Asset State enumeration
// This matches assets.Asset_State but is defined in graphics package for type safety
GPU_Asset_State :: enum {
	Raw, // Asset data loaded but not parsed
	Parsed, // Asset successfully parsed and ready for use
	Parsing, // Asset is currently being parsed (background operation)
	Error, // Asset failed to load or parse
}

// GPU-specific Render Handle
// Represents parsed, ready-to-render asset data with GPU-specific fields
// This is the graphics package's internal version with direct pointers to parsed data
GPU_RenderHandle :: struct {
	id:          u32, // Unique identifier for the asset
	asset_type:  GPU_Asset_Type, // Type of asset (SVG, RSD, etc.)
	timestamp:   i64, // Parsing timestamp for debugging

	// SVG-specific parsed data (mutually exclusive with RSD)
	svg_element: ^SVG_Element,

	// RSD-specific parsed data (mutually exclusive with SVG)
	rsd_drawing: ^Drawing,

	// Reference counting for memory management
	ref_count:   int,

	// Statistics and debugging
	parse_time:  f64, // Time taken to parse (seconds)
}

// GPU-specific Loading Handle
// This matches the assets.LoadingHandle but is defined here for type safety
GPU_LoadingHandle :: struct {
	id:         u32, // Unique identifier for the asset
	data:       []u8, // Raw asset data (not parsed)
	path:       string, // Source path for reference
	asset_type: GPU_Asset_Type, // Type of asset (SVG, RSD, etc.)
	timestamp:  i64, // Loading timestamp for debugging
}

// Asset Parse Result union
// Contains the result of parsing an asset, specific to the asset type
// Use the canonical Asset_Parse_Result from assets package
// SVG_Element_Ptr :: ^SVG_Element
// Drawing_Ptr :: ^Drawing  
// Error_String :: string

// Asset Handle structure (Legacy - to be deprecated)
// Represents a handle to a managed asset
// NOTE: This will be replaced by the new LoadingHandle/RenderHandle pattern
Asset_Handle :: struct {
	id:    u32, // Unique identifier for the asset
	valid: bool, // Indicates if this handle is valid
}

// GPU-specific Asset Render Configuration
// This matches assets.Asset_Render_Config but is defined here for type safety
GPU_Asset_Render_Config :: struct {
	x:                   f32, // X position for rendering
	y:                   f32, // Y position for rendering
	width:               f32, // Width for rendering
	height:              f32, // Height for rendering
	allow_sync_fallback: bool, // Allow synchronous fallback if asset not loaded
	tint_color:          [4]f32, // Optional tint color (RGBA)
	opacity:             f32, // Rendering opacity (0.0 to 1.0)
	preserve_aspect:     bool, // Preserve aspect ratio during scaling
}

// Default Asset Render Configuration
defaultAssetRenderConfig :: proc() -> GPU_Asset_Render_Config {
	return GPU_Asset_Render_Config {
		x                   = 0.0,
		y                   = 0.0,
		width               = 1.0,
		height              = 1.0,
		allow_sync_fallback = true,
		tint_color          = {1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0}, // White, no tint
		opacity             = 1.0,
		preserve_aspect     = true,
	}
}

// Asset Type to String conversion
// Converts an Asset_Type enum value to its string representation
assetTypeToString :: proc(asset_type: GPU_Asset_Type) -> string {
	switch asset_type {
	case .SVG:
		return "SVG"
	case .RSD:
		return "RSD"
	case .Image:
		return "Image"
	case .Font:
		return "Font"
	}
	return "Unknown"
}

// Asset State to String conversion
// Converts an Asset_State enum value to its string representation
assetStateToString :: proc(state: GPU_Asset_State) -> string {
	switch state {
	case .Raw:
		return "Raw"
	case .Parsed:
		return "Parsed"
	case .Parsing:
		return "Parsing"
	case .Error:
		return "Error"
	}
	return "Unknown"
}

// Log Asset State
// Logs the current state of an asset using the centralized logging system
logAssetState :: proc(handle: Asset_Handle, state: GPU_Asset_State, message: string) {
	logging.debug(
		.Asset,
		fmt.tprintf("[ASSET_%d] State: %s - %s", handle.id, assetStateToString(state), message),
	)
}

// Log Asset Error
// Logs an error related to an asset using the centralized logging system
logAssetError :: proc(handle: Asset_Handle, error_msg: string) {
	logging.error(.Asset, fmt.tprintf("[ASSET_%d] ERROR: %s", handle.id, error_msg))
}
