DEBUG: Source content: package graphics

import "base:runtime"
import fmt "core:fmt"
import "core:math"
import "core:math/linalg"
import rand "core:math/rand"
import logging "project:logging"
import renderer "project:renderer"
import platform "project:platform"
import sg "vendor-local:sokol-odin/sokol/gfx"

// ============================================================================
// EXPERIMENTAL / FUTURE FEATURES
// ============================================================================
// This file contains implemented systems that are not yet connected to the
// main rendering pipeline or called from production code. They compile and
// work correctly in isolation.

// Do not add production call sites to these procs without first planning
// the integration — particularly for the animation system, which needs
// per-frame update calls from the UI layer.

// Contents:
//   Animation  — Tween (scalar, vec2, vec4), Timeline sequencer, Easing, Spring physics
//   Gestures   — Tap, Long Press, Pan recognisers (caller-owned, no ctx access)
//   SDF        — CPU signed-distance evaluation for path hit testing
//   Materials  — G2D_Material, advanced sampler selection, Sprite animation
// ============================================================================

// ============================================================================
// ANIMATION CONTEXT — Gate 2.1 (caller-tick is the official pattern)
// ============================================================================
// Official pattern: CALLER-TICK.
//
// Each UI component or scene proc calls the update procs directly in its own
// per-frame render proc:
//
//   g2d_tween_f32_update(&my_tween, dt_ms)
//   g2d_spring_f32_update(&my_spring, dt_ms)
//   g2d_gesture_long_press_tick(&my_lp, dt_ms)  // long-press only; others are event-driven
//
// Rationale: caller-tick is simpler, avoids hidden global state, and gives
// each component full control over update order and lifetime. A central-tick
// model would require registering handles and managing their lifetimes with
// no meaningful benefit for the current use cases.
//
// g2d_anim_tick is retained as a no-op shim for forward compatibility.
// ============================================================================

@(private)
Anim_Context :: struct {} // No fields — caller-tick pattern requires none.

@(private)
anim_ctx: Anim_Context

// g2d_anim_tick is a no-op shim. Use caller-tick: call g2d_tween_f32_update,
// g2d_spring_f32_update, etc. directly in your per-frame render proc.
g2d_anim_tick :: proc(dt_ms: f32) {_ = dt_ms}
// end g2d_anim_tick

// ============================================================================
// GESTURE RECOGNITION SYSTEM
// ============================================================================
// Caller-owned state machines for common touch gestures.
// Designed to be embedded in UI components; no access to g2d ctx.
//
// CALLER-TICK PATTERN (current):
//   Per input event call:   g2d_gesture_tap_update / g2d_gesture_pan_update
//   Per frame call:         g2d_gesture_long_press_tick(&gs.long_press, dt_ms)
//   Example (in render proc):
//     tapped, pos := g2d_gesture_tap_update(&tile_tap, event)
//     g2d_gesture_long_press_tick(&tile_lp, dt_ms)
//     lp_fired, lp_pos := g2d_gesture_long_press_update(&tile_lp, event)
//
// All gestures follow the same lifecycle:
//   update() per input event -> recognize when conditions met
//
// Gestures are "exclusive": once a gesture recognizes, it consumes the input
// and other gestures on the same element should be reset.
// ============================================================================

// Tap gesture: recognize a short press/release with minimal movement.
Gesture_Tap :: struct {
	pressed:  bool,
	button:   platform.Mouse_Button,
	down_pos: [2]f32,
}

g2d_gesture_tap_update :: proc(
	gs: ^Gesture_Tap,
	ev: platform.Input_Event,
	max_move_px: f32 = 6.0,
) -> (
	bool,
	[2]f32,
) {
	recognized := false
	pos := [2]f32{0, 0}
	#partial switch ev.type {
	case .Mouse_Down, .Touch_Begin:
		gs.pressed = true
		gs.button = ev.button
		gs.down_pos = ev.pos
	case .Mouse_Move, .Touch_Move:
		if gs.pressed {
			dx := ev.pos.x - gs.down_pos.x
			dy := ev.pos.y - gs.down_pos.y
			if dx * dx + dy * dy > max_move_px * max_move_px {
				// Cancel tap if pointer traveled too far
				gs.pressed = false
			}
		}
	case .Mouse_Up, .Touch_End, .Touch_Cancel:
		if gs.pressed {
			if ev.type == .Mouse_Up && gs.button != ev.button {
				gs.pressed = false
				break
			}
			dx := ev.pos.x - gs.down_pos.x
			dy := ev.pos.y - gs.down_pos.y
			if dx * dx + dy * dy <= max_move_px * max_move_px {
				recognized = true
				pos = ev.pos
			}
		}
		gs.pressed = false
	}
	return recognized, pos
}
// end g2d_gesture_tap_update

// Long Press: fires once after a hold duration if movement stays within a radius.
Gesture_Long_Press :: struct {
	is_down:      bool,
	fired:        bool,
	consumed:     bool, // ensures single fire notification via update()
	button:       platform.Mouse_Button,
	down_pos:     [2]f32,
	curr_pos:     [2]f32,
	elapsed_ms:   f32,
	threshold_ms: f32,
	max_move_px:  f32,
}

// Update pointer/button state; returns (fired_now, pos) when first crossing threshold.
g2d_gesture_long_press_update :: proc(
	gs: ^Gesture_Long_Press,
	ev: platform.Input_Event,
	threshold_ms: f32 = 500.0,
	max_move_px: f32 = 6.0,
) -> (
	bool,
	[2]f32,
) {
	fired_now := false
	pos := [2]f32{0, 0}

	#partial switch ev.type {
	case .Mouse_Down, .Touch_Begin:
		gs.is_down = true
		gs.fired = false
		gs.consumed = false
		gs.elapsed_ms = 0
		gs.down_pos = ev.pos
		gs.curr_pos = ev.pos
		gs.button = ev.button
		gs.threshold_ms = threshold_ms
		gs.max_move_px = max_move_px
	case .Mouse_Move, .Touch_Move:
		if gs.is_down {
			gs.curr_pos = ev.pos
			dx := gs.curr_pos.x - gs.down_pos.x
			dy := gs.curr_pos.y - gs.down_pos.y
			if dx * dx + dy * dy > gs.max_move_px * gs.max_move_px {
				// Cancel due to excessive movement
				gs.is_down = false
				gs.fired = false
				gs.consumed = false
			}
		}
	case .Mouse_Up, .Touch_End, .Touch_Cancel:
		gs.is_down = false
	}

	if gs.fired && !gs.consumed {
		fired_now = true
		pos = gs.down_pos
		gs.consumed = true
	}
	return fired_now, pos
}
// end g2d_gesture_long_press_update

// Tick time forward while pressed. Caller supplies per-frame dt in milliseconds.
g2d_gesture_long_press_tick :: proc(gs: ^Gesture_Long_Press, dt_ms: f32) {
	if !gs.is_down || gs.fired {
		return
	}
	gs.elapsed_ms += dt_ms
	dx := gs.curr_pos.x - gs.down_pos.x
	dy := gs.curr_pos.y - gs.down_pos.y
	if gs.elapsed_ms >= gs.threshold_ms && (dx * dx + dy * dy) <= gs.max_move_px * gs.max_move_px {
		gs.fired = true
		gs.consumed = false // arm single-fire notification for update()
	}
}
// end g2d_gesture_long_press_tick

// Pan: begins when movement exceeds a small threshold; then emits deltas until end.
Gesture_Pan :: struct {
	is_down:   bool,
	active:    bool,
	button:    platform.Mouse_Button,
	start_pos: [2]f32,
	last_pos:  [2]f32,
}

// Returns (began, delta, ended). When began is true, delta is {0,0} for that frame.
g2d_gesture_pan_update :: proc(
	gs: ^Gesture_Pan,
	ev: platform.Input_Event,
	start_threshold_px: f32 = 3.0,
) -> (
	bool,
	[2]f32,
	bool,
) {
	began := false
	delta := [2]f32{0, 0}
	ended := false

	#partial switch ev.type {
	case .Mouse_Down, .Touch_Begin:
		gs.is_down = true
		gs.active = false
		gs.button = ev.button
		gs.start_pos = ev.pos
		gs.last_pos = ev.pos
	case .Mouse_Move, .Touch_Move:
		if gs.is_down {
			if !gs.active {
				dx0 := ev.pos.x - gs.start_pos.x
				dy0 := ev.pos.y - gs.start_pos.y
				if dx0 * dx0 + dy0 * dy0 >= start_threshold_px * start_threshold_px {
					gs.active = true
					began = true
					gs.last_pos = ev.pos
				}
			} else {
				delta = [2]f32{ev.pos.x - gs.last_pos.x, ev.pos.y - gs.last_pos.y}
				gs.last_pos = ev.pos
			}
		}
	case .Mouse_Up, .Touch_End, .Touch_Cancel:
		if gs.is_down {
			if gs.active {
				ended = true
			}
			gs.is_down = false
			gs.active = false
		}
	}
	return began, delta, ended
}
// end g2d_gesture_pan_update

// ============================================================================
// GESTURE_SET — UNIFIED MULTI-GESTURE ROUTING
// ============================================================================
// Bundles tap + long-press + pan for a single interactive region.
// Handles hit-testing and priority so callers don't have to.
//
// Priority / exclusion rules:
//   - Pan wins over tap once Gesture_Pan.active == true (tap is silently dropped).
//   - Long press fires once; if it fires before tap-up, tap is dropped on release.
//   - All three recognisers run in parallel; callers ignore unwanted return values.
//
// Usage:
//   gs: Gesture_Set  // zero-init is a valid starting state
//   // Per event (in event handler):
//   tapped, lp_fired, pan_began, pan_delta, pan_ended := g2d_gesture_set_update(&gs, ev, {x, y, w, h}, dt_ms)
// ============================================================================

Gesture_Set :: struct {
	tap:        Gesture_Tap,
	long_press: Gesture_Long_Press,
	pan:        Gesture_Pan,
}

// g2d_gesture_set_update routes an input event to all three recognisers, but
// only processes pointer-down/move events when the pointer is inside rect.
// Pointer-up and touch-end events are always processed (capture semantics).
// Long-press timer is ticked by dt_ms on every call (pass 0 from event handlers).
//
// Returns: (tapped, long_pressed, pan_began, pan_delta, pan_ended)
g2d_gesture_set_update :: proc(
	gs:    ^Gesture_Set,
	ev:    platform.Input_Event,
	rect:  [4]f32, // x, y, w, h in logical pixels (used if hit_poly is nil)
	dt_ms: f32 = 0, // supply frame dt for long-press tick
	hit_poly: [][2]f32 = nil, // strict hit testing polygon
) -> (tapped, long_pressed, pan_began: bool, pan_delta: [2]f32, pan_ended: bool) {
	if gs == nil do return

	// Tick long-press timer every call (harmless when dt_ms == 0)
	g2d_gesture_long_press_tick(&gs.long_press, dt_ms)

	// Decide whether to route pointer events into the inside rect/poly
	px, py := ev.pos.x, ev.pos.y
	inside: bool

	if len(hit_poly) >= 3 {
		inside = g2d_path_hit_test(hit_poly, px, py)
	} else {
		rx, ry, rw, rh := rect.x, rect.y, rect.z, rect.w
		inside = px >= rx && px < rx + rw && py >= ry && py < ry + rh
	}

	// Always process up/end events (capture: gesture started inside must finish)
	is_release :=
		ev.type == .Mouse_Up ||
		ev.type == .Touch_End ||
		ev.type == .Touch_Cancel

	should_process := inside || is_release

	if should_process {
		tapped, _ = g2d_gesture_tap_update(&gs.tap, ev)
		long_pressed, _ = g2d_gesture_long_press_update(&gs.long_press, ev)
		pan_began, pan_delta, pan_ended = g2d_gesture_pan_update(&gs.pan, ev)
	}

	// Pan wins: if pan is active and we got a tap-up on the same frame, drop the tap
	if gs.pan.active && tapped {
		tapped = false
	}

	// Long press wins: if long press fired, block tap on subsequent release
	if long_pressed && tapped {
		tapped = false
	}

	return
}
// end g2d_gesture_set_update

// g2d_gesture_set_reset resets all three recognisers to their zero state.
g2d_gesture_set_reset :: proc(gs: ^Gesture_Set) {
	if gs == nil do return
	gs.tap = {}
	gs.long_press = {}
	gs.pan = {}
}
// end g2d_gesture_set_reset

// ============================================================================
// MATERIAL AND SPRITE ANIMATION SYSTEM
// ============================================================================
// Abstraction for textured quads with tinting, and sprite sheet animation.
//
// Materials are lightweight handles that map to (image, tint) pairs.
// Sprites add frame-based animation on top of materials.
//

// TEMPORARY LAYER VIOLATION — sg.Image in public signature; use g2d_material_make_handle instead
// Resolution: Phase G.1 — remove after all callers migrated to Image_Handle
// See: 260309-RuiShin-2D-Completion-Roadmap3.md Phase G
@(private)
// Create a material from an image and tint. Caller may pass ctx.white_image for solids.
g2d_material_make :: proc(image: sg.Image, tint: [4]f32) -> renderer.G2D_Material {
	return renderer.G2D_Material{image = _sg_to_image_handle(image), tint = tint}
}
// end g2d_material_make
// This is the foundation for the future UI component system.
// ============================================================================

// Phase DX: Create a material from an Image_Handle and tint
g2d_material_make_handle :: proc(
	image: renderer.Image_Handle,
	tint: [4]f32,
) -> renderer.G2D_Material {
	return renderer.G2D_Material{image = image, tint = tint}
}

// Create a material from an image and tint. Caller may pass ctx.white_image for solids.
// NOTE: This version accepts sg.Image for compatibility during transition
// end g2d_material_make

// Convenience: solid-color material using the renderer's white texture.
g2d_material_make_solid :: proc(tint: [4]f32) -> renderer.G2D_Material {
	return renderer.G2D_Material{image = _sg_to_image_handle(ctx.white_image), tint = tint}
}
// end g2d_material_make_solid

// Material-aware image draw: maps full texture to dest rect using the material's tint.
g2d_draw_image_mat :: proc(x, y, w, h: f32, mat: renderer.G2D_Material) {
	old_image := ctx.curr_image
	ctx.curr_image = _image_handle_to_sg(mat.image)
	b := get_batch()

	p1 := apply_transform(x, y)
	p2 := apply_transform(x + w, y)
	p3 := apply_transform(x + w, y + h)
	p4 := apply_transform(x, y + h)

	c := mat.tint
	v1 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p1, {0, 0}, c}
	v2 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p2, {1, 0}, c}
	v3 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p3, {1, 1}, c}
	v4 := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p4, {0, 1}, c}

	add_vertices(b, v1, v2, v3, v1, v3, v4)
	ctx.curr_image = old_image
}
// end g2d_draw_image_mat

// Material-aware image sub-rect draw with pixel-space source rect.
g2d_draw_image_rect_mat :: proc(dest: [4]f32, src_px: [4]f32, mat: renderer.G2D_Material) {
	old_image := ctx.curr_image
	ctx.curr_image = _image_handle_to_sg(mat.image)
	b := get_batch()

	iw := f32(sg.query_image_width(_image_handle_to_sg(mat.image)))
	ih := f32(sg.query_image_height(_image_handle_to_sg(mat.image)))
	if iw <= 0 || ih <= 0 {
		// Guard: invalid image dims; nothing to draw
		ctx.curr_image = old_image
		return
	}

	u0, v0 := src_px.x / iw, src_px.y / ih
	u1, v1_uv := (src_px.x + src_px.z) / iw, (src_px.y + src_px.w) / ih

	p1 := apply_transform(dest.x, dest.y)
	p2 := apply_transform(dest.x + dest.z, dest.y)
	p3 := apply_transform(dest.x + dest.z, dest.y + dest.w)
	p4 := apply_transform(dest.x, dest.y + dest.w)

	c := mat.tint
	v_a := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p1, {u0, v0}, c}
	v_b := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p2, {u1, v0}, c}
	v_c := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p3, {u1, v1_uv}, c}
	v_d := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p4, {u0, v1_uv}, c}

	add_vertices(b, v_a, v_b, v_c, v_a, v_c, v_d)
	ctx.curr_image = old_image
}
// end g2d_draw_image_rect_mat

// Material-aware image draw with normalized UVs.
g2d_draw_image_rect_uv_mat :: proc(dest: [4]f32, uvs: [4]f32, mat: renderer.G2D_Material) {
	old_image := ctx.curr_image
	ctx.curr_image = _image_handle_to_sg(mat.image)
	b := get_batch()

	u0, v0 := uvs.x, uvs.y
	u1, v1_uv := uvs.z, uvs.w

	p1 := apply_transform(dest.x, dest.y)
	p2 := apply_transform(dest.x + dest.z, dest.y)
	p3 := apply_transform(dest.x + dest.z, dest.y + dest.w)
	p4 := apply_transform(dest.x, dest.y + dest.w)

	c := mat.tint
	v_a := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p1, {u0, v0}, c}
	v_b := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p2, {u1, v0}, c}
	v_c := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p3, {u1, v1_uv}, c}
	v_d := renderer.G2D_Vertex{p4, {u0, v1_uv}, c}

	add_vertices(b, v_a, v_b, v_c, v_a, v_c, v_d)
	ctx.curr_image = old_image
}
// end g2d_draw_image_rect_uv_mat

// Deferred version: pushes Cmd_Draw_Image_Rect_UV_Mat at .Content layer.
// Captures current sampler settings from ctx so they replay correctly.
@(private)
g2d_record_draw_image_rect_uv_mat :: proc(dest: [4]f32, uvs: [4]f32, mat: renderer.G2D_Material) {
	renderer.draw_list_push(
		&renderer.active_draw_list,
		renderer.Cmd_Draw_Image_Rect_UV_Mat{
			common = _g2d_make_common(.Content),
			mat    = mat,
			dest   = dest,
			uvs    = uvs,
			filter = ctx.curr_sampler_filter,
			repeat = ctx.curr_sampler_repeat,
			mipmap = ctx.curr_sampler_mipmap,
		},
	)
}

// Advanced texture sampling (tiling, clamp, mipmaps)
@(private)
_g2d_sampler_key :: proc(filter: G2D_Sampler_Filter, repeat: bool, mipmap: bool) -> u32 {
	f := u32(filter) & 3; rep := u32(repeat) & 1; mm := u32(mipmap) & 1
	return (f << 3) | (rep << 2) | (rep << 1) | mm
}

@(private)
_g2d_get_sampler :: proc(filter: G2D_Sampler_Filter, repeat: bool, mipmap: bool) -> sg.Sampler {
	key := _g2d_sampler_key(filter, repeat, mipmap)
	if smp, ok := ctx.sampler_cache[key]; ok && smp.id != 0 {return smp}
	desc := sg.Sampler_Desc{}
	wrap := repeat ? sg.Wrap.REPEAT : sg.Wrap.CLAMP_TO_EDGE
	desc.wrap_u = wrap; desc.wrap_v = wrap
	switch filter {
	case .Nearest:
		desc.min_filter = .NEAREST; desc.mag_filter = .NEAREST
	case .Linear:
		desc.min_filter = .LINEAR; desc.mag_filter = .LINEAR
	case .Linear_Mipmap:
		desc.min_filter = .LINEAR; desc.mag_filter = .LINEAR; desc.mipmap_filter = .LINEAR
	}
	smp := sg.make_sampler(desc)
	ctx.sampler_cache[key] = smp
	return smp
}

// Set current sampling for subsequent draws; causes new batch when sampler differs.
g2d_set_sampling :: proc(
	filter: G2D_Sampler_Filter = .Nearest,
	repeat: bool = false,
	mipmap: bool = false,
) {
	ctx.curr_sampler = _g2d_get_sampler(filter, repeat, mipmap)
	ctx.curr_sampler_filter = filter
	ctx.curr_sampler_repeat = repeat
	ctx.curr_sampler_mipmap = mipmap
}

// Reset to default canvas sampler (created in setup).
g2d_reset_sampling :: proc() {
	ctx.curr_sampler = ctx.smp
	ctx.curr_sampler_filter = .Nearest
	ctx.curr_sampler_repeat = false
	ctx.curr_sampler_mipmap = false
}

// Sprite animation support
// Create a sprite animation state (caller-owned). Starts at start and plays.
// Phase DX: Sprite make function that accepts Image_Handle directly
g2d_sprite_make_handle :: proc(
	image: renderer.Image_Handle,
	frame_w, frame_h: i32,
	start, end: i32,
	fps: f32,
	loop: bool,
) -> renderer.G2D_Sprite_Anim {
	return renderer.G2D_Sprite_Anim {
		image = image,
		frame_w = frame_w,
		frame_h = frame_h,
		start = start,
		end = end,
		fps = fps,
		loop = loop,
		frame = start,
		acc_ms = 0,
		playing = true,
	}
}

// TEMPORARY LAYER VIOLATION — sg.Image in public signature; use g2d_sprite_make_handle instead
// Resolution: Phase G.1
@(private)
g2d_sprite_make :: proc(
	image: sg.Image,
	frame_w, frame_h: i32,
	start, end: i32,
	fps: f32,
	loop: bool,
) -> renderer.G2D_Sprite_Anim {
	return renderer.G2D_Sprite_Anim {
		image = _sg_to_image_handle(image),
		frame_w = frame_w,
		frame_h = frame_h,
		start = start,
		end = end,
		fps = fps,
		loop = loop,
		frame = start,
		acc_ms = 0,
		playing = true,
	}
}
// end g2d_sprite_make

// Advance animation by dt (milliseconds). Returns true if the visible frame changed.
g2d_sprite_update :: proc(anim: ^renderer.G2D_Sprite_Anim, dt_ms: f32) -> bool {
	if anim == nil || anim.fps <= 0 || !anim.playing do return false
	frame_ms := 1000.0 / anim.fps
	anim.acc_ms += dt_ms
	changed := false
	for anim.acc_ms >= frame_ms {
		anim.acc_ms -= frame_ms
		anim.frame += 1
		if anim.frame > anim.end {
			if anim.loop {
				anim.frame = anim.start
			} else {
				anim.frame = anim.end
				anim.playing = false
				if anim.on_complete != nil {
					anim.on_complete(anim)
				}
			}
		}
		changed = true
		if !anim.loop && !anim.playing {break}
	}
	return changed
}
// end g2d_sprite_update

g2d_sprite_play :: proc(anim: ^renderer.G2D_Sprite_Anim) {
	if anim == nil do return
	anim.frame = anim.start
	anim.acc_ms = 0
	anim.playing = true
}

g2d_sprite_pause :: proc(anim: ^renderer.G2D_Sprite_Anim) {
	if anim == nil do return
	anim.playing = false
}

g2d_sprite_resume :: proc(anim: ^renderer.G2D_Sprite_Anim) {
	if anim == nil do return
	if anim.frame >= anim.end && !anim.loop do anim.frame = anim.start
	anim.playing = true
}

// Compute normalized UV rect [u0,v0,u1,v1] for current frame.
g2d_sprite_frame_uv :: proc(anim: ^renderer.G2D_Sprite_Anim) -> [4]f32 {
	if anim == nil do return [4]f32{0, 0, 1, 1}
	iw := i32(sg.query_image_width(_image_handle_to_sg(anim.image)))
	ih := i32(sg.query_image_height(_image_handle_to_sg(anim.image)))
	fw, fh := anim.frame_w, anim.frame_h
	if iw <= 0 || ih <= 0 || fw <= 0 || fh <= 0 do return [4]f32{0, 0, 1, 1}
	cols := iw / fw
	if cols <= 0 do cols = 1
	// Clamp frame index to available cells in sheet to avoid OOB
	max_frames := (iw / fw) * (ih / fh)
	idx := anim.frame
	if idx < 0 do idx = 0
	if max_frames <= 0 do max_frames = 1
	if idx >= max_frames do idx = max_frames - 1
	col := idx % cols
	row := idx / cols
	u0 := f32(col * fw) / f32(iw)
	v0 := f32(row * fh) / f32(ih)
	u1 := f32((col + 1) * fw) / f32(iw)
	v1 := f32((row + 1) * fh) / f32(ih)
	return [4]f32{u0, v0, u1, v1}
}
// end g2d_sprite_frame_uv

// Draw current frame to dest rect using provided tint (defaults to white).
g2d_draw_sprite :: proc(
	anim: ^renderer.G2D_Sprite_Anim,
	dest: [4]f32,
	tint: [4]f32 = {1, 1, 1, 1},
) {
	if anim == nil do return
	uvs := g2d_sprite_frame_uv(anim)
	g2d_draw_image_rect_uv(dest, uvs, _image_handle_to_sg(anim.image), tint)
}
// end g2d_draw_sprite

// Convenience: draw a specific frame index without an animation state.
g2d_draw_sprite_frame :: proc(
	image: sg.Image,
	frame_idx, frame_w, frame_h: i32,
	dest: [4]f32,
	tint: [4]f32 = {1, 1, 1, 1},
) {
	iw := i32(sg.query_image_width(image))
	ih := i32(sg.query_image_height(image))
	fw, fh := frame_w, frame_h
	if iw <= 0 || ih <= 0 || fw <= 0 || fh <= 0 do return
	cols := iw / fw
	if cols <= 0 do cols = 1
	max_frames := (iw / fw) * (ih / fh)
	idx := frame_idx
	if idx < 0 do idx = 0
	if max_frames <= 0 do max_frames = 1
	if idx >= max_frames do idx = max_frames - 1
	col := idx % cols
	row := idx / cols
	u0 := f32(col * fw) / f32(iw)
	v0 := f32(row * fh) / f32(ih)
	u1 := f32((col + 1) * fw) / f32(iw)
	v1 := f32((row + 1) * fh) / f32(ih)
	g2d_draw_image_rect_uv(dest, [4]f32{u0, v0, u1, v1}, image, tint)
}
// end g2d_draw_sprite_frame

// vTable compatible version: draw a specific frame from an animation state.
g2d_draw_sprite_frame_state :: proc(
	anim: ^renderer.G2D_Sprite_Anim,
	frame: i32,
	dest: [4]f32,
	tint: [4]f32 = {1, 1, 1, 1},
) {
	if anim == nil do return
	g2d_draw_sprite_frame(
		_image_handle_to_sg(anim.image),
		frame,
		anim.frame_w,
		anim.frame_h,
		dest,
		tint,
	)
}
// end g2d_draw_sprite_frame_state

// Gate 2.6: Deferred sprite draw — pushes a Cmd_Draw_Image_Rect_UV_Mat onto the draw list.
// Use this (instead of g2d_draw_sprite) when drawing inside the deferred render pipeline
// so the sprite respects the current layer, transform, opacity, and scissor state.
g2d_draw_sprite_deferred :: proc(
	anim: ^renderer.G2D_Sprite_Anim,
	dest: [4]f32,
	tint: [4]f32 = {1, 1, 1, 1},
) {
	if anim == nil do return
	uvs := g2d_sprite_frame_uv(anim)
	mat := renderer.G2D_Material{image = anim.image, tint = tint}
	g2d_record_draw_image_rect_uv_mat(dest, uvs, mat)
}
// end g2d_draw_sprite_deferred

// Gate 2.6: Deferred sprite-frame draw — deferred version of g2d_draw_sprite_frame_state.
g2d_draw_sprite_frame_deferred :: proc(
	anim: ^renderer.G2D_Sprite_Anim,
	frame: i32,
	dest: [4]f32,
	tint: [4]f32 = {1, 1, 1, 1},
) {
	if anim == nil do return
	saved := anim.frame
	anim.frame = frame
	uvs := g2d_sprite_frame_uv(anim)
	anim.frame = saved
	mat := renderer.G2D_Material{image = anim.image, tint = tint}
	g2d_record_draw_image_rect_uv_mat(dest, uvs, mat)
}
// end g2d_draw_sprite_frame_deferred

// ============================================================================
// 9-SLICE RENDERING
// ============================================================================

// Renders an image as a 9-slice scalable panel.
// dest     = destination rect [x, y, w, h]
// slices   = border sizes [left, top, right, bottom] in source pixels
// tint     = color multiplier (white = no tint)
g2d_draw_9slice :: proc(
	image:  renderer.Image_Handle,
	dest:   [4]f32,
	slices: [4]f32,
	tint:   [4]f32 = {1, 1, 1, 1},
) {
	if image.id == 0 do return

	img_w := f32(sg.query_image_width(_image_handle_to_sg(image)))
	img_h := f32(sg.query_image_height(_image_handle_to_sg(image)))
	if img_w <= 0 || img_h <= 0 do return

	// If slices are zero or bigger than image, fallback to full draw
	if slices[0] + slices[2] >= img_w || slices[1] + slices[3] >= img_h {
		g2d_draw_image_rect_uv_mat(
			dest,
			{0, 0, 1, 1},
			{image = image, tint = tint},
		)
		return
	}

	sl, st, sr, sb := slices[0], slices[1], slices[2], slices[3]
	smw, smh := img_w - sl - sr, img_h - st - sb // source middle w, h

	dx, dy, dw, dh := dest.x, dest.y, dest.z, dest.w
	// Clamp borders to not exceed dest width/height
	dl := min(sl, dw * 0.5)
	dr := min(sr, dw * 0.5)
	dt := min(st, dh * 0.5)
	db := min(sb, dh * 0.5)
	dmw, dmh := max(0, dw - dl - dr), max(0, dh - dt - db) // dest middle w, h

	mat: renderer.G2D_Material = {image = image, tint = tint}

	// 9 patches (dest rect, uv rect)
	// Top row
	if st > 0 {
		v0 := f32(0); v1 := st / img_h
		dy1 := dy
		if dl > 0 do renderer.active_renderer.drawImageRectUvMat({dx, dy1, dl, dt}, {0, v0, sl / img_w, v1}, mat)
		if dmw > 0 do renderer.active_renderer.drawImageRectUvMat({dx + dl, dy1, dmw, dt}, {sl / img_w, v0, (sl + smw) / img_w, v1}, mat)
		if dr > 0 do renderer.active_renderer.drawImageRectUvMat({dx + dl + dmw, dy1, dr, dt}, {(sl + smw) / img_w, v0, 1, v1}, mat)
	}

	// Middle row
	if dmh > 0 {
		v0 := st / img_h; v1 := (st + smh) / img_h
		dy1 := dy + dt
		if dl > 0 do renderer.active_renderer.drawImageRectUvMat({dx, dy1, dl, dmh}, {0, v0, sl / img_w, v1}, mat)
		if dmw > 0 do renderer.active_renderer.drawImageRectUvMat({dx + dl, dy1, dmw, dmh}, {sl / img_w, v0, (sl + smw) / img_w, v1}, mat)
		if dr > 0 do renderer.active_renderer.drawImageRectUvMat({dx + dl + dmw, dy1, dr, dmh}, {(sl + smw) / img_w, v0, 1, v1}, mat)
	}

	// Bottom row
	if db > 0 {
		v0 := (img_h - sb) / img_h; v1 := f32(1)
		dy1 := dy + dt + dmh
		if dl > 0 do renderer.active_renderer.drawImageRectUvMat({dx, dy1, dl, db}, {0, v0, sl / img_w, v1}, mat)
		if dmw > 0 do renderer.active_renderer.drawImageRectUvMat({dx + dl, dy1, dmw, db}, {sl / img_w, v0, (sl + smw) / img_w, v1}, mat)
		if dr > 0 do renderer.active_renderer.drawImageRectUvMat({dx + dl + dmw, dy1, dr, db}, {(sl + smw) / img_w, v0, 1, v1}, mat)
	}
}
// end g2d_draw_9slice

// ============================================================================
// TWEEN ANIMATION SYSTEM
// ============================================================================
// Lightweight tweening for smooth transitions. Pure computation — no drawing.
// Preserves call-order semantics and batching invariants.
//
// Time unit: milliseconds.
// ============================================================================

Ease_Fn :: proc(t: f32) -> f32

// Default easing: linear
g2d_ease_linear :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {return t}

// Scalar tween
Tween_F32 :: struct {
	start:       f32,
	end:         f32,
	duration_ms: f32,
	elapsed_ms:  f32,
	playing:     bool,
	ease:        Ease_Fn,
}

// Vector2 tween
Tween_V2 :: struct {
	start:       [2]f32,
	end:         [2]f32,
	duration_ms: f32,
	elapsed_ms:  f32,
	playing:     bool,
	ease:        Ease_Fn,
}

// Vector4 tween (e.g., colors)
Tween_V4 :: struct {
	start:       [4]f32,
	end:         [4]f32,
	duration_ms: f32,
	elapsed_ms:  f32,
	playing:     bool,
	ease:        Ease_Fn,
}

// Constructors (caller-owned value types)
g2d_tween_f32_make :: proc(
	start, end, duration_ms: f32,
	ease: Ease_Fn = g2d_ease_linear,
) -> Tween_F32 {
	return Tween_F32 {
		start = start,
		end = end,
		duration_ms = duration_ms,
		elapsed_ms = 0,
		playing = true,
		ease = ease,
	}
}
// end g2d_tween_f32_make

g2d_tween_v2_make :: proc(
	start, end: [2]f32,
	duration_ms: f32,
	ease: Ease_Fn = g2d_ease_linear,
) -> Tween_V2 {
	return Tween_V2 {
		start = start,
		end = end,
		duration_ms = duration_ms,
		elapsed_ms = 0,
		playing = true,
		ease = ease,
	}
}
// end g2d_tween_v2_make

g2d_tween_v4_make :: proc(
	start, end: [4]f32,
	duration_ms: f32,
	ease: Ease_Fn = g2d_ease_linear,
) -> Tween_V4 {
	return Tween_V4 {
		start = start,
		end = end,
		duration_ms = duration_ms,
		elapsed_ms = 0,
		playing = true,
		ease = ease,
	}
}
// end g2d_tween_v4_make

// TODO(Phase 13): Add LAB-space interpolation option for more perceptually uniform color transitions.
Tween_Color :: Tween_V4

g2d_tween_color_make :: proc(
	start, end: [4]f32,
	duration_ms: f32,
	ease: Ease_Fn = g2d_ease_linear,
) -> Tween_Color {
	return g2d_tween_v4_make(start, end, duration_ms, ease)
}
// end g2d_tween_color_make

// Restart helpers
g2d_tween_f32_restart :: proc(tw: ^Tween_F32) {if tw != nil {tw.elapsed_ms = 0; tw.playing = true}}
g2d_tween_v2_restart :: proc(tw: ^Tween_V2) {if tw != nil {tw.elapsed_ms = 0; tw.playing = true}}
g2d_tween_v4_restart :: proc(tw: ^Tween_V4) {if tw != nil {tw.elapsed_ms = 0; tw.playing = true}}

// Update: advance time and return (current_value, done)
g2d_tween_f32_update :: proc(tw: ^Tween_F32, dt_ms: f32) -> (f32, bool) {
	if tw == nil do return 0, true
	if tw.duration_ms <= 0 {
		tw.elapsed_ms = tw.duration_ms
		tw.playing = false
		return tw.end, true
	}
	if tw.playing {tw.elapsed_ms = math.min(tw.elapsed_ms + dt_ms, tw.duration_ms)}
	t := clamp(tw.elapsed_ms / tw.duration_ms, 0, 1)
	e := (tw.ease != nil) ? tw.ease(t) : g2d_ease_linear(t)
	val := tw.start + (tw.end - tw.start) * e
	done := tw.elapsed_ms >= tw.duration_ms
	if done {tw.playing = false}
	return val, done
}
// end g2d_tween_f32_update

g2d_tween_v2_update :: proc(tw: ^Tween_V2, dt_ms: f32) -> ([2]f32, bool) {
	if tw == nil do return [2]f32{0, 0}, true
	if tw.duration_ms <= 0 {
		tw.elapsed_ms = tw.duration_ms
		tw.playing = false
		return tw.end, true
	}
	if tw.playing {tw.elapsed_ms = math.min(tw.elapsed_ms + dt_ms, tw.duration_ms)}
	t := clamp(tw.elapsed_ms / tw.duration_ms, 0, 1)
	e := (tw.ease != nil) ? tw.ease(t) : g2d_ease_linear(t)
	val := tw.start + (tw.end - tw.start) * e
	done := tw.elapsed_ms >= tw.duration_ms
	if done {tw.playing = false}
	return val, done
}
// end g2d_tween_v2_update

g2d_tween_v4_update :: proc(tw: ^Tween_V4, dt_ms: f32) -> ([4]f32, bool) {
	if tw == nil do return [4]f32{0, 0, 0, 0}, true
	if tw.duration_ms <= 0 {
		tw.elapsed_ms = tw.duration_ms
		tw.playing = false
		return tw.end, true
	}
	if tw.playing {tw.elapsed_ms = math.min(tw.elapsed_ms + dt_ms, tw.duration_ms)}
	t := clamp(tw.elapsed_ms / tw.duration_ms, 0, 1)
	e := (tw.ease != nil) ? tw.ease(t) : g2d_ease_linear(t)
	val := tw.start + (tw.end - tw.start) * e
	done := tw.elapsed_ms >= tw.duration_ms
	if done {tw.playing = false}
	return val, done
}
// end g2d_tween_v4_update

g2d_tween_color_update :: proc(tw: ^Tween_Color, dt_ms: f32) -> ([4]f32, bool) {
	return g2d_tween_v4_update(tw, dt_ms)
}
// end g2d_tween_color_update

// ============================================================================
// TIMELINE SEQUENCER
// ============================================================================

Timeline_Action :: struct {
	delay_ms:    f32,
	duration_ms: f32,
	start_val:   f32,
	end_val:     f32,
	ease:        Ease_Fn,
}

Timeline_Track :: struct {
	actions:  [dynamic]Timeline_Action,
	val_ptr:  ^f32, // target variable to animate
}

Timeline :: struct {
	tracks:      [dynamic]Timeline_Track,
	elapsed_ms:  f32,
	duration_ms: f32,
	playing:     bool,
	loop:        bool,
}

g2d_timeline_make :: proc(loop := false) -> Timeline {
	return Timeline{
		tracks = make([dynamic]Timeline_Track),
		loop = loop,
		playing = true,
	}
}

g2d_timeline_add_action_f32 :: proc(
	tl:          ^Timeline,
	val_ptr:     ^f32,
	delay_ms:    f32,
	duration_ms: f32,
	start_val:   f32,
	end_val:     f32,
	ease:        Ease_Fn = nil,
) {
	if tl == nil || val_ptr == nil do return
	
	track_idx := -1
	for id in 0..<len(tl.tracks) {
		if tl.tracks[id].val_ptr == val_ptr {
			track_idx = id
			break
		}
	}
	if track_idx == -1 {
		track_idx = len(tl.tracks)
		append(&tl.tracks, Timeline_Track{
			actions = make([dynamic]Timeline_Action),
			val_ptr = val_ptr,
		})
	}
	
	append(&tl.tracks[track_idx].actions, Timeline_Action{
		delay_ms = delay_ms,
		duration_ms = duration_ms,
		start_val = start_val,
		end_val = end_val,
		ease = ease,
	})

	action_end := delay_ms + duration_ms
	if action_end > tl.duration_ms do tl.duration_ms = action_end
}

g2d_timeline_play :: proc(tl: ^Timeline) {
	if tl == nil do return
	tl.playing = true
}

g2d_timeline_pause :: proc(tl: ^Timeline) {
	if tl == nil do return
	tl.playing = false
}

g2d_timeline_update :: proc(tl: ^Timeline, dt_ms: f32) -> bool {
	if tl == nil || !tl.playing || tl.duration_ms <= 0 do return false
	
	tl.elapsed_ms += dt_ms
	done := tl.elapsed_ms >= tl.duration_ms
	
	for &track in tl.tracks {
		if len(track.actions) == 0 do continue
		
		val: f32 = track.actions[0].start_val
		for i in 0..<len(track.actions) {
			action := track.actions[i]
			action_end := action.delay_ms + action.duration_ms
			
			if tl.elapsed_ms < action.delay_ms {
				if i == 0 do val = action.start_val
				// We wait for this action, assuming actions are ordered conceptually over time
			} else if tl.elapsed_ms >= action_end {
				val = action.end_val
			} else {
				local_t := (tl.elapsed_ms - action.delay_ms) / action.duration_ms
				t := clamp(local_t, 0, 1)
				e := action.ease != nil ? action.ease(t) : g2d_ease_linear(t)
				val = action.start_val + (action.end_val - action.start_val) * e
				break
			}
		}
		track.val_ptr^ = val
	}
	
	if done {
		if tl.loop {
			tl.elapsed_ms = 0
		} else {
			tl.playing = false
		}
	}
	return done
}

g2d_timeline_destroy :: proc(tl: ^Timeline) {
	if tl == nil do return
	for &track in tl.tracks {
		delete(track.actions)
	}
	delete(tl.tracks)
}

// ============================================================================
// CPU SIGNED-DISTANCE FIELD EVALUATION
// ============================================================================
// Fast CPU-side distance queries for hit testing and procedural effects.
// Used by the UI system for path-based hit detection.
// ============================================================================

// Compute signed distance from point p to line segment ab
@(private)
_g2d_point_segment_dist :: proc(p, a, b: [2]f32) -> f32 {
	ab := b - a
	ap := p - a
	denom := linalg.dot(ab, ab)
	t: f32
	if denom > 0 {
		t = clamp(linalg.dot(ap, ab) / denom, 0, 1)
	} else {
		t = 0
	}
	closest := a + ab * t
	d := linalg.distance(p, closest)
	return d
}
// end _g2d_point_segment_dist

// Even-odd rule point-in-polygon test
@(private)
_g2d_point_in_polygon_evenodd :: proc(p: [2]f32, pts: [][2]f32) -> bool {
	inside := false
	n := len(pts)
	if n < 3 do return false
	j := n - 1
	for i in 0 ..< n {
		pi := pts[i]
		pj := pts[j]
		// Edge crossing test with horizontal ray to +X; strict y-test avoids double counting at vertices
		if ((pi.y > p.y) != (pj.y > p.y)) {
			dy := (pj.y - pi.y)
			// Protect against division by zero on horizontal edges
			xint := (pj.x - pi.x) * (p.y - pi.y) / (dy != 0 ? dy : 1e-12) + pi.x
			if p.x < xint {
				inside = !inside
			}
		}
		j = i
	}
	return inside
}
// end _g2d_point_in_polygon_evenodd

// Signed distance field for a polygon
@(private)
_g2d_poly_sdf :: proc(pts: [][2]f32, closed: bool, p: [2]f32) -> f32 {
	n := len(pts)
	if n == 0 do return 0
	min_d: f32 = 1e30
	limit := closed ? n : (n - 1)
	for i in 0 ..< limit {
		a := pts[i]
		b := pts[(i + 1) % n]
		d := _g2d_point_segment_dist(p, a, b)
		if d < min_d do min_d = d
	}
	if closed {
		inside := _g2d_point_in_polygon_evenodd(p, pts)
		return inside ? +min_d : -min_d
	}
	// Open polylines: treat as outside-only distance
	return -min_d
}
// end _g2d_poly_sdf

// True if point (x,y) is strictly inside the closed path given by pts.
g2d_path_hit_test :: proc(pts: [][2]f32, x, y: f32) -> bool {
	if len(pts) < 3 do return false
	return _g2d_poly_sdf(pts, true, {x, y}) > 0
}

// Evaluate signed distance to the current path at (x,y).
// Path space: local logical units before any canvas transform.
// Returns positive inside for closed paths, negative outside; open paths return negative distance.
g2d_path_sdf_eval :: proc(x, y: f32) -> f32 {
	pts := _g2d_get_flat_points()
	if len(pts) < 2 do return 0
	closed := false
	if len(pts) >= 3 {
		if linalg.distance(pts[0], pts[len(pts) - 1]) < 1e-4 {
			closed = true
		}
	}
	return _g2d_poly_sdf(pts[:], closed, [2]f32{x, y})
}
// end g2d_path_sdf_eval// ============================================================================
// EASING LIBRARY (PURE FUNCTIONS)
// ============================================================================
// Intent: immediate-mode safe helpers. Pure math; no ctx access; no allocations.
// Callers may pass these as Ease_Fn to Tween constructors. Domain: t in [0,1].
// ============================================================================

g2d_ease_in_quad :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {return t * t}
g2d_ease_out_quad :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {return 1 - (1 - t) * (1 - t)}
g2d_ease_in_out_quad :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {
	if t < 0.5 {return 2 * t * t}
	u := -2 * t + 2
	return 1 - (u * u) * 0.5
} //end proc g2d_ease_in_out_quad

g2d_ease_in_cubic :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {return t * t * t}
g2d_ease_out_cubic :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {u := (1 - t); return 1 - u * u * u}
g2d_ease_in_out_cubic :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {
	if t < 0.5 {return 4 * t * t * t}
	u := -2 * t + 2
	return 1 - (u * u * u) * 0.5
} //end proc g2d_ease_in_out_cubic

g2d_ease_in_sine :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {return 1 - math.cos((t * math.PI) * 0.5)}
g2d_ease_out_sine :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {return math.sin((t * math.PI) * 0.5)}
g2d_ease_in_out_sine :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {return -(math.cos(math.PI * t) - 1) * 0.5}

g2d_ease_in_expo :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {return t <= 0 ? 0 : math.pow(2.0, 10.0 * t - 10.0)}
g2d_ease_out_expo :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {return t >= 1 ? 1 : 1 - math.pow(2.0, -10.0 * t)}
g2d_ease_in_out_expo :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {
	if t <= 0 {return 0}
	if t >= 1 {return 1}
	if t < 0.5 {return math.pow(2.0, 20.0 * t - 10.0) * 0.5}
	return (2 - math.pow(2.0, -20.0 * t + 10.0)) * 0.5
} //end proc g2d_ease_in_out_expo

// Circular: based on the arc of a unit circle.
g2d_ease_in_circ :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {return 1 - math.sqrt(max(0, 1 - t * t))}
g2d_ease_out_circ :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {u := t - 1; return math.sqrt(max(0, 1 - u * u))}
g2d_ease_in_out_circ :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {
	if t < 0.5 {return (1 - math.sqrt(max(0, 1 - 4 * t * t))) * 0.5}
	u := -2 * t + 2
	return (math.sqrt(max(0, 1 - u * u)) + 1) * 0.5
} //end proc g2d_ease_in_out_circ

// Back: slight overshoot (anticipation feel). c1 controls overshoot amount.
g2d_ease_in_back :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {
	c1: f32 = 1.70158; c3: f32 = c1 + 1
	return c3 * t * t * t - c1 * t * t
}
g2d_ease_out_back :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {
	c1: f32 = 1.70158; c3: f32 = c1 + 1; u := t - 1
	return 1 + c3 * u * u * u + c1 * u * u
}
g2d_ease_in_out_back :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {
	c1: f32 = 1.70158; c2: f32 = c1 * 1.525
	if t < 0.5 {u := 2 * t; return (u * u * ((c2 + 1) * u - c2)) * 0.5}
	u := 2 * t - 2
	return (u * u * ((c2 + 1) * u + c2) + 2) * 0.5
} //end proc g2d_ease_in_out_back

// Elastic: spring overshoot with oscillation. c4/c5 control oscillation period.
g2d_ease_in_elastic :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {
	if t <= 0 {return 0}; if t >= 1 {return 1}
	c4: f32 = (2 * math.PI) / 3
	return -math.pow(2.0, 10 * t - 10) * math.sin((t * 10 - 10.75) * c4)
}
g2d_ease_out_elastic :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {
	if t <= 0 {return 0}; if t >= 1 {return 1}
	c4: f32 = (2 * math.PI) / 3
	return math.pow(2.0, -10 * t) * math.sin((t * 10 - 0.75) * c4) + 1
}
g2d_ease_in_out_elastic :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {
	if t <= 0 {return 0}; if t >= 1 {return 1}
	c5: f32 = (2 * math.PI) / 4.5
	if t < 0.5 {return -(math.pow(2.0, 20 * t - 10) * math.sin((20 * t - 11.125) * c5)) * 0.5}
	return (math.pow(2.0, -20 * t + 10) * math.sin((20 * t - 11.125) * c5)) * 0.5 + 1
} //end proc g2d_ease_in_out_elastic

// Bounce: cascading sub-bounces with decaying amplitude.
g2d_ease_out_bounce :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {
	n1: f32 = 7.5625; d1: f32 = 2.75; x := t
	if x < 1 / d1 {return n1 * x * x}
	else if x < 2 / d1 {x -= 1.5 / d1; return n1 * x * x + 0.75}
	else if x < 2.5 / d1 {x -= 2.25 / d1; return n1 * x * x + 0.9375}
	x -= 2.625 / d1; return n1 * x * x + 0.984375
}
g2d_ease_in_bounce :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {return 1 - g2d_ease_out_bounce(1 - t)}
g2d_ease_in_out_bounce :: proc(t: f32) -> f32 {
	if t < 0.5 {return (1 - g2d_ease_out_bounce(1 - 2 * t)) * 0.5}
	return (1 + g2d_ease_out_bounce(2 * t - 1)) * 0.5
} //end proc g2d_ease_in_out_bounce


// Intent: conservative immediate-mode helpers; caller-owned state; no ctx access.
// Equation: x-prime-prime + 2*zeta*omega*x-prime + omega^2*(x - target) = 0, with omega = 2*pi*freq_hz.
// Update integrates velocity then position. At rest snaps to target.
// ============================================================================

Spring_F32 :: struct {
	x:       f32, // position
	v:       f32, // velocity
	target:  f32,
	freq_hz: f32, // natural frequency
	zeta:    f32, // damping ratio (1=critical)
	eps_pos: f32, // settle threshold (position)
	eps_vel: f32, // settle threshold (velocity)
} // end Spring_F32

g2d_spring_f32_make :: proc(
	x, target: f32,
	freq_hz: f32 = 8.0,
	zeta: f32 = 1.0,
	eps_pos: f32 = 0.001,
	eps_vel: f32 = 0.001,
) -> Spring_F32 {
	return Spring_F32 {
		x = x,
		v = 0,
		target = target,
		freq_hz = freq_hz,
		zeta = zeta,
		eps_pos = eps_pos,
		eps_vel = eps_vel,
	}
} //end proc g2d_spring_f32_make

g2d_spring_f32_set_target :: proc(s: ^Spring_F32, target: f32) {if s != nil {s.target = target}}

g2d_spring_f32_update :: proc(s: ^Spring_F32, dt_ms: f32) -> (f32, bool) {
	if s == nil do return 0, true
	if s.freq_hz <= 0 {
		s.x = s.target; s.v = 0
		return s.x, true
	}
	dt := dt_ms * 0.001
	if dt > 0.05 {dt = 0.05} 	// stability clamp
	if dt < 0 {dt = 0}
	omega := 2.0 * math.PI * s.freq_hz
	// acceleration: a = -(2*zeta*omega*v + omega^2*(x - target))
	a := -(2.0 * s.zeta * omega * s.v + (omega * omega) * (s.x - s.target))
	s.v += a * dt
	s.x += s.v * dt
	at_rest := (math.abs(s.x - s.target) <= s.eps_pos) && (math.abs(s.v) <= s.eps_vel)
	if at_rest {s.x = s.target; s.v = 0}
	return s.x, at_rest
} //end proc g2d_spring_f32_update

Spring_V2 :: struct {
	x:       [2]f32,
	v:       [2]f32,
	target:  [2]f32,
	freq_hz: f32,
	zeta:    f32,
	eps_pos: f32,
	eps_vel: f32,
} //end Spring_V2

g2d_spring_v2_make :: proc(
	x, target: [2]f32,
	freq_hz: f32 = 8.0,
	zeta: f32 = 1.0,
	eps_pos: f32 = 0.001,
	eps_vel: f32 = 0.001,
) -> Spring_V2 {
	return Spring_V2 {
		x = x,
		v = {0, 0},
		target = target,
		freq_hz = freq_hz,
		zeta = zeta,
		eps_pos = eps_pos,
		eps_vel = eps_vel,
	}
} //end proc g2d_spring_v2_make

g2d_spring_v2_set_target :: proc(s: ^Spring_V2, target: [2]f32) {if s != nil {s.target = target}}

g2d_spring_v2_update :: proc(s: ^Spring_V2, dt_ms: f32) -> ([2]f32, bool) {
	if s == nil do return [2]f32{0, 0}, true
	if s.freq_hz <= 0 {
		s.x = s.target; s.v = {0, 0}
		return s.x, true
	}
	dt := dt_ms * 0.001
	if dt > 0.05 {dt = 0.05}
	if dt < 0 {dt = 0}
	omega := 2.0 * math.PI * s.freq_hz
	dx := s.x - s.target
	a := -(2.0 * s.zeta * omega) * s.v - (omega * omega) * dx
	s.v += a * dt
	s.x += s.v * dt
	at_rest := (linalg.length(s.x - s.target) <= s.eps_pos) && (linalg.length(s.v) <= s.eps_vel)
	if at_rest {s.x = s.target; s.v = {0, 0}}
	return s.x, at_rest
} //end proc g2d_spring_v2_update

// ============================================================================
// PARTICLE SYSTEM (PHASE A)
// ============================================================================
// Lightweight CPU-driven particle simulation.
// Suitable for small bursts, sparks, and UI accents.
// ============================================================================

Particle :: struct {
	pos:      [2]f32,
	vel:      [2]f32,
	acc:      [2]f32,
	color:    [4]f32,
	size:     f32,
	angle:    f32,
	ang_vel:  f32,
	age:      f32, // ms
	life:     f32, // ms
	active:   bool,
}

Emitter_Config :: struct {
	max_particles: int,
	spawn_rate:    f32, // particles per second
	
	life_min, life_max:    f32, // ms
	pos_spread:            [2]f32,
	vel_min, vel_max:      [2]f32,
	gravity:               [2]f32,
	
	size_start, size_end:  f32,
	color_start, color_end: [4]f32,
	
	angle_start:           f32,
	ang_vel_min, ang_vel_max: f32,
}

Particle_Emitter :: struct {
	config:      Emitter_Config,
	particles:   []Particle,
	accumulator: f32, // ms
	active_count: int,
}

g2d_emitter_make :: proc(config: Emitter_Config) -> Particle_Emitter {
	return Particle_Emitter {
		config    = config,
		particles = make([]Particle, config.max_particles),
	}
}

g2d_emitter_destroy :: proc(emitter: ^Particle_Emitter) {
	if emitter == nil do return
	delete(emitter.particles)
}

g2d_emitter_update :: proc(emitter: ^Particle_Emitter, dt_ms: f32) {
	if emitter == nil do return
	
	dt := dt_ms * 0.001
	
	// 1. Spawning
	emitter.accumulator += dt_ms
	spawn_interval := 1000.0 / emitter.config.spawn_rate
	for emitter.accumulator >= spawn_interval {
		emitter.accumulator -= spawn_interval
		
		// Find free slot
		for i in 0..<len(emitter.particles) {
			if !emitter.particles[i].active {
				p := &emitter.particles[i]
				p.active = true
				p.age = 0
				
				// Randomize based on config
				p.life = emitter.config.life_min + (emitter.config.life_max - emitter.config.life_min) * (f32(rand_int()) / f32(max(int)))
				
				p.pos = {
					(f32(rand_int()) / f32(max(int)) - 0.5) * emitter.config.pos_spread.x,
					(f32(rand_int()) / f32(max(int)) - 0.5) * emitter.config.pos_spread.y,
				}
				
				vx := emitter.config.vel_min.x + (emitter.config.vel_max.x - emitter.config.vel_min.x) * (f32(rand_int()) / f32(max(int)))
				vy := emitter.config.vel_min.y + (emitter.config.vel_max.y - emitter.config.vel_min.y) * (f32(rand_int()) / f32(max(int)))
				p.vel = {vx, vy}
				
				p.acc = emitter.config.gravity
				p.angle = emitter.config.angle_start
				p.ang_vel = emitter.config.ang_vel_min + (emitter.config.ang_vel_max - emitter.config.ang_vel_min) * (f32(rand_int()) / f32(max(int)))
				
				p.color = emitter.config.color_start
				p.size = emitter.config.size_start
				
				emitter.active_count += 1
				break
			}
		}
	}
	
	// 2. Simulation
	emitter.active_count = 0
	for i in 0..<len(emitter.particles) {
		p := &emitter.particles[i]
		if !p.active do continue
		
		p.age += dt_ms
		if p.age >= p.life {
			p.active = false
			continue
		}
		
		t := p.age / p.life
		
		// Movement
		p.vel += p.acc * dt
		p.pos += p.vel * dt
		p.angle += p.ang_vel * dt
		
		// Interpolate
		p.size = emitter.config.size_start + (emitter.config.size_end - emitter.config.size_start) * t
		p.color = linalg.lerp(emitter.config.color_start, emitter.config.color_end, t)
		
		emitter.active_count += 1
	}
}

g2d_emitter_draw :: proc(emitter: ^Particle_Emitter, x, y: f32) {
	if emitter == nil do return
	
	// Use active renderer for drawing
	r := renderer.active_renderer
	if r == nil do return
	
	for i in 0..<len(emitter.particles) {
		p := &emitter.particles[i]
		if !p.active do continue
		
		// Simple circle for now (Phase A)
		// offset by x,y for world space placement
		r.fillCircle(x + p.pos.x, y + p.pos.y, p.size * 0.5, renderer.paint_solid(p.color))
	}
}

// Private rand helper (simple LCG or similar if needed, or just use core:math/rand)
// Actually main.odin might have some or I use core:math/rand
// I'll stick to a simple internal one to avoid too many imports if possible,
// but g2d_experimental already has math. Wait I need rand.
rand_int :: proc() -> int {
	// Dummy for now, I should import core:math/rand if I want real randomness
	// Persistent state for a simple LCG
	@static state: u32 = 12345
	state = state * 1103515245 + 12345
	return int(state & 0x7FFFFFFF)
}

// ============================================================================
// FX_Emitter — full-featured particle system
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Use for: fire, smoke, explosions, magic effects — up to ~2000 particles.
// For small UI sparks/accents use Particle_Emitter above (simpler, always O(n)).
//
// Key differences vs Particle_Emitter:
//   • Free-list pool  — O(1) spawn and recycle (no linear slot search)
//   • Drag/damping    — velocity fades naturally each tick
//   • Burst mode      — emit N particles in one shot via g2d_fx_burst
//   • Spawn shapes    — Point, Rect, Circle, Ring, Cone
//   • Draw shapes     — Circle, Rect, Star
//   • Blend modes     — Alpha (normal) and Additive (glow/fire)
//   • Per-emitter RNG — seeded xorshift via core:math/rand, no shared state
// ============================================================================

FX_Spawn_Shape :: enum u8 {
	Point,   // all particles spawn at emitter origin
	Rect,    // uniform random within ±shape_radius
	Circle,  // uniform random within radius shape_radius.x
	Ring,    // annulus: inner = shape_radius.y, outer = shape_radius.x
	Cone,    // directional: dir_angle ± dir_spread, speed in [speed_min, speed_max]
}

FX_Draw_Shape :: enum u8 {
	Circle,
	Rect,
	Star,   // 5-point star; inner radius = size * 0.45
}

FX_Config :: struct {
	max_particles:           int,
	spawn_rate:              f32,   // particles/second; ignored when burst_only = true
	burst_only:              bool,  // disable continuous spawning; use g2d_fx_burst

	life_min, life_max:      f32,   // particle lifetime in ms

	// Spawn shape
	spawn_shape:             FX_Spawn_Shape,
	shape_radius:            [2]f32, // meaning depends on shape (see enum doc)
	dir_angle:               f32,   // Cone: centre direction in radians
	dir_spread:              f32,   // Cone: half-angle of the cone in radians
	speed_min, speed_max:    f32,   // Cone: launch speed range
	vel_min, vel_max:        [2]f32, // non-Cone shapes: velocity box

	gravity:                 [2]f32,
	drag_min, drag_max:      f32,   // per-particle damping coeff [0..1)/s

	size_start, size_end:    f32,
	color_start, color_end:  [4]f32,

	angle_start:             f32,
	ang_vel_min, ang_vel_max: f32,

	draw_shape:              FX_Draw_Shape,
	blend_mode:              renderer.Blend_Mode,
}

FX_Particle :: struct {
	pos:       [2]f32,
	vel:       [2]f32,
	color:     [4]f32,
	size:      f32,
	angle:     f32,
	ang_vel:   f32,
	age:       f32,      // ms elapsed
	life:      f32,      // ms total lifetime
	drag:      f32,      // per-particle damping coefficient
	active:    bool,
	next_free: int,      // free-list link; -1 when at end of chain
}

FX_Emitter :: struct {
	config:       FX_Config,
	particles:    []FX_Particle,
	free_head:    int,    // first free slot index; -1 when pool exhausted
	active_count: int,
	accumulator:  f32,    // ms leftover for rate-based spawning
	rng:          rand.Default_Random_State,
}

// g2d_fx_make allocates the particle pool and chains the free list.
g2d_fx_make :: proc(config: FX_Config, seed: u64 = 0xDEADBEEF) -> FX_Emitter {
	em := FX_Emitter {
		config    = config,
		particles = make([]FX_Particle, config.max_particles),
		free_head = 0,
		rng       = rand.create(seed),
	}
	for i in 0..<config.max_particles {
		em.particles[i].next_free = i + 1
	}
	em.particles[config.max_particles - 1].next_free = -1
	return em
}

g2d_fx_destroy :: proc(em: ^FX_Emitter) {
	if em == nil do return
	delete(em.particles)
}

@(private = "file")
fx_randf :: proc(em: ^FX_Emitter) -> f32 {
	gen := rand.default_random_generator(&em.rng)
	return rand.float32(gen)
}

@(private = "file")
fx_spawn_one :: proc(em: ^FX_Emitter, world_x, world_y: f32) {
	if em.free_head == -1 do return
	idx          := em.free_head
	em.free_head  = em.particles[idx].next_free

	p := &em.particles[idx]
	p.active  = true
	p.age     = 0
	p.life    = em.config.life_min + (em.config.life_max - em.config.life_min) * fx_randf(em)
	p.angle   = em.config.angle_start
	p.ang_vel = em.config.ang_vel_min + (em.config.ang_vel_max - em.config.ang_vel_min) * fx_randf(em)
	p.color   = em.config.color_start
	p.size    = em.config.size_start
	p.drag    = em.config.drag_min + (em.config.drag_max - em.config.drag_min) * fx_randf(em)

	// Spawn position
	switch em.config.spawn_shape {
	case .Point:
		p.pos = {world_x, world_y}
	case .Rect:
		rx := em.config.shape_radius.x; ry := em.config.shape_radius.y
		p.pos = {world_x + (fx_randf(em) - 0.5) * 2 * rx,
		          world_y + (fx_randf(em) - 0.5) * 2 * ry}
	case .Circle:
		a := fx_randf(em) * math.TAU
		r := math.sqrt(fx_randf(em)) * em.config.shape_radius.x
		p.pos = {world_x + math.cos(a) * r, world_y + math.sin(a) * r}
	case .Ring:
		a     := fx_randf(em) * math.TAU
		r_min := em.config.shape_radius.y
		r_max := em.config.shape_radius.x
		r     := r_min + fx_randf(em) * (r_max - r_min)
		p.pos = {world_x + math.cos(a) * r, world_y + math.sin(a) * r}
	case .Cone:
		p.pos = {world_x, world_y}
	}

	// Launch velocity
	switch em.config.spawn_shape {
	case .Cone:
		a     := em.config.dir_angle + (fx_randf(em) - 0.5) * 2 * em.config.dir_spread
		speed := em.config.speed_min + (em.config.speed_max - em.config.speed_min) * fx_randf(em)
		p.vel = {math.cos(a) * speed, math.sin(a) * speed}
	case .Point, .Rect, .Circle, .Ring:
		vx := em.config.vel_min.x + (em.config.vel_max.x - em.config.vel_min.x) * fx_randf(em)
		vy := em.config.vel_min.y + (em.config.vel_max.y - em.config.vel_min.y) * fx_randf(em)
		p.vel = {vx, vy}
	}

	em.active_count += 1
}

@(private = "file")
fx_retire :: proc(em: ^FX_Emitter, idx: int) {
	p := &em.particles[idx]
	p.active      = false
	p.next_free   = em.free_head
	em.free_head  = idx
	em.active_count -= 1
}

// g2d_fx_update ticks the emitter. Call once per frame with the emitter's
// world position and the frame delta in milliseconds.
g2d_fx_update :: proc(em: ^FX_Emitter, x, y, dt_ms: f32) {
	if em == nil do return
	dt := dt_ms * 0.001

	// Rate-based spawning
	if !em.config.burst_only && em.config.spawn_rate > 0 {
		em.accumulator += dt_ms
		interval := 1000.0 / em.config.spawn_rate
		for em.accumulator >= interval {
			em.accumulator -= interval
			fx_spawn_one(em, x, y)
		}
	}

	// Simulate
	for i in 0..<len(em.particles) {
		p := &em.particles[i]
		if !p.active do continue

		p.age += dt_ms
		if p.age >= p.life {
			fx_retire(em, i)
			continue
		}

		t := p.age / p.life

		drag_factor := max(0.0, 1.0 - p.drag * dt)
		p.vel  *= drag_factor
		p.vel  += em.config.gravity * dt
		p.pos  += p.vel * dt
		p.angle += p.ang_vel * dt

		p.size  = em.config.size_start + (em.config.size_end  - em.config.size_start)  * t
		p.color = linalg.lerp(em.config.color_start, em.config.color_end, t)
	}
}

// g2d_fx_burst spawns count particles immediately at (x, y).
// Useful for explosions, impact sparks, collectible pops.
g2d_fx_burst :: proc(em: ^FX_Emitter, x, y: f32, count: int) {
	if em == nil do return
	for _ in 0..<count {
		fx_spawn_one(em, x, y)
	}
}

// g2d_fx_draw renders all active particles using the active renderer.
// For .Circle particles, uses a single GPU-instanced draw call (Gate 4.1).
// ox, oy are a world-space offset added to every particle position.
// Must be called inside a valid render pass (between beginFrame/endFrame).
g2d_fx_draw :: proc(em: ^FX_Emitter, ox: f32 = 0, oy: f32 = 0) {
	if em == nil do return
	r := renderer.active_renderer
	if r == nil do return

	// Gate 4.1: circle particles — one instanced draw call instead of N fillCircle calls.
	when !#config(G2D_FX_LEGACY_DRAW, false) {
		if em.config.draw_shape == .Circle {
			g2d_fx_draw_instanced(em, ox, oy)
			return
		}
	}

	if em.config.blend_mode != .Normal {
		r.setBlendMode(em.config.blend_mode)
	}

	for i in 0..<len(em.particles) {
		p := &em.particles[i]
		if !p.active do continue

		paint := renderer.paint_solid(p.color)
		px, py := ox + p.pos.x, oy + p.pos.y
		half   := p.size * 0.5

		switch em.config.draw_shape {
		case .Circle:
			r.fillCircle(px, py, half, paint)
		case .Rect:
			r.fillRect(px - half, py - half, p.size, p.size, paint)
		case .Star:
			inner := half * 0.45
			pts   := make([dynamic][2]f32, 0, 10, context.temp_allocator)
			step  := f32(math.PI) / 5.0
			for j in 0..<10 {
				a  := f32(j) * step + p.angle - math.PI * 0.5
				rv := inner if j % 2 == 1 else half
				append(&pts, [2]f32{px + math.cos(a) * rv, py + math.sin(a) * rv})
			}
			r.fillPolygon(pts[:], paint)
		}
	}

	if em.config.blend_mode != .Normal {
		r.resetBlendMode()
	}
}

// Gate 4.1: GPU-instanced draw for circle FX particles.
// Packs all active particles into a Particle_Instance slice and records a single
// Cmd_Draw_Particles_Instanced command, reducing N draw calls to 1.
g2d_fx_draw_instanced :: proc(em: ^FX_Emitter, ox: f32 = 0, oy: f32 = 0) {
	if em == nil do return
	r := renderer.active_renderer
	if r == nil || r.drawParticlesInstanced == nil do return

	// Count active particles.
	n := 0
	for i in 0..<len(em.particles) {
		if em.particles[i].active do n += 1
	}
	if n == 0 do return

	// Build instance slice in temp allocator (copied into draw list arena by record proc).
	// Apply the full matrix transform (same as apply_transform does for regular geometry):
	//   physical_pos = (matrix * local_pos) * dpi_scale
	// For radius: scale_x component × dpi_scale (assumes uniform scale, no rotation).
	scale := ctx.curr_matrix[0, 0] * ctx.dpi_scale
	insts := make([]renderer.Particle_Instance, n, context.temp_allocator)
	ii := 0
	for i in 0..<len(em.particles) {
		p := &em.particles[i]
		if !p.active do continue
		center := apply_transform(ox + p.pos.x, oy + p.pos.y)
		insts[ii] = renderer.Particle_Instance{
			pos_size = {center.x, center.y, p.size * 0.5 * scale},
			color    = p.color,
		}
		ii += 1
	}

	r.drawParticlesInstanced(insts, em.config.blend_mode, .Content)
}

// ============================================================================

DEBUG: Source content: package graphics

import "base:intrinsics"
import "core:bytes"
import "core:encoding/endian"
import "core:fmt"
import "core:io"
import "core:math"
import "core:math/linalg"
import "core:mem"
import "core:slice"
import logging "project:logging"
import "base:runtime"

/**
@documentation: RSD/Encoder
- Implementation of the RSD serialization.
- Handles chunk emission, alignment, and model-to-binary mapping.
- Conservative memory usage via io.Writer.
*/

// is_valid_transform is defined in decoder.odin to avoid duplication

encode_drawing :: proc(d: ^Drawing, allocator := context.allocator) -> ([]u8, bool) {
	buf: bytes.Buffer
	bytes.buffer_init_allocator(&buf, 0, 1024, allocator)
	defer bytes.buffer_destroy(&buf)

	w := bytes.buffer_to_stream(&buf)
	if !encode_to_stream(d, w) {
		return nil, false
	}

	return slice.clone(bytes.buffer_to_bytes(&buf), allocator), true
}

/**
@documentation: Save Drawing to RSD File
- Encodes a Drawing and writes it to the specified filesystem path
- Returns true on success
*/
save_rsd_to_file :: proc(d: ^Drawing, path: string) -> bool {
	data, ok := encode_drawing(d, context.temp_allocator)
	if !ok do return false
	return rsd_write_file(path, data)
}

/**
@documentation: Destroy Drawing
- Frees all resources allocated by a Drawing
- Call this after encode_drawing or decode_bytes to avoid memory leaks
*/
destroy_drawing :: proc(d: ^Drawing) {
	if d == nil do return

	for &layer in d.layers {
		for item in layer.items {
			destroy_item(item)
		}
		delete(layer.items)
		delete(layer.name)
	}
	delete(d.layers)

	for grad in d.gradients {
		delete(grad.stops)
	}
	delete(d.gradients)

	for blob in d.images {
		delete(blob.data)
	}
	delete(d.images)

	for meta in d.meta {
		delete(meta.key)
		delete(meta.value)
	}
	delete(d.meta)

	free(d)
}

destroy_item :: proc(item: Item) {
	switch i in item {
	case Path_Item:
		for cmd in i.commands {
			// No dynamic allocation for commands
		}
		delete(i.commands)
		for pt in i.points {
			// No dynamic allocation for points
		}
		delete(i.points)
		delete(i.stroke.dash_array)
	case Text_Item:
		delete(i.text)
		delete(i.font.name)
	case Group_Item:
		for child in i.items {
			destroy_item(child)
		}
		delete(i.items)
	case Image_Item:
	// No dynamic allocation for image items
	}
}

encode_to_stream :: proc(d: ^Drawing, w: io.Writer) -> bool {
	// Phase 10D.3.2: Transform caching to avoid duplicates
	transform_cache := make(map[u32]matrix[3, 3]f32, context.temp_allocator)

	// 1. File Header
	header := File_Header {
		magic    = [4]u8{'R', 'S', 'D', '0'},
		version  = VERSION_V1,
		flags    = u16(File_Flags.Little_Endian),
		reserved = 0,
	}
	if !write_struct(w, header) do return false

	// 2. HEAD Chunk
	head_data := Chunk_Head {
		version    = VERSION_V1,
		flags      = 0,
		units      = u8(d.units),
		dpi        = d.dpi,
		width      = d.width,
		height     = d.height,
		background = d.background,
		view_box   = d.view_box,
	}
	if !write_chunk(w, CHUNK_ID_HEAD, head_data) do return false

	// Phase 10D.3.2: Encode transform stack if present
	if len(transform_cache) > 0 {
		// Create transform stack chunk
		tstk_schema := Chunk_Tstk {
			transform_count = u16(len(transform_cache)),
			current_index   = 0, // Default to first transform
		}

		tstk_payload: bytes.Buffer
		// Phase 6.1: Use heap allocator for file I/O operations
		bytes.buffer_init_allocator(&tstk_payload, 0, 128, runtime.default_allocator())
		tstk_pw := bytes.buffer_to_stream(&tstk_payload)

		if !write_struct(tstk_pw, tstk_schema) do return false

		// Write all cached transforms
		for _, transform in transform_cache {
			mat_schema := Chunk_Matr {
				m = transmute([3][3]f32)transform,
			}
			if !write_struct(tstk_pw, mat_schema) do return false
		}

		if !write_chunk_bytes(w, CHUNK_ID_TSTK, bytes.buffer_to_bytes(&tstk_payload)) do return false
	}

	// 3. GRAD Chunks (Phase 10D.3.1: Extended with transforms and focal points)
	// Note: Removed gradient caching as it was causing ID reference issues
	// Gradient IDs are unique and serve as the primary key for gradient lookup

	if len(d.gradients) == 0 {
		logging.warn(
			.RSD,
			"[RSD_ENCODE] WARNING: No gradients to encode - drawing may have no fills",
		)
	}

	logging.debug(.RSD, fmt.tprintf("[RSD_ENCODE] Encoding %d gradients", len(d.gradients)))

	for grad in d.gradients {
		logging.debug(
			.RSD,
			fmt.tprintf(
				"[RSD_ENCODE] Encoding gradient id=%d with %d stops",
				grad.id,
				len(grad.stops),
			),
		)

		identity := linalg.MATRIX3F32_IDENTITY
		has_transform := 0
		if grad.transform != identity {
			has_transform = 1
		}

		schema := Chunk_Grad {
			id            = grad.id,
			type          = u8(grad.type),
			units         = u8(grad.units),
			stops_count   = u16(len(grad.stops)),
			p0            = grad.p0,
			p1            = grad.p1,
			fx            = grad.fx, // Phase 10D.3.1: Focal point for radial gradients
			fy            = grad.fy, // Phase 10D.3.1: Focal point for radial gradients
			has_transform = u8(has_transform),
		}

		// Payload: Schema + optional transform + array of (t:f32, color:[4]u8)
		payload: bytes.Buffer
		// Phase 6.1: Use heap allocator for file I/O operations
		bytes.buffer_init_allocator(&payload, 0, 128, runtime.default_allocator())
		pw := bytes.buffer_to_stream(&payload)

		if !write_struct(pw, schema) do return false

		// Phase 10D.3.1: Write transform matrix if present
		if has_transform == 1 {
			for i in 0 ..< 3 {
				for j in 0 ..< 3 {
					if !write_f32(pw, grad.transform[i][j]) do return false
				}
			}
		}

		// Write gradient stops
		for stop in grad.stops {
			if !write_f32(pw, stop.t) do return false
			if !write_color(pw, stop.color) do return false
		}

		if !write_chunk_bytes(w, CHUNK_ID_GRAD, bytes.buffer_to_bytes(&payload)) do return false
	}

	// 4. LAYR Chunks
	for &layer in d.layers {
		schema := Chunk_Layr {
			name_len = u16(len(layer.name)),
			visible  = u8(layer.visible ? 1 : 0),
			opacity  = layer.opacity,
		}

		payload: bytes.Buffer
		// Phase 6.1: Use heap allocator for file I/O operations
		bytes.buffer_init_allocator(&payload, 0, 256, runtime.default_allocator())
		pw := bytes.buffer_to_stream(&payload)

		if !write_struct(pw, schema) do return false
		if _, err := io.write_string(pw, layer.name); err != nil do return false
		// Align to 4-byte boundary after name
		if over := bytes.buffer_length(&payload) % 4; over != 0 {
			pad := 4 - over
			for _ in 0 ..< pad {
				if !write_u8(pw, 0) do return false
			}
		}

		// Phase 10D.3.2: Transform caching and validation
		if layer.transform != 1 {
			// Validate transform
			if !is_valid_transform(layer.transform) {
				fmt.eprintln("Warning: Invalid transform in layer", layer.name, "- using identity")
				layer.transform = linalg.MATRIX3F32_IDENTITY
			}

			// Check if transform is already cached
			transform_id := u32(0)
			for id, cached_transform in transform_cache {
				if cached_transform == layer.transform {
					transform_id = id
					break
				}
			}

			if transform_id == 0 {
				// Add to cache with new ID
				transform_id = u32(len(transform_cache)) + 1
				transform_cache[transform_id] = layer.transform
			}

			mat_schema := Chunk_Matr {
				m = transmute([3][3]f32)layer.transform,
			}
			if !write_chunk_to_payload(pw, CHUNK_ID_MATR, mat_schema) do return false
		}

		// Layer Items
		for item in layer.items {
			if !write_item(d, pw, item) do return false
		}

		if !write_chunk_bytes(w, CHUNK_ID_LAYR, bytes.buffer_to_bytes(&payload)) do return false
	}

	// 5. META Chunks
	for m in d.meta {
		schema := Chunk_Meta {
			key_len   = u16(len(m.key)),
			value_len = u16(len(m.value)),
		}
		payload: bytes.Buffer
		bytes.buffer_init_allocator(&payload, 0, 64, context.temp_allocator)
		pw := bytes.buffer_to_stream(&payload)
		if !write_struct(pw, schema) do return false
		if _, err := io.write_string(pw, m.key); err != nil do return false
		if _, err := io.write_string(pw, m.value); err != nil do return false

		if !write_chunk_bytes(w, CHUNK_ID_META, bytes.buffer_to_bytes(&payload)) do return false
	}

	// 6. CDEF Chunks (Clip Definitions)
	for &cp in d.clip_paths {
		schema := Chunk_Cdef {
			id = cp.id,
		}
		payload: bytes.Buffer
		bytes.buffer_init_allocator(&payload, 0, 256, context.temp_allocator)
		pw := bytes.buffer_to_stream(&payload)
		if !write_struct(pw, schema) do return false
		for item in cp.items {
			if !write_item(d, pw, item) do return false
		}
		if !write_chunk_bytes(w, CHUNK_ID_CDEF, bytes.buffer_to_bytes(&payload)) do return false
	}

	// 7. MDEF Chunks (Mask Definitions)
	for &m in d.masks {
		schema := Chunk_Mdef {
			id = m.id,
		}
		payload: bytes.Buffer
		bytes.buffer_init_allocator(&payload, 0, 256, context.temp_allocator)
		pw := bytes.buffer_to_stream(&payload)
		if !write_struct(pw, schema) do return false
		for item in m.items {
			if !write_item(d, pw, item) do return false
		}
		if !write_chunk_bytes(w, CHUNK_ID_MDEF, bytes.buffer_to_bytes(&payload)) do return false
	}

	return true
}

// --- Helpers ---

write_item :: proc(d: ^Drawing, w: io.Writer, item: Item) -> bool {
	switch v in item {
	case Path_Item:
		schema := Chunk_Path {
			fill_rule = u8(v.fill_rule),
			n_cmd     = u32(len(v.commands)),
			n_pt      = u32(len(v.points)),
		}
		
		// Validate path data consistency
		if len(v.commands) > 0 && len(v.points) == 0 {
			when logging.DEBUG_RSD {
				logging.error(
					.RSD,
					fmt.tprintf("PATH WARNING: %d commands but 0 points", len(v.commands)),
					key = "rsd_path_validation",
				)
			}
		}
		payload: bytes.Buffer
		bytes.buffer_init_allocator(&payload, 0, 256, context.temp_allocator)
		pw := bytes.buffer_to_stream(&payload)

		if !write_struct(pw, schema) do return false

		// Command stream (bytes)
		for cmd in v.commands {
			if !write_u8(pw, u8(cmd)) do return false
		}
		// Align to 4-byte boundary before points
		if over := bytes.buffer_length(&payload) % 4; over != 0 {
			pad := 4 - over
			for _ in 0 ..< pad {
				if !write_u8(pw, 0) do return false
			}
		}

		// Point stream (f32)
		for pt in v.points {
			if !write_f32(pw, pt.x) || !write_f32(pw, pt.y) do return false
		}

		// STYL
		// STYL
		styl_payload: bytes.Buffer
		bytes.buffer_init_allocator(&styl_payload, 0, 128, context.temp_allocator)
		sw := bytes.buffer_to_stream(&styl_payload)

		styl := Chunk_Styl {
			stroke_rgba  = f32_to_rgba8(v.stroke.color),
			stroke_width = v.stroke.width,
			join         = u8(v.stroke.join),
			cap          = u8(v.stroke.cap),
			miter_limit  = v.stroke.miter_limit,
			dash_count   = u8(len(v.stroke.dash_array)),
			dash_offset  = v.stroke.dash_offset,
		}

		switch f in v.fill {
		case Solid_Color:
			styl.fill_kind = 1
			styl.fill_rgba = f32_to_rgba8(f)
		case Gradient_Ref:
			styl.fill_kind = 2
			styl.fill_grad_id = f.id
		case Theme_Ref:
			// Phase 10C2.5: Theme color reference
			styl.fill_kind = 3
		// Note: fill_rgba and fill_grad_id not used for Theme_Ref
		case Theme_Gradient_Ref:
			// Phase 10C2.5: Theme gradient reference
			styl.fill_kind = 3
		// Note: fill_rgba and fill_grad_id not used for Theme_Gradient_Ref
		case None:
			styl.fill_kind = 0
		}

		if !write_struct(sw, styl) do return false
		for dash in v.stroke.dash_array {
			if !write_f32(sw, dash) do return false
		}

		// Phase 10C2.5: Write variable-length Theme_Ref/Theme_Gradient_Ref data after fixed header
		// Format: [marker: u8][token_len: u16][token: UTF-8][fallback...]
		// marker: 0 = Theme_Ref, 1 = Theme_Gradient_Ref
		if (styl.fill_kind == u8(Fill_Kind.Theme_Ref)) {
			switch tref in v.fill {
			case Theme_Ref:
				// Write marker to distinguish from Theme_Gradient_Ref
				if !write_u8(sw, 0) do return false
				// Write token string (u16 length + UTF-8 bytes)
				if !write_string(sw, tref.token) do return false
				// Write fallback color (RGBA)
				if !write_color(sw, tref.fallback) do return false
			case Theme_Gradient_Ref:
				// Write marker to distinguish from Theme_Ref
				if !write_u8(sw, 1) do return false
				// Write token string (u16 length + UTF-8 bytes)
				if !write_string(sw, tref.token) do return false
				// Write fallback gradient:
				// [type: u8][units: u8][id: u32][stops_count: u16][stops...]
				if !write_u8(sw, u8(tref.fallback.type)) do return false
				if !write_u8(sw, u8(tref.fallback.units)) do return false
				if !write_u32(sw, tref.fallback.id) do return false
				if !write_u16(sw, u16(len(tref.fallback.stops))) do return false
				for stop in tref.fallback.stops {
					if !write_f32(sw, stop.t) do return false
					if !write_color(sw, stop.color) do return false
				}
			case [4]f32, Gradient_Ref, None:
			// These cases are handled in the outer switch, no additional encoding needed
			// The data was already written as part of the fixed STYL header
			}
		}

		if !write_chunk_bytes(pw, CHUNK_ID_STYL, bytes.buffer_to_bytes(&styl_payload)) do return false

		// MATR
		if v.transform != 1 {
			mat_schema := Chunk_Matr {
				m = transmute([3][3]f32)v.transform,
			}
			if !write_chunk_to_payload(pw, CHUNK_ID_MATR, mat_schema) do return false
		}

		// CLPR
		if v.clip_path_id != 0 {
			ref := Chunk_Clip_Ref {
				id = v.clip_path_id,
			}
			if !write_chunk_to_payload(pw, CHUNK_ID_CLPR, ref) do return false
		}

		// MSKR
		if v.mask_id != 0 {
			ref := Chunk_Mask_Ref {
				id = v.mask_id,
			}
			if !write_chunk_to_payload(pw, CHUNK_ID_MSKR, ref) do return false
		}

		return write_chunk_bytes(w, CHUNK_ID_PATH, bytes.buffer_to_bytes(&payload))

	case Text_Item:
		schema := Chunk_Text {
			size        = v.font.size,
			color_rgba  = f32_to_rgba8(v.color),
			anchor      = u8(v.anchor),
			line_height = v.line_height,
			text_len    = u32(len(v.text)),
		}
		// TODO: font_ref handling

		payload: bytes.Buffer
		bytes.buffer_init_allocator(&payload, 0, 128, context.temp_allocator)
		pw := bytes.buffer_to_stream(&payload)

		if !write_struct(pw, schema) do return false
		if _, err := io.write_string(pw, v.text); err != nil do return false
		if !write_string(pw, v.font.name) do return false // Restore font name
		if v.transform != 1 {
			mat_schema := Chunk_Matr {
				m = transmute([3][3]f32)v.transform,
			}
			if !write_chunk_to_payload(pw, CHUNK_ID_MATR, mat_schema) do return false
		}

		// CLPR/MSKR for text
		if v.clip_path_id != 0 {
			ref := Chunk_Clip_Ref {
				id = v.clip_path_id,
			}
			if !write_chunk_to_payload(pw, CHUNK_ID_CLPR, ref) do return false
		}
		if v.mask_id != 0 {
			ref := Chunk_Mask_Ref {
				id = v.mask_id,
			}
			if !write_chunk_to_payload(pw, CHUNK_ID_MSKR, ref) do return false
		}

		return write_chunk_bytes(w, CHUNK_ID_TEXT, bytes.buffer_to_bytes(&payload))

	case Image_Item:
		schema := Chunk_Imag {
			w = u32(v.dest_rect.z),
			h = u32(v.dest_rect.w),
		}

		payload: bytes.Buffer
		bytes.buffer_init_allocator(&payload, 0, 1024, context.temp_allocator)
		pw := bytes.buffer_to_stream(&payload)

		switch s in v.source {
		case External_Path:
			schema.kind = 0
			if !write_struct(pw, schema) do return false
			if _, err := io.write_string(pw, string(s)); err != nil do return false
		case Embedded_Image:
			schema.kind = 1
			// Find image in Drawing.images
			blob: ^Image_Blob
			for &img in d.images {
				if img.id == s.id {
					blob = &img
					break
				}
			}

			if blob != nil {
				schema.fmt = blob.kind
				schema.w = blob.width
				schema.h = blob.height
				schema.data_len = u32(len(blob.data))
				if !write_struct(pw, schema) do return false
				if _, err := io.write(pw, blob.data); err != nil do return false
				// Align to 4-byte boundary
				if over := len(blob.data) % 4; over != 0 {
					pad := 4 - over
					for _ in 0 ..< pad {
						if !write_u8(pw, 0) do return false
					}
				}
			} else {
				// No data found, write empty schema
				if !write_struct(pw, schema) do return false
			}
		}

		if v.transform != 1 {
			mat_schema := Chunk_Matr {
				m = transmute([3][3]f32)v.transform,
			}
			if !write_chunk_to_payload(pw, CHUNK_ID_MATR, mat_schema) do return false
		}

		// CLPR/MSKR for image
		if v.clip_path_id != 0 {
			ref := Chunk_Clip_Ref {
				id = v.clip_path_id,
			}
			if !write_chunk_to_payload(pw, CHUNK_ID_CLPR, ref) do return false
		}
		if v.mask_id != 0 {
			ref := Chunk_Mask_Ref {
				id = v.mask_id,
			}
			if !write_chunk_to_payload(pw, CHUNK_ID_MSKR, ref) do return false
		}

		return write_chunk_bytes(w, CHUNK_ID_IMAG, bytes.buffer_to_bytes(&payload))

	case Group_Item:
		payload: bytes.Buffer
		bytes.buffer_init_allocator(&payload, 0, 256, context.temp_allocator)
		pw := bytes.buffer_to_stream(&payload)

		if v.transform != 1 {
			mat_schema := Chunk_Matr {
				m = transmute([3][3]f32)v.transform,
			}
			if !write_chunk_to_payload(pw, CHUNK_ID_MATR, mat_schema) do return false
		}

		for child in v.items {
			if !write_item(d, pw, child) do return false
		}

		// CLPR/MSKR for group
		if v.clip_path_id != 0 {
			ref := Chunk_Clip_Ref {
				id = v.clip_path_id,
			}
			if !write_chunk_to_payload(pw, CHUNK_ID_CLPR, ref) do return false
		}
		if v.mask_id != 0 {
			ref := Chunk_Mask_Ref {
				id = v.mask_id,
			}
			if !write_chunk_to_payload(pw, CHUNK_ID_MSKR, ref) do return false
		}

		// Groups use the GRUP chunk ID for recursive structure alignment
		return write_chunk_bytes(w, CHUNK_ID_GRUP, bytes.buffer_to_bytes(&payload))
	}
	return true
}

write_chunk :: proc(
	w: io.Writer,
	id: [4]u8,
	data: $T,
) -> bool where !intrinsics.type_is_pointer(T) {
	header := Chunk_Header {
		id     = id,
		length = u32(size_of(T)),
		crc    = 0, // TODO
	}
	if !write_struct(w, header) do return false
	if !write_struct(w, data) do return false

	// Pad to 4-byte boundary
	if over := size_of(T) % 4; over != 0 {
		pad := 4 - over
		for _ in 0 ..< pad {
			if !write_u8(w, 0) do return false
		}
	}
	return true
}

write_chunk_bytes :: proc(w: io.Writer, id: [4]u8, data: []u8) -> bool {
	header := Chunk_Header {
		id     = id,
		length = u32(len(data)),
		crc    = 0,
	}
	if !write_struct(w, header) do return false
	if _, err := io.write(w, data); err != nil do return false

	// Pad to 4-byte boundary
	if over := len(data) % 4; over != 0 {
		pad := 4 - over
		for _ in 0 ..< pad {
			if !write_u8(w, 0) do return false
		}
	}
	return true
}

write_chunk_to_payload :: proc(
	w: io.Writer,
	id: [4]u8,
	data: $T,
) -> bool where !intrinsics.type_is_pointer(T) {
	return write_chunk(w, id, data)
}

write_struct :: proc(w: io.Writer, data: $T) -> bool where !intrinsics.type_is_pointer(T) {
	b := mem.any_to_bytes(data)
	_, err := io.write(w, b)
	return err == nil
}

write_u8 :: proc(w: io.Writer, v: u8) -> bool {
	return io.write_byte(w, v) == nil
}

write_u16 :: proc(w: io.Writer, v: u16) -> bool {
	b: [2]u8
	endian.put_u16(b[:], .Little, v)
	_, err := io.write(w, b[:])
	return err == nil
}

write_u32 :: proc(w: io.Writer, v: u32) -> bool {
	b: [4]u8
	endian.put_u32(b[:], .Little, v)
	_, err := io.write(w, b[:])
	return err == nil
}

write_f32 :: proc(w: io.Writer, v: f32) -> bool {
	b: [4]u8
	endian.put_f32(b[:], .Little, v)
	_, err := io.write(w, b[:])
	return err == nil
}

write_string :: proc(w: io.Writer, s: string) -> bool {
	if !write_u16(w, u16(len(s))) do return false
	_, err := io.write_string(w, s)
	return err == nil
}

write_color :: proc(w: io.Writer, c: [4]f32) -> bool {
	rgba := f32_to_rgba8(c)
	_, err := io.write(w, rgba[:])
	return err == nil
}


f32_to_rgba8 :: proc(c: [4]f32) -> [4]u8 {
	return [4]u8 {
		u8(math.clamp(c.r * 255, 0, 255)),
		u8(math.clamp(c.g * 255, 0, 255)),
		u8(math.clamp(c.b * 255, 0, 255)),
		u8(math.clamp(c.a * 255, 0, 255)),
	}
}
