// engine/app.odin — Public App/Window lifecycle API.
// NO vendor-local imports allowed here.
// All Sokol-specific code lives in engine/app_sokol.odin (same package).

import platform "project:platform"

// ============================================================================
// Re-exports — callers only need to import "engine"
// ============================================================================

Event        :: platform.Input_Event
Event_Type   :: platform.Input_Event_Type
Key_Code     :: platform.Key_Code
Mouse_Button :: platform.Mouse_Button

MODIFIER_SHIFT :: platform.MODIFIER_SHIFT
MODIFIER_CTRL  :: platform.MODIFIER_CTRL
MODIFIER_ALT   :: platform.MODIFIER_ALT
MODIFIER_SUPER :: platform.MODIFIER_SUPER
MODIFIER_LMB   :: platform.MODIFIER_LMB
MODIFIER_RMB   :: platform.MODIFIER_RMB
MODIFIER_MMB   :: platform.MODIFIER_MMB

// ============================================================================
// Callback types
// ============================================================================

Init_Fn       :: #type proc()
Scene_Fn      :: #type proc()  // called after beginFrame, before flushGpuPrePass
Post_Flush_Fn :: #type proc()  // optional: blur / post-process passes
Draw_Fn       :: #type proc()  // called after beginScreenPass, before endFrame
Event_Fn      :: #type proc(ev: platform.Input_Event)
Cleanup_Fn    :: #type proc()

// ============================================================================
// Window_Config — passed once to new_window()
// ============================================================================

Window_Config :: struct {
	title:        cstring,
	width:        i32,
	height:       i32,
	clear_color:  [4]f32,
	sample_count: i32, // 1 = no MSAA, 4 = 4x
}

// ============================================================================
// Window — owns config + per-window callbacks
// ============================================================================

Window :: struct {
	app:           ^App,
	title:         cstring,
	width:         i32,
	height:        i32,
	clear_color:   [4]f32,
	sample_count:  i32,
	on_init:       Init_Fn,
	on_scene:      Scene_Fn,
	on_post_flush: Post_Flush_Fn, // optional — skip if nil
	on_draw:       Draw_Fn,
	on_event:      Event_Fn,
	on_cleanup:    Cleanup_Fn,
}

// ============================================================================
// App — top-level container; owns windows
// ============================================================================

App :: struct {
	windows:      [dynamic]^Window,
	_initialized: bool,
}

// ============================================================================
// Builder API
// ============================================================================

new_app :: proc() -> ^App {
	app := new(App)
	app.windows = make([dynamic]^Window)
	return app
}

new_window :: proc(app: ^App, cfg: Window_Config) -> ^Window {
	win := new(Window)
	win.app          = app
	win.title        = cfg.title
	win.width        = cfg.width
	win.height       = cfg.height
	win.clear_color  = cfg.clear_color
	win.sample_count = cfg.sample_count if cfg.sample_count > 0 else 1
	append(&app.windows, win)
	return win
}

set_init_fn       :: proc(win: ^Window, fn: Init_Fn)       { win.on_init       = fn }
set_scene_fn      :: proc(win: ^Window, fn: Scene_Fn)      { win.on_scene      = fn }
set_post_flush_fn :: proc(win: ^Window, fn: Post_Flush_Fn) { win.on_post_flush = fn }
set_draw_fn       :: proc(win: ^Window, fn: Draw_Fn)       { win.on_draw       = fn }
set_event_fn      :: proc(win: ^Window, fn: Event_Fn)      { win.on_event      = fn }
set_cleanup_fn    :: proc(win: ^Window, fn: Cleanup_Fn)    { win.on_cleanup    = fn }

// ============================================================================
// run — starts the app loop; calls _platform_run, never returns
// ============================================================================

run :: proc(app: ^App) {
	_platform_run(app)
}

// ============================================================================
// Frame / window query procs — delegate to _platform_* in app_sokol.odin
// ============================================================================

dpi_scale      :: proc() -> f32 { return _platform_dpi_scale() }
frame_duration :: proc() -> f64 { return _platform_frame_duration() }
frame_count    :: proc() -> u64 { return _platform_frame_count() }
widthf         :: proc() -> f32 { return _platform_widthf() }
heightf        :: proc() -> f32 { return _platform_heightf() }
width          :: proc() -> i32 { return _platform_width() }
height         :: proc() -> i32 { return _platform_height() }
request_quit   :: proc()        { _platform_request_quit() }

// beginScreenPass is kept public for any user code that manages its own pass structure.
beginScreenPass :: proc(clear_color: [4]f32) {
	_platform_begin_screen_pass(clear_color)
}

// engine/app_sokol.odin — Sokol sapp/glue/log backend seam.
//
// SEAM FILE: This is the ONLY file in src/ that may import sokol/app,
// sokol/glue, or sokol/log. All sapp API calls are encapsulated here.
// Sokol GFX (sg.*) stays in graphics/ where it belongs, EXCEPT for
// setup/shutdown and beginPass/endPass which are engine lifecycle concerns.
//
// Implements all _platform_* procs used in engine/app.odin.

import "base:runtime"
import "core:fmt"
import sapp "vendor-local:sokol-odin/sokol/app"
import sg "vendor-local:sokol-odin/sokol/gfx"
import sglue "vendor-local:sokol-odin/sokol/glue"
import slog "vendor-local:sokol-odin/sokol/log"

import gfx "project:graphics"      // make_g2d, setDpiScale, g3d init, shutdown
import logging "project:logging"
import platform "project:platform"
import renderer "project:renderer"

// ============================================================================
// Internal state
// ============================================================================

@(private = "file")
_app: ^App

// On WASM, store the context set up in main (with emscripten allocator)
// so all proc "c" callbacks can restore it instead of using default_context().
when ODIN_ARCH == .wasm32 || ODIN_ARCH == .wasm64p32 {
	_wasm_context: runtime.Context
	set_wasm_context :: proc(ctx: runtime.Context) { _wasm_context = ctx }
}

// ============================================================================
// _platform_run — store app, start sapp loop, never returns
// ============================================================================

_platform_run :: proc(app: ^App) {
	_app = app
	assert(len(app.windows) > 0, "engine.run: no windows — call new_window() first")
	win := app.windows[0]

	sapp.run(
		{
			init_cb    = _sokol_init,
			frame_cb   = _sokol_frame,
			event_cb   = _sokol_event,
			cleanup_cb = _sokol_cleanup,
			width        = win.width,
			height       = win.height,
			window_title = win.title,
			high_dpi     = true,
			sample_count = win.sample_count,
			logger       = {func = slog.func},
		},
	)
}

// ============================================================================
// WASM entry — called from main_wasm.odin's sokol_main export.
// Sets _app so _sokol_init can use it, and returns the sapp.Desc.
// ============================================================================

when ODIN_ARCH == .wasm32 || ODIN_ARCH == .wasm64p32 {
	wasm_prepare :: proc(app: ^App) -> sapp.Desc {
		_app = app
		assert(len(app.windows) > 0, "engine.wasm_prepare: no windows")
		win := app.windows[0]
		_wasm_context = context
		return sapp.Desc{
			init_cb      = _sokol_init,
			frame_cb     = _sokol_frame,
			event_cb     = _sokol_event,
			cleanup_cb   = _sokol_cleanup,
			width        = win.width,
			height       = win.height,
			window_title = win.title,
			high_dpi     = false, // disable on WASM: avoid huge framebuffers on high-DPR displays
			sample_count = 1,     // disable MSAA on WASM: saves GPU memory
			logger       = {func = slog.func},
			html5        = {canvas_selector = "#canvas", canvas_resize = true},
		}
	}
}

// ============================================================================
// Sokol C callbacks
// ============================================================================

@(private = "file")
_sokol_init :: proc "c" () {
	when ODIN_ARCH == .wasm32 || ODIN_ARCH == .wasm64p32 {
		context = _wasm_context
		fmt.println("[engine] _sokol_init called")
	} else {
		context = runtime.default_context()
	}

	// 1. GPU backend
	sg.setup(
		{
			environment        = sglue.environment(),
			logger             = {func = slog.func},
			image_pool_size    = 512,
			view_pool_size     = 1024,
			pipeline_pool_size = 128,
		},
	)

	// 2. 2D renderer subsystem
	gfx.make_g2d()
	renderer.g2d_set_logging(.None)
	renderer.active_renderer.setup()

	// 3. Asset manager (default config)
	if !renderer.active_renderer.initAssetManager(
		renderer.active_renderer.defaultAssetManagerConfig(),
	) {
		logging.error(.Lifecycle, "[Engine] Asset manager initialisation failed")
	}

	// 4. 3D subsystem
	gfx.init() // g3d primitives init

	// 5. User app init (fonts, assets, scene graph)
	_app._initialized = true
	for win in _app.windows {
		if win.on_init != nil {
			win.on_init()
		}
	}
}

@(private = "file")
_sokol_frame :: proc "c" () {
	when ODIN_ARCH == .wasm32 || ODIN_ARCH == .wasm64p32 {
		context = _wasm_context
	} else {
		context = runtime.default_context()
	}
	defer free_all(context.temp_allocator)

	if !_app._initialized {return}

	dpi := sapp.dpi_scale()
	gfx.setDpiScale(dpi)

	w := sapp.widthf()
	h := sapp.heightf()

	renderer.active_renderer.beginFrame(w, h)

	// Engine owns the renderer arena lifetime for the frame.
	// Set it as temp_allocator so all callbacks inherit it implicitly.
	context.temp_allocator = renderer.active_renderer.getArenaAllocator()

	for win in _app.windows {
		// --- 2D deferred scene rendering + offscreen RT work ---
		if win.on_scene != nil {
			win.on_scene()
		}

		// --- Flush 2D deferred commands to GPU ---
		renderer.active_renderer.flushGpuPrePass()

		// --- Optional post-flush: blur / RT post-processing ---
		if win.on_post_flush != nil {
			win.on_post_flush()
		}

		// --- Main screen pass ---
		_platform_begin_screen_pass(win.clear_color)

		// --- Immediate / 3D draw ---
		if win.on_draw != nil {
			win.on_draw()
		}

		renderer.active_renderer.endFrame()
		renderer.active_renderer.endPass()
	}

	sg.commit()

	// Arena is safe to reset now — all GPU commands are submitted.
	free_all(renderer.active_renderer.getArenaAllocator())
}

@(private = "file")
_sokol_event :: proc "c" (ev: ^sapp.Event) {
	when ODIN_ARCH == .wasm32 || ODIN_ARCH == .wasm64p32 {
		context = _wasm_context
	} else {
		context = runtime.default_context()
	}
	ie := _convert_sapp_event(ev)
	platform.updateInputState(
		cast(^platform.Input_State)renderer.active_renderer.getInputState(),
		ie,
	)
	for win in _app.windows {
		if win.on_event != nil {
			win.on_event(ie)
		}
	}
}

@(private = "file")
_sokol_cleanup :: proc "c" () {
	when ODIN_ARCH == .wasm32 || ODIN_ARCH == .wasm64p32 {
		context = _wasm_context
	} else {
		context = runtime.default_context()
	}

	// 1. User cleanup first (frees images, scene nodes, etc.)
	for win in _app.windows {
		if win.on_cleanup != nil {
			win.on_cleanup()
		}
	}

	// 2. Engine subsystem teardown (reverse of init order)
	renderer.active_renderer.shutdown()         // calls g2d_shutdown() via vtable
	renderer.active_renderer.shutdownAssetManager()

	// 3. GPU backend last
	sg.shutdown()
}

// ============================================================================
// _platform_* query procs
// ============================================================================

_platform_dpi_scale      :: proc() -> f32  { return sapp.dpi_scale() }
_platform_frame_duration :: proc() -> f64  { return sapp.frame_duration() }
_platform_frame_count    :: proc() -> u64  { return u64(sapp.frame_count()) }
_platform_widthf         :: proc() -> f32  { return sapp.widthf() }
_platform_heightf        :: proc() -> f32  { return sapp.heightf() }
_platform_width          :: proc() -> i32  { return sapp.width() }
_platform_height         :: proc() -> i32  { return sapp.height() }
_platform_request_quit   :: proc()         { sapp.request_quit() }

_platform_begin_screen_pass :: proc(clear_color: [4]f32) {
	pa := sg.Pass_Action{}
	pa.colors[0] = {
		load_action = .CLEAR,
		clear_value = {clear_color[0], clear_color[1], clear_color[2], clear_color[3]},
	}
	pa.depth   = {load_action = .CLEAR, clear_value = 1.0}
	pa.stencil = {load_action = .CLEAR, store_action = .DONTCARE, clear_value = 0}
	renderer.active_renderer.beginPass(
		sg.Pass{action = pa, swapchain = sglue.swapchain()},
		renderer.Renderer_Pass_Type.Screen,
	)
	vp_w := sapp.width()
	vp_h := sapp.height()
	sg.apply_viewport(0, 0, vp_w, vp_h, true)
	sg.apply_scissor_rect(0, 0, vp_w, vp_h, false)
}

// ============================================================================
// _convert_sapp_event — maps sapp.Event → platform.Input_Event
// Key_Code numeric values match sapp.Keycode (GLFW-based): simple cast.
// ============================================================================

@(private = "file")
_convert_sapp_event :: proc(ev: ^sapp.Event) -> platform.Input_Event {
	ie := platform.Input_Event{}
	dpi := sapp.dpi_scale()
	inv_dpi := 1.0 / dpi

	switch ev.type {
	case .KEY_DOWN:           ie.type = .Key_Down
	case .KEY_UP:             ie.type = .Key_Up
	case .CHAR:               ie.type = .Text_Input
	case .MOUSE_DOWN:         ie.type = .Mouse_Down
	case .MOUSE_UP:           ie.type = .Mouse_Up
	case .MOUSE_SCROLL:       ie.type = .Mouse_Scroll
	case .MOUSE_MOVE:         ie.type = .Mouse_Move
	case .MOUSE_ENTER:        ie.type = .Mouse_Enter
	case .MOUSE_LEAVE:        ie.type = .Mouse_Leave
	case .TOUCHES_BEGAN:      ie.type = .Touch_Begin
	case .TOUCHES_MOVED:      ie.type = .Touch_Move
	case .TOUCHES_ENDED:      ie.type = .Touch_End
	case .TOUCHES_CANCELLED:  ie.type = .Touch_Cancel
	case .RESIZED:            ie.type = .Window_Resized
	case .FOCUSED:            ie.type = .Window_Focused
	case .UNFOCUSED:          ie.type = .Window_Unfocused
	case .ICONIFIED:          ie.type = .Window_Minimized
	case .RESTORED:           ie.type = .Window_Restored
	case .SUSPENDED:          ie.type = .App_Suspended
	case .RESUMED:            ie.type = .App_Resumed
	case .QUIT_REQUESTED:     ie.type = .Quit_Requested
	case .FILES_DROPPED:      ie.type = .Files_Dropped
	case .CLIPBOARD_PASTED:   ie.type = .Clipboard_Pasted
	case .INVALID:            ie.type = .Invalid
	}

	ie.key    = platform.Key_Code(ev.key_code)
	ie.char   = rune(ev.char_code)

	switch ev.mouse_button {
	case .LEFT:    ie.button = .Left
	case .RIGHT:   ie.button = .Right
	case .MIDDLE:  ie.button = .Middle
	case .INVALID: // leave as zero
	}

	ie.pos    = {ev.mouse_x * inv_dpi, ev.mouse_y * inv_dpi}
	ie.x      = ie.pos[0]
	ie.y      = ie.pos[1]
	ie.delta  = {ev.mouse_dx * inv_dpi, ev.mouse_dy * inv_dpi}
	ie.scroll = {ev.scroll_x, ev.scroll_y}
	ie.modifiers = ev.modifiers
	ie.repeat    = ev.key_repeat
	ie.window_size = {f32(sapp.width()) * inv_dpi, f32(sapp.height()) * inv_dpi}

	ie.num_touches = int(ev.num_touches)
	if ev.num_touches > 0 {
		ie.touch_id = uintptr(ev.touches[0].identifier)
		ie.pos      = {ev.touches[0].pos_x * inv_dpi, ev.touches[0].pos_y * inv_dpi}
		ie.x        = ie.pos[0]
		ie.y        = ie.pos[1]
	}

	return ie
}

DEBUG: Source content: package engine

import fmt "core:fmt"
import "core:math"
import "core:math/linalg"
import renderer "project:renderer"
import platform "project:platform"


Transformer :: struct {
	using node:     Node,
	target:         ^Node,
	active_handle:  int, // -1 for none, 0-7 for corners/edges, 8 for rotate
	hovered_handle: int, // -1 none; 0..3 when cursor over a corner handle
	pressed_handle: int, // -1 none; 0..3 when pressed on a corner handle
	dragging:       bool,
	last_mouse:     [2]f32,
}

TRANSFORMER_HANDLE_SIZE :: 8.0

transformerCreate :: proc(target: ^Node, name := "Transformer") -> ^Transformer {
	t := new(Transformer)
	t.name = name
	t.target = target
	t.children = make([dynamic]^Node)
	t.transform = 1
	t.global_transform = 1
	t.is_visible = true
	t.is_active = true
	t.hovered_handle = -1
	t.pressed_handle = -1
	t.hit_type = .Rect
	t.vtable = &transformer_vtable
	return t
}

@(private)
transformerUpdate :: proc(node: ^Node, dt: f32) {
	t := cast(^Transformer)node
	if t.target == nil do return

	// Sync transformer hit-test area to target's LOCAL bounds within the PARENT (the tile)
	r := t.target.bounds
	m := t.target.transform // Local transform!

	// Transform corners to screen space to find the bounding AABB for the Transformer node
	p0 := m * linalg.Vector4f32{r.x, r.y, 0, 1}
	p1 := m * linalg.Vector4f32{r.x + r.w, r.y, 0, 1}
	p2 := m * linalg.Vector4f32{r.x + r.w, r.y + r.h, 0, 1}
	p3 := m * linalg.Vector4f32{r.x, r.y + r.h, 0, 1}

	x0 := math.min(p0.x, math.min(p1.x, math.min(p2.x, p3.x)))
	x1 := math.max(p0.x, math.max(p1.x, math.max(p2.x, p3.x)))
	y0 := math.min(p0.y, math.min(p1.y, math.min(p2.y, p3.y)))
	y1 := math.max(p0.y, math.max(p1.y, math.max(p2.y, p3.y)))

	// Transformer coordinates are local to the target's PARENT (the tile)
	node.transform = 1 // Identity
	node.bounds = platform.Rect{x0 - 10, y0 - 10, (x1 - x0) + 20, (y1 - y0) + 20}
}

@(private)
transformerDraw :: proc(node: ^Node) {
	t := cast(^Transformer)node
	if t.target == nil || !t.is_visible do return
	rend := renderer.active_renderer
	if rend == nil do return

	missing_any :=
		(rend^.pushMatrix == nil) ||
		(rend^.identityMatrix == nil) ||
		(rend^.popMatrix == nil) ||
		(rend^.drawLine == nil) ||
		(rend^.fillRect == nil)
	if missing_any do return
	rend^.pushMatrix()
	rend^.identityMatrix()

	// Draw bounding box and handles
	// This uses the target's global transform to find the corners
	r := t.target.bounds
	m := t.target.global_transform

	// Standard 2D quad corners in local space
	p0 := linalg.Vector4f32{r.x, r.y, 0, 1}
	p1 := linalg.Vector4f32{r.x + r.w, r.y, 0, 1}
	p2 := linalg.Vector4f32{r.x + r.w, r.y + r.h, 0, 1}
	p3 := linalg.Vector4f32{r.x, r.y + r.h, 0, 1}

	// Transform to screen space
	tp0 := m * p0
	tp1 := m * p1
	tp2 := m * p2
	tp3 := m * p3

	// Draw lines
	rend^.drawLine(tp0.x, tp0.y, tp1.x, tp1.y, 1.0, {0, 0.5, 1, 1})
	rend^.drawLine(tp1.x, tp1.y, tp2.x, tp2.y, 1.0, {0, 0.5, 1, 1})
	rend^.drawLine(tp2.x, tp2.y, tp3.x, tp3.y, 1.0, {0, 0.5, 1, 1})
	rend^.drawLine(tp3.x, tp3.y, tp0.x, tp0.y, 1.0, {0, 0.5, 1, 1})

	// Draw handles with hover/press feedback
	handle_col := [4]f32{1, 1, 1, 1}
	h_size: f32 = TRANSFORMER_HANDLE_SIZE

	// Corner points array
	pts := [4][2]f32{{tp0.x, tp0.y}, {tp1.x, tp1.y}, {tp2.x, tp2.y}, {tp3.x, tp3.y}}
	for i in 0 ..< 4 {
		px := pts[i][0]; py := pts[i][1]
		col := handle_col
		if t.pressed_handle == i {col = [4]f32{0.75, 0.75, 0.75, 1}}
		rend^.fillRect(
			px - h_size * 0.5,
			py - h_size * 0.5,
			h_size,
			h_size,
			renderer.paint_solid(col),
		)
		if t.hovered_handle == i && t.pressed_handle != i {
			rend^.drawRect(
				px - h_size * 0.5,
				py - h_size * 0.5,
				h_size,
				h_size,
				2,
				[4]f32{1, 0, 0, 1},
			)
		}
	}

	rend^.popMatrix()
}

transformerRenderWrapper :: proc(node: ^Node, ctx: rawptr) {
	transformerDraw(node)
}


@(private)
transformerHandleInput :: proc(node: ^Node, event: platform.Input_Event, phase: Event_Phase) -> bool {
	t := cast(^Transformer)node
	if t.target == nil do return false

	if phase == .Capture || phase == .Target {
		// Only begin dragging when a small square handle is clicked; otherwise pass through
		handle_hit :: proc(t: ^Transformer, ev: platform.Input_Event) -> (bool, int) {
			// Convert screen mouse to local space of the transformer
			lx, ly := platform.screenToLocal(ev.x, ev.y, t.global_transform)

			r := t.target.bounds
			m := t.target.transform // Local to tile

			// Handles are at the corners of the target's LOCAL-to-parent box
			p0 := m * linalg.Vector4f32{r.x, r.y, 0, 1}
			p1 := m * linalg.Vector4f32{r.x + r.w, r.y, 0, 1}
			p2 := m * linalg.Vector4f32{r.x + r.w, r.y + r.h, 0, 1}
			p3 := m * linalg.Vector4f32{r.x, r.y + r.h, 0, 1}
			tp := [4]linalg.Vector4f32{p0, p1, p2, p3}

			hs: f32 = TRANSFORMER_HANDLE_SIZE
			for i in 0 ..< 4 {
				cx := tp[i].x; cy := tp[i].y
				if (math.abs(lx - cx) <= hs * 0.5) && (math.abs(ly - cy) <= hs * 0.5) {
					return true, i
				}
			}
			return false, -1
		}

		#partial switch event.type {
		case .Mouse_Leave:
			t.hovered_handle = -1
		case .Mouse_Down:
			ok, idx := handle_hit(t, event)
			if ok {
				t.pressed_handle = idx
				t.dragging = true
				t.active_handle = idx
				t.last_mouse = event.pos
				return true
			}
		case .Mouse_Move:
			if t.dragging {

				delta := event.pos - t.last_mouse
				t.last_mouse = event.pos
				move_m := linalg.matrix4_translate_f32({delta.x, delta.y, 0})
				t.target.transform = move_m * t.target.transform
				return true
			} else {
				ok, idx := handle_hit(t, event)
				if ok {t.hovered_handle = idx} else {t.hovered_handle = -1}
			}
		case .Mouse_Up, .Touch_End, .Touch_Cancel:
			if t.dragging {
				t.dragging = false
				t.pressed_handle = -1
				return true
			}
			// clear press even if not dragging
			t.pressed_handle = -1
		}
	}
	return false
}


transformer_vtable := Node_VTable {
	update       = transformerUpdate,
	render       = transformerRenderWrapper,
	handle_input = transformerHandleInput,
}

package logging

// WASM: no file I/O, no env vars — all logging goes to console only.
Log_File_Handle :: rawptr

log_get_env :: proc(key: string) -> string { return "" }

log_file_open  :: proc(path: string) -> Log_File_Handle { return nil }
log_file_close :: proc(h: Log_File_Handle) {}
log_file_write :: proc(h: Log_File_Handle, s: string) {}

DEBUG: Source content: package logging

import "core:fmt"
import "core:time"

// logging_setup.odin - Setup and configuration patterns for RuiShin logging system
// the "wiring" of the logging system

// ============================================================================
// BASIC SETUP FUNCTIONS
// ============================================================================

// Initialize logging with default configuration
init_default :: proc() {
	init()
	
	fmt.println("[Logging] init_default called")
	fmt.println("[Logging] ENABLE_RUNTIME_DEBUG_LOGS:", ENABLE_RUNTIME_DEBUG_LOGS)
	
	_init_setup()  // Common setup for both default and file logging	

} //end init_default

// Initialize logging with file output
init_with_file :: proc(filename: string) {
	init()
	fmt.println("[Logging] init_with_file called with filename:", filename)	
	_init_setup()  // Common setup for both default and file logging	^

} //end init_with_file

// Common setup for both default and file logging
_init_setup :: proc() {

	if DEBUG {
		fmt.println("[Logging] DEBUG mode enabled")
		fmt.println("[Logging] STRESS_TEST:", STRESS_TEST)
	} else {
		fmt.println("[Logging] Release mode enabled")
	}

	when DEBUG {
		// Default debug configuration - text rendering focus
		use_profile(.Minimal)

		when DEBUG_INIT {
			fmt.println("[Logging] DEBUG_INIT:", DEBUG_INIT)
        	enable_subsystem(.Lifecycle, .TRACE)
        	enable_subsystem(.Config, .TRACE)
    	}
    	when DEBUG_SVG {
			fmt.println("[Logging] DEBUG_SVG:", DEBUG_SVG)
        	enable_subsystem(.SVG, .TRACE)
    	}
    	when DEBUG_RSD {
			fmt.println("[Logging] DEBUG_RSD:", DEBUG_RSD)
        	enable_subsystem(.RSD, .TRACE)
    	}
    	when DEBUG_CLEANUP {
			fmt.println("[Logging] DEBUG_CLEANUP:", DEBUG_CLEANUP)
        	enable_subsystem(.Lifecycle, .DEBUG)
    	}
		when DEBUG_VERTICES {
			fmt.println("[Logging] DEBUG_VERTICES:", DEBUG_VERTICES)
			enable_subsystem(.G2D, .DEBUG)
		}	

		when DEBUG_OFFSCREEN {
			fmt.println("[Logging] DEBUG_OFFSCREEN:", DEBUG_OFFSCREEN)
			// enable_subsystem(.G2D, .DEBUG)
			enable_subsystem(.Lifecycle, .DEBUG)
		}

	} //end when DEBUG

	// Performance flags - enable performance logging for specific subsystems
	when PERF_RSD {
		fmt.println("[Logging] PERF_RSD:", PERF_RSD)
		enable_subsystem(.RSD, .INFO)
		enable_subsystem(.Performance, .INFO)
		fmt.println("[Logging] Performance logging enabled for RSD")
	}
	when PERF_SVG {
		fmt.println("[Logging] PERF_SVG:", PERF_SVG)
		enable_subsystem(.SVG, .INFO)
		enable_subsystem(.Performance, .INFO)
	}
	when PERF_G2D {
		fmt.println("[Logging] PERF_G2D:", PERF_G2D)
		enable_subsystem(.G2D, .INFO)
		enable_subsystem(.Performance, .INFO)
	}
	when PERF_ATLAS {
		fmt.println("[Logging] PERF_ATLAS:", PERF_ATLAS)
		enable_subsystem(.Atlas, .INFO)
		enable_subsystem(.Performance, .INFO)
	}
	when PERF_RENDERER {
		fmt.println("[Logging] PERF_RENDERER:", PERF_RENDERER)
		enable_subsystem(.Renderer, .INFO)
		enable_subsystem(.Performance, .INFO)
	}
	when PERF_SHAPING {
		fmt.println("[Logging] PERF_SHAPING:", PERF_SHAPING)
		enable_subsystem(.Shaping, .INFO)
		enable_subsystem(.Performance, .INFO)
	} //end when PERF_SHAPING

	when PERF_STATS {
		fmt.println("[Logging] PERF_STATS:", PERF_STATS)
		//enable_subsystem(.G2D, .INFO)
		enable_subsystem(.Lifecycle, .INFO)
	} //end when PERF_STATS
	
} //end _init_setup


// ============================================================================
// PREDEFINED CONFIGURATION PROFILES
// ============================================================================

// Configure for text rendering debugging
configure_text_rendering :: proc() {
	when DEBUG {
		use_profile(.Text_Rendering)
		
		// Fine-tune atlas configuration for cache debugging
		atlas_config := get_subsystem_config(.Atlas)
		atlas_config.mode = .COMPRESSED
		atlas_config.max_per_frame = 10
		atlas_config.show_count = true
		set_subsystem_config(.Atlas, atlas_config)
	}
}

// Configure for graphics debugging
configure_graphics :: proc() {
	when DEBUG {
		use_profile(.Graphics)
		
		// Enable detailed batching info
		batching_config := get_subsystem_config(.Batching)
		batching_config.mode = .AGGREGATED
		batching_config.aggregate_frame = true
		set_subsystem_config(.Batching, batching_config)
	}
}

// Configure for performance profiling
configure_performance :: proc() {
	when DEBUG {
		use_profile(.Performance)
		
		// High-frequency performance metrics
		perf_config := get_subsystem_config(.Performance)
		perf_config.mode = .AGGREGATED
		perf_config.aggregate_frame = true
		set_subsystem_config(.Performance, perf_config)
	}
}

// Configure for minimal logging (production-like)
configure_minimal :: proc() {
	when DEBUG {
		use_profile(.Minimal)
	}
}

// Configure full debug (everything)
configure_full_debug :: proc() {
	when DEBUG {
		use_profile(.Full_Debug)
	}
}

// ============================================================================
// CUSTOM CONFIGURATION EXAMPLES
// ============================================================================

// Custom configuration for font debugging
configure_font_debugging :: proc() {
	when DEBUG {
		// Enable relevant subsystems
		enable_subsystem(.Shaping, .TRACE)
		enable_subsystem(.Fonts, .DEBUG)
		enable_subsystem(.Atlas, .INFO)
		
		// Detailed shaping info
		shaping_config := get_subsystem_config(.Shaping)
		shaping_config.mode = .ALL  // No compression for detailed debugging
		shaping_config.max_per_frame = 0  // No limit
		set_subsystem_config(.Shaping, shaping_config)
	}
}

// Custom configuration for memory debugging
configure_memory_debugging :: proc() {
	when DEBUG {
		// Memory and performance subsystems
		enable_subsystem(.Memory, .DEBUG)
		enable_subsystem(.Performance, .INFO)
		enable_subsystem(.Atlas, .INFO)  // Atlas often related to memory
		
		// Show all memory warnings
		memory_config := get_subsystem_config(.Memory)
		memory_config.mode = .ALL
		memory_config.min_severity = .WARN
		set_subsystem_config(.Memory, memory_config)
	}
}

// ============================================================================
// FRAME MANAGEMENT HELPERS
// ============================================================================

// Begin a new frame with logging
begin_render_frame :: proc(frame_number: u64) {
	begin_frame(frame_number)
}

// End current frame with logging
end_render_frame :: proc() {
	end_frame()
}

// ============================================================================
// DEMONSTRATION FUNCTIONS
// ============================================================================

// Demonstrate different logging patterns
demo_logging_patterns :: proc(frame: u64) {
	// Basic info logging
	info(.Renderer, fmt.tprintf("Rendering frame %d", frame))
	
	// Debug-only verbose logging
	debug(.Renderer, fmt.tprintf("Frame %d: Pre-render state initialized", frame))
	
	// High-frequency logging with throttling
	for i in 0..<50 {
		info(.Shaping, fmt.tprintf("Shaped glyph %d", i), key = "shape_glyph")
	}
	
	// Change-only logging
	state := frame % 3 == 0 ? "Active" : "Idle"
	log_value(.Renderer, .INFO, "render_state", state, "Render state: %s")
	
	// Frame metrics (aggregated at end_frame)
	log_metric(.Batching, "draw_calls", 15)
	log_metric(.Batching, "vertices", 4523)
	
	// Conditional logging
	pressure := f32(frame) / 10.0
	log_if(pressure > 0.3, .Atlas, .WARN, 
		fmt.tprintf("Memory pressure: %.2f", pressure))
	
	// Performance tracking
	demo_expensive_operation()
	
	// Error logging
	if frame == 3 {
		error(.Renderer, "Simulated error on frame 3")
	}
}

demo_expensive_operation :: proc() {
	when DEBUG {
		scope := scoped(.Performance, "expensive_operation", log_duration = true)
		defer {}
		
		// Simulate work
		time.sleep(2 * time.Millisecond)
	}
}

// ============================================================================
// ENVIRONMENT VARIABLE DOCUMENTATION
// ============================================================================

// Documentation for release build environment variables
get_environment_help :: proc() -> string {
	return "Environment Variables for Release Build Logging:\n\n" +
	       "Predefined profiles:\n" +
	       "  LOG_PROFILE=text_rendering  - Text shaping, fonts, atlas\n" +
	       "  LOG_PROFILE=graphics        - Rendering, batching, textures\n" +
	       "  LOG_PROFILE=performance     - Performance and memory\n" +
	       "  LOG_PROFILE=full_debug      - Everything\n" +
	       "  LOG_PROFILE=minimal         - Only lifecycle and errors\n\n" +
	       "Individual subsystems:\n" +
	       "  LOG_LIFECYCLE=true    LOG_CONFIG=true       LOG_RENDERER=true\n" +
	       "  LOG_SHAPING=true      LOG_FONTS=true        LOG_ATLAS=true\n" +
	       "  LOG_BATCHING=true     LOG_TEXTURES=true     LOG_G2D=true\n" +
	       "  LOG_SVG=true          LOG_RSD=true          LOG_MEMORY=true\n" +
	       "  LOG_PERFORMANCE=true  LOG_IO=true           LOG_TESTING=true\n" +
	       "  LOG_DEBUG=true\n\n" +
	       "Output control:\n" +
	       "  LOGGING_TO_FILE=true        - Enable file logging\n" +
	       "  LOGGING_TO_CONSOLE=false     - Disable console logging\n" +
	       "  LOGGING_VERBOSE_CONTEXT=true - Show file:line:function\n\n" +
	       "Example usage:\n" +
	       "  LOG_PROFILE=text_rendering ./ruishin\n" +
	       "  LOG_ATLAS=true LOG_FONTS=true ./ruishin\n" +
	       "  LOGGING_TO_FILE=true LOG_PROFILE=performance ./ruishin"
}

// ============================================================================
// COMPILE-TIME FLAGS DOCUMENTATION
// ============================================================================

// Documentation for compile-time flags
get_compile_flags_help :: proc() -> string {
	return "Compile-Time Flags for Logging:\n\n" +
	       "Build modes:\n" +
	       "  DEBUG=true                        - Enable debug build with verbose logging\n" +
	       "  ENABLE_RUNTIME_DEBUG_LOGS=true    - Enable runtime debug logs (default: same as DEBUG)\n\n" +
	       "Output control:\n" +
	       "  LOGGING_TO_FILE=true             - Enable file logging at compile time\n" +
	       "  LOGGING_TO_CONSOLE=false          - Disable console logging\n" +
	       "  LOGGING_VERBOSE_CONTEXT=true      - Show file:line:function in logs\n\n" +
	       "Examples:\n" +
	       "  # Debug build with full logging\n" +
	       "  DEBUG=true LOGGING_VERBOSE_CONTEXT=true odin run build\n" +
	       "  \n" +
	       "  # Release build with runtime logging support\n" +
	       "  ENABLE_RUNTIME_DEBUG_LOGS=true odin run build\n" +
	       "  \n" +
	       "  # File logging only\n" +
	       "  LOGGING_TO_FILE=true LOGGING_TO_CONSOLE=false odin run build"
}

package logging

import "core:fmt"
import "core:strings"
import "core:time"
import "base:runtime"

// ============================================================================
// STATE MANAGEMENT
// ============================================================================

Log_State :: struct {
	// File logging
	file_handle: Log_File_Handle,
	
	// Subsystem configurations (runtime overrides)
	subsystem_configs: map[Subsystem]Subsystem_Config,
	
	// Tag-based enable/disable (legacy support)
	enabled_tags: map[string]bool,
	
	// Throttling state
	throttle_state: map[string]Throttle_Entry,
	
	// Frame tracking
	current_frame: u64,
	frame_metrics: map[Subsystem]Frame_Metrics,
	
	// Change detection
	last_values: map[string]string,
}

Throttle_Entry :: struct {
	count_this_frame:  int,
	count_this_second: int,
	last_frame:        u64,
	last_second_start: time.Time,
}

Frame_Metrics :: struct {
	total_logs:   int,
	by_severity:  [Severity]int,
	unique_keys:  map[string]int,
}

@(private)
state: Log_State

// ============================================================================
// INITIALIZATION
// ============================================================================

init :: proc() {
	state.file_handle = nil
	state.subsystem_configs = make(map[Subsystem]Subsystem_Config)
	state.enabled_tags = make(map[string]bool)
	state.throttle_state = make(map[string]Throttle_Entry)
	state.frame_metrics = make(map[Subsystem]Frame_Metrics)
	state.last_values = make(map[string]string)
	
	// Initialize with default configs
	for subsys in Subsystem {
		state.subsystem_configs[subsys] = get_default_config(subsys)
	}
	
	// Check environment variables for runtime control
	when ENABLE_RUNTIME_DEBUG_LOGS {
		init_from_env()
	}
}

shutdown :: proc() {
	close_file_logging()
	
	// Clear maps safely - don't delete the maps themselves
	// This avoids issues with string keys that might have been allocated with different allocators
	state.subsystem_configs = make(map[Subsystem]Subsystem_Config)
	state.enabled_tags = make(map[string]bool)
	state.throttle_state = make(map[string]Throttle_Entry)
	state.frame_metrics = make(map[Subsystem]Frame_Metrics)
	state.last_values = make(map[string]string)
}

// Initialize from environment variables
@(private)
init_from_env :: proc() {
	// Check LOG_PROFILE first
	if profile_str := log_get_env("LOG_PROFILE"); profile_str != "" {
		profile_lower := strings.to_lower(profile_str)
		switch profile_lower {
		case "silent":          use_profile(.Silent)
		case "minimal":         use_profile(.Minimal)
		case "text_rendering":  use_profile(.Text_Rendering)
		case "graphics":        use_profile(.Graphics)
		case "performance":     use_profile(.Performance)
		case "full_debug":      use_profile(.Full_Debug)
		}
		return
	}
	
	// Check individual subsystem env vars
	for env_var, subsys in env_var_to_subsystem {
		if value := log_get_env(env_var); value != "" {
			value_lower := strings.to_lower(value)
			if value_lower == "true" || value_lower == "1" {
				enable_subsystem(subsys)
			}
		}
	}
}

// ============================================================================
// FILE LOGGING
// ============================================================================

init_file_logging :: proc(filename: string) {
	when LOGGING_TO_FILE {
		path := fmt.tprintf("logs/%s", filename)
		state.file_handle = log_file_open(path)
	}
}

close_file_logging :: proc() {
	when LOGGING_TO_FILE {
		log_file_close(state.file_handle)
		state.file_handle = nil
	}
}

// ============================================================================
// PROFILE MANAGEMENT
// ============================================================================

use_profile :: proc(profile: Profile) {
	config := profiles[profile]
	
	// Disable all subsystems first
	for subsys in Subsystem {
		disable_subsystem(subsys)
	}
	
	// Enable subsystems in profile
	for subsys in config.subsystems {
		enable_subsystem(subsys, config.severity)
	}
}

// ============================================================================
// SUBSYSTEM MANAGEMENT
// ============================================================================

enable_subsystem :: proc(subsystem: Subsystem, min_severity := Severity.DEBUG) {
	if subsystem not_in state.subsystem_configs {
		state.subsystem_configs[subsystem] = get_default_config(subsystem)
	}
	
	config := state.subsystem_configs[subsystem]
	config.min_severity = min_severity
	state.subsystem_configs[subsystem] = config
	
	// Also enable legacy tag system
	tag := get_subsystem_tag(subsystem)
	state.enabled_tags[tag] = true
}

disable_subsystem :: proc(subsystem: Subsystem) {
	if subsystem not_in state.subsystem_configs {
		return
	}
	
	config := state.subsystem_configs[subsystem]
	config.min_severity = .FATAL // Effectively disable
	state.subsystem_configs[subsystem] = config
	
	// Also disable legacy tag system
	tag := get_subsystem_tag(subsystem)
	state.enabled_tags[tag] = false
}

get_subsystem_config :: proc(subsystem: Subsystem) -> Subsystem_Config {
	if config, ok := state.subsystem_configs[subsystem]; ok {
		return config
	}
	return get_default_config(subsystem)
}

set_subsystem_config :: proc(subsystem: Subsystem, config: Subsystem_Config) {
	state.subsystem_configs[subsystem] = config
}

// ============================================================================
// LEGACY TAG SYSTEM (for backward compatibility)
// ============================================================================

enable :: proc(tag: string) {
	state.enabled_tags[tag] = true
}

disable :: proc(tag: string) {
	state.enabled_tags[tag] = false
}

should :: proc(tag: string) -> bool {
	if v, ok := state.enabled_tags[tag]; ok {
		return v
	}
	return false
}

set_many :: proc(tags: []string, on: bool) {
	for t in tags {
		state.enabled_tags[t] = on
	}
}

// ============================================================================
// FRAME MANAGEMENT
// ============================================================================

begin_frame :: proc(frame_number: u64) {
	state.current_frame = frame_number
	
	// Clear frame metrics
	for subsys in Subsystem {
		if metrics, ok := &state.frame_metrics[subsys]; ok {
			delete(metrics.unique_keys)
		}
		state.frame_metrics[subsys] = Frame_Metrics{
			unique_keys = make(map[string]int),
		}
	}
}

end_frame :: proc() {
	// Output frame summaries for subsystems that requested them
	for subsys in Subsystem {
		config := get_subsystem_config(subsys)
		if !config.aggregate_frame do continue
		
		if metrics, ok := state.frame_metrics[subsys]; ok {
			if metrics.total_logs > 0 {
				output_frame_summary(subsys, metrics)
			}
		}
	}
}

@(private)
output_frame_summary :: proc(subsys: Subsystem, metrics: Frame_Metrics) {
	tag := get_subsystem_tag(subsys)
	msg := fmt.tprintf("Frame %d summary: %d logs total", 
		state.current_frame, metrics.total_logs)
	
	// Add severity breakdown if meaningful
	has_multiple_severities := false
	for sev in Severity {
		if metrics.by_severity[sev] > 0 {
			if has_multiple_severities {
				break
			}
			has_multiple_severities = true
		}
	}
	
	if has_multiple_severities {
		msg = fmt.tprintf("%s (", msg)
		first := true
		for sev in Severity {
			count := metrics.by_severity[sev]
			if count > 0 {
				if !first do msg = fmt.tprintf("%s, ", msg)
				msg = fmt.tprintf("%s%s:%d", msg, get_severity_string(sev), count)
				first = false
			}
		}
		msg = fmt.tprintf("%s)", msg)
	}
	
	write_output(.INFO, tag, msg, runtime.Source_Code_Location{})
}

// ============================================================================
// CORE LOGGING API
// ============================================================================

// Main logging entry point with all features
log :: proc(
	subsystem: Subsystem,
	severity: Severity,
	message: string,
	key := "",
	loc := #caller_location,
) {
	// Check if subsystem is enabled
	config := get_subsystem_config(subsystem)
	if !should_log(subsystem, severity, config) {
		return
	}
	
	// Handle different output modes
	tag := get_subsystem_tag(subsystem)
	
	switch config.mode {
	case .ALL:
		write_output(severity, tag, message, loc)
		
	case .COMPRESSED:
		log_compressed(subsystem, severity, tag, message, key, config, loc)
		
	case .CHANGES:
		log_on_change(subsystem, severity, tag, message, key, loc)
		
	case .SAMPLED:
		log_sampled(subsystem, severity, tag, message, key, config, loc)
		
	case .AGGREGATED:
		log_aggregated(subsystem, severity, message, key)
	}
	
	// Track metrics
	if metrics, ok := &state.frame_metrics[subsystem]; ok {
		metrics.total_logs += 1
		metrics.by_severity[severity] += 1
		if key != "" {
			metrics.unique_keys[key] += 1
		}
	}
}

// ============================================================================
// SEVERITY-SPECIFIC CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS
// ============================================================================

trace :: proc(subsystem: Subsystem, message: string, key := "", loc := #caller_location) {
	when DEBUG {
		log(subsystem, .TRACE, message, key, loc)
	}
}

debug :: proc(subsystem: Subsystem, message: string, key := "", loc := #caller_location) {
	when DEBUG {
		log(subsystem, .DEBUG, message, key, loc)
	}
}

info :: proc(subsystem: Subsystem, message: string, key := "", loc := #caller_location) {
	when ENABLE_RUNTIME_DEBUG_LOGS {
		log(subsystem, .INFO, message, key, loc)
	}
}

warn :: proc(subsystem: Subsystem, message: string, key := "", loc := #caller_location) {
	log(subsystem, .WARN, message, key, loc)
}

error :: proc(subsystem: Subsystem, message: string, key := "", loc := #caller_location) {
	log(subsystem, .ERROR, message, key, loc)
}

fatal :: proc(subsystem: Subsystem, message: string, loc := #caller_location) {
	log(subsystem, .FATAL, message, "", loc)
	// Fatal errors always get output regardless of config
	fmt.eprintf("[FATAL] %s\n", message)
}

// Direct logging - bypasses throttling for performance-critical logs
// Always available regardless of compile-time flags, but still integrates with logging framework
direct :: proc(subsystem: Subsystem, message: string, loc := #caller_location) {
	// Call the core log function to maintain framework integration (metrics, etc.)
	// but use empty key to bypass throttling logic
	log(subsystem, .WARN, message, "", loc)
	
	// Also output directly to ensure visibility under high load
	// This follows the same pattern as fatal() for reliability
	tag := get_subsystem_tag(subsystem)
	// Debug: Check if message is empty
	if len(message) == 0 {
		fmt.eprintf("[DEBUG] Empty message in logging.direct()\n")
	} else {
		fmt.eprintf("[INFO][%s] %s\n", tag, message)
	}
}

// Direct logging with format - avoids temporary allocator issues by doing formatting in-place
directf :: proc(subsystem: Subsystem, fmt_str: string, loc: runtime.Source_Code_Location = #caller_location) {
	// Format the message safely using a string builder to avoid temp alloc issues
	sb := strings.builder_make()
	defer strings.builder_destroy(&sb)
	fmt.sbprintf(&sb, fmt_str)
	message := strings.to_string(sb)
	
	// Now call the regular direct function
	direct(subsystem, message, loc)
}

// Direct logging with format and 3 int arguments - specific version for common case
directf3 :: proc(subsystem: Subsystem, fmt_str: string, a, b, c: int, loc: runtime.Source_Code_Location = #caller_location) {
	// Format the message safely using a string builder to avoid temp alloc issues
	sb := strings.builder_make()
	defer strings.builder_destroy(&sb)
	fmt.sbprintf(&sb, fmt_str, a, b, c)
	message := strings.to_string(sb)
	
	// Now call the regular direct function
	direct(subsystem, message, loc)
}

// ============================================================================
// LEGACY API (for backward compatibility)
// ============================================================================

// Legacy debug - compile-time guarded
debug_legacy :: proc(tag: string, msg: string) {
	when DEBUG {
		fmt.printf("[DEBUG][%s] %s\n", tag, msg)
		log_to_file("DEBUG", tag, msg)
	}
}

// Legacy info - runtime controlled
info_legacy :: proc(tag: string, msg: string) {
	if should(tag) {
		fmt.printf("[INFO][%s] %s\n", tag, msg)
		log_to_file("INFO", tag, msg)
	}
}

// ============================================================================
// OUTPUT FUNCTIONS
// ============================================================================

@(private)
write_output :: proc(
	severity: Severity,
	tag: string,
	message: string,
	loc: runtime.Source_Code_Location,
) {
	severity_str := get_severity_string(severity)
	
	// Build output string
	output: string
	
	when LOGGING_VERBOSE_CONTEXT {
		// Source_Code_Location is always valid when LOGGING_VERBOSE_CONTEXT is enabled
		output = fmt.tprintf("[%s][%s][%s:%d:%s] %s",
			severity_str, tag,
			loc.file_path, loc.line, loc.procedure,
			message)
	} else {
		output = fmt.tprintf("[%s][%s] %s", severity_str, tag, message)
	}
	
	// Console output
	when LOGGING_TO_CONSOLE {
		fmt.println(output)
	}
	
	// File output
	when LOGGING_TO_FILE {
		log_to_file(severity_str, tag, message)
	}
	
	// Future: Debug window output
	when LOGGING_TO_DEBUG_WINDOW {
		// TODO: Send to on-screen overlay
	}
}

@(private)
log_to_file :: proc(level: string, tag: string, msg: string) {
	when LOGGING_TO_FILE {
		s := fmt.tprintf("[%s][%s] %s\n", level, tag, msg)
		log_file_write(state.file_handle, s)
	}
}

// ============================================================================
// RENDERER HELPERS (legacy support)
// ============================================================================

RENDER_TAG_SHAPING :: "renderer.shaping"
RENDER_TAG_FONTS :: "renderer.fonts"

set_renderer_logging :: proc(mode: string) {
	m := strings.to_lower(mode)
	switch m {
	case "none":
		disable(RENDER_TAG_SHAPING)
		disable(RENDER_TAG_FONTS)
	case "fonts":
		enable(RENDER_TAG_FONTS)
		disable(RENDER_TAG_SHAPING)
	case "shaping":
		enable(RENDER_TAG_SHAPING)
		disable(RENDER_TAG_FONTS)
	case "all":
		enable(RENDER_TAG_SHAPING)
		enable(RENDER_TAG_FONTS)
	}
}

DEBUG: Source content: #+feature dynamic-literals

package logging

import "core:fmt"
import "core:time"
import "base:runtime"

// ============================================================================
// COMPRESSED LOGGING (combines similar messages)
// ============================================================================

@(private)
log_compressed :: proc(
	subsystem: Subsystem,
	severity: Severity,
	tag: string,
	message: string,
	key: string,
	config: Subsystem_Config,
	loc: runtime.Source_Code_Location,
) {
	// Use key or full message as throttle key
	throttle_key := key if key != "" else message
	full_key := fmt.tprintf("%s:%s", tag, throttle_key)
	
	// Get or create throttle entry
	entry := state.throttle_state[full_key] or_else Throttle_Entry{}
	
	// Check if we're in a new frame
	if entry.last_frame != state.current_frame {
		// New frame - reset frame counter, but output previous count if > 1
		if entry.count_this_frame > 1 && config.show_count {
			count_msg := fmt.tprintf("%s (x%d last frame)", message, entry.count_this_frame)
			write_output(severity, tag, count_msg, loc)
		}
		entry.count_this_frame = 0
		entry.last_frame = state.current_frame
	}
	
	// Check per-frame limit
	if config.max_per_frame > 0 && entry.count_this_frame >= config.max_per_frame {
		entry.count_this_frame += 1
		state.throttle_state[full_key] = entry
		return
	}
	
	// Check per-second limit
	now := time.now()
	if time.diff(entry.last_second_start, now) > time.Second {
		entry.count_this_second = 0
		entry.last_second_start = now
	}
	
	if config.max_per_second > 0 && entry.count_this_second >= config.max_per_second {
		entry.count_this_second += 1
		entry.count_this_frame += 1
		state.throttle_state[full_key] = entry
		return
	}
	
	// Output the log
	if config.show_count && entry.count_this_frame > 0 {
		count_msg := fmt.tprintf("%s (x%d this frame)", message, entry.count_this_frame + 1)
		write_output(severity, tag, count_msg, loc)
	} else {
		write_output(severity, tag, message, loc)
	}
	
	// Update counters
	entry.count_this_frame += 1
	entry.count_this_second += 1
	state.throttle_state[full_key] = entry
}

// ============================================================================
// CHANGE-ONLY LOGGING (only log when value changes)
// ============================================================================

@(private)
log_on_change :: proc(
	subsystem: Subsystem,
	severity: Severity,
	tag: string,
	message: string,
	key: string,
	loc: runtime.Source_Code_Location,
) {
	// Use key to track value changes
	if key == "" {
		// No key provided, just log normally
		write_output(severity, tag, message, loc)
		return
	}
	
	full_key := fmt.tprintf("%s:%s", tag, key)
	
	// Check if value changed
	last_value, exists := state.last_values[full_key]
	if !exists || last_value != message {
		write_output(severity, tag, message, loc)
		state.last_values[full_key] = message
	}
}

// ============================================================================
// SAMPLED LOGGING (every Nth occurrence)
// ============================================================================

@(private)
log_sampled :: proc(
	subsystem: Subsystem,
	severity: Severity,
	tag: string,
	message: string,
	key: string,
	config: Subsystem_Config,
	loc: runtime.Source_Code_Location,
) {
	// Use key or message as sample key
	sample_key := key if key != "" else message
	full_key := fmt.tprintf("%s:%s", tag, sample_key)
	
	// Get or create throttle entry
	entry := state.throttle_state[full_key] or_else Throttle_Entry{}
	
	// Sample every Nth call (use max_per_frame as sample interval)
	sample_interval := config.max_per_frame if config.max_per_frame > 0 else 10
	
	if entry.count_this_frame % sample_interval == 0 {
		sample_msg := message
		if config.show_count {
			sample_msg = fmt.tprintf("%s (sample %d/%d)", 
				message, entry.count_this_frame + 1, sample_interval)
		}
		write_output(severity, tag, sample_msg, loc)
	}
	
	entry.count_this_frame += 1
	state.throttle_state[full_key] = entry
}

// ============================================================================
// AGGREGATED LOGGING (collect and output at frame end)
// ============================================================================

@(private)
log_aggregated :: proc(
	subsystem: Subsystem,
	severity: Severity,
	message: string,
	key: string,
) {
	// Just track in metrics, will be output at end_frame()
	if metrics, ok := &state.frame_metrics[subsystem]; ok {
		if key != "" {
			metrics.unique_keys[key] += 1
		}
	}
}

// ============================================================================
// SPECIALIZED LOGGING FUNCTIONS
// ============================================================================

// Log a value with automatic change detection
log_value :: proc(
	subsystem: Subsystem,
	severity: Severity,
	key: string,
	value: $T,
	format_str: string,
	loc := #caller_location,
) {
	message := fmt.tprintf(format_str, value)
	log_on_change(subsystem, severity, get_subsystem_tag(subsystem), message, key, loc)
}

// Log a metric that will be aggregated at frame end
log_metric :: proc(
	subsystem: Subsystem,
	key: string,
	value: int,
) {
	if metrics, ok := &state.frame_metrics[subsystem]; ok {
		current := metrics.unique_keys[key] or_else 0
		metrics.unique_keys[key] = current + value
	}
}

// Conditional logging - only log if condition is true
log_if :: proc(
	condition: bool,
	subsystem: Subsystem,
	severity: Severity,
	message: string,
	loc := #caller_location,
) {
	if condition {
		log(subsystem, severity, message, "", loc)
	}
}

// ============================================================================
// SCOPED LOGGING (for function entry/exit tracking)
// ============================================================================

Scope_Logger :: struct {
	subsystem: Subsystem,
	name:      string,
	start:     time.Time,
	loc:       runtime.Source_Code_Location,
}

// Begin a logging scope (for TRACE level function tracking)
begin_scope :: proc(
	subsystem: Subsystem,
	name: string,
	loc := #caller_location,
) -> Scope_Logger {
	when DEBUG {
		trace(subsystem, fmt.tprintf("→ %s", name), "", loc)
	}
	
	return Scope_Logger{
		subsystem = subsystem,
		name      = name,
		start     = time.now(),
		loc       = loc,
	}
}

// End a logging scope and optionally log duration
end_scope :: proc(scope: Scope_Logger, log_duration := false) {
	when DEBUG {
		if log_duration {
			duration := time.since(scope.start)
			trace(scope.subsystem, 
				fmt.tprintf("← %s (%.2fms)", scope.name, time.duration_milliseconds(duration)),
				"", scope.loc)
		} else {
			trace(scope.subsystem, fmt.tprintf("← %s", scope.name), "", scope.loc)
		}
	}
}

// Defer-friendly scope logging
scoped :: proc(
	subsystem: Subsystem,
	name: string,
	log_duration := false,
	loc := #caller_location,
) -> Scope_Logger {
	scope := begin_scope(subsystem, name, loc)
	return scope
}

// ============================================================================
// PERFORMANCE HELPERS
// ============================================================================

// Time a block of code and log if it exceeds threshold
timed :: proc(
	subsystem: Subsystem,
	name: string,
	threshold_ms: f64,
	loc := #caller_location,
) -> time.Time {
	return time.now()
}

check_timing :: proc(
	subsystem: Subsystem,
	name: string,
	start: time.Time,
	threshold_ms: f64,
	loc := #caller_location,
) {
	duration := time.since(start)
	duration_ms := time.duration_milliseconds(duration)
	
	if duration_ms > threshold_ms {
		warn(subsystem, 
			fmt.tprintf("%s took %.2fms (threshold: %.2fms)", name, duration_ms, threshold_ms),
			"", loc)
	}
}

// ============================================================================
// DEBUG ASSERTIONS
// ============================================================================

// Debug-only assertion with logging
assert_debug :: proc(
	condition: bool,
	subsystem: Subsystem,
	message: string,
	loc := #caller_location,
) {
	when DEBUG {
		if !condition {
			error(subsystem, fmt.tprintf("ASSERTION FAILED: %s", message), "", loc)
			panic(message)
		}
	}
}

// Runtime assertion with logging (always compiled)
assert_runtime :: proc(
	condition: bool,
	subsystem: Subsystem,
	message: string,
	loc := #caller_location,
) {
	if !condition {
		fatal(subsystem, fmt.tprintf("ASSERTION FAILED: %s", message), loc)
		panic(message)
	}
}

package logging

import "base:runtime"

// logging_config.odin - the configuration constant of the logging system
// the "vocabulary" of the logging system

// ============================================================================
// COMPILE-TIME CONFIGURATION
// ============================================================================

// Build modes
DEBUG :: #config(DEBUG, false)
ENABLE_RUNTIME_DEBUG_LOGS :: #config(ENABLE_RUNTIME_DEBUG_LOGS, DEBUG)

// Context verbosity - includes file/function/line in logs
LOGGING_VERBOSE_CONTEXT :: #config(LOGGING_VERBOSE_CONTEXT, DEBUG)

// Output destinations - can be combined
LOGGING_TO_CONSOLE :: #config(LOGGING_TO_CONSOLE, true)
LOGGING_TO_FILE :: #config(LOGGING_TO_FILE, false)
LOGGING_TO_DEBUG_WINDOW :: #config(LOGGING_TO_DEBUG_WINDOW, false) // Future: on-screen overlay

//overall debug flag to enable all diagnostics and stress tests
STRESS_TEST :: #config(STRESS_TEST, false)

// 2D renderer burn/limit test — activates high-load tile 33 in release mode.
// Deliberately has NO logging side-effects (no || STRESS_TEST).
// Build: STRESS_2D=true odin run build/build.odin -file
STRESS_2D :: #config(STRESS_2D, false)

// Debug Flags - These flags are true if either STRESS_TEST or their specific flag is enabled
DEBUG_INIT :: #config(DEBUG_INIT, false) || STRESS_TEST // Initialization process debugging
DEBUG_SVG :: #config(DEBUG_SVG, false) || STRESS_TEST // SVG loading and processing debugging
DEBUG_RSD :: #config(DEBUG_RSD, false) || STRESS_TEST // RSD format operations debugging
DEBUG_TILE :: false // Tile rendering debugging
DEBUG_CLEANUP :: #config(DEBUG_CLEANUP, false) || STRESS_TEST // Cleanup operations debugging
DEBUG_VERTICES :: #config(DEBUG_VERTICES, false) || STRESS_TEST // Log vertex data for G2D rendering (can be very verbose)
DEBUG_ATLAS :: #config(DEBUG_ATLAS, false) || STRESS_TEST // Atlas debugging and diagnostics
DEBUG_OFFSCREEN :: #config(DEBUG_OFFSCREEN, false) || STRESS_TEST // Offscreen rendering debugging (verbose)
DEBUG_G2D :: #config(DEBUG_G2D, false) || STRESS_TEST // G2D rendering debugging // Enable for G2D/blur diagnostics

// Performance Flags - These flags enable performance measurement for specific subsystems
// Follows the same pattern as debug flags - enabled by STRESS_TEST or specific flag
PERF_RSD :: #config(PERF_RSD, false) || STRESS_TEST // RSD performance measurement
PERF_SVG :: #config(PERF_SVG, false) || STRESS_TEST // SVG performance measurement
PERF_G2D :: #config(PERF_G2D, false) || STRESS_TEST // G2D performance measurement
PERF_ATLAS :: #config(PERF_ATLAS, false) || STRESS_TEST // Atlas performance measurement
PERF_RENDERER :: #config(PERF_RENDERER, false) || STRESS_TEST // Renderer performance measurement
PERF_SHAPING :: #config(PERF_SHAPING, false) || STRESS_TEST // Text shaping performance measurement
PERF_STATS :: #config(PERF_STATS, false) || STRESS_TEST  // Performance diagnostics and statistics

// Asset Management Architecture Flags , not wired yet
USE_NEW_ASSET_ARCHITECTURE :: #config(USE_NEW_ASSET_ARCHITECTURE, true) // Enable new asset management system (now default)
DEBUG_NEW_ASSETS :: #config(DEBUG_NEW_ASSETS, false) || STRESS_TEST // Debug new asset architecture

// Detailed Logging Flags - These control specific logging outputs
// Text and Shaping
LOG_VERBOSE_SHAPING :: #config(LOG_VERBOSE_SHAPING, false) // Verbose text shaping information
LOG_TEXT_SPAN :: #config(LOG_TEXT_SPAN, false) // Log text span processing
LOG_BIDI_RAW :: #config(LOG_BIDI_RAW, false) // Log raw bidi processing
LOG_HB_GLYPHS :: #config(LOG_HB_GLYPHS, false) // Log HarfBuzz glyph processing
LOG_HB_WARN_NAIVE :: #config(LOG_HB_WARN_NAIVE, false) // Warn about naive shaping
LOG_BIDI :: #config(LOG_BIDI, false) // Log bidirectional text processing

// Atlas Debugging
LOG_ATLAS_EVICT :: #config(LOG_ATLAS_EVICT, false) // Log atlas eviction events
LOG_ATLAS_WRAP :: #config(LOG_ATLAS_WRAP, false) // Log atlas wrapping events
LOG_ATLAS_PRESSURE :: #config(LOG_ATLAS_PRESSURE, false) // Log atlas memory pressure
LOG_ATLAS_VRAM :: #config(LOG_ATLAS_VRAM, false) // Log VRAM usage
LOG_ATLAS_DIRTY :: #config(LOG_ATLAS_DIRTY, false) // Log dirty atlas regions
LOG_ATLAS_MISS :: #config(LOG_ATLAS_MISS, false) // Log atlas cache misses
LOG_GI_STALE :: #config(LOG_GI_STALE, false) // Log stale glyph info
LOG_GI_PREUSE :: #config(LOG_GI_PREUSE, false) // Log glyph info pre-use
LOG_ATLAS_ROWS :: #config(LOG_ATLAS_ROWS, false) // Log atlas row operations
LOG_ATLAS_CACHE :: #config(LOG_ATLAS_CACHE, false) // Log atlas caching
LOG_ATLAS_TOUCH :: #config(LOG_ATLAS_TOUCH, false) // Log atlas touch events
LOG_GI_FENCE :: #config(LOG_GI_FENCE, false) // Log glyph info fencing
LOG_UV_STABILITY :: #config(LOG_UV_STABILITY, false) // Log UV stability
LOG_CACHE_STABILITY :: #config(LOG_CACHE_STABILITY, false) // Log cache stability
LOG_ATLAS_RING_PIN :: #config(LOG_ATLAS_RING_PIN, false) // Log ring pinning
LOG_ATLAS_DEFER :: #config(LOG_ATLAS_DEFER, false) // Log deferred operations
LOG_ATLAS_REPACK :: #config(LOG_ATLAS_REPACK, false) // Log repacking operations
LOG_ATLAS_SUM :: #config(LOG_ATLAS_SUM, false) // Log atlas summaries
LOG_ATLAS_COMPACT :: #config(LOG_ATLAS_COMPACT, false) // Log compaction events
LOG_ATLAS_INVARIANT :: #config(LOG_ATLAS_INVARIANT, false) // Log invariant checks
LOG_ATLAS_EVICT_GATE :: #config(LOG_ATLAS_EVICT_GATE, false) // Log eviction gating
LOG_ATLAS_BARRIER_SKIP :: #config(LOG_ATLAS_BARRIER_SKIP, false) // Log barrier skipping

// Performance and Memory
LOG_PERF_SUMMARY :: #config(LOG_PERF_SUMMARY, false) // Log performance summaries
LOG_TEXT_COLOR_CHECK :: #config(LOG_TEXT_COLOR_CHECK, false) // Log text color checks

// Memory tracking — compile with -define:MEMORY_TRACKING=true to enable leak detection.
// Activates allocation-site dump every 300 frames and a full leak report on cleanup.
// Separate from DEBUG and LOG_MEMORY; adds no noise from other subsystems.
// Build: BUILD_MODE=debug odin run build/build.odin -file -define:MEMORY_TRACKING=true
MEMORY_TRACKING :: #config(MEMORY_TRACKING, false)

// Optional: force pipeline MSAA sample count to 1 for performance A/B
//G2D_FORCE_SAMPLE1 :: #config(G2D_FORCE_SAMPLE1, false)

// Diagnostics flags for text spans
//LOG_TEXT_SPAN :: #config(LOG_TEXT_SPAN, false)
//LOG_TEXT_COLOR_CHECK :: #config(LOG_TEXT_COLOR_CHECK, false)
// Always-on lightweight perf summary (prints every ~60 frames)
//LOG_PERF_SUMMARY :: #config(LOG_PERF_SUMMARY, false)
// Inter-run gap factor in "em" (space width). Applied between spans when direction/script changes
//RUN_GAP_EM_PCT :: #config(RUN_GAP_EM_PCT, 0.05)
// Debug: pad the first glyph quad slightly to the left to safeguard negative side-bearing


// ============================================================================
// SEVERITY LEVELS
// ============================================================================

Severity :: enum {
	TRACE,  // Finest detail (function entry/exit, every iteration)
	DEBUG,  // Development debugging (state dumps, detailed flow)
	INFO,   // Important operational events (cache misses, state changes)
	WARN,   // Potential issues (high pressure, degraded performance)
	ERROR,  // Recoverable errors (failed to load resource)
	FATAL,  // Unrecoverable errors (assertion failures)
}

severity_strings := [Severity]string {
	.TRACE = "TRACE",
	.DEBUG = "DEBUG",
	.INFO  = "INFO",
	.WARN  = "WARN",
	.ERROR = "ERROR",
	.FATAL = "FATAL",
}

// ============================================================================
// SUBSYSTEMS
// ============================================================================

Subsystem :: enum {
	// Core application
	Lifecycle,    // App startup, shutdown, major state transitions
	Config,       // Configuration loading and validation
	
	// Rendering
	Renderer,     // High-level rendering operations
	Shaping,      // Text shaping (HarfBuzz, bidi)
	Fonts,        // Font loading and management
	Atlas,        // Font atlas and glyph caching
	Batching,     // Render batching and draw calls
	Textures,     // Texture management
	
	// Graphics
	G2D,          // 2D graphics primitives
	SVG,          // SVG parsing and rendering
	RSD,          // RSD format operations
	
	// System
	Memory,       // Memory allocation and tracking
	Performance,  // Performance metrics and profiling
	IO,           // File I/O operations
	Asset,        // Asset loading and management
	
	// Development
	Testing,      // Test execution and results
	Debug,        // General debug output
}

subsystem_tags := [Subsystem]string {
	.Lifecycle   = "lifecycle",
	.Config      = "config",
	.Renderer    = "renderer",
	.Shaping     = "renderer.shaping",
	.Fonts       = "renderer.fonts",
	.Atlas       = "renderer.atlas",
	.Batching    = "renderer.batching",
	.Textures    = "renderer.textures",
	.G2D         = "g2d",
	.SVG         = "svg",
	.RSD         = "rsd",
	.Memory      = "memory",
	.Performance = "perf",
	.IO          = "io",
	.Asset       = "asset",
	.Testing     = "testing",
	.Debug       = "debug",
}

// ============================================================================
// OUTPUT MODES
// ============================================================================

Output_Mode :: enum {
	ALL,        // Every log line immediately (best for debugger)
	AGGREGATED, // Group by subsystem, show counts at frame/second end
	COMPRESSED, // Similar messages combined: "Shaped text (x47 this frame)"
	CHANGES,    // Only log when value changes from previous
	SAMPLED,    // Every Nth occurrence
}

// ============================================================================
// SUBSYSTEM-SPECIFIC CONFIGURATION
// ============================================================================

Subsystem_Config :: struct {
	min_severity:     Severity,     // Minimum severity to log
	mode:             Output_Mode,  // How to output logs
	max_per_frame:    int,          // Max logs per frame (0 = unlimited)
	max_per_second:   int,          // Max logs per second (0 = unlimited)
	show_count:       bool,         // Show repetition count in compressed mode
	aggregate_frame:  bool,         // Show frame summary
}

// Default configurations per subsystem
default_subsystem_configs := #partial [Subsystem]Subsystem_Config {
	.Lifecycle = {
		min_severity    = .WARN,  // Reduced from .INFO to .WARN to reduce log volume
		mode            = .ALL,
		max_per_frame   = 0,
		max_per_second  = 0,
		show_count      = false,
		aggregate_frame = false,
	},
	.Config = {
		min_severity    = .WARN,  // Reduced from .INFO to .WARN to reduce log volume
		mode            = .ALL,
		max_per_frame   = 0,
		max_per_second  = 0,
		show_count      = false,
		aggregate_frame = false,
	},
	.Shaping = {
		min_severity    = .INFO,
		mode            = .COMPRESSED,
		max_per_frame   = 10,
		max_per_second  = 100,
		show_count      = true,
		aggregate_frame = true,
	},
	.Fonts = {
		min_severity    = .INFO,
		mode            = .CHANGES,
		max_per_frame   = 5,
		max_per_second  = 50,
		show_count      = true,
		aggregate_frame = false,
	},
	.Atlas = {
		min_severity    = .INFO,
		mode            = .COMPRESSED,
		max_per_frame   = 8,
		max_per_second  = 80,
		show_count      = true,
		aggregate_frame = true,
	},
	.Batching = {
		min_severity    = .DEBUG,
		mode            = .AGGREGATED,
		max_per_frame   = 5,
		max_per_second  = 50,
		show_count      = true,
		aggregate_frame = true,
	},
	.Textures = {
		min_severity    = .INFO,
		mode            = .CHANGES,
		max_per_frame   = 5,
		max_per_second  = 50,
		show_count      = true,
		aggregate_frame = false,
	},
	.G2D = {
		min_severity    = .DEBUG,
		mode            = .ALL,  // Changed from .COMPRESSED to .ALL to ensure performance logs aren't compressed
		max_per_frame   = 20,    // Increased from 10 to 20 to allow more performance logs
		max_per_second  = 200,   // Increased from 100 to 200 to handle performance logging bursts
		show_count      = true,
		aggregate_frame = false,
	},
	.SVG = {
		min_severity    = .INFO,
		mode            = .ALL,
		max_per_frame   = 0,
		max_per_second  = 0,
		show_count      = false,
		aggregate_frame = false,
	},
	.RSD = {
		min_severity    = .INFO,
		mode            = .ALL,
		max_per_frame   = 0,
		max_per_second  = 0,
		show_count      = false,
		aggregate_frame = false,
	},
	.Memory = {
		min_severity    = .WARN,
		mode            = .CHANGES,
		max_per_frame   = 3,
		max_per_second  = 30,
		show_count      = true,
		aggregate_frame = true,
	},
	.Performance = {
		min_severity    = .INFO,
		mode            = .AGGREGATED,
		max_per_frame   = 1,
		max_per_second  = 10,
		show_count      = false,
		aggregate_frame = true,
	},
	.IO = {
		min_severity    = .INFO,
		mode            = .ALL,
		max_per_frame   = 0,
		max_per_second  = 0,
		show_count      = false,
		aggregate_frame = false,
	},
	.Asset = {
		min_severity    = .INFO,
		mode            = .CHANGES,
		max_per_frame   = 5,
		max_per_second  = 50,
		show_count      = true,
		aggregate_frame = false,
	},
	.Testing = {
		min_severity    = .INFO,
		mode            = .ALL,
		max_per_frame   = 0,
		max_per_second  = 0,
		show_count      = false,
		aggregate_frame = false,
	},
	.Debug = {
		min_severity    = .DEBUG,
		mode            = .ALL,
		max_per_frame   = 0,
		max_per_second  = 0,
		show_count      = false,
		aggregate_frame = false,
	},
}

// ============================================================================
// LOGGING PROFILES
// ============================================================================
// Predefined combinations for common debugging scenarios

Profile :: enum {
	Silent,          // No logging
	Minimal,         // Only lifecycle and errors
	Text_Rendering,  // Text shaping, fonts, atlas
	Graphics,        // Rendering, batching, textures
	Performance,     // Performance and memory
	Full_Debug,      // Everything
}

Profile_Config :: struct {
	subsystems: []Subsystem,
	severity:   Severity,
}

profiles := [Profile]Profile_Config {
	.Silent = {
		subsystems = {},
		severity   = .FATAL,
	},
	.Minimal = {
		subsystems = {.Lifecycle, .Config},
		severity   = .INFO,
	},
	.Text_Rendering = {
		subsystems = {.Shaping, .Fonts, .Atlas},
		severity   = .DEBUG,
	},
	.Graphics = {
		subsystems = {.Renderer, .Batching, .Textures, .G2D},
		severity   = .DEBUG,
	},
	.Performance = {
		subsystems = {.Performance, .Memory, .Atlas},
		severity   = .INFO,
	},
	.Full_Debug = {
		subsystems = {
			.Lifecycle, .Config, .Renderer, .Shaping, .Fonts, .Atlas,
			.Batching, .Textures, .G2D, .SVG, .RSD, .Memory,
			.Performance, .IO, .Testing, .Debug,
		},
		severity   = .TRACE,
	},
}

// ============================================================================
// ENVIRONMENT VARIABLE MAPPING
// ============================================================================
// Maps environment variables to subsystems for runtime control in release builds

env_var_to_subsystem := map[string]Subsystem {
	"LOG_LIFECYCLE"    = .Lifecycle,
	"LOG_CONFIG"       = .Config,
	"LOG_RENDERER"     = .Renderer,
	"LOG_SHAPING"      = .Shaping,
	"LOG_FONTS"        = .Fonts,
	"LOG_ATLAS"        = .Atlas,
	"LOG_BATCHING"     = .Batching,
	"LOG_TEXTURES"     = .Textures,
	"LOG_G2D"          = .G2D,
	"LOG_SVG"          = .SVG,
	"LOG_RSD"          = .RSD,
	"LOG_MEMORY"       = .Memory,
	"LOG_PERFORMANCE"  = .Performance,
	"LOG_IO"           = .IO,
	"LOG_TESTING"      = .Testing,
	"LOG_DEBUG"        = .Debug,
}

// ============================================================================
// HELPER FUNCTIONS
// ============================================================================

get_subsystem_tag :: proc(subsystem: Subsystem) -> string {
	return subsystem_tags[subsystem]
}

get_severity_string :: proc(severity: Severity) -> string {
	return severity_strings[severity]
}

get_default_config :: proc(subsystem: Subsystem) -> Subsystem_Config {
	return default_subsystem_configs[subsystem]
}

should_log :: proc(subsystem: Subsystem, severity: Severity, config: Subsystem_Config) -> bool {
	return severity >= config.min_severity
}

DEBUG: Source length: 876
DEBUG: Source content: #+build !js
package logging

import "core:os"

// Native: file handle is os.File
Log_File_Handle :: ^os.File

// Read environment variable, returning empty string if unset.
log_get_env :: proc(key: string) -> string {
	return os.get_env_alloc(key, context.temp_allocator)
}

// Open log file for writing; no-op if LOGGING_TO_FILE is false.
log_file_open :: proc(path: string) -> Log_File_Handle {
	when LOGGING_TO_FILE {
		if !os.exists("logs") do os.make_directory("logs")
		h, err := os.open(path, os.O_WRONLY | os.O_CREATE | os.O_TRUNC, 0o666)
		if err == nil do return h
	}
	return nil
}

// Close log file handle.
log_file_close :: proc(h: Log_File_Handle) {
	when LOGGING_TO_FILE {
		if h != nil do os.close(h)
	}
}

// Write string to log file.
log_file_write :: proc(h: Log_File_Handle, s: string) {
	when LOGGING_TO_FILE {
		if h != nil do os.write_string(h, s)
	}
}
